SCHEMA-BLDG-CONST.generation_note: Engineered BOQ template v1.0 — civil building construction activities; ENR_CCI regional basis unchanged from skeleton. | v1.0-priced: usd_cost_baseline + multiplier tables (USA-base; public-benchmark, verify=true).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-BLDG-CONST.spec_example: 500 m² · RCC M25 · Industrial shed · 2 floors

fields.label: Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the BOQ activity line. This drives visible specification fields and the quantity unit (m², m³, kg, or No.).

fields.label: Structure Type
fields.hint: Building typology; applied as a structure_type_factor in budget and detailed cost build-up.

fields.label: No. of floors
fields.hint: Number of storeys for the building or block this line belongs to (1–20).

fields.label: RCC Grade
fields.hint: Concrete grade for PCC/RCC lines. Shown when the activity is PCC or RCC.

fields.label: Steel Grade
fields.hint: Reinforcement grade for TMT steel supply lines.

fields.label: Brick/Block Type
fields.hint: Masonry unit type for brick or block work lines.
fields.conditional_display: activity_subtype === "Brick Masonry (Common)" || activity_subtype === "Brick Masonry (Facing)" || activity_subtype === "AAC Block Masonry"

fields.label: Thickness
fields.hint: Thickness in mm (typical plaster 12–20; epoxy systems often 2–3). Used for compliance checks on plaster, flooring, epoxy, and false floor/ceiling lines.
fields.conditional_display: activity_subtype === "12mm Cement Plaster (Internal)" || activity_subtype === "15mm Cement Plaster (External)" || activity_subtype === "Gypsum Plaster" || activity_subtype === "Ceramic Floor Tiles (600×600mm)" || activity_subtype === "Granite Flooring" || activity_subtype === "Vitrified Tiles (Heavy Duty)" || activity_subtype === "Epoxy Flooring" || activity_subtype === "False Flooring (Raised Access)" || activity_subtype === "False Ceiling (Gypsum Board)" || activity_subtype === "False Ceiling (Mineral Fiber)" || activity_subtype === "False Ceiling (Metal Pan)"

fields.label: Finish Grade
fields.hint: Quality tier for paint and flooring finishes; drives finish_grade_factor in the full cost formula.
fields.conditional_display: activity_subtype === "Emulsion Paint (Interior)" || activity_subtype === "Texture Paint (Exterior)" || activity_subtype === "Ceramic Floor Tiles (600×600mm)" || activity_subtype === "Granite Flooring" || activity_subtype === "Vitrified Tiles (Heavy Duty)" || activity_subtype === "Epoxy Flooring" || activity_subtype === "False Flooring (Raised Access)"

fields.label: Concrete member
fields.hint: Structural member for PCC/RCC lines; used with clear cover for IS 456 footing checks.

fields.label: Clear cover to reinforcement
fields.hint: Nominal clear cover (mm) for RCC; for footings, values below 25 mm conflict with IS 456:2018.

fields.label: Masonry wall thickness
fields.hint: Finished wall thickness (mm) for load-bearing masonry checks per IS 1905:1987.
fields.conditional_display: activity_subtype === "Brick Masonry (Common)" || activity_subtype === "Brick Masonry (Facing)" || activity_subtype === "AAC Block Masonry"

fields.label: Load-bearing masonry wall
fields.hint: Set to Yes for structural masonry; triggers wall thickness check against IS 1905:1987.
fields.conditional_display: activity_subtype === "Brick Masonry (Common)" || activity_subtype === "Brick Masonry (Facing)" || activity_subtype === "AAC Block Masonry"

fields.label: Quantity unit
fields.hint: Measurement unit for this line: m² (finishes, formwork, cladding), m³ (earthwork, concrete), kg (rebar), No. (doors/windows).

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Measured quantity in the unit selected above (bill quantity for this activity).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (activity_subtype == "RCC M25" OR activity_subtype == "RCC M30") AND concrete_member == "Footing" AND clear_cover_mm > 0 AND clear_cover_mm < 25
compliance_rules.message: Clear cover to reinforcement is below 25 mm for footing-grade RCC; verify against IS 456:2018 clause 26.4.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 456:2018 clause 26.4

compliance_rules.condition_expr: load_bearing_masonry == "Yes" AND masonry_wall_thickness_mm > 0 AND masonry_wall_thickness_mm < 230
compliance_rules.message: Load-bearing masonry wall thickness below 230 mm may not satisfy IS 1905:1987; confirm structural design.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: activity_subtype == "15mm Cement Plaster (External)" AND thickness_mm > 0 AND thickness_mm < 12
compliance_rules.message: External cement plaster thickness below 12 mm may not meet IS 1661:1972 typical provisions; confirm specification.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: activity_subtype == "Epoxy Flooring" AND thickness_mm > 0 AND thickness_mm < 2
compliance_rules.message: Epoxy flooring thickness below 2 mm is below common manufacturer minimums; verify product data sheet.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Manufacturer specification minimum

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_rate_per_m2 * structure_type_factor * quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_rate_per_m2 * structure_type_factor * finish_grade_factor * quantity

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-BLOWER-FAN.generation_note: Engineered BOQ template v1.0 — blower/fan site activities; ENR_CCI regional basis preserved from output file. | RATE TABLE ADDED v1.0-priced: usd_cost_baseline (6 activities, USD/Job, USA-base) + cost_multiplier_tables (size_factor, foundation_factor). Public-benchmark estimates, all verify=true — calibrate against vendor/DSR data.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick ±30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget ±20%. Tier 3 = Detailed ±10%.

fields.label: Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Fan/Blower Size
fields.hint: Impeller/wheel diameter in mm

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Rated motor power in kW

fields.label: Foundation Type
fields.hint: Support/foundation arrangement for the fan or blower.

fields.label: Alignment Required
fields.hint: Coupling/shaft alignment method for commissioning.

fields.label: Ductwork Connection
fields.hint: Interface between equipment and ductwork.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: motor_power > 110 AND alignment_required == 'Not required'
compliance_rules.message: Motors >110 kW require laser alignment — IS 12075 recommendation
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 12075 / HI standards for rotating equipment

compliance_rules.condition_expr: fan_size > 1500 AND foundation_type == 'Anti-vibration mounts'
compliance_rules.message: Large fans >1500mm dia — verify AV mount load rating
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: AMCA Publication 201 fan application guide

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Blower vibration analysis' AND NOT motor_power
compliance_rules.message: Enter motor power for vibration severity assessment per ISO 10816
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 10816-3 mechanical vibration standard

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_rate_per_job * size_factor * quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_rate_per_job * size_factor * foundation_factor * quantity

usd_cost_baseline.note: Base = USD per Job at USA labour level (matches ENR CCI base 14250 @ 2024). Engine: base_rate_per_job = usd_cost_baseline.items[equipment_subtype].usd_per_job, then x ENR_current/14250 x regional_factor x sub_region labour_factor. All rates are PUBLIC-BENCHMARK ESTIMATES (verify=true) pending calibration against vendor quotes / Indian DSR.

Blower – installation.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 4500 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 5725.45. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Blower balancing.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 14000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 18945.27. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Blower vibration analysis.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 14000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 18945.27. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Fan installation (industrial).basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 4500 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 5725.45. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Fan balancing.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 12000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 16238.81. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Cooling tower fan installation.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 4500 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 5725.45. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

size_factor._basis: By Fan/Blower Size (mm). Reference band 601-1200 mm = 1.00.

foundation_factor._basis: Relative to standard concrete plinth = 1.00; reflects added leveling/isolation labour. verify=true.

SCHEMA-BULK-EQUIP.generation_note: Engineered BOQ template v1.0 — bulk handling equipment; CEPCI regional basis unchanged from skeleton.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick ±30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget ±20%. Tier 3 = Detailed ±10%.

SCHEMA-BULK-EQUIP.spec_formula: {quantity} No. × {equipment_subtype} · {motor_power} kW · {moc} · L {length} m · Ø {diameter} m
SCHEMA-BULK-EQUIP.spec_example: 2 No. × Belt Conveyor · 22 kW · SS304 · L 50 m · Ø 2.5 m

fields.label: Equipment Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Rated motor power at shaft (kW) for drives, fans, or mill/crusher duty.

fields.label: Length
fields.hint: Centreline length for conveyors or similar transport paths (m).

fields.label: Width
fields.hint: Belt width (mm) for conveyors, or nominal deck width for screens where applicable.

fields.label: Belt speed
fields.hint: Belt line speed (m/s); primary for belt conveyors (typical 0.8–2.5 m/s).

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Nominal internal or batch volume reference (L) for mills, granulators, or similar.

fields.label: Height
fields.hint: Lift height (m) for bucket elevators or vertical conveying paths.

fields.label: Diameter
fields.hint: Shell or drum nominal diameter (m) for rotary dryers, coolers, granulators, and screens.

fields.label: Rotational speed
fields.hint: Rotor or drum speed (RPM) for mills, granulators, dryers, and coolers.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum process or gas temperature for dryers, coolers, or hot-side equipment (°C).

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Primary material of construction in contact with product or gas.

fields.label: Fuel Type
fields.hint: Heat source for fired dryers; use N/A for non-fired equipment.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == "Belt Conveyor" AND belt_speed_m_s > 2.5
compliance_rules.message: Belt speed above typical CEMA practice; verify belt rating, idler spacing, and drive sizing.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CEMA Belt Book / ISO 5049 bulk handling

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == "Bucket Elevator" AND height > 40
compliance_rules.message: High-lift bucket elevators need casing strength, take-up, and guarding review per bulk handling safety practice.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B20.1 / local machinery safety rules

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == "Rotary Dryer" OR equipment_subtype == "Rotary Cooler") AND design_temperature > 350
compliance_rules.message: Elevated shell temperatures require confirmation of refractory, expansion joints, and personnel protection.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Good engineering practice / API 560 (where fired)

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_rate * (motor_power ** 0.6) * moc_factor * quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_rate * (motor_power ** 0.6) * moc_factor * size_factor(diameter, length) * quantity

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only — not used in calculation.

SCHEMA-CABINET-JB.generation_note: Engineered BOQ template v1.0 — cabinets, junction boxes, glands, terminations, SPDs, instrument installation; regional matrix unchanged. | v1.0-priced: usd_cost_baseline + multiplier tables (USA-base; public-benchmark, verify=true).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick ±30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget ±20%. Tier 3 = Detailed ±10%.

SCHEMA-CABINET-JB.spec_example: 5 Job × Marshalling Cabinet (Small) · 24 V · 10 A · IP55 · MS powder coated

fields.label: Equipment Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: Nominal circuit or system voltage (V AC or DC as applicable).

fields.label: Current
fields.hint: Rated or design current per pole / branch (A).

fields.label: Fault Level
fields.hint: Prospective fault level at terminals (kA rms) for device selection.

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Enclosure ingress / Ex protection per IEC 60529 / IEC 60079.

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Enclosure or body material (cabinet, JB, or gland body as applicable).

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == "Junction Box (Hazardous Area)" AND enclosure != "Ex d" AND enclosure != "Ex e"
compliance_rules.message: Hazardous-area junction boxes require suitable Ex protection (e.g. Ex d or Ex e) — verify IEC 60079 / local codes.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079 (explosive atmospheres)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: current > 63 AND enclosure == "IP20"
compliance_rules.message: High current with IP20 enclosure may lack adequate touch protection; confirm segregation and barriers per IEC 61439 / local rules.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Surge Protector' IN equipment_subtype AND voltage > 48
compliance_rules.message: Confirm SPD maximum continuous operating voltage (Uc) matches the system voltage category.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61643 series

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_rate * enclosure_factor * quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_rate * enclosure_factor * moc_factor * ratings_factor(voltage, current) * quantity

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Agitator/rotor drive power. Typical range 11–90 kW for thin film; 30–200 kW for large rotary/spray dryers.

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: For vacuum dryers: typically 0.05–0.2 bar(g) (FV to slight positive). For spray/flash dryers: near atmospheric. Enter jacket/shell design pressure.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum shell/jacket design temperature. Typical: 120–200°C for vacuum dryers; up to 400°C for rotary/flash dryers with direct firing.

fields.label: Area
fields.hint: Heat transfer area (evaporators) or filter/tray area (dryers). For spray dryers, enter chamber cross-section area.

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Gross internal volume. For batch dryers (tray, vacuum conical): working volume. For continuous dryers: drum/chamber volume.

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of construction for product-contact surfaces. SS316L is standard for general chemical/pharmaceutical service. Select higher alloys (Hastelloy, Inconel, Titanium) for corrosive or high-temperature applications.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure < -0.1 OR design_pressure > 0.5
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure outside valid range (-0.1 to 0.5 bar(g)). Above 0.5 bar(g) full ASME VIII pressure vessel code applies. Verify with mechanical datasheet.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME VIII Div.1 / PED 2014/68/EU - vessels under external pressure require vacuum rating; above 0.5 bar(g) full pressure vessel code applies.

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_cost = 45000 × (volume/316)^0.6 × (motor_power/11)^0.35 × moc_multiplier × quantity, where moc_multiplier from cost_build_up.moc_multipliers
cost_build_up.tier_1_formula_note: Baseline USD 45,000 for SS316L ATFE (316L, 11kW, CEPCI 816). Market range typically USD 60,000-90,000 - validate against vendor quotes before production use.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: cost = tier_1_cost × pressure_factor × temperature_factor × area_factor, where pressure_factor = 1.0 if design_pressure ≤ 0.1 bar(g) else 1.08 if design_pressure ≤ 0.2 bar(g) else 1.15, temperature_factor = 1.0 if design_temperature ≤ 150°C else 1.12 if design_temperature ≤ 200°C else 1.25 if design_temperature ≤ 250°C else 1.4, area_factor = (area/10)^0.45, and tier_1_cost already includes moc_multiplier
cost_build_up.full_formula: cost = tier_2_cost × regional_index_factor × equipment_factor × labour_factor × civil_factor × (current_cepci / 816), where regional_index_factor from cost_build_up.regional_cost_index.regional_index[country].factor (converts USA base to target country), equipment_factor/labour_factor/civil_factor from cost_build_up.regional_factors.regions[country].sub_regions[sub_region], and CEPCI escalation applied for project year vs base year 2024

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Agitator drive motor power. Typical range 11–30 kW for reactors up to 10,000 L. Higher end for high-viscosity or gas-dispersion service.

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Shell/jacket design pressure. Glass-lined reactors: max 6 bar(g). SS316L and Hastelloy: up to 15 bar(g). Enter positive gauge pressure; for vacuum service enter 0.5 bar(g) FV.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum design temperature of shell/jacket. Glass-lined: max 150°C to avoid thermal delamination. SS316L: up to 200°C. Hastelloy C276: up to 200°C for chemical service.

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Gross internal volume for reactors (batch). Typical range 316 L (pilot) to 10,000 L (production). Used as primary cost-sizing parameter for reactor sub-types only.

conditional_display.show_if: equipment_subtype IN ['Reactor Jacketed Glass Lined', 'Reactor SS316L','Reactor Hastelloy C276']

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Gross internal volume for crystallizers. Typical range 30–50 m³ for DTB and OSLO type. Used as primary cost-sizing parameter for crystallizer sub-types only.

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of construction for product-contact surfaces. SS316L: standard chemical service. Glass Lined (DIN 28136): acid/corrosive service, max 6 bar(g), 150°C. Hastelloy C276: severe corrosion, halide-rich environments — cost premium ~4× SS316L.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc == 'Glass Lined' AND design_pressure > 6.0
compliance_rules.message: Glass-lined reactors are rated to a maximum of 6 bar(g). Design pressure entered exceeds lining design limit. Reduce pressure or change MOC to SS316L / Hastelloy C276.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: DIN 28136 / ASME Section VIII Div.1 UG-125 — Glass-lined pressure vessel design limits

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc == 'Glass Lined' AND design_temperature > 150
compliance_rules.message: Design temperature exceeds 150°C for glass-lined MOC. Thermal cycling above this limit risks lining delamination. Confirm with vessel vendor before proceeding.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: DIN 28136 Clause 5.3 — Thermal limits for glass-lined equipment

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc == 'Hastelloy C276'
compliance_rules.message: Hastelloy C276 selected. Cost premium is approximately 4× SS316L baseline. Verify corrosion data sheet for service fluid and confirm MOC selection with PMC/client before finalizing BOQ.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NACE MR0175 / ASTM B574 — Hastelloy C276 corrosion service guidelines

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula:
PEC = base_cost[equipment_subtype] × (volume / 1000)^0.6 × quantity
Where base_cost (USD):
  Reactor SS316L = 55000
  Reactor Glass Lined = 80000
  Reactor Hastelloy C276 = 230000
  Crystallizer DTB = 180000
  Crystallizer OSLO = 210000
For crystallizers: volume_L = volume_m3 × 1000 before applying formula
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula:
PEC = tier_1_PEC × pressure_factor × temperature_factor
pressure_factor:
  ≤6 bar(g)  → 1.00
  ≤10 bar(g) → 1.10
  ≤15 bar(g) → 1.22
temperature_factor:
  ≤150°C → 1.00
  ≤180°C → 1.08
  ≤200°C → 1.15
cost_build_up.full_formula:
Installed Cost = PEC × (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation + contingency) × regional_index_factor × sub_region.equipment_factor × (current_CEPCI / 816)
Where regional_index_factor = regional_cost_index.regional_index[project_country].factor

usd_cost_baseline.note: Base = USD at USA level (matches CEPCI base 816 @ 2024). Engine: base_rate = usd_cost_baseline.items[equipment_subtype].usd_per_*, then x index_now/816 x regional_factor x fx. verify=true entries are public-benchmark estimates pending calibration.

fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Drive motor power for centrifuge/agitator. Centrifuge (Basket): 5–22 kW. Centrifuge (Decanter): 15–55 kW. Centrifuge (Pusher): 22–75 kW. Mixer-Settler/Extractor: 5–30 kW.

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Bowl/basket internal volume for centrifuges. Typical 316 L for standard industrial basket centrifuge. Used as primary sizing parameter for centrifuge sub-types only.

fields.label: Flow Rate
fields.hint: Process fluid volumetric flow rate. For filters: feed slurry flow. For columns/scrubbers: gas or liquid throughput. For extractors: combined feed flow. Typical range 20–100 m³/hr for most chemical plant duties.

conditional_display.show_if: equipment_subtype IN ['Filter Press (Recessed)', 'Filter Press (Membrane)', 'Rotary Vacuum Filter', 'Pressure Leaf Filter', 'Bag Filter Housing', 'Cartridge Filter Housing', 'Self-Cleaning Filter', 'Distillation Column', 'Packed Column', 'Scrubber (Packed Bed)', 'Scrubber (Venturi)', 'Absorption Tower', 'Stripping Column', 'Extractor (Pulse)', 'Mixer-Settler', 'Liquid-Liquid Extractor', 'Solid-Liquid Extractor']

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Shell internal volume for columns, towers, and extractors. For distillation/absorption columns: total shell volume including packing/tray space. For extractors: active contact zone volume. Range 10–50 m³ typical.

conditional_display.show_if: equipment_subtype IN ['Distillation Column', 'Packed Column', 'Scrubber (Packed Bed)', 'Absorption Tower', 'Stripping Column', 'Extractor (Pulse)', 'Mixer-Settler', 'Liquid-Liquid Extractor', 'Solid-Liquid Extractor']

fields.label: Speed
fields.hint: Centrifuge bowl/basket rotational speed. Basket centrifuge: 800–1200 RPM. Decanter: 2000–4000 RPM. Pusher: 200–600 RPM. Enter design operating speed, not maximum mechanical speed.

fields.label: Area
fields.hint: Effective filtration area. Filter press: total plate area. Rotary vacuum filter: drum surface area. Pressure leaf filter: total leaf area. Bag/cartridge: total media area. Range 50–100 m² typical for chemical plant scale.

conditional_display.show_if: equipment_subtype IN ['Filter Press (Recessed)', 'Filter Press (Membrane)', 'Rotary Vacuum Filter', 'Pressure Leaf Filter', 'Bag Filter Housing', 'Cartridge Filter Housing', 'Self-Cleaning Filter']

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Shell/casing design pressure. Bag/cartridge filters and venturi scrubbers: 0.5–2.0 bar(g). Centrifuges, columns, pressure leaf filters: 6–10 bar(g). Enter jacket/shell design pressure.

fields.label: Diameter
fields.hint: Internal shell diameter for columns and towers. Distillation/absorption/stripping: 1.5–3.0 m typical for chemical plant scale. Scrubber (Packed Bed): 1.5–3.0 m. Primary cost-sizing parameter for column sub-types.

conditional_display.show_if: equipment_subtype IN ['Distillation Column', 'Packed Column', 'Scrubber (Packed Bed)', 'Scrubber (Venturi)', 'Absorption Tower', 'Stripping Column']

fields.label: Efficiency
fields.hint: Separation efficiency. For filters: solids removal efficiency, typically 95%+. For scrubbers: gas-side collection efficiency. Single reference value — contact vendor for actual performance guarantee.

conditional_display.show_if: equipment_subtype IN ['Filter Press (Recessed)', 'Filter Press (Membrane)', 'Rotary Vacuum Filter', 'Pressure Leaf Filter', 'Bag Filter Housing', 'Cartridge Filter Housing', 'Self-Cleaning Filter', 'Scrubber (Packed Bed)', 'Scrubber (Venturi)']

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of construction for product-contact surfaces. SS316L: standard for most chemical service including corrosive liquids. FRP (Fibre Reinforced Plastic): for strongly acidic gas scrubbing service (HCl, H₂SO₄ mist) where stainless is unsuitable. FRP not rated for pressure above 2 bar(g) — check design pressure before selecting.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc == 'FRP' AND design_pressure > 2.0
compliance_rules.message: FRP construction is not rated for design pressures above 2 bar(g). Select SS316L or verify FRP pressure rating with manufacturer before proceeding.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: BS 4994 / ASME RTP-1 — Design of vessels in reinforced plastics; pressure limitations for FRP equipment

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Centrifuge' IN equipment_subtype AND speed > 1500 AND design_pressure > 8.0
compliance_rules.message: High speed centrifuge (>1500 RPM) combined with design pressure >8 bar(g). Verify mechanical seal and casing integrity rating with vendor. Both conditions together increase failure risk.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 610 Annex H / ATEX Directive 2014/34/EU — Rotating equipment under pressure: combined speed-pressure limits

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Distillation Column' OR equipment_subtype == 'Stripping Column' OR equipment_subtype == 'Absorption Tower'
compliance_rules.message: Column/tower selected. Cost estimate covers shell and internals (trays or packing) at indicative level only. Detailed column costing requires number of trays/packing height, internals type, and nozzle schedule. Treat as +-30% order-of-magnitude at this stage.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: AACE International RP 18R-97 — Cost estimate classification for process industries

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: PEC = base_cost[equipment_subtype] × sizing_ratio × moc_factor × quantity. Where sizing_ratio by family: Centrifuges: (motor_power / baseline_motor)^0.6 × (volume_L / 316)^0.3; Filters: (flow_rate / baseline_flow)^0.5 × (area / baseline_area)^0.5; Columns/Towers: (diameter / baseline_dia)^1.2 × (volume_m3 / baseline_vol)^0.4, Exception — Scrubber (Venturi): (flow_rate / 50)^0.6 (no diameter or volume term); Extractors: (volume_m3 / baseline_vol)^0.6. moc_factor: SS316L=1.0, FRP=0.65
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: PEC = tier_1_PEC × pressure_factor. pressure_factor: ≤6 bar(g) → 1.00; ≤8 bar(g) → 1.08; ≤10 bar(g) → 1.18
cost_build_up.full_formula: Installed Cost = PEC × (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation + contingency) × regional_cost_index.regional_index[country].factor × regional_factors.regions[country].sub_regions[sub_region].equipment_factor × (current_CEPCI / 816)

usd_cost_baseline.note: Base = USD at USA level (matches CEPCI base 816 @ 2024). Engine: base_rate = usd_cost_baseline.items[equipment_subtype].usd_per_*, then x index_now/816 x regional_factor x fx. verify=true entries are public-benchmark estimates pending calibration.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Column erection (heavy lift)' AND quantity > 3
compliance_rules.message: More than 3 heavy lift column erections in a single BOQ line. Verify that crane mobilisation cost is not being under-counted — each lift may require separate crane setup. Consider splitting into individual line items per column.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: AACE International RP 59R-10 — Crane and rigging cost estimating; lift plan requirements per ASME B30.5

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Distillation column erection' OR equipment_subtype == 'Absorber column' OR equipment_subtype == 'Stripper column'
compliance_rules.message: Supply + erect item selected. Base rate covers shell, internals, and erection at indicative level only (+-30%). Final cost is highly dependent on column height, diameter, MOC, number of trays/packing height, and nozzle count. Obtain vendor budget quote before Class 3 estimate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: AACE International RP 18R-97 — Cost estimate classification; Class 4 accuracy +-20 to +-30% for lumpsum activities

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula:
Cost = base_rate[equipment_subtype] × quantity
Where base_rate (USD, CEPCI 816, 2024):
  Column tray installation:       35000
  Column packing installation:    28000
  Column erection (heavy lift):   95000
  Distillation column erection:  380000
  Absorber column:               220000
  Stripper column:               180000
Note: base_rates include direct labour, subcontractor, and
material (for supply+erect items). Indirect costs added via adders.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula:
No Tier 2 fields defined. tier_2_formula = tier_1_formula.
Detailed breakdown available from discipline engineer on request.
cost_build_up.full_formula:
Installed Cost = Cost × (1 + contingency) × regional_factors.regions[country].sub_regions[sub_region].labour_factor × (current_CEPCI / 816)
Note: labour_factor only (no regional_index.factor) — this is a construction activity schema with labour-based regional scaling. freight_insurance and erection_installation adders are NOT applied — they are already embedded in base_rates for installation activities.

Column tray installation.scope: Labour + scaffold + consumables to install trays in one column section
Column tray installation.basis: Per job (one column internals installation campaign)

Column packing installation.scope: Labour + scaffold + consumables to load structured or random packing

Column erection (heavy lift).scope: Crane hire + rigging + civil grouting to erect one column shell on foundation
Column erection (heavy lift).basis: Per lift (one column shell set operation)

Distillation column erection.scope: Supply CS/SS316L column shell + trays/packing + nozzles + erect + set
Distillation column erection.basis: Per column (complete supply and erect package, 2m dia × 15m T-T)

Absorber column.scope: Supply SS316L or FRP column shell + structured packing + support rings + erect
Absorber column.basis: Per column (complete supply and erect, 2m dia × 10m T-T)

Stripper column.scope: Supply SS316L column shell + trays + reboiler nozzles + erect
Stripper column.basis: Per column (complete supply and erect, 1.5m dia × 12m T-T)

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation. NOTE: For activity schemas, regional_index.factor is NOT applied — use labour_factor from regional_factors instead.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

fields.hint: Select the specific commissioning activity, training, spare parts, maintenance, or documentation item.

fields.label: Applied Standard
fields.hint: Governing engineering standard for this activity. IEC 61511: SIS/safety systems. IEEE C37.2/IEC 60255: protection relays. NFPA 72: fire & gas. IEC 60079: hazardous area. ISO 9001: general quality.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of jobs or contracts for this activity.

fields.label: Scope Units
fields.hint: Enter the count applicable to this activity. Commissioning: number of loops, panels, or circuits. Training: number of trainees. Documentation: number of drawings or manuals. Spare parts / maintenance: enter 1 for lump-sum scope.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'SIS' IN equipment_subtype AND standard != 'IEC 61511'
compliance_rules.message: SIS commissioning and proof testing should follow IEC 61511. Verify selected standard covers the safety lifecycle requirements.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61511-1:2016 — Functional safety: safety instrumented systems for the process industry

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'SIS' IN equipment_subtype AND (standard == 'NFPA 72' OR standard == 'IEEE C37.2')
compliance_rules.message: NFPA 72 and IEEE C37.2 are not applicable to SIS proof testing. Select IEC 61511 or IEC 61508.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61511-1:2016 Cl. 16 — Proof test requirements for safety instrumented systems

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Cable insulation resistance (IR) testing' AND scope_complexity_units < 1
compliance_rules.message: Minimum 1 cable circuit must be defined for IR testing scope.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60364-6 / BS 7671 — Initial verification of low voltage electrical installations

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Commissioning: Fire & Gas Test' AND standard != 'NFPA 72'
compliance_rules.message: Fire & Gas system commissioning is typically performed to NFPA 72 or equivalent. Verify selected standard covers detection coverage requirements.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NFPA 72 — National Fire Alarm and Signaling Code, Chapter 14: Inspection, Testing and Maintenance

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: PEC = base_rate[equipment_subtype] × quantity × scope_complexity_units × regional_index[country].factor × (1 + mobilisation_pct + contingency)
cost_build_up.full_formula: Installed Cost = base_rate[equipment_subtype] × quantity × scope_complexity_units × regional_index[country].factor × regional_factors[country].sub_regions[sub_region].labour_factor × (1 + mobilisation_pct + contingency) × (current_ENR_CCI / 14250)

SCHEMA-COMP-ACTIVITY.generation_note: v1.0-FINAL: conditional_display and compliance_rule expressions converted to spec_key variable syntax; equipment_origin and output_currency restored to arrays; import_duty corrected to Services not applicable across all 8 regions.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-COMP-ACTIVITY.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | Type: {compressor_type} | Power: {driver_power_kw} kW | Stages: {number_of_stages} | Discharge: {discharge_pressure_bar_g} bar g | Qty: {quantity} Job
SCHEMA-COMP-ACTIVITY.spec_example: Air compressor – installation | Type: Centrifugal | Power: 1500 kW | Stages: 2 | Discharge: 12 bar g | Qty: 1 Job

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific compressor activity. Each sub-type has different labour, tooling, and duration profiles — this selection drives the baseline cost rate.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

fields.label: Compressor Driver Power
fields.hint: Nameplate power of the prime mover (electric motor, steam turbine, or gas engine). Affects crane/rigging, foundation design, and alignment complexity. Typical range: 7.5 kW (small reciprocating) to 5000 kW (large centrifugal).

conditional_display.show_if: equipment_subtype !== 'Compressor performance testing' && equipment_subtype !== 'Commissioning of compressor'

fields.label: Compressor Type
fields.hint: Select compressor type. Reciprocating: skid-mounted, bolt-down, pulsation dampeners. Centrifugal: precision coupled train, laser alignment. Screw: package unit, simpler installation. Axial: large, complex machines.

fields.label: Number of Stages
fields.hint: Number of compression stages (1 to 6). Multi-stage units require more inter-stage piping, coolers, and lube oil system complexity — increases flushing volume and performance test duration.

conditional_display.show_if: equipment_subtype === 'Compressor lube oil flushing' || equipment_subtype === 'Compressor performance testing'

fields.label: Discharge Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum discharge pressure at design conditions. High pressure (>100 bar g) triggers special foundation pulsation analysis and gasket requirements per API 618 / API 617.

conditional_display.show_if: equipment_subtype === 'Compressor foundation preparation' || equipment_subtype === 'Compressor performance testing'

compliance_rules.condition_expr: discharge_pressure_bar_g > 100
compliance_rules.message: Discharge pressure exceeds 100 bar g. Foundation design must be reviewed for high-pressure pulsation loads per API 618 / API 617.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 618 Sec. 2.3 (pulsation analysis) and API 617 Sec. 4.5 (foundation dynamic loads)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: compressor_type == 'Centrifugal' AND equipment_subtype == 'Compressor alignment'
compliance_rules.message: Centrifugal compressor alignment tolerance per API 686 Clause 7: max offset 0.05 mm/m, angular 0.025 mm/m. Laser alignment recommended.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 686 Recommended Practice for Machinery Installation and Installation Design, Clause 7 — Alignment Tolerances

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Compressor performance testing' AND compressor_type != 'Reciprocating'
compliance_rules.message: For non-reciprocating compressors, ASME PTC 10 Type 2 test (equivalent gas) may be required. Verify test gas selection and correction factors with vendor.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME PTC 10 — Performance Test Code on Compressors and Exhausters, Sec. 5.2 Test Types

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Compressor lube oil flushing'
compliance_rules.message: ISO cleanliness target NAS 1638 Class 6 or ISO 4406 16/14/11 must be achieved and verified by lab analysis before first start. Confirm filter sizing and flush duration with vendor.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 614 (Lubrication Systems for General-Purpose Applications) and ISO 4406:2021 — Hydraulic fluid power: solid contamination levels

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: PEC = base_rate[equipment_subtype] × (1 + min(0.5, driver_power_kw / 5000)) × (1 + (number_of_stages - 1) × 0.05) × (1 + min(0.3, discharge_pressure_bar_g / 350)) × quantity × regional_index[country].factor × regional_factors[country].sub_regions[sub_region].labour_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: Installed Cost = base_rate[equipment_subtype] × (1 + min(0.5, driver_power_kw / 5000)) × (1 + (number_of_stages - 1) × 0.05) × (1 + min(0.3, discharge_pressure_bar_g / 350)) × quantity × regional_index[country].factor × regional_factors[country].sub_regions[sub_region].labour_factor × (1 + mobilisation_pct + contingency) × (current_ENR_CCI / 14250)

usd_cost_baseline.note: USA-base USD (index base year 2024). Engine applies index_now/base x regional x fx. GENERATED public-benchmark ESTIMATES sized by discipline order-of-magnitude; ALL verify=true — every rate must be calibrated against vendor/DSR data before quotation use.

Air compressor – installation.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 4500 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 5725.45. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Commissioning of compressor.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 8000 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 10178.57. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Compressor foundation preparation.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 85000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 115024.88. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Compressor alignment.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 85000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 115024.88. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Compressor lube oil flushing.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 85000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 115024.88. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Compressor performance testing.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 85000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 115024.88. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region labour_factor applied on top of regional_index factor.

SCHEMA-COMP-EQUIP.generation_note: v2.0-FINAL: library expanded from 4 to 23 items covering reciprocating, screw, centrifugal, and special gas service families; custom entry remarks field added for non-library compressors; usd_cost_baseline covers all 23 items.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-COMP-EQUIP.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | {custom_description} | MOC: {moc} | Power: {driver_power_kw} kW | DP: {discharge_pressure_bar_g} bar g | Stages: {number_of_stages} | Seal: {seal_type} | Qty: {quantity} No.
SCHEMA-COMP-EQUIP.spec_example: Hydrogen compressor |  | MOC: Low Alloy Steel (HIC) | Power: 300 kW | DP: 200 bar g | Stages: 4 | Seal: Dry Gas Seal | Qty: 1 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the closest matching compressor type. If none match, select 'Compressor - other (specify in remarks)' and describe the equipment in the Remarks field below. Cost estimate for 'other' uses screw compressor as proxy — adjust manually after export.

fields.label: Remarks / Custom Description
fields.hint: Required when 'Compressor - other (specify in remarks)' is selected. Enter make, model, gas service, and any other details needed to identify this equipment. Example: Lobe blower, biogas service, 0.5 bar g, 45 kW.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - visible only when Other selected

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of compressor units on this BOQ line.

fields.label: Driver Power
fields.hint: Nameplate driver power at design conditions. Primary cost sizing parameter — cost scales as Power^0.6. Reciprocating: 15–3000 kW. Screw: 7.5–750 kW. Centrifugal: 150–50000 kW. Hydrogen: 75–3000 kW. CO2: 150–5000 kW. Refrigeration: 15–1500 kW.

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of construction for pressure-retaining parts. Carbon Steel: standard dry non-corrosive gas. Low Alloy Steel (HIC): mandatory for hydrogen service (NACE MR0175). SS316L: wet CO2 or mildly corrosive gas. Duplex SS: sour or high-chloride gas. Hastelloy C-276: chlorine or highly corrosive halogen service. Monel: HF acid or fluorine service.

fields.label: Discharge Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum discharge pressure at design conditions. Significant cost multiplier above 40 bar g. Screw/air: up to 40 bar g. Refrigeration: up to 30 bar g. Natural gas reciprocating: up to 100 bar g. CO2: up to 150 bar g. Hydrogen: up to 350 bar g. Chlorine: up to 20 bar g.

fields.label: Number of Stages
fields.hint: Number of compression stages. Multi-stage units require inter-stage coolers, separators, and additional instrumentation — each additional stage adds approximately 12% to base cost. Screw: 1–2. Refrigeration: 1–3. CO2/Natural gas: 1–4. Hydrogen: 1–6.

fields.label: Seal Type
fields.hint: Shaft seal type. Dry Gas Seal: required for H2, CO2, and toxic gas service — adds USD 50,000–150,000 per stage. Oil Film Seal: centrifugal compressors in clean hydrocarbon service. Mechanical Seal: standard for screw and refrigeration. Labyrinth: non-contacting, non-hazardous gas only.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Hydrogen compressor' OR equipment_subtype == 'Reciprocating compressor – hydrogen') AND moc != 'Low Alloy Steel (HIC)'
compliance_rules.message: Hydrogen compressor must use HIC-resistant Low Alloy Steel per NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156. Carbon Steel and standard stainless are susceptible to hydrogen-induced cracking. Change MOC before proceeding.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156-2 — Materials for use in H2S-containing environments; applicable to hydrogen service for HIC risk assessment

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Chlorine compressor' AND (moc == 'Carbon Steel' OR moc == 'Low Alloy Steel (HIC)')
compliance_rules.message: Chlorine compressor requires Hastelloy C-276 or Monel for all wetted parts in contact with Cl2 gas. Carbon Steel and Low Alloy Steel will experience rapid catastrophic corrosion in dry chlorine above 100 deg C or in any wet chlorine service.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NFPA 652 / Chlorine Institute Pamphlet 74 — Materials of construction for chlorine compression; Hastelloy C-276 or Monel required for Cl2 service

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Hydrogen compressor' OR equipment_subtype == 'Reciprocating compressor – hydrogen') AND discharge_pressure_bar_g > 200
compliance_rules.message: Hydrogen compressor discharge pressure exceeds 200 bar g. All downstream piping, flanges, and fittings must comply with ASME B31.12. Verify material qualification and pressure class for high-pressure hydrogen service.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.12:2019 — Hydrogen Piping and Pipelines; material and pressure class requirements above 200 bar g

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'CO2 compressor' OR equipment_subtype == 'Centrifugal compressor – CO2 service' OR equipment_subtype == 'Refrigeration compressor – CO2 (transcritical)') AND moc == 'Carbon Steel'
compliance_rules.message: Carbon Steel selected for CO2 compressor. Wet CO2 (>50 ppm H2O) is highly corrosive to carbon steel. Use SS316L or verify gas is guaranteed dry throughout operating envelope including start-up and shutdown.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NACE TM0177 / EFC Publication No. 23 — Wet CO2 corrosion control; SS316L or corrosion-resistant alloy recommended for wet CO2 service

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Ammonia compressor' OR equipment_subtype == 'Refrigeration compressor – ammonia (NH3)') AND (moc == 'SS316L' OR moc == 'Duplex SS')
compliance_rules.message: Stainless steel is not recommended for ammonia compressor service. Ammonia causes stress corrosion cracking (SCC) in austenitic stainless steels. Use Carbon Steel or Low Alloy Steel for NH3 service. Also ensure no copper alloy components anywhere in the system.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IIAR Bulletin 110 / BS EN 378 — Ammonia refrigeration system materials; SCC risk of stainless in NH3; copper alloys prohibited

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Compressor – other (specify in remarks)' AND (custom_description IS None OR custom_description == '')
compliance_rules.message: Custom compressor selected but Remarks field is empty. Enter equipment description, gas service, and operating conditions in the Remarks field to allow cost review and vendor enquiry.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: BOQ completeness requirement — custom items require full description for accurate cost estimation and procurement

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Compressor – other (specify in remarks)'
compliance_rules.message: Cost estimate for this item uses Screw compressor as proxy baseline. Actual cost may vary significantly. Review and adjust the estimate manually after export based on vendor budgetary quotation.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: AACE International RP 18R-97 — Cost estimate classification; custom items require vendor quotation for Class 3 or better accuracy

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: PEC = base_cost[equipment_subtype] × (driver_power_kw / baseline_power[equipment_subtype])^0.6 × moc_factor[moc] × pressure_factor × stage_factor × seal_factor × quantity. Where: pressure_factor: <=40 bar g=1.00, <=100=1.15, <=200=1.35, >200=1.60. stage_factor: 1 + (number_of_stages - 1) x 0.12. seal_factor: Dry Gas=1.18, Oil Film=1.10, Mechanical=1.00, Labyrinth=0.95
cost_build_up.full_formula: Installed Cost = PEC × (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation + contingency) × regional_cost_index[country].factor × regional_factors[country].sub_regions[sub_region].equipment_factor × (current_CEPCI / 816)

usd_cost_baseline.note: Base = USD at USA level (matches CEPCI base 816 @ 2024). Engine: base_rate = usd_cost_baseline.items[equipment_subtype].usd_per_*, then x index_now/816 x regional_factor x fx. verify=true entries are public-benchmark estimates pending calibration.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-CONCRETE-RCC.spec_example: Plain Cement Concrete (PCC) 1:4:8 | Qty: 50 m³ | Eff: 5% | Vol: 0.2 L/m²

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific concrete or formwork activity type. Controls which fields are shown below and the applicable unit rate.

fields.label: Efficiency / Wastage Allowance
fields.hint: Material wastage and placement inefficiency allowance as a percentage of net volume. Typical range 3–7% for concrete (IS 4926 / ACI 304R). Use 5% for standard in-situ pours; increase to 7% for congested reinforcement or complex formwork.

fields.label: Curing Compound Volume
fields.hint: Volume of curing compound per m² of cured surface area. Typical application rate 0.15–0.25 L/m² per coat (ASTM C309 / IS 9476). Use 0.2 L/m² for a single coat on horizontal slabs; increase to 0.35–0.40 L/m² for two-coat application on vertical surfaces or high-performance concrete.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Net quantity of concrete, formwork area (m²), or reinforcement (MT) as applicable. Measure net finished dimensions per IS 1200 / CESMM4. Do not pre-inflate for wastage — use the Efficiency field for that.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Reinforced Cement Concrete (RCC) M35' AND quantity > 0 AND efficiency < 3
compliance_rules.message: Wastage allowance below 3% is unrealistic for M35 RCC. M35 and above mixes have tighter w/c ratios and are more sensitive to placement losses. Minimum 3% recommended per ACI 304R-00.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ACI 304R-00 Guide for Measuring, Mixing, Transporting and Placing Concrete; IS 4926:2003 Ready-Mixed Concrete — Code of Practice.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Curing (Curing Compound)' AND volume > 0.4
compliance_rules.message: Curing compound application rate above 0.4 L/m² is unusually high and may indicate double entry. Verify whether a two-coat application is intended.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASTM C309-19 Standard Specification for Liquid Membrane-Forming Compounds for Curing Concrete; manufacturer typical coverage 0.15–0.25 L/m² per coat.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Centering & Staging (Extra Height)' AND quantity > 5000
compliance_rules.message: Centering & staging quantity exceeds 5,000 m². Confirm that extra-height premium is applied only to soffit areas above 3.5 m from working platform level, per standard re-measurement practice.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CESMM4 Class G; CPWD Specification 2019 Section 5.3 — Formwork measurement rules for extra-height staging.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Plain Cement Concrete (PCC) 1:4:8' AND quantity > 500
compliance_rules.message: Large PCC quantities (>500 m³) are typically associated with mass blinding or levelling courses. Consider whether a lean concrete or controlled low-strength material (CLSM) specification is more appropriate and cost-effective.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 456:2000 Clause 8.2.1 — Nominal mix 1:4:8 permitted for PCC blinding only; ACI 229R for CLSM alternatives.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_rate × quantity × (1 + efficiency / 100)
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_rate × quantity × (1 + efficiency / 100) × regional_factor × sub_region_civil_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct / 100) × (1 + import_duty_total_landed_adder_pct / 100) × (current_ENR_CCI / base_ENR_CCI) × (1 + contingency_pct / 100)

base_unit_rates_usd_per_m3.note: USA average (RSMeans 2024). Escalate using ENR CCI ratio. Formwork items in USD/m²; reinforcement in USD/MT.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: item_type corrected E->A: unit-rate construction/installation work, field-labour dominated (matches PLASTERING / STORM-DRAIN / EARTHING library convention). ENR CCI retained, base 14250 (2024).

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-CT-VT.spec_example: Current Transformer (Wound) 100/1A | Vsys:11kV | Isec:1A | FL:25kA | Std:IEC 60044 | Qty:3

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: System Voltage
fields.hint: Rated system (primary) voltage in kV. LV: 0.415 kV. MV: 3.3 / 6.6 / 11 / 33 kV. HV: 66 / 110 / 132 kV. This sets the insulation level (Um) and is the primary cost driver alongside equipment sub-type.

fields.label: Fault Level
fields.hint: Symmetrical rated short-circuit current (kA rms) the CT/VT must withstand for 1 s (Ith per IEC 61869-1). Standard values: 25 / 31.5 / 40 kA. Higher fault level → heavier core clamping, larger cross-section → cost premium. Obtain from system fault study.

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: Rated secondary voltage of a Voltage / Potential Transformer (V). Standard: 110 V (phase-to-phase) or 110/√3 V (63.5 V phase-to-earth). LV metering VTs: 100–110 V. 415 V applies to low-voltage instrument VTs. Leave blank for Current Transformer items.

fields.label: Current
fields.hint: Rated secondary current of a Current Transformer (A). 1 A: preferred for indoor switchgear and long secondary cable runs (lower burden losses). 5 A: used for outdoor metering with short secondary leads. IEC 61869-2 standard values. Leave blank for VT/PT items.

fields.label: Standard
fields.hint: Design and type-test standard. IEC 61869-2: current transformers (replaces IEC 60044-1). IEC 61869-3: inductive VTs (replaces IEC 60044-2). IEC 61869-5: CVTs (replaces IEC 60044-5). ANSI C57.13: USA projects. Select legacy IEC 60044 only for retrofit / brownfield matching existing plant.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: system_voltage > 36 AND 'wound' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: Wound-type CT selected at system voltage above 36 kV. Wound CTs are not suitable above 36 kV due to insulation and thermal limitations. Select a Bar/Slot-type CT or an outdoor free-standing CT (e.g. 33 kV Outdoor Metering or 132 kV Outdoor High Voltage).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61869-2:2012 Cl. 7.3 — wound-type CT voltage class limitation; IEC 62271-200 switchgear insulation co-ordination

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'capacitor voltage' IN equipment_subtype AND system_voltage < 52
compliance_rules.message: Capacitor Voltage Transformer (CVT) selected at system voltage below 52 kV. CVTs are economical only at 66 kV and above; use an inductive VT/PT at lower voltage classes.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61869-5:2011 Cl. 3.1 — CVT applicability above Um = 52 kV; ENA Engineering Recommendation P27

compliance_rules.condition_expr: fault_level > 40
compliance_rules.message: Fault level exceeds 40 kA — outside the standard IEC 61869 short-circuit withstand catalogue. Confirm rated short-time current (Ith) with vendor and specify dynamic current (Idyn ≥ 2.5 × Ith) for order.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61869-1:2023 Cl. 7.2.302 — rated short-time thermal current Ith standard values up to 40 kA

compliance_rules.condition_expr: standard AND 'IEC 60044' IN standard AND equipment_subtype AND !'legacy' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: IEC 60044 is a withdrawn standard (superseded by IEC 61869 series from 2011). For new equipment, specify IEC 61869-2 (CT) or IEC 61869-3 (VT/PT). Use IEC 60044 only when matching existing brownfield plant.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61869-1:2011 replaced IEC 60044-1/-2/-5/-6; confirmed withdrawn by IEC.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'ps class' IN equipment_subtype AND standard AND 'ANSI' IN standard
compliance_rules.message: PS Class (Protection Special / Knee-point) CT specified with ANSI/IEEE C57.13 standard. PS Class is an IEC designation; the ANSI equivalent is Class C/T. Confirm the relay manufacturer's burden requirement to align the standard.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: current == 5 AND system_voltage >= 11 AND 'wound' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: 5 A secondary current selected on a wound-type MV CT. Consider 1 A secondary for MV switchgear installations to reduce secondary cable burden losses over long runs (typical MV control cable > 10 m).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61869-2:2012 Cl. 6.2 — secondary current rating selection guidance; GE/ABB protection relay application guides

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) × voltage_class_factor(system_voltage) × fault_level_factor(fault_level) × secondary_current_factor(current) × standard_factor(standard) × quantity × regional_factor(region, sub_region)
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) × voltage_class_factor(system_voltage) × fault_level_factor(fault_level) × secondary_current_factor(current) × standard_factor(standard) × quantity × regional_factor(region, sub_region) × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: Switched ENR->CEPCI: procured/manufactured equipment, supply-cost dominated. verify=true

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
options: Field Junction Box (FJB) Stainless Steel ATEX
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Efficiency
fields.hint: Power-supply conversion efficiency (%). Typical DCS/SCADA system PSUs: 85–93 %. Legacy SMPS: 50–60 %. Leave blank for non-PSU items.

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: Nominal supply voltage. DCS I/O cards & controllers: 24 V DC. Operator/engineering stations: 120 V AC (USA) or 230 V AC (IEC). Field junction boxes & HART mux: 24 V DC. Enter rated voltage.

fields.label: Current
fields.hint: Rated load current (A). I/O cards: 2–5 A. Controllers: 5–20 A. System PSUs: 20–40 A. Used for power budget and cable sizing.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum ambient operating temperature (°C). Indoor DCS cabinets: 0–55 °C. Outdoor / ATEX field enclosures: –20 to +65 °C. Drives enclosure cooling specification and ATEX T-class selection.

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Instrument air accumulator or manifold volume (L). Applicable to Instrument Air Distribution Manifold items only. Typical: 100–500 L. Leave blank for electronic DCS items.

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Design pressure for instrument air manifold (bar g). Instrument air supply header: 7–10 bar g. Leave blank for electronic DCS/SCADA equipment.

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Ingress Protection rating (IEC 60529). Indoor DCS cabinets / control rooms: IP30–IP40. Field junction boxes in clean areas: IP55. ATEX outdoor / wash-down areas: IP65–IP66. Submerged or hose-proof: IP67.

fields.label: IP rating (numeric)
fields.hint: Numeric value derived from 'enclosure' for size/rating threshold compliance rules.

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of Construction for the enclosure. GRP (Glass-Reinforced Polyester): standard for field JBs in non-corrosive areas. SS316: coastal, offshore, and chloride-rich environments. SS316L: low-carbon grade for welded assemblies in highly corrosive service.

fields.label: Standard
fields.hint: Governing design/certification standard. IEC 61131: PLC/DCS programming (non-safety). IEC 61511: Safety Instrumented Systems (SIS/ESD) — requires SIL assessment. IEC 62443: Industrial cybersecurity — applies to DCS network and SCADA.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: standard == 'IEC 61511' AND NOT equipment_subtype IN ['DCS Controller (Safety - SIS)', 'Safety Instrumented System (SIS) — Logic Solver', 'Safety Instrumented System (SIS/ESD)']
compliance_rules.message: IEC 61511 standard selected but the chosen equipment sub-type is not a Safety Instrumented System component. Select a SIS/ESD item or change the Standard to IEC 61131 / IEC 62443.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61511-1:2016 Cl. 5 — SIS scope and applicability

compliance_rules.condition_expr: ip_rating_num < 55 AND 'Field Junction Box' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: Field Junction Box selected with enclosure rating below IP55. IEC 60529 requires minimum IP55 for outdoor field-mounted enclosures. Verify installation location and upgrade if exposed to weather.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60529:2013 — IP55 minimum for outdoor field equipment

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc == 'GRP' AND design_temperature > 60
compliance_rules.message: GRP enclosure selected with design temperature above 60 °C. Standard GRP composites have a heat deflection temperature of ~65 °C. Consider SS316 enclosure or verify vendor's high-temperature GRP grade.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 62208 — Enclosure thermal classification

compliance_rules.condition_expr: standard == 'IEC 62443' AND !'Software' IN equipment_subtype AND !'Network' IN equipment_subtype AND !'Gateway' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: IEC 62443 cybersecurity standard selected on a non-network item. IEC 62443 applies primarily to network devices, software, gateways, and system-level SCADA components. Confirm this is intentional.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 62443-3-3:2013 — System Security Requirements

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

SCHEMA-DG-SET.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | {power} kVA / {motor_power} kW | {voltage} V, {frequency} Hz, {speed} RPM | Output current: {current} A | Enclosure: {enclosure} | Qty: {quantity} No.
SCHEMA-DG-SET.spec_example: Diesel Generator (500kVA - Standby) | 500 kVA / 400 kW | 415 V, 50 Hz, 1500 RPM | Output current: 695 A | Enclosure: IP54 acoustic canopy, outdoor weatherproof | Qty: 1 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Rated shaft output power of the diesel engine (prime mover) in kW. kW = kVA × power factor. At 0.8 pf: 150 kVA DG → 120 kW; 500 kVA DG → 400 kW. Used for fuel consumption and engine sizing calculations.

fields.label: Power
fields.hint: Alternator apparent power output in kVA (standby rating per ISO 8528-1). Library sizes: 150 kVA and 500 kVA. Prime (continuous) rating is typically 90% of standby rating. This is the primary cost-sizing parameter for the DG set.

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: Output voltage of the DG alternator: 415 V (3-phase, 4-wire, L-L) is standard for LV industrial supply in India, GCC, and UK. For AMF Panel, enter DC control circuit voltage (typically 24 V). US/Canada: 480 V (industrial) or 208/120 V (commercial).

fields.label: Frequency
fields.hint: Grid frequency of the project country. 50 Hz: India, GCC, UK, Europe, most of Asia and Africa. 60 Hz: USA, Canada, parts of Latin America and Japan. Determines engine governor and alternator winding — cannot be changed after manufacture.

fields.label: Speed
fields.hint: Synchronous speed of the 4-pole engine-alternator set. 1500 RPM → 50 Hz grid; 1800 RPM → 60 Hz grid. Should match Frequency field selection. 1500 RPM (50 Hz) is standard for standby DG sets in India and GCC; lower speed extends engine service life and reduces vibration.

fields.label: Current
fields.hint: Full-load output current of the alternator. Auto-calculated: I (A) = kVA × 1000 / (1.732 × V). 150 kVA / 415 V ≈ 209 A; 500 kVA / 415 V ≈ 695 A. Used for outgoing cable sizing and ACB/MCCB rating selection. Override only if a non-standard voltage is used.

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Ingress protection rating of the acoustic enclosure and alternator. IP54 outdoor acoustic canopy is standard for most industrial DG sets. IP23 suits clean indoor machine rooms with forced ventilation. IP55 required for coastal, high-dust, or process-plant locations. Acoustic canopy adds approximately 8-12% to base DG set price.

fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Diesel Generator' IN equipment_subtype AND power > 1000
compliance_rules.message: DG sets above 1000 kVA fall outside standard LV switchgear ratings (up to 6300 A). A Medium Voltage (MV/HT) generator with step-down transformer is typically required. Review system voltage architecture.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60034-1 (rotating machines); IS 4722:2001; LV switchgear bus ratings per IEC 60439-1

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Diesel Generator' IN equipment_subtype AND frequency == '50' AND speed == '1800'
compliance_rules.message: Speed set to 1800 RPM but frequency is 50 Hz. A 4-pole machine at 1800 RPM produces 60 Hz output, not 50 Hz. Correct speed to 1500 RPM for 50 Hz, or change frequency to 60 Hz.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Synchronous speed formula: Ns = 120f/P. For P=4 poles: 50 Hz → 1500 RPM; 60 Hz → 1800 RPM. IEC 60034-1.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Diesel Generator' IN equipment_subtype AND frequency == '60' AND speed == '1500'
compliance_rules.message: Speed set to 1500 RPM but frequency is 60 Hz. Correct speed to 1800 RPM for 60 Hz grid, or change frequency to 50 Hz.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Synchronous speed formula: Ns = 120f/P. IEC 60034-1.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Diesel Generator' IN equipment_subtype AND motor_power > power * 0.95
compliance_rules.message: Engine kW rating cannot exceed alternator kVA rating. At 0.8 power factor, engine kW should be ≤ kVA × 0.8 (±5% tolerance). Check if kW and kVA entries are for the same DG set.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 8528-1:2018 Cl. 6 — DG set power definitions and matching; engine derating at altitude/temperature

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Diesel Generator' IN equipment_subtype AND enclosure == 'IP23 (indoor, open ventilated)' AND installation_type == 'Outdoor'
compliance_rules.message: IP23 open-ventilated enclosure is not suitable for outdoor installation. Minimum IP44 for semi-outdoor; IP54 acoustic canopy for fully outdoor locations.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60529 IP ratings; IS 4691; DG manufacturer installation guidelines for outdoor duty

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Diesel Generator' IN equipment_subtype AND installation_type == 'Indoor' AND exhaust_routed != 'Yes'
compliance_rules.message: Indoor DG sets require a properly designed exhaust system with insulated flue piping routed to outside. Carbon monoxide accumulation is a life-safety hazard.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NBC 2016 Part 8 Sec. 3 (DG installation); NFPA 110 Cl. 7.2; CPCB emission norms for gensets

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'AMF Panel (Auto Mains Failure)' AND amf_rating_kva < power
compliance_rules.message: AMF Panel current rating must be equal to or greater than the connected DG set kVA rating. Under-rated AMF panel will cause overheating and tripping under load transfer.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 8623-1:1993 LV switchgear assemblies; IEC 60947-2 (ACB ratings); DG-AMF coordination practice

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'AMF Panel (Auto Mains Failure)' AND bus_transfer_scheme != 'open_transition' AND bus_transfer_scheme != 'closed_transition'
compliance_rules.message: AMF Panel must specify bus transfer scheme: Open Transition (break-before-make, standard for most loads) or Closed Transition (make-before-break, required for sensitive / UPS-backed loads).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60947-6-1 (transfer switching equipment); NFPA 110 Cl. 6.4; IEEE 446 (recommended practice for emergency power)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Diesel Generator' IN equipment_subtype AND quantity > 1 AND load_sharing_scheme == 'None (single set)'
compliance_rules.message: Multiple DG sets specified without a load-sharing / synchronising panel. Parallel operation requires isochronous governors, AVR droop settings, and a synchronising relay (25 relay). Specify synchronising panel separately.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 10000 Part 3 (DG parallel operation); IEC 60034-3; IEEE C37.95 (generator protection)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Diesel Generator' IN equipment_subtype AND power >= 500 AND noise_db IS None
compliance_rules.message: DG sets 500 kVA and above are subject to CPCB noise emission norms (≤ 75 dB(A) at 1 m from enclosure surface). Confirm acoustic canopy specification meets this limit. Noise measurement test report required at commissioning.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CPCB Notification SO 1198(E) 2002 — Noise standards for DG sets; IS 15683 (acoustic enclosures)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Diesel Generator' IN equipment_subtype AND fuel_tank_capacity IS None
compliance_rules.message: Base fuel tank capacity not specified. CPCB and NBC guidelines recommend minimum 8 hours of full-load autonomy for standby DG sets. Confirm fuel tank volume is included in the scope.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NBC 2016 Part 8 Sec. 3.5; CPCB DG guidelines; typical standby autonomy practice (8 hr minimum, 24 hr preferred for critical facilities)

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_equipment_cost(equipment_subtype, power_kVA) × enclosure_factor(enclosure) × quantity × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100)
cost_build_up.full_formula: [ dg_set_ex_works(power_kVA, motor_power_kW, frequency, speed, enclosure) + amf_panel_cost(amf_rating_kVA) + accessories_cost(fuel_tank, exhaust_system, battery_charger) ] × quantity × regional_index_factor × (1 + import_duty_pct/100) × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: Switched ENR->CEPCI: procured/manufactured equipment, supply-cost dominated. verify=true

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-DIST-BOARD.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | {voltage} V | {current} A | FL={fault_level} kA | {standard} | {enclosure} | Qty={quantity} No.
SCHEMA-DIST-BOARD.spec_example: Distribution Board (Main - 800A) | 415 V | 800 A | FL=50 kA | IEC 61439 | IP54 | Qty=1 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: System nominal voltage. Common values: 230 V (1-ph LN, IEC), 415 V (3-ph LL, IEC/India), 480 V (3-ph, USA/Canada), 400 V (3-ph, Europe). Maximum IEC low-voltage limit is 1 000 V AC. Enter line-to-line voltage for three-phase boards.

fields.label: Current
fields.hint: Incomer / busbar continuous current rating. Standard frame sizes: 125 A (lighting DB), 160–250 A (sub-DB), 400–800 A (main DB), 1 000–4 000 A (MCC / PMCC). Select the next standard frame size above the calculated demand current.

fields.label: Fault Level
fields.hint: Prospective symmetrical fault current (PSCC) at the board incomer. Obtain from the system short-circuit study or upstream equipment data. Typical values: lighting DB 10 kA, sub-DB 25 kA, main DB / MCC 50 kA. Board must be type-tested to at least this level per IEC 61439-1.

fields.label: Standard
fields.hint: Design and type-test standard for the assembly. IEC 61439 — international default (Parts 1, 2 for DB; Part 4 for MCC). NEC 408 (NFPA 70) — mandatory for USA/Canada projects. IS 8623 — India-specific LV switchgear assemblies (harmonised with IEC 61439). Confirm with authority having jurisdiction (AHJ) for the project country.

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Ingress protection rating per IEC 60529. IP43 — indoor, clean, dry locations (office / control room). IP54 — indoor industrial, dusty or splash-prone areas (factory floor, plantroom). IP65 — outdoor or wash-down locations (process plant, water treatment). Higher IP rating increases enclosure cost by ~10–25 %.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: fault_level > 50
compliance_rules.message: Fault level exceeds 50 kA — this is beyond the standard type-test rating for LV distribution boards. A custom-engineered PMCC or HV solution may be required. Consult the switchgear manufacturer.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61439-1 Cl. 10.11: maximum standard type-test fault level for LV assemblies is 50 kA (1 s). Above this, bespoke testing or HV switchgear (IEC 62271) applies.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: current > 800 AND equipment_subtype != 'Distribution Board (Motor Control - Local)'
compliance_rules.message: Incomer current rating > 800 A for a standard distribution board. Consider reclassifying as a Main LV Switchboard (MLVS) or PMCC, which attracts different type-test and busbar design requirements.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61439-2: distribution boards (Form 1–4) are typically rated to 800 A incomer; above 800 A switchgear assemblies require MLVS/MCC classification and Form 3b or Form 4 segregation.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: enclosure == 'IP43' AND equipment_subtype == 'Distribution Board (Motor Control - Local)'
compliance_rules.message: IP43 is not recommended for a local motor control board. Process plant environments typically require IP54 or IP65 to protect against dust and splash ingress.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60529 / IEC 61439-4 (MCCs): local control panels in process plant are conventionally specified to minimum IP54; ATEX / NEC hazardous areas require additional Ex-rated enclosures.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: voltage > 1000
compliance_rules.message: Voltage exceeds 1 000 V AC — this is above the IEC low-voltage boundary. The board must be designed and certified as a high-voltage (MV) switchgear assembly under IEC 62271, not IEC 61439.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60038 / IEC 61439-1 Scope: low voltage is defined as ≤ 1 000 V AC. Systems above 1 kV fall under IEC 62271 medium-voltage switchgear standards.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: standard == 'NEC 408 (NFPA 70)' AND voltage == 415
compliance_rules.message: NEC 408 selected but voltage is 415 V — confirm this is correct. North American systems use 480 V (three-phase) or 120/240 V (single-phase); 415 V is an IEC / India system voltage.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NFPA 70 NEC Art. 408 covers switchboards and panelboards for North American system voltages (120/208/240/277/480 V). IEC system voltages (415 V) require IEC 61439 compliance instead.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Distribution Board (Main - 800A)' AND fault_level < 25
compliance_rules.message: Main 800 A board with fault level below 25 kA — verify the short-circuit study result. Main incomer boards close to the transformer are usually subject to higher fault levels (typically 35–50 kA at 415 V / 1 600 kVA transformer).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60909 short-circuit calculation: a 1 600 kVA, 6% Zk transformer at 415 V yields ~35 kA fault current at secondary terminals. Main DB located within a few metres of the transformer should be rated accordingly.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype) × current_scale_factor(current)^0.65 × fault_level_factor(fault_level) × voltage_factor(voltage) × enclosure_factor(enclosure) × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype) × current_scale_factor(current)^0.65 × fault_level_factor(fault_level) × voltage_factor(voltage) × enclosure_factor(enclosure) × standard_factor(standard) × regional_cost_index.factor × sub_region.equipment_factor × (1 + adders.freight_insurance + adders.erection_installation + adders.contingency) × quantity

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
