compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Fire Resistant Door (2hr)' AND fire_rating_certified != 'Yes'
compliance_rules.message: 2-hour fire resistant door must carry a third-party certification (e.g., BIS/UL/FM) confirming the rated assembly (frame + shutter + hardware). Uncertified assemblies cannot be used as fire barriers.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 3614 (Part 2):1992 Fire check doors; NBC 2016 Part 4 Cl. 4.8; UL 10C (positive pressure fire test)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Fire Resistant Door (2hr)' AND door_closer_provided != 'Yes'
compliance_rules.message: Self-closing device (overhead door closer) is mandatory on all fire doors. A fire door held open without an automatic release mechanism is non-compliant.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NBC 2016 Part 4 Cl. 4.8.2; IS 3614 Part 2; NFPA 80 Cl. 4.8

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Fire Resistant Door (2hr)' AND door_width > 1200
compliance_rules.message: Single-leaf fire door width exceeds 1200 mm. Double-leaf (pair) fire door assembly required above this width. Each leaf must independently satisfy the 2-hour rating.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 3614 Part 2; NBC 2016 Part 4; standard fire-door manufacturer span limits

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Rolling Shutter (MS)' AND shutter_width > 6000
compliance_rules.message: Rolling shutter width exceeds 6 m. Motorised operation with limit switches is recommended above 6 m; manual operation requires excessive effort and poses a safety risk.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 6248:1986 Metal rolling shutters; BIS guidelines; operational ergonomics (>15 kg manual effort limit)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Rolling Shutter (MS)' AND shutter_height > 4500 AND motorised != 'Yes'
compliance_rules.message: Rolling shutter height exceeds 4.5 m without motorised drive. The dead weight of the shutter curtain will exceed safe manual-operation limits. Specify motorised operator.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 6248:1986 Cl. 5; standard motor selection charts for MS curtain weight/area

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Rolling Shutter (MS)' AND location == 'Fire exit'
compliance_rules.message: MS rolling shutters must not be used as a means-of-escape (fire exit) door. A swinging fire-rated personnel door is required for escape routes.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NBC 2016 Part 4 Cl. 4.5 (means of escape); NFPA 101 Cl. 7.2.1.9

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Wooden Door Frame (Hardwood)' AND exposure_zone == 'High humidity'
compliance_rules.message: Hardwood door frames in high-humidity or splash zones require pressure-treatment (LOSP or CCA) or use of naturally durable species (teak/sal). Untreated frames will deteriorate rapidly.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 4891:1968 Wood preservation for frames; IS 1141:1993 Seasoning of timber; IS 287 moisture content limits

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Wooden Door Shutter (Panel)' AND door_height > 2400
compliance_rules.message: Wooden panel door shutter height above 2400 mm requires intermediate horizontal rail to prevent warping and racking. Verify with joinery fabricator.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 1003 (Part 1):1991 Timber door shutters; SP 7 (NBC) joinery guidelines; practical joinery limits for solid-panel construction

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Flush Door Shutter (Commercial)' AND location == 'External'
compliance_rules.message: Commercial flush door shutters (IS 2202 Type I/II) are intended for internal use only. External duty requires a moisture-resistant (MR) or boiling water-resistant (BWR) grade core with suitable external finish.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 2202 (Part 1):1999 Wooden flush door shutters (solid core) — Grade classification; IS 303 BWR grade plywood

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Sliding Door (Aluminum)' AND door_width > 3600
compliance_rules.message: Aluminium sliding door opening wider than 3.6 m: verify section profile and track load capacity with system supplier. Thermal expansion gap at jambs becomes critical above this width.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 1948:1961 Aluminium doors/windows; AAMA 101/I.S.2/A440 (North America); supplier technical data for track deflection and expansion joints

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) × door_area(door_width, door_height) × quantity × size_complexity_factor × regional_factor × sub_region_civil_factor × (1 + hardware_grade_adder)
cost_build_up.full_formula: [ frame_material_cost(frame_material, door_width, door_height) + shutter_material_cost(shutter_material, door_area) + hardware_cost(hardware_grade, fire_rating) + finish_cost(finish, door_area) + door_closer_cost(fire_rating) ] × quantity × regional_index_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

SCHEMA-EARTHING.generation_note: v1.0 — rebuilt from scratch (original file had been overwritten with FIRED-EQUIP content); IS 3043 / IEC 62305 basis. [note synced to v1.0-priced release]

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-EARTHING.spec_example: Earth Pit (GI Pipe Electrode); qty 12; soil 85 ohm-m; target 1 ohm

compliance_rules.condition_expr: soil_resistivity > 100 AND (equipment_subtype == 'Earth Pit (GI Pipe Electrode)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Earth Pit (Copper Plate Electrode)')
compliance_rules.message: Soil resistivity above 100 ohm-m: conventional electrodes may not achieve target resistance — consider chemical/maintenance-free earth pits, deeper electrodes, or soil treatment.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 3043 (2018) — electrode resistance proportional to soil resistivity; treatment recommended in high-resistivity soils.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: target_resistance_ohm > 0 AND target_resistance_ohm > 1 AND equipment_subtype == 'Earth Grid / Mat (Substation)'
compliance_rules.message: Substation earth grids are normally designed for ≤1 ohm combined earth resistance.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 3043 / IEEE 80 practice for substation grounding grids.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: hazardous_area != 'No' AND equipment_subtype == 'Equipment Earthing Connection'
compliance_rules.message: Hazardous-area equipment earthing: bonding resistance and connection type must meet IEC 60079-14; dedicated bonding conductors and certified clamps required.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079-14 — electrical installations in explosive atmospheres.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: structure_height_m > 60 AND equipment_subtype == 'Lightning Air Termination (Conventional - Franklin)'
compliance_rules.message: Structures above 60 m require side-flash protection and additional air-termination/down-conductor provisions per IEC 62305-3.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 62305-3 — protection of structures, tall-structure provisions.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Lightning Arrester (ESE Type)'
compliance_rules.message: ESE air terminals are not recognised by IEC 62305; acceptance depends on local code (e.g. NFC 17-102). Verify approval basis for the project jurisdiction.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 62305 vs NFC 17-102 — ESE recognition differs by jurisdiction.

cost_build_up.method: Unit-rate installed cost (supply + installation), USA labour basis, escalated via ENR CCI.
cost_build_up.note: This schema covers 5 heterogeneous items with 3 measurement bases: (1) No. of support locations for Pipe support installation and load calculations; (2) kg of structural steel for Pipe support structure; (3) No. of joints/sleeves for expansion joints and sleeves. The cost engine selects the appropriate rate from usd_cost_baseline by matching equipment_subtype and applying the relevant sizing field.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: tier_1 × soil/depth difficulty factor (where applicable)
cost_build_up.full_formula: tier_2 × hazardous-area factor + test & certification adder
cost_build_up.scope_inclusions: For pipe support installation: supply and installation of all support hardware (hanger rods, clevis, turnbuckle, clamp, shoe, U-bolt, baseplate) to the structural steel or concrete anchor. For pipe support structure: structural steel supply, surface preparation, primer coat, fabrication at workshop, and site erection including anchor bolts. For load calculations: stress analysis software (ASME B31.3 compliance check), calculation report, and revision cycle. For expansion joints: supply of joint from manufacturer and installation including tie rods (if tied), flange bolting, and initial alignment check. For pipe sleeves: supply of sleeve pipe, installation in wall/slab, annular gap seal, and fire stop packing.
cost_build_up.scope_exclusions: For pipe support structure: foundation concrete anchor bolts (separate civil item), access scaffolding above 4.5 m (separate item), shop painting beyond one primer coat. For expansion joints: pipe spool pieces connecting joint to adjacent elbows/valves. For pipe sleeves: wall or slab core drilling if sleeve not cast in (separate civil item). Engineering QA/QC review of load calculations.

adders.note: For activity (type A) items, freight_insurance = 0.0 and erection_installation = 0.0 because all labour and materials are in the unit rate. Exception: Pipe expansion joints are manufactured items (item_type E conceptually) but are priced here as installed cost inclusive. Contingency 0.12 reflects complexity of spring hanger settings, interface with structural steel anchor points, and site measurement uncertainty for custom expansion joint spool pieces.

support_type_factor.note: Cost multiplier on base Pipe support installation unit rate (resting/shoe CS base = 1.0). Applied for Tier 2 estimate.

expansion_joint_type_factor.note: Cost multiplier on base expansion joint unit rate (single-ply metallic bellows SS316L = 1.0).

usd_cost_baseline.note: Base = installed USD at USA labour level (matches ENR CCI base 14250 @ 2024). verify=true entries are public-benchmark estimates pending calibration.

SCHEMA-EARTHWORK.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | Vol: {volume} m³ | Area: {area} m² | Motor: {motor_power} kW | Eff: {efficiency}% | Qty: {quantity}
SCHEMA-EARTHWORK.spec_example: Site Clearance & Surface Dressing | Area: 5000 m² | Qty: 1

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific activity type. Controls which quantity fields (area or volume) and which parameters are shown below.

fields.label: Area
fields.hint: Plan area of the site activity in m². Used for surface-based activities: Site Clearance & Surface Dressing. Typical industrial plant site clearance: 2,000–50,000 m².

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Site Clearance & Surface Dressing']

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Bulk volume of earthwork in m³ (measured in-situ / bank measure). Used for excavation, backfill, and disposal activities. Note: apply a swell factor of 1.20–1.35 for loose/disposal volumes. Typical foundation excavation: 100–5,000 m³; large basements or rock cuts: 500–50,000 m³.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Earthwork Excavation (Foundation)', 'Earthwork Excavation (Basement/Rock)', 'Backfilling with Excavated Earth', 'Backfilling with Imported Sand', 'Disposal of Surplus Earth']

fields.label: Pump Efficiency
fields.hint: Overall pump efficiency (%) for dewatering systems. Submersible pumps: typically 60–80%. Well point vacuum systems: 70–85%. Used to calculate energy consumption cost component. Leave blank for non-dewatering activities.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Dewatering (Well Point System)', 'Dewatering (Submersible Pumps)']

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Rated motor power per pump unit in kW. For submersible dewatering pumps: typically 4–37 kW (5–50 HP). For well point vacuum pump sets: typically 7–22 kW. Used to size temporary power supply and estimate energy running cost.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Dewatering (Well Point System)', 'Dewatering (Submersible Pumps)']

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of discrete line items of this activity. Normally 1. Use >1 only when identical activities apply to multiple separate locations within the same BOQ line (e.g. 4 identical pump sumps).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Earthwork Excavation (Basement/Rock)' AND volume > 5000
compliance_rules.message: Rock excavation volume exceeds 5,000 m³ — confirm rock classification (rippable vs. blasting-grade) from geotechnical investigation report. Blasting-grade rock requires regulatory permits and significantly higher unit rates (typically 3–6× soft excavation).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 3764 (excavation safety); IS 4081 (blasting operations); BS 6031:2009 Clause 6 (earthworks — rock classification)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['Dewatering (Well Point System)', 'Dewatering (Submersible Pumps)'] AND motor_power IS None
compliance_rules.message: Motor power is required for dewatering activities to size temporary electrical supply and estimate running costs. Enter pump motor rating in kW.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 9759 (dewatering guidelines); BS EN 805 (water supply — requirements for systems)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['Dewatering (Well Point System)', 'Dewatering (Submersible Pumps)']
compliance_rules.message: Dewatering discharge may require environmental consent or a groundwater abstraction licence depending on jurisdiction. Confirm with the project environmental engineer before mobilisation.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 3764 Clause 8 (groundwater handling); Environment Protection Act 1986 (India); EU WFD 2000/60/EC Article 11 (programme of measures for groundwater)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Disposal of Surplus Earth' AND volume > 10000
compliance_rules.message: Disposal volume exceeds 10,000 m³ — confirm approved disposal site, haulage distance, and tipping license. Costs are highly sensitive to haul distance; update unit rate if disposal site is >10 km from works.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Environment Protection Act 1986 (India — Hazardous Waste Rules 2016 if contaminated); WRAP Good Practice Guide for Earthworks (UK); 40 CFR Part 257 (USA — solid waste disposal)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Backfilling with Imported Sand' AND volume > 500
compliance_rules.message: Imported sand quantities above 500 m³ should have a confirmed quarry/borrow source with approved material specification (grading, plasticity, compaction). Include lead and haul cost in unit rate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 2720 Part 8 (soil compaction test); IS 1498 (soil classification); BS 6031:2009 Clause 8 (fill material specification)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Site Clearance & Surface Dressing' AND area > 100000
compliance_rules.message: Site clearance area exceeds 10 ha — Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) or Forest Clearance approval may be required depending on land classification. Confirm statutory approvals before mobilisation.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: EIA Notification 2006 (India — MoEF&CC); EU EIA Directive 2014/52/EU Annex II; NEPA 1969 (USA)

cost_build_up.cost_basis_note: Earthwork is an in-situ civil activity priced per unit of measure (m² or m³). There is no 'equipment supply' component. All cost is labour + plant hire + materials (imported fill only). Adders reflect contractor preliminaries and site overheads, not freight/erection.
cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) × primary_quantity × quantity × regional_factor × (1 + mob_demob_pct) × (1 + contingency)
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) × primary_quantity × soil_factor(soil_type) × quantity × regional_factor × sub_region_labour_factor × (1 + mob_demob_pct) × (1 + site_overheads_pct) × (1 + contingency)
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) × primary_quantity × soil_factor(soil_type) × depth_factor(depth) × quantity × regional_factor × sub_region_labour_factor × (1 + import_duty_pct/100) × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + mob_demob_pct) × (1 + site_overheads_pct) × (1 + contingency)

primary_quantity_mapping.note: Select the correct quantity field as the primary cost driver for each activity sub-type.
primary_quantity_mapping.Dewatering (Well Point System): volume (m³) of excavation being dewatered, or lump sum per week — confirm with contractor
primary_quantity_mapping.Dewatering (Submersible Pumps): volume (m³) of excavation being dewatered, or lump sum per week — confirm with contractor

base_unit_rates_usd.note: USD unit rates, USA average basis (2024, RSMeans-aligned). Apply regional_cost_index factor to convert to project region. Rates are all-in: labour + plant hire + fuel. Imported fill materials add separately.

Site Clearance & Surface Dressing.includes: Vegetation removal, grubbing, surface stripping 150mm topsoil, haul to spoil within 500m

Earthwork Excavation (Foundation).includes: Soft to medium soil, JCB/excavator, depths up to 3m, stockpile or load to cart

Earthwork Excavation (Basement/Rock).includes: Hard rock — hydraulic breaker; blasting-grade rock add 40–80 USD/m³ for drilling and blasting

Backfilling with Excavated Earth.includes: Place, spread, and compact in 200mm layers to 95% Proctor, dozer + vibrating roller

Backfilling with Imported Sand.includes: Supply, haul (assume ≤20 km), place, and compact; rate sensitive to haul distance

Disposal of Surplus Earth.includes: Load, haul up to 10 km, and tip at approved disposal site; add 1.50 USD/m³ per additional 5 km

Dewatering (Well Point System).includes: Mobilise, install well point headers and risers, operate vacuum pump set; per week running cost; demob separate

Dewatering (Submersible Pumps).includes: Supply, install, and operate submersible pump(s) including discharge hose and power; per week running cost

soil_factor.note: Applied to excavation unit rate to account for soil/rock class.

depth_factor.note: Applied to excavation and backfill rates for deep excavations requiring shoring or battering.

swell_factor_guidance.note: Convert bank (in-situ) m³ to loose m³ for haulage and disposal pricing.

adders.mob_demob_note: Contractor mobilisation and demobilisation of plant. Typically 5–8% of earthworks direct cost for industrial projects. Reduce to 3% for large-volume works (>50,000 m³) where unit rate absorbs mob cost.
adders.site_overheads_note: Contractor site preliminaries: supervision, surveying/setting out, temporary roads, welfare, insurance. Typically 10–15% of direct earthworks cost.
adders.freight_insurance_note: Not applicable — earthwork is an in-situ activity with no shipped equipment. Any imported fill material haulage cost is embedded in the unit rate.
adders.erection_installation_note: Not applicable — labour and plant are fully included in the unit rate.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: item_type corrected E->A: unit-rate construction/installation work, field-labour dominated (matches PLASTERING / STORM-DRAIN / EARTHING library convention). ENR CCI retained, base 14250 (2024).

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-ETP-EQUIP.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | Q={flow_rate} m³/day | V={volume} m³ | P={motor_power} kW | MOC={moc} | Qty={quantity} No.
SCHEMA-ETP-EQUIP.spec_example: Bar Screen (Coarse) | Q=300 m³/day | V=10 m³ | P=2.2 kW | MOC=SS304 | Qty=1 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Flow Rate
fields.hint: Daily design flow to be treated. Typical range 30–1 000 m³/day for industrial ETPs. Use peak daily flow, not average.

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Net working volume of the tank or vessel. Typical HRT-based values: EQ tank 8–24 h, aeration tank 6–12 h. Exclude freeboard (add 15–20 % for gross volume).

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Installed motor power per unit. Aeration blowers: 5–75 kW typical; surface aerators: 1.5–22 kW; sludge centrifuges: 7.5–75 kW.

fields.label: Flow Rate
fields.hint: Instantaneous flow rate through the unit. For membranes use net permeate flow after accounting for recovery ratio. Typical: UF 5–200 m³/hr, RO 2–100 m³/hr.

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Volume of chemical storage / dosing tank. Size for ≥ 7-day autonomy at maximum dose rate. Typical: 100–500 L for pH dosing, larger for flocculant systems.

fields.label: Speed
fields.hint: Shaft / drum rotational speed. RBC: 1–3 RPM; flash mixers: 60–150 RPM; sludge centrifuges: 2 000–4 000 RPM. Enter the operating speed, not synchronous speed.

fields.label: Diameter
fields.hint: Internal diameter of circular vessel or clarifier. Circular clarifiers: 5–30 m (overflow rate 0.6–1.5 m/h). Anaerobic digesters: 5–20 m. Thickeners: 5–25 m.

fields.label: Area
fields.hint: Effective plan area of the unit. Sand filter: loading rate 5–10 m/h → area = Q / rate. Sludge drying bed: 1–3 m²/kg DS/day. Lamella clarifier: settler projected area.

fields.label: Efficiency
fields.hint: Overall process removal efficiency for the primary pollutant. COD removal: aeration tank 85–95 %, UASB 60–80 %; BOD removal: trickling filter 60–80 %; SS removal: DAF 80–95 %, clarifier 60–75 %.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Design operating temperature. Mesophilic digestion: 35–37 °C; thermophilic: 50–55 °C; sludge incinerator: 800–850 °C. Leave blank for ambient-temperature units.

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure (gauge). Pressure sand filters: 3–6 bar(g); RO pressure vessels: 8–70 bar(g); UF modules: 2–6 bar(g). Enter 0 for open/atmospheric vessels.

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of construction for wetted parts. SS304 for clean water / neutral pH; SS316 where chlorides > 200 mg/L or pH < 4 / > 10. Select higher grade for aggressive industrial effluents.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 10 AND 'Sand Filter' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds 10 bar(g) for a sand filter — verify filter vessel pressure rating and select a certified pressure vessel to IS 2825 / ASME VIII.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 2825 / ASME Boiler & Pressure Vessel Code Section VIII Div.1; typical sand filter design pressure 3–6 bar(g)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: flow_rate > 0 AND volume > 0 AND (volume / (flow_rate / 24)) < 2
compliance_rules.message: Calculated HRT is below 2 hours — too short for effective biological treatment. Review tank volume or flow rate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Metcalf & Eddy, Wastewater Engineering 5th Ed., Chapter 7 — minimum HRT for activated sludge 4–8 h; EQ tank ≥ 8 h

compliance_rules.condition_expr: motor_power > 75 AND equipment_subtype == 'Blower (Aeration)'
compliance_rules.message: Single aeration blower motor > 75 kW — consider N+1 standby arrangement and VFD for energy optimisation.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 1239 / CPCB ETP guidelines; energy benchmark 0.8–2.0 kWh/kg BOD removed

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature > 55 AND NOT equipment_subtype IN ['Sludge Incinerator','Anaerobic Digester']
compliance_rules.message: Design temperature > 55 °C is unusual for this equipment type — confirm process conditions and vessel insulation requirements.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Standard ETP operating temperatures: biological processes 15–40 °C; thermophilic digestion ≤ 55 °C

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc == 'SS304' AND ('Reverse Osmosis' IN equipment_subtype OR 'Nanofiltration' IN equipment_subtype)
compliance_rules.message: SS316 is generally preferred for RO / NF pressure vessels due to chloride stress-corrosion risk — verify feed water chloride content.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASTM A276 / NACE MR0175; SS316 recommended where Cl⁻ > 200 mg/L

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'UASB Reactor' AND design_temperature < 25
compliance_rules.message: UASB reactor operating below 25 °C — methanogenic activity severely inhibited. Provide influent heating or select an alternative biological process.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Lettinga et al., UASB design criteria: optimal 30–37 °C; below 20 °C performance degrades sharply

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype) × scale_factor(flow_rate, volume) × moc_factor(moc) × pressure_factor(design_pressure) × temperature_factor(design_temperature) × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype) × scale_factor(flow_rate, volume) × moc_factor(moc) × pressure_factor(design_pressure) × temperature_factor(design_temperature) × motor_factor(motor_power) × efficiency_factor(efficiency) × area_factor(area) × regional_cost_index.factor × sub_region.equipment_factor × (1 + adders.freight_insurance + adders.erection_installation + adders.contingency) × quantity

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Compound Wall (Brick)' AND height > 3.6
compliance_rules.message: Brick compound wall height exceeds 3.6 m. Structural design with RCC columns and band beams is mandatory above this height. Obtain structural engineer sign-off.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 1905:1987 Cl. 5.4 - unreinforced masonry wall height limit; IS 4326 for seismic zones

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Compound Wall (Precast)' AND height > 4.5
compliance_rules.message: Precast compound wall height exceeds 4.5 m. Foundation and panel connection design must be verified by structural engineer.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 15916:2010 Precast concrete - structural design; manufacturer span/height tables

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Barbed Wire Fence' AND height < 1.8
compliance_rules.message: Barbed wire fence height below 1.8 m may not meet industrial perimeter security requirements. Typical minimum is 1.8 m (3 plain + 3 barbed wire strands).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 278:2011 (Galvanized steel barbed wire); CISF/Factory Act perimeter security guidelines

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Chain Link Fence' AND height < 1.5
compliance_rules.message: Chain link fence below 1.5 m height is generally inadequate for site perimeter. Minimum 1.5 m recommended; 1.8 m with barbed wire top for security.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 2502:1975 Chain link fencing; General security practice for industrial sites

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'MS Gate (Sliding - Main)' OR equipment_subtype == 'MS Gate (Swing - Pedestrian)') AND gate_width > 12
compliance_rules.message: Gate width exceeds 12 m. For spans >12 m, a bi-parting sliding gate or cantilever design is required. Single-leaf swing gates are not structurally viable above this width.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 800:2007 Steel structures; standard gate fabrication practice; post/hinge load limits

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'MS Gate (Sliding - Main)' AND automation == 'Yes' AND gate_width > 9
compliance_rules.message: Motorised sliding gate wider than 9 m requires gear-rack drive or heavy-duty wheel assembly. Confirm actuator capacity with gate weight.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: FAAC / BFT gate automation selection guides; IS 800 for gate leaf fabrication

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Watch Tower (Steel)' AND height > 6
compliance_rules.message: Watch tower above 6 m requires wind load analysis per IS 875 Part 3 and structural drawings stamped by a licensed structural engineer before fabrication.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 875 (Part 3):2015 Wind loads; IS 800:2007 Cl. 10 - stability and connection design

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Bollard (Concrete)' AND spacing < 1.0
compliance_rules.message: Bollard spacing below 1.0 m may obstruct emergency pedestrian egress. Minimum clear spacing of 1.2 m recommended to allow stretcher/wheelchair access.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NBC 2016 Part 4 - Fire and life safety; IS 800 for vehicle impact; ADA/barrier-free access guidelines

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Security Cabin (RCC)' AND area < 4.5
compliance_rules.message: Security cabin floor area below 4.5 m² (e.g. 1.5 m × 3 m) may not comply with minimum workstation space requirements under Factories Act / local building bylaws.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Factories Act 1948 Sec. 16 (space per worker); local municipal building bylaws

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) × quantity × height_factor × length × regional_factor × sub_region_civil_factor × (1 + foundation_type_adder)
cost_build_up.full_formula: [ material_cost(equipment_subtype, material_grade, length, height) + labour_cost(equipment_subtype, labour_hours_per_unit × sub_region_labour_factor) + foundation_cost(foundation_type, soil_condition) ] × quantity × regional_index_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: item_type corrected E->A: unit-rate construction/installation work, field-labour dominated (matches PLASTERING / STORM-DRAIN / EARTHING library convention). ENR CCI retained, base 14250 (2024).

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Power
fields.hint: Rated power consumption of the unit in kVA. Applies primarily to ESD control panels and F&G system controllers. Typical value: 1.0 kVA for panel-mounted units.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['ESD Control Panel (Fire & Gas)', 'ESD Control Panel (Process Shutdown)', 'ESD Control Panel (Combustion)', 'Fire & Gas Detection System']

fields.label: Efficiency
fields.hint: Power supply efficiency for panel units (%). For purely passive/field devices (detectors, call points, sounders), set to 100%. Range 80–100% for SMPS-equipped panels.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['ESD Control Panel (Fire & Gas)', 'ESD Control Panel (Process Shutdown)', 'ESD Control Panel (Combustion)', 'Fire & Gas Detection System']

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: Supply voltage in VDC. Standard FGS/ESD field devices operate at 24 VDC. ESD panels may also accept 110/240 VAC input. Confirm with vendor datasheet.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum ambient design temperature at the installation location (°C). Affects enclosure rating and equipment certification. Typical outdoor tropical: 40–57°C. Offshore North Sea: 8–25°C.

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Enclosure protection rating. Ex d (flameproof) or Ex ia (intrinsically safe) required in Zone 1/2 hazardous areas per IEC 60079. IP65/IP66 for outdoor general-purpose. IP54/IP55 for indoor or sheltered locations.

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of construction for the enclosure/housing. SS316 is standard for offshore and corrosive environments (H2S / chloride). GRP suits high-corrosion areas without spark risk. Polycarbonate for indoor fire alarm devices.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

fields.label: Hazardous Area Zone
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature > 55
compliance_rules.message: Design temperature exceeds 55°C — verify equipment ATEX/IECEx T-class rating and confirm vendor approval for this ambient. Many SIL-rated detectors are rated to 55°C max.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079-0 Clause 7.4 (temperature class); IEC 61511-1 Clause 11.4 (equipment suitability for environment)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: enclosure IN ['IP54', 'IP55'] AND equipment_subtype IN ['Gas Detector (Toxic - H2S)', 'Gas Detector (Combustible - LEL)', 'Flame Detector (UV/IR)'] AND design_temperature > 40
compliance_rules.message: IP54/IP55 enclosure is not recommended for outdoor gas/flame detectors in tropical or high-humidity environments. Upgrade to IP65 minimum per IEC 60529.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60529 (IP ratings); IEC 60079-29-1 Clause 5.1 (gas detector suitability)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: enclosure NOT IN ['Ex d', 'Ex ia'] AND equipment_subtype IN ['Gas Detector (Toxic - H2S)', 'Gas Detector (Combustible - LEL)', 'Flame Detector (UV/IR)', 'Emergency Shutdown Button', 'Emergency Shutdown Station'] AND hazardous_area_zone IN ['Zone 1', 'Zone 0']
compliance_rules.message: Equipment installed in Zone 0 or Zone 1 must carry Ex d or Ex ia certification. Standard IP enclosures are not sufficient.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079-10-1 (hazardous area classification); IEC 60079-14 Clause 5 (equipment selection for zone)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['ESD Control Panel (Fire & Gas)', 'ESD Control Panel (Process Shutdown)', 'ESD Control Panel (Combustion)'] AND voltage == 24 AND power > 5
compliance_rules.message: Panel power demand >5 kVA at 24 VDC implies >208 A draw — verify UPS/battery backup sizing and cable cross-section per IEC 60364.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61511-1 Clause 11.6 (power supply integrity); IEC 60364-5-52 (cable sizing)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['ESD Control Panel (Fire & Gas)', 'ESD Control Panel (Process Shutdown)', 'Fire & Gas Detection System']
compliance_rules.message: SIL assessment required. Confirm Safety Integrity Level (SIL) allocation per HAZOP/LOPA study and verify SIL certification of selected logic solver (IEC 61511 / IEC 61508).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61511-1 Clause 9 (SIL determination); IEC 61508 Parts 2–3 (hardware/software SIL requirements)

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) × moc_factor(moc) × quantity × regional_factor × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + contingency)
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) × moc_factor(moc) × enclosure_factor(enclosure) × voltage_factor(voltage) × quantity × regional_factor × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) × moc_factor(moc) × enclosure_factor(enclosure) × voltage_factor(voltage) × temperature_factor(design_temperature) × quantity × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × (1 + import_duty_pct/100) × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

base_unit_rates_usd.note: USD ex-works USA list price basis (2024). Apply regional_cost_index factor to convert to project region.

voltage_factor.note: 24 VDC is the baseline. Higher voltages for panels may affect PSU cost.

temperature_factor.note: Applied when design_temperature > 55°C — special high-temp certification surcharge.

cost_build_up.installation_note: Erection/installation factor of 0.22 covers loop testing, cable terminations, hook-up, functional testing, and commissioning. Increase to 0.30 for ESD panels requiring SIL proof-test witnessing and FAT/SAT activities.

compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NFPA 13 / NFPA 24 / IS 15105 — each fire protection system installation is treated as a minimum one-job scope; zero quantity produces a meaningless cost estimate.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: quantity > 50
compliance_rules.message: Quantity exceeds 50 Jobs. Verify this is correct — large repetitive scopes may warrant a dedicated detailed estimate rather than a unit-rate approach.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Engineering judgment — unit-rate accuracy degrades for very large repetitive scopes; a detailed bill of materials estimate is preferred above 50 identical installations.

cost_build_up.method_note: Fire & Safety systems are supplied and installed as complete packaged scopes. Cost is derived from published industry unit rates (USD, USA basis, CEPCI 816) per system type, scaled by quantity, regional factors, and adders. Rates are median installed-cost benchmarks from NFPA/Richardson data (2024). Fireproofing uses a separate m2-based rate but is wrapped into a per-job figure here for Tier 1/2.

unit_rates_usd_per_job.source: Richardson Process Plant Construction Estimating Standards (2024 ed.) + NFPA 13/24 installed-cost benchmarks. Rates represent a mid-size industrial plant scope (approx. 10 hydrants / 2000 m2 sprinkler coverage / 500 m ring main / 1000 m2 fireproofing). Scale linearly with quantity.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: quantity × unit_rates_usd_per_job[equipment_subtype] × (current_CEPCI / 816) × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + import_duty_total_landed_adder_pct/100)
cost_build_up.full_formula: quantity × unit_rates_usd_per_job[equipment_subtype] × (current_CEPCI / 816) × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + import_duty_total_landed_adder_pct/100) × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

adders.freight_insurance_note: 3% of equipment supply cost. Fire & safety packaged systems are typically road-freighted domestically or sea-freighted as break-bulk; lower than rotating equipment due to compact, non-fragile nature. Basis: Richardson 2024 + industry benchmarks.
adders.erection_installation_note: 35% of supply cost. Fire & safety systems are labour-intensive to install (pipe routing, nozzle/hydrant placement, pressure testing, commissioning, tie-ins to alarm panels). Higher than simple piping spools but lower than heavy mechanical equipment. Basis: NFPA 13/24 contractor survey data; adjust to +/-10% for site-specific access difficulty.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

Missing sizing fields F-003 to F-009 added; cost method corrected from equipment_equation to composite unit_rate (labour + material); regional_cost_index corrected to construction labour basis (ENR/RSMeans). CEPCI is inappropriate for field labour activities. | R02 promoted 0.6-engineering-complete->1.0-priced after structural completion + benchmark pricing (validator-gated; no remaining blocking errors).

SCHEMA-FIRED-EQUIP.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | H: {height} m × ID: {diameter} m | {duty_MW} MW | Refractory: {refractory_thickness} mm | Qty: {quantity} Job
SCHEMA-FIRED-EQUIP.spec_example: Furnace refractory lining | H: 12 m × ID: 3.5 m | Refractory: 150 mm castable | Qty: 1 Job

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Height / Length
fields.hint: Overall erected height of the stack or flare, or shell length of the furnace/firebox. Primary cost driver for stack erection (driving crane size and man-hours). Typical ranges: flare stack 20–120 m; process stack 15–60 m; furnace firebox height 6–18 m.

fields.label: Diameter / Width
fields.hint: Internal diameter of stack or flare tip, or width/ID of furnace firebox. Drives refractory volume, shell plate tonnage, and coil bundle dimensions. Typical ranges: flare/stack 0.3–4 m; furnace firebox 2–10 m. For box-type furnaces, enter the larger plan dimension.

fields.label: Fired Duty
fields.hint: Total fired heat release (absorbed duty ÷ thermal efficiency). Required for burner installation (determines burner count and size) and furnace coil installation (determines coil surface area). Typical range: 1–200 MW. Leave blank for stack/flare erection activities where duty is not applicable.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Furnace burner installation', 'Furnace coil installation', 'Furnace refractory lining']

fields.label: Refractory Thickness
fields.hint: Total thickness of refractory lining (all layers combined). Drives material volume and installation labour directly. Typical single-layer castable: 100–200 mm. Multi-layer (hot face + insulating back-up): 150–300 mm total. Required for 'Furnace refractory lining' activity; optional for other sub-types.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Furnace refractory lining']

fields.label: Refractory Type
fields.hint: Refractory material type determines unit material cost and installation method (cast-in-place, gunned, or dry-lay brick). Ceramic fibre is lightest and fastest to install but has lower abrasion resistance; dense castable is most common for floor/arch; brick suits high-severity zones (burner quarl, arch). Drives unit rate multiplier in cost formula.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Furnace refractory lining']

fields.label: Number of Burners
fields.hint: Total number of burner assemblies to be installed (including pilot burners and igniters). Drives installation man-hours directly — each burner requires alignment, fuel/air connection, and commissioning. Typical: 2–8 burners for process heaters; up to 40+ for large reformer furnaces. Required for 'Furnace burner installation'.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Furnace burner installation']

fields.label: Coil / Tube Bundle Weight
fields.hint: Estimated weight of furnace coil / tube bundle including headers and supports. Primary driver of rigging and installation cost (crane size, man-hours). Typical range: 5–150 t for refinery process heater coils. Required for 'Furnace coil installation'. Obtain from vendor datasheet or estimate at 35–50 kg/m² of coil surface area.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Furnace coil installation']

fields.label: Stack / Flare Material
fields.hint: Shell/plate material of the stack or flare structure. Affects both supplied-material cost and any pre-installation surface treatment (blasting, painting, hot-face coating). CS is standard for flue gas stacks above 200 °C dew point; acid-resistant lining or SS required below sulphur dew point (~150 °C). FRP used for cold, corrosive effluent stacks only.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Stack erection', 'Flare stack erection']

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical jobs of this sub-type. A 'job' covers one complete unit activity (e.g. one stack erection, one furnace lining). Enter 1 unless multiple identical items are being installed under the same scope.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Flare stack erection' AND height > 60
compliance_rules.message: Flare stack height exceeds 60 m. Structural design must comply with API 537 (Flare Details for General Refinery and Petrochemical Service) and ASME STS-1 (Steel Stacks). A licensed structural engineer's stamp is mandatory. Wind load analysis per ASCE 7 / IS 875 Part 3 required. Aviation obstruction lighting (ICAO Annex 14) required above 60 m AGL.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 537 Section 5 (structural); ASME STS-1; ASCE 7-22 Chapter 27 (wind loads on stacks); ICAO Annex 14 Table 6-1 (obstacle lighting ≥ 60 m AGL); IS 875 Part 3 (India wind loading).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: height > 45 AND (equipment_subtype == 'Stack erection' OR equipment_subtype == 'Flare stack erection')
compliance_rules.message: Stack height exceeds 45 m. Consider whether a guy-wire-supported or self-supporting design is specified. Self-supporting stacks above 45 m require detailed modal analysis for wind-induced vortex shedding (Strouhal resonance). Confirm structural design basis with civil/structural discipline before proceeding.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME STS-1 Section 4.3 (dynamic wind analysis); CICIND Model Code for Steel Chimneys; IS 6533 Part 2 (Code of Practice for Design and Construction of Steel Chimneys).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Furnace burner installation' AND duty_MW > 0 AND num_burners > 0 AND (duty_MW / num_burners) > 10
compliance_rules.message: Burner duty per burner exceeds 10 MW. Verify burner type and vendor rating. Individual burner heat release above 10 MW (34 MMBtu/hr) is atypical for most process heater designs and may indicate an input error. If correct, confirm NOx compliance with local air quality permit limits and applicable emission standards.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 560 Section 7 (process heater burner selection); EPA 40 CFR Part 63 Subpart DDDDD (NESHAPs for major source boilers); EU IED Directive 2010/75/EU BAT-AEL for NOx.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Furnace refractory lining' AND refractory_type == 'Ceramic fibre blanket' AND duty_MW > 20
compliance_rules.message: Ceramic fibre blanket selected for a high-duty furnace (>20 MW). Ceramic fibre is unsuitable for zones with high-velocity impingement, abrasive particulate, or direct flame contact. Confirm fibre selection is limited to back-up insulation only; specify dense castable or brick for hot-face zones per refractory engineer's recommendation.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASTM C892 (High-Temperature Fibre Blanket Specification); API 560 Annex D (refractory selection guide); NFPA 86 Section 7.4 (furnace refractory requirements).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Flare stack erection' OR equipment_subtype == 'Stack erection'
compliance_rules.message: Confirm that atmospheric dispersion modelling (ADM) has been completed and approved by the environmental authority before finalising stack height. Stack height determined solely by structural convenience without ADM approval may not satisfy local air quality / EIA permit conditions.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: US EPA AP-42; EU Directive 2008/50/EC (CAFE); India Environment Protection Act 1986 Rule 14 & CPCB stack emission standards; World Bank Group EHS Guidelines (ambient air quality).

cost_build_up.method_note: item_type A (Activity). Cost is built as: (material_unit_rate × quantity_of_material) + (labour_unit_rate × labour_quantity) + (crane_rigging_lumpsum). No separate equipment supply cost — this schema covers field installation activities only, not equipment procurement. CEPCI is not applicable; construction cost index (ENR CCI / RSMeans) used instead.
cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: cost = base_unit_rate × size_parameter × subtype_factor × quantity; where size_parameter = height × diameter for stacks/flares, height × diameter² × PI/4 for furnaces (shell area proxy); base_unit_rate in USD/m² of shell or lining area (2024 ENR CCI 12000 basis)
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: cost = (material_rate × refractory_volume + labour_rate × labour_hours + crane_rate × crane_days) × subtype_factor × quantity; refractory_volume = PI × diameter × height × refractory_thickness/1000 [m³]; labour_hours = volume × productivity_factor [m³/man-hr per refractory_type]
cost_build_up.full_formula: cost = SUM_over_zones(material_unit_rate_zone × volume_zone) + (num_burners × burner_installation_rate) + (coil_weight_t × rigging_rate_per_tonne) + (height/10 × crane_shift_rate) + (stack_material_factor × shell_area × plate_rate); shell_area = PI × diameter × height

base_unit_rates_USD_2024.note: Unit rates are all-in field installed cost (material + labour + tools + supervision, excluding crane) at USA average (ENR CCI 12000, 2024). Apply regional_factor and sub_region labour_factor.

Flare stack erection.unit: USD/tonne of steel erected + USD/m height adder
Flare stack erection.note: Height adder accounts for increased crane size and scaffolding above 30 m

crane_rates_USD_2024.note: Crane day rates (mobilisation amortised over typical job duration). Select crane class based on lift weight and radius.
crane_rates_USD_2024.note_selection: For stacks/flares: crane capacity ≈ 1.5 × stack section weight at maximum radius. Minimum 100t crane for stacks > 30 m.

adder_notes.freight_insurance: 2% of total activity cost. Covers small tools, consumables and material transport to site. Near-zero because this is a field-installation activity — no major equipment FOB shipment. Increase to 5% if refractory materials are imported.
adder_notes.erection_installation: 0% adder — erection IS the scope of this activity. All labour and rigging costs are captured directly in the unit rates and crane_rates above.
adder_notes.contingency: 15% applied to the sum of all cost elements. Higher than the default 10% to reflect the greater uncertainty inherent in field installation activities (weather, access, concurrent operations, scaffold dependency) versus equipment supply. Aligns with AACE Class 4 practice for field-installed scope.

regional_cost_index.note: CEPCI is an equipment fabrication index and is NOT appropriate for field-installed activities. ENR CCI tracks construction labour and material costs. Cost = base_rate × (current_ENR_CCI / 12000) × regional_labour_factor × sub_region_labour_factor. regional_labour_factor converts USA base to project region.

ASEAN.local_ref: Turner & Townsend International Construction Market Survey

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines labour_factor for field installation work. Equipment_factor and civil_factor are less relevant for pure-activity items but retained for consistency with BOQ model.

import_duty.note: Applicable to imported refractory materials or structural steel sections only. Most field activity costs are labour-dominant and do not attract import duty.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Wastage / Cutting Allowance
fields.hint: Additional material allowance for cutting, breakage and pattern matching. Typical range 5–15%. Use 5% for regular rooms, 10% for diagonal/herringbone lay, 15% for complex shapes or large-format natural stone.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Net floor area to be covered (measured flat, excluding obstructions). Wastage allowance is applied separately via the Wastage field.

fields.label: Surface Preparation Confirmed (epoxy)
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Epoxy Flooring (Industrial)' AND quantity > 0 AND surface_prep_confirmed != 'Yes'
compliance_rules.message: Epoxy flooring requires concrete substrate moisture content ≤ 5% (ASTM F2170) and minimum compressive strength of 25 MPa before application. Confirm substrate preparation scope is included.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASTM F3010 / BS 8204-6: Surface preparation and primer coat mandatory for industrial epoxy. Failure to comply leads to delamination.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'False Flooring (Raised Access)' AND quantity > 5000
compliance_rules.message: Large raised access floor installations (> 5 000 m²) should specify panel load rating (light-duty ≥ 3 kN/m², heavy-duty ≥ 12 kN/m²) and seismic restraint requirements. Confirm structural load path with structural engineer.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: BS EN 12825 / CISCA Guidelines: Load class and pedestal seismic bracing requirements for raised access flooring.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: efficiency > 15 AND equipment_subtype IN ['Ceramic Floor Tiles (300×300mm)', 'Ceramic Floor Tiles (600×600mm)', 'Porcelain Tiles (Heavy Duty)']
compliance_rules.message: Wastage allowance above 15% is unusually high for standard tile formats. Verify layout complexity or confirm non-standard room geometry justifies this allowance.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Industry benchmark: RIBA / BCIS guidance — standard rectangular rooms 5–8%, diagonal/feature layouts 10–12%, irregular rooms max 15%.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['Granite Flooring', 'Marble Flooring'] AND quantity > 0
compliance_rules.message: Natural stone flooring requires sealing after installation. Confirm stone protection (impregnating sealer) cost and periodic re-sealing maintenance are included in the project scope.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 1237 / BS 8000-11: Natural stone floors require surface treatment to prevent staining and moisture ingress in service.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Vinyl Flooring (Sheet)' AND quantity > 0
compliance_rules.message: Sheet vinyl requires a smooth, level subfloor (deviation ≤ 3 mm under 2 m straightedge). Include floor levelling compound in scope if substrate is uneven.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: BS 8203 / ASTM F1066: Subfloor flatness tolerance for resilient sheet flooring installation.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: total_cost = quantity × (1 + efficiency / 100) × base_unit_rate[equipment_subtype] × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)
cost_build_up.full_formula: total_cost = quantity × (1 + efficiency / 100) × base_unit_rate[equipment_subtype] × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + import_duty_pct / 100) × (1 + contingency)

base_unit_rates_usd_per_m2.note: USA base rates (USD/m²) inclusive of material supply only, ex-works / FOB. Installation (labour + bedding mortar / adhesive / primer) is captured in erection_installation adder. Source: RSMeans 2024, Gordian, Craftsman Cost Data.

adder_notes.freight_insurance: 4% on material supply cost. Covers inland freight from port/warehouse to site plus cargo insurance. Adjust upward for remote sites per regional inland_freight_pct factors.
adder_notes.erection_installation: 45% on material supply cost. Covers: substrate preparation (scarifying, levelling compound), bedding mortar or adhesive, grouting, tile/sheet laying labour, edge trims and movement joints, site protection during curing. Ranges from ~35% for simple large-format plain tiles to ~60% for natural stone with complex patterns.
adder_notes.contingency: 10% default. Increase to 15% for natural stone (Granite, Marble) due to material variability and specialist handling. Increase to 15–18% in remote or ASEAN Class 5 regions.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: item_type corrected E->A: unit-rate construction/installation work, field-labour dominated (matches PLASTERING / STORM-DRAIN / EARTHING library convention). ENR CCI retained, base 14250 (2024).

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-HV-SWITCHGEAR.generation_note: AUTO-GENERATED SKELETON v5 - Regional cost index matrix (item_type × region). TODOs resolved 2026-05-05. | R02 promoted 0.5-skeleton->1.0-priced after structural completion + benchmark pricing (validator-gated; no remaining blocking errors).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-HV-SWITCHGEAR.spec_example: HV Switchgear (33kV Incoming - VCB) | 33kV | 1250A | FL 25kA | IEC 62271

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: System Voltage
fields.hint: Nominal system voltage in kV. IEC standard HV levels for this template: 6.6 kV (industrial MV), 11 kV (primary distribution), 33 kV (sub-transmission). For LV items (MCC/PCC/ACB/Soft Starter) leave at 0.415 kV or use the Voltage (V) field. Directly determines insulation level, VCB vacuum interrupter rating, and panel dimensions — primary cost driver.

fields.label: Current
fields.hint: Rated continuous current (Ir) of the VCB or switchgear panel busbar. IEC 62271 standard frames: 200 A, 400 A, 630 A, 800 A, 1250 A, 2500 A, 3150 A. For motor feeders use full-load current of the largest motor plus 25% diversity. Incomer/bus-coupler ratings typically match transformer secondary FLC. Major cost driver — drives VCB interrupter size and busbar cross-section.

fields.label: Fault Level
fields.hint: Prospective symmetrical short-circuit current (Isc) at the switchgear busbars (kA rms). Typical values: 25 kA (11 kV distribution networks), 40 kA (large industrial 11 kV), 50 kA (utility-connected 11 kV or 6.6 kV), 25 kA (33 kV sub-transmission). Must match or exceed the network Isc study result. Drives VCB breaking capacity, switchgear panel bracing, and busbar size — major cost driver.

fields.label: Rated Power / Output
fields.hint: Rated output power or compensation capacity relevant to the selected sub-type. Use cases: VFD — rated motor power in kW (e.g. 2500 kW); Soft Starter — rated motor power in kW (e.g. 250 kW, 315 kW); PFC Panel — reactive power compensation in kvar (e.g. 7% detuned filter bank capacity). Leave blank for plain VCB panels, incomers, bus couplers, MCC, PCC, and ACB items where motor_power or panel capacity is the primary driver.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Variable Frequency Drive (VFD) 11kV 2500kW' || equipment_subtype === 'Soft Starter 415V 250kW' || equipment_subtype === 'Soft Starter Panel' || equipment_subtype === 'HV Switchgear (PFC Panel - 6.6kV)'

fields.label: Voltage (LV)
fields.hint: Nominal LV supply voltage in volts. Applicable only to LV items in this template: MCC 415V TPN, PCC 415V, ACB 415V 2500A, Soft Starter 415V 250kW, and Soft Starter Panel. Standard IEC value is 415 V (3-phase, 50 Hz). For HV switchgear (VCB panels, incomers, feeders) this field is not required — use System Voltage (kV) instead.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Motor Control Centre (MCC) 415V TPN' || equipment_subtype === 'Power Control Centre (PCC) 415V' || equipment_subtype === 'Air Circuit Breaker (ACB) 415V 2500A' || equipment_subtype === 'Soft Starter 415V 250kW' || equipment_subtype === 'Soft Starter Panel'

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Shaft output power of the driven motor in kW. Applicable to VFD and Soft Starter items only. Standard sizes: 250 kW, 315 kW, 400 kW, 500 kW, 630 kW, 800 kW, 1000 kW, 1250 kW, 1600 kW, 2000 kW, 2500 kW. For 11 kV VFD, power rating is the primary cost driver (drives converter topology, transformer rating, and harmonic filter size). For 415 V soft starters, drives thyristor stack and bypass contactor rating.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Variable Frequency Drive (VFD) 11kV 2500kW' || equipment_subtype === 'Soft Starter 415V 250kW' || equipment_subtype === 'Soft Starter Panel'

fields.label: Standard
fields.hint: Governing IEC standard. IEC 62271: High-voltage switchgear and controlgear — applies to all VCB panels, incomers, feeders, bus couplers, MCC, PCC, ACB (IEC 62271-200 for metal-enclosed, IEC 62271-100 for VCBs). IEC 60871: Shunt capacitors for AC power systems — applies to PFC Panel - 6.6kV items only. Auto-suggested based on equipment sub-type; update if project-specific derogation applies.

fields.label: Drive Type
fields.hint: Motor starting and speed control method. VFD (Variable Frequency Drive): full variable-speed control, ~2.5–3× cost of soft starter for equivalent kW; use for pumps/fans with >20% speed variation or energy-saving requirement. Soft Starter: reduced-voltage starting only, no speed control; cost-effective for DOL alternatives on fixed-speed motors. Required for VFD and Soft Starter sub-types — not applicable to switchgear panels, MCC, PCC, or ACB items.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Variable Frequency Drive (VFD) 11kV 2500kW' || equipment_subtype === 'Soft Starter 415V 250kW' || equipment_subtype === 'Soft Starter Panel'

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: IEC 60529 ingress protection rating of the switchgear enclosure. IP20: indoor, clean, air-conditioned electrical rooms only — lowest cost. IP42: standard indoor switchroom (dust-protected, drip-proof) — most common for MV switchgear and MCC/PCC in industrial plants. IP52: dust-protected with splash-proof protection — for semi-exposed plant rooms or locations near washdown areas. Higher IP ratings add 5–15% to enclosure fabrication cost.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: system_voltage > 1.0 AND standard != 'IEC 62271'
compliance_rules.message: HV switchgear operating above 1 kV must comply with IEC 62271 (High-voltage switchgear and controlgear). Update the Standard field.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 62271-1:2017 Cl. 1 — applies to all AC switchgear and controlgear for rated voltages above 1 kV. IEC 62271-200 covers metal-enclosed switchgear; IEC 62271-100 covers alternating-current circuit-breakers.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: system_voltage > 1.0 AND system_voltage < 6.6
compliance_rules.message: System voltage is in the range 1–6.6 kV. Verify this is intentional — IEC standard MV levels are 3.3 kV, 6.6 kV, 11 kV, or 33 kV. Non-standard voltages may restrict manufacturer options and increase cost.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60038:2009 Table 3 — standard voltages above 1 kV and up to and including 35 kV. Deviating from standard nominal voltages incurs custom transformer and switchgear fabrication premiums.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: fault_level > 40 AND system_voltage == 33.0
compliance_rules.message: Fault level exceeds 40 kA at 33 kV. Maximum standard IEC 62271 type-tested breaking capacity at 33 kV is 40 kA for most manufacturers. Verify network Isc study and confirm availability of 50 kA rated 33 kV VCBs with project engineer.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 62271-100:2021 Table 1 — preferred rated short-circuit breaking currents at 36 kV: 25 kA, 31.5 kA, 40 kA. 50 kA is non-standard at 36 kV and requires special order.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'HV Switchgear (PFC Panel - 6.6kV)' AND standard != 'IEC 60871'
compliance_rules.message: PFC capacitor bank panels should reference IEC 60871 (shunt capacitors for AC power systems above 1 kV). Update the Standard field.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60871-1:2014 — shunt capacitors for AC power systems having a rated voltage above 1 kV. Capacitor units within the bank also subject to IEC 60871-2 for internal fusing.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'VFD' IN equipment_subtype AND motor_power > 2500
compliance_rules.message: Motor power exceeds 2500 kW for a VFD. Above this threshold, multi-level converter topologies (NPC, CHB) and harmonic filter studies are mandatory. Confirm topology with VFD manufacturer and update cost estimate accordingly.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61800-3:2017 — adjustable speed electrical power drive systems; EMC requirements. IEEE Std 519-2022 — harmonic limits at PCC; studies required for drives >500 kW.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: enclosure == 'IP20' AND (system_voltage >= 6.6 OR 'MCC' IN equipment_subtype OR 'PCC' IN equipment_subtype)
compliance_rules.message: IP20 enclosure selected for switchgear or MCC/PCC. Verify that the installation area is a fully controlled indoor electrical room with no risk of dust ingress or drip. Most industrial switchgear specifications require a minimum of IP42.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60529:2013 — degrees of protection provided by enclosures. IEC 62271-200 Cl. 7.101 — standard internal arc classification and enclosure requirements for MV switchgear; IP2X minimum for accessible parts.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype, system_voltage, current, fault_level) × quantity × current_adder_factor × fault_level_adder_factor × enclosure_adder_factor × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype, system_voltage, current, fault_level) × quantity × current_adder_factor × fault_level_adder_factor × motor_power_factor × drive_type_factor × enclosure_adder_factor × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (1 + import_duty_pct/100) × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

base_unit_rates_usd.note: USD supply-only ex-works reference rates at ENR CCI 14250 (2024 base). Scale by regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor for landed cost. Rates assume IP42 enclosure, standard fault level, and IEC 62271 type-tested panels.

HV Switchgear (33kV Incoming - VCB).basis: 36 kV class, 1250 A, 25 kA, metal-enclosed withdrawable VCB panel with protection relay (REF615 class), metering CT/VT, busbar. Market ref: ABB SafeRing/UniGear / Schneider PIX / Siemens NXAIR 2024.

HV Switchgear (33kV Feeder - VCB).basis: 36 kV class, 630 A, 25 kA, withdrawable VCB feeder panel, IDMT protection relay, CT. No metering VT.

HV Switchgear (11kV Incoming - VCB).basis: 12 kV class, 1250 A, 40 kA, metal-clad withdrawable VCB incomer panel, numerical protection relay, metering CT/VT, busbar section.

HV Switchgear (11kV Feeder - VCB).basis: 12 kV class, 630 A, 40 kA, withdrawable VCB feeder panel, overcurrent/earth fault protection relay.

HV Switchgear (11kV Bus Coupler - VCB).basis: 12 kV class, 1250 A, 40 kA, withdrawable VCB bus section/coupler panel with tie protection and interlock scheme.

HV Switchgear (6.6kV Motor Feeder - VCB).basis: 7.2 kV class, 200–630 A, 40 kA, withdrawable VCB motor feeder with motor protection relay (thermal overload, differential, undercurrent).

HV Switchgear (6.6kV Incomer - VCB).basis: 7.2 kV class, 1250 A, 50 kA, withdrawable VCB incomer panel with protection and metering.

HV Switchgear (6.6kV Bus Coupler - VCB).basis: 7.2 kV class, 1250 A, 50 kA, withdrawable VCB bus coupler panel with interlock.

HV Switchgear (PFC Panel - 6.6kV).basis: 6.6 kV PFC capacitor bank panel, 7% detuned reactor filter, 1 Mvar stepped, capacitor contactors, protection relay. Market ref: 2024 supply price for complete factory-assembled panel.

11kV Vacuum Circuit Breaker (VCB) Panel.basis: 12 kV class standalone VCB panel (fixed or withdrawable), 630 A, 40 kA, numerical protection relay, suitable for retrofit/extension bays.

11kV Switchgear Panel with Metering (Inc.basis: 12 kV class incomer panel with revenue-grade metering CT/VT, energy meter, SCADA interface, numerical protection relay, 1250 A, 40 kA.

Motor Control Centre (MCC) 415V TPN.basis: 415 V TPN MCC cubicle, IEC 61439 Form 3 or 4, MCCB incomer + 12–16 DOL/Y-D outgoing starters, motor protection relays. Market ref: ABB MNS / Schneider Okken MCC 2024.

Power Control Centre (PCC) 415V.basis: 415 V PCC (main distribution board to MCC/DB), IEC 61439 type-tested, ACB/MCCB incomer 2500–4000 A, 8–12 MCCB outgoing feeders to MCCs, power monitoring.

Air Circuit Breaker (ACB) 415V 2500A.basis: 415 V 2500 A draw-out ACB, 65 kA breaking capacity, electronic trip unit, communications (Modbus). Market ref: ABB Emax2 / Schneider Masterpact MTZ 2024.

Variable Frequency Drive (VFD) 11kV 2500.basis: 11 kV medium-voltage VFD, 2500 kW, CHB or NPC topology, input isolation transformer, bypass contactor, harmonic filter. Market ref: ABB ACS6000 / Siemens SINAMICS GM150 / Rockwell PowerFlex 6000 2024. Highly motor_power dependent — scale with power_factor.

Soft Starter 415V 250kW.basis: 415 V 250 kW thyristor soft starter, bypass contactor, electronic overload, standard enclosure. Market ref: ABB PSR/PSTX / Schneider Altistart / Siemens 3RW 2024.

Soft Starter Panel.basis: 415 V soft starter supplied in free-standing panel with incomer MCCB, HRC fuses, bypass contactor, control wiring, and door-mounted display. Assumed 315 kW. Scale with rated_power_output.

current_adder_factor.note: Multiplier on base rate for VCB panel current rating. Base = 630 A for feeders / 1250 A for incomers.

fault_level_adder_factor.note: Applied to all switchgear panel types. Higher breaking capacity requires heavier VCB interrupters and busbar bracing.

motor_power_factor.note: For VFD and Soft Starter items. Cost scales approximately as (kW/reference_kW)^0.65 (six-tenths rule for power electronics). Reference: VFD 2500 kW, Soft Starter 250 kW.

drive_type_factor.note: Cost multiplier relative to a Soft Starter baseline at equivalent kW. VFD is 2.5–3× a soft starter due to converter topology and input transformer.

enclosure_adder_factor.note: Adder to base panel cost for higher IP ratings.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: Switched ENR->CEPCI: procured/manufactured equipment, supply-cost dominated. verify=true

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.
