SCHEMA-HVAC-EQUIP.generation_note: AUTO-GENERATED SKELETON v5 + engineering fill (v1.0). All TODOs resolved; F-009 relabelled from 'Volume (m³)' to 'Air Volume Flow (m³/hr)' and spec_key corrected to avoid collision with F-006. Voltage default corrected to 415 V (3-phase industrial India). Standards list expanded (ASME I / VIII / EN 12952 / IBR 1950 / IS 1228). Fuel type options expanded. 6 compliance rules added covering IBR 1950, refrigerant phase-out, Legionella, cleanroom MOC, voltage mismatch, and diesel opex advisory. | R02 promoted 0.6-engineered->1.0-priced after structural completion + benchmark pricing (validator-gated; no remaining blocking errors). [note synced to v1.0-priced release]

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% (sub-type, motor power / air flow, MOC, quantity). Tier 2 = Budget +-20% (add area, design P/T, efficiency, voltage). Tier 3 = Detailed +-10% (add standard, fuel type, volume, current — vendor quote required).

SCHEMA-HVAC-EQUIP.spec_example: Shell & Tube Heat Exchanger (HVAC) | Area: 22 m² | DP: 10 barg | DT: 37°C | MOC: SS316L | Qty: 1 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Rated motor power of the largest drive on this equipment unit (compressor, fan, or pump motor). Typical values: FCU 0.18–0.75 kW, AHU small 1.5–5.5 kW, AHU large 11–75 kW, Air-cooled chiller 55–500 kW, Water-cooled chiller 75–1 400 kW. Not applicable for Shell & Tube HX or Plate HX (leave blank).

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure on the pressure-containing side. Typical HVAC range: 10 bar(g) for steam boilers (low-pressure), 10–16 bar(g) for shell & tube HX hot-side. Applies to: Shell & Tube HX, Plate HX, Steam Boiler. Leave blank for chillers, AHUs, FCUs, cooling towers.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum design temperature on the process / steam side. Reference points: 7°C = chilled water supply (chiller evaporator), 12°C = chilled water return, 28–37°C = condenser water range, ~184°C = saturated steam at 10 bar(g). Applies to: Shell & Tube HX, Plate HX, Steam Boiler. Leave blank for air-side equipment (AHU, FCU, cooling tower, split AC).

fields.label: Area
fields.hint: Effective heat transfer area (tube-side for shell & tube; plate area for PHE). This is the primary sizing parameter for HX/PHE cost build-up (cost ∝ area^0.65 correlation). Typical HVAC range: 5–200 m². Applies to: Shell & Tube HX (HVAC), Plate HX (HVAC). Leave blank for all other sub-types.

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Water-side volume of the pressure vessel shell (fire-tube boiler) or capacity of associated expansion / buffer vessel. Single library data point: 316 L (small fire-tube boiler). Typical range for fire-tube boilers: 200–5 000 L. Applies to: Steam Boiler (Fire Tube). Leave blank for all other sub-types.

fields.label: Efficiency
fields.hint: Thermal or refrigeration efficiency of the equipment unit. For steam boilers: gross thermal efficiency (NCV basis), typical range 82–95% (fire-tube), up to 97% (condensing). For chillers: use COP or EER field instead if available; this field may represent isentropic efficiency of the compressor. Higher efficiency specifications attract a cost premium of 5–20%. Applies primarily to: Steam Boiler (Fire Tube).

fields.label: Flow Rate
fields.hint: Chilled water or condenser water flow rate through the equipment. Typical values: Water-cooled chiller 300–3 400 m³/hr, Cooling Tower 520–13 600 m³/hr (recirculation rate). For Shell & Tube HX: tube-side water flow rate. Not applicable for air-side equipment (AHU, FCU, split AC) — use Air Volume Flow (F-009) for those. Applies to: Water-Cooled Chiller, Cooling Tower, Shell & Tube HX, Plate HX.

fields.label: Air Volume Flow
fields.hint: Rated air volume flow through the equipment (supply air side). Typical values: FCU 300–1 200 m³/hr, AHU small 1 000–5 000 m³/hr, AHU medium 5 000–20 000 m³/hr, AHU large 20 000–100 000 m³/hr, Cooling Tower (air side) up to 500 000 m³/hr. Note: library data (300–13 600 m³/hr) corresponds to AHU medium–large range. Not applicable for water-side-only equipment (chillers, HX, boilers). Applies to: AHU (all sizes), FCU, Cooling Tower, Split AC.

fields.label: Current
fields.hint: Rated full-load current (FLC) of the equipment. Used for MCC/switchgear sizing and cable selection — not an independent cost driver (derived from motor kW and supply voltage). Typical values: AHU/chiller small ~134 A, large chiller ~410 A. Verify against vendor data sheet before electrical panel design.

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: Supply voltage for the equipment. Standard industrial supply in India: 415 V, 3-phase, 50 Hz. Single-phase 230 V applies only to small FCUs and split AC units (< 5 kW). WARNING: The library contains only 230 V — verify that large equipment (AHU, chiller) is not incorrectly assigned single-phase supply. US standard: 208 V single-phase or 480 V, 3-phase, 60 Hz.

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of construction for the pressure-containing or wetted parts (casing, tube bundle, plate pack, or boiler shell). CS = Carbon Steel (IS 2062 / ASTM A36) — standard for cooling water service. SS304 = Austenitic stainless, food-grade / mild corrosion service. SS316L = Low-carbon austenitic stainless, chloride-containing or chemical service. FRP = Fibre-reinforced plastic — cooling tower basin/casing (non-metallic). MOC shift from CS → SS316L adds approximately 2.5–4× material cost for HX. Applies to: Shell & Tube HX, Plate HX, Cooling Tower. Leave blank for equipment where casing MOC is fixed by design (AHU, chiller).

fields.label: Standard
fields.hint: Design / manufacturing code for pressure-containing equipment. ASME I: Power Boilers (steam boilers > 15 psig). ASME VIII Div.1: Pressure Vessels (HX, receivers). EN 12952: European Water-tube Boilers standard. IBR 1950: Indian Boilers Regulation — statutory requirement for all steam boilers in India regardless of ASME certification. IS 1228: Indian standard for steel boilers. Note: In India, IBR 1950 compliance is mandatory for all steam-generating equipment regardless of which design code is used for fabrication. Applies to: Steam Boiler (Fire Tube), Shell & Tube HX (HVAC). Leave blank for AHU, FCU, chiller, cooling tower, split AC.

fields.label: Fuel Type
fields.hint: Primary fuel type for the boiler burner system. Diesel: pressure-jet burner, onsite tank storage required (IS 1447 tank design). Natural gas: premix/nozzle-mix burner, PNGRB-regulated supply required. LPG: cylinder bank or bulk tank (PESO approval required in India). Dual fuel: higher burner cost (~15% premium) but operational flexibility. Biomass: stoker/travelling grate, significantly higher capital cost. Fuel type affects burner system cost (~10–25% of boiler package), flue gas train, and O&M provisions. Applies to: Steam Boiler (Fire Tube) only. Leave blank for all other sub-types.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Steam Boiler (Fire Tube)' AND standard != 'IBR 1950'
compliance_rules.message: Steam boilers in India are subject to the Indian Boilers Regulation (IBR) 1950 under the Boilers Act 1923, regardless of the fabrication design code (ASME I, IS 1228). IBR registration, third-party inspection by a competent person, and annual inspection certificate are mandatory. Ensure IBR 1950 compliance is explicitly in the equipment scope.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Indian Boilers Regulation 1950 (IBR) under the Boilers Act 1923; ASME BPVC Section I: Power Boilers; IS 1228: Steel Boilers

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Water Cooled Chiller' OR equipment_subtype == 'Air Cooled Chiller') AND motor_power > 200
compliance_rules.message: Large chiller (motor power > 200 kW) selected. Confirm refrigerant type: HCFCs (R-22) are banned for new equipment in India from 2030 (ODS Rules 2014). Specify HFC (R-134a, R-410A) or low-GWP HFO (R-1234ze, R-1233zd) refrigerant. For water-cooled chillers > 200 kW, also confirm cooling tower water chemistry and Legionella risk management plan per ASHRAE Guideline 12.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Environment Protection (ODS) Rules 2014 (India); Montreal Protocol: HCFC phase-out schedule; ASHRAE Guideline 12-2000: Minimising the Risk of Legionellosis

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Cooling Tower (Induced Draft)'
compliance_rules.message: Cooling towers are a high-risk environment for Legionella pneumophila growth (warm, aerosolised water, 25–45°C). A mandatory Water Safety Plan (WSP) including biocide dosing, drift eliminator specification (< 0.001% drift loss), and quarterly microbiological sampling is required. Ensure HVAC designer includes chemical water treatment package and blowdown system in scope.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASHRAE Guideline 12-2000: Minimising the Risk of Legionellosis; BS 8580-1:2019: Water Quality Risk Assessment for Legionella; IS 11329: Cooling Water Treatment for Industrial Systems

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Air Handling Unit (Large)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Air Handling Unit (Medium)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Air Handling Unit (Small)') AND moc == 'SS316L'
compliance_rules.message: SS316L MOC selected for AHU casing. Standard AHU casings are galvanised steel or aluminium. SS316L is warranted only for pharmaceutical cleanroom (ISO 14644), food processing (GMP), or highly corrosive ambient environments. Verify that the premium (~2.5–3× casing cost) is justified by the application. If clean-room rated, also confirm HEPA filter bank (H13/H14 per EN 1822) and face velocity ≤ 2.5 m/s are specified.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 14644-1: Cleanrooms and Associated Controlled Environments; EN 1822: High Efficiency Air Filters (EPA, HEPA, ULPA); ASHRAE 52.2: Method of Testing General Ventilation Air-Cleaning Devices

compliance_rules.condition_expr: voltage == 230 AND motor_power > 5
compliance_rules.message: Voltage is set to 230 V (single-phase) but motor power exceeds 5 kW. Single-phase motors above 5 kW are non-standard and operationally problematic (high starting current, poor power factor). For equipment > 5 kW in India, supply should be 415 V, 3-phase, 50 Hz (IS 12360). Verify voltage specification against vendor data sheet and electrical SLD.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 12360: Voltage Bands for Electrical Installations; IS 900: Code of Practice for Installation and Maintenance of Induction Motors; IEC 60034-1: Rotating Electrical Machines

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Steam Boiler (Fire Tube)' AND fuel_type == 'diesel'
compliance_rules.message: Diesel-fired boiler selected. For long-term opex optimisation, evaluate natural gas or dual-fuel option if PNGRB-regulated pipeline supply is available at the project location. Diesel typically costs 2.5–3× natural gas on a per-GJ basis in India. Ensure HSD storage tank (IS 1447), bund wall, and fire suppression system are included in the civil scope per OISD-117.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: OISD Standard 117: Fire Protection Facilities for Petroleum Depots, Terminals; IS 1447: Code of Practice for Storage and Handling of Petroleum Products; PNGRB Act 2006: Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board (pipeline access)

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: quantity × base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype) × capacity_factor(motor_power, air_volume_flow) × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: quantity × base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype) × capacity_factor(motor_power, air_volume_flow, area_m2) × moc_factor(moc) × design_condition_factor(design_pressure, design_temperature) × efficiency_premium_factor(efficiency) × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: (quantity × base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype) × capacity_factor(motor_power, air_volume_flow, area_m2, volume_L) × moc_factor(moc) × design_condition_factor(design_pressure, design_temperature) × efficiency_premium_factor(efficiency) × standard_compliance_factor(standard) × fuel_system_factor(fuel_type) × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)) × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (current_CEPCI / base_CEPCI)

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

fields.label: Quantity

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Heat exchanger – supply' AND quantity > 1
compliance_rules.message: Multiple heat exchangers selected as a single supply line item. Verify that all units share identical TEMA class, design pressure, design temperature, shell/tube MOC, and heat duty. If specifications differ, split into separate line items for accurate cost build-up.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: TEMA (Tubular Exchanger Manufacturers Association) Standards 10th Ed. – Section 1: Class R/C/B designation; ASME BPVC Section VIII Div.1: Pressure Vessel Design

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Heat exchanger – erection' AND quantity > 0
compliance_rules.message: Erection activity requires confirmed exchanger dry weight (kg) and overall dimensions before rigging plan can be costed. Ensure certified rigging study, crane selection (rated capacity ≥ 1.25 × exchanger weight), and nozzle load certificate from vendor are in scope.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 686 (PIP REIE686): Recommended Practice for Machinery Installation and Installation Design; ASME B30.9: Slings; IS 3696 Part 1: Safety Code for Scaffolds and Ladders

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Heat exchanger tube cleaning'
compliance_rules.message: Tube cleaning activity: confirm cleaning method (hydrojetting, mechanical rodding, chemical circulation, or foam ball). Hydrojetting at >700 bar requires confined-space PTW and high-pressure safety plan per OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 (LOTO). Chemical cleaning requires neutralisation and effluent disposal route.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147: Control of Hazardous Energy (LOTO); TEMA Section 9: Cleaning and Inspection of Heat Exchangers; API 510: Pressure Vessel Inspection Code

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Heat exchanger tube bundle insertion'
compliance_rules.message: Bundle insertion requires confirmed bundle weight, shell bore clearance (min 3 mm diametral), and bundle pull-out force calculation. Hydraulic bundle extractor must be rated for ≥ 1.5 × calculated pull-out force. Include gasket replacement (shell-side and channel covers) in scope.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: TEMA Section 9: Bundle Extraction; API 660: Shell-and-Tube Heat Exchangers for General Refinery Services (Cl. 8.3 – Bundle Removal); ASME BPVC Section VIII: Flange and Gasket Reassembly torque requirements

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Heat exchanger – supply'
compliance_rules.message: For budget-class estimate: confirm TEMA class (R/C/B), shell-and-tube or plate type, design pressure (barg), design temperature (°C), shell MOC, tube MOC, heat transfer area (m²), and number of passes. Each variable can shift cost by 20–100%. Obtain vendor budgetary quote for Class 3 estimate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: AACE International Recommended Practice 18R-97: Cost Estimate Classification System; API 660: General Refinery HX specification checklist

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: quantity × base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) × heat_transfer_area_factor(area_m2) × material_grade_factor(shell_moc, tube_moc) × regional_factor × sub_region_labour_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: (quantity × base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) × heat_transfer_area_factor(area_m2) × material_grade_factor(shell_moc, tube_moc) × pressure_temperature_factor(design_pressure_barg, design_temp_c) × tema_class_factor(tema_class) × tube_pass_factor(no_of_passes) × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)) × regional_factor × sub_region_labour_factor × (current_cci / base_cci)

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

NOTE: base_unit_cost figures are order-of-magnitude ROM (±30%) for USA list prices in USD (2024 basis). Adjust with regional_factor × equipment_factor × import_duty as documented.

SCHEMA-INST-CTRL-VALVE.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | {standard} | MOC: {moc} | Encl: {enclosure} | P_design: {design_pressure} bar(g){voltage_clause} | Qty: {quantity} No.

spec_formula_notes.voltage_clause: Appended as ' | V: {voltage} V' only when equipment_subtype is 'Solenoid Valve 24VDC ½" SS' or 'Motorised Actuator (Quarter Turn)'. Omit entirely for pneumatic subtypes.
spec_formula_notes.example_pneumatic: Control Valve Globe Type Pneumatic 2" 300# | IEC 60534 / ANSI ISA-75 | MOC: SS316 | Encl: IP67 | P_design: 30 bar(g) | Qty: 1 No.
spec_formula_notes.example_solenoid: Solenoid Valve 24VDC ½" SS | IEC 61000 (EMC - Solenoid) | MOC: SS316 | Encl: IP67 | P_design: 10 bar(g) | V: 24 V | Qty: 2 No.
spec_formula_notes.example_motorised: Motorised Actuator (Quarter Turn) | IEC 62061 / IEC 61511 (SIL) | MOC: SS316 | Encl: IP67 | P_design: 50 bar(g) | V: 415 V | Qty: 1 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.default_note: 30 bar(g) chosen to be consistent with the default subtype (Globe 2" 300#). ASME Class 300# MAWP at 38°C for WCB/SS316 is approximately 51 bar(g); 30 bar(g) is a representative mid-range value within that class. Previous default of 10 bar(g) was inconsistent (falls in ASME 150# range).
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure (MAWP) the valve body must contain. Determines pressure class selection: ASME 150# ≈ up to 20 bar(g), 300# up to 51 bar(g), 600# up to 102 bar(g), 900# up to 153 bar(g). Drives body wall thickness and cost significantly. Typical range: 2–250 bar(g).

fields.label: Voltage
fields.mandatory_note: Mandatory when equipment_subtype is Solenoid Valve or Motorised Actuator (enforced by compliance rules R-002 and R-003). Not applicable to pneumatic subtypes — leave blank.
fields.hint: Supply voltage for the actuator or solenoid. Solenoid valves: typically 24 VDC or 110/240 VAC. Motorised actuators: typically 240 VAC (single-phase) or 415 VAC (three-phase). Not applicable to pneumatic globe/butterfly control valves — leave blank unless an electric positioner is specified.

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of construction for valve body and trim wetted parts. SS316 is the standard for most process applications. Carbon steel WCB suits non-corrosive hydrocarbons. Duplex SS or Hastelloy C-276 required for chloride-containing, high-H₂S, or highly corrosive services — these carry a significant cost premium (1.5× to 3× over SS316).

fields.label: Standard
fields.hint: Governing design and test standard. IEC 60534 / ANSI ISA-75 applies to modulating control valves (globe, butterfly). API 6D applies to pipeline ball valves and ESDVs in pipeline service. API 6FA additionally mandates fire-safe testing — required for ESDVs in hydrocarbon service. IEC 61000 applies to solenoid valves for EMC. Select IEC 62061 / IEC 61511 if the valve is part of a Safety Instrumented Function (SIF) with a defined SIL target.

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Ingress Protection (IEC 60529) rating for the actuator or positioner enclosure. Applies to motorised actuators, solenoid valves, and smart positioners. IP67 is the standard industrial outdoor rating. IP68 required for permanently submerged or deep wash-down applications. Not applicable to bare pneumatic actuators — leave blank.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Emergency Shutdown Valve (ESDV) 4" 600# Ball' AND NOT standard IN ['API 6D', 'API 6FA (Fire Safe)', 'IEC 62061 / IEC 61511 (SIL)']
compliance_rules.message: ESDVs must comply with API 6D as a minimum. For hydrocarbon service, API 6FA (fire-safe) is mandatory. Please update the Standard field.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 6D (Pipeline Valves) and API 6FA (Fire Test for Valves): mandatory for ESDVs on hydrocarbon pipelines and process plants per IEC 61511 SIS requirements.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Solenoid Valve 24VDC ½" SS' AND (voltage IS None OR voltage IS None)
compliance_rules.message: Supply voltage is mandatory for solenoid valves. Please enter the voltage (e.g. 24 VDC or 240 VAC).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61000 / vendor datasheets: solenoid coil sizing is voltage-specific; incorrect voltage causes coil burnout or non-actuation.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Motorised Actuator (Quarter Turn)' AND (voltage IS None OR voltage IS None)
compliance_rules.message: Supply voltage is mandatory for motorised actuators. Please enter the voltage (e.g. 240 VAC or 415 VAC).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60034 (Rotating Electrical Machines): motor actuator sizing and MCC protection coordination require confirmed supply voltage.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 100 AND equipment_subtype == 'Control Valve Rotary (Butterfly) 6" 150#'
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds 100 bar(g), which is beyond the typical ASME 150# rating for butterfly valves. Consider upgrading to a higher pressure class or a globe-type valve.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.34 (Valves — Flanged, Threaded and Welding End): Class 150# flanged butterfly valve MAWP at 38°C is approximately 19.6 bar(g) for carbon steel. Verify pressure class against MAWP table.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc == 'CS (Carbon Steel WCB)' AND design_pressure > 150
compliance_rules.message: Carbon steel WCB above 150 bar(g) approaches its elevated-temperature pressure-temperature rating limit. Verify P-T ratings per ASME B16.34 Table 2-1.1 for the design temperature.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.34: WCB pressure-temperature ratings decrease significantly above 200°C; confirm suitability at actual design temperature and pressure class.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc == 'Hastelloy C-276' AND equipment_subtype == 'Motorised Actuator (Quarter Turn)'
compliance_rules.message: Hastelloy C-276 is selected for a Motorised Actuator. Note that Hastelloy typically applies to wetted body/trim only — confirm whether the actuator housing and yoke also require exotic alloy (rare, significant cost uplift) or if SS316 actuator with Hastelloy body/trim is the correct interpretation.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Engineering practice: actuator bodies are typically carbon steel or aluminium regardless of wetted-trim MOC. Hastelloy actuator housings are non-standard and require custom fabrication. Validate scope boundary with process and materials engineer.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Control Valve Rotary (Butterfly) 6" 150#' AND design_pressure > 19.6
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds the ASME Class 150# MAWP of approximately 19.6 bar(g) at 38°C for this butterfly valve. Verify the selected pressure class matches the design pressure — a Class 300# or higher body may be required.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.34 Table 2-1.1: Class 150 WCB/SS316 MAWP = 19.6 bar(g) at 38°C. Operating above this rating without upgrading the pressure class is a code violation.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: standard == 'IEC 62061 / IEC 61511 (SIL)' AND equipment_subtype != 'Emergency Shutdown Valve (ESDV) 4" 600# Ball'
compliance_rules.message: SIL standard selected for a non-ESDV item. Ensure a formal SIL determination (Layer of Protection Analysis / HAZOP) has been completed and the SIL target is documented in the Safety Requirements Specification (SRS).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61511-1 Clause 8: SIL assignment must be based on a recognised hazard analysis method. Ad-hoc SIL selection without a backing SRS is non-compliant.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype) × moc_factor(moc) × pressure_class_factor(design_pressure) × standard_factor(standard) × enclosure_factor(enclosure) × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype) × moc_factor(moc) × pressure_class_factor(design_pressure) × standard_factor(standard) × enclosure_factor(enclosure) × voltage_factor(voltage, equipment_subtype) × quantity

base_unit_cost_usd.basis_note: Order-of-magnitude ROM list prices (±30%) for mid-range branded equipment (Emerson Fisher / Flowserve / Rotork / IMI Norgren equivalent). Prices are per-unit, supply-only, EXW factory. Apply regional_factor × equipment_factor × import_duty adders for landed project cost. Pressure class embedded in subtype label — price reflects stated class. DN/class upgrade not modelled at this tier; use Tier 3 expansion when available.

Control Valve Globe Type Pneumatic 2\" 3.engineering_basis: 2" Class 300 globe-body pneumatic control valve with smart positioner (HART). Fisher ED / Flowserve Valtek equivalent. Includes valve body, bonnet, trim, pneumatic actuator spring-return, positioner, handwheel. Excludes process isolation valves and instrument air supply.

Control Valve Rotary (Butterfly) 6\" 150.engineering_basis: 6" Class 150 high-performance double-eccentric butterfly valve with pneumatic rack-and-pinion actuator and smart positioner. Fisher V250 / Flowserve DRCX equivalent. Lower cost than globe due to simpler body casting; larger bore compensates. Includes positioner and limit switches.

Emergency Shutdown Valve (ESDV) 4\" 600#.engineering_basis: 4" Class 600 full-bore trunnion ball valve with double-acting pneumatic actuator, solenoid pilot valve, manual override, limit switches, and partial-stroke test device. API 6FA fire-tested. Cameron / Velan equivalent. High cost reflects heavy-wall Class 600 forged body, ATEX actuator, SIL-capable accessories. Excludes hydraulic power unit if applicable.

Motorised Actuator (Quarter Turn).engineering_basis: Quarter-turn electric actuator only (no valve body). Rotork IQ3 / Biffi ICON equivalent for DN100–DN200 range. Includes integral controls, local panel, HART/Modbus, 240/415 VAC motor. Supply-only cost. Note: Actuator is priced standalone — if retrofitting to an existing valve, no valve body cost applies. If supplied as a package with a new valve, add valve body cost separately.
Motorised Actuator (Quarter Turn).standalone_actuator_note: This subtype represents the actuator only. It has no valve body base cost included. If the actuator is being ordered as a mounted package with a new ball or butterfly valve, add the relevant valve body cost line separately in the BOQ.

Solenoid Valve 24VDC ½\" SS.engineering_basis: ½" two-way or three-way solenoid valve, SS316 body, PTFE seat, DIN coil, 24 VDC. ASCO / Parker Hannifin equivalent. Low cost reflects small bore and commodity item status. Includes coil, manual override, and DIN plug. ATEX-rated variant adds ~35% premium (not modelled here — treat as minor scope item).

pressure_class_factor.note: Applied as step function: map design_pressure (bar(g)) to ASME class, then apply factor. Thresholds based on ASME B16.34 Table 2-1.1 WCB MAWP at 38°C.

voltage_factor.note: Applies only to Solenoid Valve and Motorised Actuator subtypes. For all other subtypes (pneumatic), set voltage_factor = 1.0. Factor accounts for coil/motor winding differences, terminal box upgrades, and cable gland specifications at higher voltages.

12_to_50_VDC_VAC.note: 24 VDC nominal (typical solenoid). Standard coil, low-voltage safe, no special terminal box required.

51_to_130_VAC.note: 110 VAC nominal. Minor uplift for insulated coil winding and compliant terminal shrouding.

131_to_260_VAC.note: 240 VAC nominal (single-phase motorised actuators). Standard industrial voltage — moderate uplift for motor winding, isolator, and MCC interface.

261_to_450_VAC.note: 415 VAC nominal (three-phase motorised actuators). Three-phase motor, star-delta or VSD interface, uprated terminal box and cable entries. Most common for large quarter-turn actuators.

451_to_690_VAC.note: 690 VAC — high-voltage industrial motor. Requires specialist motor winding, uprated insulation class (Class F/H), special MCC protection. Uncommon for valve actuators; verify necessity with electrical engineer.

freight_insurance.note: 5% of supply cost. Covers sea/air freight and marine insurance for imported equipment. Adjust for local procurement — reduce to 0.01–0.02 for locally sourced items.

erection_installation.note: 25% of supply cost. Covers mechanical installation (bolting to line), pneumatic/electrical hook-up, loop check, and SIL FAT (if applicable). Higher than typical pipe/vessel installation due to instrument loop commissioning effort. Apply regional labour_factor on top of this adder for non-USA regions.

contingency.note: 10% contingency for Tier 1/2 estimates. Increase to 15–18% for ASEAN/China/GCC regions per regional_factors.contingency_pct. Reduce to 5% at Tier 3 (detailed) stage.

worked_example.description: Globe Valve 2" 300#, SS316, 30 bar(g), IEC 60534, IP67, Qty 2 — India, Major City (Pune/Hyderabad/Bangalore)
worked_example.step_11_note: India: BCD 10% + SWS 1% + IGST 18% = 30.8% landed adder
worked_example.total_installed_cost_note: Apply factors sequentially per project cost estimating procedure. Convert final USD figure to INR at project FX rate. This example is illustrative — regional_cost_index and equipment_factor are partially overlapping indices; confirm with your estimating procedure which take precedence to avoid double-counting.
worked_example.double_count_warning: IMPORTANT: regional_cost_index.factor and regional_factors.sub_regions.equipment_factor are both < 1.0 for India. Do NOT multiply both together without checking your estimating procedure. Best practice: use ONE cost-level index (either the regional_cost_index OR the equipment_factor from regional_factors, not both) unless your procedure explicitly stacks them with a documented basis.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: Market rates (brand-sensitive - no universal published index). Base = USA list price (Emerson/ABB/Yokogawa/Rotork equivalent, 2024).
regional_cost_index.base_value_note: Set to 1.0 (dimensionless ratio basis). The USA regional_index.factor = 1.0 is the reference. All other regions are expressed as a fraction or multiple of USA list price. No external published index number is applicable — this is a direct market-rate multiplier table.
regional_cost_index.escalation_note: Apply CEPCI or Nelson-Farrar escalation index for years beyond 2024 if required by project estimating procedure. Instrumentation tracks broadly with CEPCI Equipment sub-index.
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_unit_cost_usd × regional_factor × (regional_factors sub_region equipment_factor if stacking — see double_count_warning in worked_example). local_ref = regional index for validation only.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines equipment/labour/civil factors and inland freight. See double_count_warning in worked_example before stacking with regional_cost_index.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level — apply to all items in the BOQ. country/region/sub_region must be set before any cost output is generated. output_currency drives display formatting; set to 'Local' to use the currency defined in regional_factors for the selected region, or specify a currency code (e.g. 'USD') to override.
project_settings.country_note: Populate with one of: India | USA | Middle East (GCC) | UK | Europe | China | Japan | ASEAN
project_settings.region_note: Populate with the applicable sub_region key from regional_factors for the selected country.
project_settings.sub_region_note: Same as region — retained for backward compatibility. If both region and sub_region are populated, sub_region takes precedence.
project_settings.equipment_origin_note: Set to 'Imported' if equipment is sourced internationally (import duty applies). Set to 'Local' if procured domestically (import duty = 0, use local market pricing).
project_settings.output_currency_note: Set to 'Local' to display costs in the regional currency (INR for India, GBP for UK, etc.) or specify an ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. 'USD') to override. FX rate must be set separately at project level.
project_settings.fx_rate_note: Project FX rate: local currency per 1 USD. Required if output_currency ≠ USD. Example: 83.5 for INR/USD. Set at project level and lock for estimate.
project_settings.city_tier_factor_note: Additional location adjustment within a sub-region if warranted (e.g. a remote industrial zone within a Metro sub-region). Default 1.0 (no adjustment). Typical range: 0.9–1.15.

usd_cost_baseline.note: Base = USD at USA level (matches MARKET_RATES base 14250 @ 2024). Engine: base_rate = usd_cost_baseline.items[equipment_subtype].usd_per_*, then x index_now/14250 x regional_factor x fx. verify=true entries are public-benchmark estimates pending calibration.

Control Valve Globe Type Pneumatic 2\" 3.basis: Restructured from existing schema rate source (cost_multiplier_tables.base_unit_cost_usd.values.Control Valve Globe Type Pneumatic 2" 300#) — same pricing basis, exposed as standard rate table. verify=true

Control Valve Rotary (Butterfly) 6\" 150.basis: Restructured from existing schema rate source (cost_multiplier_tables.base_unit_cost_usd.values.Control Valve Rotary (Butterfly) 6" 150#) — same pricing basis, exposed as standard rate table. verify=true

Emergency Shutdown Valve (ESDV) 4\" 600#.basis: Restructured from existing schema rate source (cost_multiplier_tables.base_unit_cost_usd.values.Emergency Shutdown Valve (ESDV) 4" 600# Ball) — same pricing basis, exposed as standard rate table. verify=true

Solenoid Valve 24VDC ½\" SS.basis: Restructured from existing schema rate source (cost_multiplier_tables.base_unit_cost_usd.values.Solenoid Valve 24VDC ½" SS) — same pricing basis, exposed as standard rate table. verify=true

Motorised Actuator (Quarter Turn).basis: Restructured from existing schema rate source (cost_multiplier_tables.base_unit_cost_usd.values.Motorised Actuator (Quarter Turn)) — same pricing basis, exposed as standard rate table. verify=true

SCHEMA-INST-DETECTOR.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs resolved. Engineering judgement applied for field sections, hints, mandatory flags, cost drivers, compliance rules, cost build-up formula, and project settings defaults.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-INST-DETECTOR.spec_example: Vibration Monitor (Proximity Probe) | API 670 | Encl: IP66 | MOC: SS316 | T_design: 85°C | Qty: 1 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum process/ambient temperature the instrument must withstand. Drives enclosure specification and hazardous-area suitability. Typical range 85–200 °C; values above 120 °C may require special cable glands or sun shields.

fields.label: Efficiency
fields.hint: Detection or measurement efficiency as a percentage. For gas/flame detectors: percentage of test-gas events correctly detected. For weighing systems: accuracy as % of full scale. For vibration monitors: not applicable — leave blank.

fields.label: Capacity
fields.hint: Applicable to Weighing System (Belt Scale) only — enter the conveyor belt design throughput capacity. Leave blank for all other instrument sub-types.

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Ingress Protection (IEC 60529) rating. IP66 = dust-tight, high-pressure water jets (standard outdoor/industrial). IP67 = additionally withstands temporary submersion up to 1 m — select for wash-down areas or submerged mounting.

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of Construction for wetted parts and housing. SS316 (316L stainless steel) is the standard for corrosion resistance in industrial environments. Additional grades may be added for severe-service applications.

fields.label: Standard
fields.hint: Governing design/test standard. Select API 670 for rotating-machine vibration monitors (proximity probe, velocity, acceleration, machine health systems). Select IEC 60529 for detectors, CCTV, weighing systems and other field instruments where enclosure IP rating is the primary compliance reference.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Vibration Monitor' IN equipment_subtype AND standard != 'API 670'
compliance_rules.message: Vibration Monitors (Proximity Probe, Velocity, Acceleration) and Vibration Monitoring Systems must comply with API 670. Please change Standard to 'API 670'.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 670 (5th Ed.) – Machinery Protection Systems: mandatory for all rotating-equipment vibration monitoring on API-scope trains.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Gas Detector Fixed LEL/Oxygen Analyser' AND (design_temperature IS None OR design_temperature IS None)
compliance_rules.message: Design Temperature is recommended for fixed gas detectors to confirm ATEX/IECEx T-class suitability in the hazardous area. Please specify a value.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079-29-1 – Gas detectors: performance requirements for flammable gases. T-class selection depends on max surface temperature.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Weighing System (Belt Scale)' AND (capacity IS None OR capacity IS None)
compliance_rules.message: Capacity (t/hr) is mandatory for Belt Scale costing. Please enter the conveyor throughput.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: OIML R 50-1 – Continuous totalising automatic weighing instruments (belt weighers): rated capacity is a primary design parameter.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature > 120
compliance_rules.message: Design Temperature exceeds 120 °C. Verify that selected enclosure rating and cable gland specification are suitable for high-temperature service.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079-14 – Electrical installations in explosive atmospheres: de-rating required above 80 °C ambient for most sensor electronics.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype) × standard_factor(standard) × enclosure_factor(enclosure) × moc_factor(moc) × temperature_factor(design_temperature) × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype) × standard_factor(standard) × enclosure_factor(enclosure) × moc_factor(moc) × temperature_factor(design_temperature) × capacity_factor(capacity, equipment_subtype) × quantity

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

library_items: Orifice Plate Assembly with Flange Taps
SCHEMA-INST-FLOW.generation_note: TODOs filled with engineering judgment — ready for internal review. IMPORTANT: Three schema bugs corrected — (1) duplicate spec_key 'flow_rate' across F-006/F-009/F-011 remapped to flow_rate_liquid / flow_rate_gas_nm3 / flow_rate_lph; (2) duplicate spec_key 'volume' across F-004/F-007 remapped to vessel_volume_litres / flow_rate_gas_m3; (3) Standard options: 'IS0' typo corrected to 'ISO 5167'; 'API 6D' (valve standard) removed and replaced with applicable flow measurement standards. Subtype options list consolidated from 24 near-duplicate names to 16 canonical names. Base unit rates USD 2024; verify against vendor quotations before use in formal estimates. | v1.0-priced: usd_cost_baseline + multiplier tables (USA-base; public-benchmark, verify=true).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-INST-FLOW.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | MOC:{moc} | Encl:{enclosure} | FlowRate:{flow_rate_liquid} {flow_rate_gas} {flow_rate_lph} {capacity_mass} | DP:{design_temperature} | Std:{standard} | Qty:{quantity}
SCHEMA-INST-FLOW.spec_example: Orifice Plate with DPT (Steam) | MOC:SS316 | Encl:IP65 | FlowRate:100 m³/hr | DT:350°C | Std:ISO 5167 | Qty:3

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the flow measurement technology. This is the dominant cost driver — Coriolis and Ultrasonic spool-piece meters are 5–10× the cost of orifice plates. Note: the original library contained near-duplicate names (e.g. 'Vortex Flowmeter' vs 'Vortex Flow Meter') that have been consolidated into the canonical list above.

fields.label: Pressure Rise
fields.hint: Differential pressure (DP) across the primary element, typically the permanent pressure loss for sizing the DP transmitter range. Used for orifice plates and averaging pitot tubes. Note: the label 'Pressure Rise' appears to be a misnomer — this field represents ΔP (pressure drop), not a pressure rise. Typical values: 500 mmWC (low-loss) to 2500 mmWC (standard orifice). Only applicable to differential-pressure based technologies (F-001 = Orifice Plate, Venturi, Annubar).
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype IN ['Orifice Plate with DPT (Liquid)', 'Orifice Plate with DPT (Steam)', 'Averaging Pitot Tube (Annubar)', 'Venturi Tube Flowmeter']

fields.label: Efficiency
fields.hint: Beta ratio (β = d/D): ratio of orifice bore diameter to pipe internal diameter. Note: this field is labelled 'Efficiency' in the library — the values (0.05–0.30) and context indicate it represents the orifice beta ratio. Valid ISO 5167 range: 0.20–0.75. Values seen in library: 0.05, 0.075, 0.10, 0.15, 0.30. Only applicable to Orifice Plate subtypes. No direct cost impact — affects plate bore size.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype IN ['Orifice Plate with DPT (Liquid)', 'Orifice Plate with DPT (Steam)', 'Orifice Plate Assembly with Flange Taps', 'Orifice Plate with Flange Tappings']

fields.label: Vessel / Spool Volume
fields.hint: Internal volume of the associated vessel or meter spool in litres. Used for Coriolis meter sizing (hold-up volume affects tube size selection) and for totalisers. Values seen in library: 316 L, 1000 L. Not required for clamp-on, orifice plate, or averaging pitot technologies.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype IN ['Coriolis Mass Flowmeter', 'Coriolis Mass Flowmeter (Large Bore)', 'Ultrasonic Flowmeter (Spool Piece)', 'Venturi Tube Flowmeter']

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: DC supply voltage to the transmitter. 24 V DC is the industry-standard 2-wire loop power for HART/4–20 mA devices. Non-standard supply (e.g. 110 V AC, FOUNDATION Fieldbus) requires an isolator or separate power supply — adds moderate cost premium.

fields.label: Volumetric Flow Rate
fields.hint: Maximum (design) volumetric flow rate of the process fluid in m³/hr. This is the primary sizing parameter: it determines meter bore size, which is the biggest cost driver after technology selection. Typical range: 30–20,000 m³/hr. For gas service at normal conditions, use F-009 (Nm³/hr) instead.

fields.label: Gas Flow Rate (Actual m³/hr)
fields.hint: Actual volumetric flow rate for gas service in m³/hr (at actual pressure and temperature). Note: the library stored identical values (30–500) in both this field and F-006, suggesting the field was used interchangeably for gas volumetric flow. For gas at standard/normal conditions use F-009 (Nm³/hr). Only applicable to gas-phase flow meters (Thermal Mass, Vortex, Ultrasonic, Annubar).
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype IN ['Thermal Mass Flowmeter', 'Averaging Pitot Tube (Annubar)', 'Vortex Flowmeter', 'Vortex Flowmeter (Steam Service)', 'Ultrasonic Flowmeter (Spool Piece)', 'Ultrasonic Flowmeter (Clamp-on)']

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum process fluid temperature at the meter location. Library values (350–450°C) reflect high-pressure steam service. Temperature is a major cost driver: above 200°C requires high-temp transmitter heads and remote seal/capillary arrangements; above 350°C (superheated steam) requires special high-temperature body versions with alloy internals. Cryogenic service (<−40°C) also requires special materials and insulation — flag separately.

fields.label: Gas Flow Rate (Normal m³/hr)
fields.hint: Gas flow rate at normal conditions (0°C, 1 atm) in Nm³/hr. Used for gas service meters (Thermal Mass, Vortex for gas, Annubar, Ultrasonic gas). Library value: 5000 Nm³/hr. For thermal mass flowmeters this is the primary sizing input. Convert to actual m³/hr using operating P and T before selecting meter bore size.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype IN ['Thermal Mass Flowmeter', 'Averaging Pitot Tube (Annubar)', 'Vortex Flowmeter', 'Vortex Flowmeter (Steam Service)']

fields.label: Capacity
fields.hint: Mass flow rate capacity in metric tonnes per hour (t/hr). Primarily applicable to Coriolis mass flowmeters, where the vendor sizes the tube bore directly from mass flow rate. Library value: 30 t/hr. For Coriolis (Large Bore) > 30 t/hr, apply the large-bore base rate in the lookup table.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype IN ['Coriolis Mass Flowmeter', 'Coriolis Mass Flowmeter (Large Bore)']

fields.label: Small Flow Rate (LPH)
fields.hint: Small volumetric flow rate in litres per hour (LPH). Used for rotameter/variable-area flowmeters which are typically applied to low-flow chemical dosing, purge, or sample streams. Library value: 1000 LPH (= 1 m³/hr). Full-scale range selection directly sizes the float and tube; larger ranges increase cost moderately.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype IN ['Rotameter (Variable Area Flowmeter)', 'Variable Area Flowmeter (Rotameter - Large)']

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of construction for the wetted parts (body, tube, electrodes). CS is lowest cost, suitable for non-corrosive hydrocarbons. SS316/316L is the process industry standard for general chemical service. Rubber-lined or PTFE-lined CS is used for magnetic flowmeters on slurry or abrasive fluids. Hastelloy C276 / Duplex SS for highly corrosive acids or seawater. MOC upgrade can add 30–150% to base meter price.

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Transmitter/converter housing ingress protection rating per IEC 60529, or hazardous area classification per IEC 60079. IP65 is the minimum for outdoor process plant. IP67/68 for submersible or high-wash-down. Ex d (flameproof) or Ex ia (intrinsically safe) required for Zone 1/2 hazardous areas — adds 20–40% to transmitter price and requires third-party ATEX/IECEx certification.

fields.label: Standard
fields.hint: Applicable design or type-test standard. ISO 5167 governs orifice plates, Venturi tubes, nozzles, and pitot tubes — select this for most differential-pressure flow elements. API MPMS chapters apply to custody-transfer metering in oil & gas — adds 15–25% premium for certified meter calibration and documentation. Note: 'API 6D' in the original library is a pipeline valve standard and does not apply to flowmeters. 'IS0' (original library) corrected to 'ISO 5167'.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Magnetic' IN equipment_subtype AND (moc == 'CS' OR moc == 'SS304' OR moc == 'SS316' OR moc == 'SS316L')
compliance_rules.message: Magnetic flowmeters require a non-conductive liner (rubber, PTFE, or ceramic) on the bore to prevent short-circuiting the measurement signal. A bare metal bore is not valid for mag-flow. Select 'Rubber-lined CS' or 'PTFE-lined CS' for the MOC of the flow tube.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60534-2 / manufacturer design requirements: electromagnetic flowmeter operation requires electrical isolation of the fluid from the meter body. Bare SS or CS tube is electrically conductive — the induced EMF signal would be shorted to zero.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Orifice Plate' IN equipment_subtype AND efficiency IS not None AND (efficiency < 0.2 OR efficiency > 0.75)
compliance_rules.message: Beta ratio (β) is outside the valid ISO 5167 range of 0.20–0.75. Values below 0.20 give very high differential pressure and excessive noise; values above 0.75 make the discharge coefficient inaccurate. Revise the orifice sizing.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 5167-2:2022 Cl.5.1 — valid range of diameter ratio β for orifice plates: 0.20 ≤ β ≤ 0.75. Outside this range, the standard's uncertainty equations are not applicable.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature > 200 AND 'Magnetic' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: Standard magnetic flowmeter liners (rubber, PTFE) are rated to a maximum of 130–180°C. Above 200°C, select a high-temperature ceramic-lined version, or change technology (e.g. Vortex or Coriolis for high-temperature liquid service).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Endress+Hauser Promag/ABB AquaMaster liner temperature ratings: hard rubber max 90°C; soft rubber max 130°C; PTFE max 180°C; ceramic max 350°C. IEC 60529 and manufacturer data sheets.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Vortex' IN equipment_subtype AND flow_rate_liquid IS not None AND flow_rate_liquid < 5
compliance_rules.message: Vortex flowmeters have a minimum measurable flow (low-flow cut-off) — typically equivalent to a Reynolds number > 20,000. At flow rates below ~5 m³/hr in standard bore sizes, vortex shedding becomes unreliable. Consider a thermal mass or Coriolis meter for low-flow service.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 17089-1 / IEC 60534 — vortex meter minimum Re ≥ 20,000 for ±1% accuracy. EMERSON Rosemount 8800 datasheet: minimum velocity typically 0.3–0.5 m/s depending on bore.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Ultrasonic' IN equipment_subtype AND 'Clamp-on' IN equipment_subtype AND moc IS not None AND ('lined' IN moc OR moc == 'Cast Iron')
compliance_rules.message: Clamp-on ultrasonic flowmeters have reduced accuracy or may not function on rubber/PTFE-lined pipes or cast iron pipes with internal scale build-up. The liner attenuates the ultrasonic signal significantly. Consider an inline spool-piece ultrasonic meter or alternative technology.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Flexim / Krohne UFM 3030 application notes: clamp-on transducers require acoustic coupling through the pipe wall. Thick liners (>3mm) or porous cast iron cause signal attenuation exceeding 20 dB, making measurement unreliable.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature > 350 AND !'Orifice' IN equipment_subtype AND !'Venturi' IN equipment_subtype AND !'Annubar' IN equipment_subtype AND !'Steam' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: Design temperature > 350°C is typical of high-pressure superheated steam service. At these conditions, only differential-pressure primaries (orifice plate, Venturi, Annubar) or steam-rated Vortex meters are normally specified. Verify that the selected meter technology has a certified high-temperature version.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC/TR 62714; Spirax Sarco / Yokogawa steam flow application guidance. At >300°C saturated / superheated steam: Coriolis, magnetic, and thermal mass meters are generally unsuitable without special engineering.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: standard == 'API MPMS Ch.5' OR standard == 'API MPMS Ch.6' OR standard == 'AGA-3'
compliance_rules.message: Custody transfer metering standard selected. This requires: (1) third-party flow calibration with traceable uncertainty certificate, (2) approved meter type/model per applicable authority, (3) flow computer with approved firmware, and (4) periodic in-situ proving. Allow 20–30% cost premium and 8–16 weeks additional lead time in the estimate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API MPMS Chapter 5 (liquid) / Chapter 6 (Coriolis) / AGA-3 (gas orifice): custody transfer metering has statutory accuracy and documentation requirements distinct from process/monitoring duty. Regulators (OIML, NMi, PTB) may require type approval.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Turbine Flowmeter' AND moc == 'CS'
compliance_rules.message: Turbine flowmeters in CS are susceptible to rotor bearing wear on dirty or viscous fluids. Confirm fluid is clean and low-viscosity (<10 cP). For dirty service, consider a magnetic or Coriolis meter. For custody-transfer gas, consider AGA-7 compliant turbine.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 9951 (turbine meters for gas) / API MPMS Ch.5.3 (turbine meters for liquid): turbine meters require clean, lubricating fluid; strainer upstream is mandatory. Bearing wear increases measurement error > 0.5% in <6 months on dirty service.

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) * moc_factor(moc) * flow_size_factor(flow_rate_liquid OR flow_rate_gas_nm3 OR capacity_mass) * quantity
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) * moc_factor(moc) * flow_size_factor(flow_rate_liquid OR flow_rate_gas_nm3 OR capacity_mass) * enclosure_factor(enclosure) * temperature_factor(design_temperature) * quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) * moc_factor(moc) * flow_size_factor(flow_rate_liquid OR flow_rate_gas_nm3 OR capacity_mass) * enclosure_factor(enclosure) * temperature_factor(design_temperature) * standard_premium(standard) * quantity * (1 + freight_insurance) * (1 + erection_installation) * (1 + contingency)

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
regional_cost_index.market_rate_note: Procured equipment: rates are market-based; CEPCI used only as time-escalation anchor.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

flow_size_factor._note: Stepped by nominal bore DN (process connection size). Base rate is for DN50–DN100 (2"–4"). Apply the factor for the actual bore derived from the flow rate and fluid velocity.

SCHEMA-INST-JB.spec_example: Junction Box (Steel - 300×300×150mm) | MOC:Steel (Mild Steel) | Encl:IP65 | Qty:5

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Ingress protection rating per IEC 60529 / ATEX classification. IP65 = dust-tight + water jet; IP66 = dust-tight + powerful water jet (outdoor standard); IP67 = temporary immersion. Ex e (increased safety) is required for Zone 1/2 hazardous areas per IEC 60079-7 — adds significant cost and requires third-party ATEX/IECEx certification. Select IP66 minimum for outdoor installations in process plants.

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of the enclosure body. Steel (MS) is lowest-cost and suitable for non-corrosive indoor/sheltered duty. GRP (Glass Reinforced Polyester) is preferred for coastal, offshore, or chemically aggressive outdoor environments — lighter than steel and inherently corrosion-resistant. Stainless 316 is specified for marine, offshore, or corrosive chemical duty. Polycarbonate suits lighter-duty instrument hook-up boxes. Aluminium die-cast is standard for EEx d/e certified enclosures.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: enclosure == 'Ex e' AND moc == 'Steel (Mild Steel)'
compliance_rules.message: Ex e (Increased Safety) ATEX/IECEx enclosures must not use uncoated Mild Steel. Select Stainless 316, GRP, or Aluminium LM6 die-cast to prevent corrosion compromising the explosion-protection integrity over service life.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079-7 Ed.5 Cl.7.4 — Enclosure material requirements for increased-safety apparatus. MS boxes are permitted only with a verified coating system and periodic inspection programme; for budget estimates default to non-ferrous or SS.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: enclosure == 'Ex e' AND equipment_subtype == 'Pull Box (Steel - 600×400×200mm)'
compliance_rules.message: Pull Boxes are passive cable-routing enclosures and are not normally Ex-certified as increased-safety apparatus. Use an Ex e certified Junction Box instead, or verify the pull-box design carries a valid IECEx/ATEX certificate before specifying for hazardous area.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079-14 Cl.9.3 — cable routing enclosures in Zone 1/2 require certified equipment. Standard pull boxes do not carry Ex e certification.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: enclosure == 'IP55' AND 'GRP' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: GRP enclosures typically achieve IP66 as standard. Specifying IP55 on a GRP box may indicate a mismatch between the enclosure product and the required protection level. Confirm with vendor datasheet — consider upgrading to IP65/IP66.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60529; GRP moulded enclosures (e.g. Fibox, Rittal KS series) are rated IP66/67 ex-works. IP55 is an atypically low rating for GRP and may result in incorrect product selection.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc == 'Steel (Mild Steel)' AND enclosure != 'Ex e'
compliance_rules.message: Mild Steel enclosures in outdoor or process plant environments require hot-dip galvanising or epoxy powder-coat finish. Ensure surface treatment is included in the procurement specification to prevent premature corrosion — especially in coastal or high-humidity sites.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: BS EN ISO 12944 (corrosion protection of steel structures by paint systems); NACE SP0169 for corrosion control. Process plant outdoor duty is typically corrosivity category C3–C4.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: quantity > 20
compliance_rules.message: More than 20 junction boxes on a single line item. Consider splitting by area/sub-system for better cost tracking, or confirm this is a bulk BOQ entry. Volume discounts typically apply at 10+ units from most JB manufacturers.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Engineering BOQ best practice — large bulk quantities can mask area-specific installation cost differences (inland freight, labour rates). Split by sub-area if project phasing differs.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) * moc_factor(moc) * enclosure_factor(enclosure) * quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) * moc_factor(moc) * enclosure_factor(enclosure) * quantity * (1 + freight_insurance) * (1 + erection_installation) * (1 + contingency)

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure of the vessel/pipeline at the tapping point. Drives flange rating (ASME 150# to 2500#). Typical range: 25–100 bar(g).

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum process temperature at the tapping point. Affects seal/wetted material selection. Typical range: 40–400 °C.

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Internal vessel volume in litres. Used to size displacer cage and determine float/probe length for certain technologies. Typical value: 316 L.

fields.label: Efficiency
fields.hint: Volumetric or transmission efficiency factor (0–1). Used for nuclear gauge source-strength calculation and for RF-capacitance calibration correction. Typical range: 0.25–0.5.

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: DC supply voltage to the transmitter/switch loop. 24 V DC is the industry-standard 2-wire loop power supply for HART/4–20 mA devices.

fields.label: Current
fields.hint: Maximum loop current drawn. Standard NAMUR NE 43 defines 3.6–20.5 mA active range; 10 A here likely refers to loop/solenoid trip current — confirm with data sheet.

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of Construction for wetted parts (float, displacer, probe, chamber). SS316L preferred for corrosive/hygienic service; CS for non-corrosive hydrocarbons.

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: IP rating per IEC 60529 for the transmitter/switch housing. IP65 = dust-tight + water jet; IP67 = temporary immersion; IP68 = continuous immersion.

fields.label: Standard
fields.hint: Applicable design/type-test standard. API 2545 covers servo tank-gauging. Verify required standard with SIL/functional-safety requirements and client spec.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 40 AND (moc == 'CS')
compliance_rules.message: Carbon Steel wetted parts are not recommended above 40 bar(g). Upgrade MOC to SS316 or SS316L to maintain corrosion allowance per ASME B31.3.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Table A-1 stress limits; API RP 14E corrosion allowance guidance. CS flanges rated Class 300 (51 bar) but corrosion margin erodes rapidly above 40 bar(g) without inhibition.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature > 200 AND equipment_subtype == 'Capacitance Level Switch'
compliance_rules.message: Standard RF-capacitance probes are limited to ~200 °C. Select a high-temperature version with ceramic insulation, or switch technology to Guided Wave Radar.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61511 selection guidance; typical PTFE probe insulation rated 200 °C max. Ceramic/HT versions extend to 400 °C but require separate data sheet verification.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Nuclear Level Gauge (Gamma)' AND NOT standard
compliance_rules.message: Nuclear gauge applications require regulatory approval (AERB/NRC/IAEA) and radiation source licence. Confirm regulatory clearance before procurement.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IAEA Safety Series No. 16; AERB Safety Code SC/IRF (India); US NRC 10 CFR Part 34. Source activity and shielding must be declared at FEED stage.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 100
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds template library range (100 bar(g)). Verify instrument pressure rating — Class 2500 flange or high-pressure inline connection may be required.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.5 flange pressure-temperature rating tables. Class 900 ≈ 148 bar(g) at 38 °C; Class 1500 ≈ 255 bar(g). High-HP level instruments are specialty items requiring RFQ.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: enclosure == 'IP65' AND 'Transmitter' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: IP65 is acceptable for sheltered indoor installation. For outdoor or wash-down duty, consider IP67 minimum per IEC 60529.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60529 ingress protection; NEMA 4X (≈ IP66) commonly specified for outdoor process instruments in PETROCHEMICAL applications.

cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype) * moc_factor(moc) * pressure_class_factor(design_pressure) * temperature_factor(design_temperature) * enclosure_factor(enclosure) * standard_premium(standard) * quantity * (1 + freight_insurance) * (1 + erection_installation) * (1 + contingency)

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Design Pressure (gauge)
fields.hint: Maximum gauge process pressure the instrument must withstand. Drives pressure rating class, Bourdon tube wall thickness, and process connection rating. Typical values: low pressure (2.5–10 bar), medium (10–100 bar), high pressure (>100 bar). For absolute-pressure transmitters use the Design Pressure (absolute) field below.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype

fields.label: Differential Pressure Span
fields.hint: Calibrated differential pressure span (DP range) across the transmitter or gauge. Used for DP flow and DP level instruments. Typical values: low-range (0.04–0.075 bar for liquid level or low-flow), standard (1.0 bar), high-range (1.6 bar). Not applicable to gauge pressure or absolute transmitters — leave blank.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum process temperature at the instrument connection point. Drives diaphragm seal fill-fluid selection, Bourdon tube alloy, and transmitter electronics temperature de-rating. Typical values: ambient service (85°C), steam/hot process (200°C). For diaphragm seal gauges above 150°C, high-temperature fill fluid is required.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype

fields.label: Pressure Rise
fields.hint: Static pressure rise (draft) measured in millimetres of water column. Used exclusively for draft gauges and manometers measuring furnace draft, duct static pressure, or HVAC systems. Typical values: low draft (25 mmWC for furnace draft measurement), medium (2500 mmWC), high (5000 mmWC for forced-draft fan outlets). Not applicable to Bourdon tube or transmitter types.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Internal enclosure volume of the Fire & Gas Detection Panel cabinet. Used to size the panel enclosure and determine cable gland capacity. Typical value: 316 L for a standard 19-inch rack-mounted panel. Not applicable to field instruments — leave blank for all gauge and transmitter sub-types.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: Supply voltage for loop-powered or smart transmitters and F&G panels. Standard 2-wire HART/4-20mA transmitters operate at 24 V DC. Not applicable to mechanical gauges or manometers — leave blank if not electrically powered.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype

fields.label: Design Pressure (absolute)
fields.hint: Absolute process pressure for absolute-pressure transmitters (vacuum to positive pressure referenced to absolute zero). Applies only to Smart Pressure Transmitter (Absolute). Typical value: 2.5 bar(a) covers near-vacuum through low-pressure absolute applications. Distinct from gauge pressure — do not use both fields for the same instrument.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype

fields.label: Switch Contact Rating (Current)
fields.hint: Maximum switching current rating of the SPDT relay contact in the pressure switch. Standard industrial pressure switches are rated at 10 A resistive at 240 V AC. Applies only to Pressure Switch Adjustable SPDT. Verify contact material (AgCdO or AgNi) if switching inductive loads.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of Construction for wetted parts: Bourdon tube, diaphragm, process connection, and manifold block. SS316 is standard for general process service (hydrocarbons, steam, water, weak acids). SS316L preferred for corrosive media, low-carbon weld-sensitive fabrication, or chloride-bearing fluids. For highly corrosive service (HF, concentrated H2SO4) specify Hastelloy C276 or PTFE-lined — these are not listed and require a non-standard item.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Ingress Protection (IP) rating of the instrument or panel housing per IEC 60529. IP65: dust-tight + low-pressure water jet — standard for outdoor field transmitters and switches. IP67: dust-tight + temporary immersion up to 1 m for 30 min — required in flooding risk zones, wash-down areas, or offshore splash zones. Mechanical gauges (Bourdon, diaphragm seal) do not carry an IP rating — the enclosure field does not apply and will be hidden.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype

fields.label: Standard
fields.hint: Governing design or certification standard for the selected equipment sub-type. API 520 (Parts I & II): sizing and selection of pressure-relieving devices — applicable to Safety Relief Valves (Pilot Operated). ASME Section VIII Div.1: pressure vessel and relief device design code — applicable to relief valves and high-pressure transmitters where code-stamping is required. IEC 61000: electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) standard — applicable to Fire & Gas Detection Panels and smart transmitters in electrically noisy environments. Select based on the project's applicable code jurisdiction.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure_gauge > 160 AND equipment_subtype != 'Smart Pressure Transmitter (High Pressure)' AND equipment_subtype != 'Safety Relief Valve Pilot Operated (Instrumentation)'
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds 160 bar(g). Standard pressure gauges and general-purpose transmitters are typically rated to Class 2500 (≈160 bar). Select 'Smart Pressure Transmitter (High Pressure)' for pressures above 160 bar, or verify the instrument URL with the manufacturer for a high-pressure option.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.5 Table 2-1.1 — Class 2500 flange pressure-temperature rating; EN 837-1 — Bourdon tube pressure gauges, upper range limit guidance.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Safety Relief Valve Pilot Operated (Instrumentation)' AND (standard IS None OR standard == '')
compliance_rules.message: A governing design standard (API 520 or ASME VIII) must be specified for Safety Relief Valves. This is mandatory for code compliance, sizing calculations, and procurement documentation.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 520 Part I Section 3 — applicability of standard to pressure-relieving and depressuring systems; ASME Boiler & Pressure Vessel Code Section VIII UG-125.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pressure Gauge (Diaphragm Seal)' AND design_temperature > 150
compliance_rules.message: Diaphragm seal gauges above 150°C require a high-temperature fill fluid (e.g. Syltherm 800 or food-grade silicone oil). Standard glycerine fill is rated to 120°C only. Confirm fill fluid selection and capillary length with the supplier.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B40.100 — Pressure gauges and gauge attachments; diaphragm seal fill-fluid temperature limits per manufacturer data (Wika, Ashcroft).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Smart Pressure Transmitter (High Pressure)' AND (standard IS None OR standard != 'ASME VIII')
compliance_rules.message: High-pressure transmitters (>160 bar) on pressure-containing systems typically require ASME Section VIII code compliance. Confirm with the project pressure-vessel design code whether code-stamping is required.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME BPVC Section VIII Div.1 UG-125 — requirements for pressure relief devices; API RP 551 — process measurement instrumentation.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Fire & Gas Detection Panel' AND (standard IS None OR standard != 'IEC 61000')
compliance_rules.message: Fire & Gas Detection Panels installed in industrial environments should meet IEC 61000 EMC requirements to ensure reliable detector response and avoid spurious trips from electrical noise.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61000-4 series — Electromagnetic compatibility testing; IEC 61508 — Functional safety of E/E/PE safety-related systems (applicable to F&G panels in SIL-rated loops).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc == 'SS316' AND design_temperature > 120 AND (equipment_subtype == 'Pressure Gauge (Diaphragm Seal)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Differential Pressure Transmitter' OR equipment_subtype == 'Smart DP Transmitter (Level)')
compliance_rules.message: For chloride-bearing process fluids at elevated temperatures, SS316 may be susceptible to stress corrosion cracking above 60°C. Confirm process fluid composition. If chlorides are present, consider upgrading to SS316L or specifying a PTFE-lined diaphragm.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156 — Materials for use in H2S-containing environments; ASTM A276 — susceptibility of austenitic stainless to chloride SCC above 60°C.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_rate(equipment_subtype) × moc_factor(moc) × pressure_adder(design_pressure_gauge) × temp_adder(design_temperature) × enclosure_factor(enclosure) × standard_factor(standard) × quantity × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × (1 + import_duty_pct/100)
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_rate(equipment_subtype) × moc_factor(moc) × pressure_adder(design_pressure_gauge) × temp_adder(design_temperature) × enclosure_factor(enclosure) × standard_factor(standard) × dp_span_factor(dp_span) × quantity × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × (1 + import_duty_pct/100) × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

pressure_adder.description: Multiplier on base_rate based on design_pressure_gauge range (bar(g))

standard_factor.description: Premium for code-compliant supply, third-party inspection, and documentation

dp_span_factor.description: Multiplier for DP instruments based on calibrated span (bar). Low-range spans require higher-accuracy sensors.

base_rates_usd.description: USD list-price benchmarks (USA basis, 2024). Apply regional_factor to convert.

cost_build_up.erection_installation_note: 22% of supply cost covers instrument mounting, impulse/capillary tubing, manifold block installation, cable termination, loop testing, and commissioning assist. F&G panels additionally include detector zone wiring and cause-and-effect verification — increase to 35% for panels. Increase overall to 30–40% for remote or offshore sites.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
options: Thermocouple Assembly Type K with Thermowell
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum process temperature the instrument must withstand. Drives sensor element selection, thermowell material, and housing rating. Typical values: ambient (0–50°C), steam/heat transfer (100–200°C), furnace/high-temp (>300°C).

fields.label: Efficiency
fields.hint: Thermal or signal efficiency factor for multipoint scanners and transmitters (expressed as a fraction, e.g. 0.95 = 95%). Leave blank for mechanical gauges. Range: 0.25–1.0.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: Supply voltage for loop-powered or smart transmitters. Standard 2-wire loop-powered transmitters operate at 24 V DC. Not applicable to mechanical gauges — leave blank if not electrically powered.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Internal cavity or housing volume, applicable to multipoint scanner enclosures or thermowell assemblies with integral housings. Typical value: 316 L for standard rack-mounted scanner. Leave blank for simple gauges and transmitters.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of Construction for wetted parts (thermowell, sensor sheath, process connection). SS316 is the standard for general chemical/process service. SS316L preferred for corrosive or low-carbon weld-sensitive applications. SS304 for non-corrosive utility service only.

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Ingress Protection (IP) rating of the instrument housing per IEC 60529. IP65: dust-tight + low-pressure water jet (standard outdoor). IP66: dust-tight + high-pressure jet (process areas). IP67: dust-tight + temporary immersion (wash-down/flooding risk). IP54: dust and splash protection (indoor non-hazardous panels only).

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature > 400 AND moc == 'SS304'
compliance_rules.message: SS304 is not recommended above 400°C due to sensitisation risk. Upgrade MOC to SS316 or SS316L, or specify a high-temperature alloy (e.g. Inconel 600).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Table A-1; ASTM A276 / EN 10088 — SS304 carbide precipitation above 425°C reduces intergranular corrosion resistance.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature > 850 AND (equipment_subtype == 'RTD PT100 Assembly with Thermowell' OR equipment_subtype == 'Smart Temperature Transmitter (RTD)')
compliance_rules.message: RTD elements (PT100) are typically rated to a maximum of 850°C. Consider switching to a Thermocouple (Type K up to 1260°C, Type S/R up to 1450°C) for this temperature range.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60751 — Industrial platinum resistance thermometers; DIN EN 60751 — upper limit for standard PT100 elements.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: enclosure == 'IP54' AND (equipment_subtype != 'Multipoint Temperature Scanner')
compliance_rules.message: IP54 provides only splash protection and is not suitable for outdoor process areas or wash-down zones. Consider IP65 or higher for field-mounted instruments.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60529 — Degrees of protection provided by enclosures (IP Code); NEMA 250 equivalent NEMA 4 / 4X.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Temperature Gauge (Capillary)' AND design_temperature > 300
compliance_rules.message: Capillary-type gauges above 300°C require high-temperature fill fluid (e.g. silicone oil). Verify fill fluid compatibility with process temperature and confirm supplier rating.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B40.200 — Thermometers, Direct Reading and Remote Indicating; capillary fill fluid thermal limits.

cost_build_up.full_formula: base_rate(equipment_subtype) × moc_factor(moc) × temp_adder(design_temperature) × enclosure_factor(enclosure) × voltage_factor(voltage) × efficiency_factor(efficiency) × quantity × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × (1 + import_duty_pct/100) × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)
