compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Cryogenic insulation' AND (surface_temp IS None OR surface_temp >= -50)
compliance_rules.message: Cryogenic insulation requires confirmed service temperature below -50°C. Verify process conditions before proceeding.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASTM C591 (rigid cellular polyisocyanurate) / BS 5970 - cryogenic insulation design temperature threshold

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Fire-resistant insulation' AND fire_rating_hours IS None
compliance_rules.message: Fire-resistant insulation requires a declared fire rating (hours). Specify per project fire protection philosophy.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NFPA 850 / API RP 2218 - fire protection of process plant; UL 1709 rapid-rise fire test

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Hot insulation (mineral wool)' AND operating_temp > 650
compliance_rules.message: Operating temperature exceeds 650°C — standard mineral wool (refractory ceramic fibre required above this limit). Verify insulation material grade.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASTM C547 / IS 8183 - mineral wool upper service temperature limit ~650°C; switch to RCF/AES per ASTM C892

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Epoxy painting' AND surface_preparation_grade IS not None AND surface_preparation_grade != 'Sa 2.5' AND surface_preparation_grade != 'Sa 3'
compliance_rules.message: Epoxy coatings require minimum Sa 2.5 (near-white blast cleaning) surface preparation. Lower grades risk premature coating failure.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: SSPC-SP 10 / ISO 8501-1 Sa 2.5 — minimum surface cleanliness for epoxy primer application per NACE SP0169 / SSPC Paint 22

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Tank painting (internal)' AND ('Acid' IN service_fluid OR 'Caustic' IN service_fluid OR 'Solvent' IN service_fluid)
compliance_rules.message: Internal coating for corrosive service (acid/caustic) — specify chemical-resistant lining (vinyl ester, glass flake epoxy, or rubber lining). Standard epoxy not adequate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 652 (lining of aboveground petroleum storage tanks) / NACE SP0188 — chemical resistance selection matrix

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Anti-condensation insulation' AND ambient_rh < 60
compliance_rules.message: Ambient RH below 60% — verify that anti-condensation insulation is required for this location. Consider vapour barrier effectiveness.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASHRAE Fundamentals Handbook — condensation control; BS 5970 vapour barrier requirements for cold service

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Acoustic insulation' AND noise_reduction_db IS None
compliance_rules.message: Acoustic insulation requires target noise attenuation (dB) to be specified. Without NR value, insulation thickness and material cannot be determined.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: OSHA 1910.95 / ISO 11690 — industrial noise control; acoustic performance specification per IL/NR criteria

SCHEMA-LIGHTING.spec_example: Lighting Fixture (Industrial Floodlight LED) | 20000lm | 240V | Enclosure:IP65 | Area Class:Safe Area | Qty:4

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below. Note: earthing items (Earth Electrode, Earthing Strip) are priced per metre run or per electrode; lighting fixtures are priced per unit.

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: Supply voltage for the fitting or panel. Typical: 240V AC single-phase for standard lighting fixtures; 415V AC three-phase for distribution boards and street lighting panels; 24V DC for some emergency/exit fittings. 12V only for low-voltage landscape/feature lighting.

fields.label: Lumen Output
fields._label_correction_note: Original skeleton had label='Quantity' with unit='LM'/'LF'. LM = lumens; the library values (130, 360, 2200, 4000, 5200) are clearly lumen outputs, not quantities and not linear metres. Label corrected to 'Lumen Output'. The LF (linear feet) US unit was also incorrect for lumens - corrected to 'lm' for both SI and US.
fields.hint: Total lumen output of the fitting. Typical values by type: LED Tube 20W ≈ 2200lm; LED Panel 40W ≈ 4000lm; LED High Bay 150W ≈ 15000lm; LED High Bay 200W ≈ 20000lm; LED Flood 200W ≈ 20000lm; LED Street 150W ≈ 15000lm; Flameproof 40W ≈ 4000lm; Emergency/Exit 3W ≈ 130–360lm. Used to verify luminance design targets.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - Quick Estimate (always visible for fixtures)

fields.label: Rated Current (Panel / Board)
fields.hint: Incoming main breaker rating of the lighting distribution board or panel. Common ratings: 32A, 63A, 100A, 125A, 160A, 250A. Only applicable to Lighting Panel (Distribution Board) and Lighting Distribution Board (Street). Not required for individual light fittings.

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Ingress protection or explosion protection rating. IP20 = finger-proof, indoor only (offices, clean rooms). IP40 = dust-protected, indoor (warehouses, workshops). IP55 = splash-proof, sheltered outdoor. IP65 = dust-tight / low-pressure jet wash, general outdoor and industrial. IP66 = heavy hose-down / sea spray. Ex d = flameproof, Zone 1/2 hazardous areas (IEC 60079-1) - mandatory for Flameproof LED Fitting.

fields.label: Area Classification
fields.hint: IEC 60079-10-1 hazardous area classification at the installation location. Safe Area = no explosive atmosphere risk, standard fittings acceptable. Zone 2 = explosive atmosphere unlikely in normal operation (Cat 3G, Ex nA/e/d). Zone 1 = explosive atmosphere likely occasionally (Cat 2G, Ex d/e/ia). Zone 0 = explosive atmosphere present continuously (Cat 1G, Ex ia only). Drives Ex protection requirement and cost uplift.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical units. For earthing strips and buried conductors, enter total run length in metres using the Earthing Run Length field instead; keep this field as 1 per earthing circuit.

fields.label: Earthing Run Length
fields.hint: Total length of earthing strip or buried conductor run in metres. Applies to 'Earthing Strip GI 50x6mm' and 'Earthing Strip (GI/Cu Buried)' items only. Priced per metre of installed strip including trench backfill. Typical project runs: 10–200m.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - Quick Estimate (always visible for earthing strip items)

fields.label: Pole Height
fields.hint: Lighting pole height above finished ground level. Standard industrial/road heights: 8m (pedestrian/access roads), 10–12m (main plant roads, open yard), 14–16m (high-mast / large open areas). Taller poles require larger base plate, heavier foundation and anchor bolts - significant civil cost impact.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (area_class == 'Zone 1' OR area_class == 'Zone 0') AND enclosure != 'Ex d'
compliance_rules.message: Zone 1 and Zone 0 locations require Ex d (flameproof) or Ex e (increased safety) enclosure rating. Standard IP-rated fittings are not permitted. Change enclosure to 'Ex d' or revise area classification.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079-0 Equipment for explosive atmospheres - General requirements. IEC 60079-1 Flameproof enclosures. Zone 1 requires Category 2G (EPL Gb) minimum; Zone 0 requires Category 1G (EPL Ga). Non-Ex fittings in these zones violate ATEX/IECEx certification requirements.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: area_class == 'Zone 2' AND enclosure == 'IP20' OR enclosure == 'IP40'
compliance_rules.message: Zone 2 locations require at minimum Ex nA (non-sparking) or Ex e (increased safety) protection. IP20/IP40 fittings without Ex certification are not Zone 2 compliant. Verify fitting has IECEx/ATEX Zone 2 certification (Cat 3G).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079-15 (Ex nA) and IEC 60079-7 (Ex e) - Zone 2 permits Category 3G equipment. IP rating alone does not confer Ex certification; the fitting must carry IECEx or ATEX marking.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Flameproof LED Fitting 40W Zone-1' AND area_class == 'Safe Area'
compliance_rules.message: Flameproof (Ex d) fitting specified in a Safe Area. This is technically permissible but significantly over-specified. Consider a standard IP65 fitting to reduce cost.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Engineering economy - Ex d fittings carry a 2.5-3x cost premium over equivalent IP65 fittings. Over-specification in safe areas wastes project budget.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Emergency Light' IN equipment_subtype AND voltage > 240
compliance_rules.message: Emergency lighting units above 240V AC are non-standard. Emergency and exit fittings are typically 110V or 240V AC (with internal battery/inverter) or 24V DC central battery system. Verify supply voltage.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60598-2-22 Luminaires for emergency lighting. BS 5266-1 / NFPA 101 Life Safety Code - emergency lighting supply requirements.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Lighting Pole' IN equipment_subtype AND pole_height > 12
compliance_rules.message: Poles above 12m require structural wind load analysis and engineered foundations. Confirm with civil/structural discipline and include foundation cost separately in the civil BOQ.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60598-1 / EN 40 Lighting columns - structural requirements. Wind loading per local building code (IS 875 Part 3 / ASCE 7 / BS EN 1991-1-4). Foundation design by structural engineer for poles >12m.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_rate[equipment_subtype] * lumen_factor[lumen_output] * enclosure_factor[enclosure] * area_class_factor[area_class] * current_factor[current] * pole_height_factor[pole_height] * quantity * regional_factor[region] * sub_region_factor[sub_region]
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_rate[equipment_subtype] * lumen_factor[lumen_output] * enclosure_factor[enclosure] * area_class_factor[area_class] * voltage_factor[voltage] * current_factor[current] * pole_height_factor[pole_height] * earthing_run_length * quantity * regional_factor[region] * sub_region_factor[sub_region] * (1 + freight_insurance) * (1 + erection_installation) * (1 + contingency)

base_unit_rates_usd_2024.note: FOB USA supply-only list price basis, 2024. Apply regional_cost_index factor to convert to project region. Earthing strip/electrode rates are per metre (m) or per electrode respectively.

adders._erection_note: 25% for lighting is higher than instruments (15%) because installation includes conduit/trunking, cable pulling, bracket fabrication, and for poles includes foundation excavation, concrete and backfill. Street lighting poles with foundations may warrant 35-45% - override at item level.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate x (current_index / base_value) x regional_factor x sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: Supply or control circuit voltage. Typical values: 24V DC (field devices / solenoids), 110V AC (control circuits), 415/480V AC (power circuits for MCC panels). Range: 24–500V.

fields.label: Current
fields.hint: Rated contact or load current. Limit switches / push buttons: 5–10A. Solenoid valves: typically governed by coil wattage not current rating. Required for MCC panels and marshalling cabinets.

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure of the process connection. Required for pressure switches, level switches, solenoid valves. Not applicable for purely electrical items (marshalling cabinets, push button stations).

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum process fluid or ambient temperature the device must withstand. Drives seal material, body rating and Ex-protection selection. Enter in °C; range −40 to +200°C for standard industrial devices.

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Ingress protection or explosion protection rating. IP55 = dust/water splash (indoor). IP65 = dust-tight / low-pressure jet (general outdoor). IP66 = heavy sea / hose-down. Ex d = flameproof for Zone 1/2 hazardous areas (IEC 60079-1). Select Ex d when area classification requires it.

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of construction for the enclosure body. GRP (Glass Reinforced Polyester) = lightweight, corrosion-resistant, preferred for coastal/chemical environments. SS316 = stainless steel, food/pharma-grade, seawater or aggressive chemical service.

fields.label: Drive Type
fields.hint: Motor starting / speed control method hosted in or controlled by the panel. VFD (Variable Frequency Drive) = variable speed, highest cost. DOL (Direct-On-Line) = simplest, lowest cost, ≤11kW typical. Star-Delta = reduced voltage start, 11–45kW. Soft Starter = electronic reduced voltage, 15–200kW. Applies to Local Control Panel (MCC Interface) only.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

fields.label: Area Classification
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 16
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds 16 bar(g). Verify pressure switch or solenoid valve pressure rating class (ASME Class 300 or PN25/PN40 body required). Confirm with vendor datasheet.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61010-1 / ASME B31.1 - pressure equipment safety; PN16 is the standard catalogue rating for industrial switches and solenoid valves. Above this requires upgraded body / trim.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: enclosure != 'Ex d' AND area_classification == 'Zone 1'
compliance_rules.message: Hazardous area Zone 1 requires Ex d (flameproof) or Ex e (increased safety) enclosure. Standard IP-rated enclosures are not permitted. Revise enclosure selection.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079-0 & IEC 60079-1: Equipment for explosive atmospheres. Zone 1 mandates Category 2G protection level minimum.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: voltage > 110 AND 'Proximity Switch' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: Proximity switches typically operate at 24V DC or max 48V AC/DC. Voltage entered exceeds typical ratings. Verify sensor supply voltage against manufacturer specification.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60947-5-2: Proximity switches - rated operational voltage typically 10–30V DC (2-wire) or up to 48V AC/DC (3-wire).

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_unit_rate[equipment_subtype] * enclosure_factor[enclosure] * moc_factor[moc] * quantity * regional_factor[region] * sub_region_factor[sub_region]
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_rate[equipment_subtype] * enclosure_factor[enclosure] * moc_factor[moc] * voltage_factor[voltage] * drive_factor[drive_type] * pressure_factor[design_pressure] * quantity * regional_factor[region] * sub_region_factor[sub_region]
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_rate[equipment_subtype] * enclosure_factor[enclosure] * moc_factor[moc] * voltage_factor[voltage] * current_factor[current] * drive_factor[drive_type] * pressure_factor[design_pressure] * temperature_factor[design_temperature] * quantity * regional_factor[region] * sub_region_factor[sub_region] * (1 + freight_insurance) * (1 + erection_installation) * (1 + contingency)

base_unit_rates_usd_2024.note: FOB USA list price basis. Apply regional_cost_index factor to convert to project region.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: Nominal system voltage. Typical values: 415 V (IEC 3-phase LV), 500 V (mining/industrial), 750 V (DC traction). Must match switchgear rated voltage.

fields.label: Current
fields.hint: Rated continuous current (Icw) of the switchboard or busbar. Standard IEC 61439 frames: 630 A, 1000 A, 1600 A, 2500 A, 4000 A, 6300 A. Drives busbar cross-section and ACB frame size — primary cost driver for switchgear items.

fields.label: Fault Level
fields.hint: Prospective short-circuit current (Icc) at the busbars (symmetrical RMS). Typical industrial: 35–50 kA. High-fault utility-connected MSBs: 65 kA. Dictates ACB/MCCB breaking capacity and busbar bracing — major cost driver for main switchgear.

fields.label: Power Factor Correction (kvar)
fields.hint: Applicable to Capacitor Bank sub-type only. Total reactive power compensation in kvar. Typical steps: 50, 100, 200, 300, 600 kvar. A single library item showed 7 (likely 7% detuned filter reactor harmonic tuning factor, not a kvar value) — confirm with project engineer. Leave blank for non-capacitor items.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype == 'LV Switchgear (Capacitor Bank - 415V)'

fields.label: System Voltage (kV)
fields.hint: System voltage expressed in kV for IEC classification. LV is defined as ≤1 kV (IEC 60038). For all items in this template the value is 1.0 kV (equivalent to ≤1000 V). Do not change unless project deviates from standard IEC LV definition.

fields.label: Standard
fields.hint: Governing IEC standard for the item. IEC 61439: low-voltage switchgear & controlgear assemblies (MSB/DB). IEC 60502: power cables 1–30 kV (power cables). IEC 60245: rubber-insulated cables (flexible/elastomeric). IEC 60831: capacitors for power factor correction. Auto-suggested based on equipment sub-type.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: fault_level > 50 AND current >= 4000 AND standard != 'IEC 61439'
compliance_rules.message: Main Distribution switchgear at 4000 A / >50 kA fault level must be designed and verified to IEC 61439-1/-2. Select IEC 61439.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61439-1:2020 Cl. 8.3 — type-tested assemblies required for Icc ≥ 50 kA; IEC 61439-2 for power switchgear & controlgear assemblies.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'LV Switchgear' IN equipment_subtype AND voltage > 690
compliance_rules.message: Voltage exceeds 690 V AC — verify equipment is rated and marked as 'High Voltage LV' per IEC 60038 / IEC 61439. Standard ACB ratings typically max at 690 V.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60038:2009 Table 1 — LV systems ≤1 kV. ACB ratings per manufacturer catalogues (e.g. ABB Emax2, Schneider Masterpact) top out at 690 V for standard frames.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'LV Switchgear (Capacitor Bank - 415V)' AND standard != 'IEC 60831'
compliance_rules.message: Capacitor bank items should reference IEC 60831 (shunt power capacitors). Update the Standard field.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60831-1:2014 — shunt power capacitors of the self-healing type for AC systems up to and including 1 kV.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Power Cable' IN equipment_subtype AND standard != 'IEC 60502'
compliance_rules.message: Power cables (PVC-insulated, 1 kV) should reference IEC 60502-1. Update the Standard field.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60502-1:2004 — power cables with extruded insulation and accessories for rated voltages from 1 kV up to 30 kV.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Flexible Cable (Elastomeric - 16mm²)' AND standard != 'IEC 60245'
compliance_rules.message: Elastomeric flexible cables are governed by IEC 60245. Consider updating the Standard field.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60245-1:2003 — rubber-insulated cables of rated voltages up to and including 450/750 V.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype, current, fault_level) × quantity × voltage_adder_factor × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype, current, fault_level, standard) × quantity × voltage_adder_factor × pfc_kvar_factor × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (1 + import_duty_pct/100) × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

base_unit_rates_usd.note: USD supply-only ex-works reference rates at ENR CCI 14250 (2024 base). Scale by regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor for landed cost.

LV Switchgear (Main Distribution - 4000A.basis: 4000 A ACB + IEC 61439 type-tested metal-clad cubicle, 65 kA braced busbars, MV/LV transformer feeder incomer + 8–12 outgoing MCCB ways. Market ref: ABB MNS / Schneider Okken / Siemens SIVACON S8 typical supply price 2024.

LV Switchgear (Distribution Board - 1600.basis: 1600 A MCCB incomer + IEC 61439 form 2/3 DB, ~12 outgoing MCB/MCCB ways. Market ref: ABB ArTu / Schneider Prisma P 2024.

LV Switchgear (Capacitor Bank - 415V).basis: 200 kvar stepped PFC bank, 415 V, with 7% detuned reactor filter, contactors and controller. Adjust pfc_kvar_factor for other sizes.

Low Voltage Power Cable (PVC - 240mm²).basis: 3C+E 240mm² XLPE/PVC armoured cable (IEC 60502-1). Market ref: Nexans / Prysmian 2024 list.

voltage_adder_factor.note: Multiplier applied when voltage deviates from 415 V base. Higher voltage ratings require heavier insulation and rated components.

fault_level_adder_factor.note: Applied to switchgear items only. Higher Icc requires heavier busbars, higher-rated ACBs/MCCBs.

pfc_kvar_factor.note: Capacitor bank cost scales approximately linearly with kvar. Base = 200 kvar.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-MASONRY.generation_note: AUTO-GENERATED SKELETON v5 - Regional cost index matrix (item_type × region). Search TODO for engineering input. | R02 promoted 0.5-skeleton->1.0-priced after structural completion + benchmark pricing (validator-gated; no remaining blocking errors).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the masonry type. Determines unit rate and applicable material/workmanship standard. Common brick = load-bearing or internal partition walls in fired clay brick. Facing brick = architectural/exposed external finish, higher cost and quality. AAC block = lightweight autoclaved aerated concrete, excellent thermal performance, fast lay rate. Hollow concrete block = dense aggregate hollow core block, structural or non-structural. Random rubble stone = uncoursed field stone or quarried stone laid in random bond — retaining walls, compound walls. Coursed stone = dressed/squared stone laid in regular horizontal courses — higher quality finish, higher labour cost.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Volume of masonry in cubic metres (m³), measured as the gross built volume including mortar joints but excluding openings >0.1 m². Standard measurement convention per IS 1200 Part 6 (India), SMM7/NRM2 (UK), or CSI MasterFormat (USA). For walls, calculate as: length × height × thickness. Deduct openings larger than 0.1 m² (doors, windows). For random rubble retaining walls, include battered face in volume calculation.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Stone Masonry (Random Rubble)' AND quantity > 500
compliance_rules.message: Large volume of Random Rubble Stone Masonry (>500 m³) specified. Confirm this is not a load-bearing structural wall application — random rubble is not recommended for load-bearing walls above 4.5 m height or where seismic loading applies. Consider Coursed Stone Masonry or reinforced block/brick masonry for structural applications.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 1905:1987 Cl. 5.3 (unreinforced masonry — height/thickness limits); IS 4326:2013 (earthquake-resistant construction); EN 1996-1-1 (Eurocode 6) Cl. 3.1 — random rubble has undefined characteristic compressive strength and should not be used as primary structural element in seismic zones III–V.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Block Masonry (AAC)' AND quantity > 50
compliance_rules.message: AAC Block Masonry specified. Confirm that AAC blocks are not used below damp-proof course (DPC) level, in permanently wet or submerged locations, or as load-bearing walls carrying more than two storeys without structural engineer sign-off. AAC has low density and reduced compressive strength vs dense concrete block — minimum grade G2/600 kg/m³ required for structural use.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 2185 Part 3:2005 (AAC blocks — specification); IS 6041:1985 (construction using AAC); EN 771-4 (AAC block specification); ASTM C1693 (AAC standard). AAC grade G2 (600 kg/m³, 3.5 MPa) is minimum for load-bearing use; G1 (400 kg/m³) is non-structural partition only.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Brick Masonry (Facing)'
compliance_rules.message: Facing Brick Masonry selected. Ensure specification includes brick grade (Class A/B/C per IS 1077, or engineering brick per BS EN 771-1), mortar designation (M4 or M6 minimum for external exposure), weathering profile of perpend/bed joints, and any required efflorescence, frost, or chemical resistance class. Unit rate includes brick, mortar, pointing and scaffold — confirm if decorative pointing or special joint profiles apply as these carry additional cost.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 1077:1992 (common burnt clay building bricks — classification); BS EN 771-1:2011 (clay masonry units); IS 2212:1991 (code of practice for brickwork) Cl. 6 — external facing brickwork requires M6 mortar minimum and Class A or Special Quality bricks for exposed conditions.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: quantity < 0.1
compliance_rules.message: Quantity is below the minimum measurable masonry volume (0.1 m³). Check input — masonry works are not measured below this threshold. Small volumes below 0.1 m³ (e.g. brick piers, copings, isolated columns) should be consolidated with adjacent wall volumes or priced as lump sum item.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 1200 Part 6:1973 (method of measurement for masonry); NRM2 Section 14 (RICS — masonry measurement rules). Both standards specify that isolated small volumes are absorbed into adjacent items rather than measured separately.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Stone Masonry (Coursed)'
compliance_rules.message: Coursed Stone Masonry selected. This is a high-skill, high-labour item. Confirm availability of dressed stone and qualified stone masons at project location — in many regions, skilled coursed stone masons are scarce and this significantly affects programme and cost. Unit rate assumes quarried stone dressed on site; pre-dressed/supplied stone will increase material cost but may reduce labour.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 1597 Part 1:1992 (rubble stone masonry — construction) and Part 2 (ashlar masonry); BS 5628-1 (code of practice for use of masonry). Coursed (ashlar/semi-ashlar) stone masonry requires stone with minimum 7 MPa characteristic compressive strength and tolerances of ±3 mm on bed faces.

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_cost = unit_rate[equipment_subtype] × quantity. unit_rate is all-in USD/m³ (materials + mortar + labour + scaffold) at ENR CCI 14250 basis. Apply regional_cost_index factor and sub_region civil_factor.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: No Tier 2 fields defined — Tier 1 formula is used for both quick and budget estimates. Sub-type selection and volume together deliver ±20–30% accuracy, which is consistent with AACE Class 4. For finer accuracy (Class 3, ±10%), add wall thickness, mortar grade, and height-above-ground fields at Tier 3.
cost_build_up.full_formula: total_cost = unit_rate[equipment_subtype] × quantity × regional_cost_index_factor × sub_region_civil_factor × (1 + contingency). Masonry is an in-situ civil activity — no freight/insurance or separate erection adder applies. Materials, mortar, labour, and scaffold are included in the all-in unit rate.

unit_rates_USD_per_m3_all_in.note: All-in USD/m³ at ENR CCI 14250 (2024 USA base). Includes: masonry units (bricks/blocks/stone), mortar (cement:sand mix, M4–M6), labour (laying, jointing, pointing), scaffold (up to 4 m height), curing and clean-down. Excludes: DPC, lintels, reinforcement, wall ties, special finishes, work above 4 m height (add scaffold premium), and below-ground work (add 10–15% for dewatering/confined space).

unit_rate_notes.Brick Masonry (Common): Fired clay common brick (IS 1077 Class B or ASTM C62 SW), 1:6 cement-sand mortar, 10 mm bed and perpend joints. Approximately 500 bricks/m³ including 15% mortar volume.
unit_rate_notes.Brick Masonry (Facing): Facing brick IS 1077 Class A / BS EN 771-1, 1:4 cement-sand M6 mortar, flush or recessed pointing, factory-colour-matched mortar. 15–25% premium over common brick due to material quality, selection losses, and pointing labour.
unit_rate_notes.Block Masonry (AAC): AAC block IS 2185 Pt 3 Grade G2 (600 kg/m³), 3–5 mm thin-bed adhesive mortar (not sand-cement), quick lay — approximately 8–10 blocks/m² at 200 mm thick. Rate reflects adhesive mortar premium vs conventional mortar saving.
unit_rate_notes.Block Masonry (Hollow Concrete): Hollow concrete masonry unit (HCMU) IS 2185 Pt 1 Grade B (5.0 MPa), 1:6 cement-sand mortar, 10 mm joints, hollow cores unfilled unless specified. Fill cores with concrete where structural or seismic requirement applies — add USD 35/m³ for core fill.
unit_rate_notes.Stone Masonry (Random Rubble): Uncoursed quarried or field stone, 1:6 cement-sand mortar, chinked with spalls. Labour-intensive stone selection and fitting — rate varies by stone availability. Add 20% if stone requires long-haul transport (>50 km).
unit_rate_notes.Stone Masonry (Coursed): Coursed rubble or semi-ashlar, stone dressed on site, 1:4 cement-sand mortar, raked joints. High skill labour — rate is sensitive to mason availability. Pre-dressed stone increases material cost by ~30% but reduces site labour.

scaffold_premium.note: Base unit rates assume wall height up to 4 m. Add the following premiums to the all-in unit rate for greater heights.

adder_notes.freight_insurance: 0% — masonry materials (bricks, blocks, aggregate, cement, stone) are sourced locally or regionally in virtually all markets. No international freight or marine insurance applies. Inland haulage cost is embedded in the unit rate materials element.
adder_notes.erection_installation: 0% — masonry is an in-situ construction activity, not an installed piece of equipment. Labour, scaffold, mortar and curing costs are fully included within the all-in unit rate (USD/m³). There is no separate installation adder.
adder_notes.contingency: 10% on total masonry cost. AACE Class 4 standard. Increase to 15% for stone masonry in remote locations with uncertain stone supply, or where below-ground or seismic-zone conditions complicate the specification.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_civil_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. For masonry, use sub_region civil_factor (not equipment_factor) since this is a labour+material civil activity. local_ref = regional index for validation only.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: item_type corrected E->A: unit-rate construction/installation work, field-labour dominated (matches PLASTERING / STORM-DRAIN / EARTHING library convention). ENR CCI retained, base 14250 (2024).

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight. For masonry, the civil_factor sub-region multiplier is the operative factor — masonry cost is dominated by local labour and local material prices.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-MCC-MOTOR.spec_example: Motor Control Center (MCC - 4000A Main) | 415 V | 4000 A | IEC 61439 | Qty: 1

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Rated shaft output power of the controlled motor. This is the primary cost driver for MCC bucket sizing — it determines the contactor, overload relay, MCCB and cable rating. Not applicable to Feeder or PLC Interface buckets; enter 0 for those. Typical range: 7.5 kW (small pumps/fans) to 160 kW (large compressors/mills). For the MCC Main section, enter the total connected load in kW.

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: System supply voltage at the MCC bus. Standard LV values: 415 V (IEC, 50 Hz — India/UK/ASEAN/Middle East), 400 V (IEC, 50 Hz — Europe), 480 V (USA, 60 Hz). Control circuit voltage is typically 24 V DC (enter 24 for PLC Interface bucket). Voltages above 1000 V are MV — use the MV Switchgear template instead.

fields.label: Current
fields.hint: Full-load current (FLC) of the motor or feeder circuit. Used to verify contactor, MCCB and cable sizing. For motors at 415 V, approximate FLC ≈ motor_power (kW) × 2.0 A/kW. For the MCC 4000A main section, enter the incoming bus rating (4000 A). Feeder bucket: enter the feeder circuit FLC. If unknown, leave blank and derive from motor power.

fields.label: Fault Level
fields.hint: Prospective short-circuit current (PSCC) at the MCC incomer. Determines the required breaking capacity (Icu) of the MCCB and the withstand rating (Icw) of busbars. Typical LV MCC values: 25–50 kA for industrial plants; 65 kA for heavy industrial/utility close to transformer. Must not exceed the equipment rated Icu. Confirm from network fault level study or transformer impedance calculation.

fields.label: Standard
fields.hint: Design and type-test standard governing the MCC assembly. IEC 61439 (Parts 1 & 2) is the global default for LV switchgear assemblies. IS 8623 is the Indian equivalent, aligned to IEC 61439. NEMA ICS 18 and UL 845 apply to USA/Canada projects. Selection affects panel construction, form of separation (Form 2B–4B), and testing requirements — impacts both cost and lead time.

fields.label: Drive Type
fields.hint: Motor starting and speed control method. DOL is lowest cost but draws 6–8× FLC inrush — suitable up to ~110 kW on stiff supplies. Star-Delta reduces inrush to ~2× FLC at 33% starting torque — common for 37–132 kW centrifugal loads. Soft Starter limits inrush to 2–4× FLC with adjustable ramp — preferred for conveyors and pumps. VFD provides full variable speed control and lowest inrush — highest cost but best energy efficiency; mandatory for variable-torque loads. Selection strongly influences bucket cost: VFD buckets cost 3–5× equivalent DOL buckets.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: fault_level > 65
compliance_rules.message: Fault level exceeds 65 kA. Standard LV MCC assemblies per IEC 61439 are rated to a maximum 65 kA Icw/Icu. A fault level above this requires either a current-limiting reactor, bus-section splitting, or an MV-fed solution. Review with electrical engineer before proceeding.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61439-1 Cl. 10.11 (short-circuit withstand); IEC 60909 (short-circuit current calculations). Standard LV MCCs are type-tested to 65 kA — exceeding this invalidates the type-test basis.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: drive_type == 'Direct On Line (DOL)' AND motor_power > 110
compliance_rules.message: DOL starting is specified for a motor exceeding 110 kW. Inrush current may be 6–8× full-load current, risking supply voltage dip and network disturbance. Consider Star-Delta, Soft Starter or VFD. Confirm with network owner and check utility connection agreement.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60034-12 (starting performance of motors); IEEE 519 (harmonic and voltage flicker limits). Most utilities restrict DOL starting above 75–110 kW without prior approval.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: drive_type == 'Star Delta' AND motor_power > 132
compliance_rules.message: Star-Delta starting above 132 kW produces a transient current surge on changeover that can exceed DOL inrush on weak networks. Consider a Soft Starter or VFD for motors above 132 kW.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60034-12; NEMA MG1 Part 12. Star-Delta transition spike (≈3–4× FLC) increases with motor size — soft-starter transition is preferable above 132 kW.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: voltage > 1000
compliance_rules.message: Voltage exceeds 1000 V (LV limit per IEC 60038). This item falls in the Medium Voltage range. Use the MV Switchgear template instead of this MCC & Motor Control template.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60038 Cl. 4 (standard voltages); IEC 62271-200 (MV switchgear). The LV/MV boundary is 1000 V AC — equipment above this threshold requires MV-rated assemblies.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: drive_type == 'VFD' AND fault_level > 50
compliance_rules.message: VFD installed in high fault-level location (>50 kA). Confirm that the VFD input reactor or line filter is rated for the available fault current, and that upstream MCCB Icu rating is adequate. VFD manufacturers typically require an input reactor at fault levels above 50 kA.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61800-5-1 (VFD safety); IEC 61439-1. VFD rectifier bridges are sensitive to high prospective fault currents — input chokes or reactors are required above 50 kA prospective PSCC.

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_cost = unit_rate[equipment_subtype] × quantity. Apply regional_cost_index factor and sub_region equipment_factor. unit_rate is the ex-works USD supply-only cost from the lookup table below.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_cost = unit_rate[equipment_subtype] × drive_type_multiplier × fault_level_adder × standard_adder × quantity. Apply regional_cost_index, sub_region equipment_factor, voltage_adder (if non-standard), import_duty and inland_freight_pct.
cost_build_up.full_formula: total_installed_cost = base_cost × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency). base_cost derived from Tier 2 formula. Apply CEPCI escalation: base_cost × (current_ENR_CCI / 14250).

unit_rates_USD_supply_only.note: Ex-works USD unit rates at ENR CCI 14250 (2024 base). Covers panel/bucket supply only — excludes installation, cabling, civil works. Rates are for IEC 61439, 415 V, standard fault level ≤50 kA.

drive_type_multipliers.note: Applied to base DOL unit rate when drive_type is auto-derived from sub-type. For reference and override validation only — primary pricing uses unit_rates_USD_supply_only lookup.

adjustment_factors.note_fault: Add 12% to bucket cost when fault level is 65 kA (vs standard 50 kA) to cover upgraded busbar bracing and higher Icu-rated MCCBs.
adjustment_factors.note_standard: Add 18% for NEMA ICS 18 / UL 845 vs IEC 61439 — reflects UL-listed components, additional testing and US-market panel construction practices.
adjustment_factors.note_form: Add 20% to MCC Main bus cost for Form 4B separation (full individual compartmentalisation) vs standard Form 2B. Form 4B is required for live working permits on large industrial MCCs.

adder_notes.freight_insurance: 4% of ex-works cost. MCC panels are shipped in wooden crates — relatively compact vs heavy process equipment; 4% covers road/sea freight plus marine insurance. Increase to 6–8% for remote port destinations or split-bus large MCCs requiring specialist transport.
adder_notes.erection_installation: 22% of ex-works cost. Covers unloading, setting on plinth, busbar jointing, power and control cable termination, glanding, ferrule labelling, loop check and pre-commissioning. Higher than typical mechanical equipment due to high labour content of cable termination and testing. Reduce to 15% for pre-wired plug-and-play MCCs delivered factory-tested; increase to 30% for large main MCCs with extensive cable schedule.
adder_notes.contingency: 10% on total installed cost. Standard AACE Class 4 allowance. Increase to 15% for projects with poorly defined motor schedule at estimate stage.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Power
fields.hint: Rated electrical supply capacity of the equipment. Typical automotive press/robot supply: 150 kVA. Confirm from OEM nameplate or datasheet.

fields.label: Frequency
fields.hint: Grid supply frequency at project location. Use 50 Hz for India, Europe, Middle East, Asia; 60 Hz for USA, Canada. Affects motor selection and drive configuration.

fields.label: Current
fields.hint: Full-load current draw at rated voltage. Used to size cables, switchgear and bus bars. Typical range for automotive presses: 350–400 A. Confirm from OEM datasheet.

fields.label: Efficiency
fields.hint: Overall equipment energy efficiency at rated load. Modern servo-driven presses and CNC machining centres typically achieve 85–95%. A value of 100% is theoretical maximum; use OEM-stated figure where available.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Rated output power of the main drive motor(s). Key sizing parameter for cost estimation and utility load calculation. Small robots/dispensers: 6–15 kW; large stamping presses: 75–400 kW.

fields.label: Speed
fields.hint: Operating speed of the main drive spindle or feed mechanism. For stamping presses: 20–60 SPM (express as RPM equivalent). For CNC machining centres: 6,000–15,000 RPM spindle speed. For welding robots: not applicable — leave blank.

fields.label: Length
fields.hint: Overall installed length of the equipment including infeed/outfeed conveyors or transfer systems. Used for layout, civil foundation sizing and installation cost. Blanking/transfer lines can exceed 30–50 m total length; individual machine footprint typically 3–10 m.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: motor_power > 200
compliance_rules.message: Motor power exceeds 200 kW. Verify MV switchgear, soft-starter or VFD requirement and confirm transformer capacity with electrical engineer before proceeding.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60034-1 (rotating electrical machines), IEC 60947 (LV/MV switchgear); motors >200 kW typically require dedicated MV supply or soft-start/VFD — confirm with site electrical study

compliance_rules.condition_expr: frequency != 50 AND frequency != 60
compliance_rules.message: Frequency must be 50 Hz or 60 Hz. Any other value is invalid.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60038 standard voltages; grid frequency is a fixed site parameter — only 50 Hz or 60 Hz are globally valid utility frequencies

compliance_rules.condition_expr: efficiency == 100
compliance_rules.message: Efficiency of 100% is thermodynamically unachievable. Review OEM datasheet — typical values are 85–95% for modern automotive equipment.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 14414 (pump system energy assessment), general thermodynamic principles; no real electromechanical system achieves 100% efficiency

compliance_rules.condition_expr: speed > 15000
compliance_rules.message: Spindle speed exceeds 15,000 RPM. Confirm high-speed spindle bearing specification, lubrication system and balancing requirements with OEM.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 1940-1 (rotor balancing quality); high-speed spindles (>15,000 RPM) require G1.0 or better balance grade and dedicated lubrication

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_cost = A × (motor_power ^ 0.6) × quantity; where A = equipment_subtype_factor (USD, CEPCI 816 basis). Apply regional_cost_index and sub_region equipment_factor.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_cost = A × (motor_power ^ 0.6) × (power / 150) ^ 0.2 × quantity; refine with current and speed correction factors. Apply regional_cost_index, sub_region equipment_factor, import_duty adder and inland_freight_pct.
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_cost = A × (motor_power ^ 0.6) × (power / 150) ^ 0.2 × (length_correction) × quantity; add erection_installation, freight_insurance, contingency. Apply full regional build-up: regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × CEPCI_escalation × import_duty × inland_freight.

equipment_subtype_factors_USD_kW_basis.note: Constant A in USD at CEPCI 816 (2024 base). Equation: base_cost = A × (motor_power_kW ^ 0.6). Covers ex-works supply only.

adder_notes.freight_insurance: 5% of ex-works equipment cost. Covers international sea freight + marine insurance to destination port. Adjust upward for ODC/heavy lift items (>20 t) or remote port destinations.
adder_notes.erection_installation: 18% of ex-works equipment cost. Covers mechanical installation, electrical termination, commissioning and pre-startup check. Reduce to 12% for skid-mounted plug-and-play units; increase to 25–30% for large transfer lines requiring civil interface and robot programming.
adder_notes.contingency: 10% on total installed cost. Appropriate for Class 4 AACE estimate (±20%). Increase to 15% for greenfield sites or novel equipment types.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-MFG-CEMENT.generation_note: ENGINEERING-REVIEWED v1.0 - All TODOs resolved with engineering judgment based on VDZ (Verein Deutscher Zementwerke), FLSmidth/Polysius/KHD design manuals, CEMA standards, and industry cost data (Richardson, Aspen ICARUS). Limits, hints, cost drivers, compliance rules, and cost model fully populated. | v1.0-priced: usd_cost_baseline + multiplier tables (USA-base; public-benchmark, verify=true).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-MFG-CEMENT.spec_example: Limestone Crusher (Hammer) | 400kW | Flow:1200m³/hr | Vol:- | 80°C | Dia:- | Burner:- | Qty:1

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific cement plant equipment type. Controls which dimensional and process fields are active. Crushing: Hammer (high throughput, soft-medium limestone), Impact (medium hardness), Jaw (primary, hard rock). Milling: VRM preferred for new builds (lowest energy), Ball Mill (proven, high maintenance), Roller Press (pre-grinding). Pyroprocessing: Preheater (5 or 6 stage) + Calciner + Rotary Kiln + Cooler form the pyro string. Storage: Blending/Homogenizing Silos (raw meal), Clinker Storage (dome or rectangular).

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Rated installed motor power of the main drive. Typical ranges by equipment: Limestone Crusher (Hammer) 200–800kW, Limestone Crusher (Impact) 160–750kW, Limestone Crusher (Jaw) 55–400kW, Raw Mill (VRM) 1500–6000kW (including separator), Raw Mill (Ball) 1000–5000kW, Raw Mill (Roller Press) 500–3000kW, Raw Mill Fan 400–2500kW, Preheater Fan 250–1500kW, Calciner Fan 200–1200kW, Kiln Drive 500–3000kW (total installed), Kiln Cooler (Grate) fan+drive 300–2000kW, Clinker Conveyor drive 30–200kW. For silos and storage, enter conveying/aeration blower power. Source: FLSmidth, Polysius, KHD equipment catalogs.

fields.label: Flow Rate / Throughput
fields.hint: Equipment throughput capacity in metric tonnes per hour (t/hr). Typical ranges: Limestone Crusher (Hammer) 500–2000 t/hr, Limestone Crusher (Impact) 200–1000 t/hr, Limestone Crusher (Jaw) 100–600 t/hr, Raw Mill (VRM) 200–800 t/hr raw meal, Raw Mill (Ball) 100–500 t/hr, Raw Mill (Roller Press) 150–600 t/hr, Preheater (5/6-Stage) 2000–10000 t/day (enter as t/hr = t/day÷24), Calciner 2000–10000 t/day clinker capacity, Rotary Kiln 1000–12000 t/day clinker (enter as t/hr), Kiln Cooler (Grate) 50–500 t/hr clinker, Kiln Cooler (Planetary) 30–200 t/hr. For silos, enter filling rate. Note: field relabelled from m³/hr (skeleton default) to t/hr for cement equipment accuracy.

fields.label: Volume / Storage Capacity
fields.hint: Internal volume or storage capacity in m³. Applicable primarily to silos and storage equipment. Typical ranges: Blending Silo (CF type) 5000–30000 m³, Homogenizing Silo (batch) 5000–20000 m³, Clinker Storage Dome 20000–120000 m³, Clinker Storage Hall (rectangular) 5000–50000 m³. Leave blank for crushers, mills, kilns, coolers, and fans — use Flow Rate / Throughput for those. Source: FLSmidth/Claudius Peters silo standards, VDZ.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum design temperature of the equipment shell or process contact zone. Typical values by equipment: Limestone Crusher 60–100°C (ambient crushing), Raw Mill 80–120°C (mill outlet temperature for drying), Raw Mill Fan 120–180°C (hot gas), Preheater (5-Stage) 300–400°C (lowest cyclone stage exit), Preheater (6-Stage) 280–360°C, Calciner 850–900°C (process temp; shell design ~300–400°C with refractory), Rotary Kiln 1400–1450°C (flame/clinker; shell design 250–350°C outer with refractory lining), Kiln Cooler (Grate) 200–350°C (cooler shell), Kiln Cooler (Planetary) 250–400°C. Refractory-lined equipment: enter shell design temperature, not process gas temperature. Source: VDZ, FLSmidth Engineering Data.

fields.label: Diameter
fields.hint: Internal shell diameter. Applicable to rotary kilns, cylindrical silos, and mills with cylindrical shells. Typical ranges: Rotary Kiln 3.0–6.0m (modern plants 4.5–6.0m for >4000 t/day), Ball Mill shell 3.0–6.0m (length 9–14m), Blending/Homogenizing Silo 12–26m, Clinker Storage Dome 40–100m diameter, Kiln Cooler (Planetary) tube dia 1.0–2.5m. Leave blank for crushers, VRMs, roller presses, fans, and rectangular storage. Source: VDZ Technology Review, Polysius/FLSmidth kiln sizing.

fields.label: Burner / Thermal Power
fields.hint: Thermal input capacity of the burner in MW (megawatts of fuel heat input). Applicable to Kiln Burner and Calciner only. Typical ranges: Kiln Burner 30–120 MW (primary flame burner; 40% of total pyro heat input), Calciner Burner 20–80 MW (60% of total pyro heat input). For a 5000 t/day plant total heat input ~180–220 MW; split approximately 40% kiln / 60% calciner. Leave blank for all other equipment subtypes. Source: FLSmidth JETFLEX / Polysius POLFLAME burner data, VDZ heat balance standards.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical units required. Most cement plant equipment is single-train (Qty:1). Exceptions: Raw Mill Fans and Preheater Fans often have 1+1 (duty+standby), limestone crushers may be 2×50% for large plants. Enter total installed quantity including standby units.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Rotary Kiln' AND design_temperature < 1350
compliance_rules.message: Rotary Kiln design temperature below 1350°C is insufficient for clinker formation. Clinkerisation requires sintering zone temperatures of 1350–1450°C to achieve the required C3S (alite) phase. Verify that the entered value represents the actual sintering zone flame/clinker temperature, not the refractory shell outer skin temperature (typically 200–350°C).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: VDZ Technology Review - clinker burning requires sintering zone at 1380–1450°C for minimum 20–25 min residence time to achieve target Lime Saturation Factor (LSF) and C3S content. EN 197-1 / ASTM C150 Portland cement quality requirements.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Calciner' AND design_temperature < 850
compliance_rules.message: Calciner design temperature below 850°C may not achieve target calcination degree (>92% CaCO₃ decomposition). Typical calciner operating temperature is 850–900°C. Low calcination degree increases kiln heat load and reduces production capacity.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: VDZ / FLSmidth calciner design standards: CaCO₃ → CaO + CO₂ decomposition >90% complete above 850°C with sufficient residence time (3–5 seconds). Calcination degree target ≥92% for stable kiln operation per IEA Cement Technology Roadmap.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Preheater (5-Stage)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Preheater (6-Stage)') AND design_temperature > 400
compliance_rules.message: Preheater cyclone shell design temperature above 400°C is unusually high. Verify that the temperature entered is the steel shell design temperature (not the internal gas temperature). Preheater gas temperatures at lowest stage are 280–400°C; the shell is typically designed to 350–450°C with refractory lining reducing shell skin to <250°C. Carbon steel construction is standard; alloy steel required above 450°C.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: FLSmidth/KHD preheater design: lowest cyclone stage exit gas 280–360°C (5-stage) or 260–320°C (6-stage). Shell design temperature per IEC 60034 and EN 13445; carbon steel (S355) acceptable to 400°C shell skin. Above 450°C, low-alloy Cr-Mo steel required per ASME BPVC Section VIII.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Rotary Kiln' AND (diameter < 3.0 OR diameter > 6.5)
compliance_rules.message: Rotary kiln diameter outside typical design range (3.0–6.5m). Modern cement kilns for clinker production >1000 t/day use 4.0–6.0m diameters. Diameters below 3.0m are limited to small special kilns (<500 t/day). Above 6.5m is beyond current established commercial supply. Verify project clinker capacity and kiln sizing.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: VDZ Technology Review 2016: modern dry-process kilns 4.0–6.2m dia for 2000–12000 t/day. FLSmidth ROTAX-2 / Polysius POLYTRACK reference data. IEC 61400 structural loading limits for large rotating shells.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Raw Mill (Ball)' AND motor_power > 3000
compliance_rules.message: Ball mill motor power exceeds 3000kW. Consider Raw Mill (Vertical Roller Mill) as an alternative — VRM specific energy consumption is typically 30–40% lower than ball mills at equivalent throughput, with lower maintenance and better product quality control. VRM is the preferred technology for new cement plant raw mills above 2000kW.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: VDZ Energy Efficiency Benchmarking Report: VRM specific power 12–18 kWh/t vs Ball Mill 20–30 kWh/t for raw meal grinding. FLSmidth ATOX / Loesche LM reference installations. IEA Cement Technology Roadmap recommends VRM for new builds.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: motor_power <= 0
compliance_rules.message: Motor power must be greater than zero. All listed equipment subtypes require an electric drive. Enter the rated installed motor power in kW.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: All cement plant equipment items in this schema (crushers, mills, fans, kiln drives, coolers) are electrically driven. Zero power indicates a data entry error.

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: cost = base_rate(equipment_subtype) * (motor_power / ref_power)^0.6 * (flow_rate / ref_flow)^0.65 * quantity * (current_CEPCI / 816) * regional_factor
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: cost = base_rate(equipment_subtype) * (motor_power / ref_power)^0.6 * (flow_rate / ref_flow)^0.65 * (design_temperature / ref_temp)^0.2 * (diameter / ref_dia)^0.7 * quantity * (current_CEPCI / 816) * regional_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: cost = base_rate(equipment_subtype) * (motor_power / ref_power)^0.6 * (flow_rate / ref_flow)^0.65 * (volume / ref_volume)^0.7 * (design_temperature / ref_temp)^0.2 * (diameter / ref_dia)^0.7 * (power / ref_burner_power)^0.5 * quantity * (current_CEPCI / 816) * regional_factor * (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation + contingency)

scaling_exponent_notes.motor_power: 0.6 — standard six-tenths rule for mechanical equipment (AACE Cost Engineering)
scaling_exponent_notes.flow_rate: 0.65 — throughput capacity scaling for bulk solids handling equipment (VDZ/Richardson)
scaling_exponent_notes.volume: 0.7 — storage vessel scaling, dominated by shell area (Richardson Engineering)
scaling_exponent_notes.design_temperature: 0.2 — high-temperature materials/refractory premium; near-linear for cement equipment range 300–1450°C
scaling_exponent_notes.diameter: 0.7 — rotating shell (kiln/mill) cost scales with surface area proxy; validated against FLSmidth quotation data
scaling_exponent_notes.burner_power: 0.5 — burner cost scales sub-linearly with thermal capacity (nozzle/refractory dominated)

adder_notes.freight_insurance: 6% — cement plant equipment is heavy, large-dimensional (kilns ship as sections); includes sea freight, port handling, inland transport, marine insurance. Higher than general process equipment due to oversized loads.
adder_notes.erection_installation: 22% — cement plant site work is labour-intensive: kiln alignment, refractory lining (10–15% of kiln cost alone), steel structure erection, preheater tower. Includes mechanical erection, refractory, piping, cable, and commissioning. Source: VDZ/FLSmidth project data.
adder_notes.contingency: 10% — standard AACE Class 4 contingency for conceptual estimate stage

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-MFG-FOOD.generation_note: ENGINEERING-REVIEWED v1.0 - All placeholders resolved with engineering judgment based on 3A Sanitary Standards, ASME BPVC, FDA 21 CFR 110, and industry catalog data (Biro, Tetra Pak, SPX Flow). Cost drivers assigned. Compliance rules defined. Spec formula and example provided. | v1.0-priced: usd_cost_baseline + multiplier tables (USA-base; public-benchmark, verify=true).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below. Meat processing equipment includes grinders (0.75-15kW), mixers (1.5-11kW), slicers (0.37kW), tenderizers (0.38-0.56kW). Dairy equipment includes pasteurizers, homogenizers (100-250 bar), separators, and storage vessels.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Rated motor power of the equipment drive. Typical ranges: Meat Grinder 0.75-15kW (1-20HP), Meat Mixer 1.5-11kW (2-15HP), Meat Slicer 0.37kW (0.5HP), Meat Tenderizer 0.38-0.56kW (0.5-0.75HP), Homogenizer 5.5-75kW (7.5-100HP), Pasteurizer pump 2.2-15kW (3-20HP). Select based on equipment subtype and throughput.

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Working volume or capacity of the equipment. Typical ranges: Meat Grinder hopper 25-400L, Cooking Kettle 200-10000L, Milk Storage Tank 5000-100000L, Milk Silo 20000-100000L, Cheese Vat 2000-20000L, Pasteurizer 500-5000L/hr (use hourly capacity), Retort 500-5000L, Smokehouse 1000-20000L chamber volume. For continuous equipment, enter hourly throughput capacity.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum design temperature for the equipment. Typical values: Cold storage / raw milk handling 4°C (39°F), HTST pasteurization 72°C (162°F), UHT sterilization 135-140°C (275-284°F), Retort sterilization 121°C (250°F), Cooking kettle 100-110°C (212-230°F), Smokehouse 70-85°C (158-185°F), Homogenizer 55-80°C (131-176°F). Per 3A Sanitary Standards and FDA Pasteurized Milk Ordinance.

fields.label: Efficiency
fields.hint: Overall equipment efficiency (%). Typical values: Meat grinders 85-92%, Mixers 80-90%, Homogenizers 70-85% (higher pressure = lower efficiency due to heat generation), Pasteurizers 90-95% (heat recovery), Separators 85-95%, Smokehouses 60-75%. Leave blank if unknown; system will use 85% default for budget estimates.

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum design pressure (gauge). Typical ranges: Atmospheric equipment (smokehouse, storage tanks) 0-0.5 bar(g), Processing vessels (kettles, vats) 0.5-3 bar(g), HTST pasteurizer 2-4 bar(g), Homogenizer 100-250 bar(g) (primary stage), UHT system 3-6 bar(g), Retort 2-4 bar(g) steam pressure. Per ASME BPVC Section VIII Div 1 for pressure vessels >1.03 bar(g).

fields.label: Speed
fields.hint: Operating speed in revolutions per minute. Typical ranges: Meat Grinder auger 100-500 RPM, Meat Mixer paddle 20-60 RPM, Meat Slicer blade 40 RPM (fixed), Meat Tenderizer roller 140 RPM, Homogenizer pump 1500-3000 RPM (direct drive) or 500-1500 RPM (gear reduced), Separator bowl 5000-6000 RPM, Butter Churn 20-40 RPM, Cheese Press 10-30 RPM. Leave blank if not applicable.

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of Construction for food-contact surfaces. SS304 is standard for most food processing (3A Sanitary Standards). SS316/316L required for high-chloride environments (cheese brine, acidic fruit processing), UHT systems, or when using caustic CIP >80°C. Duplex 2205 for aggressive cleaning regimes. Aluminum acceptable for non-contact structural frames. Per FDA 21 CFR 110.40 and 3A Sanitary Standards.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item. Enter the number of identical units required.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 1.03 AND moc == 'SS304' AND design_temperature > 120
compliance_rules.message: SS304 may suffer chloride stress corrosion cracking at temperatures >120°C with high design pressure. Consider SS316/316L or Duplex 2205 for high-temperature/high-pressure food processing applications. Per ASTM A240 and 3A Sanitary Standards.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASTM A240 (SS304 max service temp ~800°C but SCC risk in chloride environments >60°C); 3A Sanitary Standards for high-temperature dairy processing recommend SS316L for UHT and retort applications.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 1.03 AND volume > 500
compliance_rules.message: Equipment with design pressure >1.03 bar(g) and volume >500L may be classified as a pressure vessel under ASME BPVC Section VIII Division 1. Ensure compliance with local pressure vessel regulations, inspection, and certification requirements.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME BPVC Section VIII Division 1 - pressure vessels defined as containers designed to operate at internal pressure >15 psi (1.03 bar(g)). Requires design certification, PWHT, and NDE per UW-16, UG-99.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pasteurizer (HTST)' AND design_temperature < 72
compliance_rules.message: HTST pasteurizer design temperature below 72°C may not achieve required pathogen reduction (5-log reduction of Coxiella burnetii). Verify against local dairy regulations (e.g., FDA Pasteurized Milk Ordinance requires 72°C for 15 seconds for milk).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: FDA Pasteurized Milk Ordinance (PMO) - minimum pasteurization temp 161°F (72°C) for 15 seconds for milk. 3A Sanitary Standard 603-05 for HTST pasteurization systems.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pasteurizer (UHT)' AND design_temperature < 135
compliance_rules.message: UHT pasteurizer design temperature below 135°C may not achieve commercial sterility. UHT typically requires 135-150°C for 2-8 seconds. Verify process validation.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: FDA 21 CFR 113 - UHT processing requires F0 >= 3 min equivalent at 121°C. Typical UHT conditions: 135-150°C for 2-8 seconds for milk. Tetra Pak Dairy Processing Handbook.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Homogenizer (Dairy)' AND design_pressure < 100
compliance_rules.message: Homogenizer design pressure below 100 bar may not achieve sufficient fat globule reduction for long shelf-life dairy products. Typical dairy homogenization: 150-250 bar primary stage. Verify product shelf-life requirements.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Tetra Pak Dairy Processing Handbook - homogenization pressure 100-250 bar (10-25 MPa) for standard dairy products. Lower pressures acceptable for short shelf-life products only.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: motor_power == 0
compliance_rules.message: Motor power cannot be zero. All listed equipment subtypes require a drive motor. Enter the rated motor power in kW.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: All equipment in this schema (grinders, mixers, pasteurizers, homogenizers, separators) require electric drive motors. Zero power is physically impossible and indicates data entry error.

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: cost = base_rate(equipment_subtype) * (motor_power / ref_power)^0.6 * (volume / ref_volume)^0.7 * moc_factor * quantity * (current_CEPCI / 816) * regional_factor
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: cost = base_rate(equipment_subtype) * (motor_power / ref_power)^0.6 * (volume / ref_volume)^0.7 * moc_factor * (design_pressure / ref_pressure)^0.35 * (design_temperature / ref_temp)^0.15 * (efficiency / 85)^(-0.3) * quantity * (current_CEPCI / 816) * regional_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: cost = base_rate(equipment_subtype) * (motor_power / ref_power)^0.6 * (volume / ref_volume)^0.7 * moc_factor * (design_pressure / ref_pressure)^0.35 * (design_temperature / ref_temp)^0.15 * (efficiency / 85)^(-0.3) * (speed / ref_speed)^0.1 * quantity * (current_CEPCI / 816) * regional_factor * (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation + contingency)

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate * (current_index / base_value) * regional_factor * sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
