Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use. Consumption tax (JCT) 10% applies on importation.

import_duty.components: ASEAN MFN avg 5% (varies by member state; ATIGA 0% intra-ASEAN)
import_duty.note: Indicative only. Duty rates vary significantly by member state. Verify with local customs authority. ATIGA preferential rates apply for ASEAN-origin equipment.

base_rates_USD.note: Purchased equipment cost (PEC) in USD at CEPCI 816 (2024 basis), USA Gulf Coast, for reference equipment at ref_power, ref_volume, ref_pressure, ref_temp. Scale using full_formula. Sources: Biro Manufacturing, Tetra Pak Dairy Processing Handbook, SPX Flow, Richardson Engineering, Aspen ICARUS.

moc_factors.note: Multiply base_rate_USD by moc_factor to adjust for material of construction. SS304 = 1.0 baseline. Sources: CCPS Guidelines for Chemical Process Quantitative Risk Analysis, Richardson Engineering, Sinnott & Towler Chemical Engineering Design.

SS304.basis: Baseline - 3A Sanitary Standard food grade, austenitic SS, most common food processing MOC

SS304L.basis: Low-carbon grade; slight premium for reduced sensitisation risk during welding; interchangeable with SS304 in most food apps

SS316.basis: Mo-alloyed grade; superior chloride/pitting resistance vs SS304; standard for cheese brine, acid fruit, CIP >80°C

SS316L.basis: Low-carbon SS316; preferred for UHT, retort, caustic CIP; avoids weld sensitisation; FDA 21 CFR 110 compliant

Duplex 2205.basis: Duplex austenitic-ferritic; highest chloride SCC resistance; for aggressive CIP / high-salt brine; higher weld complexity

Aluminum (Food Grade).basis: 6061-T6 / 5052-H32; lower material cost; used for non-wetted frames and panels; not suitable for alkaline CIP >pH 10 or acidic products

Mild Steel (Painted/Epoxy).basis: Carbon steel with FDA-approved epoxy or food-grade paint; structural/non-contact only; not acceptable for product-contact surfaces per 3A

Copper (Traditional).basis: Traditional material for butter churns and brewing kettles; antimicrobial properties; not permitted in many modern dairy facilities under 3A Sanitary Standards

SS304 with PTFE lining.basis: SS304 shell with PTFE/PVDF lining; used where non-stick or chemically inert contact surface required (e.g. high-sugar cooking, sticky products)

global_ref_values.note: Default reference values used in scaling formula when equipment-subtype-specific ref values are not available. Always prefer subtype-specific values from base_rates_USD.

usd_cost_baseline.note: USA-base USD (matches index base year 2024). Engine applies index_now/base x regional x sub_region x fx. Public-benchmark ESTIMATES, verify=true. base_rate at ref datum.

Separator (Cream).basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 85000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 115024.88. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Separator (Bacteria).basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 85000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 115024.88. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Milk Storage Tank.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 45000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 60895.52. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Milk Silo.basis: P&T-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 1200000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 1623880.6. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

SCHEMA-MFG-GENERAL.generation_note: AUTO-GENERATED SKELETON v5 - Regional cost index matrix (item_type × region). TODOs filled with engineering judgment 2026-05-02. | R02 promoted 0.5-skeleton->1.0-priced after structural completion + benchmark pricing (validator-gated; no remaining blocking errors).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-MFG-GENERAL.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | Motor: {motor_power}kW | {work_envelope} | CNC: {cnc_control_tier} | Automation: {automation_level} | Supply: {supply_voltage}V {supply_phase}Ph | Qty: {quantity} No.
SCHEMA-MFG-GENERAL.spec_example: CNC Lathe (Heavy Duty) | Motor: 22kW | Swing 500mm × Centre dist. 2000mm | CNC: Fanuc 0i-TF (Mid-range) | Automation: Manual load | Supply: 415V 3Ph | Qty: 1 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Main Motor Power
fields.hint: Rated power of the main drive motor (spindle motor for CNC/grinders; hydraulic power unit motor for press brake/pipe bender; cutting head motor for saws/shears). Typical ranges by subtype — Band Saw: 1.5–5.5kW; CNC Lathe/VMC: 7.5–37kW; HMC: 15–45kW; Boring Machine: 11–37kW; Press Brake: 4–22kW (hydraulic unit); EDM: 3–11kW; Laser: 3–30kW (excluding laser source); Plasma: 5–22kW; Water Jet: 18–45kW; Grinders: 2.2–15kW. Motor power is a primary cost-scaling parameter.

fields.label: Spindle Speed (Max)
fields.hint: Maximum spindle speed. Applicable to rotating-spindle machines only. CNC Lathe (heavy duty): 1,500–3,500 RPM; VMC: 8,000–12,000 RPM (standard), up to 24,000 RPM (high-speed); HMC: 6,000–10,000 RPM; Drilling (radial): 50–2,000 RPM; Milling (universal): 50–1,800 RPM; Boring: 10–1,500 RPM; Surface grinder (wheel): 1,500–3,000 RPM; Cylindrical grinder: 100–500 RPM work, 1,200–1,800 RPM wheel. Higher spindle speed = higher spindle bearing grade = higher cost.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype in ['CNC Lathe (Heavy Duty)', 'CNC Machining Center (Vertical)', 'CNC Machining Center (Horizontal)', 'Boring Machine (Horizontal)', 'Milling Machine (Universal)', 'Drilling Machine (Radial)', 'Surface Grinder', 'Cylindrical Grinder', 'Tool & Cutter Grinder']

fields.label: Electrical Supply Current (FLA)
fields.hint: Full-load ampere (FLA) draw at supply terminals for electrical infrastructure sizing (cable, MCB, isolator). If not available from datasheet, estimate as: FLA ≈ (motor_power_kW × 1000) / (√3 × supply_voltage_V × power_factor × efficiency). Typical PF 0.85, efficiency 0.92. Used for electrical supply planning, NOT for machine performance sizing. Range: 10A (small grinders/band saws at 415V) to 200A (large WMC/water jet at 415V).

fields.label: Hydraulic System Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum hydraulic system working pressure. Relevant only for hydraulically actuated machines: Press Brake (200–350 bar typical, up to 400 bar for high-tonnage), Pipe Bending Machine (150–250 bar), Rolling Machine with hydraulic clamping (100–200 bar), Shearing Machine (hydraulic type, 150–300 bar). Note: 4000 bar in library data appears erroneous — 4000 psi (≈275 bar) is likely the intended value. Verify against machine datasheet. Not applicable to CNC machining centres, grinders, saws, or EDM.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype in ['Press Brake', 'Bending Machine (Pipe)', 'Rolling Machine', 'Shearing Machine', 'Boring Machine (Horizontal)']

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of machines of this sub-type with identical specification. For machines with different specifications (e.g. two VMCs with different spindle speeds), create separate line items.

fields.label: Work Envelope / Key Capacity
fields.hint: Primary capacity dimension — the single biggest cost driver after motor power. Enter in free text matching the machine type: CNC Lathe: 'Swing Ø×mm, Centre dist. Ymm' (e.g. Ø500 × 2000mm); VMC: 'Table X×Ymm, Z-travel Zmm' (e.g. 1000×600mm, Z=600mm); HMC: 'Pallet size X×Ymm' (e.g. 630×630mm); Boring: 'Spindle dia. Xmm, X-travel Ymm'; Press Brake: 'Tonnage T, Bending length Lmm' (e.g. 160T × 3200mm); Shearing: 'Thickness×Width mm' (e.g. 8×3200mm); Rolling: 'Plate thickness × width mm'; Pipe Bending: 'Max pipe OD mm'; Band Saw: 'Blade length mm or throat depth mm'; Grinders: 'Table/chuck size mm or max Ø×length mm'; Laser/Plasma/Water Jet: 'Cutting bed X×Ymm'; EDM Wire: 'X×Y×Z travel mm'; EDM Die Sinker: 'Max workpiece mass kg, X×Y travel'.

fields.label: CNC Control Tier
options: High-end CNC (Fanuc 30i/31i, Siemens 840D sl, Heidenhain TNC 640)
Not applicable (EDM / Saw / Grinder / Press Brake)
fields.hint: CNC control system tier is a major price differentiator. Entry-level controls (GSK, Syntec) are common on Chinese-built machines and reduce cost by 20–40% vs mid-range. High-end controls (Fanuc 30i, Siemens 840D sl) add 15–30% premium over mid-range. 5-axis/RTCP capability adds further 25–50% premium. Select 'Not applicable' for non-CNC machines (manual mills, surface grinders, press brakes, band saws).

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype in ['CNC Lathe (Heavy Duty)', 'CNC Machining Center (Vertical)', 'CNC Machining Center (Horizontal)', 'EDM Machine (Wire Cut)', 'EDM Machine (Die Sinker)', 'Boring Machine (Horizontal)', 'Laser Cutting Machine', 'Plasma Cutting Machine', 'Water Jet Cutting']

fields.label: Machine Origin / Manufacturer Tier
options: Tier 1 - Premium (DMG Mori, Mazak, Okuma, Makino, Trumpf, Bystronic, ANCA)
Tier 2 - Mid-range (Haas, Doosan, Hwacheon, Amada, Hurco, Studer, Kellenberger)
Tier 3 - Economy - Asia Pacific (SMTCL, SHENYANG, Victor, Chevalier, Kent, Accuway)
Tier 4 - Local / Indian manufacture (HMT, ACE Designers, Jyoti CNC, Bharat Fritz Werner)
fields.default: Tier 2 - Mid-range (Haas, Doosan, Hwacheon, Amada, Hurco, Studer, Kellenberger)
fields.hint: Machine origin is the single largest cost driver after capacity — Tier 1 premium machines command 2–4× the price of equivalent Tier 3/4 machines. Tier 1: European/Japanese OEMs, ISO 9001 + ISO 10791, best geometric accuracy and longevity, highest spares/service cost. Tier 2: Korean/Taiwanese/US, good accuracy, established service networks. Tier 3: Chinese/Taiwanese economy, satisfactory for general workshop use. Tier 4: Indian domestically built, best for local content requirements, competitive in India. Select based on accuracy requirements, duty cycle, and spares strategy.

fields.label: Automation Level
fields.hint: Automation level adds significant capital cost but reduces direct labour. Bar feeder adds ~10–20% to CNC lathe price. APC 2-pallet adds ~15–25% to HMC/VMC price. Multi-pallet FMS adds 50–150%. Robot cell (standalone) adds USD 80,000–250,000 depending on robot payload. Select 'Manual load / unload' unless project scope explicitly includes automation. Affects installation complexity and civil/structural requirements (robot requires anchor bolts, safety fencing).

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype in ['CNC Lathe (Heavy Duty)', 'CNC Machining Center (Vertical)', 'CNC Machining Center (Horizontal)', 'Boring Machine (Horizontal)', 'Milling Machine (Universal)']

fields.label: Electrical Supply Voltage
fields.hint: Machine supply voltage as per nameplate. 415V 3-phase 50Hz is standard in India, UK, Australia, GCC. 400V 3-phase 50Hz is European standard. 480V 3-phase 60Hz is USA/Canada. 380V is common for Asian-manufactured machines shipped to 50Hz markets. Verify with machine datasheet — mismatched supply requires a transformer which adds cost and space. Small bench-top grinders and band saws may be 230V 1-phase.

fields.label: Laser / Plasma / Jet Source Power
fields.hint: Beam/source power for energy-based cutting machines. Laser: fibre laser source output power — 1kW (thin sheet), 2–6kW (general), 10–30kW (thick plate / high speed). This is distinct from motor_power (F-002) which covers ancillary drives. Plasma: plasma arc power in kW (relates to cutting current × arc voltage). Water Jet: pump pressure in MPa is the primary parameter — see design_pressure field. Not applicable to mechanical cutting machines.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype in ['Laser Cutting Machine', 'Plasma Cutting Machine']

fields.label: Press Brake / Shear Tonnage
fields.hint: Maximum press force / clamping force. Press Brake: typical range 40–3000 tonne-force; most common workshop sizes 80–400T. Shearing Machine: maximum shear force; select capacity to cut maximum plate thickness at full width (rule of thumb: 25T per mm thickness per metre width for mild steel). Bending Machine (Pipe): maximum pipe OD determines required ram force — use machine manufacturer's capacity chart. Not applicable to other machine types.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype in ['Press Brake', 'Shearing Machine', 'Bending Machine (Pipe)', 'Rolling Machine']

fields.label: Number of CNC Axes
fields.hint: Number of controlled CNC axes. 3-axis VMC/lathe is baseline. 4-axis adds a rotary table (B or A axis). 5-axis simultaneous is required for complex aerospace/mould components and carries a significant cost premium (typically 60–120% more than equivalent 3-axis). Turning centres with live tooling are effectively 3-axis (X, Z + C). Multi-axis HMCs (B-axis table) are 4-axis. Select 'Not applicable' for manual/conventional machines.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype in ['CNC Lathe (Heavy Duty)', 'CNC Machining Center (Vertical)', 'CNC Machining Center (Horizontal)', 'Boring Machine (Horizontal)', 'Milling Machine (Universal)']

compliance_rules.condition_expr: motor_power > 0.37 AND (supply_voltage == '230V 1-phase 50Hz' OR supply_voltage IS None) AND motor_power > 2.2
compliance_rules.message: Motors above 2.2kW should not be connected to a 1-phase supply. Specify a 3-phase supply. Single-phase supply for large motors causes high starting current, vibration, and overheating.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60034-1:2017 — Rotating electrical machines; IEC 60364-5-52 — Cable sizing for motor circuits. Standard practice: motors >2.2kW require 3-phase supply.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype in ['CNC Machining Center (Vertical)', 'CNC Machining Center (Horizontal)', 'CNC Lathe (Heavy Duty)'] AND (cnc_control_tier == 'Manual / NC only (no CNC)' OR cnc_control_tier == 'Not applicable (EDM / Saw / Grinder / Press Brake)')
compliance_rules.message: CNC control tier is marked as 'Manual/NC' for a CNC machine sub-type. Verify selection — CNC machining centres and CNC lathes require a CNC control system. If a manual machine is intended, select a different equipment sub-type.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 10791-1:2015 — Test conditions for machining centres. CNC designation implies closed-loop control with programmable axes per ISO 2806.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype in ['Laser Cutting Machine'] AND (source_power IS None OR source_power < 1.0)
compliance_rules.message: Laser source power not specified or below 1kW. Laser cutting machines require the fibre/CO₂ laser source output power to be defined for cost estimation and electrical supply sizing. Minimum practical cutting power is 1kW.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60825-1:2014 — Safety of laser products. Laser source power is the primary cost driver for laser cutting machines (source typically 40–60% of total machine cost).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 400
compliance_rules.message: Hydraulic system pressure exceeds 400 bar. This is unusually high for standard machine tool hydraulics. Verify units — the library value of 4000 may be in psi (≈276 bar) rather than bar. Confirm with machine datasheet before proceeding.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 4413:2010 — Hydraulic fluid power, general rules for systems. Standard machine tool hydraulic systems operate at 100–350 bar. Systems above 350 bar require high-pressure rated components and specialist commissioning.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: supply_voltage == '380V 3-phase 50Hz'
compliance_rules.message: Machine supply voltage is 380V but site standard is 415V. A step-down transformer or autotransformer will be required. Add transformer to BOQ and allow space on foundation drawing. Typical transformer efficiency loss 1–2%.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60038:2009 — Standard voltages. 415V (IEC preferred) vs 380V (older IEC standard, still common for Asian-manufactured machines). BS 7671:2018 permits ±10% voltage tolerance but sustained 380V→415V operation without transformation is inadvisable.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Laser Cutting Machine' AND source_power > 0 AND (cnc_control_tier == 'Manual / NC only (no CNC)' OR cnc_control_tier == 'Not applicable (EDM / Saw / Grinder / Press Brake)')
compliance_rules.message: Laser cutting machines are always CNC-controlled for beam path management and safety interlock compliance. Manual laser cutting is not an acceptable configuration. Update CNC control tier.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60825-1:2014 — Laser safety requires interlocked enclosures and controlled beam management that mandates CNC control. EN ISO 11553-1:2020 — Safety of machinery, laser processing machines.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: automation_level in ['Robot cell (standalone robot + machine)', 'Gantry loader / pick-and-place'] AND quantity > 1
compliance_rules.message: Multiple machines with robot/gantry automation specified. Confirm whether each machine has a dedicated robot or whether a single robot serves multiple machines (multi-machine tending cell). Multi-machine cells require different civil/structural scope and a separate robot cell line item.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 10218-2:2011 — Robots and robotic devices — Safety requirements for industrial robot systems and integration. Cell design must be assessed per ISO/TS 15066 for collaborative robots.

cost_build_up.notes: Manufacturing machines are discrete capital items purchased as complete units. Cost is estimated as a lump-sum supply price per machine (ex-works), scaled by manufacturer tier factor, then adjusted for installation, freight, and regional factors. The equipment_equation method is not appropriate here — there is no continuous power-law relationship across dissimilar machine types in this group.
cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_supply_price(equipment_subtype, manufacturer_tier) × quantity × regional_factor × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_supply_price(equipment_subtype, manufacturer_tier) × work_envelope_factor × cnc_control_factor × automation_adder × quantity × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_supply_price(equipment_subtype, manufacturer_tier) × work_envelope_factor × cnc_control_factor × cnc_axes_factor × automation_adder × source_power_factor × quantity × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

indicative_base_supply_prices_usd_tier1_.note: USD ex-works, Tier 1 Premium manufacturer, standard capacity, 2024 basis. Multiply by manufacturer_tier_factor, cnc_control_factor, and axes_factor to get adjusted supply price.

notes.freight_insurance: 5% covers sea freight from origin + marine insurance. Heavy/large machines (boring mills, HMCs) may require special OOG (out-of-gauge) shipping — add 3–5% surcharge for machines >5 tonnes or >3m in any dimension.
notes.erection_installation: 12% covers unloading, positioning on foundation, levelling, grouting, electrical connection, pneumatic/hydraulic connection, commissioning, and operator training. CNC machines with automation (robot cells, APC) should use 18–22% due to integration and safety fencing scope. Manual conventional machines (band saws, drilling machines) are at the low end (8–10%).

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-MFG-STEEL.generation_note: SKELETON v5 — All placeholders resolved. Cost method: equipment_equation (power-law). Base rates: USD 2024 basis (SMS/Primetals/Danieli/World Steel benchmarks). BF exponent 0.60 @ 5,000 m³ ref; Sinter Plant exponent 0.70 @ 500 m² ref. Freight 5%, installation 45%. | R02 promoted 0.5->1.0-priced after structural completion + benchmark pricing (validator-gated; no remaining blocking errors).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-MFG-STEEL.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | Vol: {volume} m³ | Area: {area} m² | Qty: {quantity} No.
SCHEMA-MFG-STEEL.spec_example: Blast Furnace | Vol: 5000 m³ | Area: N/A | Qty: 1 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Internal working volume of the Blast Furnace (useful volume, m³). Applicable only to Blast Furnace sub-type. Library reference value: 5,000 m³ (large BF, ~5 Mt/yr hot metal output). Typical range: 1,500 m³ (small BF) to 5,500 m³ (world-class large BF). Leave blank for all other equipment sub-types — rolling mills, furnaces, and casters are sized by output tonnage, not volume.

fields.label: Area
fields.hint: Effective sintering strand area (m²). Applicable to Sinter Plant sub-type. Library reference value: 500 m² (large sinter plant, ~6 Mt/yr sinter output). Typical range: 100 m² (small plant) to 600 m² (world-class plant). Leave blank for all other equipment sub-types. Note: strand area = effective strand length × strand width (e.g. 100 m × 5 m = 500 m²).

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: volume IS not None AND volume > 0 AND equipment_subtype != "Blast Furnace"
compliance_rules.message: Volume (m³) has been entered for a non-Blast Furnace sub-type. In this template, vessel volume is a sizing parameter applicable only to Blast Furnace. For all other sub-types (rolling mills, furnaces, casters), leave Volume blank and use output tonnage or strand area as appropriate. Verify input before proceeding.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Steel plant equipment sizing convention: Blast Furnace capacity is universally expressed as useful inner volume (m³) per ISO 4700 and SMS/Primetals design practice. Rolling mills, EAF/BOF, continuous casters, and secondary metallurgy units are sized by annual throughput (t/yr), heat size (t/heat), or strand dimensions — not vessel volume. Ref: AISI/World Steel Association Steel Statistical Yearbook; Primetals Technologies Ironmaking Reference Manual 2023.

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_rate_usd = {"Blast Furnace": 350000000, "Coke Oven Battery": 180000000, "Sinter Plant": 120000000, "Pellet Plant": 200000000, "Basic Oxygen Furnace (BOF)": 150000000, "Electric Arc Furnace (EAF)": 80000000, "Ladle Furnace (LF)": 15000000, "Vacuum Degasser (VD)": 25000000, "Vacuum Oxygen Decarburizer (VOD)": 30000000, "Argon Oxygen Decarburizer (AOD)": 20000000, "Continuous Caster (Slab)": 120000000, "Continuous Caster (Bloom)": 80000000, "Continuous Caster (Billet)": 50000000, "Hot Strip Mill": 600000000, "Plate Mill": 350000000, "Section Mill": 200000000, "Bar & Rod Mill": 150000000, "Wire Rod Mill": 180000000, "Cold Rolling Mill (Tandem)": 400000000, "Cold Rolling Mill (Reversible)": 150000000}[equipment_subtype]; equipment_cost = base_rate_usd * quantity
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: volume_ref = 5000.0; area_ref = 500.0; capacity_factor = (  equipment_subtype === "Blast Furnace" && volume != null && volume > 0     ? Math.pow(volume / volume_ref, 0.60)   : equipment_subtype === "Sinter Plant" && area != null && area > 0     ? Math.pow(area / area_ref, 0.70)   : 1.0); equipment_cost_t2 = base_rate_usd * quantity * capacity_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: total_equipment_cost = base_rate_usd * quantity * capacity_factor; installed_cost = total_equipment_cost * (1 + freight_insurance) * (1 + erection_installation) * (1 + contingency) * regional_cost_index_factor * equipment_sub_region_factor

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-MFG-TEXTILE.generation_note: AUTO-GENERATED SKELETON v5 - Regional cost index matrix (item_type × region). Search TODO for engineering input. | R02 promoted 0.5-skeleton->1.0-priced after structural completion + benchmark pricing (validator-gated; no remaining blocking errors).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-MFG-TEXTILE.spec_example: Blow Room Line | 110 kW | - | Eff: -% | Qty: 1 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Total installed motor power for the machine or line (kW). This is the primary within-type sizing parameter and the main continuous cost driver. Typical ranges by sub-type: Blow Room Line 75–250 kW (entire opening-cleaning-blending line); Carding Machine 5.5–15 kW; Draw Frame 3–7.5 kW; Comber 5.5–11 kW; Speed Frame 5.5–15 kW; Ring Spinning Frame 3–15 kW (per machine, not per spindle); Open End Spinning 11–55 kW; Air Jet Spinning 15–75 kW; Winding Machine 3–11 kW; Doubling / Twisting Machine 5.5–22 kW; Warping Machine 7.5–30 kW; Sizing Machine 30–200 kW (includes steam/electric heating drive); Loom (Shuttle) 0.75–3 kW; Loom (Projectile/Rapier/Air Jet/Water Jet) 3–11 kW. Values seen in library: 3, 5.5, 7.5, 11, 15 kW.

fields.label: Efficiency
fields.hint: Machine efficiency (%) — ratio of actual production output to theoretical maximum output at rated speed, accounting for piecing losses, doffing stops, yarn breaks, and maintenance downtime. Applies primarily to spinning machines (Ring Spinning Frame, Open End Spinning, Air Jet Spinning). Typical values: Ring Spinning Frame 85–95% (modern auto-doffing); Open End Spinning 88–96% (low piecing loss); Air Jet Spinning 90–97% (very low end-down rate); Speed Frame 88–94%. Leave blank for preparatory machines (Carding, Draw Frame, Blow Room) and weaving machines where production rate is entered as Speed (picks/min). The library observed value of 18% is atypical — verify whether this represents a specific spindle yield metric rather than machine efficiency before use.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype in ['Ring Spinning Frame', 'Open End Spinning', 'Air Jet Spinning', 'Speed Frame (Roving)', 'Winding Machine', 'Doubling Machine', 'Twisting Machine']

fields.label: Speed
fields.hint: Characteristic operating speed of the machine (RPM or picks/min as noted). The meaning differs by sub-type — enter the value appropriate to the equipment selected: Ring Spinning Frame: spindle speed (RPM), typically 12,000–25,000 RPM; Open End Spinning: rotor speed (RPM), typically 80,000–130,000 RPM; Air Jet Spinning: equivalent twist-insertion speed (RPM), typically 150,000–200,000 RPM; Winding Machine: drum/spindle speed (RPM), typically 800–1,800 RPM; Warping Machine (Direct/Sectional) / Beam Warping: beam speed (m/min is preferred but enter as RPM if known), typically 400–1,200 RPM drum equivalent; Loom (Shuttle): picks/min, typically 180–280 picks/min (enter as RPM field); Loom (Projectile): picks/min 200–400; Loom (Rapier): picks/min 300–600; Loom (Air Jet): picks/min 600–1,200; Loom (Water Jet): picks/min 400–900. Leave blank for Blow Room Line, Carding Machine, Draw Frame, Comber, Speed Frame, Sizing Machine — speed is not a user-specified cost driver for those items. Values seen in library: 1,200 (loom picks/min); 10,000; 25,000 (OE rotor); 150,000; 200,000 (air-jet nozzle equivalent).

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype in ['Ring Spinning Frame', 'Open End Spinning', 'Air Jet Spinning', 'Winding Machine', 'Warping Machine (Direct)', 'Warping Machine (Sectional)', 'Beam Warping', 'Loom (Shuttle)', 'Loom (Projectile)', 'Loom (Rapier)', 'Loom (Air Jet)', 'Loom (Water Jet)']

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item. For ring spinning frames, looms, and winding machines this is typically the number of individual machine units (not spindles or positions). For Blow Room Line and Sizing Machine, quantity is usually 1 per production line.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Ring Spinning Frame' AND (speed < 8000 OR speed > 25000)
compliance_rules.message: Ring spinning frame spindle speed must be between 8,000 and 25,000 RPM. Values below 8,000 RPM indicate sub-economic output; above 25,000 RPM is beyond the design limit of standard spindle bearings and traveller systems for staple fibre yarn.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 1370 / ISO 2456 — textile machinery performance standards; OEM design limits for ring spinning frames (Rieter G38, LMW LR60, Toyota RX300 series); spindle bearing fatigue limits per USTER Statistics

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Open End Spinning' AND (speed < 50000 OR speed > 135000)
compliance_rules.message: Open-end rotor speed must be between 50,000 and 135,000 RPM. Below 50,000 RPM gives unacceptably low yarn twist; above 135,000 RPM exceeds standard rotor bearing design limits and risks catastrophic rotor failure.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 10283 — open-end spinning rotor speed classification; OEM limits for Rieter R37, Saurer BD series, Schlafhorst Autocoro rotors; rotor bearing DN limit (diameter × speed) constraints

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Blow Room Line' AND motor_power < 30
compliance_rules.message: Blow Room Line motor power below 30 kW is very low for a complete opening-cleaning-blending line. A full blow room line (bale opener + cleaner + mixer + fine opener + condenser) typically requires 75–250 kW. Confirm this is not a single machine within a blow room rather than a complete line.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Textile plant engineering norms (Rieter, Truetzschler blow room system specifications); typical installed power for 500–1000 kg/hr cotton processing

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Loom (Water Jet)' AND motor_power > 5.5
compliance_rules.message: Water jet loom motor power above 5.5 kW per machine is unusually high. Standard water jet looms are compact and typically rated 1.5–3.7 kW per unit. Verify this is not the total installed power for a bank of looms.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ITMA loom specification benchmarks; Tsudakoma ZW series / Toyota LWT series rated power data; ISO 8602 textile machinery safety standard

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Air Jet Spinning' AND speed < 100000
compliance_rules.message: Air jet spinning nozzle-equivalent speed below 100,000 RPM is unusually low for this technology. Air-jet spinning operates at 150,000–200,000 RPM equivalent twist insertion rates. Values below 100,000 RPM may indicate a data entry error or confusion with ring spinning speed.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Murata Vortex Spinner (MVS) and Rieter J 26 air-jet spinning OEM technical data; twist insertion rate mechanics for fasciated yarn formation

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Sizing Machine' AND motor_power < 30
compliance_rules.message: Sizing machine motor power below 30 kW may be undersized. A complete sizing machine (with wet zone, drying cylinders, and beam winding) typically requires 30–200 kW depending on working width and heating method (steam or electric). Confirm if this is a laboratory-scale or narrow-width machine.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Karl Mayer and Benninger sizing machine specifications; ITMA 2023 exhibitor technical data; typical installed power for 1.8–3.6 m working width sizing ranges

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Loom (Air Jet)' AND speed > 1200
compliance_rules.message: Air jet loom speed above 1,200 picks/min is at the upper limit of current production machines. Confirm this is achievable with the intended yarn count and fabric construction. Higher speeds require premium air compressor capacity and increase energy cost significantly.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Toyota JAT series / Tsudakoma ZAX series air jet loom rated speeds; ATIRA energy audit norms for air jet weaving; compressed air consumption at high picks/min per USTER Airjet benchmarks

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype, motor_power) × speed_tier_multiplier(equipment_subtype, speed) × quantity × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100)
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype, motor_power) × speed_tier_multiplier(equipment_subtype, speed) × quantity × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

base_unit_cost_USD.note: USD supply-only cost per machine unit, ex-works Europe/Japan OEM, CEPCI 816 (2024). For Blow Room Line, cost is per complete line. Interpolate linearly within power bands.

speed_tier_multiplier.note: Applied at Tier 2. Accounts for higher-speed variants commanding a price premium due to more precise bearings, higher-grade electronic control, and tighter manufacturing tolerances.

adder_notes.freight_insurance: 4% — textile machinery is shipped in purpose-built OEM wooden crates/frames, well-protected; standard sea freight + marine insurance for precision machinery. Slightly lower than heavy process equipment due to lower breakage risk and established shipping lanes (Europe/Japan → India/ASEAN).
adder_notes.erection_installation: 13% — includes machine levelling, foundation bolt setting, drive belt/chain tensioning, pneumatic/electrical hook-up, thread-up, and OEM-supervised commissioning trial runs. Lower than process plant (18–20%) because textile machines are modular, plug-and-play units with no process piping. Does not include civil works for false flooring, humidity control HVAC, or overhead creel structures.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-MV-CABLE.generation_note: AUTO-GENERATED SKELETON v5 - Regional cost index matrix (item_type × region). TODOs filled with engineering judgment 2026-05-02. | R02 promoted 0.5-skeleton->1.0-priced after structural completion + benchmark pricing (validator-gated; no remaining blocking errors).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-MV-CABLE.spec_example: Medium Voltage Cable (33kV - XLPE) | 33kV | 185mm² | 3C | XLPE | PVC | SWA | Qty: 500m

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: System Voltage
fields.hint: Nominal system voltage (Uo/U). Select from standard IEC voltage levels: 1.1kV (LV), 6.6kV, 11kV, or 33kV. Must match switchgear/transformer secondary rating.

fields.label: Rated Current Capacity
fields.hint: Continuous current-carrying capacity (ampacity) at 90°C conductor temperature, installed in ground (touching trefoil). Typical range for MV cables: 200–630A. Verify with IEC 60287 or manufacturer data for actual installation conditions.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype in ['Medium Voltage Cable (11kV - XLPE)', 'Medium Voltage Cable (33kV - XLPE)', 'Medium Voltage Cable (6.6kV - XLPE)', 'HV XLPE Cable 11kV 3×185mm²', 'LV Power Cable 1.1kV 3.5×240mm²', 'Flexible Cable (VFD/Motor Leads)']

fields.label: Conductor Cross-Section
fields.hint: Standard IEC 60228 Class 2 stranded conductor sizes. Select based on ampacity and voltage-drop calculations. 185mm² is typical for 11kV feeders; 240–300mm² for high-load MV circuits. For LV: 240mm² standard. For control/instrumentation: 1.5–2.5mm².

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype not in ['Fibre Optic Cable Multimode 24 Core', 'Thermocouple Extension Cable Type K 2×1.5mm²', 'MV Cable Termination Kit (11kV - Indoor)', 'MV Cable Termination Kit (33kV - Indoor)', 'MV Cable Joint Kit (11kV)']

fields.label: Design Standard
fields.hint: Primary design/manufacturing standard. IEC 60502-2 applies to most MV cables up to 30kV. Use IEC 60840 for 33kV and above in some jurisdictions. Cross-check with project specification and local utility requirements.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item. For cables, enter total route length in metres (field MV-CABLE-F-007). This field counts discrete items (e.g. termination kits, joint kits). For cable runs, set to 1 and use length field.

fields.label: Cable Route Length
fields.hint: Total installed cable length including all bends, vertical drops, and a 10% wastage/overlap allowance. Measure route centre-line on drawings. Add 2–3m at each end for termination tails.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype in ['Medium Voltage Cable (11kV - XLPE)', 'Medium Voltage Cable (33kV - XLPE)', 'Medium Voltage Cable (6.6kV - XLPE)', 'HV XLPE Cable 11kV 3×185mm²', 'LV Power Cable 1.1kV 3.5×240mm²', 'Control Cable 1.1kV 12C×2.5mm²', 'Instrumentation Cable Multicore Screened 12C×1.5mm²', 'Thermocouple Extension Cable Type K 2×1.5mm²', 'Fibre Optic Cable Multimode 24 Core', 'Flexible Cable (VFD/Motor Leads)']

fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - always visible for cable items

fields.label: Number of Cores
fields.hint: Number of current-carrying conductors. MV power cables: 3-core (three-phase) standard. LV power: 3.5C (3 phase + half-neutral) or 4C. Control: 7C, 10C, 12C, 19C. Instrumentation: 12C, 24C (paired). Thermocouple: 2C.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype not in ['MV Cable Termination Kit (11kV - Indoor)', 'MV Cable Termination Kit (33kV - Indoor)', 'MV Cable Joint Kit (11kV)', 'Fibre Optic Cable Multimode 24 Core']

fields.label: Insulation Type
fields.hint: Insulation material determines max conductor temperature and voltage rating. XLPE (90°C) is the industry standard for MV cables per IEC 60502-2. EPR (90°C) offers superior flexibility for cables with repeated flexing. PVC (70°C) is restricted to LV ≤ 1.1kV. LSZH required in enclosed/occupied spaces per local fire codes.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype not in ['Fibre Optic Cable Multimode 24 Core', 'MV Cable Termination Kit (11kV - Indoor)', 'MV Cable Termination Kit (33kV - Indoor)', 'MV Cable Joint Kit (11kV)']

fields.label: Armour Type
fields.hint: SWA is standard for direct buried and exposed installations. AWA preferred for single-core MV cables (avoids eddy current losses in steel). Unarmoured acceptable for cables fully enclosed in conduit/tray with no mechanical damage risk. DSTA for high mechanical protection zones.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype not in ['MV Cable Termination Kit (11kV - Indoor)', 'MV Cable Termination Kit (33kV - Indoor)', 'MV Cable Joint Kit (11kV)', 'Fibre Optic Cable Multimode 24 Core', 'Flexible Cable (VFD/Motor Leads)']

fields.label: Outer Sheath Material
fields.hint: PVC outer sheath is standard for most installations. Specify LSZH for tunnels, substations, cable basements, and any enclosed spaces with restricted egress per IEC 60332 / BS 7671. PE sheath for direct buried with UV exposure. CSP for chemical/oil-resistant environments.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype not in ['MV Cable Termination Kit (11kV - Indoor)', 'MV Cable Termination Kit (33kV - Indoor)', 'MV Cable Joint Kit (11kV)']

fields.label: Installation Method
fields.hint: Installation method determines ampacity derating, civil works scope, and labour rate. Direct buried requires sand bedding and cable tiles; budget 40–60% extra on civil vs tray. Duct provides future pull-through capability. Select predominant method for the run.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype not in ['MV Cable Termination Kit (11kV - Indoor)', 'MV Cable Termination Kit (33kV - Indoor)', 'MV Cable Joint Kit (11kV)']

fields.label: Conductor Material
fields.hint: Copper is preferred for MV cables due to superior conductivity and termination reliability. Aluminium is 40–50% cheaper by weight and used for large LV feeders (≥ 95mm²) where weight/cost savings justify extra termination care. Check utility/client specification before specifying aluminium for MV.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype not in ['Fibre Optic Cable Multimode 24 Core', 'MV Cable Termination Kit (11kV - Indoor)', 'MV Cable Termination Kit (33kV - Indoor)', 'MV Cable Joint Kit (11kV)', 'Thermocouple Extension Cable Type K 2×1.5mm²']

fields.label: Screen / Shield Type
fields.hint: MV cables (≥ 3.6kV) require individual conductor screening per IEC 60502-2 to control electric field distribution and prevent partial discharge. Control/instrumentation cables use collective foil screen for EMI rejection. LV power cables ≤ 1.1kV: screen optional unless in noisy environments.

fields.label: Number of Fibre Optic Cores
fields.hint: Multimode 24-core is standard for industrial plant LAN, SCADA backbone, and substation PSCADA comms. OM3/OM4 50/125µm recommended for runs > 300m. Single-mode (OS2 9/125µm) for runs > 2km or future-proofing. Always specify spare capacity ≥ 50% of used cores.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype == 'Fibre Optic Cable Multimode 24 Core'

compliance_rules.condition_expr: system_voltage > 30 AND standard == 'IEC 60502-2 (MV 1kV–30kV)'
compliance_rules.message: IEC 60502-2 applies to cables up to 30kV. For 33kV systems, consider IEC 60840 or verify cable Uo/U rating (e.g. 19/33kV) is within IEC 60502-2 scope.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60502-2:2014 Clause 1 (Scope) — rated voltage up to and including 30kV. IEC 60840 covers 30kV–150kV.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: system_voltage > 0 AND (system_voltage < 1.1 OR (system_voltage > 1.1 AND system_voltage < 6.6) OR (system_voltage > 6.6 AND system_voltage < 11.0) OR (system_voltage > 11.0 AND system_voltage < 33.0))
compliance_rules.message: Non-standard system voltage entered. Standard IEC voltage levels for this template are 1.1kV, 6.6kV, 11kV, and 33kV. Verify with system single-line diagram.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60038:2009 — Standard voltages. Table 3 (MV): 6.6kV, 11kV, 33kV are preferred standard values in the 1kV–35kV range.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: current > 0 AND conductor_size == 185 AND current > 340
compliance_rules.message: Rated current exceeds typical ampacity of 3×185mm² Cu XLPE cable (340A in ground, trefoil). Upsize conductor or apply grouping derating. Verify per IEC 60287.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60287-1-1:2006 — current capacity calculations. Typical 3×185mm² Cu XLPE SWA, 90°C, direct buried at 20°C soil: ~340A. Grouping/depth derating per IEC 60287-2-1.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: system_voltage >= 3.6 AND insulation_type == 'PVC (Polyvinyl Chloride)'
compliance_rules.message: PVC insulation is not permitted for MV cables ≥ 3.6kV. PVC dielectric strength is insufficient for medium voltage service. Use XLPE or EPR.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60502-2:2014 — applies only to cables with extruded XLPE or EPR insulation rated 3.6kV to 30kV. PVC is covered only under IEC 60502-1 for voltages ≤ 1kV.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: system_voltage >= 3.6 AND (screen_type == 'None')
compliance_rules.message: MV cables ≥ 3.6kV must have individual conductor screening to ensure uniform electric field distribution and prevent partial discharge. Screen is mandatory per IEC 60502-2.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60502-2:2014 Clause 9 — metallic screen over insulation required for cables rated 3.6kV and above.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: conductor_material == 'Aluminium (Al)' AND conductor_size < 95
compliance_rules.message: Aluminium conductor below 95mm² is not recommended. Small aluminium conductors are prone to oxide layer formation and termination failure. Use copper for conductors < 95mm².
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60228:2004 — Class 2 aluminium conductors start at 16mm², but industry practice discourages use below 95mm² due to termination reliability concerns (AS/NZS 3000:2018 commentary, NEC 310.14 aluminium conductor use).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: installation_method == 'Direct buried in ground' AND armour_type == 'Unarmoured'
compliance_rules.message: Unarmoured cables installed direct buried in ground are at risk of mechanical damage. Consider SWA armour or install in concrete-encased duct.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60364-5-52:2009 Table B.52.1 — direct burial installation requires protection against mechanical damage. BS 7671:2018 Regulation 522.8.

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: unit_rate_per_metre(equipment_subtype, system_voltage, conductor_size=185) × route_length × quantity × regional_factor
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: unit_rate_per_metre(equipment_subtype, system_voltage, conductor_size, conductor_material, armour_type, insulation_type) × route_length × quantity × installation_method_factor × regional_factor × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)
cost_build_up.full_formula: supply_rate(equipment_subtype, system_voltage, conductor_size, conductor_material, no_of_cores, armour_type, insulation_type, sheath_material, screen_type) × route_length × quantity × installation_method_factor × terrain_factor × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + contingency)

notes.unit_rate_basis: Supply-only rate per metre, ex-works. Erection covers pull-through, clamping, termination at both ends, pressure test, and as-built documentation. Termination kits and joint kits are lump-sum items — use quantity × kit_unit_rate, not length.

indicative_supply_rates_usd_per_metre.note: USD/m supply-only, ex-works, 2024 basis. Multiply by regional_factor and apply formula above.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: Switched ENR_CCI -> CEPCI: supply-dominated material item (copper/XLPE commodity content dominates cost; install labour minor share). CEPCI base 816 (2024) per admin canonical.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Current
fields.hint: Rated auxiliary / control circuit current. Applicable to Top Drive and Rotary Table. Range 6–16 A per library data. Not applicable to static equipment (wellhead, BOP, manifolds, mast). Leave blank if not applicable.

fields.label: Speed
fields.hint: Rated output speed at the drill string / rotary table. Applicable to Rotary Table, Top Drive, and Mud Pump (stroke rate converted to RPM). Standard value: 200 RPM per library data. Not applicable to static equipment (wellhead, BOP, manifolds, mast, drawworks). Leave blank if not applicable.

fields.label: Standard
fields.hint: Applicable API design standard governing pressure class, material, and test requirements. Select the standard that matches the equipment sub-type: API 6A — Wellhead Assembly, Christmas Tree, Choke Manifold, Kill Manifold; API 16A — BOP Stack; API 4F — Mast/Substructure; API 7K — Mud Pump (Triplex), Rotary Table; API 8C — Drawworks, Top Drive (hoisting components).

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == "BOP Stack" AND standard != "API 16A"
compliance_rules.message: BOP Stack must be designed and certified to API 16A. The selected standard is not applicable to this equipment sub-type. Correct the Standard field before proceeding.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 16A (4th Ed.) – Specification for Drill-Through Equipment: mandates design, material, testing, and marking requirements for all BOP equipment. Also required by BSEE 30 CFR §250.442 (offshore USA) and NORSOK D-010 §8 (Norwegian sector). Non-compliance invalidates well-control certification.

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: unit_rate_usd = {"Wellhead Assembly": 120000, "Christmas Tree": 350000, "BOP Stack": 2500000, "Choke Manifold": 180000, "Kill Manifold": 150000, "Mud Pump (Triplex)": 600000, "Drawworks": 1200000, "Top Drive": 1800000, "Rotary Table": 250000, "Mast/Substructure": 2000000}[equipment_subtype]; equipment_cost = unit_rate_usd * quantity
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: standard_multiplier = {"API 6A": 1.0, "API 16A": 1.0, "API 4F": 1.0, "API 7K": 1.0, "API 8C": 1.0}[standard]; speed_factor = (speed === 200 || speed == null) ? 1.0 : (speed / 200) ** 0.25; equipment_cost_t2 = unit_rate_usd * quantity * standard_multiplier * speed_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: total_equipment_cost = unit_rate_usd * quantity * standard_multiplier * speed_factor; installed_cost = total_equipment_cost * (1 + freight_insurance) * (1 + erection_installation) * (1 + contingency) * regional_cost_index_factor * equipment_sub_region_factor

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Total installed motor power for the unit (kW). For Shale Shakers: typically 1.5–3 kW (vibratory motor only). For Desanders/Desilters: 15–30 kW (feed pump motor). For Mud Cleaners: 22–37 kW (combined). For Centrifuge (Decanter): 22–75 kW (bowl + conveyor drive). For Degassers: 2.5–15 kW (vacuum pump + agitator). This is the primary sizing parameter for budget pricing. Values seen in library: 2.5, 15, 22, 30, 45 kW.

fields.label: Speed
fields.hint: Bowl rotational speed of the Centrifuge (Decanter) only (RPM). Standard drilling-grade decanters operate at 3,200 RPM, generating approximately 2,000 g centrifugal force for barite-weight mud recovery. Leave blank for all other sub-types (Shale Shaker, Desander, Desilter, Mud Cleaner, Degasser — speed is not a user-specified cost driver for those items). Values seen in library: 3,200 RPM.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Centrifuge (Decanter)'

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Centrifuge (Decanter)' AND (speed < 1800 OR speed > 4000)
compliance_rules.message: Decanter centrifuge bowl speed must be between 1,800 and 4,000 RPM. Values outside this range are outside standard drilling-grade equipment design envelopes and may indicate a data entry error.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API RP 13C (ISO 13501) — Drilling Fluids: Processing Equipment Evaluation; typical OEM design envelope for drilling decanter centrifuges (e.g. Derrick, Schlumberger, GN Solids)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Shale Shaker' AND motor_power > 7.5
compliance_rules.message: Shale shaker motor power above 7.5 kW is unusually high. Vibratory motors on a single shaker deck are typically 1.5–3 kW each. Confirm this is not the feed pump power or the sum of a multi-deck unit.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API RP 13C (ISO 13501) Table 1 — shaker screen classification and typical vibratory drive ratings; IADC Drilling Engineering solids-control guidelines

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Degasser' AND motor_power > 30
compliance_rules.message: Degasser motor power above 30 kW is high for a standard atmospheric or vacuum degasser. Verify that this is not the mud agitator or that a large trip gas degasser is intended.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API RP 13C (ISO 13501); typical atmospheric degasser ratings 2.5–15 kW; vacuum degassers up to 22 kW for high-flow applications

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Mud Cleaner' AND motor_power < 15
compliance_rules.message: Mud cleaner motor power below 15 kW may be undersized. A mud cleaner combines a desilter cone bank with a fine-screen shaker; feed pump power alone is typically 15–22 kW for a standard unit.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API RP 13C (ISO 13501) — solids control equipment evaluation; mud cleaner design practice (combined hydrocyclone + fine-screen unit)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Centrifuge (Decanter)' AND speed IS None
compliance_rules.message: Bowl speed not entered for Centrifuge (Decanter). Defaulting to 3,200 RPM for cost estimation. Specify if a low-speed (barite recovery, ~1,800–2,200 RPM) or high-speed (unweighted mud, 3,200–4,000 RPM) unit is required, as this affects bearing specification and cost.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API RP 13C (ISO 13501); IADC solids-control classification — low-speed vs high-speed decanter centrifuge duty

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype, motor_power) × speed_multiplier(equipment_subtype, speed) × quantity × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100)
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype, motor_power) × speed_multiplier(equipment_subtype, speed) × quantity × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

base_unit_cost_USD.note: USD supply-only cost at US Gulf Coast basis (USGC), CEPCI 816 (2024). Interpolate linearly within power bands.

speed_multiplier.note: Applies to Centrifuge (Decanter) only. All other sub-types → 1.0.

adder_notes.freight_insurance: 6% — drilling solids-control units are bulky, skid-mounted items; sea freight + marine insurance typically 5–7% of EXW for O&G equipment to remote sites.
adder_notes.erection_installation: 15% — includes skid setting, drive alignment, mud-line hook-up, electrical termination, and commissioning. Lower than general process equipment (18–20%) because units are self-contained skids with pre-wired junction boxes. Does not include mud-pit civils or overhead crane.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-OG-PRODUCTION.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | DP: {design_pressure} bar(g) | Vol: {volume} m³ | Std: {standard} | Qty: {quantity}
SCHEMA-OG-PRODUCTION.spec_example: Mud Gas Separator | DP: 10 bar(g) | Vol: 50 m³ | Std: API 12J | Qty: 1

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure (MAWP) for which the vessel or skid is designed and stamped. Typical O&G ranges: low-pressure production separators and KO drums 10–20 bar(g); medium-pressure gas separators and free water knockouts 20–50 bar(g); high-pressure slug catchers and export separators 50–100 bar(g); flare system knockout drums 3–10 bar(g). This is the single largest cost driver for pressure vessels — shell thickness and flange class increase significantly above 50 bar(g). Per ASME VIII Div.1 or API 12J as applicable.

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Shell (geometric) volume of the pressure vessel. Not applicable to flare stacks, flare tips, pig traps, or metering skids — leave blank for those items. Typical ranges: mud gas separators 5–30 m³; test separators 5–50 m³; production separators 30–500 m³; free water knockouts 30–300 m³; heater-treaters 20–200 m³; electrostatic treaters 50–400 m³; knockout drums 5–200 m³; slug catchers 100–2000 m³. Combined with design pressure via the six-tenths rule to scale equipment cost.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype IN ['Mud Gas Separator','Knockout Drum','Test Separator','Production Separator','Heater-Treater','Free Water Knockout','Electrostatic Treater','Slug Catcher']

fields.label: Standard
fields.hint: Governing design standard for the equipment. API 12J (Specification for Oil and Gas Separators): covers field-fabricated separators, free water knockouts, and heater-treaters — lower cost than ASME VIII. API 537 (Specification for Flare Details): mandatory for flare stacks and flare tips. ASME VIII Div.1 (Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code): universally applicable; required by most IOC, NOC, and EPC specifications; adds ~10–20% cost premium over API 12J due to U-stamp, third-party inspection, PWHT, and full material traceability. Pig launchers/receivers and metering skids are typically ASME VIII with ASME B31.3 process piping.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 99 AND standard == 'API 12J'
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds the API 12J scope limit of approximately 99 bar(g) (1440 psi). At this pressure, ASME VIII Div.1 or Div.2 is required. Please change the Design Standard to ASME VIII.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 12J (Specification for Oil and Gas Separators), latest edition — scope is limited to vessels with design pressures not exceeding 1440 psi (approximately 99 bar(g)). Above this threshold ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code Section VIII applies.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Flare Stack' OR equipment_subtype == 'Flare Tip') AND standard != 'API 537'
compliance_rules.message: Flare stacks and flare tips should be designed to API 537. The selected standard may produce an incorrect cost estimate and could result in a non-compliant flare system design. Verify with the project specification.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 537 (Flare Details for General Refinery and Petrochemical Service) is the industry-standard governing document for flare stack and tip design, materials, and performance. Most NOC/IOC and EPC project specifications mandate API 537 compliance for flare systems.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Pig Launcher' OR equipment_subtype == 'Pig Receiver' OR equipment_subtype == 'Scraper Trap' OR equipment_subtype == 'Metering Skid' OR equipment_subtype == 'Flare Stack' OR equipment_subtype == 'Flare Tip') AND volume != ''
compliance_rules.message: Volume is not a primary sizing parameter for this equipment type and will be excluded from the cost calculation. For pig launchers/receivers, size by pipeline nominal bore (inches). For metering skids, size by maximum flow rate (m³/h or MMscfd). For flare stacks, size by stack height (m) and base diameter.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Pig traps are sized by pipeline internal diameter per ASME B31.3, B31.4, and B31.8 and API 6D (Specification for Line Valves). Metering skids are sized by volumetric flow rate per AGA-7 (turbine metering), AGA-9 (ultrasonic metering), or ISO 17089. Flare stacks are sized by stack height and wind loading per API 537.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Electrostatic Treater' AND (design_pressure IS None OR design_pressure < 5)
compliance_rules.message: Electrostatic treaters operate at elevated pressures to maintain crude oil above bubble point. A design pressure below 5 bar(g) is unusual — verify that the operating pressure and bubble point of the crude have been correctly assessed.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Electrostatic treaters (FWKO with electrostatic grid) are typically designed to 10–50 bar(g) to suppress vapour evolution across the electrical grid, which can cause arcing. API 12J and vendor literature (e.g. Natco/Cameron) recommend minimum operating pressures above crude bubble point.

cost_build_up.cost_basis_note: Base equipment costs in USD, CEPCI 816 (2024 basis), carbon steel construction, at reference volume and design pressure as listed in unit_rates_usd_2024. Scale using the six-tenths rule for volume and an exponential pressure factor. Apply regional_cost_index and regional_factors for project location.

unit_rates_usd_2024.note: Budgetary ex-works USD prices at reference V_ref (m³) and P_ref = 10 bar(g). Sources: Richardson's Process Equipment Cost Estimation, Aspen ICARUS benchmarks, and vendor budget quotations for mid-tier fabricators.

Flare Stack.sizing_note: Per stack; base rate for 30 m self-supported stack. Scale by height^1.5 for taller stacks.

Flare Tip.sizing_note: Per tip; standard utility/steam-assisted tip. Multi-point or enclosed ground flare tips multiply by 3–8×.

Pig Launcher.sizing_note: Per unit; base rate for 12" NB bore, 50 bar(g). Scale by (bore_in/12)^1.8 for other bore sizes.

Pig Receiver.sizing_note: Per unit; base rate for 12" NB bore, 50 bar(g). Typically 10–15% more than launcher due to larger closure.

Scraper Trap.sizing_note: Per launcher+receiver pair skid-mounted, 12" NB bore, 50 bar(g).

Metering Skid.sizing_note: Per skid; base rate for fiscal-grade 3-stream gas metering with ultrasonic meters (AGA-9), flow computers, and prover connection. Liquid fiscal metering multiply by 1.3×.

scaling_equations.volume_scaling_note: Six-tenths rule (Williams' rule) for fabricated pressure vessels: Cost ∝ Volume^0.6. Valid across a 10:1 volume range. For extreme ratios (>100:1) apply in segments.
scaling_equations.pressure_factor_note: Exponential pressure factor referenced to 10 bar(g). Yields P_factor ≈ 1.0 at 10 bar, ≈ 1.50 at 50 bar, ≈ 2.46 at 100 bar. Reflects increased shell thickness, flange class upgrades (ASME B16.5 Class 300 → 600 → 900), and PWHT requirements.

standard_factor.note: ASME VIII premium of ~15% over API 12J reflects U-stamp fees, mandatory third-party inspection (AI), PWHT documentation, full MTRs, and NDE requirements.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: unit_rates_usd_2024[equipment_subtype].base_cost_usd × (volume / V_ref)^0.6 × exp(0.009 × (design_pressure - 10)) × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: unit_rates_usd_2024[equipment_subtype].base_cost_usd × (volume / V_ref)^0.6 × exp(0.009 × (design_pressure - 10)) × standard_factor[standard] × quantity × (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation + contingency) × regional_cost_index[region].factor × sub_region.equipment_factor

adders.freight_insurance_note: 7% for large/heavy pressure vessels requiring special transport (low-loader, crane lifts). Includes marine/inland cargo insurance. Use 0.05 for small items (pig traps, flare tips).
adders.erection_installation_note: 22% of FOB equipment cost. Covers heavy lift crane, skid setting on foundations, process piping hook-up, nozzle connections, instrument loop checks, pressure test, commissioning, and pre-start-up safety review (PSSR). Consistent with Hand and Lang factor methods for onshore process plant. Offshore installations apply 1.5–2.5× multiplier on top.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: Supply voltage to the panel. Typical values: 24 V (control/24 VDC panels), 110 V (single-phase LV), 415 V (three-phase LV). Determines insulation class and switchgear rating.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Rated shaft power of the largest motor controlled by this panel (kW). Used to size contactors, overload relays, and cable derating. Leave blank for panels with no direct motor load (e.g., UPS distribution, lighting). Seen in library: 30–75 kW.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype in ['Control Panel (Local - Small)', 'Control Panel (Local - Medium)', 'Control Panel (VFD - Local)', 'Control Panel (Soft Starter - Local)', 'Control Panel (MCC Interface)', 'Control Panel (MCC - Remote I/O)', 'Control Panel (Fire Pump - Auto)']

fields.label: Power
fields.hint: Apparent power capacity of the panel (kVA). Used for UPS distribution, generator AMF, HVAC-BMS, and lighting panels where total VA load—not a single motor—governs sizing. Seen in library: 10 kVA.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype in ['Control Panel (UPS - Distribution)', 'Control Panel (Generator - AMF)', 'Control Panel (HVAC - BMS)', 'Control Panel (Lighting - Smart)', 'Control Panel (PLC - Small)', 'Control Panel (PLC - Medium)']

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Ingress Protection rating per IEC 60529. IP42 = indoor clean/dry areas (control rooms). IP54 = dust-protected, splash-proof (general plant/outdoor sheltered). IP55 = dust-protected, low-pressure jet wash (washdown areas, outdoor exposed). Higher IP ratings add 8–15% to enclosure cost.

fields.label: Drive Type
fields.hint: Select the motor-starting/speed control technology. VFD (Variable Frequency Drive) = full variable speed, highest cost, requires harmonic filter if THD>5%. Soft Starter = reduced-voltage starting only, no speed control, lower cost. Leave blank for DOL or star-delta starters.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype in ['Control Panel (VFD - Local)', 'Control Panel (Soft Starter - Local)', 'Control Panel (MCC Interface)', 'Control Panel (MCC - Remote I/O)']

fields.label: Fuel Type
fields.hint: Fuel type for the associated generator set (applicable to Generator - AMF and Fire Pump - Auto panels only). Diesel is the standard for standby generation. Affects AMF relay logic for fuel monitoring and interlock wiring scope.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype in ['Control Panel (Generator - AMF)', 'Control Panel (Fire Pump - Auto)']

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: drive_type == 'VFD' AND motor_power > 75
compliance_rules.message: VFD panels above 75 kW require harmonic analysis (THD study) per IEEE 519 / IEC 61000-3-12. Ensure harmonic filter or 12-pulse rectifier is included in scope.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEEE 519-2022 Table 2 (current distortion limits); IEC 61000-3-12 (harmonic current emission limits for ≥75 A equipment)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Control Panel (Fire Pump - Auto)' AND enclosure < 'IP54'
compliance_rules.message: Fire pump controller enclosures must be minimum IP54 (NEMA 12 equivalent). IP42 is not acceptable for fire pump duty.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NFPA 20-2022 Cl. 10.4.1 (controller listing and rating); IEC 60529 ingress protection equivalence

compliance_rules.condition_expr: voltage == 24 AND motor_power > 5
compliance_rules.message: 24 V supply is typically limited to control circuits. Motor power >5 kW at 24 V is unusual — confirm this is a 24 VDC control panel feeding a separate power circuit, not a direct motor drive.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60364-4-41 protective extra-low voltage (PELV) scope limitations; practical switchgear availability at 24 V

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Control Panel (Generator - AMF)' AND fuel_type IS None
compliance_rules.message: Fuel type must be specified for Generator AMF panels to correctly define fuel monitoring interlocks and AMF relay logic.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60947-6-1 automatic transfer switch requirements; diesel generator AMF standard interlock scope (IEC 62271-6)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: motor_power > 200 AND drive_type IS None
compliance_rules.message: Motor power >200 kW without a VFD or soft starter: confirm DOL or star-delta starting is acceptable. Large DOL starts cause significant voltage dip — check with the utility or site power authority.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60034-1 motor starting; IEC 61000-3-11 voltage fluctuation limits; typical utility restrictions on direct-on-line starting above 150–200 kW

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype, motor_power_or_power) × drive_type_multiplier(drive_type) × enclosure_multiplier(enclosure) × quantity × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100)
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype, motor_power_or_power) × drive_type_multiplier(drive_type) × enclosure_multiplier(enclosure) × voltage_multiplier(voltage) × quantity × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Current
fields.hint: Full-scale rated current of the meter. For CT-fed ammeters enter the CT secondary rating (typically 1 A or 5 A). For direct-connect instruments enter the circuit full-load current. Not required for voltmeters, power factor meters, frequency meters, insulation monitors, or earth fault relays.

fields.label: Accuracy Class
fields.hint: IEC 60051 accuracy class expressed as percentage of full-scale deflection (e.g. 1.5 = Class 1.5, meaning ±1.5% FSD error). Standard accuracy classes are 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.5. Analog panel meters are typically Class 1.5. Not applicable to earth fault relays or insulation monitors.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype NOT IN ['Earth Fault Relay (Static)', 'Insulation Monitor (415V)']

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: System operating voltage (V AC rms). For voltmeters, power factor meters and multifunction meters, enter the VT secondary or direct measurement voltage. For insulation monitors enter 415 V (device is rated for 415 V AC systems). For digital meters with separate DC auxiliary supply, enter DC supply voltage (e.g. 24 V DC). Not required for pure CT-fed ammeters.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype IN ['Analog Meter (Voltmeter - 96×96mm)', 'Digital Multimeter (Panel Mount)', 'Insulation Monitor (415V)', 'Multifunction Meter (Digital)', 'Power Factor Meter (96×96mm)']

fields.label: Frequency
fields.hint: System nominal power frequency (Hz). Standard values are 50 Hz (IEC regions: India, Europe, UK, GCC, ASEAN) or 60 Hz (ANSI regions: USA, parts of ASEAN). For frequency meters, this defines the mid-scale (nominal) point of the display range (typically ±5 Hz around nominal). For all other meter types this field is for documentation only; use 50 or 60 as applicable.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Insulation Monitor (415V)' AND voltage != 415
compliance_rules.message: Insulation Monitor (415V) is rated for 415 V AC systems only. The voltage entered does not match. Verify that this device is suitable for the system voltage, or select a different instrument type.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61557-8 (insulation monitoring devices for IT systems). The 415 V designation in the device name indicates the rated system voltage; applying to a different voltage class requires manufacturer confirmation.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Analog Meter (Ammeter - 96×96mm)' AND current > 5 AND current < 100
compliance_rules.message: For ammeters with full-load current between 5 A and 100 A, a current transformer (CT) secondary of 5 A is strongly recommended. Direct-connect ammeters above 5 A are non-standard for 96×96 mm panel instruments and may not be commercially available.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60051-1 (direct-acting indicating analogue electrical measuring instruments). 96×96 mm panel ammeters are typically designed for 1 A or 5 A CT secondary input at currents above 5 A full scale.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Frequency Meter (96×96mm)' AND (frequency < 48 OR frequency > 52) AND (frequency < 58 OR frequency > 62)
compliance_rules.message: Frequency entered is outside standard 50 Hz (±2 Hz) and 60 Hz (±2 Hz) nominal ranges. Confirm the system frequency and that the selected meter is calibrated for this range.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60051-1 and IEC 60038 (standard voltages and frequencies). System frequency should be 50 Hz ±1% (IEC) or 60 Hz ±0.1 Hz (ANSI C84.1). Non-standard frequencies require special-order instruments.

unit_rates_usd_2024.note: Budgetary ex-works USD prices, 2024 basis. Apply regional_cost_index and regional_factors for project location. Sources: typical market rates for mid-tier (e.g. Rishabh, Entes, Schneider, ABB catalogue range).

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: unit_rate[equipment_subtype] × quantity × voltage_factor × current_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: unit_rate[equipment_subtype] × quantity × voltage_factor × current_factor × accuracy_factor × (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation + contingency) × regional_cost_index.factor × sub_region.equipment_factor

voltage_factor.note: Multiplier on equipment unit rate based on voltage rating. Base = 415 V.

current_factor.note: Multiplier on equipment unit rate for ammeter current range. Base = 5 A CT secondary.

accuracy_factor.note: Multiplier for accuracy class. Base = Class 1.5.

cost_build_up.installation_basis: Panel mounting into pre-punched 96×96 mm cutout, DIN rail or screw termination wiring (typically 0.5–1.0 man-hour per instrument including CT/VT secondary loop connection and commissioning check).

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
