fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Total installed motor power of the equipment drive system. Typical range: 12 kW (small autoclave / fluid bed) to 100 kW (large tablet press or packaging line). Used for utility load estimation.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Tablet Compression Machine','Fluid Bed Processor','Blister Packaging Line']

fields.label: Airflow Rate (HVAC / Process Air)
fields.hint: Supply/exhaust airflow for Clean Room HVAC or process air for Fluid Bed Processor. Clean Room ISO 7 typically 5000–15000 m³/hr per zone. Fluid bed: 500–3000 m³/hr depending on batch size. Not applicable to Autoclave or Tablet Press.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Clean Room System (ISO 7)','Fluid Bed Processor']

fields.label: Chamber / Vessel Volume
fields.hint: Working volume of the pressure vessel or processing chamber. Autoclave: 316–2000 L. Fluid Bed Processor: 100–1500 L. Tablet Press: N/A (use throughput instead). Directly drives vessel cost via surface-area/thickness correlation.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Autoclave (Steam Sterilizer)','Fluid Bed Processor']

fields.label: Clean Room Volume
fields.hint: Internal envelope volume of the clean room suite. Typical ISO 7 suites: 100–5000 m³. Drives HVAC sizing, wall panel area, and filtration cost. Provide gross internal volume (L × W × H).

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype == 'Clean Room System (ISO 7)'

fields.label: Clean Room Floor Area
fields.hint: Net floor area of the clean room. Used to estimate wall/ceiling panel quantities, flooring, and civil works. Typical ISO 7 rooms: 20–500 m². If volume is known and ceiling height is ~3 m, Area ≈ Volume / 3.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype == 'Clean Room System (ISO 7)'

fields.label: HEPA Filter Efficiency
fields.hint: Terminal HEPA filter efficiency (DOP test at 0.3 µm). ISO 7 minimum: 99.97% (H14 class per EN 1822). Upgrade to 99.999% (ULPA) adds ~15–20% to filtration cost. Typically fixed at 99.97% for ISO 7 clean rooms.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype == 'Clean Room System (ISO 7)'

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure (MAWP) of the pressure vessel. Autoclave steam sterilizers typically operate at 1–3 bar(g) corresponding to 121–134 °C. Must comply with ASME Section VIII or PED 2014/68/EU. Drives vessel wall thickness and thus cost.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Autoclave (Steam Sterilizer)','Fluid Bed Processor']

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum design temperature of the vessel or process. Autoclave sterilization: 121 °C (15 psi steam) or 134 °C (30 psi flash). Fluid Bed: 60–90 °C inlet air. Must be ≥ operating temperature + 10 °C margin per ASME VIII. Affects SS316L yield strength and flange rating selection.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Autoclave (Steam Sterilizer)','Fluid Bed Processor']

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of construction for product-contact surfaces. SS316L is the pharma industry standard (GMP, FDA 21 CFR, cGMP) — low carbon grade minimises carbide precipitation during welding. Hastelloy/Ti for aggressive chloride or high-acid products. Carbon steel not acceptable for product contact.

fields.label: Applicable Standard
fields.hint: Primary design and certification standard. ASME VIII for pressure vessels (USA, India, most export projects). PED for EU supply. ISO 14644-1 for clean room classification and qualification. EN 285 for large autoclaves in EU pharma. Selection affects documentation, third-party inspection, and stamping cost (+5–15% for ASME U-stamp).

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: efficiency < 99.97
compliance_rules.message: HEPA filter efficiency below 99.97% does not meet ISO 14644-1 minimum for ISO 7 clean room classification. Upgrade to H14 grade (≥99.97% at 0.3 µm).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 14644-1:2015 Table 1 — ISO Class 7 requires ≤352,000 particles/m³ at ≥0.5 µm, achievable only with H14 HEPA (EN 1822:2009, ≥99.97% DOP).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 3.5 AND standard == 'ASME VIII Div.1' AND moc == 'SS316L'
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds typical autoclave/fluid bed range (>3.5 bar(g)). Verify vessel classification — above 3.5 bar(g) the vessel may require ASME VIII Div.2 or special corrosion allowance review for SS316L.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME BPVC Section VIII Div.1 UG-16: wall thickness must satisfy t = P·R/(S·E - 0.6P). At >3.5 bar(g) on SS316L, re-verify S at design temperature (ASME IID Table 1A). Div.2 applies above design pressure thresholds per scope definition.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature < 121 AND equipment_subtype == 'Autoclave (Steam Sterilizer)'
compliance_rules.message: Autoclave design temperature below 121 °C is insufficient for steam sterilization per pharmacopeial requirements. Minimum sterilization temperature is 121 °C (F₀ ≥ 12 min cycle).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: USP <1211>, EP 5.1.1, WHO TRS 908 — saturated steam sterilization at 121 °C / 15 psi for ≥15 min (minimum F₀ = 12) required for terminal sterilization of product/equipment in pharmaceutical manufacturing.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc != 'SS316L' AND equipment_subtype IN ['Autoclave (Steam Sterilizer)', 'Fluid Bed Processor', 'Tablet Compression Machine']
compliance_rules.message: Non-SS316L MOC selected for GMP product-contact equipment. Ensure material is approved per FDA 21 CFR and cGMP guidelines, and that surface finish (Ra ≤ 0.8 µm) is achievable.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: FDA Guidance for Industry — cGMP; ISPE Baseline Guides Vol. 5: product-contact surfaces shall be non-reactive, non-additive, non-absorptive and cleanable to Ra ≤ 0.8 µm. SS316L is preferred; alternative alloys require documented material qualification.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: standard == '21 CFR Part 11' OR standard == 'GAMP 5 (Software/Automation)'
compliance_rules.message: 21 CFR Part 11 / GAMP 5 applies to electronic records and audit trails. Ensure PLC/SCADA system is included in equipment scope with IQ/OQ/PQ validation protocol. Budget 10–15% uplift on automation scope.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: 21 CFR Part 11 (FDA, 1997) — electronic records and signatures; ISPE GAMP 5 (2008) — risk-based approach to GxP computerised systems validation. Applicable to all automated pharma equipment with electronic batch records.

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_cost = C_ref × (size_param / size_ref)^n × moc_factor × quantity; where size_param = volume (L) for vessels, area (m²) for clean rooms, motor_power (kW) for mechanical; n = 0.6 (six-tenths rule); C_ref from CEPCI-indexed database at base_year 2024.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: tier_2_cost = base_cost × pressure_factor × temperature_factor × standard_factor; pressure_factor = 1 + 0.05 × (design_pressure - 1.0) [for pressure vessels]; temperature_factor = 1.0 if design_temperature ≤ 130°C else 1.08; standard_factor = 1.12 if standard contains 'ASME VIII' else 1.0 (includes U-stamp TPI cost).
cost_build_up.full_formula: installed_cost = tier_2_cost × (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation + contingency) × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × cepci_escalation; cepci_escalation = current_cepci / base_value.

Autoclave (Steam Sterilizer).note: Reference: 500 L SS316L ASME VIII autoclave, FOB, 2024 basis

Fluid Bed Processor.note: Reference: 300 L top-spray FBP, SS316L, GMP grade, 2024 basis

Clean Room System (ISO 7).note: Unit rate USD 2500/m² for turnkey ISO 7 modular clean room incl. HVAC, HEPA, wall panels, controls. 2024 basis.

Tablet Compression Machine.note: Reference: 15 kW rotary tablet press, 36-station, SS316L contact parts, 2024 basis

Blister Packaging Line.note: Reference: 18 kW automatic blister line, 200 blisters/min, SS316L, 2024 basis

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

usd_cost_baseline.note: Base = USD at USA level (matches CEPCI base 816 @ 2024). Engine: base_rate = usd_cost_baseline.items[equipment_subtype].usd_per_*, then x index_now/816 x regional_factor x fx. verify=true entries are public-benchmark estimates pending calibration.

All TODOs resolved with engineering judgment. | R02 promoted 0.6-engineering-complete->1.0-priced after structural completion + benchmark pricing (validator-gated; no remaining blocking errors).

SCHEMA-PHARMA-LIQUID.spec_example: Fluid Bed Dryer (FBD) | 316 L | MOC: SS316L | 11 kW | Qty: 1

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Installed motor power of main drive. Typical range for this equipment class: 4–75 kW. Common values: 4, 5, 7.5, 8, 11 kW. Enter nameplate kW from datasheet where available.

fields.label: Flow Rate
fields.hint: Volumetric throughput of liquid/slurry at process conditions. Applicable mainly to Homogenizers, Spray Dryers and Bottle Washers. Enter 0 or leave blank for batch equipment (FBD, Tray Dryer, Lyophilizer). Library value: 5000 L/hr.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Homogenizer', 'Spray Dryer', 'Bottle Washing Machine']

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum design temperature of the vessel or chamber wall. Use process temperature + 10 °C safety margin per good engineering practice. Typical range: 40 °C (ambient wash) to 180 °C (spray dryer inlet). Values seen in library: 40, 50, 80, 85, 180 °C.

fields.label: Working Volume
fields.hint: Net working (useful) volume of the bowl, chamber or tank in litres. Primary sizing parameter for FBDs, Lyophilizers, Rotary Vacuum Dryers and Homogenizers. Values seen in library: 316, 2000, 5000 L. Use gross vessel volume × 0.65–0.75 fill factor if working volume is not stated.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Fluid Bed Dryer (FBD)', 'Freeze Dryer (Lyophilizer)', 'Rotary Vacuum Dryer', 'Homogenizer', 'Vacuum Tray Dryer']

fields.label: Chamber / Plenum Volume
fields.hint: Gross internal chamber volume in cubic metres. Used for Spray Dryers and large Freeze Dryers where m³ is the industry-standard sizing unit. Library value: 5000 m³ (large spray dryer installation). Leave blank for equipment sized in litres (F-005).

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Spray Dryer']

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure (MAWP) of the pressure-containing boundary in bar gauge. Applicable to Homogenizers (high-pressure) and jacketed vessels. Most dryers operate near atmospheric — enter 0 or 0.5 bar(g) for jacket steam rating. Library value: 200 bar(g) (high-pressure homogenizer). Triggers PED / ASME compliance checks above 0.5 bar(g).

fields.label: Agitator / Drum Speed
fields.hint: Rotational speed of the main agitator, drum or atomiser. Relevant to Rotary Vacuum Dryers (5–20 RPM), Homogenizers (up to 3600 RPM impeller), Bottle Washers (indexing speed). Library value: 12 RPM (Rotary Vacuum Dryer drum). Leave blank for static equipment (Tray Dryers, Lyophilizers).

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Rotary Vacuum Dryer', 'Homogenizer', 'Bottle Washing Machine']

fields.label: MOC (Material of Construction)
fields.hint: Material of construction for product-contact surfaces. SS316L is the pharmaceutical industry standard (low carbon for weld corrosion resistance, cGMP compliant). SS316 acceptable for non-sterile API. Hastelloy C-276 for highly corrosive solvents or HCl-containing streams. Select 'GMP Polished SS316L' if surface finish certificate (Ra ≤ 0.8 µm) is required by URS.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 0.5
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds 0.5 bar(g). Equipment is subject to Pressure Equipment Directive (PED 2014/68/EU) or ASME Section VIII (USA). Ensure vendor supplies CE-marked / ASME U-stamp design and third-party inspection (TPI) is included in the procurement package.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: EU PED 2014/68/EU Article 4; ASME BPVC Section VIII Div. 1; Indian IBR regulations (IS 2825) for steam-jacketed vessels. Threshold 0.5 bar(g) per PED Category I boundary.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc == 'SS316' AND equipment_subtype == 'Freeze Dryer (Lyophilizer)'
compliance_rules.message: SS316 (standard carbon) selected for Lyophilizer. cGMP guidance (ISPE Baseline Guide Vol. 5) recommends SS316L (low carbon ≤ 0.03% C) for sterile product-contact surfaces to prevent weld sensitisation and pitting corrosion from CIP/SIP cycles. Confirm with process owner before proceeding.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISPE Baseline Pharmaceutical Engineering Guide Vol. 5 - Commissioning & Qualification; FDA 21 CFR Part 211.65; ASME BPE (Bioprocessing Equipment) Standard.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature > 150 AND equipment_subtype != 'Spray Dryer'
compliance_rules.message: Design temperature above 150 °C is unusually high for this equipment sub-type (expected only for Spray Dryers). Verify inlet air temperature is not being entered instead of vessel wall design temperature.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Typical GMP dryer wall design temperatures: FBD 80–120 °C; RVD 100–140 °C; Tray Dryer 80–120 °C. Spray dryer inlet air 150–220 °C (NIRO / GEA design references).

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_cost = A × (volume_litres ^ 0.6) × moc_factor × quantity; where A = 18000 USD (SS316L reference, 2024 CEPCI 816)
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_cost = A × (volume_litres ^ 0.6) × moc_factor × pressure_factor × temperature_factor × quantity; pressure_factor = 1 + 0.004 × max(0, design_pressure - 6); temperature_factor = 1 + 0.002 × max(0, design_temperature - 100)
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_cost = A × (volume_litres ^ 0.6) × moc_factor × pressure_factor × temperature_factor × speed_factor × flow_factor × quantity; speed_factor = 1 + 0.001 × max(0, speed - 50); flow_factor = 1 + 0.00002 × max(0, flow_rate - 1000)

equipment_A_factors_USD.note: Base cost coefficient A per equipment sub-type (SS316L, 2024 CEPCI 816 basis, FOB works). Exponent 0.6 (six-tenths rule). Validate against vendor budgetary quotes.

adder_notes.freight_insurance: 5% of FOB equipment cost. Covers sea freight (FCL), marine insurance and port handling for imported equipment. Adjust to 2–3% for local/domestic supply or 8–10% for remote sites.
adder_notes.erection_installation: 18% of FOB equipment cost. Covers mechanical erection, alignment, initial commissioning (no IQ/OQ). Increase to 25–30% for Lyophilizers (complex utility connections, clean-room rigging) or for GCC/Europe regions. Excludes civil/structural works (captured in regional_factors.civil_factor).
adder_notes.contingency: 10% applied to the sum of all above cost elements (equipment + freight + erection). Aligns with AACE Class 4 estimate practice.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

Fixed volume min, design temperature range, MOC options, sections, cost drivers, compliance rules, and cost build-up formulas. | v1.0-priced: usd_cost_baseline + multiplier tables added (USA-base ENR/CEPCI; public-benchmark, all verify=true).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-PHARMA-SOLID.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | {volume} L | {throughput} kg/h | {motor_power} kW | MOC: {moc} | Finish: {surface_finish} | cGMP: {cgmp_level} | OEB: {oeb_containment} | CIP: {cip_provision}
SCHEMA-PHARMA-SOLID.spec_example: Coating Pan (Auto) | 250 L | — | 7.5 kW | MOC: SS316L EP | Finish: Mirror ≤0.4 µm Ra | cGMP: Schedule M+ | OEB: OEB-1 (open) | CIP: CIP-ready spray ball

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Installed motor power of the main drive. Typical ranges by type — RMG: 11–37 kW; Blenders: 1.5–15 kW; Coating Pan: 3–11 kW; Multi Mill: 3–22 kW; Blister Machine: 1.5–7.5 kW; Deduster/Polisher: 0.3–1.5 kW.

fields.label: Working Volume
fields.hint: Working / batch volume of the vessel or bowl. Applicable to blenders, RMG, coating pans, mixers. Not applicable to throughput-only equipment (Multi Mill, Vibro Sifter, Deduster, Blister Machine) — use Throughput (F-008) for those. Lab/pilot scale starts at 10 L; production blenders/RMG up to 2000 L; coating pans up to 600 L.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 — Quick Estimate (always visible when applicable)

fields.label: Speed
fields.hint: Main shaft / pan / impeller speed. Typical ranges — Blenders (tumble): 6–25 RPM; RMG impeller: 150–450 RPM; Coating Pan: 2–20 RPM (variable); Multi Mill rotor: 750–3000 RPM; Vibro Sifter: 900–1500 RPM; Colloid Mill: 3000–11000 RPM.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum design temperature the equipment jacket or process zone must withstand. Most solid-dosage equipment operates at ambient (25–40 °C) and requires no elevated design temperature. Coating pans with heated air plenum: typically 80–120 °C. Sigma/Planetary mixers with steam jacket: up to 150 °C. Leave at default 40 °C unless a heated or jacketed variant is specified.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 — Budget Estimate; only cost-relevant for jacketed/heated variants

fields.label: MOC (Product-Contact Parts)
fields.hint: Material of construction for all product-contact surfaces. SS316L EP (electropolished to ≤0.4 µm Ra) is the baseline for new cGMP solid-dosage equipment per ISPE Baseline Guide & Schedule M. SS316 (non-L) is acceptable for non-sterile solid dosage but verify carbon content. Hastelloy variants required for corrosive APIs (e.g. HCl salt forms, wet granulation with aggressive solvents). HDPE-lined or polymer options for deduster/polisher product chutes only.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

fields.label: Throughput
fields.hint: Rated throughput for continuous or semi-continuous equipment. Mandatory for Multi Mill (50–2000 kg/h), Vibro Sifter (50–3000 kg/h), Sifter cum Multi Mill, Tablet Deduster (20,000–400,000 tab/h — enter as tablet count in the Notes field and convert at ~0.3–1.2 g/tablet), Tablet/Capsule Polishing Machine, Capsule Sorting Machine, and Blister Machine. Not applicable to batch blenders or RMG — use Working Volume (F-003) instead.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 — primary sizing parameter for throughput-driven equipment

fields.label: cGMP Compliance Level
fields.hint: Regulatory standard governing design, documentation, and qualification requirements for this equipment. Schedule M+ (India revised 2023) mandates upgraded construction, IQ/OQ/PQ protocols, and enhanced documentation. EU GMP / US FDA requirements drive higher vendor qualification costs, FAT/SAT, and 21 CFR Part 11-compliant controls. Non-GMP or R&D equipment carries no regulatory documentation premium. Selection directly impacts vendor qualification cost adder (5–25%).
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 — Quick Estimate (always visible); drives compliance cost adder

fields.label: Surface Finish (Product-Contact Ra)
fields.hint: Surface roughness of product-contact wetted or powder-contact surfaces. ISPE Baseline Guide and Schedule M+ mandate ≤0.4 µm Ra (electropolished) for most solid-dosage product contact surfaces. ≤0.8 µm Ra mechanical polish is acceptable for non-sterile applications with adequate cleaning validation. Bead-blast / as-rolled finishes are for structural non-contact parts only. Finer finishes increase fabrication cost ~5–20%.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 — Budget Estimate; surface finish is a material cost driver in fabricated vessels

fields.label: CIP / WIP Provision
options: CIP-ready (spray balls, drain port, skid connections)
Manual clean (removable parts, no integrated wash provision)
fields.hint: Cleaning provision integrated into the equipment. CIP-ready requires spray balls, sloped drain, tri-clamp connections, and compatibility with automated CIP skid — typical for coating pans, RMG, and large blenders in high-throughput facilities. WIP (manual hose-down) is the most common provision for solid-dosage powder equipment; requires drain valve and appropriate surface finish. Manual clean (disassemble and clean off-equipment) is acceptable for small lab-scale or R&D. Not applicable for dedusters, metal detectors, blister machines.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 — Budget Estimate; CIP provision adds ~8–15% to equipment base cost

fields.label: OEB Containment Level
fields.hint: Occupational Exposure Band for the API being processed. OEB-1/2 covers the vast majority of standard solid-dosage products (paracetamol, ibuprofen, metformin, etc.) and requires no containment hardware premium. OEB-3 requires contained charging/discharging ports and local exhaust ventilation interlocks. OEB-4/5 (potent compounds, cytotoxics, hormones) require split butterfly valves (SBV), Rapid Transfer Ports (RTP), gloveboxes, and continuous containment monitoring — these add 30–200%+ to base equipment cost. Selection must match the API OEL/OEB band from the toxicology assessment.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 — Quick Estimate (always visible); OEB-4/5 is a transformative cost driver

fields.label: Instrumentation & Control Level
options: Basic (local panel, manual controls, no data logging)
Fully automated (PLC/SCADA, electronic batch records, 21 CFR Part 11)
PAT-integrated (inline NIR/Raman, real-time release, MES interface)
fields.hint: Level of automation and data integrity for the equipment control system. Basic (manual) is acceptable for R&D and non-regulated markets. Semi-automated (PLC + local HMI) is the current industry standard for Schedule M+/WHO GMP. Fully automated with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic batch records is mandatory for US FDA / EU GMP facilities. PAT-integrated systems (inline NIR for blend uniformity, moisture etc.) carry a significant premium but reduce in-process testing costs. Control level adds 5–40% over base equipment cost.

fields.label: Vendor Qualification Requirement
options: DQ + IQ/OQ + FAT + SAT (site acceptance test)
fields.hint: Qualification scope required from the equipment vendor. DQ (Design Qualification) + IQ/OQ documents are baseline for Schedule M+ and WHO GMP. FAT is strongly recommended and often mandatory for automated equipment, large blenders, coating pans, and blister machines — allows defects to be caught before shipment. SAT adds commissioning support at site. Each stage adds cost: DQ/IQ/OQ ~2–5% of EPC, FAT ~1–3%, SAT ~1–2%. Factor applies as a percentage adder on the equipment supply cost.

fields.label: Explosion-proof / ATEX Requirement
options: ATEX Zone 22 / IEC 61241 (dust, external motor only)
ATEX Zone 21 / IEC 61241 (dust, full Exd enclosure)
ATEX Zone 2 / IEC 60079 (flammable vapour, group IIA/IIB)
ATEX Zone 1 / IEC 60079 (flammable vapour, group IIC)
fields.hint: Required for equipment installed in classified hazardous areas — typically wet granulation areas where flammable solvents (IPA, ethanol, acetone) are used, or in areas with combustible dusts (fine powders, Kst > 200 bar·m/s). ATEX Zone 21/22 (dust) applies to most granulation suites. ATEX Zone 1/2 (vapour) applies to solvent-based coating or granulation. Explosion-proof (Exd) motors and controls add 15–40% to equipment electrical cost.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 — Detailed Estimate; only applicable where classified area is confirmed

fields.label: Jacket / Heat Transfer Provision
fields.hint: Heating or cooling jacket provision on the main vessel or bowl. Applicable primarily to Sigma Mixer, Planetary Mixer, Coating Pan (heated air plenum, not a jacket per se), and Ribbon Blender. Jacketed vessels require pressure-rated shell, additional nozzles, insulation, and safety relief device — adds 15–35% to vessel fabrication cost. Most blenders, V-blenders, bin blenders, and dedusters operate at ambient and do not require a jacket.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 — Detailed Estimate; shown only for jacketing-eligible equipment types

compliance_rules.condition_expr: volume IS not None AND volume < 10
compliance_rules.message: Working Volume below 10 L is outside the validated range of this template. For micro-scale or R&D equipment below 10 L, raise a manual enquiry.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Minimum 10 L reflects smallest commercial pharma lab-scale blenders/RMG (e.g. LMG-10, Bohle PM-10). Values below this are research glassware, not capital equipment items.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature > 80 AND (jacket_provision == 'No jacket (ambient operation)' OR jacket_provision IS None)
compliance_rules.message: Design Temperature above 80 °C is specified but no jacket provision is selected. Confirm whether a heated air plenum (coating pan) or steam jacket is intended, and update Jacket / Heat Transfer Provision accordingly.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Coating pans use heated inlet air (not a pressure jacket) — design temperature refers to the air plenum rating. Sigma/Planetary mixers with steam jackets require pressure vessel design per IS 2825 / ASME Section VIII. Mismatch between temperature and jacket selection flags inconsistent specification.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc == 'SS304' AND cgmp_level != 'Non-GMP / R&D'
compliance_rules.message: SS304 selected for a regulated (GMP) application. SS304 has inferior corrosion resistance and is not recommended for product-contact surfaces under Schedule M+, EU GMP, or US FDA. Upgrade to SS316L EP unless a specific engineering justification is documented.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISPE Baseline Guide Vol. 3 (Sterile Manufacturing Facilities) and Schedule M+ (India, 2023 revision) specify SS316L as the minimum standard for product-contact surfaces. SS304 is permitted only for non-contact structural parts or with written technical justification.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: cgmp_level == '21 CFR Part 211 (US FDA)' OR cgmp_level == 'EU GMP Annex 15 (EU/UK)'
compliance_rules.message: US FDA or EU GMP compliance selected. Ensure vendor qualification scope includes DQ + IQ/OQ + FAT as a minimum. 21 CFR Part 11-compliant electronic batch records will be required for automated equipment. Budget a regulatory documentation adder of 8–15% on equipment cost.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: 21 CFR Part 211 (US FDA cGMP for finished pharmaceuticals) and EU GMP Annex 15 (qualification and validation) require documented equipment qualification protocols. Electronic records under 21 CFR Part 11 require audit trail, access control, and system validation.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: oeb_containment == 'OEB-4 (high containment, 1–10 µg/m³, SMEPAC required)' OR oeb_containment == 'OEB-5 (isolator-grade, <1 µg/m³, split butterfly valves)'
compliance_rules.message: OEB-4 or OEB-5 selected. Standard equipment pricing does not apply — containment hardware (split butterfly valves, rapid transfer ports, glovebox interfaces, continuous air monitoring) adds 30–200%+ to base equipment cost. Engage a specialist containment consultant and revise the cost estimate accordingly.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISPE Good Practice Guide: Assessing the Particulate Containment Performance of Pharmaceutical Equipment (SMEPAC) defines test protocols for OEB-4/5. Equipment must demonstrate containment performance ≤1 µg/m³ TWA (OEB-5) or ≤10 µg/m³ (OEB-4) via surrogate powder SMEPAC testing before regulatory submission.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: atex_requirement IS not None AND atex_requirement != 'Not required (non-flammable dust/solvent, Zone 0 absent)'
compliance_rules.message: ATEX / hazardous area classification selected. Confirm area classification study (IEC 60079-10-1 for gas/vapour, IEC 60079-10-2 for dust) is complete and approved before finalising equipment specification. ATEX-rated equipment requires ATEX-certified motor, controls, and all electrical accessories — obtain confirmed ATEX adder from vendor.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079 series (explosive atmospheres) and ATEX Directive 2014/34/EU govern equipment certification for use in classified hazardous areas. In India, IS 5571 and PESO certification apply. Area classification must precede equipment specification — misclassification creates safety and insurance risk.

cost_build_up.basis_notes: Base cost is ex-works India (INR) for standard SS316L EP construction, Schedule M+ cGMP level, OEB-1, WIP provision, semi-automated PLC controls. Adder multipliers are applied sequentially on the base equipment cost. Regional cost index converts to project region. All costs in 2024 INR unless output_currency overridden.
cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_cost(equipment_subtype, volume OR throughput, motor_power) × oeb_multiplier(oeb_containment) × cgmp_multiplier(cgmp_level) × quantity
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_cost(equipment_subtype, volume OR throughput, motor_power) × oeb_multiplier(oeb_containment) × cgmp_multiplier(cgmp_level) × moc_multiplier(moc) × cip_multiplier(cip_provision) × surface_finish_multiplier(surface_finish) × instrumentation_multiplier(instrumentation_level) × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_cost(equipment_subtype, volume OR throughput, motor_power) × oeb_multiplier × cgmp_multiplier × moc_multiplier × cip_multiplier × surface_finish_multiplier × instrumentation_multiplier × jacket_multiplier(jacket_provision) × atex_multiplier(atex_requirement) × qualification_multiplier(vendor_qualification) × quantity

adders.notes: Freight/insurance: 4% ex-works to site (domestic India road freight, insured). Erection/installation: 12% — includes mechanical rigging, utilities hook-up, initial commissioning checks (lower than process vessels due to modular skid-mounted delivery). Commissioning: 5% — includes vendor engineer support, trial runs, SOPs. Contingency: 10% (Class 4 estimate). All adders on base equipment supply cost.

indicative_base_costs_usd_2024.note: Ex-works India approximate base costs (SS316L EP, Schedule M+, OEB-1, WIP, semi-auto PLC) at USA regional factor = 1.0, CEPCI 816. Apply multipliers and regional index for final estimate. Ranges reflect lab/pilot (lower) to large production scale (upper).

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only — not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level — apply to all items in the BOQ
project_settings.country: TODO: user selects at project level

usd_cost_baseline.note: USA-base USD (matches index base year 2024). Engine applies index_now/base x regional x sub_region factor x fx. ALL rates are public-benchmark ESTIMATES (verify=true) pending vendor/DSR calibration. base_cost USD per unit at stated datum; OEB/cGMP/MOC factors.

Multi Mill.basis: P&T-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 2800000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 3789054.73. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Sifter cum Multi Mill.basis: P&T-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 2800000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 3789054.73. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Colloid Mill.basis: P&T-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 2800000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 3789054.73. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Tablet Metal Detector.basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 900 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 1217.91. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

SCHEMA-PIPE-COATING.generation_note: Completed v1.0 - All engineering TODOs filled. Two sub-types with distinct cost mechanics: 3LPE (yard-applied per ISO 21809-1, dominated by chemicals + yard throughput) vs Wrapping (field-applied per AWWA C214 / NACE SP0245, dominated by labour). Cost model per m² of coated surface; surface area derived from NPS-to-OD lookup × pipe length.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-PIPE-COATING.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | System: {coating_system_3lpe}{wrap_tape_type} {wrap_configuration} | NPS {nps} × {total_length} m | Class {thickness_class} | Cutback {cutback_length} mm | Service <= {service_temperature} deg C | Application: {application_location} | Surface prep: {surface_preparation} | CWC: {concrete_weight_coating}
SCHEMA-PIPE-COATING.spec_example: Pipeline coating (3LPE) | System: 3LPE (PE topcoat) | NPS 12 × 1500 m | Class B (3.0 mm) | Cutback 150 mm | Service <= 65 deg C | Application: Coating yard (factory) | Surface prep: SSPC-SP10 / Sa 2.5 (near-white) | CWC: No

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: 3LPE = factory/yard-applied 3-layer polyethylene anti-corrosion system per ISO 21809-1 / DIN 30670 (FBE + adhesive + PE topcoat). Wrapping = field-applied tape system per AWWA C214 / NACE SP0245. 3LPE costs ~2-3x wrapping per m² but offers 30+ year design life vs 10-15 for tape wrap.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - always visible. Drives entire cost model branch (yard process vs field labour).

fields.label: 3LPE Coating System
fields.hint: 3LPE: workhorse for buried oil/gas/water pipelines, -40 to +80 deg C. 3LPP: high-temp service up to +110 deg C (DIN 30678). FBE alone: thin cathodic-disbondment-resistant primer for HDD (horizontal directional drilling). DFBE / FBE+ARO for rocky terrain. CTE is being phased out due to PAH environmental restrictions.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - shown only for 3LPE sub-type. System factor: 3LPE=1.00 (base), 3LPP=1.30, 2LPE=0.65, FBE=0.55, DFBE=0.85, FBE+ARO=1.15, CTE=0.95.

fields.label: Wrap Tape Type
options: Coal Tar Enamel + Glass Fiber (legacy hot-applied)
fields.hint: PE/PVC: standard cold-applied buried-pipe tape per AWWA C214. Butyl: better adhesion, slightly higher cost. Petrolatum (Denso): premium long-life >35 yrs, used for splash-zone and complex shapes. Bitumen: budget option. CTE: legacy, restricted in many jurisdictions. HSS: girth-weld field joints.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - shown only for Wrapping sub-type. Tape factor: PE/PVC=1.00 (base), Butyl=1.20, Petrolatum=1.40, Bitumen=0.90, CTE=1.10, HSS=2.20 (per joint), Wax=1.30.

fields.label: Wrap Configuration
fields.hint: Double-layer is the AWWA C214 / NACE SP0245 minimum for buried service > 5 yr design life. Single layer for above-ground or short-life service only. Triple layer adds rockshield/mechanical protection for rocky soil, road crossings, and HDD pull-back.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - shown only for Wrapping sub-type. Config factor: Single=0.65, Double=1.00 (base), Triple=1.45, Spiral=0.80.

fields.label: Coating Thickness Class
options: Class A (2.0 mm) - light duty / small bore
Class C (3.0-4.0 mm) - heavy duty / rocky soil
fields.hint: Per DIN 30670 / ISO 21809-1: Class A for NPS < 6 in protected service, Class B for standard buried service NPS 6-24, Class C for NPS > 24 or rocky soil / mechanical exposure. FBE thickness if FBE-only system: typically 350-500 microns regardless of class.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - shown only for 3LPE sub-type. Thickness factor: A=0.75, B=1.00 (base), C=1.25, Custom=1.10 (specialty).

fields.label: Service Temperature (max design)
fields.hint: Maximum continuous operating temperature of the fluid. 3LPE: -40 to +80 deg C. 3LPP: -20 to +110 deg C. FBE: up to +95 deg C. PE tape: up to +50 deg C. Butyl: up to +70 deg C. Petrolatum: up to +60 deg C. Service > 110 deg C requires specialty system (FBE+ARO or syntactic foam).
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - Budget. Temp factor: <= 80 deg C = 1.00, 80-110 deg C = 1.25 (forces 3LPP), > 110 deg C = 1.50 (specialty system).
fields.compliance_rule: If service_temperature > 80 and coating_system_3lpe includes 3LPE => warning (upgrade to 3LPP).

fields.label: Nominal Pipe Size (NPS)
fields.hint: Nominal Pipe Size per ASME B36.10M. Drives outer diameter (OD) for surface area calculation: surface_per_m = pi × OD. NPS 12 OD = 0.324 m gives 1.018 m²/m. For mixed-size scopes use the size-weighted average OD.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - Primary cost driver. Surface area scales linearly with OD (which scales with NPS for B36.10M sizes).

fields.label: Total Length to be Coated
fields.hint: Total linear pipe length receiving the coating treatment, excluding cutback ends. Typical pipeline coating yard scope is 5,000-50,000 m per project. For wrapping, scope is usually fittings/repairs (10-500 m) or whole-line (1,000-5,000 m).
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - Primary cost driver. Cost scales linearly with length.

fields.label: Pipe OD Override (non-standard)
fields.hint: Optional override of the OD lookup from NPS. Use only for non-ASME B36.10M sizes (e.g., DIN/EN sizes, custom pipeline pipe). Leave blank to use standard ASME OD lookup. Example: DN 600 metric = 610 mm OD vs ASME NPS 24 = 609.6 mm.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - Detailed. If left blank, formula uses NPS-to-OD lookup table per ASME B36.10M.

fields.label: Application Location
fields.hint: Application location is a major cost driver. Fixed coating yards are most efficient (high throughput, controlled environment, automated process). Mobile yards add 15%. Field application adds 40% (weather delays, manual labour, lower QC). 3LPE almost always yard-applied; wrapping usually field-applied.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - Major cost driver. App factor: Yard=1.00 (base), Mobile yard=1.15, Field=1.40, Off-site shop=1.20, Joint only=1.60 (per joint, low coverage rate).

fields.label: Surface Preparation Standard
options: SSPC-SP10 / Sa 2.5 (near-white blast - mandatory for FBE/3LPE)
SSPC-SP6 / Sa 2 (commercial blast - tape wrap OK)
fields.default: SSPC-SP10 / Sa 2.5 (near-white blast - mandatory for FBE/3LPE)
fields.hint: Per SSPC / ISO 8501-1 / NACE classifications. FBE-bonded systems (3LPE/3LPP/FBE/DFBE) require Sa 2.5 minimum for adhesion. Tape wraps tolerate Sa 2 commercial blast. Anchor profile typically 50-100 microns measured by replica tape per ASTM D4417.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - Budget. Surface prep factor: SP10=1.00 (base), SP5=1.10, SP6=0.85, SP3=0.70, SP11=1.05, SP1=0.55.

fields.label: Cutback Length (uncoated end)
fields.hint: Length of bare pipe at each end (uncoated) to allow field welding without coating damage. Standard 150 mm per ISO 21809-3. Smaller cutback (50-100 mm) requires shielded welding. Larger (300 mm) for HDD pull or thick-wall coating systems.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - Budget. Affects field-joint coating scope downstream.
fields.compliance_rule: If cutback_length < 100 => warning (insufficient for field weld + heat shrink sleeve).

fields.label: Concrete Weight Coating (CWC)
options: Yes - 40 mm CWC (river crossing / shallow water)
Yes - 100 mm CWC (heavy ballasting, deep water)
fields.hint: Concrete weight coating is applied over 3LPE for negative buoyancy in subsea pipelines per DNV-OS-F101 / ISO 21809-5. Concrete density 2400-3040 kg/m³. CWC adds 1.5-2.5x to coating cost. Not used for onshore buried lines.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - Budget. CWC factor: No=1.00 (base), 40 mm=1.55, 60 mm=1.85, 80 mm=2.20, 100 mm=2.50.

fields.label: Anti-Rodent / Anti-Termite Layer
fields.hint: Pipelines through rodent-prone areas (rural India, parts of SE Asia) or termite-prone soils need additional protection. Anti-rodent/termite additives blended into PE outer layer. Mechanical barrier (steel mesh) is the most reliable but adds 10-15%.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - Detailed. Anti-pest factor: None=1.00, Anti-rodent=1.06, Anti-termite=1.05, Both=1.12, Mech barrier=1.13.

fields.label: Field Joint Coating Type
options: Heat Shrink Sleeve (HSS, primary modern method)
fields.default: Heat Shrink Sleeve (HSS, primary modern method)
fields.hint: Field joint coating per ISO 21809-3 — covers the cutback zone after girth welding. HSS is the standard for 3LPE pipelines. Liquid epoxy for high-temp service. FBE patch for HDD/critical service. Each girth weld = 1 sleeve; estimate count from total_length / pipe_joint_length (typically 12 m / DRL).
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - Detailed. Field joint adder per joint (USD): HSS=$45-150 by NPS, Liquid epoxy=$25-80, FBE patch=$60-180, Cold tape=$15-40, Visco=$80-200.

fields.label: Holiday (Jeep) Test Voltage
fields.hint: DC holiday detection per NACE SP0490 / RP0274. Rule of thumb: V = sqrt(C × thickness_in_mils) for DC holiday detector, or simplified 5 kV/mm of dielectric thickness. Typical: 3LPE 3 mm = 15-25 kV; FBE 0.5 mm = 1.5-3 kV; Tape wrap 1.5 mm = 7-10 kV.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - Detailed. Test voltage informational; impacts QC rate (more rejections = rework cost adder).
fields.compliance_rule: If holiday_voltage < 5 * thickness_mm => info (review NACE SP0490 V-formula).

fields.label: Peel Adhesion Test Specification
options: Per DIN 30670 (>= 100 N/cm at 23 deg C)
Per ISO 21809-1 Annex H (>= 35 N/cm hot-applied test)
Per ASTM D4541 (Pull-off > 12 MPa for FBE)
fields.default: Per DIN 30670 (>= 100 N/cm at 23 deg C)
fields.hint: Adhesion test confirms long-term bond between pipe and coating. DIN 30670 for 3LPE, ASTM D4541 (pull-off) for FBE, AWWA C214 for tape. Hot-applied PE adhesion test at elevated temp is more discriminating than 23 deg C test.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - Detailed. QC test factor: standard tests=1.00, project spec=1.03, none=0.98.

fields.label: Cathodic Disbondment (CD) Test
options: ASTM G8 (28 days, +65 deg C, < 8 mm radius)
ASTM G42 (24 hr, elevated temp, qualification)
ASTM G80 (28 days, full qualification, < 6.5 mm radius)
ISO 21809-1 Annex L (28 days, 65 deg C)
fields.default: ISO 21809-1 Annex L (28 days, 65 deg C)
fields.hint: Mandatory for pipelines with cathodic protection (CP). Tests resistance to coating disbondment at CP-induced holidays. 3LPE without CD qualification has failed catastrophically on multiple high-profile pipelines. ASTM G8/G42/G80 are progressively more stringent.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - Detailed. CD test factor: standard=1.00, G80 stringent=1.05, project-spec=1.07, None=0.97 (only for non-CP service).
fields.compliance_rule: If equipment_subtype = '3LPE' and cd_test = 'None' and service_category includes 'buried' => warning (CD qualification mandatory for CP-protected lines).

fields.label: Coating Inspector / NACE Certification Level
fields.hint: Per NACE No. 1 / SP0188. Level 2 (project-level QC) is the de-facto minimum for pipeline coating projects. Level 3 (senior inspector) for offshore / sour service / Cat M. FROSIO for Norwegian / European offshore. Inspector cost ~$200-500/day depending on level and region.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - Detailed. Inspector factor: L1=0.97, L2=1.00 (base), L3=1.04, BGAS=1.04, FROSIO=1.06, Owner rep=0.99.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical coating scopes (Jobs). Each Job applies the coating system to total_length above. For a single coating contract, leave at 1.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipeline coating (3LPE)' AND '3LPE' IN coating_system_3lpe AND !'3LPP' IN coating_system_3lpe AND service_temperature > 80
compliance_rules.message: 3LPE is rated to +80 deg C continuous service per DIN 30670 / ISO 21809-1. For 80-110 deg C upgrade to 3LPP (3-Layer Polypropylene per DIN 30678). Above 110 deg C use FBE+ARO or syntactic foam systems. Continued use of 3LPE above 80 deg C leads to thermal degradation, adhesion loss, and topcoat embrittlement.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: DIN 30670 Sec 4.2 (PE topcoat thermal limit); ISO 21809-1 Sec 4.4 Table 4 (service temperature classification); DIN 30678 (3LPP for high-temp).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipeline coating (3LPE)' AND ('3LPE' IN coating_system_3lpe OR '3LPP' IN coating_system_3lpe OR 'FBE' IN coating_system_3lpe) AND surface_preparation != 'SSPC-SP10 / Sa 2.5 (near-white blast - mandatory for FBE/3LPE)' AND surface_preparation != 'SSPC-SP5 / Sa 3 (white metal - specialty)'
compliance_rules.message: FBE-bonded systems (3LPE / 3LPP / FBE / DFBE) require near-white blast cleaning (SSPC-SP10 / Sa 2.5 per ISO 8501-1) at minimum to achieve specified peel adhesion (>= 100 N/cm) and pass cathodic disbondment qualification. Lower surface prep grades cause field disbondment within 2-5 years of CP exposure.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 21809-1 Sec 7.3 (Surface preparation); NACE SP0394 Sec 4.2 (FBE substrate prep); ISO 8501-1 (visual standard); ASTM D4417 (anchor profile measurement).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: cutback_length < 100
compliance_rules.message: Cutback length below 100 mm leaves insufficient bare metal for field girth welding plus heat shrink sleeve coverage. Standard practice per ISO 21809-3 is 150 mm cutback (range 100-300 mm). Insufficient cutback risks coating damage during welding (charring, blistering) and inadequate sleeve overlap onto factory coating.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 21809-3 Sec 6 (Field joint coatings - cutback dimensions); ASME B31.4 Sec 434.6 (welding offset from coating).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: holiday_test_voltage > 0 AND holiday_test_voltage < 5
compliance_rules.message: Holiday detection voltage may be below NACE SP0490 / RP0274 recommendations. Rule of thumb: V_DC = 5 kV per mm of dielectric thickness (range 4-8 kV/mm). For 3LPE 3.0 mm, use 15-25 kV. For FBE 0.5 mm, use 2-3 kV. For tape wrap 1.5 mm, use 7-10 kV. Sub-threshold voltage misses small holidays which become field corrosion cells.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NACE SP0490-2007 (formerly RP0490) Sec 4.2; NACE RP0274 Sec 5; ASTM G62 Method B (DC high-voltage holiday detection).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipeline coating (3LPE)' AND cathodic_disbondment_test == 'None (low CP exposure, e.g., utility)' AND ('3LPE' IN coating_system_3lpe OR 'FBE' IN coating_system_3lpe)
compliance_rules.message: Cathodic disbondment (CD) qualification is mandatory for pipelines with cathodic protection per ISO 21809-1 / NACE SP0394. 3LPE / FBE coatings without CD qualification have failed catastrophically (Trans-Mediterranean, Yamal, several Indian gas grids) due to disbondment cells under CP at <2 years service. Specify ASTM G8 or ISO 21809-1 Annex L minimum.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 21809-1 Annex L (CD test method); NACE SP0394 Sec 6.6; ASTM G8 / G42 / G80; CSA Z245.20 Sec 12 (FBE CD requirement).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipeline wrapping' AND wrap_configuration == 'Single layer (1 wrap, 50% overlap)' AND wrap_tape_type != 'Petrolatum tape (Denso / Trenton, NACE SP0245)'
compliance_rules.message: Single-layer tape wrap provides marginal long-term protection for buried service. NACE SP0245 / AWWA C214 Sec 4 specify minimum double-wrap (Inner + Outer at 50% overlap each = 100% effective coverage) for design lives > 5 years. Petrolatum tape is the only single-wrap exception due to self-healing properties. Single PE/PVC tape commonly delaminates within 8-10 years in soil with active CP.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: AWWA C214 Sec 4.3 (minimum two-layer system); NACE SP0245 Sec 6 (tape wrap configuration); ASTM D1000 (tape adhesion test).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipeline coating (3LPE)' AND concrete_weight_coating != 'No (onshore / non-buoyant)' AND 'FBE' IN coating_system_3lpe
compliance_rules.message: Concrete Weight Coating (CWC) over FBE-only system is unusual. Standard subsea pipeline construction is 3LPE + CWC (or 3LPP + CWC for high-temp). FBE alone provides corrosion protection but lacks the mechanical robustness for CWC application + lay barge handling. Confirm system suitability with CWC supplier.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: DNV-OS-F101 Sec 7 (subsea pipeline coating systems); ISO 21809-5 (concrete weight coating); API RP 1111 Sec 6.4.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipeline wrapping' AND wrap_tape_type == 'Coal Tar Enamel + Glass Fiber (legacy hot-applied)'
compliance_rules.message: Coal Tar Enamel (CTE) coatings are restricted in EU, UK, several US states, and Australia due to PAH (polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon) and benzo[a]pyrene environmental and worker-health concerns. Disposal of CTE-coated pipe is regulated as hazardous waste in many jurisdictions. Verify regulatory acceptance and consider 3LPE/FBE alternatives before specifying.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: EU REACH Regulation Annex XVII Entry 50 (PAH restriction); US EPA RCRA Subtitle C; AS/NZS 4321; NACE SP0185-2014 Appendix A (legacy material discussion).

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: tier_1_cost_USD = quantity × total_length × pipe_surface_per_m × base_rate × system_factor × app_loc_factor; where pipe_surface_per_m = pi × OD_m (OD lookup from NPS per ASME B36.10M: NPS 1=0.0334 m, 2=0.0603 m, 4=0.1143 m, 6=0.1683 m, 8=0.2191 m, 10=0.2731 m, 12=0.3239 m, 14=0.3556 m, 16=0.4064 m, 18=0.4572 m, 20=0.5080 m, 24=0.6096 m, 30=0.7620 m, 36=0.9144 m, 42=1.0668 m, 48=1.2192 m); base_rate (USD/m² of pipe surface): 3LPE branch base = 18 USD/m² (3LPE Class B 3 mm at coating yard); Wrapping branch base = 12 USD/m² (PE/PVC double-wrap field-applied); system_factor (3LPE branch): 3LPE=1.00, 3LPP=1.30, 2LPE=0.65, FBE=0.55, DFBE=0.85, FBE+ARO=1.15, CTE=0.95; system_factor (Wrap branch) = wrap_tape_factor × wrap_config_factor; wrap_tape_factor: PE/PVC=1.00, Butyl=1.20, Petrolatum=1.40, Bitumen=0.90, CTE=1.10, HSS=2.20, Wax=1.30; wrap_config_factor: Single=0.65, Double=1.00, Triple=1.45, Spiral=0.80; app_loc_factor: Yard=1.00, Mobile yard=1.15, Field=1.40, Off-site shop=1.20, Joint only=1.60
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: tier_2_cost_USD = tier_1_cost × thickness_class_factor × surface_prep_factor × service_temp_factor × cwc_factor + cutback_adjustment; where thickness_class_factor (3LPE only): A=0.75, B=1.00, C=1.25, Custom=1.10 (Wrap branch: not applicable, set to 1.00); surface_prep_factor: SP10=1.00, SP5=1.10, SP6=0.85, SP3=0.70, SP11=1.05, SP1=0.55; service_temp_factor: T <= 80 deg C = 1.00, 80 < T <= 110 = 1.25, T > 110 = 1.50; cwc_factor (3LPE only): No=1.00, 40 mm=1.55, 60 mm=1.85, 80 mm=2.20, 100 mm=2.50 (Wrap branch: not applicable, set to 1.00); cutback_adjustment: subtract (2 × cutback_length_m × pipe_surface_per_m × base_rate × system_factor) per pipe joint (typically 12 m DRL) to account for uncoated ends
cost_build_up.full_formula: total_cost_USD = tier_2_cost × anti_pest_factor × inspector_factor × cd_test_factor × peel_test_factor + field_joint_total + (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency) × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + import_duty_total_landed_adder_pct/100 if equipment_origin = Imported else 0); where anti_pest_factor: None=1.00, Anti-rodent=1.06, Anti-termite=1.05, Both=1.12, Mech barrier=1.13; inspector_factor: L1=0.97, L2=1.00, L3=1.04, BGAS=1.04, FROSIO=1.06, Owner rep=0.99, Not specified=0.97; cd_test_factor: standard tests=1.00, G80 stringent=1.05, project-spec=1.07, None=0.97; peel_test_factor: standard tests=1.00, project spec=1.03, none=0.98; field_joint_total = (total_length / pipe_joint_length_m) × cost_per_joint; pipe_joint_length_m = 12 (DRL) typical; cost_per_joint by NPS and field_joint_coating type: HSS NPS 4 = $45, NPS 12 = $80, NPS 24 = $130, NPS 36 = $180; Liquid epoxy = 0.55 × HSS rate; FBE patch = 1.30 × HSS rate; Cold tape = 0.30 × HSS rate; Visco = 1.50 × HSS rate; Not in scope = 0

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation. NOTE: regional values inherited from auto-generator are tagged for steel/process equipment; coating service is more labour-dominated and these factors are conservative for India/China/ASEAN (actual labour-driven factor would be 0.30-0.45 vs 0.42-0.48 shown). Acceptable accuracy at +-30% Tier-1 estimate level.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight. For coating service, the equipment_factor represents specialty chemicals + yard plant; labour_factor dominates for field-applied wrapping.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code (typically 3208/3209 for paints, 3919 for tape) and current rate with customs broker before use.

SCHEMA-PIPE-CS-SUPPLY.generation_note: Completed v1.0 - All engineering TODOs filled. CS grades (A53/A106/API 5L) and AS grades (A335 P-series) handled via conditional fields. Cost model uses NPS^1.6 weight scaling with material/schedule/process factors per 2024 USD baseline.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-PIPE-CS-SUPPLY.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | Grade {material_grade_cs}{material_grade_as} | NPS {nps} {schedule} | {mfg_process} {pipe_end_type} | {total_length} m | DP {design_pressure} barg @ DT {design_temperature} deg C | Service: {service_category} | Coating: {external_coating}
SCHEMA-PIPE-CS-SUPPLY.spec_example: Carbon steel pipe supply | Grade ASTM A106 Gr B | NPS 4 Sch 40 | Seamless BE | 250 m | DP 25 barg @ DT 200 deg C | Service: Process - Flammable (B31.3 Normal Fluid) | Coating: Shop primer + paint system

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the pipe material family. Carbon steel covers A53/A106/API 5L grades for general process and pipeline service. Alloy steel covers A335 P-series (Cr-Mo) for high-temperature and creep service. Controls which grade dropdown is shown below.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - always visible. Determines material family and base cost band (CS ~1x, AS 2-8x).

fields.label: Carbon Steel Grade
options: ASTM A106 Gr A (seamless, low strength)
ASTM A106 Gr B (seamless, standard process)
ASTM A106 Gr C (seamless, high strength)
fields.default: ASTM A106 Gr B (seamless, standard process)
fields.hint: ASTM A53 = utility/firewater; A106 Gr B = workhorse for process service up to 482 deg C; A333 Gr 6 = impact-tested for cryogenic / low-temp; API 5L PSL2 grades for pipeline / high pressure. IS grades for India domestic procurement.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - shown only when sub-type = Carbon steel. Material factor range 0.75-1.65x.

fields.label: Alloy Steel Grade
options: ASTM A335 P1 (0.5Mo, up to 510 deg C)
ASTM A335 P11 (1.25Cr-0.5Mo, up to 565 deg C)
ASTM A335 P22 (2.25Cr-1Mo, up to 580 deg C)
ASTM A335 P91 (9Cr-1Mo-V-Nb, USC steam up to 620 deg C)
ASTM A335 P92 (advanced 9Cr, USC up to 625 deg C)
fields.default: ASTM A335 P22 (2.25Cr-1Mo, up to 580 deg C)
fields.hint: Cr-Mo seamless ferritic alloy pipe per ASME B31.1/B31.3 high-temperature service. P11/P22 for refinery and steam; P91/P92 for ultra-supercritical power. P91/P92 require strict heat treatment (N+T) and PWHT after welding.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - shown only when sub-type = Alloy steel. Material factor range 1.8-8.5x vs CS A106 B base.

fields.label: Manufacturing Process
options: Furnace Butt Welded (FBW) - small bore only
fields.hint: Seamless is mandatory for A335 alloy pipe and high-pressure hydrocarbon service. ERW for utility / low-pressure CS up to NPS 24. LSAW/SSAW for large-diameter pipeline (NPS 16+). All welded grades require long-seam NDT for hazardous service.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - Budget. Mfg factor: SMLS=1.00, ERW=0.70, LSAW=0.85, SSAW=0.75, FBW=0.55.

fields.label: Pipe End Type
options: Beveled End (BE) - 30 deg / 37.5 deg per ASME B16.25
Threaded End (TE) - NPT per ASME B1.20.1
fields.default: Beveled End (BE) - 30 deg / 37.5 deg per ASME B16.25
fields.hint: BE is standard for butt-welded process piping. Threaded only for small bore (NPS 2 and below) galvanized utility. Grooved for fast mechanical-coupling installation. Flanged-ended is uncommon (flanges usually welded at fab shop).
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - Budget. End factor: PE=1.00, BE=1.04, TE=1.10, GE=1.08, FE=1.30, SC=0.98.

fields.label: Nominal Pipe Size (NPS)
fields.hint: Nominal Pipe Size per ASME B36.10M. Use the dominant size in the supply scope; for mixed-size scopes use the size-weighted average. Pipe weight (and cost) scales approximately as NPS^1.6 for a fixed schedule.

fields.label: Schedule / Wall Thickness
fields.hint: Pipe wall thickness per ASME B36.10M. Verify thickness adequacy by Barlow's formula t = (P x D) / (2 x (S x E + P x Y)) per ASME B31.3 Sec 304.1. Schedule cost factor relative to Sch 40: Sch 10 = 0.55, Sch 80 = 1.40, Sch 160 = 2.20, XXS = 2.50.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - Primary cost driver. Wall thickness drives steel weight per meter linearly.

fields.label: Total Pipe Length
fields.hint: Total linear length of pipe in this supply scope (excluding fittings, flanges). Add 3-5% wastage for cut-offs and trim allowance separately at quote stage if not already included.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - Primary cost driver. Cost scales linearly with length.

fields.label: Pipe Length per Piece
fields.hint: DRL minimizes field welds and is preferred for process piping. SRL for handling-constrained sites. Fixed-length increases unit cost by 3-7% due to mill cut and trim loss.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - Detailed. Length-per-piece factor: DRL=1.00, SRL=1.02, Fixed=1.05, Mill std=0.99.

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum design pressure governing wall thickness selection per ASME B31.3 Sec 304.1. For sustained pressures > 50 barg, verify schedule selection against Barlow's formula.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - Budget. Drives schedule selection check; pressure-class adder above 100 barg.
fields.compliance_rule: If design_pressure > 50 and schedule in [Sch 5, Sch 10, Sch 20, Sch 30] => warning (verify wall thickness).

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum design temperature. CS limited to 425 deg C continuous (creep / graphitization), 482 deg C absolute per ASME B31.3 Table A-1. Above 425 deg C use A335 P-series. Below -29 deg C use impact-tested grade (A333 Gr 6 or austenitic SS).
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - Budget. Drives material grade selection; high-temp surcharge for AS implicit in grade choice.
fields.compliance_rule: If design_temperature > 425 and equipment_subtype = Carbon steel => error (graphitization risk, switch to A335).

fields.label: Service Category
options: Steam - Superheated (> 425 deg C, alloy required)
fields.hint: Service category drives ASME B31.3 fluid classification, MTC requirement, NDT level, and impact-test requirement. Sour service requires NACE MR0175 hardness control. Cryogenic requires impact-tested grade. Cat M requires 100% NDT.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - Budget. Service factor: Utility=1.00, Cat M=1.20, Sour=1.30, Cryogenic=1.40.

fields.label: Corrosion Allowance
fields.hint: Corrosion allowance added to calculated pressure-design wall thickness. Typical: 0 mm for SS / non-corrosive utility, 1.5 mm for clean process, 3 mm for hydrocarbon, 6 mm for sour / sea-water / amine service.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - Detailed. Larger CA may force schedule upgrade.

fields.label: Mill Test Certificate (EN 10204)
fields.hint: 3.1 is the de-facto minimum for process piping. 3.2 (third-party witness, e.g., Lloyd's, BV, TUV, DNV) required for offshore, cryogenic, Cat M, and most owner-operator specs (Shell DEP, ExxonMobil, Aramco SAES).

fields.label: Hydrostatic Test Pressure
fields.hint: Mill hydrotest pressure per ASME B31.3 Sec 345.4.2 / API 5L PSL2 Sec 9.4. Standard rule: P_hydro = 1.5 x P_design (cold). For pipeline service, use the lesser of mill standard hydro and 1.5 x MAOP.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - Detailed. Defaults to 1.5 x design_pressure if not overridden.
fields.compliance_rule: If hydrotest_pressure < 1.5 * design_pressure => info (review against B31.3 Sec 345).

fields.label: NDT Requirement (Mill / Pipe Body)
fields.hint: Mill-level NDT only (field weld NDT is in piping erection scope). LSAW/SSAW pipe for hydrocarbon / Cat M service requires 100% long-seam RT or UT per API 5L PSL2 Sec 9.7. Sour service adds hardness survey per NACE.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - Detailed. NDT factor: Mill std=1.00, Spot RT=1.03, 100% RT=1.10, 100% UT=1.08, Full NDT=1.20.

fields.label: Heat Treatment Condition
fields.hint: A335 P11/P22 require N+T (normalize + temper). A335 P91/P92 require strict N+T at 1040-1080 deg C / 730-800 deg C with hardness 180-250 HBW per ASME B31.1. Sour service CS should be Normalized (not as-rolled) to control hardness <= 22 HRC.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - Detailed. HT factor: As-rolled=1.00, N=1.03, N+T=1.06, Q+T=1.08, Annealed=1.05.

fields.label: External Coating / Protection
options: Shop primer + paint system (epoxy/PU per spec)
fields.default: Shop primer + paint system (epoxy/PU per spec)
fields.hint: External corrosion protection per project paint spec. 3LPE/FBE for buried pipeline (NACE SP0169). Galvanized for utility / firewater. Most aboveground process pipes get shop primer + final paint applied at site after erection.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - Budget. Coating factor: None=1.00, Primer=1.03, Paint sys=1.08, Galv=1.15, FBE=1.18, 3LPE=1.20.

fields.label: Internal Lining
options: Cement Mortar Lining (CML) per AWWA C205
Liquid Epoxy Lining per AWWA C210
fields.hint: Internal lining for water service (CML for raw/firewater above NPS 6), or aggressive process fluids. CML adds ~25% to pipe cost. Most hydrocarbon / steam service does NOT require internal lining.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - Detailed. Lining factor: None=1.00, CML=1.25, Epoxy=1.18, FBE int=1.15, Rubber=1.30.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical supply scopes (Jobs). Each Job contains the total_length entered above. For a single supply package, leave at 1.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Carbon steel pipe supply' AND design_temperature > 425
compliance_rules.message: Carbon steel pipe is not suitable for continuous service above 425 deg C due to creep and graphitization risk. Switch sub-type to 'Alloy steel pipe supply' and select A335 P11 (up to 565 deg C), P22 (up to 580 deg C), or P91 (up to 620 deg C) based on design temperature.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Table A-1 (Allowable stresses) + ASME II Part D. ASME B31.1 PG-19 limits CS to 425 deg C for power piping; B31.3 Sec 323 sets material temperature limits.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (service_category == 'Cryogenic (< -29 deg C)' OR design_temperature < -29) AND equipment_subtype == 'Carbon steel pipe supply' AND !'A333' IN material_grade_cs
compliance_rules.message: Cryogenic / low-temperature service below -29 deg C requires impact-tested grade. Use ASTM A333 Gr 6 (Charpy impact at -45 deg C) or austenitic stainless steel (A312 TP304/316). Standard A53/A106 fails brittle-fracture requirements per ASME B31.3 Sec 323.2.2.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Sec 323.2.2 + Fig 323.2.2A (low-temperature curve). ASTM A333 Gr 6 specifies Charpy impact testing at -45 deg C with 27 J avg / 20 J min.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: service_category == 'Sour service (NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156)' AND (heat_treatment == 'As-rolled / As-extruded' OR '2.1' IN mtc_type OR '2.2' IN mtc_type)
compliance_rules.message: Sour service per NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156-2 requires hardness <= 22 HRC (250 HV10) and Sulfide Stress Cracking (SSC) test compliance. Specify Normalized (or N+T) heat treatment, EN 10204 3.1 minimum certificate, and request mill hardness survey + SSC qualification report.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156-2:2020 Sec 7. Carbon steels in sour service must satisfy hardness limits and SSC qualification (NACE TM0177 Method A).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Alloy steel pipe supply' AND ('P91' IN material_grade_as OR 'P92' IN material_grade_as) AND heat_treatment != 'Normalized + Tempered (N+T)'
compliance_rules.message: ASTM A335 P91/P92 (modified 9Cr-1Mo) requires strict Normalized + Tempered heat treatment: N at 1040-1080 deg C, T at 730-800 deg C, with delivered hardness 180-250 HBW. Mandatory Charpy V-notch impact testing at room temperature (40 J avg / 27 J min). PWHT also required after field welding.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.1 PG-19 + ASTM A335 Sec 7.2 + EPRI P91 Steels Materials and Welding Guidelines. P91 sub-critical tempering (e.g., during PWHT) is critical to achieve required creep strength.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: ('LSAW' IN mfg_process OR 'SSAW' IN mfg_process OR 'ERW' IN mfg_process) AND ('Hydrocarbon' IN service_category OR 'Hazardous' IN service_category OR 'Sour' IN service_category OR 'Flammable' IN service_category) AND ndt_requirement == 'Mill standard (per ASTM / API spec)'
compliance_rules.message: Welded pipe (ERW/LSAW/SSAW) for hazardous / hydrocarbon / sour service should have 100% radiographic or ultrasonic inspection of the longitudinal weld seam. Upgrade NDT to '100% RT' or '100% UT of long seam'. API 5L PSL2 includes seamless or 100% UT'd seam by default.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 5L PSL2 Sec 9.7 (mandatory long-seam UT). ASME B31.3 Table 341.3.2 Examination Requirements for Normal Fluid Service. Cat M requires 100% RT/UT of all welds.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: ('Hazardous' IN service_category OR 'Sour' IN service_category OR 'Cryogenic' IN service_category OR 'Cat M' IN service_category) AND ('2.1' IN mtc_type OR '2.2' IN mtc_type)
compliance_rules.message: Hazardous / Cat M / sour / cryogenic service requires EN 10204 3.1 inspection certificate minimum. For offshore, Cat M, and most owner-operator specifications (Shell DEP 31.38.01, Aramco SAES-L-100, ExxonMobil GP), 3.2 (third-party witness) is recommended.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: EN 10204:2004 + ASME B31.3 Sec 300.2 fluid service categories. 3.1 = manufacturer inspection certificate; 3.2 = independent inspection (Lloyd's / BV / TUV / DNV witnessed).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 50 AND (schedule == 'Sch 5' OR schedule == 'Sch 10' OR schedule == 'Sch 20' OR schedule == 'Sch 30')
compliance_rules.message: Selected schedule may be inadequate for design pressure > 50 barg. Verify wall thickness using Barlow's formula t = (P x D) / (2 x (S x E + P x Y)) per ASME B31.3 Sec 304.1.2, including corrosion allowance, mill tolerance (-12.5% on wall), and threading/grooving deductions.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Sec 304.1.2 (Internal Pressure Design - Straight Pipe). Mill under-tolerance per ASTM A106 Sec 12.4 = 12.5% on nominal wall.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: hydrotest_pressure > 0 AND hydrotest_pressure < (1.5 * design_pressure)
compliance_rules.message: Mill hydrostatic test pressure should be at least 1.5 x design pressure (cold) per ASME B31.3 Sec 345.4.2 and API 5L PSL2 Sec 9.4. Confirm test is held >= 5 seconds (mill) and visual examination per ASTM standard.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Sec 345.4.2 (Leak Test) + API 5L PSL2 Sec 9.4.3 (Hydrostatic Inspection). Pipe mill hydro is the certification basis; field leak test follows separately during commissioning.

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: tier_1_cost_USD = quantity × total_length × base_rate_4in_Sch40_A106B × nps_factor × schedule_factor × material_factor; where base_rate_4in_Sch40_A106B = 35 USD/m (2024 Q4 baseline ex-works); nps_factor = (NPS_inch / 4)^1.6 [e.g., NPS 1=0.18, NPS 4=1.00, NPS 12=4.30, NPS 24=12.0]; schedule_factor: Sch 5=0.45, Sch 10=0.55, Sch 20=0.70, Sch 30=0.85, Sch STD/40=1.00, Sch 60=1.20, Sch XS/80=1.40, Sch 100=1.60, Sch 120=1.80, Sch 140=2.00, Sch 160=2.20, Sch XXS=2.50; material_factor (CS): A53 B=0.75, A106 A=0.95, A106 B=1.00, A106 C=1.10, A333 Gr 6=1.45, API 5L Gr B PSL1=1.05, X42=1.15, X52=1.25, X60=1.40, X65=1.50, X70=1.65, IS 1239=0.80, IS 3589=0.85; material_factor (AS): P1=1.80, P11=2.50, P22=3.20, P5=4.00, P9=5.00, P91=6.50, P92=8.50, A691=2.80
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: tier_2_cost_USD = tier_1_cost × mfg_factor × end_factor × coating_factor × pressure_class_adder × service_factor; where mfg_factor: SMLS=1.00, ERW=0.70, LSAW/DSAW=0.85, SSAW/HSAW=0.75, FBW=0.55; end_factor: PE=1.00, BE=1.04, TE=1.10, GE=1.08, FE=1.30, SC=0.98; coating_factor: None=1.00, Shop primer=1.03, Primer+paint=1.08, Galv=1.15, 3LPE=1.20, 3LPP=1.22, FBE ext=1.18, CTE=1.10, Insul jacket=1.30, Acid-resist=1.12; pressure_class_adder = 1.0 if design_pressure ≤ 100 barg else 1 + 0.005 × (design_pressure - 100); service_factor: Utility=1.00, Firewater=1.02, Cat D=1.02, Normal Fluid=1.05, Cat M=1.20, Steam LP=1.05, Steam HP=1.10, Steam SH=1.15, Sour=1.30, HC light=1.05, HC heavy=1.05, Cryogenic=1.40, Caustic/Acid=1.25
cost_build_up.full_formula: total_cost_USD = tier_2_cost × mtc_factor × ndt_factor × ht_factor × lining_factor × length_per_piece_factor × corrosion_allowance_factor × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency) × regional_factor × sub_region_equipment_factor × (1 + inland_freight_pct/100) × (1 + import_duty_total_landed_adder_pct/100 if equipment_origin = Imported else 0); where mtc_factor: 2.1=1.00, 2.2=1.02, 3.1=1.05, 3.2=1.10; ndt_factor: Mill std=1.00, Hydrotest only=1.02, Spot RT=1.03, 100% RT=1.10, 100% UT seam=1.08, 100% UT body=1.15, ECT=1.04, Full NDT=1.20, Hardness survey=1.05; ht_factor: As-rolled=1.00, N=1.03, N+T=1.06, Q+T=1.08, Annealed=1.05, Solution=1.07, PWHT=1.08; lining_factor: None=1.00, CML=1.25, Epoxy=1.18, Phenolic=1.20, FBE int=1.15, GRE=1.35, Rubber=1.30; length_per_piece_factor: SRL=1.02, DRL=1.00, Fixed=1.05, Mill std=0.99; corrosion_allowance_factor = 1.0 (informational; CA already drives schedule selection upstream; apply only +2% if CA > 3 mm to capture extra wall in non-standard wall pipe)

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.
