SCHEMA-PIPE-FABR-ERECT.generation_note: Completed v1.0 - Pipe fabrication & erection (9 library items). Unit-rate method, ENR CCI-indexed (14,250 base, 2024). conditional_display surfaces only fields relevant for fab vs erect and pipe vs rack/support. Covers ASME B31.3, B16.5, B36.10M/19M, A333 LTCS.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-PIPE-FABR-ERECT.spec_example: Carbon steel pipe fabrication | NPS 6" | Sch 40 (Std) | ASTM A106 Gr.B (CS seamless) | Hydrocarbon liquid (non-flammable at ambient) | DP 25 barg | DT 150 °C | Qty 1

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of BOQ line items of this sub-type. Typically 1. Use scope fields below for the physical quantity (metres, spools, welds).

fields.label: Pipe Size (NPS)
fields.hint: Nominal pipe size per ASME B36.10M / B36.19M. Drives wall-thickness, weight, and labour-hour rate. Not applicable for pure rack/support erection - enter 4" as placeholder.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary cost driver alongside material

fields.label: Pipe Schedule / Wall Thickness
fields.hint: Wall thickness schedule per ASME B36.10M (carbon steel) or B36.19M (SS). Heavier schedule -> higher material + welding cost.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - directly sets material weight and weld prep effort

fields.label: Total Pipe Length
fields.hint: Total centre-line length of pipe in metres (for fabrication, pipe erection, rack, and support activities). For spool erection, use the Spool Count field instead.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary quantity driver for pipe-length unit-rate costing

fields.label: Spool Count
fields.hint: Number of pre-fabricated pipe spools to be fabricated or erected. Each spool typically 3-12 m pipe + 2-6 welds.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary quantity driver for spool activities

fields.label: Material Grade
options: API 5L Gr.X42 / X52 / X65 (CS line pipe)
Structural carbon steel (A36 / S275 / IS 2062) - for rack/support only
fields.hint: Material grade per applicable ASTM/API/EN standard. Alloy and SS grades attract significant material and welding cost premiums. A333 Gr.6 required for CS service below -29 °C. Structural grade only for rack/support.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - major cost driver; select for budget accuracy

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum allowable operating pressure (MAOP). Determines ASME B16.5 / B16.47 flange class and minimum wall thickness per ASME B31.3. Not applicable for pure rack/support erection.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - governs pressure class, flange rating and wall thickness uplift

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum operating temperature. Temperatures > 200 °C may require alloy steel or stress-relief PWHT; < -29 °C triggers low-temperature impact testing (LTCS A333 Gr.6). Not applicable for rack/support.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - triggers material class change and PWHT cost adders

fields.label: Service Fluid / Fluid Category
options: Toxic / lethal (Category M per ASME B31.3)
fields.hint: Fluid category per ASME B31.3 Table 300.4. Category M (toxic/lethal) mandates 100% radiographic examination. Sour service triggers NACE MR0175/ISO 15156 material restrictions.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - drives NDE scope, painting spec and insulation requirement

fields.label: Flange Rating (ASME B16.5 / B16.47)
fields.hint: Pressure class for flanges and fittings. Higher classes (>= #600) require forged fittings, BW joints, and RFWN flanges - substantial cost premium.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - drives flange and fitting material cost; #600+ approx 3x #150 cost

fields.label: NDE / Inspection Level
options: Normal (5% RT/UT, 10% PT/MT - ASME B31.3 Cl.Normal)
Category D (visual only - ASME B31.3 Cl.D)
Category M (100% RT - ASME B31.3 Cl.M lethal/toxic)
High-integrity (100% RT + PWHT + hardness - B31.3 Cl.High-Pressure)
IBR (100% RT per Indian Boiler Regulations)
fields.default: Normal (5% RT/UT, 10% PT/MT - ASME B31.3 Cl.Normal)
fields.hint: NDE scope drives inspection man-hours and rework allowances. 100% RT (Category M / HP) can add 15-25% to fabrication cost.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - NDE adds 5-25% to fabrication cost depending on class

fields.label: Insulation / Heat Tracing
fields.hint: Insulation and heat tracing are typically 8-15% of total piping installed cost and specified separately; include here for scope completeness.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - scope completeness; insulation priced separately

fields.label: Painting / Surface Treatment
options: 3-coat system (primer + MIO intermediate + finish) - NORSOK M-501 System 1
fields.default: 3-coat system (primer + MIO intermediate + finish) - NORSOK M-501 System 1
fields.hint: Surface prep and painting typically 3-8% of piping installed cost. NORSOK M-501 / ISO 12944 governs corrosion category selection. Select 'None' for SS/alloy passivated pipe.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - minor at spool level; important for pipe rack / structural scope

fields.label: PWHT Required
options: Required - per ASME B31.3 mandatory (alloy / thick wall)
fields.hint: PWHT mandatory for P-No.4 (Cr-Mo) and P-No.5 alloys and CS >= 19mm wall per ASME B31.3 cl.331. Local field PWHT approx 10-15% premium on weld cost.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - PWHT adds 10-15% on weld cost for applicable welds

fields.label: Shop vs. Field Fabrication Split
fields.hint: Field welding is 1.5-2.5x more expensive per weld than shop fabrication. Higher field content increases cost; shop prefab improves quality and speed.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - field vs. shop split significantly affects labour productivity

fields.label: Number of Welds / Joints
fields.hint: Weld count drives labour hours directly. Rule-of-thumb: approx 1 weld per 6m pipe + fittings allowance. Leave as 0 for tier-1 estimate (auto-calculated from length); enter actual count for tier-3 accuracy. For erection, count field joints only.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - precise weld count gives +-10% accuracy on labour

fields.label: Complexity Factor
options: Simple - straight runs, large bore, open area (factor 0.90)
Very Complex - offshore module / live plant (factor 1.65)
fields.hint: Complexity multiplier applied to base labour-hour rate. Congested / elevated routing, restricted access, and tie-in conditions all increase complexity.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 103
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds ASME B16.5 Class 600 (1480 psi / 103 bar) limit at ambient temperature. Upgrade to Class 900 or Class 1500 flanges and verify pipe wall thickness per ASME B31.3.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.5-2017 Table 2-1.1 - Pressure-Temperature Rating Class 600; ASME B31.3-2022 cl.304.1

compliance_rules.condition_expr: schedule == 'Sch 5S' AND ('Carbon steel' IN equipment_subtype OR 'Pipe rack' IN equipment_subtype)
compliance_rules.message: Sch 5S is only permitted for stainless steel (A312) welded pipe per ASME B36.19M. Confirm applicability; minimum wall for CS process service is typically Sch 40 / STD.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B36.19M-2018 - thin-wall SS pipe dimensions; ASME B31.3-2022 cl.304.1.2 minimum wall

compliance_rules.condition_expr: ('TP316' IN material_grade OR 'TP304' IN material_grade) AND 'Carbon steel' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: Stainless steel material grade selected for a Carbon Steel activity sub-type. Verify consistency - consider switching to 'Stainless steel pipe fabrication/erection'.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Process consistency check - ASTM A312 / A376 (SS pipe) vs. A106 / A53 (CS pipe)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature < -29 AND !('A333' IN material_grade OR '304' IN material_grade OR '316' IN material_grade OR 'Duplex' IN material_grade OR 'Inconel' IN material_grade)
compliance_rules.message: Design temperature below -29 °C requires low-temperature impact testing (Charpy V-notch) per ASME B31.3. CS A106/A53 not rated below -29 °C. Specify LTCS (A333 Gr.6) or austenitic SS.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3-2022 Table 323.2.2 - minimum temperature without impact testing; ASTM A333 Gr.6 for LTCS

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Category M' IN service_fluid AND !'Category M' IN nde_level
compliance_rules.message: Category M (lethal) service mandates 100% radiographic examination and leak testing at 1.5x design pressure per ASME B31.3. NDE level must be updated to 'Category M'.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3-2022 cl.300.2 (Category M definition) and Appendix M - mandatory 100% RT and hydro at 1.5x DP

compliance_rules.condition_expr: ('P11' IN material_grade OR 'P22' IN material_grade OR 'P91' IN material_grade) AND pwht_required == 'Not required'
compliance_rules.message: P-No.4 (P11) and P-No.5 (P22/P91) Cr-Mo alloy steels require mandatory PWHT per ASME B31.3 Table 331.1.1 regardless of wall thickness. Update PWHT field.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3-2022 Table 331.1.1 - mandatory PWHT for P-No.4 and P-No.5A/5B materials

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'A333' IN material_grade AND design_temperature > 345
compliance_rules.message: LTCS A333 Gr.6 is intended for low-temperature service and not recommended above +345 °C (design code upper limit). For elevated temperature use A106 Gr.B or alloy steel.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASTM A333/A333M - scope; ASME B31.3 Table A-1 allowable stress for A333 Gr.6 (max 343 °C)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Sour service' IN service_fluid AND 'TP304' IN material_grade
compliance_rules.message: Standard TP304/304L is not recommended for sour (H2S-containing) service per NACE MR0175 due to chloride+H2S stress corrosion cracking risk. Use 316L, duplex 2205 (min PREN 32), or 825/625 nickel alloys.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156-3 Table A.2 - acceptable austenitic SS for sour service; PREN >= 32 for duplex

compliance_rules.condition_expr: pwht_required == 'Required - local induction PWHT (field)' AND (pipe_size_nps == '24\"' OR pipe_size_nps == '30\"' OR pipe_size_nps == '36\"')
compliance_rules.message: Local field induction PWHT at NPS >= 24" is practically challenging - heat-soak time > 4 hrs and uniform temperature control difficult. Prefer furnace PWHT in shop, or plan for extended duration and full thermocouple coverage.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 cl.331.1.3 - PWHT heat-up/cool-down rates; API RP 577 - welding inspection & PWHT practice

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (flange_rating == '#1500' OR flange_rating == '#2500' OR flange_rating == 'PN100') AND 'erection' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: Class 1500 / 2500 (and PN 100) flanged joints require hydraulic/bolt-tensioning equipment and certified torque records per ASME PCC-1. Include flange management procedure and tensioning rig mobilisation in the erection scope.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME PCC-1-2019 - Guidelines for Pressure Boundary Bolted Flange Joint Assembly; ASME B16.5 / B16.47

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Category D' IN nde_level AND design_pressure > 10
compliance_rules.message: Category D service (visual only, per ASME B31.3) is limited to non-flammable, non-toxic fluids at temperature -29 to +186 °C and pressure <= ~10.3 bar. Verify service meets Category D requirements or upgrade NDE level.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3-2022 cl.300.2 (Category D definition) - non-flammable, non-toxic, T <= 186 °C, P <= 150 psig

cost_build_up.engineering_basis: Unit-rate method: pipe fabrication and erection priced per metre of pipe (or per spool for spool subtypes), with adders for welds, fittings, flanges, NDE, PWHT, insulation, painting, and a complexity multiplier. Base rates USA-average (TX/IL/OH), ENR CCI 14,250 (2024 base).
cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: For fabrication/pipe/rack subtypes: Cost = pipe_length_m * unit_rate_USD_per_m(pipe_size_nps, equipment_subtype) * regional_labour_factor * quantity. For spool subtypes: Cost = spool_count * unit_rate_USD_per_spool(pipe_size_nps, equipment_subtype) * regional_labour_factor * quantity.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: Cost = [ (scope_quantity * base_unit_rate(pipe_size_nps, schedule, material_grade)) + (weld_count_estimated * weld_unit_rate(pipe_size_nps, material_grade, nde_level)) + flange_fitting_allowance(flange_rating, pipe_size_nps, scope_quantity) ] * complexity_factor_value(complexity_factor) * regional_cost_index_factor * regional_labour_factor * quantity; where scope_quantity = pipe_length_m (fab/pipe/rack) or spool_count (spools).
cost_build_up.full_formula: Cost = [ (scope_quantity * base_unit_rate(pipe_size_nps, schedule, material_grade)) + (weld_count * weld_unit_rate(pipe_size_nps, material_grade, nde_level)) + pwht_adder_if_applicable(pwht_required, weld_count) + flange_fitting_allowance(flange_rating, pipe_size_nps) + insulation_allowance_if_applicable(insulation_type, scope_quantity) + painting_allowance(painting_spec, pipe_size_nps, scope_quantity) + field_weld_premium_if_applicable(shop_field_split, weld_count) ] * complexity_factor_value(complexity_factor) * regional_cost_index_factor * (current_ENR_CCI / 14250) * regional_labour_factor * sub_region_labour_factor * quantity; scope_quantity = pipe_length_m for fab/pipe/rack subtypes, spool_count for spool subtypes.

adders.erection_installation: For fabrication subtypes only: 0.22 adder for site erection if not priced separately. For erection subtypes: 0 (already included in base rate).
adders.note: erection_installation adder applies only to fabrication subtypes when the estimator has not separately priced an erection line item.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index - tracks construction labour and bulk materials)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate * (current_index / base_value) * regional_factor * sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight. For fab+erect activities, labour_factor is the dominant driver.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Labour services are not dutied. If pipe material is imported, apply HS 7304 (seamless) / 7305-7306 (welded) steel duty.
Services not dutied. Imported steel pipe subject to Section 232 tariffs.
Services not dutied. Imported steel subject to GCC 5% CET.
Services not dutied. Imported steel subject to UK Global Tariff + safeguard.
Services not dutied. Imported steel subject to EU safeguard.
Services subject to VAT not duty. Imported steel subject to MFN + VAT.
Services not dutied. Imported steel MFN 2.8%.
Services not dutied. Imported steel ATIGA 0-12%.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ
project_settings.country: TODO: user selects at project level

usd_cost_baseline.note: Base = USD at USA level (matches ENR_CCI base 14250 @ 2024). Engine: base_rate = usd_cost_baseline.items[equipment_subtype].usd_per_*, then x index_now/14250 x regional_factor x fx. verify=true entries are public-benchmark estimates pending calibration.

Carbon steel pipe fabrication.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Carbon steel pipe erection.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Stainless steel pipe fabrication.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Stainless steel pipe erection.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Pipe support fabrication.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Pipe rack fabrication.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Pipe rack erection.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Pipe spool fabrication.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Pipe spool erection.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

SCHEMA-PIPE-MISC.generation_note: Completed v1.0 - 13 heterogeneous piping-works activities. Composite cost method: each subtype uses its own natural unit (per bend, per cut, per metre, per crossing, per marker, per engineering-hour). conditional_display surfaces only the fields relevant for each subtype. Base rates USD USA-average, ENR CCI 13500 (2024 base). | v1.0-priced: usd_cost_baseline + multiplier tables added (USA-base ENR/CEPCI; public-benchmark, all verify=true).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%. Counts reflect all 30 fields; any individual subtype uses a subset via conditional_display.

SCHEMA-PIPE-MISC.spec_example: Pipe bending | DN 100 mm | CS A106 Gr.B | 3 Bends | Hot-bend induction | Qty 1 Job

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific activity type. Controls which fields are shown below and the unit-of-measure used for costing.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of job line items of this sub-type. Typically 1 per BOQ line. Use scope fields below for the physical quantity (metres, bends, crossings, etc.).

fields.label: Nominal Pipe Diameter
fields.hint: Nominal bore (DN / NPS) of the pipe being worked on. Primary cost driver for all labour-intensive activities - time scales roughly as diameter^0.6 to 0.8. Not required for Pipe stress analysis or Pipeline marker installation.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary physical cost driver for all pipe-work activities

fields.label: Pipe Material
fields.hint: Pipe material controls bend-ability (min bend radius, heat requirements), insulation compatibility, weld procedure, and material cost multiplier. Exotic alloys (Duplex, P91) attract specialist labour surcharges of 2-4x. Not required for Pipe stress analysis, Pipeline marker installation, or Trenching.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - material type governs specialist labour factor and compliance regime

fields.label: Pipe Wall Thickness Schedule
fields.hint: Schedule affects bend tooling, cutting time, and cold-springing force. Heavier wall = longer cutting/beveling time and higher bending force; add 10-30% to labour for Sch 80+.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - wall thickness directly affects bending and cutting effort

fields.label: Number of Bends
fields.hint: Total number of bends to be fabricated in this work order. Each bend is priced individually based on diameter and bending method.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary scope quantity for pipe bending

fields.label: Bending Method
fields.hint: Induction bending is the standard for process piping >= DN 80. Cold bending is only permissible where ASME B31.3 Table 302.3.5 thinning limits are met. Furnace bending required for heavy wall / large bore. Induction is ~2x cold-bend unit cost; furnace ~4x.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - bending method is the dominant labour cost variable after diameter

fields.label: Bend Angle
fields.hint: Bend angle band in degrees: 45 (<=45°), 90 (46-90°), 135 (91-135°), 180 (136-180° return bend). Return bends are quoted separately. Standard 90° elbow-equivalent is baseline; angles <=45° attract a setup surcharge of ~20% per bend.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - angle surcharge refines estimate for non-standard bends

fields.label: Number of Cuts / Bevels
fields.hint: Each cut generates two beveled ends. Priced per cut event. Machine cutting (orbital / plasma) is standard for shop; disc-cutting for field. GTAW weld prep beveling included.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary scope quantity for cutting activity

fields.label: Cutting Environment
options: In-situ on live plant (hot work permit required)
fields.hint: Field cutting adds ~30% over shop rates due to set-up, alignment, and temporary staging. Live-plant hot-work-permit cutting includes PTW administration, gas testing, and fire watch - typically adds 60-100% over field rate.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - environment is the key labour rate modifier for cutting

fields.label: Insulation Length
fields.hint: Straight-pipe run length to be insulated, removed, or repaired. Fittings (elbows, tees, flanges) are priced at 2x the per-metre rate as equivalent length. Measure along pipe centreline.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary scope quantity for insulation activities

fields.label: Insulation Type
options: Mineral wool / rock wool (general duty, -20°C to 650°C)
fields.default: Mineral wool / rock wool (general duty, -20°C to 650°C)
fields.hint: Insulation type determines material cost, thickness, and cladding requirements. Aerogel is 6-10x mineral wool unit cost but 40% thinner. Cryogenic cellular glass requires vapour barrier and is 3-5x mineral wool. Select based on design temperature.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - insulation type is the primary material cost driver

fields.label: Insulation Thickness
fields.hint: Insulation thickness per design / heat-loss calculation. Typical range: 25-150 mm for process. Cladding (Al / SS) is included in the unit rate. Thickness > 100 mm usually requires double-layer installation - add 20% to labour.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - thickness drives both material and labour cost (volume of insulation)

fields.label: Cladding / Facing Material
fields.hint: Cladding protects insulation from mechanical damage and weather. SS cladding is ~2.5x Al cost but required in chloride-rich environments (coastal, offshore). No cladding acceptable for temporary or indoor below-ambient only.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - cladding material refines material cost at detailed stage

fields.label: Routing Length (Isometric)
fields.hint: Total pipe centreline length to be routed and installed per the isometric drawing(s). Includes offsets, risers, and horizontal runs. Priced as installed pipe length (supply-and-erect unit rate). Fittings priced separately or via allowance.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary scope quantity for routing activities

fields.label: Pipe Class / Pressure Rating
fields.hint: Pipe class per ASME B16.5 / ASME B31.3. Affects flange and fitting specifications within the isometric routing. Higher class -> heavier components -> higher handling and torqueing labour. Class 600+ requires hydraulic torqueing equipment surcharge.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - pipe class governs fitting weights and flange labour

fields.label: Number of Isometric Drawings
fields.hint: Number of spoolpiece isometrics to be executed. Each isometric typically covers one pipe spool (field-run). Used for overhead / drawing-management cost allocation at detailed stage.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - drawing count used for document control / inspection overhead allocation

fields.label: Stress Analysis Scope
options: Screening / flexibility check only (ASME B31.3 Appendix P)
Formal Caesar II / CAEPIPE analysis - simple system (< 20 nodes)
Formal Caesar II / CAEPIPE analysis - complex system (20-100 nodes)
Full dynamic analysis (seismic / slug flow / water hammer)
fields.default: Formal Caesar II / CAEPIPE analysis - simple system (< 20 nodes)
fields.hint: Pipe stress analysis is priced as an engineering manhour activity. Screening is 4-8 hrs/system. Simple formal analysis 16-40 hrs/system. Complex 60-200 hrs. Dynamic analysis 150-500 hrs depending on load cases. Rates per senior piping engineer hour.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - scope level determines the engineering manhour bucket

fields.label: Number of Pipe Systems (Stress Analysis)
fields.hint: Each independent pipe system (defined by process line number from P&ID) is analysed separately. Typical plant may have 20-200 systems requiring formal analysis.

fields.label: Cold Spring Distance
fields.hint: Designed cold spring gap per stress analysis output. Typically 50-100% of calculated thermal expansion for the line. Measured in mm of gap cut into the pipe prior to connection. Positive cold spring only (negative cold spring not recommended per ASME B31.3 Cl. 319.2.4).
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - cold spring distance determines jack force and rigging requirement

fields.label: Number of Cold Spring Joints
fields.hint: Each cold spring joint requires a dedicated rigging and welding operation under temporary jack load. Typically 1-3 joints per pipe system. Additional joints are additive cost.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - each joint is a discrete cost event

fields.label: Trench Length
fields.hint: Total length of trench to be excavated. Includes pipe zone, bedding, and reinstatement. Measured along centreline. Width and depth are set by pipe diameter and cover requirements.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - trench length is the primary scope quantity

fields.label: Trench Depth
fields.hint: Depth in metres: 0.8 (shallow, < 1.0 m, no shoring), 1.2 (standard, 1.0-1.5 m, no shoring), 2.0 (deep, 1.5-3.0 m, shoring required), 3.5 (very deep, > 3.0 m, certified shoring + de-watering). Per OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart P (USA) / BS 6031 (UK). De-watering adds 15-30% to deep trench cost.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - depth controls excavation volume and shoring requirement

fields.label: Ground Condition
fields.hint: Ground condition is the dominant trenching cost variable. Hard rock with blasting is 8-15x the cost of normal soil. Soft rock ripping is 2-4x. Urban areas add reinstatement (asphalt/concrete) costs separately.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - single largest variable in trenching unit rate

fields.label: Reinstatement Required
fields.hint: Road / pavement reinstatement can add USD 80-300/m to trench cost depending on road class and local authority specification. Confirm reinstatement specification with highways authority prior to pricing.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - reinstatement can double the total trench cost in urban areas

fields.label: Crossing Type
options: Open cut with temporary traffic management (road only)
Open cut with track possession (railway - rarely approved)
fields.hint: Select method matching the crossing subtype chosen above. Railway crossings almost always require pipe jacking per Network Rail (UK) / UIC / AREMA requirements. Open-cut road crossings require traffic management plan and local authority permit. HDD is preferred for major road crossings to avoid traffic disruption.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - crossing method drives the majority of the cost differential

fields.label: Crossing Length (centreline)
fields.hint: Bore/drill/jack length measured along the pipe centreline. For roads: road width + 2-5 m each side for entry/exit pits. For railways: track width + buffer zone per infrastructure owner's standard (typically 3-5 m from nearest rail). Mobilisation cost is fixed regardless of length.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - crossing length scales the variable cost component

fields.label: Number of Crossings
fields.hint: Number of individual road or railway crossings. Each crossing incurs a mobilisation / pit-setup fixed cost (USD 8,000-25,000 per crossing depending on method). Batch crossings on the same route may share mobilisation.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - each crossing carries a significant fixed mobilisation cost

fields.label: Number of Markers
fields.hint: Pipeline markers (above-ground posts or buried electronic markers) installed at changes of direction, road/rail crossings, and regular intervals per authority specification (typically every 100-200 m along buried pipelines).
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary scope quantity for marker activity

fields.label: Marker Type
options: Electronic marker (EMS ball or disc, buried - no surface post)
fields.hint: GRP posts are the industry standard for buried pipelines per IGE/TD/3 (UK) and ASME B31.8 (USA). Electronic markers required at crossings per many DNO and gas network specifications. RFID tags support inline inspection data correlation.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - marker type drives unit supply cost (3x range between GRP post and RFID combo)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipe bending' AND 'P91' IN pipe_material AND bending_method != 'Hot-bend - furnace / forge (DN > 300 or Sch 80+)'
compliance_rules.message: P91 (Grade 91 alloy steel) pipe must be hot-bent using furnace/forge method. Cold or induction bending is not permitted without specific PWHT qualification per ASME B31.3 and EPRI P91 bending guidelines. Change bending method.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Cl. 332.2; EPRI Report 1013370 'Recommendations for Bending of Grade 91 Steel Piping'; API 579-1/ASME FFS-1

compliance_rules.condition_expr: pipe_class == 'Class 1500 (PN 250)' OR pipe_class == 'Class 2500 (PN 420)'
compliance_rules.message: Class 1500 / 2500 flanged joints require hydraulic torque equipment and calibrated bolt tensioning. Ensure flange management procedure and torque record sheets are included in the scope. Labour rate surcharge of 40% applies.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME PCC-1-2019 Guidelines for Pressure Boundary Bolted Flange Joint Assembly; ASME B16.5 Table 2-1.2

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipe cold springing' AND cold_spring_mm > (3 * nominal_diameter_mm)
compliance_rules.message: Cold spring gap exceeds 3 x pipe DN - this is large and unusual. Verify against pipe stress analysis output. Excessive cold spring can cause misalignment and residual load on connected equipment nozzles per ASME B31.3 Cl. 319.2.4.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3-2022 Cl. 319.2.4 Cold Spring; MSS SP-58 pipe support design; typical design cold spring is 50-100% of calculated thermal expansion, rarely exceeds 2-3 x DN.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipe cutting & beveling' AND cutting_environment == 'In-situ on live plant (hot work permit required)'
compliance_rules.message: Hot work on live plant requires PTW (Permit to Work), gas test clearance, fire watch, and hydrocarbon-free zone confirmation before each cut. Budget 2-4 hrs of permit/safety overhead per cut event in addition to cutting time.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 PSM; IEC 61511; Energy Institute 'Hot Work on Plant' guidelines; site-specific PTW procedures

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipe insulation' AND 'Cellular glass' IN insulation_type AND 'SS' IN pipe_material
compliance_rules.message: Cellular glass insulation on stainless steel pipe requires a vapour barrier and anti-wicking tape at all joints. Direct contact of wet cellular glass with SS can promote stress corrosion cracking (SCC). Specify vapour barrier in insulation spec.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NACE SP0198 (Control of Corrosion Under Thermal Insulation); EN ISO 23999; BS 5970 Cl. 7.4 - CUI / SCC risk management

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Trenching for pipelines' AND trench_depth_m > 3.0
compliance_rules.message: Trench depth exceeds 3.0 m. A certified temporary works designer must prepare a shoring scheme. Confined-space entry procedures and de-watering plan required. Estimate accuracy reduces to +-30% without geotechnical investigation.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart P (Excavations); BS 6031:2009 Code of practice for earthworks; local HSE / statutory requirements

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipeline crossing (railway)' AND 'Open cut' IN crossing_type
compliance_rules.message: Open-cut railway crossings require track possession (line block) approved by the infrastructure manager (e.g. Network Rail, Indian Railways, Amtrak). This is rarely granted and adds significant programme risk. Pipe jacking or HDD is strongly recommended.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Network Rail Standard NR/SP/CIV/006 (UK); UIC Code 706-R (Europe); AREMA Chapter 1 Art. 2.1.4 (USA); Indian Railways Bridge Rules Cl. 19

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipe stress analysis' AND 'dynamic' IN stress_analysis_scope
compliance_rules.message: Dynamic pipe stress analysis (seismic / slug / water hammer) requires process input data (slug frequency, flow regime, seismic zone / PGA). Confirm availability of process licensor data and applicable seismic code (IBC / IS 1893 / EN 1998) before starting.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 App. P (dynamic loads); ASCE 7-22 (seismic); API 579-1 (fitness-for-service); Caesar II Dynamic Analysis User Guide

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipe rerouting' AND pipe_class IS not None AND ('600' IN pipe_class OR '900' IN pipe_class OR '1500' IN pipe_class OR '2500' IN pipe_class)
compliance_rules.message: Rerouting of Class 600+ pipework requires a formal re-stress analysis of the revised routing before reinstatement. Do not close-out the rerouting without updated isometrics and stress sign-off.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Cl. 319; PFI ES-7 (pipe fabrication); plant MOC (Management of Change) procedure

cost_build_up.engineering_basis: Composite method: each sub-type uses its own natural unit (Nr, m, crossing, etc.) and base unit rate. Rates are labour-dominant (materials are minor for most PIPE-MISC activities). Base rates in USD, USA-average (TX/IL/OH), ENR CCI 13500 (2024). Scale by regional labour_factor for all activities.

base_unit_rates_usd._note: All rates USA-average basis, ENR CCI 13500 (2024). Diameter factors apply where shown. These are fully installed / activity-complete rates (labour + tools + supervision + minor consumables). Major material supply (insulation, casing pipe) is additional.

Pipe bending.method_note: Rate per bend by DN and bending method. Cold bend = baseline. Induction ~2x. Furnace ~4x.

Pipe cutting & beveling.method_note: Includes machine setup, cut, bevel, and deburr. Field rate = shop x environment_factor.

Pipe insulation.method_note: Supply + install rate. Mineral wool baseline. Thickness and material multipliers applied. Double-layer (t > 100 mm) adds 20% to labour.
Pipe insulation.thickness_factor: 1.0 at 50 mm; scale proportional to (t/50)^0.7 for material; +20% labour for t > 100 mm (double-layer)

Pipe insulation removal.method_note: Demolition, bagging, and disposal of existing insulation. Rate includes skip / waste disposal. Hazardous material (asbestos) must be segregated and priced separately under specialist removal contract.
Pipe insulation removal.hazardous_surcharge_note: Add USD 150-400/m for confirmed or suspected asbestos-containing materials (ACM). Asbestos removal requires licensed contractor under Control of Asbestos Regulations 2012 (UK) / 29 CFR 1910.1001 (USA).

Pipe insulation repair.method_note: Patch repair of existing insulation (remove, replace, re-clad). Includes access and making good. Rate = 70% of new installation rate for same pipe/insulation type.
Pipe insulation repair.note: Minimum charge applies because of mobilisation and access cost even for small patch repairs.

Pipe routing as per isometric.unit: USD per metre (supply-and-erect, pipe + supports, excluding major valves)
Pipe routing as per isometric.method_note: Composite rate covering spool fabrication, transportation, erection, and bolt-up. CS A106 Gr.B Sch 40 baseline. Material and class multipliers applied.

Pipe rerouting.method_note: Rerouting = demolish existing + re-route new. Rate = new routing rate x 1.5 (to account for disconnect, demolition, and additional tie-in welds). If existing pipe is reused, reduce material by 40%.

Pipe stress analysis.method_note: Engineering-only activity. Rate = engineering manhours x blended piping engineer rate (USD/hr). USA basis: USD 145/hr for senior piping engineer.
Pipe stress analysis.note: Engineering rates do not scale by region in the same way as construction labour. Apply a 0.85x factor for India/ASEAN offshore engineering centres vs USA.

Pipe cold springing.method_note: Per cold spring joint: includes rigging/jacking, cutting the gap, making the final closure weld under load, and PWHT if required. Price by diameter band.
Pipe cold springing.note: PWHT of closure weld is a separate line if required. Cold spring closure welds on P91 always require PWHT per ASME B31.3.

Trenching for pipelines.unit: USD per metre (open trench, excavate + bed + backfill, normal soil, 1.2 m depth)
Trenching for pipelines.method_note: Machine excavation. Rate = baseline x depth_factor x ground_factor x reinstatement_adder.
Trenching for pipelines.trench_width_guidance: Trench width = pipe OD + 300 mm each side (min). For DN > 600 mm, structural assessment of trench wall required.

Pipeline crossing (road).unit: USD per crossing (mobilisation fixed + variable per metre)
Pipeline crossing (road).diameter_factor_note: HDD and jacking rates above are for DN <= 200. Scale by (DN/200)^0.8 for larger diameters.

Pipeline crossing (railway).unit: USD per crossing (mobilisation fixed + variable per metre)
Pipeline crossing (railway).method_note: Railway crossings carry 25-40% premium over road crossings of same method due to strict working-hours restrictions, mandatory railway protection officer, and insurance requirements.
Pipeline crossing (railway).note: Track possession charges vary by network operator. Verify with infrastructure manager before pricing.

Pipeline marker installation.note: Rates include setting out, excavation (for buried types), backfill, and painting / labelling per pipeline operator's specification.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: total_cost = base_unit_rate[equipment_subtype][diameter_band] * scope_quantity * method_multiplier * material_multiplier * environment_factor * regional_labour_factor * (1 + contingency_pct/100) * (1 + inland_freight_pct/100)
cost_build_up.full_formula: total_cost = (base_unit_rate[equipment_subtype][diameter_band] * scope_quantity * method_multiplier * material_multiplier * schedule_surcharge * environment_factor + mobilisation_fixed[where_applicable]) * regional_labour_factor * (ENR_CCI_current / 13500) * (1 + contingency_pct/100) * (1 + inland_freight_pct/100)

adders.freight_insurance_note: PIPE-MISC activities are predominantly labour services or minor material supply. No significant ocean freight. Any specialist equipment (HDD rigs, induction benders) include mobilisation costs in the base unit rates above.
adders.erection_installation: included in base unit rates - all rates are fully installed / activity-complete
adders.contingency_note: Default 10% for Class 4 estimate. Override with regional contingency_pct from regional_factors where higher (e.g. ASEAN 18%, GCC 15%).

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News-Record Construction Cost Index - tracks construction labour and bulk material costs; appropriate for install-scope / activity items)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate * (current_index / base_value) * regional_factor * sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

India.local_ref: CPWD Plinth Area Rates + WPI 'General engineering services' sub-index

USA.local_ref: ENR CCI USA 20-city average; RSMeans construction labour rates

Middle East (GCC).local_ref: Turner GCC Construction Cost Index; expat labour / project contractor rates

UK.local_ref: BCIS General Building Cost Index; Spon's mechanical & piping rates

Europe.local_ref: Eurostat construction cost index; DESTATIS (Germany), INSEE (France) labour rates

China.local_ref: China construction cost index; provincial bureau of statistics labour rates

Japan.local_ref: MLIT construction deflator; JGCA industrial construction rates

ASEAN.local_ref: Regional construction cost index; Langdon & Seah Asia report

regional_cost_index.base_value_note: Normalized 13500->14250: base rates set at 2024 USA-benchmark level in v1.0-priced; denominator matched to 2024 ENR CCI epoch.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight. For these activities, labour_factor is the dominant driver.

import_duty.category: Piping activity (services - no duty on labour)
import_duty.components: Services; duty applies only to imported specialist equipment mobilisation
import_duty.note: Labour services are not subject to import duty. If specialist imported equipment (HDD rig, induction bender) is mobilised from abroad, apply equipment-specific duty separately.
Services not subject to import duty.

import_duty.components: N/A - services; GCC CET 5% applies to imported bulk materials only
import_duty.note: Services not subject to import duty.
Services not subject to import duty.
Services not subject to import duty.

import_duty.components: N/A - services; VAT on services at 6%
import_duty.note: Services subject to VAT not import duty.
Services not subject to import duty.
Services not subject to import duty.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ
project_settings.country: TODO: user selects at project level

usd_cost_baseline.note: USA-base USD (matches index base year 2024). Engine applies index_now/base x regional x sub_region factor x fx. ALL rates are public-benchmark ESTIMATES (verify=true) pending vendor/DSR calibration. base_unit_rate by equipment_subtype x primary scope unit. Where dia-sensitive, sub-keyed by diameter band.

Pipe bending.basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/m 28 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 37.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Pipe cutting & beveling.basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/m 28 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 37.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Pipe insulation.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/m2 80 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 101.79. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Pipe routing as per isometric.basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/m 28 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 37.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Pipe stress analysis.basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/m 28 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 37.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Pipe cold springing.basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/m 28 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 37.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Trenching for pipelines.basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/m 28 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 37.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Pipeline crossing (road).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/m 28 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 37.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Pipeline crossing (railway).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/m 28 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 37.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Pipeline marker installation.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 4500 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 5725.45. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Pipe insulation removal.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/m2 80 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 101.79. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Pipe insulation repair.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/m2 80 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 101.79. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Pipe rerouting.basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/m 28 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 37.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

SCHEMA-PIPE-PLASTIC.generation_note: Completed v1.0 - HDPE & PVC pipe installation (activity). Unit-rate method: material (USD/m FOB) + installation labour (USD/m installed), ENR CCI-indexed (construction activity). Covers ISO 4427 (HDPE PE100) and ISO 4422 (uPVC) with open-cut, HDD, pipe-jacking, and slip-lining methods. | v1.0-priced: usd_cost_baseline + multiplier tables added (USA-base ENR/CEPCI; public-benchmark, all verify=true).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-PIPE-PLASTIC.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | DN {nominal_diameter_mm} mm | PN {pressure_rating_bar} | SDR {sdr_class} | L={pipe_length_m} m | Qty {quantity} Job
SCHEMA-PIPE-PLASTIC.spec_example: HDPE pipe installation | DN 110 mm | PN 10 | SDR 17 | L=500 m | Qty 1 Job

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

fields.label: Nominal Diameter
fields.hint: Outside diameter per ISO 4427 (HDPE) / ISO 4422 (PVC). Common sizes 25-630 mm. Controls wall thickness and unit rate.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary cost driver after subtype

fields.label: Pipe Length
fields.hint: Total installed length of pipe including fittings allowance (add 5-10% for fittings equivalent). Measured along centreline.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - directly scales material and labour cost

fields.label: Pressure Rating (PN)
fields.hint: Maximum operating pressure in bar at 20°C water. PN to SDR mapping (HDPE PE100 / ISO 4427): PN 4 = SDR 41, PN 6 = SDR 26, PN 8 = SDR 21, PN 10 = SDR 17, PN 12.5 = SDR 13.6, PN 16 = SDR 11, PN 20 = SDR 9. Higher PN = thicker wall = higher material cost. PVC limited to PN 16 per ISO 4422.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - affects wall thickness and material cost +-15%

fields.label: SDR Class
fields.hint: Standard Dimension Ratio = OD / wall thickness. Auto-derived from PN and subtype per ISO 4427 / ISO 4422. Manual override allowed for PE80 or non-standard grades.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - computed, shown for reference and spec string

fields.label: Pipe Material Grade
fields.hint: Material grade. For HDPE subtype: PE100 (MRS 10 MPa, current standard), PE80 (legacy), PE100-RC (enhanced crack resistance for no-dig/HDD). For PVC subtype: uPVC (standard), mPVC (modified, higher impact), CPVC (hot service to 93°C). Select the grade matching the sub-type chosen above. Affects unit rate +-10-20%.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - material grade affects cost and applicable pressure ratings

fields.label: Installation Method
fields.hint: Open cut is lowest cost. HDD and pipe jacking add 3-8x the open-cut labour rate but eliminate reinstatement and traffic disruption costs. Rock trench adds 40-80% to excavation cost.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - installation method is the single largest labour cost variable

fields.label: Jointing Method
fields.hint: Select the jointing method matching the sub-type chosen above. HDPE: butt fusion is standard for DN >= 90; electrofusion for smaller sizes or in-situ repairs. PVC: solvent cement is standard for pressure pipe to DN 315; RRJ for larger diameter and gravity mains. Mechanical couplings and flanges are used at transitions or where fusion is impractical.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - affects labour rate and weld testing scope

fields.label: Trench Depth
fields.hint: Cover depth in metres: 0.8 m (shallow, < 1.0 m), 1.2 m (standard, 1.0-1.5 m), 2.0 m (deep, 1.5-3.0 m), 3.5 m (very deep, > 3.0 m, shoring required). Per AWWA C600 / EN 805 minimum cover. Deep trenches require shoring and de-watering - adds 25-60% to excavation cost. Not applicable for above-ground or HDD installation.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - significant impact on excavation cost beyond 1.5 m

fields.label: Fluid Service / Application
fields.hint: Fluid service drives material selection, standards compliance, and lining requirements. Potable water requires NSF-61 / WRAS / BIS certification. Chemical duty may require special grade HDPE or specialist PVC. Slurry service requires abrasion-resistant liners or increased wall thickness.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - determines compliance regime and any material adder

fields.label: Operating Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum sustained fluid temperature. HDPE PE100 rated at PN full pressure to 20°C; de-rate per ISO TR 9080 above 20°C. uPVC limited to 60°C max; CPVC to 93°C. Affects pressure rating selection.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - required when temperature exceeds 30°C to check de-rating

fields.label: Fittings Allowance
fields.hint: Fittings (bends, tees, reducers, flanges) as % of pipe material cost. 5% for long straight runs, 10% for typical distribution, 20% for congested/plant area, 30% for headers/manifolds. Applied as an adder to the pipe material rate before applying the labour factor.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - fittings can add 10-30% to pipe material cost

fields.label: Testing Requirement
fields.hint: Testing adds 2-5% to installation cost for mobilisation, water fill, hold period and disposal. Pneumatic testing on large-diameter plastic pipe carries safety risk; limit to low-pressure applications per ISO 14917.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - minor cost impact but specification completeness

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'PVC pipe installation' AND pressure_rating_bar > 16
compliance_rules.message: PVC (uPVC/mPVC) pipe is not rated above PN 16 per ISO 4422. Reduce pressure rating to <= PN 16 or change subtype to HDPE.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 4422-1:2014 Cl. 4.2; EN 1452-1 Table 1 - uPVC max PN 16 at 20°C water

compliance_rules.condition_expr: nominal_diameter_mm > 400 AND equipment_subtype == 'PVC pipe installation'
compliance_rules.message: PVC pressure pipe above DN 400 has limited manufacturer availability and long lead times. Consider HDPE or GRP for diameters > DN 400.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Market availability observation: major manufacturers (JM Eagle, Wavin, Finolex, Supreme) typically stock PVC pressure pipe to DN 400; larger sizes are made-to-order with 8-16 week lead times. ISO 4422-2 covers up to DN 630 but production is regional.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: trench_depth_m > 3.0
compliance_rules.message: Trench depth > 3.0 m requires certified shoring design, confined-space entry procedures, and de-watering assessment. Estimate accuracy reduces to +-30%. Verify with geotechnical report.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart P (Excavations); BS 6031:2009 (Code of practice for earthworks); local HSE regulations for excavation works > 1.2 m typically require protective systems.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: fluid_service == 'Potable water (treated)'
compliance_rules.message: Potable water contact materials must carry NSF-61, WRAS (UK/EU drinking water), or BIS IS:4984/IS:4985 (India) certification. Confirm grade certification before procurement and verify site-applicable regulator.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NSF/ANSI 61 (Drinking Water System Components - Health Effects); WRAS Water Regulations Advisory Scheme; IS 4984:2016 (HDPE for potable water) / IS 4985:2000 (uPVC for potable water).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'PVC pipe installation' AND material_grade != 'CPVC' AND operating_temp_c > 60
compliance_rules.message: uPVC/mPVC is limited to 60°C maximum service temperature. Use CPVC (to 93°C) or HDPE at elevated temperatures.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 4422-1 Cl. 6.2; ASTM D1784; uPVC Heat Deflection Temperature typically 72-78°C - apply 20% safety margin for sustained service.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'HDPE pipe installation' AND operating_temp_c > 30 AND pressure_rating_bar >= 10
compliance_rules.message: HDPE PE100 pressure rating de-rates above 20°C. At 40°C apply factor 0.87x; at 60°C apply 0.63x per ISO TR 9080. Verify selected PN is adequate after de-rating.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO TR 9080:2012 (Plastics piping and ducting systems - long-term hydrostatic strength); ISO 4427-1 Annex A de-rating factors for PE100.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: installation_method == 'Horizontal Directional Drilling (HDD)' AND nominal_diameter_mm >= 250
compliance_rules.message: HDD of HDPE at DN >= 250 requires verification of minimum bend radius (typically 20-25 x OD for PE100-RC) and pullback tensile load against SDR-dependent allowable stress. Specify PE100-RC grade for no-dig applications.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASTM F1962 (Use of Maxi-Horizontal Directional Drilling for Placement of PE Pipe); PPI TR-46 (Guidelines for Use of Mini-HDD); ISO 21225-1 for PE100-RC performance.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'HDPE pipe installation' AND nominal_diameter_mm < 90 AND jointing_method == 'Butt fusion (electrofusion welding)'
compliance_rules.message: Butt fusion below DN 90 is impractical due to narrow fusion-bead geometry and cooling rates. Use electrofusion coupling for DN < 90.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 21307 (Butt fusion jointing procedures for PE pipes); WIS 4-32-08 - butt fusion machines typically size-limited DN 90 to DN 1600.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: installation_method == 'Above ground / elevated on supports' AND equipment_subtype == 'HDPE pipe installation' AND material_grade == 'PE80'
compliance_rules.message: Above-ground HDPE requires UV-stabilised grade (carbon black content >= 2.0 - 2.5 wt% per ISO 4427-1). Confirm PE80 grade specification includes UV stabilisation or upgrade to PE100.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 4427-1:2019 Cl. 4.3 (material requirements for PE pipes - weathering and UV); EN 12201-1 Cl. 4.2.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: fluid_service == 'Slurry / abrasive'
compliance_rules.message: Slurry service accelerates wear; specify minimum SDR 11 (PN 16) wall thickness or higher, and consider PE100-RC grade for enhanced abrasion resistance. Design velocity < 3 m/s recommended to limit wear rate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: DIN 8075 Annex B (abrasion resistance of PE pipes); Darmstadt slurry wear test data; industry practice for mineral slurry pipelines (iron ore, fly ash, tailings).

cost_build_up.engineering_basis: Unit rate method: pipe material cost (USD/m) x length (m) x PN factor x regional factor, plus fittings adder, plus installation labour (USD/m) x installation method factor. Base rates are USD FOB manufacturer at ENR CCI 13,500 (2024) - activity indexing (ENR CCI captures construction labour + bulk materials, appropriate for install-scope items).

base_unit_rates_usd_per_m.note: Supply-only material cost, USD/m, HDPE PE100 PN10 SDR17 and uPVC PN10, FOB mill gate, 2024 basis. Scale for other PN: multiply by (PN_selected/10)^0.65 (wall thickness exponent for plastic pipe).

installation_labour_rates_usd_per_m.note: Labour supply-and-install cost, USD/m, USA average basis (TX/IL/OH). Scale by regional labour_factor. Includes pipe laying, jointing, bedding, backfill, and compaction for open-cut methods.

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: material_cost = base_unit_rate[equipment_subtype][nominal_diameter_mm] * pipe_length_m * (pressure_rating_bar / 10)^0.65 * regional_factor; labour_cost = open_cut_standard_labour[nominal_diameter_mm] * pipe_length_m * regional_labour_factor; total_before_adders = (material_cost * (1 + fittings_allowance_pct/100)) + labour_cost
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: material_cost = base_unit_rate[equipment_subtype][nominal_diameter_mm] * pipe_length_m * (pressure_rating_bar / 10)^0.65 * regional_factor; labour_cost = open_cut_standard_labour[nominal_diameter_mm] * installation_method_factor * pipe_length_m * regional_labour_factor; total_before_adders = (material_cost * (1 + fittings_allowance_pct/100)) + labour_cost + testing_cost
cost_build_up.full_formula: material_cost = base_unit_rate[equipment_subtype][nominal_diameter_mm] * pipe_length_m * (pressure_rating_bar / 10)^0.65 * temperature_derate_factor * regional_factor * (ENR_CCI_current / 13500); labour_cost = effective_labour_rate[installation_method][nominal_diameter_mm] * pipe_length_m * regional_labour_factor * (ENR_CCI_current / 13500); fittings_cost = material_cost * (fittings_allowance_pct / 100); testing_cost = (material_cost + labour_cost) * 0.03; subtotal = material_cost + fittings_cost + labour_cost + testing_cost; contingency = subtotal * contingency_pct; total = (subtotal + contingency) * (1 + inland_freight_pct/100)

adders.erection_installation: included in labour_cost line (unit rate method - labour is priced per metre installed)
adders.engineering_note: freight_insurance 3% covers ocean freight + cargo insurance for imported pipe to port. Inland freight handled separately via inland_freight_pct from regional_factors. erection/installation costs are embedded in the per-metre labour rate - do not double-count.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News-Record Construction Cost Index - tracks construction labour and bulk material costs; appropriate for install-scope activity items)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate x (current_index / base_value) x regional_factor x sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

India.local_ref: CPWD Plinth Area Rates + WPI India 'Plastic products' sub-index (HDPE/PVC pipe)

USA.local_ref: ENR CCI USA average + PPI 'Plastics pipe and fittings' (NAICS 326122)

Middle East (GCC).local_ref: Turner GCC Construction Cost Index; regional polymer pricing (Borouge/SABIC HDPE)

UK.local_ref: BCIS General Building Cost Index; ONS PPI 'Manufacture of plastics products'

Europe.local_ref: Eurostat construction cost index; Borealis/INEOS PE pricing

China.local_ref: China construction cost index; Sinopec/CNPC HDPE resin pricing

Japan.local_ref: MLIT construction deflator; Mitsui Chemicals / Prime Polymer pricing

ASEAN.local_ref: Regional construction cost index; PTT/Chandra Asri HDPE pricing

regional_cost_index.base_value_note: Normalized 13500->14250: base rates set at 2024 USA-benchmark level in v1.0-priced; denominator matched to 2024 ENR CCI epoch.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight. For this activity, labour_factor is the dominant driver (installation labour typically 50-70% of total installed cost).

import_duty.note: Indicative only. HS 3917 covers plastic tubes/pipes. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
