usd_cost_baseline.note: USA-base USD (matches index base year 2024). Engine applies index_now/base x regional x sub_region factor x fx. ALL rates are public-benchmark ESTIMATES (verify=true) pending vendor/DSR calibration. base_unit_rate USD/m by nominal_diameter_mm band.

HDPE pipe installation.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 4500 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 5725.45. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

PVC pipe installation.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 4500 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 5725.45. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

open_cut_standard_labour._basis: Open-cut lay labour by dia. verify=true.

SCHEMA-PIPE-SS-SUPPLY.generation_note: Completed v1.0 - Stainless steel pipe supply. Unit-rate method keyed to NPS + Schedule + Grade + Length, CEPCI-indexed. Covers ASME B36.19M sizes, ASTM A312 grades (304/304L/316/316L/321/347), duplex (A790), and common end/surface finishes. | v1.0-priced: usd_cost_baseline + multiplier tables added (USA-base ENR/CEPCI; public-benchmark, all verify=true).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-PIPE-SS-SUPPLY.spec_formula: {nps} {schedule} {grade} {mfg_type} pipe, {end_finish} ends, {length}m, ASTM A312/A358 as applicable, ASME B36.19M dimensions
SCHEMA-PIPE-SS-SUPPLY.spec_example: 6" NPS Sch 40S ASTM A312 TP316L seamless pipe, PE ends, 6 m random length, ASME B36.19M, mill test certificate EN 10204 3.1, PMI on 100%

fields.label: Nominal Pipe Size (NPS)
fields.hint: Nominal Pipe Size per ASME B36.19M. NPS is a dimensionless designator; actual OD is fixed per standard and independent of schedule.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary cost driver (sets pipe OD and weight per metre)
fields.compliance_rule: ASME B36.19M-2020 / ASME B36.10M (for hybrid grades)

fields.label: NPS (numeric, derived)
fields.hint: Numeric NPS derived from the NPS selector; used by size-threshold compliance rules. Fractions resolved exactly (1-1/2" = 1.5).

fields.label: Schedule / Wall Thickness Class
fields.hint: Wall thickness class per ASME B36.19M (5S/10S/40S/80S are the SS-specific thin-wall series). Schedules 40/80/160/XXS follow B36.10M and are used where ASME B31.3 pressure design demands heavier wall.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary cost driver (sets wall thickness and thus kg/m)
fields.compliance_rule: ASME B36.19M / ASME B36.10M; wall thickness to satisfy ASME B31.3 para 304.1.2

fields.label: Material Grade (MOC)
fields.hint: Austenitic grades TP304/304L/316/316L cover ~80% of process service. 'L' = low carbon (≤0.035%) for weldability / IGC resistance. 'H' = controlled higher carbon for elevated temperature creep service. Duplex for chloride SCC / sour service. 904L/825/625 for high-corrosion duties.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary cost driver (grade premium over TP304 baseline: 316L ~1.15x, 321 ~1.25x, 904L ~3.5x, duplex ~1.8x, super duplex ~2.8x, 625 ~6x)
fields.compliance_rule: ASTM A312/A358 (austenitic) / A790 (duplex) / B-series (nickel alloys); NACE MR0175 if sour service

fields.label: Total Length Required
fields.hint: Total linear meters of this NPS/Schedule/Grade combination. Random mill lengths are typically 5.8-6.4 m (single) or 10.6-12.8 m (double); include cut-waste allowance (typ. 5-8%) in this figure.

fields.label: Quantity (number of line items)
fields.hint: Number of identical pipe-supply line items in the BOQ (normally 1 - use length field above for metres).

fields.label: Manufacturing Type
fields.hint: Seamless is default up to NPS 24" and preferred for severe service, high-pressure, and cyclic duty. EFW (A358) is common for NPS > 8" in chemical/cryogenic service. ERW is economical for low-pressure utility. Welded grades take a 0.85 joint-efficiency penalty per ASME B31.3 Table A-1B unless 100% RT upgrades it.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - seamless ~1.25-1.4x welded cost; welded above NPS 24" often only viable option
fields.compliance_rule: ASTM A312 (SMLS & welded austenitic) / A358 (EFW austenitic, typ. ≥8" high-temp/corrosive) / A790 (duplex)

fields.label: End Finish
options: Threaded End (TE) - NPT per ASME B1.20.1
fields.hint: BE is standard for butt-welded construction and is the default for process piping ≥ NPS 2". TE only for small-bore utility / instrument lines (NPS ≤ 2"). Grooved for low-pressure utility in short durations.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - BE adds ~2-4% over PE; TE adds ~6-10% on small bore
fields.compliance_rule: ASME B16.25 (bevel geometry) / ASME B1.20.1 (NPT threads)

fields.label: Surface Finish / Service Class
fields.hint: Mill pickled-and-passivated is standard for chemical/petrochemical. Food-grade requires internal polishing. Pharma BPE demands electropolishing, low-ferrite, high-purity fabrication, certified Ra, and delta-ferrite reporting per ASME BPE SF-2.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - food-grade ~1.3x mill; pharma BPE EP ~2.5-3.5x mill; high-purity semi ~4-5x
fields.compliance_rule: ASME BPE SF-2 (biopharm surface finish) / ASTM A380 (cleaning & descaling) / ASTM A967 (passivation)

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum design pressure the pipe will see in service. Used to validate wall thickness selection against ASME B31.3 para 304.1.2 minimum thickness equation.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - informs schedule selection, not a direct cost multiplier
fields.compliance_rule: ASME B31.3 para 304.1.2 (pressure design of straight pipe)

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum design temperature. Drives allowable stress (ASME B31.3 Table A-1), grade selection (H-grades for creep service above ~525 °C), and impact-test requirements below -29 °C.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - informs grade/heat-treat selection rather than base cost
fields.compliance_rule: ASME B31.3 Table A-1 (allowable stress vs temperature); ASME B31.3 para 323.2.2 (low-temp impact testing)

fields.label: Heat Treatment
fields.hint: Solution anneal + rapid quench is mandatory for austenitic and duplex SS to restore corrosion resistance and break up sigma/chi phases. Stabilising anneal (~900 °C) for 321/347 improves stabilisation of Ti/Nb carbides for high-temp service.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - standard is included in base rate; special HT adds 3-8%
fields.compliance_rule: ASTM A312 section 6 (heat treatment for austenitic) / A790 section 6 (duplex: 1040 °C min + water quench)

fields.label: NDE / PMI Requirements
options: Mill standard (hydro + eddy current per A312)
+ Full traceability + hardness survey (sour service NACE)
fields.hint: PMI via handheld XRF / OES is standard owner's requirement to prevent material mix-up. RT/UT adders apply to welded grades. Sour service per NACE MR0175 requires hardness ≤ 22 HRC verified on every heat.
fields.compliance_rule: API RP 578 (PMI program) / ASTM E1086 (OES) / NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156 for sour service

fields.label: Certification Level
fields.hint: 3.1 MTC is the standard owner's requirement for process piping. 3.2 (third-party inspected and witnessed, e.g. Lloyd's / BV / DNV / TÜV) is typical for hydrocarbon, offshore, and nuclear scope.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - 3.1 is typically included in base rate; 3.2 TPIA adds 2-5%

fields.label: Supply Origin
fields.hint: Local procurement avoids import duty and ocean freight but may limit grade/size availability. Indian mills (Jindal, Ratnamani, ISMT) cover most A312 austenitic and duplex. Japanese/European premium for high-integrity / nuclear / offshore.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - triggers import duty adder per regional matrix

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 40 AND (schedule == 'Sch 5S' OR schedule == 'Sch 10S')
compliance_rules.message: Schedule 5S/10S wall is insufficient for design pressure > 40 barg. Upgrade to Sch 40S minimum and verify thickness against ASME B31.3 para 304.1.2.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3-2022 para 304.1.2 (pressure design of straight pipe); thin-wall SS series 5S/10S is intended for low-pressure utility and instrumentation service.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temp > 425 AND (grade == 'TP304 (A312)' OR grade == 'TP316 (A312)')
compliance_rules.message: At design temperature > 425 °C, unstabilised grades (TP304/316) risk carbide precipitation. Specify H-grade (TP304H/316H) for creep, or 321/347 for stabilised service.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Table A-1 (allowable stress vs temperature); sensitisation window ~425–900 °C per ASTM A262 / NACE SP0170.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temp < -29 AND !'L' IN grade AND !'Duplex' IN grade AND !'904L' IN grade AND !'Alloy' IN grade
compliance_rules.message: Design temperature below -29 °C requires impact-tested low-temperature grade. Use 304L/316L (impact tested) or verify Charpy data per ASME B31.3 para 323.2.2.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 para 323.2.2 (impact testing requirements) and Table A-1; austenitic L-grades are generally acceptable to -254 °C without supplementary Charpy for standard thickness.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Duplex' IN grade AND heat_treatment != 'Solution annealed + water quenched (duplex required)'
compliance_rules.message: Duplex and super-duplex grades mandate solution anneal ≥ 1040 °C followed by water quench to avoid sigma/chi phase embrittlement. Update heat treatment selection.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASTM A790 / A928 heat treatment clauses; sigma-phase precipitation window 600-950 °C.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Welded' IN mfg_type AND design_pressure > 20 AND 'RT' not IN nde_pmi
compliance_rules.message: Welded pipe at > 20 barg without 100% RT takes a joint-efficiency penalty (E = 0.85 per ASME B31.3 Table A-1B). Specify RT 100% to gain E = 1.0 or upgrade to seamless.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Table A-1B (quality factor Ej for welded pipe); RT 100% per ASTM A358 S1 raises Ej from 0.85 to 1.00.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: nps_dn >= 8 AND mfg_type == 'Seamless (SMLS)' AND 'Duplex' IN grade
compliance_rules.message: Seamless duplex above NPS 8" has limited mill availability and lead times > 6 months. Verify with supplier; consider A928 longitudinal welded duplex as alternative.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASTM A790 (seamless) vs A928 (welded) availability; industry lead-time norm for seamless duplex > NPS 8".

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'BPE' IN surface_finish AND grade != 'TP316L (A312)'
compliance_rules.message: ASME BPE recommends TP316L as the default bioprocess grade (low carbon, low ferrite). Confirm grade selection with process owner if deviating.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME BPE-2022 MM-2.2 (material requirements); ferrite content ≤ 0.5% FN typically specified for pharmaceutical product contact surfaces.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Threaded' IN end_finish AND nps_dn > 2
compliance_rules.message: Threaded ends not recommended above NPS 2" per ASME B31.3 para 314 (limit on threaded joints). Switch to bevelled ends for butt welding.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 para 314 (threaded joints - size limits and service restrictions).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: '316' IN grade AND design_temp > 60 AND design_temp < 100 AND ('Mill finish' IN surface_finish OR 'Industrial' IN surface_finish)
compliance_rules.message: If service contains chlorides > 50 ppm at 60-100 °C, TP316L is at risk of chloride stress-corrosion cracking. Evaluate duplex (S32205) or higher-alloy upgrade.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NACE SP0170 / API TR 942 guidance on chloride SCC of austenitic SS; 316L threshold ~60 °C for dilute chlorides.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'NACE' IN nde_pmi AND 'L' not IN grade AND 'Duplex' not IN grade
compliance_rules.message: For sour service per NACE MR0175, restrict to low-carbon or duplex grades with hardness ≤ 22 HRC. H-grades and standard-carbon austenitics are not compliant.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156-3 Table A.2 (austenitic SS materials for sour service).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: length > 0 AND length < 6
compliance_rules.message: Length below 6 m per line item is smaller than typical random mill length. Expect cut-length / short-length premium of 15-30% over standard pricing.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Mill practice: standard random length 5.8-6.4 m (single), 10.6-12.8 m (double); cut-length attracts processing and scrap premium.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: grade == 'TP304 (A312)' AND design_pressure > 50
compliance_rules.message: Standard TP304 is uneconomic at > 50 barg due to lower allowable stress vs 316. Evaluate TP316/316L or higher-strength duplex for wall-thickness optimisation.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Table A-1 allowable stress comparison TP304 vs TP316 vs duplex; duplex offers ~2x allowable stress over TP304 at room temp.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: certification == 'EN 10204 Type 3.2 (TPIA-witnessed, third-party)'
compliance_rules.message: 3.2 third-party inspection requires TPIA nomination in the PO, ITP approval, and witness points. Typical schedule impact: +4-8 weeks lead time.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: EN 10204:2004 cl. 4.3 (Type 3.2 requires inspection representative independent of manufacturer).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: supply_origin == 'Local / domestic mill' AND 'Super Duplex' IN grade
compliance_rules.message: Super-duplex (S32750/S32760) is not widely produced by domestic mills in most regions. Verify source availability or plan for imported material with lead-time and duty.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Industry observation: super-duplex mill base is concentrated (Sandvik, Outokumpu, Sumitomo, Tubacex, Salzgitter); regional availability varies.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: nps_dn >= 24 AND mfg_type == 'Seamless (SMLS)'
compliance_rules.message: Seamless SS pipe at NPS ≥ 24" has a very limited mill base globally. Budget for 20-35% premium over welded (A358 EFW) and verify lead time.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASTM A312 vs A358 industry availability; large-bore seamless production limited to a handful of mills worldwide.

unit_rate_base.basis: NPS 4", Sch 40S, TP316L seamless, PE, mill finish, EN 10204 3.1, USA mill, 2024

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_cost_USD = unit_rate_base × nps_factor[nps] × schedule_factor[schedule] × grade_factor[grade] × length × quantity × (CEPCI_current / 816) × regional_factor
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_cost_USD = unit_rate_base × nps_factor[nps] × schedule_factor[schedule] × grade_factor[grade] × mfg_factor[mfg_type] × end_factor[end_finish] × surface_factor[surface_finish] × length × quantity × (CEPCI_current / 816) × regional_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_cost_USD = unit_rate_base × nps_factor[nps] × schedule_factor[schedule] × grade_factor[grade] × mfg_factor[mfg_type] × end_factor[end_finish] × surface_factor[surface_finish] × pmi_factor[nde_pmi] × cert_factor[certification] × length × quantity × (CEPCI_current / 816) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor; then add freight_insurance, erection_installation, import_duty (if Imported), and contingency

adders.note: Erection/installation is 0 for this template — scope is material supply only. Fabrication, welding, erection, hydrotest, and insulation are covered by separate piping-activity templates. Apply freight+insurance on FOB price; apply import duty from regional_factors.import_duty only if Supply Origin ≠ Local.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ
project_settings.country: TODO: user selects at project level

usd_cost_baseline.note: USA-base USD (matches index base year 2024). Engine applies index_now/base x regional x sub_region factor x fx. ALL rates are public-benchmark ESTIMATES (verify=true) pending vendor/DSR calibration. Single unit_rate_base USD/m, scaled by nps/sched/grade factors.

Stainless steel pipe supply.basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/m 36 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 48.72. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

SCHEMA-PIPE-SUPPORTS.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs filled. This schema covers 5 heterogeneous items with 3 different measurement bases (No. of supports, kg of steel, No. of joints/sleeves/calculations). Quantity unit corrected from 'Job' to 'No.' for most items; steel structure uses kg. Added pipe_size_nb (primary cost driver for support install, expansion joints, sleeves, and load calcs), support_type (major for support install), structure_weight_kg (primary for pipe support structure), and expansion_joint_type (major for expansion joints). Composite method with conditional_display routing each sizing field to appropriate sub-types. freight_insurance and erection_installation both 0.0. import_duty corrected for site activity. usd_cost_baseline as flat unit-rate lookup nested inside cost_build_up. Based on ASME B31.3, MSS SP-58, MSS SP-69, EJMA standards, RSMeans Mechanical 2024, EPC contractor piping norms (2024 USD).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-PIPE-SUPPORTS.spec_example: Pipe support installation | DN150 | Variable spring hanger | — | 12 No.

fields.label: Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the pipe support or associated activity type. Pipe support installation: site installation of standard catalogue supports (clevis hangers, pipe shoes, resting supports, spring hangers, clamps, trunnions) on already-erected pipe. Measured per individual support location (No.). Pipe support structure: fabrication and erection of custom structural steel pipe rack members, stanchions, and trunnion support frames. Measured in kg of structural steel. Pipe support load calculation: engineering activity — stress analysis and calculation of support loads at each support location, used to specify spring hanger settings and anchor loads. Measured per calculation sheet (No.). Pipe expansion joint installation: installation of metallic bellows, rubber expansion joints, or slip-type expansion joints to accommodate thermal expansion. Measured per joint (No.) by pipe size and type. Pipe sleeve installation: installation of pipe sleeves (casing pipes) where the process pipe passes through a wall, floor slab, or beam. Measured per sleeve (No.) by pipe size and wall/slab thickness.

fields.label: Pipe Nominal Bore (NB / DN)
fields.hint: Nominal bore of the pipe being supported, measured, or fitted with an expansion joint or sleeve. For pipe support installation: bore determines support hardware size (hanger rod, clamp, shoe) and hence material and labour cost. For expansion joint installation: bore directly determines the joint diameter and cost. For pipe sleeve installation: bore determines sleeve diameter and annular clearance. For load calculations: bore is used as a proxy for pipe weight and support load magnitude. Not applicable to Pipe support structure (which is measured by kg of steel — enter structural steel weight in the Structure Steel Weight field instead).
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - always visible for applicable sub-types

fields.label: Support Type
options: Pipe shoe with low-friction PTFE slide plate
fields.hint: Type of pipe support hardware being installed. This is the major cost driver after pipe size. Simple resting supports and clamps are the cheapest (~USD 80-250/support at DN150). Spring hangers (constant-load or variable) are significantly more expensive due to the precision spring mechanism: a variable spring hanger at DN150 costs ~USD 350-800; a constant-load hanger at DN150 costs ~USD 800-2500. Anchor and fixed-point supports require heavy welding and bolting to structural steel — labour cost is 2-3x a simple resting support. Trunnion supports for large vertical pipes (DN300 and above) require custom fabrication and lifting. PTFE slide plates add material cost but are required under pipe shoes on equipment nozzles and expansion loops to allow thermal movement. Alloy/high-temperature hangers for Cr-Mo and P91 systems require certified alloy rod and fittings.

fields.label: Structural Steel Weight
fields.hint: Total weight (kg) of structural steel for the pipe support structure being fabricated and erected. This is the primary sizing basis for pipe rack and pipe support structure items. Includes all structural sections (columns, beams, bracing, baseplates) and gussets but excludes pipe clamps and hardware (priced under Pipe support installation). Typical structural steel density for structural sections: 7850 kg/m3. Rule of thumb for plant pipe racks: 15-25 kg of structural steel per metre of pipe rack length for a 2-tier DN150-DN400 rack. For a single-pipe trunnion stanchion: 50-200 kg depending on pipe size and height. Rate is per kg of erected structural steel, supplied, fabricated, and installed.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - visible for Pipe support structure sub-type

fields.label: Expansion Joint Type
options: Untied metallic bellows (angular or lateral movement)
Rubber expansion joint (EPDM / NBR - utility service)
fields.hint: Type of expansion joint. Metallic bellows joints (single-ply or multi-ply): used for high-temperature, high-pressure service and aggressive chemicals. Single-ply SS316L is standard for process piping. Multi-ply (2-3 plies) provides longer cycle fatigue life for thermal cycling service such as steam systems. Tied joints restrain thrust load on the bellows (pressure thrust is transferred to adjacent anchors) — used in straight runs. Untied joints allow angular and lateral deflection — used at elbows and offset runs but require anchor points to handle pressure thrust. Rubber expansion joints: lower cost, used for vibration isolation and thermal movement in cooling water, utilities, and low-temperature chemical service. PTFE-lined rubber joints for chemical service add 40-60% to cost. Slip-type joints: used on steam mains and low-pressure utility lines; mechanical seal requires periodic maintenance. Joint type is the major cost driver after pipe size: metallic bellows at DN150 ranges from USD 1200-3500; rubber joint at DN150 is USD 400-900.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: For Pipe support installation: total number of individual support locations (hangers, shoes, clamps, spring hangers). One support = one location on the isometric drawing. For Pipe support structure: enter total weight in kg in the Structural Steel Weight field above; set Quantity = 1 (the weight field carries the sizing). For Pipe support load calculation: number of calculation sheets or support analysis reports. Typically 1 calculation per support location for spring hangers; 1 calculation per anchor/fixed point for stress checks. For Pipe expansion joint installation: number of individual expansion joints. For Pipe sleeve installation: number of individual pipe sleeve locations (one per wall/slab penetration). Rule of thumb: process plant piping has approximately 1 support per 3-5 m of pipe run, and 1 expansion joint per 20-40 m of straight high-temperature pipe run.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipe support installation' AND 'spring' IN support_type AND equipment_subtype == 'Pipe support installation'
compliance_rules.message: Spring hangers (constant-load or variable) require a pre-installation load calculation per MSS SP-58 and ASME B31.3 Appendix S to determine the spring setting and travel range. Ensure that a Pipe support load calculation BOQ item is included in the estimate for every spring hanger installation. Hangers installed without a validated load calculation will require resetting during hot commissioning, at additional cost.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Appendix S (Stress analysis of flexibility and support); MSS SP-58 (Pipe hangers and supports - materials, design and manufacture); MSS SP-69 (Pipe hangers and supports - selection and application)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipe support structure' AND (structure_weight_kg IS None OR structure_weight_kg IS None OR structure_weight_kg <= 0)
compliance_rules.message: Pipe support structure is priced per kg of structural steel. The Structural Steel Weight field is required for this sub-type. Enter the total weight of the structure in kg to enable cost calculation. If the detailed weight is not yet known, use a parametric estimate: a typical 2-tier pipe rack section (8 m span, DN100-DN300 pipes) weighs approximately 800-1500 kg per bay.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: AISC Manual of Steel Construction; SP3D / PDMS structural steel model takeoff; IS 800 (Code of practice for general construction in steel)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipe support installation' AND (support_type == 'Resting / shoe support (carbon steel)' OR support_type == 'Clamp / U-bolt (standard CS)') AND (pipe_size_nb == 'DN400 (16 in NPS)' OR pipe_size_nb == 'DN500 (20 in NPS)' OR pipe_size_nb == 'DN600 (24 in NPS)')
compliance_rules.message: Simple resting supports and U-bolts are not normally used as the sole support for DN400 and above pipes. Large-bore pipes typically require engineered shoe supports with web stiffeners and welded baseplates, or trunnion supports for vertical pipes. Verify the support type against the pipe stress isometric or support standard drawing. Undersized or inadequate supports for large pipes can lead to excessive sag, overstress at support points, and crevice corrosion under the shoe.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Para 321 (Design of pipe supporting elements); MSS SP-58; Pipe stress analysis (Caesar II / AutoPIPE); NACE SP0169 (underfilm corrosion)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipe expansion joint installation' AND 'Untied' IN expansion_joint_type AND quantity > 0
compliance_rules.message: Untied metallic bellows expansion joints do not restrain pressure thrust — the full pressure thrust force (pi/4 x OD^2 x test pressure) must be resisted by anchors in the piping system. Ensure that anchor loads from untied bellows are included in the pipe stress analysis and that anchor structural steel is adequately sized. Undersized anchors for untied bellows are a common cause of pipe system failure during pressurisation.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: EJMA Standards (Expansion Joint Manufacturers Association) 10th Edition; ASME B31.3 Para 319.4 (Expansion and flexibility); EN 14917 (Metal bellows expansion joints)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipe sleeve installation' AND (pipe_size_nb == 'DN400 (16 in NPS)' OR pipe_size_nb == 'DN500 (20 in NPS)' OR pipe_size_nb == 'DN600 (24 in NPS)')
compliance_rules.message: Pipe sleeves for DN400 and above through concrete walls or slabs require significant core drilling or pre-formed blockout engineering. Verify that the civil structural drawing confirms adequate clearance, fire stop detail, and seal type. Large bore sleeves may require structural penetration approval by the civil engineer. The sleeve installation cost at DN400+ is significantly higher than the unit rate at smaller bores due to weight, drilling equipment, and sealing complexity.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 875 (Code of practice for structural loading); ACI 318 (Building code for structural concrete); BS EN 1992-1-1 (Eurocode 2 - concrete structures); Fire stop standard UL 2079

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipe support installation' AND 'stainless' IN support_type AND !'DN' IN pipe_size_nb
compliance_rules.message: Stainless steel supports are required where carbon steel supports would cause bimetallic galvanic corrosion (SS pipe on CS shoe in wet or humid environments) or chloride concentration under the shoe (coastal, offshore, or immersed service). For offshore and marine service, SS316L shoe and clamp materials are mandatory. Verify the corrosion protection requirements with the materials engineer before changing to CS support hardware.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NACE SP0169 (Control of external corrosion); ISO 15589-1 (Cathodic protection of pipelines); EFC Publication No.16 (Guidelines on materials requirements for carbon and low alloy steels)

cost_build_up.note: This schema covers 5 heterogeneous items with 3 measurement bases: (1) No. of support locations for Pipe support installation and load calculations; (2) kg of structural steel for Pipe support structure; (3) No. of joints/sleeves for expansion joints and sleeves. The cost engine selects the appropriate rate from usd_cost_baseline by matching equipment_subtype and applying the relevant sizing field.
cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: C = unit_rate(equipment_subtype, pipe_size_nb OR structure_weight_kg) * quantity * regional_index_factor
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: C = unit_rate(equipment_subtype, pipe_size_nb OR structure_weight_kg) * support_type_factor(support_type) * expansion_joint_type_factor(expansion_joint_type) * quantity * regional_index_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: C_total = C * (1 + height_access_factor) * (1 + contingency) * sub_region_labour_factor
cost_build_up.scope_inclusions: For pipe support installation: supply and installation of all support hardware (hanger rods, clevis, turnbuckle, clamp, shoe, U-bolt, baseplate) to the structural steel or concrete anchor. For pipe support structure: structural steel supply, surface preparation, primer coat, fabrication at workshop, and site erection including anchor bolts. For load calculations: stress analysis software (ASME B31.3 compliance check), calculation report, and revision cycle. For expansion joints: supply of joint from manufacturer and installation including tie rods (if tied), flange bolting, and initial alignment check. For pipe sleeves: supply of sleeve pipe, installation in wall/slab, annular gap seal, and fire stop packing.
cost_build_up.scope_exclusions: For pipe support structure: foundation concrete anchor bolts (separate civil item), access scaffolding above 4.5 m (separate item), shop painting beyond one primer coat. For expansion joints: pipe spool pieces connecting joint to adjacent elbows/valves. For pipe sleeves: wall or slab core drilling if sleeve not cast in (separate civil item). Engineering QA/QC review of load calculations.

adders.note: For activity (type A) items, freight_insurance = 0.0 and erection_installation = 0.0 because all labour and materials are in the unit rate. Exception: Pipe expansion joints are manufactured items (item_type E conceptually) but are priced here as installed cost inclusive. Contingency 0.12 reflects complexity of spring hanger settings, interface with structural steel anchor points, and site measurement uncertainty for custom expansion joint spool pieces.

support_type_factor.note: Cost multiplier on base Pipe support installation unit rate (resting/shoe CS base = 1.0). Applied for Tier 2 estimate.

expansion_joint_type_factor.note: Cost multiplier on base expansion joint unit rate (single-ply metallic bellows SS316L = 1.0).

usd_cost_baseline.source: RSMeans Mechanical Cost Data 2024, MSS SP-58 / SP-69 support hardware catalogues (Lisega / Bergen-Paterson / Piping Tech), EJMA member pricing, EPC contractor piping norms (2024 USD)
usd_cost_baseline.pricing_basis: Fully installed cost per unit (support, joint, sleeve, or calculation) at USA base rate. Apply support_type_factor or expansion_joint_type_factor for Tier 2. Apply regional_index_factor for location.

items.sizing_basis: Nominal bore - rate per support location (USD/No.) at resting/shoe CS base
items.notes: Per support location, resting shoe / clamp type, carbon steel, at up to 4.5 m working height. Includes supply of standard catalogue support hardware (hanger rod, clevis, clamp, shoe per MSS SP-58) and installation labour. Apply support_type_factor for spring hangers, anchors, and specialty types.
items.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

items.sizing_basis: Structural steel weight (kg) - rate per kg erected (USD/kg)
items.notes: Fabrication and erection of pipe support structural steel (pipe racks, trunnion frames, stanchions). Rate per kg of erected steel includes: steel section supply (A36/SS400), workshop cutting, drilling, and primer coat, site erection and anchor bolt tightening. Complex structures (multi-tier racks, elevated platforms) are at the upper end of the range; simple single-leg stanchions are at the lower end. Rate excludes access scaffolding above 4.5 m.

Engineering deliverable: ASME B31.3 / B31.1 pipe stress analysis and support load calculation per support location. Includes Caesar II / AutoPIPE analysis, spring hanger specification (cold and hot loads, travel setting), anchor load summary, and calculation report. Rate per calculation (per spring hanger or anchor location). Simple restraint checks for resting supports are included at 20% of the spring hanger rate.
items.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

items.sizing_basis: Nominal bore - rate per joint installed (USD/No.) at single-ply metallic bellows SS316L base
items.notes: Fully installed cost per expansion joint (supply + installation) for single-ply metallic bellows, SS316L, single convolution, PN16 flanged ends per EJMA standard. Includes joint supply from manufacturer, installation labour, flange alignment, bolting, and initial leak check. Apply expansion_joint_type_factor for other types. Rubber joint rates are 0.40x metallic bellows at the same NB.
items.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

items.sizing_basis: Nominal bore - rate per sleeve installed (USD/No.)
items.notes: Supply and installation of pipe sleeve (casing pipe) in wall, floor, or beam penetration. Includes: sleeve pipe supply (CS pipe, 2 diameters larger than process pipe), welding of end plates or guide rings, installation in cast opening (pre-formed blockout) or core-drilled hole, bitumen-wrap or fire stop packing of annular space, and water seal at ends. Rate assumes pre-formed blockout in new construction; core drilling in existing concrete adds approximately 50-80% to the rate and should be priced as a separate civil item.
items.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Pipe support activities are labour-dominated (60-70% labour). ENR CCI is the appropriate index. regional_factor converts USA base to project region.

SCHEMA-PIPE-TESTING.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs filled. Quantity unit corrected from 'Job' to 'm' (linear metres of pipe tested/flushed) and 'No. (pig runs)' for pigging, controlled by conditional_display. Added pipe_size_nb (primary cost driver - determines water volume and pump capacity), test_pressure (Tier 2 - drives pump rating and temporary connection class), test_medium (Tier 2 - water vs pneumatic vs service fluid have radically different costs). freight_insurance and erection_installation both 0.0 (site activity). import_duty corrected. usd_cost_baseline as USD/m lookup by NB size and activity, nested inside cost_build_up. Based on ASME B31.3, ASME B31.4, IS 2825, API 1110, API 570, EPC contractor piping test norms (2024 USD).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-PIPE-TESTING.spec_example: Hydrotesting of pipelines | DN150 | Potable water | 22.5 bar(g) | 250 m

fields.label: Testing Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the pipeline testing or cleaning activity. Hydrotesting: the primary pressure integrity test for all process piping before commissioning. The pipeline or test section is filled with water (or other approved liquid), pressurised to 1.5x design pressure (ASME B31.3) or 1.25x MAOP (ASME B31.4 / API 1110), held for a minimum of 10 minutes (process piping) to 4-8 hours (transmission pipelines), and inspected for leaks. Mandatory for all process piping per ASME B31.3 before initial operation. Flushing: hydraulic or chemical cleaning of pipelines to remove mill scale, weld spatter, construction debris, and temporary assembly materials before introducing process fluid. Involves high-velocity water flow (minimum 1.5 m/s) through the line. Required as a pre-commissioning activity for catalyst lines, compressor suction lines, and instrument air systems. Pigging: mechanical cleaning and/or inspection using a pig (cylindrical tool propelled by fluid pressure) through the pipeline bore. Used for long-distance transmission pipelines (oil, gas, water) to remove wax, scale, and debris, and for inline inspection (intelligent pigging / MFL). Applicable to onshore and offshore transmission lines; not normally used for plant piping below DN100.

fields.label: Pipe Nominal Bore (NB / DN)
fields.hint: Nominal bore of the pipeline being tested or flushed. Pipe size is the primary cost driver because: (1) Water volume required for hydrotesting scales with bore area — a DN600 line requires 16x the water of a DN150 line per metre of length. (2) Pump capacity and pressure rating must match the bore and volume. (3) Number of temporary test heads, vents, and drains scales with pipe size. (4) Water disposal or treatment after test is proportional to volume filled. For pigging, the pig size and launcher/receiver size are determined by the pipe bore. For a mixed-bore system, create separate BOQ items for each dominant pipe size.

fields.label: Test Medium
options: Compressed air (pneumatic test - low pressure utility)
Service fluid (air, gas, or product test per design)
fields.hint: Test or flushing medium. Potable or treated water is standard for hydrotest per ASME B31.3 (must be clean, non-corrosive, and free of suspended solids). For SS316L, duplex, and high-alloy stainless piping: use inhibited water with chloride content <50 ppm to prevent stress corrosion cracking — treated water adds approximately 15-25% to hydrotest cost for water treatment chemicals and analysis. Pneumatic testing with nitrogen is used where water cannot be tolerated (oxygen service, food-grade, catalyst systems, equipment structurally unsuitable for water fill). Pneumatic test is significantly more dangerous (stored energy vs liquid) and requires a detailed safety plan per ASME B31.3 Appendix F, safety exclusion zone, and much slower pressurisation — adds approximately 40-60% over equivalent hydrotest cost. Service fluid test (using actual process fluid such as air or inert gas) is only permitted in limited cases per code.

fields.label: Test Pressure
fields.hint: Test pressure at the highest point of the test section. Per ASME B31.3: hydrotest pressure = 1.5 x design pressure (P_design), but not less than the flange MAWP at test temperature. Per ASME B31.4 and API 1110 for transmission pipelines: test pressure = 1.25 x MAOP (minimum) to 1.5 x MAOP. Test pressure drives: (1) Pump class required — for test pressures above 40 bar(g) a high-pressure positive displacement pump and stainless temporary connections are needed. (2) Temporary test head and blind flange pressure rating — at 150 bar(g) test pressure, Class 900 or 1500 temporary fittings are required vs Class 150 at low pressure. Higher pressure tests cost approximately 20-40% more per metre due to pump and fitting premium. For pneumatic tests the pressure is typically 1.1 x design pressure (lower than hydrotest by code).

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: For Hydrotesting and Flushing: total length of pipeline (in metres) in the test section. Measure the longest continuous test section that can be isolated by temporary blanks or permanent block valves. Typical plant piping test section length: 50-500 m. Long-distance pipelines: 1-20 km per test section. For Pigging: number of pig passes (runs) through the pipeline. A single cleaning sequence typically involves 2-3 progressively stiffer pigs plus a final gauge plate run. An inline inspection pig (intelligent pig / MFL) counts as 1 run.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pigging of pipelines' AND (pipe_size_nb == 'DN25 (1 in NPS)' OR pipe_size_nb == 'DN40 (1.5 in NPS)' OR pipe_size_nb == 'DN50 (2 in NPS)' OR pipe_size_nb == 'DN80 (3 in NPS)')
compliance_rules.message: Pigging is generally not economical or practical for plant piping below DN100. Mechanical pigs require pig launcher and receiver installation and are not normally used in process plant piping networks with multiple bends, branches, and reducers. For DN25-DN80 plant piping, use hydraulic flushing (high-velocity water flush) instead of pigging. Pigging is applicable to long-distance transmission pipelines (oil, gas, water) and larger plant headers above DN200.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 1160 (Managing System Integrity for Hazardous Liquid Pipelines); API 570 (Piping Inspection Code); ASME B31.4 (Pipeline Transportation Systems for Liquids)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: test_medium == 'Potable water (standard hydrotest)' AND (equipment_subtype == 'Hydrotesting of pipelines')
compliance_rules.message: For austenitic stainless steel (SS304/316), duplex stainless, and high-alloy piping: verify that the water used for hydrotesting has chloride content below 50 ppm (for SS316L) or below 25 ppm (for duplex SS 2205) to prevent stress corrosion cracking (SCC) during or after the test. Standard potable water may contain 50-200 ppm chloride depending on the local source. Use 'Treated / inhibited water' option if chloride content is not confirmed acceptable. After hydrotest, the line must be fully drained and dried within 24 hours to prevent pitting corrosion.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Para 345.4.2 (Test medium for stainless steel); NACE SP0169; BS PD 8010-1 (Pipeline systems); Sandvik / Outokumpu stainless steel welding and testing guidelines

compliance_rules.condition_expr: test_medium == 'Nitrogen (pneumatic test - process piping)' AND test_pressure > 40
compliance_rules.message: Pneumatic testing above 40 bar(g) presents significant stored energy risk. At this pressure level, a detailed safety analysis per ASME B31.3 Appendix F is mandatory, including brittle fracture evaluation, corrosion assessment, and blast exclusion zone calculation. A minimum safety exclusion radius of (test pressure x pipe volume)^0.5 / 4 should be established. Pneumatic test above 40 bar(g) may require client HSE approval and regulatory notification. Consider hydrotesting if the system can tolerate water.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Appendix F (Precautionary considerations for pneumatic testing); API 1110; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146; EPC pneumatic test safety procedure requirements

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Hydrotesting of pipelines' AND test_pressure > 0 AND test_pressure < 1.0
compliance_rules.message: Test pressure below 1.0 bar(g) is insufficient for a meaningful pressure integrity test. Minimum hydrotest pressure per ASME B31.3 is 1.5 x design pressure, with an absolute minimum of 1.4 bar(g) gauge (equivalent to 1.0 bar design pressure for the minimum Class 150 rating). Verify design pressure and update test pressure.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Para 345.4 (Hydrostatic leak test); ASME B16.5 (Pipe flanges - pressure-temperature ratings); IS 2825 (Unfired pressure vessels)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Flushing of pipelines' AND pipe_size_nb == 'DN1200 (48 in NPS)'
compliance_rules.message: Hydraulic flushing of DN1200 pipelines requires very high flow rates (minimum 1.5 m/s velocity = approximately 8500 m3/h for DN1200). A dedicated high-capacity temporary pumping arrangement and large diameter temporary hoses are required. Verify that the flushing sequence plan and temporary water supply volume have been confirmed by the project engineer before pricing. This is not a routine flushing activity.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 570 Para 7.1 (Flushing and cleaning); ASME B31.3 Para 341.1 (Examination before initial operation); Industry cleaning velocity standard minimum 1.5 m/s

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Hydrotesting of pipelines' AND quantity > 5000
compliance_rules.message: Test section length exceeds 5000 m. For very long test sections, verify that: (1) the static head difference between highest and lowest point does not cause the test pressure at the lowest point to exceed the pressure rating of any component, (2) sufficient temporary vents and drains are included in the BOQ, and (3) a dedicated test pump and pressure chart recorder are deployed. For sections above 2 km, a dedicated test pack (pump, chemical dosing, separator) mobilisation cost should be added as a separate lump sum item.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.4 Para 437.4 (Hydrostatic test for pipelines); API 1110; PNGRB Technical Standard T-4 (India - pipeline hydrotesting)

cost_build_up.note: Pipe testing activities are billed per linear metre (m) of pipe tested or flushed, at the specified nominal bore and test conditions. For pigging, billed per pig run. Cost = unit_rate(activity, pipe_size_nb) * test_medium_factor(test_medium) * pressure_factor(test_pressure) * quantity * regional_index_factor.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: C = unit_rate(equipment_subtype, pipe_size_nb) * test_medium_factor(test_medium) * pressure_factor(test_pressure) * quantity * regional_index_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: C_total = C * (1 + section_complexity_factor) * (1 + contingency) * sub_region_labour_factor
cost_build_up.scope_inclusions: Labour for temporary blind installation and removal, filling and draining of test section, pump operation, pressure monitoring and charting, test witnessing, leak inspection, NDE of any suspect areas revealed by test, water disposal or reuse. For flushing: temporary hose connections, strainer installation and cleaning. For pigging: pig run monitoring, pig tracking, pig receiving inspection.
cost_build_up.scope_exclusions: Water supply (potable water utility connection or water trucking is priced separately if not available at site), water treatment chemicals for inhibited water (separate BOQ item), disposal of contaminated test water (regulatory compliance cost if applicable), temporary pig launcher/receiver fabrication and installation (separate equipment BOQ item for large transmission pipeline piggers), pressure chart recorders if client-supplied, hydrostatic test packs for very long pipeline sections (mobilisation as lump sum).

adders.note: Site labour activity items have freight_insurance = 0.0 and erection_installation = 0.0 because the unit rate is fully inclusive of all labour and consumables. Contingency of 0.12 (12%) reflects: failed test and retest probability (repair and refill cycle), water temperature and weather effects on hold time, unexpected leaks at flanged joints requiring make-up torquing, and access difficulty at test sections with complex geometry.

test_medium_factor.note: Cost multiplier on base hydrotest unit rate (potable water base = 1.0). Accounts for medium cost, handling procedures, and safety requirements.

pressure_factor.note: Cost multiplier based on test pressure range. Higher pressure requires higher-rated pumps, fittings, and safety procedures. Base = 1.0 for 10-25 bar(g) (typical process piping Class 150/300).

factors.note: Low pressure utility / drain piping
Standard process piping - Class 150/300 flanges
High pressure - Class 300/600 flanges, HP pump
Very high pressure - HP positive displacement pump, Class 600/900 temp connections
Extreme pressure - specialised HP pump, Class 1500/2500 fittings
Ultra high pressure - specialist contractor required

usd_cost_baseline.source: RSMeans Mechanical Cost Data 2024, API 1110 (Testing pipelines), EPC contractor piping test productivity norms (Bechtel / Fluor / Technip), PNGRB T-4 pipeline testing norms (India), industry benchmarks (2024 USD)
usd_cost_baseline.pricing_basis: Per linear metre of pipeline for hydrotesting and flushing (potable water, 10-25 bar test pressure, USA base). Per pig run per km for pigging. Apply test_medium_factor, pressure_factor, and regional_index_factor for other conditions.
usd_cost_baseline.note: Rates include temporary connections (test heads, vents, drains), filling and draining labour, pump rental and operation, and test chart/documentation. Does NOT include water supply, water treatment, disposal of contaminated water, or permanent valve and instrument costs.

items.sizing_basis: Nominal bore - rate per linear metre of pipe (USD/m)
items.notes: Hydrostatic leak test per ASME B31.3 Para 345.4. Rate per metre for plant/process piping in test sections of 50-500 m. Includes filling, pressurisation to 1.5x design pressure, 10-min minimum hold, inspection, depressurisation, and draining. For longer transmission pipeline sections (>1 km) per ASME B31.4 / API 1110, rates may reduce 10-20% due to economies of scale from fewer test heads per metre.
items.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

items.sizing_basis: Nominal bore - rate per linear metre of pipe (USD/m)
items.notes: High-velocity hydraulic flush per API 570 / ASME B31.3 pre-commissioning. Minimum velocity 1.5 m/s (turbulent regime). Rate includes temporary hose connections, strainer installation upstream of sensitive equipment, flushing water circulation until clear/clean by strainer inspection, strainer cleaning, and final drain. Approximately 40-45% of equivalent hydrotest cost because no pressure testing equipment or pressure charting is involved.
items.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

items.sizing_basis: Nominal bore - rate per pig run per km of pipeline (USD/km/run)
items.notes: Rate per pig run per km of pipeline, including pig launcher/receiver hire and operation, pig tracking, pig reception and inspection, and run documentation. A standard cleaning sequence = 3-4 runs (foam pig, wire brush pig, foam pig, gauge plate run). An inline inspection (MFL, caliper, UT) intelligent pig is 1 run but significantly more expensive — multiply rate by 8-15 for intelligent pig. Excludes permanent launcher/receiver fabrication (separate equipment BOQ item). Not applicable to DN25-DN80 plant piping.
items.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

regional_cost_index.note: Pipeline testing is a labour-dominated activity (labour ~65% of cost; pump and equipment hire ~35%). ENR CCI labour component is the most appropriate index. regional_factor converts USA base to project region.

SCHEMA-PIPE-WELDING.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs filled. Quantity unit corrected from 'Job' to 'No. (weld joints)'. Added pipe_size_nb (primary cost driver, entirely absent from skeleton), pipe_material (major - drives filler, pre-heat, qualification), wall_thickness_schedule (Tier 2), and nde_requirement (Tier 2 - single largest cost modifier after size). freight_insurance and erection_installation both 0.0 (type-A activity, unit rate is all-inclusive). usd_cost_baseline as USD/joint lookup by NB size and process, nested inside cost_build_up. import_duty corrected for site labour activity. Based on ASME B31.3, ASME IX, IS 2825, NFPA 55, RSMeans Mechanical 2024, CPCL/EPC contractor welding norms (2024 USD).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-PIPE-WELDING.spec_example: Pipe welding (GTAW/TIG) | DN80 | SS316L | Sch 40S | NDE: 100% RT

fields.label: Welding Process
fields.hint: Select the welding process. SMAW (Shielded Metal Arc Welding) — manual stick welding using coated electrodes. Most common field process for CS and low-alloy piping above DN50. Relatively low equipment cost but slower deposition rate; suitable for all positions. GTAW / TIG (Gas Tungsten Arc Welding) — high-quality root and fill pass for stainless steel, duplex, titanium, and high-alloy piping. Mandatory for SS and exotic alloys where porosity or lack of fusion in root is not acceptable. Significantly slower and more expensive than SMAW. SMAW fill over GTAW root (GTAW/SMAW combination) is common for CS high-pressure piping — price as GTAW. Automatic / mechanised welding — orbital TIG or SAW (submerged arc) for shop or site spooling; high capital cost offset by very high productivity on repetitive joints. Most cost-effective above DN200 with high joint counts.

fields.label: Pipe Nominal Bore (NB / DN)
fields.hint: Nominal bore (DN / NPS) of the pipe being welded. This is the dominant cost driver for pipe welding — weld time scales approximately with the pipe circumference (pi x OD) and wall thickness, meaning cost rises roughly as NB^1.0 to NB^1.3. A DN600 butt weld takes 15-20x longer than a DN50 weld. Use the actual pipe size from the isometric drawing or piping material takeoff. For reducing joints, use the larger pipe size.

fields.label: Pipe Material (MOC)
fields.hint: Pipe material (MOC) directly affects welding cost through: (1) Filler metal cost — stainless and alloy fillers cost 5-15x carbon steel electrodes by weight. (2) Pre-heat and post-weld heat treatment (PWHT) — Cr-Mo and P91 require pre-heat to 200-300 deg C and PWHT at 700-760 deg C; this requires induction heating equipment and adds 30-60% to joint cost. (3) Purge gas — stainless and titanium root passes require inert back-purge with argon to prevent oxidation, adding argon gas cost and purging time. (4) Welder qualification — exotic alloys require separately certified welders per ASME IX. (5) Interpass temperature control for duplex (max 150 deg C) slows deposition rate significantly.

fields.label: Wall Thickness / Schedule
options: Sch 5S / Sch 5 (thin wall - low pressure utility)
Sch 40 / Sch STD (standard weight - most common)
Sch XXS (double extra strong - high pressure)
fields.default: Sch 40 / Sch STD (standard weight - most common)
fields.hint: Pipe schedule (wall thickness class) per ASME B36.10 / B36.19. Wall thickness directly determines weld cross-section area and hence the number of weld passes (fill passes) required. At DN100: Sch 40 has 6mm wall (3-4 passes); Sch 160 has 17.1mm wall (8-12 passes). Cost scales approximately with the number of passes. Thin wall (Sch 5S / 10S) requires more care to avoid burn-through but fewer passes overall. Heavy wall (Sch 120, 160, XXS) is used in high-pressure and lethal service and significantly increases weld time. Sch 40S is the standard for stainless steel piping.

fields.label: NDE / NDT Requirement
options: VT only (visual inspection - Category D / utility)
100% RT (full radiography - ASME B31.3 Category M / lethal)
fields.hint: Non-destructive examination (NDE) level required by the applicable piping code and service category. This is the single most impactful Tier 2 cost modifier after pipe size. RT (radiographic testing) requires: isotope or X-ray source, film/CR, developer, witness, interpretation, and possible weld repair and retest cycle. Cost breakdown at DN150 SMAW: VT only ~USD 180/joint; 10% RT adds ~USD 35/joint amortised; 100% RT adds ~USD 280/joint; 100% PWHT adds ~USD 150/joint (induction heating, PWHT chart, hardness test). For ASME B31.3 Category M (lethal service) and ASME B31.1 power piping: 100% RT is mandatory. Phased array UT (PAUT) is increasingly preferred over RT for thick-wall (>30mm) joints. PWHT is a separate but always co-specified requirement for P11, P22, P91, and P5 alloys.

fields.label: Quantity (No. of Weld Joints)
fields.hint: Total number of butt weld joints of this pipe size, material, and process combination. Count each individual butt weld as one joint. Socket welds and fillet welds are separate items. For a rough BOQ take-off, a rule of thumb for industrial piping is 1 weld joint per 3-5 m of pipe run (accounting for fittings, branches, flanges). A typical process plant with 500 m of DN100 CS piping has approximately 100-170 butt joints.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (pipe_material == 'Stainless Steel SS304 / SS304L' OR pipe_material == 'Stainless Steel SS316 / SS316L' OR pipe_material == 'Duplex Stainless Steel (2205 / 2507)' OR pipe_material == 'Titanium (Gr.2 / Gr.7)' OR pipe_material == 'Hastelloy C-276 / Incoloy 825') AND equipment_subtype == 'Pipe welding (SMAW)'
compliance_rules.message: SMAW (stick) welding is not the preferred process for stainless steel, duplex, titanium, or high-alloy piping. GTAW/TIG root pass (at minimum) is required for SS and exotic alloys to achieve adequate corrosion resistance and mechanical properties per ASME B31.3. SMAW on SS produces slag inclusions and may cause sensitisation without back-purge. Change welding process to GTAW/TIG or GTAW root + SMAW fill as applicable.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Process Piping, Table 341.3.2 (examination requirements); ASME Section IX (Welding qualifications); AWS D10.11 (root pass purging)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (pipe_material == 'Low Alloy Steel (1.25Cr-0.5Mo / 2.25Cr-1Mo / P91)') AND (nde_requirement == 'VT only (visual inspection - Category D / utility)' OR nde_requirement == '10% RT (spot radiography - normal process)')
compliance_rules.message: Low alloy Cr-Mo and P91 piping for high-temperature service requires 100% RT (or PAUT) per ASME B31.1 and most power piping codes, plus mandatory PWHT. VT only or 10% spot RT is not acceptable for this material in pressure service above 50 bar(g) or 400 deg C. Update NDE requirement to 100% RT + PWHT.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.1 Power Piping Para 136.4; ASME B31.3 Para 341.4.1; API 582 (Welding guidelines for the chemical and petroleum industries)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (pipe_material == 'Titanium (Gr.2 / Gr.7)' OR pipe_material == 'Hastelloy C-276 / Incoloy 825') AND equipment_subtype != 'Pipe welding (GTAW/TIG)'
compliance_rules.message: Titanium and high-nickel alloy (Hastelloy, Incoloy) piping must be welded exclusively by GTAW (TIG) with full inert gas back-purge and trailing shield. No other process is acceptable. These materials are extremely sensitive to atmospheric contamination during welding. Change process to GTAW/TIG.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3; AWS D10.6 (Gas Tungsten Arc Welding of Titanium); Haynes International welding guide; SME Welding Handbook Vol.2

compliance_rules.condition_expr: nde_requirement == 'VT only (visual inspection - Category D / utility)' AND pipe_material != 'Carbon Steel (CS) - A106 / A53' AND pipe_material != 'HDPE / PP (thermoplastic - butt fusion)'
compliance_rules.message: Visual inspection only (no RT/UT) is appropriate only for Category D (non-flammable, non-toxic, low pressure) service per ASME B31.3 or utility piping in non-hazardous service. For alloy steel, stainless, or exotic material piping, VT only is unlikely to meet the applicable code examination requirements. Verify service category and update NDE level.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Table 341.3.2 and Para 341.4 (Required examination); ASME B31.3 Appendix M (Category M service); API 570 (Piping inspection code)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pipe welding (automatic)' AND (pipe_size_nb == 'DN15 (0.5 in NPS)' OR pipe_size_nb == 'DN20 (0.75 in NPS)' OR pipe_size_nb == 'DN25 (1 in NPS)')
compliance_rules.message: Automatic / orbital welding for DN15-DN25 small-bore piping is technically feasible (orbital TIG is commonly used in pharmaceutical / semiconductor piping) but the capital cost of orbital heads for small bores may not be economical for low joint counts. Verify that the joint count justifies orbital equipment deployment. For pharmaceutical hygienic piping (ASME BPE), orbital TIG is the standard process regardless of joint count.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME BPE (Bioprocessing Equipment standard); ASME B31.3; AWS C5.4 (Torch brazing and heating guide)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: pipe_material == 'HDPE / PP (thermoplastic - butt fusion)' AND equipment_subtype != 'Pipe welding (automatic)'
compliance_rules.message: Thermoplastic (HDPE / PP) pipe joining is by butt fusion or electrofusion, not by arc welding. SMAW and GTAW processes do not apply. Use 'automatic' sub-type to represent fusion welding machines, or re-classify this item under the correct piping works category.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASTM F2620 (Heat fusion of polyethylene piping); ISO 21307 (Butt fusion of HDPE); DIN 19636 (Plastics piping)

cost_build_up.note: Pipe welding is an activity item billed per weld joint. Cost = unit_rate(welding_process, pipe_size_nb) * material_factor(pipe_material) * schedule_factor(wall_thickness_schedule) * nde_factor(nde_requirement) * quantity * regional_index_factor. Unit rates are per completed butt weld joint including all passes, electrode/filler, gas, and basic consumables.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: C = unit_rate(equipment_subtype, pipe_size_nb) * material_factor(pipe_material) * schedule_factor(wall_thickness_schedule) * nde_factor(nde_requirement) * quantity * regional_index_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: C_total = C * (1 + access_difficulty_factor) * (1 + contingency) * sub_region_labour_factor
cost_build_up.scope_inclusions: All welding labour (certified welder + helper), consumables (electrodes, TIG wire, shielding gas, purge gas for SS/Ti), joint preparation (grinding, bevelling check), tack welding, inter-pass cleaning, weld visual inspection (VT per ASME B31.3), welder qualification allowance (WPS/PQR amortised over joint count), and basic positioning equipment. NDE cost included as part of nde_factor.
cost_build_up.scope_exclusions: Pipe material supply (separate BOQ item), pipe cutting and bevelling machining (separate item), pipe supports and hangers (separate item), flange bolting-up (separate item), hydrostatic test (separate item), insulation, painting, PWHT furnace mobilisation for P91 (separate BOQ), radiographic film and source supply if client-furnished, pre-heating equipment rental if specified separately.

adders.note: For activity (type A) items, freight_insurance and erection_installation are 0.0 because there is no equipment to freight and the welding labour IS the installation. Contingency of 0.12 (12%) reflects weld repair and retest probability, access difficulty variability, and weather / shutdown window uncertainty — slightly higher than the standard 10% due to the quality-sensitive nature of welding activities.

material_factor.note: Cost multiplier on base unit rate (CS SMAW base = 1.0) to account for material-driven cost increases: filler metal cost, back-purge gas, pre-heat, interpass temperature control, and welder qualification premium.

schedule_factor.note: Cost multiplier for wall thickness relative to standard Sch 40 / STD. Reflects additional weld passes for heavier wall.

nde_factor.note: Cost multiplier to account for NDE level. Base = 1.0 for 10% spot RT (standard normal fluid service per ASME B31.3). Factors include NDE labour, equipment, consumables (film/CR plates), and probability of repair/retest cycle.

usd_cost_baseline.source: RSMeans Mechanical Cost Data 2024, NFPA 55 installation norms, EPC contractor welding productivity norms (Bechtel / Fluor / L&T), DS&S piping construction manual, industry benchmarks (2024 USD)
usd_cost_baseline.pricing_basis: Per completed butt weld joint, SMAW process, CS pipe, Sch 40, 10% spot RT. USA Gulf Coast base rate. Apply material_factor, schedule_factor, nde_factor, and regional_index_factor for other conditions.
usd_cost_baseline.note: Rates include welder + helper labour, electrodes, gases, tack welds, inter-pass cleaning, and basic VT. Does NOT include pipe material, pipe cutting / bevelling, radiographic testing equipment separately if VT only, PWHT, or hydro test.

items.notes: SMAW (stick) process, CS A106 Gr.B, Sch 40 STD, 10% spot RT per ASME B31.3 normal fluid service. USA Gulf Coast base rates. Apply factors for other materials, schedules, and NDE levels.
items.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

items.notes: Full GTAW (TIG) process all passes. Typically used for SS, duplex, Ti, and high-alloy piping where root quality is critical. Rate approx 1.8x SMAW for CS equivalent due to slower deposition rate. For GTAW root + SMAW fill combination (common on CS high-pressure piping), use GTAW rate x 0.85 as an approximation.
items.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

items.notes: Orbital TIG or mechanised SAW / GMAW process. Lower per-joint cost than manual above DN150 due to high deposition rate and consistent quality reducing repair rate. Rate advantage increases with joint count (equipment mobilisation cost is amortised). Rates assume minimum 50 joints per deployment to justify orbital head setup. For pharmaceutical orbital TIG (ASME BPE), add 30% for enhanced surface finish and documentation requirements.
items.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Pipe welding is a pure labour activity — regional cost is almost entirely driven by welder wage rates and crew productivity. The ENR CCI labour component is the most appropriate index. regional_factor converts USA Gulf Coast base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only.

regional_factors.equipment_duty_category: Piping site activity - no import duty applicable
regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines labour and civil factors. For site activity items, the labour_factor is the dominant driver; equipment_factor has minimal relevance (no imported equipment in the welding activity itself).

import_duty.category: Piping site activity - no import duty applicable
import_duty.components: Site labour activity - no goods imported
import_duty.note: Welding is a site labour activity. No import duty applies. Welding consumables (electrodes, wire) are covered in the unit rate.

import_duty.category: Piping site activity - no import duty applicable
import_duty.note: Site labour activity. No import duty.
