SCHEMA-PLASTERING.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs filled. item_type corrected E->A (activity/work item, not equipment supply). Degenerate area range corrected (10-15 m2 was library data, not input range). Separate area field removed - quantity IS the m2 area for unit-rate activities. Added surface_type and number_of_coats Tier 2 fields that materially affect paint/plaster rate. import_duty corrected for civil construction materials (cement/paint: 0-5% vs 7.5-10% for process equipment). usd_cost_baseline as unit-rate USD/m2 table nested inside cost_build_up. Based on CPWD DSR 2023-24, IS 1661 / IS 2395, RSMeans Finish Construction, Rawlinsons Australian Construction Cost Guide 2024.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-PLASTERING.spec_example: 12mm Cement Plaster (Internal) | 500 m2 | Surface: Brick masonry | Coats: 1

fields.label: Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the plastering, painting, or temporary structure activity type. Items are grouped into three categories: (1) Plastering - cement plaster in various thicknesses and locations (internal / external / damp areas), neat cement punning (thin finishing coat over plaster), and gypsum plaster for smooth dry-wall finish. (2) Painting - texture paint for exterior facades, emulsion paint for interior walls and ceilings, oil/enamel paint for woodwork and metalwork, and anti-corrosive paint systems for structural steel. (3) Temporary structures - site office, labour hutment, and material store billed per m2 of floor area. Sub-type selection controls which specification fields are shown.

fields.label: Surface Type / Substrate
fields.hint: Substrate or surface type on which the plaster or paint is being applied. This is the key Tier 2 cost driver because surface preparation and material adhesion requirements vary significantly. Rough or porous substrates (brick, rough concrete, stonework) require a scratch coat or bonding agent and more material. Smooth substrates (fair-face concrete, planed timber) need less preparation. Steel surfaces require blast cleaning (Sa2.5 per ISO 8501-1) before anti-corrosive paint, adding significant cost. Concrete block (AAC) absorbs more plaster mortar and needs primer coat first. Surface type also determines whether a PCC dubbing-out coat is needed before the main plaster.

fields.label: Number of Coats
fields.hint: Total number of coats including primer, undercoat, and finish coats. Applies to paint items and determines material quantity and labour time. Typical values: Emulsion paint (interior walls): 1 primer + 2 finish = 3 coats total (standard), or 1 primer + 1 finish = 2 coats (economy). Texture paint (exterior): 1 sealer + 1 texture = 2 coats. Oil paint (wood/metal): 1 red oxide primer + 1 undercoat + 2 finish = 4 coats (standard). Anti-corrosive paint (steel): 1 inhibiting primer + 1 intermediate + 2 finish = 4 coats (standard IS 1477 system); for immersed steel 6-8 coats may be specified. For plaster items, this field is not applicable (plaster is a single-operation activity).

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Measured quantity in m2 for plastering, painting, and punning items. For temporary structures (Site Office, Labour Hutment, Material Store): enter the total built-up floor area in m2. Typical project quantities: Small building: 500-2000 m2 of plaster. Industrial plant building: 2000-10000 m2. Large complex: 10000-50000 m2. For a quick estimate, plastered area is approximately 3.5 x floor area for a single-storey building (walls + soffit).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == '20mm Cement Plaster (Damp Areas)' AND surface_type != 'Concrete (fair face / smooth)' AND surface_type != 'Concrete block / AAC block'
compliance_rules.message: 20mm cement plaster for damp areas (bathrooms, basements, retaining walls) should be applied on concrete or masonry substrate with a waterproofing admixture (e.g. Sika 1 or Krystalline). Verify that a waterproofing compound is included in the unit rate or as a separate BOQ item. Without waterproofing admixture, thick plaster alone will not provide effective damp resistance.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 1661 (Code of practice for use of cement and cement-lime plaster finishes); IS 2402 (Code of practice for external rendered finishes); NBC 2016 Part 5

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Anti-Corrosive Paint (Steel)' AND (surface_type == 'Steel / metal (primed)' OR surface_type IS None OR surface_type IS None)
compliance_rules.message: Anti-corrosive paint systems on structural steel require surface preparation to minimum Sa2.5 (near-white blast cleaning) per ISO 8501-1 before primer application. If the surface is already primed, verify that the primer is compatible with the finish coat system (epoxy primer under epoxy finish; zinc-rich primer under two-pack polyurethane). Incompatible primer-topcoat combinations will cause delamination.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 1477 Parts 1 & 2 (Code of practice for painting of ferrous metals); ISO 8501-1 (Preparation of steel substrates before application of paints); SSPC-SP 10 (Near-white blast cleaning)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Gypsum Plaster' AND (surface_type == 'Concrete (fair face / smooth)' OR surface_type == 'Brick masonry (standard)')
compliance_rules.message: Gypsum plaster applied directly to brick or concrete requires a bonding agent (PVA or polymer slurry) to prevent delamination due to differential thermal expansion. Ensure bonding agent is included in the unit rate or as a separate item. Gypsum plaster must not be used in wet or damp areas.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 2547 (Gypsum plaster board); IS 1315 (Gypsum for building purposes); BS 8000-10 (Plastering and rendering)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Anti-Corrosive Paint (Steel)' AND number_of_coats < 3
compliance_rules.message: Anti-corrosive paint system for structural steel with fewer than 3 coats (primer + intermediate + finish) may not meet minimum film thickness requirements for corrosion category C3 or higher per ISO 12944. Standard process plant and industrial environments (C3/C4) require minimum 4 coats with total dry film thickness (DFT) >= 200 microns. Verify with the corrosivity category and DFT specification.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 12944 Parts 1-8 (Corrosion protection of steel structures by protective paint systems); IS 1477 Part 2; SSPC Paint Application Guide No.3

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == '15mm Cement Plaster (External)'
compliance_rules.message: External cement plaster is a single-coat or two-coat operation (scratch coat + float coat), not the same as a number of paint coats. If a finish coat of texture paint or weather coat is specified over the external plaster, price that as a separate Texture Paint (Exterior) item.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 1661 (Code of practice for use of cement and cement-lime plaster finishes); NBC 2016 Part 5 Clause 5.3 (External rendering)

cost_build_up.note: All items in this schema are activity items billed per m2 (or m2 of floor area for temporary structures). Cost = unit_rate(activity_subtype) * surface_factor(surface_type) * coats_factor(number_of_coats) * quantity * regional_index_factor. No equipment supply, no import duty, and no separate erection adder - labour and material are embedded in the unit rate.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: C = unit_rate(equipment_subtype) * surface_factor(surface_type) * coats_factor(number_of_coats) * quantity * regional_index_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: C_total = C * (1 + scaffolding_factor) * (1 + contingency) * sub_region_labour_factor
cost_build_up.scope_inclusions: All labour (plastering / painting tradesman and helper), materials (cement, sand, lime, paint, primer, gypsum), mixing / preparation, application tools, and basic scaffolding up to 4.5 m height for wall items. Temporary structure rates include materials and labour for construction and dismantling.
cost_build_up.scope_exclusions: Scaffolding above 4.5 m height (add scaffolding as separate BOQ item); surface blasting for anti-corrosive paint (Sa2.5 blast cleaning is a separate activity); waterproofing compound admixtures (price separately); bonding agent / PVA primer; protection of adjacent surfaces; making good defects (crack repair, dubbing out); inspection and DFT measurement.

scaffolding_factor.note: Additional cost factor for work at heights above 4.5 m. Applied to the base unit rate. Not applicable to floor-level or ground-level work.

factors.note: Included in base unit rate (low scaffolding / hop-up)
Double-lift scaffolding required
Triple or quadruple lift
Tower scaffold or suspended cradle
Suspended cradle or mast climber required

adders.note: For activity (type A) items, freight_insurance and erection_installation are both 0.0 because the unit rate already includes all material supply and labour. The scaffolding_factor table handles height-related cost uplift. Contingency of 0.10 (10%) applies to cover access difficulties, surface condition variability, weather delays, and minor rework.

surface_factor.note: Cost multiplier applied to the base unit rate to account for substrate type and preparation requirements. Base = 1.0 for standard brick masonry.

coats_factor.note: Cost multiplier based on number of coats for paint items. Base = 1.0 for 2 coats (standard emulsion or oil paint). Each additional coat adds approximately 40-45% of the base rate (material + labour for one additional coat).

factors.note: Single coat only - touch-up or very light duty
Standard 2-coat system - base
Standard 3-coat system (primer + 2 finish)
4-coat system - anti-corrosive steel standard
5-coat system - high corrosivity C4/C5
6-coat system - immersed or C5-M marine
7-coat - severe marine or chemical immersion
8-coat - pipeline / buried steel maximum

usd_cost_baseline.source: CPWD DSR 2023-24 (converted to USD), RSMeans Finish Construction Cost Data 2024, Rawlinsons Australian Construction Cost Guide 2024, BCIS UK Elemental Cost Analyses 2024, industry procurement benchmarks (2024 USD)
usd_cost_baseline.pricing_basis: Fully inclusive unit rate per m2 (labour + material + basic scaffolding to 4.5 m). USA base rate. Apply regional_index_factor and surface_factor / coats_factor for Tier 2 estimate.

items.notes: 1:4 cement-sand mortar, 12mm thick, applied to internal masonry walls and soffits. Single coat with wood float finish. Includes surface hacking, wetting, application, and curing for 7 days. Rate for standard brick masonry substrate at up to 4.5 m height.

1:6 cement-sand mortar, 15mm thick, applied to external masonry. Single coat with steel-trowel finish to give smooth surface ready for texture coat or weather coat. Includes surface hacking, wetting, application, and 28-day curing. Higher rate than internal plaster due to external access and weather risk.

1:3 cement-sand mortar with waterproofing admixture, 20mm thick, applied to bathrooms, wet areas, basements, and retaining walls. Two-coat application (undercoat + finishing coat). Higher material cost due to richer mix and waterproofing compound. Note: waterproofing admixture priced separately if not included.

Thin cement slurry (neat cement + water) applied as a finishing layer over green plaster or concrete to fill pinholes and provide a smooth hard surface. Average 1-3 mm thick. Used over floor screeds, plaster, and concrete prior to painting. Quick to apply - rate reflects labour only (very low material content).

Machine or hand-applied gypsum plaster, 12-13mm thick, on masonry, concrete, or drywall. Provides a smooth white finish without painting primer. Includes bonding agent application, skim coat application, and rubbing to smooth finish. Higher material cost than cement plaster (gypsum is more expensive than cement-sand). Not suitable for wet areas.

Polymer-based exterior texture paint applied over primed cement plaster. Rate is per coat. Standard: 1 sealer coat + 1 texture coat = 2 coats total. Apply coats_factor for additional coats. Includes surface priming with sealer, texture application, and protection of adjacent surfaces. Rate for smooth external plaster substrate.

Acrylic emulsion paint on internal plastered walls and ceilings. Base rate is per coat. Standard: 1 primer coat + 2 emulsion finish coats = 3 coats total. Apply coats_factor. Includes preparation (filling nail holes, minor cracks), application by brush or roller. Premium paints (washable, low-VOC) add 20-30% to material cost.

Synthetic enamel / alkyd oil paint on woodwork (doors, windows, frames) and metalwork (handrails, grilles, pipes). Base rate is per coat. Standard system: 1 red oxide primer + 1 undercoat + 2 finish = 4 coats. Apply coats_factor. Higher rate per m2 than wall paint due to complex shapes, masking, and slower coverage on metal surfaces.

Heavy-duty anti-corrosive coating system on structural steel, pipework, and equipment steelwork. Base rate is per coat for zinc-rich epoxy primer or similar. Standard process plant system (ISO 12944 C3): 4 coats total (zinc-rich primer + epoxy intermediate + 2 polyurethane finish). Apply coats_factor. Excludes blast cleaning (Sa2.5) which is a separate BOQ activity. Rate reflects skilled specialist painting contractor.

Prefabricated or site-built temporary site office, per m2 of floor area. Includes structural frame, roof, wall cladding, floor (PCC slab or timber), doors, windows, electrical points, and basic fittings. No AC or plumbing included. Typically 15-25 m2 per site manager. Rate covers construction and dismantling at end of project.

Temporary labour hutment (dormitory / barracks) for construction workforce, per m2 of floor area. Basic structure: galvanised corrugated sheet roof and walls on steel frame, concrete floor, communal sanitation connection. Typically 2-3 m2 per worker for budget purposes. Rate includes construction and dismantling.

Temporary covered material store / warehouse, per m2 of floor area. GI sheet roof on structural steel or timber frame, open or walled sides, PCC floor. Used for storage of construction materials, equipment, and tools. Rate includes construction and dismantling.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = unit_rate * (current_index / base_value) * regional_factor * sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. For finishing and painting trades, labour is the dominant cost component (60-70% of total). Regional labour rate differences drive most of the regional variation. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines labour and civil factors. For activity items, the equipment_factor is less relevant (no equipment import); the labour_factor and civil_factor govern regional cost variation.

import_duty.components: Cement and paints: BCD 0-5% (cement Nil, paints 5%); most construction materials exempt under project import concessions
import_duty.note: Construction finishing materials (cement, sand, aggregates) are largely sourced locally in India. Imported specialty paints (epoxy, polyurethane) attract 5% BCD + 18% IGST. Verify HS code with customs broker.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

SCHEMA-PLC-SYSTEM.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs filled. Added vendor_brand Tier 1 field. Frequency changed to dropdown. Conditional_display applied to voltage/current/frequency/enclosure. Ambient temperature relabelled and changed to dropdown. MARKET_RATES index retained. usd_cost_baseline nested inside cost_build_up. Based on Rockwell/Siemens/Schneider 2024 list prices.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-PLC-SYSTEM.spec_example: PLC CPU (Standard - Redundant) | Brand: Tier 1 - Rockwell (ControlLogix) | 24 V DC | Enclosure: IP20 | Amb: 0 to 60 deg C

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
options: PLC Analog Input Module (8 Ch - HART)
PLC Digital Output Module (16 Ch - Relay)
PLC Digital Output Module (16 Ch - Transistor)
fields.hint: Select the PLC component type. Items fall into 6 groups: (1) CPU modules - standard redundant or SIL3 safety rated. (2) Power supplies - 24 V DC or 120/240 V AC input, rack-mounted. (3) Racks / backplanes - chassis holding modules; 13 or 19 slots; remote I/O racks extend the system over Ethernet or fieldbus. (4) I/O modules - analog input (4-20 mA / HART), analog output, digital input (24 V DC or 120 V AC), digital output (relay or transistor), thermocouple, RTD. (5) Communication modules - Ethernet/IP, Modbus TCP, Profibus DP, DeviceNet. (6) HMI panels and safety items - operator interface panels and SIL-rated safety I/O. Sub-type controls which specification fields are shown.

fields.label: Vendor / Brand Tier
fields.hint: Vendor brand tier is the single largest cost driver for PLC hardware — equivalent I/O modules from Tier 1 global brands (Rockwell, Siemens) cost 30-50% more than Tier 2, and 60-80% more than Tier 3 generic makes. Tier 1 is mandatory for SIL2/3 safety systems, petrochemical (IEC 61511), and projects where OEM software licenses are tied to the platform. Tier 2 (Beckhoff, Mitsubishi) suits machine builders and OEMs. Tier 3 (local Indian makes) applies to non-critical discrete control where long-term support and certifications are less critical. Mixing brands within a system is strongly discouraged due to software tool incompatibility.

fields.label: Supply Voltage
fields.hint: Input supply voltage to the module or power supply. Standard values: 24 V DC (most I/O modules, CPUs, and comms modules); 120 V AC or 240 V AC (AC-input digital input modules, panel power supplies with AC feed). For power supply modules this is the AC input voltage. For I/O modules this is the field-side wetted voltage. Directly affects module cost for digital input modules (24 V DC vs 120 V AC modules differ in design and price).

value: PLC Digital Output Module (16 Ch - Relay)

fields.label: Rated Output Current
fields.hint: For power supply modules: rated DC output current at the 24 V DC bus. Typical values: I/O rack power supply: 2-10 A; CPU rack power supply (with redundancy): 5-20 A. Determines how many I/O modules can be powered per rack (each module draws 150-500 mA typically). For output modules: rated load current per channel (relay: 2-5 A; transistor: 0.5-2 A). Not applicable to CPU modules, racks, or comms modules.

value: PLC Digital Output Module (16 Ch - Relay)
PLC Digital Output Module (16 Ch - Transistor)

fields.label: Operating Ambient Temperature Range
options: 0 to 55 deg C (Standard with derating above 55 deg C)
-20 to 60 deg C (Extended low - cold climate)
-40 to 70 deg C (Wide range - outdoor / arctic)
fields.hint: Ambient operating temperature range at the module or panel location. Standard industrial PLC modules operate 0-60 deg C inside an air-conditioned or ventilated panel. Extended range (-20 to 60 deg C or -40 to 70 deg C) modules are required for outdoor installations, arctic sites, or unheated enclosures and add a 15-25% price premium. This is the ambient air temperature around the electronics, not the process fluid temperature.

fields.label: Supply Frequency
fields.hint: AC supply frequency. Relevant only for power supply modules with AC mains input. Most modern PLC power supplies are universal-input (50/60 Hz, 100-240 V AC) at no cost difference. Not applicable to DC-powered modules, CPU modules, I/O modules, or comms modules.

fields.label: Enclosure / Protection Rating
options: IP20 (Standard - panel mounted in clean room)
IP54 (Dust and splash protected - light industrial)
IP65 (Dust tight and jet-water protected - harsh area)
IP66 (Dust tight and powerful water jet - outdoor/marine)
Ex d / Ex e Zone 1 (IECEx / ATEX hazardous area)
fields.default: IP20 (Standard - panel mounted in clean room)
fields.hint: Ingress protection rating per IEC 60529. For standard rack-mounted PLC modules in a steel panel: IP20 is the module-level rating; the panel provides higher IP protection. For field-mounted HMI panels, safety controllers, and remote I/O racks: IP54 or IP65 is typical. For Zone 1 hazardous area installations: ATEX/IECEx Ex d or Ex e enclosures are required, adding 80-150% to panel cost. IP rating is the major cost driver for HMI panels and remote I/O housings.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical items. For I/O modules: total cards of this type across all racks. For racks: count individual chassis. For CPUs: typically 2 for redundant configuration (primary + standby). For power supplies: typically 2 per rack for redundant systems. For HMI panels: count screens. For safety I/O modules: count per SIL zone.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'PLC CPU (Safety - SIL3)' OR equipment_subtype == 'PLC Safety Controller (Standalone)' OR equipment_subtype == 'PLC Safety Input Module (8 Ch)' OR equipment_subtype == 'PLC Safety Output Module (4 Ch)') AND ('Tier 2' IN vendor_brand OR 'Tier 3' IN vendor_brand)
compliance_rules.message: SIL2/SIL3-rated safety PLC components must be sourced from vendors with IEC 61508 / IEC 61511 third-party certification (TUV / EXIDA) for the required SIL. Tier 2 and Tier 3 brands do not carry SIL3 certification for CPUs or safety I/O modules. Use Tier 1 only: Rockwell GuardLogix, Siemens S7 F-CPU, Schneider Modicon Safety, Yokogawa ProSafe-RS.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61508 (Functional Safety of E/E/PE Safety-related Systems Parts 1-7); IEC 61511 (Safety Instrumented Systems for the Process Industry); ISA-84.00.01

compliance_rules.condition_expr: enclosure == 'Ex d / Ex e Zone 1 (IECEx / ATEX hazardous area)' AND ('Tier 2' IN vendor_brand OR 'Tier 3' IN vendor_brand)
compliance_rules.message: Hazardous area certified (ATEX / IECEx) PLC hardware for Zone 1 requires OEM certification of the complete enclosure and module combination. Tier 2 and Tier 3 vendors may not carry ATEX Zone 1 certification for all module types. Verify certification documents before procurement.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079-0 (General requirements for explosive atmospheres); IEC 60079-11 (Ex i intrinsic safety); ATEX Directive 2014/34/EU; IECEx scheme

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'PLC CPU (Standard - Redundant)' AND quantity < 2
compliance_rules.message: A redundant CPU configuration requires a minimum of 2 CPU modules (primary + hot-standby). Quantity = 1 for a redundant CPU sub-type appears to be a configuration error. Update quantity to 2, or change sub-type to a non-redundant CPU if redundancy is not required.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61131-3 (PLC programming); Rockwell ControlLogix Redundancy System User Manual (Pub 1756-UM535); Siemens S7-400H Manual

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'PLC Communication Module (Profibus DP)' AND 'Rockwell' IN vendor_brand
compliance_rules.message: Profibus DP is not natively supported by Rockwell ControlLogix as a master interface. A third-party gateway (e.g. Prosoft MVI56E-PDPMV1) is required, adding approximately USD 1200-1800 per node. Consider Ethernet/IP or DeviceNet for Rockwell-based systems, or change PLC brand if Profibus DP is mandatory across many nodes.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61784-1 (Industrial communication networks - Profiles); Profibus International PI Specification; Rockwell 1756-series compatibility matrix

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'PLC Analog Input Module (8 Ch - HART)' AND 'Tier 3' IN vendor_brand
compliance_rules.message: HART-enabled analog input modules from Tier 3 vendors may not support full HART 7 pass-through to the DCS historian or asset management system. Verify HART multiplexer interface and hand-held communicator compatibility (HART 275 / 475) before specification.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HART Communication Foundation Specification Rev. 7; NAMUR NE107 (Self-monitoring and diagnosis of field devices); IEC 62591

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'PLC Communication Module (DeviceNet)' AND quantity > 5
compliance_rules.message: DeviceNet is a legacy fieldbus with declining OEM support. For new installations with more than 5 DeviceNet modules, consider migrating to Ethernet/IP which provides equivalent device connectivity with better bandwidth, diagnostics, and long-term OEM support.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ODVA EtherNet/IP Specification; ODVA DeviceNet Specification; IEC 62026-3

cost_build_up.note: PLC hardware items are catalogue/list-price items. Cost = unit_rate(equipment_subtype, vendor_brand_factor) * enclosure_factor(enclosure) * ambient_factor(operating_ambient_temperature) * quantity * regional_index_factor. No power-law equation applies.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: C_equip = unit_rate(equipment_subtype) * vendor_brand_factor(vendor_brand) * enclosure_factor(enclosure) * ambient_factor(operating_ambient_temperature) * quantity * regional_index_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: C_installed = C_equip * (1 + freight_insurance + installation_commissioning) * (1 + import_duty_pct) * (1 + contingency) * sub_region_factor
cost_build_up.scope_inclusions: Hardware module or panel supply ex-OEM factory including standard firmware, I/O terminal bases (for modules requiring them), module keying, and standard documentation.
cost_build_up.scope_exclusions: PLC software licenses (IEC 61131-3 programming tool, SCADA OPC server, redundancy add-on), PLC programming and configuration engineering, cable and termination, control panel steelwork and assembly (price panel as separate BOQ item), FAT, SAT, training, and spare parts.

adders.note: Installation / commissioning (0.08) covers rack insertion, signal wiring termination to I/O terminals, loop checking, and commissioning support. Significantly lower than mechanical equipment (0.20-0.35) because PLC modules are plug-in cards. HMI panels have higher installation content (~0.15) due to panel mounting, cabling, and HMI configuration. FAT, programming, and software licenses are separately scoped.

enclosure_factor.note: Multiplier applied to HMI panels, standalone safety controllers, and remote I/O racks that are field-mounted with elevated IP rating. Not applied to standard rack-mounted modules (IP20 is module-level; panel provides enclosure).

ambient_factor.note: Multiplier for extended temperature range modules. Standard 0-60 deg C = 1.0.

vendor_brand_factor.note: Multiplier applied on top of Tier 1 Rockwell base price. usd_cost_baseline is calibrated to Tier 1 Rockwell / Siemens list prices.

usd_cost_baseline.source: Rockwell Automation published list prices 2024, Siemens Industry Mall 2024, Schneider Electric pricelist 2024, ABB catalogue, industry procurement benchmarks (2024 USD)
usd_cost_baseline.note: Unit list price (supply only) calibrated to Tier 1 Rockwell ControlLogix equivalent. Apply vendor_brand_factor for other brands. Apply enclosure_factor for field-mounted items. OEM list prices; actual project prices are 15-30% below list price depending on project volume and distributor agreements.

items.notes: Dual-port hot-standby redundant CPU, 4 MB program memory, built-in Ethernet, USB programming port. ControlLogix L85E equivalent. Requires 2 units per redundant system plus redundancy chassis kit (~USD 3500 additional). Racks and I/O not included.

SIL3-certified safety CPU, IEC 61511 / IEC 61508. Rockwell GuardLogix / Siemens S7-F-CPU equivalent. Includes built-in safety partner module for 1oo2D architecture. TUV assessment certificate included.
24 V DC or 120/240 V AC input, regulated 5/24 V DC output, 10 A rated. Redundant pair per CPU rack recommended. Includes diagnostic LED and current sharing.
24 V DC input, 5 V DC output for I/O rack backplane, 5 A rated. One per I/O rack; redundant pair for critical systems.
19-slot local CPU chassis / backplane. Houses CPU, power supply, communications, and I/O modules. Includes mounting hardware.
19-slot I/O expansion chassis for local or remote expansion. Paired with communication adapter module.
13-slot remote I/O chassis with integrated Ethernet/IP or ControlNet adapter for distributed I/O. Mounted in field panel. Includes IP65 enclosure option (apply enclosure_factor separately).
16-channel analog input, 4-20 mA / 1-5 V, 16-bit resolution. Includes terminal base. Not HART-capable.

items.sub_type: PLC Analog Input Module (8 Ch - HART)
items.notes: 8-channel analog input with per-channel HART 7 pass-through. 4-20 mA, 16-bit. Allows HART device diagnostics and secondary variables.
8-channel analog output, 4-20 mA, 12-bit, current sourcing. For control valve positioners, VFD speed references. Includes terminal base.
32-channel digital input, 24 V DC, source or sink configurable. Filtered to 1 ms. For pushbuttons, limit switches, proximity sensors.
16-channel digital input, 120 V AC, 47-63 Hz. Used where field devices or existing wiring are 120 V AC (common in North America). Higher cost than 24 V DC module.

items.sub_type: PLC Digital Output Module (16 Ch - Relay)
items.notes: 16-channel digital output, relay contact, 2 A per channel at 240 V AC / 30 V DC. Galvanic isolation. For direct load switching. Mechanical life: 10 million operations.

items.sub_type: PLC Digital Output Module (16 Ch - Transistor)
items.notes: 16-channel digital output, transistor (solid-state), 0.5 A per channel at 24 V DC. Faster switching than relay (1 ms vs 10-15 ms). For drives interfaces, solenoids on 24 V DC.
8-channel thermocouple input, Type J/K/T/E/R/S/B, 16-bit, built-in CJC. Isolated per channel. For furnaces, reactors, heat exchangers.
4-channel RTD input, Pt100 / Pt1000 / Ni100 / Ni1000, 3-wire and 4-wire, 24-bit resolution. Higher accuracy than thermocouple. For HVAC, utilities, cryogenic applications.
Ethernet/IP adapter, 2 x RJ45, 100 Mbps, CIP protocol. For remote I/O racks, SCADA/DCS integration, inter-PLC communication. Native to Rockwell ecosystem.
Modbus TCP/IP module, RJ45, 100 Mbps. For third-party devices (drives, meters, analysers) using Modbus register protocol. Some brands implement as firmware feature of CPU.
Profibus DP master/slave, 9-pin D-sub, 12 Mbps max. Native to Siemens S7. Third-party gateway required for Rockwell systems (see compliance rule).
DeviceNet scanner, 5-pin open-style, 500 kbps. Legacy network for sensor/actuator level. Consider Ethernet/IP for new designs.
10-inch colour TFT touchscreen HMI, 800x600, Ethernet, USB, 24 V DC. Local operator panel for machine or skid control. IP65 front-face. Allen-Bradley PanelView Plus / Siemens KTP1000 equivalent.
15-inch colour TFT touchscreen HMI, 1024x768, dual Ethernet, USB, CF/SD card. For process unit operator stations. IP65 front-face. PanelView Plus 7 / Siemens TP1500 equivalent.
19-inch industrial panel PC, Core i5/i7, 8 GB RAM, 256 GB SSD, Windows 10 IoT, dual Ethernet, serial ports. For SCADA client, engineering workstation, or historian client. IP65 front-face.

Standalone safety PLC (SIL2/3 certified), pre-packaged with CPU, power supply, and basic I/O in dedicated enclosure. For ESD, F&G, or machinery safety. Pilz PNOZ m / Rockwell SmartGuard / Siemens ET200SP F-CPU equivalent. Includes TUV/EXIDA SIL certificate.
8-channel safety digital input, SIL2/3 certified, 24 V DC, 2-channel voting per safety loop. Diagnostic coverage >99%. For ESD valves, flame detectors, manual call points, safety switches.
4-channel safety digital output, SIL2/3 certified, 24 V DC, 2 A per channel. Solid-state with internal diagnostics. For ESD solenoid valves, deluge valves, trip relays.

regional_cost_index.note: PLC hardware prices are OEM list-price driven, not tracked by CEPCI or ENR CCI. regional_factor reflects local distributor pricing relative to USA list price.

India.local_ref: India distributor price - includes import duty offset by lower margin

USA.local_ref: OEM list price - Rockwell / Siemens / Schneider

SCHEMA-POWER-AUX.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs filled. 19 heterogeneous aux items handled via conditional_display on all sizing fields. Corrected degenerate field ranges. ENR_CCI corrected to CEPCI. usd_cost_baseline nested inside cost_build_up. Based on HEI Standards, CTI ATC-105, ASME PTC 12.2, Aspen ICARUS, GE/ABB/Emerson published data (2024 USD).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-POWER-AUX.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | {power} MW plant | Flow: {flow_rate} m3/h | Area: {area} m2 | Vol: {volume} m3 | MOC: {moc}
SCHEMA-POWER-AUX.spec_example: Condenser (Surface) | 100 MW plant | 8000 m3/h | 8500 m2 | — | CS+Ti tubes

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select power plant auxiliary equipment type. Condensers reject turbine exhaust heat. Cooling Towers reject condenser heat. Feedwater Heaters regenerate feedwater temperature from extraction steam. Deaerators remove dissolved oxygen from feedwater. Steam Ejectors and Vacuum Pumps maintain condenser vacuum. Excitation System, Synchronizing Panel, and Turbine Governor are electrical/control packages. Coal Mills, Feeders, and Crushers are coal handling items for coal-fired plants only. Sub-type controls which sizing fields appear.

fields.label: Associated Turbine / Plant Power
fields.hint: Rated output of the turbine or power unit this auxiliary serves. Primary Tier 1 sizing anchor for items without dedicated flow/area/volume (excitation, governor, sync panel, ejectors, deaerators). For condensers and cooling towers, specific sizing fields are the direct cost drivers and plant power is a sanity check. Typical range: 5-600 MW.

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: MAWP for pressure vessels and heat exchangers in this group. For surface condensers: waterbox design pressure typically 3-6 bar(g). For HP feedwater heaters: shell-side up to 50-80 bar(g). For LP feedwater heaters: 2-20 bar(g). For deaerators: 1-10 bar(g). Not applicable to cooling towers, coal handling, ejectors, or electrical control items.

fields.label: Heat Transfer Area
fields.hint: Total heat transfer area. For surface condensers: 100-150 m2 per MW turbine output typical. For air-cooled condensers: fin-tube face area, typically 5000-60000 m2. For HP feedwater heaters: 200-2000 m2 per heater. For LP feedwater heaters: 100-1000 m2 per heater. Not applicable to cooling towers, deaerators, ejectors, coal items, or electrical control items.

fields.label: Cooling Water Flow
fields.hint: Cooling water or circulating water volumetric flow. Primary sizing basis for cooling towers and secondary for surface condensers. Approximate rule of thumb: 50-80 m3/h per MW of heat rejected. Typical values: 50 MW plant: 3000-5000 m3/h; 100 MW: 5000-10000 m3/h; 300 MW: 15000-30000 m3/h. Not applicable to feedwater heaters, deaerators, ejectors, coal items, or electrical control items.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - Quick Estimate (visible for cooling items)

fields.label: Storage Tank Volume
fields.hint: Gross internal volume of the deaerator storage tank. Typically sized for 10-20 minutes of boiler feed flow. Approximate values: 50 MW plant: 50-150 m3; 100 MW: 150-300 m3; 300 MW: 500-1000 m3; 600 MW: 1000-2000 m3. Not applicable to condensers, cooling towers, heat exchangers, ejectors, coal items, or electrical control items.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum design temperature for pressure vessels and heat exchangers. HP feedwater heaters: 150-250 deg C. LP feedwater heaters: 50-150 deg C. Deaerators: 100-120 deg C. Surface condensers: shell-side 50-60 deg C design. Not applicable to cooling towers, coal handling, ejectors, or electrical control items.

fields.label: Motor / Drive Power
fields.hint: Installed motor power for mechanically driven items. Cooling Tower (Mechanical): fan motor per cell 15-250 kW. Cooling Tower (Induced Draft): fan motor 50-500 kW. Vacuum Pump (Liquid Ring): 15-110 kW. Coal Mill (Ball): 200-3000 kW. Coal Mill (Vertical): 100-2000 kW. Coal Feeder: 2-15 kW. Coal Crusher: 30-500 kW. Motor power is the primary cost driver for these mechanical items.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - Quick Estimate (visible for motor-driven items)

fields.label: Cooling Tower Effectiveness
fields.hint: Cooling tower thermal effectiveness = range / (range + approach) x 100. Higher effectiveness requires more fill depth and height, increasing cost. Typical values: mechanical draft: 60-80%; induced draft: 65-75%; natural draft: 55-70%. Not applicable to condensers, feedwater heaters, deaerators, coal items, or electrical control items.

fields.label: MOC (Material of Construction)
fields.hint: Material of construction for primary wetted or structural components. Surface condensers: CS shell with Ti tubes for seawater (CS+Ti, MOC factor 2.2x); admiralty brass or SS316L for freshwater. Cooling towers: FRP casing and PP fill for mechanical/induced draft; reinforced concrete shell for natural draft. Feedwater heaters and deaerators: CS (SA-516 Gr.70) standard. Coal handling: wear-resistant CS or Cr-iron alloys.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - Quick Estimate (visible where MOC is a major cost driver)

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical units. Cooling towers: price per cell; total cells typically 4-12 for large plant. Condensers / FW heaters: 1-2 per turbine. Deaerators: 1 per boiler feed system. Ejectors: 2 x 100% (one standby). Vacuum pumps: 2 x 100%. Coal mills: N+1 configuration (4-6 per boiler). Coal feeders: one per mill. Coal crushers: 2 (one standby).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Condenser (Surface)' AND area > 0 AND power > 0 AND (area / power) < 80
compliance_rules.message: Condenser heat transfer area per MW of turbine output is below 80 m2/MW. Typical sizing is 100-150 m2/MW at design back-pressure. An undersized condenser raises turbine back-pressure and reduces output and efficiency.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HEI Standards for Steam Surface Condensers (12th Edition); ASME PTC 12.2 (Steam Condensers)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Cooling Tower (Natural Draft)' AND power < 100
compliance_rules.message: Natural draft (hyperboloid) cooling towers are not economical below approximately 100 MW of heat rejection duty. For smaller plants, mechanical or induced draft towers have lower capital cost. Consider Cooling Tower (Mechanical) or Cooling Tower (Induced Draft).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CTI Bulletin ATC-105 (Acceptance Test Code for Water Cooling Towers); Black & Veatch Power Plant Engineering

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Steam Ejector (Single Stage)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Steam Ejector (Two Stage)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Vacuum Pump (Liquid Ring)') AND quantity < 2
compliance_rules.message: Vacuum-maintaining equipment is normally provided in 2 x 100% configuration (one duty, one standby) for continuous power generation duty. A single unit without standby is unusual.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HEI Standards for Steam Jet Ejectors (4th Edition); ASME PTC 24 (Steam Ejectors)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Coal Mill (Ball)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Coal Mill (Vertical)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Coal Feeder' OR equipment_subtype == 'Coal Crusher') AND power < 50
compliance_rules.message: Coal handling equipment is only applicable to pulverized coal or CFBC boiler plant. For power plants below 50 MW associated with coal firing, verify that the plant is coal-fired and not biomass, oil/gas, or HRSG-based.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Babcock & Wilcox Steam (41st Edition) - Chapter on Pulverisers; IS 8781 (Coal mill safety)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Feedwater Heater (HP)' AND design_pressure < 10
compliance_rules.message: HP feedwater heater shell-side design pressure below 10 bar(g) appears low. HP heaters typically handle high-pressure extraction steam and are designed for 30-80 bar(g) shell side. Verify whether this should be an LP heater sub-type.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HEI Standards for Closed Feedwater Heaters (8th Edition); ASME Section VIII Div.1; TEMA Class R

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Excitation System' AND power > 300
compliance_rules.message: For generators above 300 MW, excitation systems are typically Static Excitation Systems (SES) with high-capacity thyristor bridges or brushless rotating exciters. These are often quoted on a custom OEM basis. Verify the excitation system MVA rating against the generator short-circuit ratio.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEEE 421.5 (Excitation System Models); IEC 60034-16 (Excitation Systems for Synchronous Machines)

cost_build_up.cost_engine_note: 19 heterogeneous items. Engine matches equipment_subtype to usd_cost_baseline items and applies the correct primary_sizing_var: area for condensers/FW heaters, flow_rate for cooling towers/surface condenser secondary, volume for deaerators, motor_power for mechanical/coal items, plant power MW for fixed lump-sum items (ejectors, excitation, governor, sync panel).
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: C_equip = usd_cost_baseline(equipment_subtype, primary_sizing_var) * moc_factor(moc) * pressure_temp_modifier(design_pressure, design_temperature) * quantity * regional_index_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: C_installed = C_equip * (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation) * (1 + import_duty_pct) * (1 + contingency) * sub_region_factor
cost_build_up.scope_inclusions: Equipment supply including all integral components per usd_cost_baseline item notes. Local instruments and connections. Factory test.
cost_build_up.scope_exclusions: Civil foundations, cooling water pump house and pipework, interconnecting piping between items, electrical MCC and cabling, DCS I/O terminations, commissioning, and chemicals (anti-scalant, biocide for cooling towers).

adders.note: Erection / installation weighted average of 0.22 across 19 diverse items. Large items (surface condensers, natural draft cooling towers) have high civil content ~0.35. Packaged items (ejectors, vacuum pumps, excitation panels, governor) are 0.08-0.12. Cooling tower fill packing and natural draft concrete shell are the most field-intensive elements.

moc_factors.note: Cost multiplier on base equipment price. Base = 1.0 for CS. Applied primarily to condensers and feedwater heaters.

usd_cost_baseline.source: HEI Standards, CTI ATC-105, ASME PTC 12.2, GE/Alstom/Siemens auxiliary data, Foster Wheeler cost correlations, Aspen ICARUS 2024, industry benchmarks (2024 USD)
usd_cost_baseline.note: Supply-only (ex-works) cost. primary_sizing_var noted per item. Apply moc_factor, regional index, adders, and import duty for installed cost.

items.notes: Single or two-pass steam surface condenser, CS shell, admiralty brass or stainless tubes. Ti tubes: apply MOC factor 2.2. Includes hotwell, air extraction connections, expansion joint. Excludes CW pumps and pipework.

Air-cooled condenser (ACC), A-frame finned-tube modules, induced draft fans. Cost per m2 fin-tube face area. Includes fan motors, structural steel, and plenum chambers. Typically 2-4x cost of equivalent wet surface condenser due to larger required area.

Reinforced concrete hyperboloid natural draft cooling tower. Includes concrete shell, water distribution, fill packing, drift eliminators, basin. Civil foundation included. Very high erection content (40-50% of equipment cost). Minimum economic size ~100 MW plant.
Counter-flow or cross-flow induced draft cooling tower, FRP casing, PP fill, multi-cell. Includes fans, motors, water distribution, drift eliminators, and CS basin. Civil foundation separate.

Forced or induced draft mechanical cooling tower, cross-flow or counter-flow. FRP or galvanised steel casing. Includes fan motors and controls. Similar to induced draft but typically smaller per cell.
High-pressure closed feedwater heater, U-tube, TEMA R, HEI Class A. SA-516 Gr.70 shell, SA-179 CS tubes. Design pressure up to 80 bar(g) shell side. Includes drain cooler and desuperheating zone.
Low-pressure closed feedwater heater. SA-516 shell, design pressure 2-20 bar(g). Less expensive per m2 than HP heater due to thinner shell.
Spray-tray deaerator with integral storage tank. Operating pressure 1.1-4 bar(a). SA-516 Gr.70 CS. Includes spray nozzles, tray stack, vent condenser, and safety valve connections.
Vacuum deaerator operating below atmospheric pressure (0.1-0.8 bar abs). Higher cost than spray-tray due to vacuum-rated vessel design and associated vacuum pump or ejector system.
Single-stage steam jet air ejector for condenser vacuum. Includes inter-condenser, after-condenser, motive steam nozzle. CS construction. Typically supplied 2 x 100%. Cost per ejector unit.
Two-stage steam jet ejector with inter-stage condensing for higher vacuum and lower steam consumption. Approximately 65% more expensive than single-stage at same turbine size.
Liquid ring vacuum pump for condenser air extraction. Includes mechanical seal, service liquid separator, and coupling guard. Typically 2 x 100% configuration.
Static excitation system (SES) including thyristor bridge, AVR, power transformer, field breaker, and cubicle. IEEE 421.5 / IEC 60034-16.
Synchronizing and paralleling panel. Includes synchroscope, check synchronizer relay, voltage and frequency matching controls. IEC 60255 / IEEE C37.102.
Digital electro-hydraulic governor (DEH) for steam turbine speed and load control. Includes hydraulic actuation unit, servo valves, speed controller, and HMI. IEC 61511 / API 670.
Horizontal ball (tube) mill for coal pulverisation. Heavy-duty CS drum with forged steel balls. Includes primary air fan, classifier, and seal air system.
Vertical spindle roller mill (Raymond / MPS / Loesche type). More efficient than ball mill. Includes grinding rollers, classifier, journal housing, and rotating table.
Gravimetric (weigh-belt) or volumetric coal feeder for mill raw coal feed. Includes load cells, belt, drive, and local control panel. Gravimetric feeders add ~30% to cost.
Primary coal crusher (ring granulator or hammer mill). Wear-resistant Cr-iron liners. Includes screen, dust suppression connection, and lube system.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate * (current_index / base_value) * regional_factor * sub_region_factor. Schema contains mixed process/mechanical and electrical/control items. CEPCI used as single index for simplicity; electrical item escalation (excitation, governor, sync panel) may deviate slightly from CEPCI.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-POWER-BOILER.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs filled. Added mandatory steam flow / thermal duty sizing field (primary cost driver missing from skeleton). Corrected regional_cost_index from ENR_CCI to CEPCI (boilers are process/mechanical equipment). usd_cost_baseline nested inside cost_build_up. Based on ASME I, EN 12952, API 560, Nag (Boiler Technologist Handbook), Aspen ICARUS, Doosan/Babcock & Wilcox/Foster Wheeler published cost data (2024 USD).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-POWER-BOILER.spec_example: HRSG (Triple Pressure) | 250 t/h | 100 bar(g) / 540 deg C | Eff: 88%

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select boiler or HRSG type. HRSG (Single Pressure): unfired heat recovery steam generator with one steam drum, coupled to a gas turbine exhaust. HRSG (Triple Pressure): three pressure-level HRSG for combined-cycle power plants (HP/IP/LP drums). Steam Boiler (Pulverized Coal): large utility boiler burning finely ground coal, typically 100-600 MWth. Steam Boiler (CFBC): circulating fluidised bed combustion boiler — fuel-flexible, lower NOx, suitable for low-grade coal, biomass, and waste. Steam Boiler (Biomass): dedicated solid biomass (wood chips, bagasse, rice husk) water-tube boiler. Steam Boiler (HRSG) and Steam Boiler (Heat Recovery): packaged heat recovery boilers on process gas or engine exhaust streams. Sub-type drives the cost equation.

fields.label: Rated Steam Output
fields.hint: Rated continuous steam output (tonnes per hour) at MCR (Maximum Continuous Rating). This is the primary sizing parameter and dominant cost driver for all boiler and HRSG types. Typical ranges: Steam Boiler (Heat Recovery / packaged): 5-100 t/h; Steam Boiler (Biomass): 20-300 t/h; Steam Boiler (CFBC): 50-1000 t/h; Steam Boiler (Pulverized Coal): 200-2000 t/h; HRSG (Single Pressure): 30-300 t/h per GT; HRSG (Triple Pressure): 100-600 t/h per GT. Equipment cost scales approximately as (steam_flow)^0.65-0.75.

fields.label: Steam Drum Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure (MAWP) at the steam drum, per ASME I or EN 12952. For HRSG single pressure: typically 40-100 bar(g). For HRSG triple pressure: HP drum 100-130 bar(g); enter HP drum pressure here. For coal / biomass boilers: typically 60-130 bar(g). Pressure is a major cost driver — higher pressure requires thicker drum and header walls (SA-516 or SA-387 alloy steel), heavier steam piping (ASME B31.1), and higher-class valves. Supercritical boilers (>221 bar abs) are outside this template scope.

fields.label: Superheated Steam Temperature
fields.hint: Final superheated steam temperature leaving the superheater / reheater outlet. Must be at least 20 deg C superheat above saturation temperature at the design pressure (e.g. at 80 bar(g), saturation is ~296 deg C, so minimum SH outlet is ~316 deg C). Typical values: Industrial boilers / HRSG: 320-400 deg C; Sub-critical power plant: 480-540 deg C. Temperature above 480 deg C requires Cr-Mo alloy (P11, P22, P91) superheater tubes, significantly increasing cost. Above 565 deg C, austenitic stainless (TP304H, TP347H) or nickel alloy tubes are required.

fields.label: Boiler / HRSG Thermal Efficiency
fields.hint: Gross thermal efficiency on Lower Heating Value (LHV) basis. Used as a cost-tier discriminator — higher efficiency requires additional heat transfer surface area (economiser, air preheater stages), increasing cost. Indicative ranges: Packaged fire-tube or heat recovery boiler: 83-88%; Industrial water-tube (biomass / coal): 85-90%; HRSG single pressure: 75-85% (heat recovery from GT exhaust, limited by exhaust temperature); HRSG triple pressure with reheat: 85-92%; Pulverized coal utility boiler: 88-92%. Not applicable as a primary cost input; used only as a modifier.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical boiler or HRSG units. Combined-cycle plants typically have one HRSG per gas turbine. Industrial plants commonly use 2 x 50% or 1 x 100% boiler configurations. Multiple units share common flue gas ducting and stack in some configurations (cost those separately).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 150
compliance_rules.message: Steam drum design pressure exceeds 150 bar(g), approaching supercritical threshold (221 bar abs). Supercritical once-through boilers (Benson / Sulzer design) do not have a steam drum and require a fundamentally different cost model. This template covers subcritical drum-type boilers only. Consult OEM for supercritical pricing.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code Section I (Power Boilers); EN 12952 (Water-tube boilers); ASME B31.1 Power Piping

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature > 565
compliance_rules.message: Superheated steam temperature exceeds 565 deg C. Austenitic stainless (TP304H, TP347H) or nickel-based alloy superheater tubes are required. This is beyond the standard P91 alloy steel range and significantly increases both equipment cost and delivery lead time. Cost correlation uncertainty rises to +-35%. Consult OEM.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME Section I PG-6; ASTM A213 Grade TP347H; EN 10216-2; EPRI Advanced USC material specifications

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature < (0.0029 * design_pressure * design_pressure - 0.0093 * design_pressure + 180)
compliance_rules.message: Superheated steam temperature may be below or close to saturation temperature at the specified drum pressure. Verify that at least 20 deg C of superheat is maintained above the saturation temperature for the given design pressure (e.g., at 80 bar(g) saturation is ~296 deg C, minimum SH outlet ~316 deg C).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Steam saturation tables (IAPWS-IF97); ASME Section I PG-61 (minimum superheat requirements)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'HRSG (Single Pressure)' OR equipment_subtype == 'HRSG (Triple Pressure)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Steam Boiler (HRSG)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Steam Boiler (Heat Recovery)') AND design_pressure > 130
compliance_rules.message: HRSG drum pressure above 130 bar(g) is unusual for heat recovery applications and implies a very high gas turbine exhaust temperature. Verify the GT exhaust temperature is sufficient to generate steam at this pressure with adequate pinch-point margin (typically 8-15 deg C). High-pressure HRSGs have significantly longer delivery lead times.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NFPA 85 (Boiler and Combustion Systems Hazards Code); API 560 (Fired Heaters); ASME PTC 4.4 (Gas Turbine Heat Recovery Steam Generators); HRSG User Group guidelines

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Steam Boiler (Pulverized Coal)' AND steam_flow < 200
compliance_rules.message: Pulverized coal (PC) boilers below 200 t/h are rarely cost-effective compared to CFBC or stoker-fired alternatives. For capacities below 200 t/h, consider Steam Boiler (CFBC) or Steam Boiler (Biomass) sub-types which have lower minimum economical scale and better fuel flexibility.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEA Clean Coal Centre; EPRI Technical Report TR-107837; Babcock & Wilcox Steam: Its Generation and Use (41st Edition)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: efficiency < 78
compliance_rules.message: Boiler / HRSG thermal efficiency below 78% is unusually low. Verify the efficiency basis (LHV vs HHV) and whether flue gas heat recovery equipment (economiser, air preheater) is included in scope. An efficiency this low may indicate a bare waste-heat boiler on a low-temperature exhaust stream; if so, the cost correlation may overestimate equipment cost.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: EN 12952; ASME PTC 4 (Fired Steam Generators Performance Test Code); IS 13979 (Indian standard for boiler efficiency)

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: C_equip = usd_cost_baseline(equipment_subtype, steam_flow) * pressure_factor(design_pressure) * temperature_factor(design_temperature) * efficiency_factor(efficiency) * quantity * regional_index_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: C_installed = C_equip * (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation) * (1 + import_duty_pct) * (1 + contingency) * sub_region_factor
cost_build_up.scope_inclusions: Pressure parts (drum, headers, tubes, economiser, superheater, evaporator, reheater if applicable), insulation cladding, drum internals (cyclones, demisters), safety valves, boiler blowdown system, local instrument connections, factory hydraulic test per ASME I / EN 12952.
cost_build_up.scope_exclusions: Stack and flue gas ductwork, induced draft (ID) and forced draft (FD) fans, air preheater (APH), electrostatic precipitator (ESP) or baghouse, SCR / SNCR NOx control, feed water pumps, deaerator, chemical dosing system, steam drum level gauges and transmitters (if supplied separately), civil foundations, refractory and lagging of ducts, commissioning, fuel supply and handling systems.

adders.note: Erection / installation (0.35) is higher than most equipment categories because boilers require extensive field assembly (drum placement by crane, tube-by-tube field welding of water walls and superheater banks, refractory installation, hydrotest, steam blow). HRSG modules are shop-assembled and field-connected, with lower erection cost (~0.22). Coal/biomass boilers with tall structural steel frames and field-erection are at the high end (~0.40-0.45). Contingency raised to 0.12 (vs 0.10 standard) reflecting higher field scope uncertainty.

usd_cost_baseline.source: Doosan Heavy Industries, Babcock & Wilcox published HRSG / boiler cost data; Foster Wheeler capital cost correlations; Aspen ICARUS 2024; IEA World Energy Investment Report 2024; EPRI Technical Reports; Nag - Power Plant Engineering (5th Ed.); industry benchmarks (2024 USD)
usd_cost_baseline.note: Supply and erect (ex-works + field erection) cost for complete pressure-part assembly including superheater, evaporator, economiser, and drum. Does NOT include fans, stack, APH, ESP, feedwater system, or civil foundations. Apply regional index, adders, and import duty to obtain total installed cost.

items.notes: Unfired single-pressure HRSG with one HP drum, superheater, evaporator, and economiser module. Horizontal gas flow, modular design. Typical duty: gas turbine exhaust heat recovery at 450-560 deg C inlet gas temperature. Cost includes HRSG casing, bypass damper connection, and module shipping. GT exhaust duct and stack are separate.

Triple-pressure (HP/IP/LP) HRSG with optional reheat section for combined-cycle plant. More pressure levels, reheater, and condensate preheater add ~100% cost premium over single-pressure HRSG at same steam flow. Typically supplied as pre-fabricated modules for field assembly.

Pulverized coal (PC) utility boiler — tower or two-pass type — including water walls, superheater, reheater, economiser, and coal burner windboxes. Excludes pulverisers (mills), ESP, FD/ID fans, and SCR. Very field-intensive erection scope; structural steel frame included.

Circulating fluidised bed combustion (CFBC) boiler. Includes cyclone separators, loop seals, and refractory-lined furnace. Fuel-flexible: accepts coal, lignite, biomass, petcoke, and waste fuels. ~20-25% cost premium over equivalent PC boiler at same steam flow due to cyclones and refractory. Excludes limestone sorbent handling and bag filter.

Dedicated biomass (wood chips, agricultural residues, bagasse, rice husk) water-tube or hybrid boiler. Higher cost than coal at same capacity due to larger fuel handling, grate or spreader-stoker, and lower energy density of biomass fuel. Excludes fuel storage, conveying, and ash handling.

Packaged heat recovery steam generator on engine or process gas turbine exhaust. Simpler than utility HRSG: single pressure, no supplementary firing (unfired). Typically horizontal gas flow, natural circulation. Lower cost than HRSG (Single Pressure) due to smaller duty and lower pressure.

Process waste-heat boiler recovering heat from furnace flue gas, process reactor effluent, or kiln exhaust. Fire-tube or water-tube design depending on duty. Lower inlet gas temperature (250-450 deg C) than GT-HRSG, smaller physical footprint. Commonly used in refineries, cement, and chemical plants.

pressure_factor.note: Cost multiplier applied to account for elevated steam drum design pressure. Base = 1.0 at 60-80 bar(g) (typical industrial sub-critical).

factors.note: Low-pressure steam; thin-wall drum, simple design
Medium-pressure; standard SA-516 drum plate
Standard industrial subcritical — base cost
High pressure; heavier drum, alloy headers required
Very high pressure; SA-387 Cr-Mo drum, thick headers

factors.factor: Not in scope - once-through supercritical boiler required

temperature_factor.note: Cost multiplier for superheater tube alloy selection driven by steam temperature. Applied on top of pressure_factor.

factors.note: Saturated or low superheat; carbon steel (T11) tubes
Standard; T11 / T22 Cr-Mo alloy tubes
Elevated; T22 / T91 alloy required
High superheat; P91 / T91 alloy headers and tubes
Near-limit; austenitic SS (TP347H) required. Higher uncertainty.

efficiency_factor.note: Cost multiplier reflecting additional heat transfer surface for higher thermal efficiency. Applied as a minor modifier.

factors.note: Minimal heat recovery surface
Standard surface area — base cost
Enhanced economiser or additional superheater stages
Deep economiser, condensing air preheater; premium design

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate * (current_index / base_value) * regional_factor * sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. Boilers and HRSGs are classified as process / mechanical equipment — CEPCI is the appropriate index (not ENR CCI which is for civil construction). local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

SCHEMA-POWER-GENERATOR.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs filled with engineering judgment. Based on IEC 60034, IEEE C50.13, IEEE C50.12, BS EN 60034, CIGRE, and industry cost benchmarks (Aspen ICARUS, GE/Siemens/ABB generator price data 2024 USD). usd_cost_baseline nested inside cost_build_up.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-POWER-GENERATOR.spec_example: Generator (Turbo) | 50 MW | 11 kV | 3000 RPM | Fuel: N/A

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select generator type. Turbo generators are high-speed (1500-3600 RPM) cylindrical-rotor machines directly coupled to steam or gas turbines, typically 5-500 MW. Hydro generators are low-speed (60-500 RPM) salient-pole machines coupled to water turbines, typically 5-500 MW. Diesel generators are medium-speed (750-1500 RPM) salient-pole machines coupled to diesel or gas engines, typically 0.25-20 MW. Sub-type selection controls which fields are shown.

fields.label: Rated Output Power
fields.hint: Rated active power output (MW) at the generator terminals at rated power factor. This is the dominant cost driver. Typical ranges: Turbo generator: 5-500 MW; Hydro generator: 5-500 MW; Diesel generator: 0.25-20 MW. For budget estimates, power rating alone drives approximately 80% of the cost variation. Note: this is the generator MW rating, which should match the prime mover shaft output minus mechanical losses (typically 1-2%).

fields.label: Stator Terminal Voltage
fields.hint: Generator stator terminal (line-to-line) voltage. Common industrial values: 6.6 kV (smaller units up to 15 MW), 11 kV (10-100 MW standard), 13.8 kV (US practice, 10-100 MW), 15-22 kV (large turbo generators >100 MW). Higher voltage ratings reduce stator current and conductor cross-section but require enhanced winding insulation, slightly increasing cost. Voltage selection also determines the step-up transformer primary rating.

fields.label: Rotor Speed
fields.hint: Synchronous rotor speed in RPM. Standard values: 3000 RPM (2-pole, 50 Hz) or 3600 RPM (2-pole, 60 Hz) for turbo generators; 1500 RPM (4-pole, 50 Hz) or 1800 RPM (4-pole, 60 Hz) for medium-speed; 250-600 RPM for hydro salient-pole machines (dependent on number of poles). Speed has a major cost impact for large machines: 3000 RPM turbo generators require high-strength alloy steel rotors (cylindrical), whereas slow-speed hydro generators use bulky salient-pole rotors with larger diameter, increasing material cost at low MW/kg.

fields.label: Fuel Type
fields.hint: Fuel type for the prime mover driving the generator. Used for scope definition and auxiliary system costing — fuel type does not change the generator cost directly but determines fuel treatment auxiliaries. For Generator (Turbo): select Steam or Gas as appropriate. For Generator (Hydro): select Water. For Generator (Diesel): select Diesel, Natural Gas, or Dual-Fuel. Dual-fuel adds approximately 8-12% to the genset cost for the additional gas train and valve train.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical generator units. Each unit is priced individually. Power stations typically operate with 2 x 50% or 3 x 33% generator configurations. Hydro plants may have 4-10 identical units.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: system_voltage < 6.6 AND power > 5
compliance_rules.message: Stator voltage below 6.6 kV for a generator above 5 MW results in very high stator currents, requiring large conductor cross-sections. Verify voltage selection — 6.6 kV or 11 kV is standard for this power range. Low voltage at high power significantly increases busbar and cabling costs.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60034-1 (Rotating electrical machines - Rating and performance); IEEE C50.13 (Cylindrical-Rotor Synchronous Generators); NEMA MG-1

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Generator (Turbo)' AND speed != 3000 AND speed != 3600
compliance_rules.message: Turbo generators are normally manufactured at 3000 RPM (50 Hz) or 3600 RPM (60 Hz) for direct grid coupling. Non-standard speed requires a gearbox or special multi-pole winding. Verify this is intentional and add gearbox cost (8-15% of generator cost) to the estimate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60034-1; IEEE C50.13; BS EN 60034-3 (specific requirements for synchronous generators)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Generator (Hydro)' AND speed > 600
compliance_rules.message: Hydro generators above 600 RPM are unusual except for very small units. Verify the runner type (Pelton / Francis / Kaplan) and head conditions. High-head Pelton turbines may run faster, but most hydro generators are in the 60-500 RPM range. High speed may indicate a micro-hydro application.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60034-1; IEEE C50.12 (Salient-Pole 50 Hz and 60 Hz Synchronous Generators); IEC 61116 (Small hydro installations)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Generator (Diesel)' AND power > 20
compliance_rules.message: Diesel generators above 20 MW are uncommon as single units. Above this threshold, a gas turbine generator set is typically more cost-effective. Consider splitting into multiple units or re-classifying the prime mover as Gas Turbine.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Wartsila / MAN Energy Solutions / Caterpillar generator set product range data; IEEE 1115 (Sizing Lead-Acid Batteries for Stationary Standby Applications)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: system_voltage > 15 AND power < 30
compliance_rules.message: Stator voltage above 15 kV for a generator below 30 MW is unusual. This voltage class is typically used for machines above 100 MW. Verify the voltage level — a step-up transformer is the normal solution for smaller machines feeding an HV grid.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60034-1; IEEE C50.13; standard OEM voltage class offerings (GE, Siemens, ABB)

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: C_equip = usd_cost_baseline(equipment_subtype, power) * speed_factor(speed) * quantity * regional_index_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: C_installed = C_equip * (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation) * (1 + import_duty_pct) * (1 + contingency) * sub_region_factor
cost_build_up.scope_inclusions: Generator stator and rotor, excitation system (static or brushless AVR), stator terminal box, bearing pedestals, hydrogen or air cooling system, local control panel, vibration monitoring, and factory acceptance test (FAT) / type test per IEC 60034.
cost_build_up.scope_exclusions: Step-up (unit) transformer, generator circuit breaker (GCB), isolated phase busduct (IPB), protection relays and panels, switchgear, civil foundation and anchor bolts, cooling water supply system, seal oil system (for hydrogen-cooled machines, if separate), commissioning, and first fill of hydrogen or cooling gas.

adders.note: Erection / installation (0.20) covers generator stator lowering into pit, rotor insertion, bearing alignment, coupling to prime mover shaft, terminal connections, and pre-commissioning checks. Hydro generators have higher civil complexity but lower mechanical erection risk vs turbo generators. Large turbo generators (>100 MW) may reach 0.28 due to precision alignment and hydrogen filling.

usd_cost_baseline.source: GE Power, Siemens Energy, ABB generator price lists and published project data; Aspen ICARUS 2024; IEA World Energy Investment Report 2024; IRENA Renewable Power Generation Costs 2023 (hydro); industry benchmarks (2024 USD)
usd_cost_baseline.note: Supply-only (ex-works) generator cost including excitation system, AVR, terminal box, and factory test. Does NOT include step-up transformer, GCB, IPB, protection panels, civil works, or grid connection. Apply regional index, adders, and import duty to obtain installed cost.

items.notes: Air-cooled (up to ~50 MW) or hydrogen-cooled (>50 MW) cylindrical-rotor synchronous generator directly coupled to steam or gas turbine at 3000 or 3600 RPM. IEC 60034 / IEEE C50.13. Cost exponent 0.60 reflects strong economies of scale. Hydrogen cooling system adds approximately 8% for units above 50 MW.

Salient-pole synchronous generator for hydro duty per IEC 60034 / IEEE C50.12. Larger diameter and slower speed result in higher cost per MW vs turbo generators. Cost includes umbrella or suspended frame, thrust bearing, and guide bearings. Stator transported in segments for large machines. Runner and turbine are separate line items.

Open-drip-proof (ODP) or totally enclosed fan-cooled (TEFC) salient-pole synchronous generator coupled to diesel or gas engine. Typically supplied as part of a packaged genset skid. Cost shown is generator only; for full genset (including engine) use SCHEMA-POWER-TURBINE Diesel Engine or Gas Engine items. Low-voltage (400 V) packaged diesel gensets below 1 MW are considerably cheaper per kW.

speed_factor.note: Cost adjustment for non-standard rotor speeds. Applied to Tier 2 formula. Base factor = 1.0 at standard synchronous speed (3000 or 3600 RPM for turbo; rated speed for hydro and diesel at given pole count).

factors.note: Standard turbo generator speed — base cost
4-pole winding adds minor cost premium
Multi-pole salient-pole rotor — larger frame diameter
Very low speed — large diameter rotor, segment transport required
Extreme low speed — special manufacture, high material cost per MW

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate * (current_index / base_value) * regional_factor * sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: Switched ENR->CEPCI: procured/manufactured equipment, supply-cost dominated. verify=true

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
