SCHEMA-POWER-TURBINE.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs filled with engineering judgment. Based on IEC 60034, ASME PTC 22, API 616, NEMA MG-1, Garrett & Ruhle cost correlations, and industry benchmarks (Gas Turbine World Handbook, Diesel & Gas Turbine Worldwide).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-POWER-TURBINE.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | {power} MW | {design_pressure} bar(g) / {design_temperature} deg C | {speed} RPM | n={efficiency}% | Fuel: {fuel_type}
SCHEMA-POWER-TURBINE.spec_example: Steam Turbine (Condensing) | 50 MW | 100 bar(g) / 540 deg C | 3000 RPM | n=40% | Fuel: N/A

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the prime mover type. Steam turbines are driven by high-pressure steam and are common in power plants, refineries, and cogeneration. Gas turbines burn natural gas directly and suit peaking or combined-cycle duty. Gas and diesel engines are reciprocating prime movers used for standby, island-mode, or distributed generation. Sub-type selection controls which fields (inlet pressure, temperature, fuel type) are shown.

fields.label: Rated Output Power
fields.hint: Rated gross electrical output at generator terminals (ISO conditions for gas turbines; VWO / MCR for steam turbines). This is the single strongest cost driver. Typical ranges: Gas Engine / Diesel Engine: 0.5-10 MW; Gas Turbine (industrial): 5-150 MW; Steam Turbine (industrial): 1-150 MW. Diesel and gas engines above 20 MW are unusual; use Gas Turbine sub-type instead.

fields.label: Inlet Steam Pressure
fields.hint: Turbine inlet (throttle) steam pressure. Applicable to steam turbines only. Industrial range: 30-110 bar(g). Sub-critical power plant: 60-100 bar(g). Does not apply to gas turbines or reciprocating engines.

fields.label: Inlet Temperature
fields.hint: For steam turbines: superheated steam inlet temperature. Typical sub-critical plant: 480-540 deg C. For gas turbines: turbine inlet temperature (TIT) — typically 950-1300 deg C. Not applicable to diesel or gas engines.

fields.label: Shaft Speed
fields.hint: Synchronous speed of the turbine shaft or engine crankshaft. For directly coupled generators: 3000 RPM (50 Hz grid) or 3600 RPM (60 Hz grid). Aeroderivative gas turbines: 6000-20000 RPM with reduction gearbox. Reciprocating engines: 750-1500 RPM.

fields.label: Thermal / Electrical Efficiency
fields.hint: Net electrical efficiency at ISO / rated conditions. Used as a cost-tier discriminator. Indicative ranges: Steam turbine condensing: 30-42%; Steam turbine back-pressure: 20-35%; Gas turbine heavy-frame simple cycle: 30-40%; Gas turbine aeroderivative: 38-45%; Gas engine: 38-46%; Diesel engine: 40-48%.

fields.label: Fuel Type
fields.hint: Primary fuel for combustion-based prime movers. Applies to Gas Turbine, Gas Engine, and Diesel Engine sub-types only. Steam turbines are steam-driven — not applicable. Dual-fuel engines add ~8-12% to cost. HFO requires additional treatment systems increasing auxiliary cost.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical prime mover units. Power plants commonly use 2 x 50% or 3 x 33% configurations for redundancy. Each unit is priced individually; no bulk discount is applied at this level.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 100 AND (equipment_subtype == 'Steam Turbine (Condensing)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Steam Turbine (Back Pressure)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Steam Turbine (Extraction)')
compliance_rules.message: Inlet steam pressure exceeds 100 bar(g). Approaching supercritical conditions (>220 bar abs). Verify turbine and boiler ratings. Supercritical steam plant is outside the standard cost correlation range — consult specialist estimator.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME PTC 6 (Steam Turbines); IEC 60045-1; ASME B31.1 Power Piping

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature > 565 AND (equipment_subtype == 'Steam Turbine (Condensing)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Steam Turbine (Back Pressure)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Steam Turbine (Extraction)')
compliance_rules.message: Steam inlet temperature exceeds 565 deg C. Advanced (A-USC) materials required for blades and casings. Cost uncertainty rises to +-35%. Consult turbine OEM.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME PTC 6; EPRI Advanced Ultra-Supercritical Program; VGB-Standard 450L

compliance_rules.condition_expr: speed != 3000 AND speed != 3600
compliance_rules.message: Shaft speed is not at a standard synchronous grid frequency (3000 RPM / 50 Hz or 3600 RPM / 60 Hz). A reduction gearbox or VFD will be required. Add gearbox cost (approx. 8-15% of turbine cost) to the estimate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60034-1; NEMA MG-1; API 613 (Special Purpose Gears)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Diesel Engine' OR equipment_subtype == 'Gas Engine') AND power > 20
compliance_rules.message: Reciprocating engines above 20 MW are uncommon as single units. Consider multiple smaller units, or re-classify as Gas Turbine. Above 20 MW, gas turbine packages are typically more cost-effective.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Wartsila / MAN Energy Solutions product range data; Gas Turbine World Handbook

compliance_rules.condition_expr: efficiency > 48 AND (equipment_subtype == 'Diesel Engine' OR equipment_subtype == 'Gas Engine')
compliance_rules.message: Claimed efficiency exceeds 48% for a reciprocating engine. Verify the value — use 40-46% for budget estimates. Best-in-class large gas engines achieve up to 50% only at specific load points.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Wartsila engine data; MAN Energy Solutions 51/60G data sheet; ISO 3046-1

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Gas Turbine' AND design_temperature < 900
compliance_rules.message: Gas turbine inlet temperature (TIT) below 900 deg C is low for modern units. Typical TIT: 950-1300 deg C. Verify this is not exhaust gas temperature (EGT) or compressor discharge temperature.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 616; GE, Siemens, MHI gas turbine data sheets; ASME PTC 22

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: C_equip = usd_cost_baseline(equipment_subtype, power) * efficiency_premium_factor(efficiency) * pressure_temperature_premium_factor(design_pressure, design_temperature) * quantity * regional_index_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: C_installed = C_equip * (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation) * (1 + import_duty_pct) * (1 + contingency) * sub_region_factor
cost_build_up.scope_inclusions: Packaged unit on baseplate, integrated generator, AVR, local control panel, lube oil / seal oil system, inlet filtration, exhaust silencer, factory acceptance test (FAT).
cost_build_up.scope_exclusions: Step-up transformer, MV/HV switchgear, protection relays, generator circuit breaker, civil foundation, cooling water system, fuel gas compression / conditioning, steam condenser (condensing turbines), HRSG, grid connection, commissioning, initial fuel fill.

adders.note: Erection / installation (0.25) covers mechanical rigging, alignment, piping tie-ins, electrical terminations, and commissioning support. Isolated or offshore sites may reach 0.40.

usd_cost_baseline.source: Gas Turbine World Handbook, Turbo Machinery International, Garrett & Ruhle capital cost correlations, IEA World Energy Investment Reports, Aspen ICARUS (2024 USD)
usd_cost_baseline.note: Supply-only (ex-works) packaged unit cost. Does NOT include step-up transformer, switchgear, civil foundations, or grid connection.

items.notes: Condensing multi-stage axial turbine. Includes SSS clutch and governing valve. Condenser and cooling tower are separate line items. Price valid for 60-100 bar(g) / 480-540 deg C.
Back-pressure (non-condensing) turbine. Exhausts to process steam header. Cost is 15-20% lower than equivalent condensing machine.
Extraction/induction turbine with controlled extraction ports. More complex valve and casing design adds ~12% premium over standard condensing turbine.
Industrial heavy-frame gas turbine package (API 616). Includes inlet filter, exhaust diffuser, DLE combustors. Aeroderivative units 15-20% more expensive. Excludes HRSG.
Spark-ignited 4-stroke natural gas engine genset on skid. Includes radiator, gas train, control panel, acoustic enclosure. Dual-fuel option adds ~10%.
Compression-ignition 4-stroke diesel genset on skid. Includes day tank, radiator, fuel injection system, acoustic enclosure. HFO-capable engines add ~20% for treatment system.

efficiency_premium_factors.note: Additional cost multiplier for high-efficiency machines. Applied on top of base cost equation.

factors.note: Below-average — older design or simple configuration
Standard industrial — base cost
Above-average — advanced combustion / blade design
High efficiency — aeroderivative GT or premium engine
Best-in-class. High estimating uncertainty.

pressure_temperature_premium_factors.note: Steam turbine cost premium based on inlet conditions. Applied to steam turbine sub-types only.

factors.note: Advanced alloys required. Confirm with OEM.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate * (current_index / base_value) * regional_factor * sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: Switched ENR->CEPCI: procured/manufactured equipment, supply-cost dominated. verify=true

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-PROC-HX.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs filled with engineering judgment. Based on TEMA, API 660/661, ASME VIII, Townsend & Mulet cost correlations.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-PROC-HX.spec_example: Shell & Tube Heat Exchanger (Process) | 120 m² | SS316L | 15 bar(g) / 250 °C | API 660

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific heat exchanger type. Shell & Tube is the most common process type. Air Cooled (ACHE) is used where cooling water is scarce. HRSG is used for waste heat recovery from gas turbines or fired heaters. Selection drives sizing basis and cost model.

fields.label: Fan Motor Power
fields.hint: Total installed fan motor power for Air Cooled Heat Exchangers (ACHE) only. Typical range: 15–800 kW per bay. For HRSG forced draft fans: 800–3500 kW. Not applicable to shell & tube or plate exchangers. Drives cost of motor, drive, and fan bundle.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - Quick Estimate (always visible when applicable)

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure (MAWP) per ASME VIII / PED. Use shell-side design pressure for shell & tube; maximum circuit pressure for plate HX. Typical process range: 8–25 bar(g). High-pressure S&T: up to 55 bar(g). Directly affects shell wall thickness, flange rating (ASME B16.5 Class), and cost.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum design temperature per ASME VIII / PED. Use highest of shell-side or tube-side design temperature. Typical process HX: 150–350 °C. High-temperature HRSG duty: up to 550 °C. Temperature determines allowable stress values, gasket selection, and MOC eligibility. CS is limited to ~425 °C; above this use Cr-Mo alloys.

fields.label: Shell Volume
fields.hint: Gross shell-side volume (including heads). Used for kettle reboilers and HRSG drums to assess inventory, hold-up, and pressure vessel sizing. Typically 100–2000 L for kettle reboilers. Not applicable to plate HX or ACHE. Used as a secondary cost driver alongside heat transfer area.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - Quick Estimate (always visible when applicable)

fields.label: Heat Transfer Area
fields.hint: Total effective heat transfer area (tube OD basis for S&T; plate pack area for PHE; face area × rows for ACHE). This is the primary sizing parameter and dominant cost driver. Typical ranges: S&T process 20–500 m², high-pressure S&T 10–200 m², kettle reboiler 20–300 m², PHE 5–200 m², ACHE 200–2500 m², HRSG 500–5000 m².

fields.label: MOC (Material of Construction)
fields.hint: Material of construction for pressure-wetted parts (shell, tubes, tubesheets). CS (carbon steel A516 Gr.70 / A106) is standard for non-corrosive hydrocarbon service. SS316L for mildly corrosive / chloride service. Duplex for high chloride / sour gas. Titanium for seawater coolers. MOC is a major cost multiplier: SS316L ≈ 2.0–2.5× CS; Titanium ≈ 4–6× CS.

fields.label: Design Standard
fields.hint: Governing design and fabrication standard. API 660 / TEMA R: refinery and heavy process shell & tube (most stringent, highest cost). API 661: air-cooled exchangers. ASME VIII Div.1: general pressure vessel code. ASME VIII Div.2 for high-pressure (>200 bar or fatigue cases). ASME I / EN 12952 for HRSG boiler drums. Standard selection affects NDT requirements, documentation, and fabrication cost (API 660 TEMA-R ≈ 15–25% premium over TEMA-C).

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical heat exchanger units. For parallel trains, enter total units. Note: bulk discounts typically apply beyond 3 identical units.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 40
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds 40 bar(g). Verify ASME VIII Div.2 or special design is selected, and confirm flange rating ≥ Class 600 (ASME B16.5). High-pressure S&T sub-type is recommended.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME VIII Div.1 UG-28; ASME B16.5 pressure-temperature ratings; API 660 §6.1.3

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature > 425 AND moc == 'CS'
compliance_rules.message: CS is not recommended above 425 °C due to graphitization risk. Consider Cr-Mo alloy (1.25Cr-0.5Mo or 2.25Cr-1Mo) or austenitic stainless steel.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME VIII Div.1 UCS-66; API 941 Nelson curves (H2 service); ASTM A387

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Air Cooled Heat Exchanger (API 661)' AND standard != 'API 661'
compliance_rules.message: Air Cooled Heat Exchangers must be designed to API 661. Please update the Design Standard field.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API Standard 661, 7th Edition: Air-cooled Heat Exchangers for General Refinery Service

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Heat Recovery Steam Generator (HRSG)' AND standard != 'ASME I (boiler / HRSG)'
compliance_rules.message: HRSGs generating steam are typically governed by ASME Section I (Power Boilers) or EN 12952 for pressure parts. Verify governing code with the project authority.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME Boiler & Pressure Vessel Code Section I; NFPA 85; API 560

compliance_rules.condition_expr: area > 1000 AND equipment_subtype == 'Plate Heat Exchanger (Process)'
compliance_rules.message: Heat transfer area > 1000 m² for a plate HX is unusually large. Verify this is not a total area across multiple units. PHE single-frame limit is typically 200–500 m².
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: TEMA; Alfa Laval / GEA plate HX design guidelines

usd_cost_baseline.source: Townsend & Mulet (2013), Matches Process Equipment Cost Estimator, Aspen ICARUS, industry benchmarks
usd_cost_baseline.note: FOB fabricated cost, ex-works, carbon steel CS base. Apply MOC factor, pressure factor, regional index, and adders to get installed cost.

items.notes: TEMA Class R, single pass, CS/CS, 150# flange. Exponent 0.65 per six-tenths rule.
Design pressure >40 bar(g), Class 300–600 flanges, ASME VIII Div.1 or Div.2. ~1.8× standard S&T cost.
Kettle-type (TEMA K shell). Includes oversize shell for vapour disengagement. ~1.4× standard S&T.
Gasketed plate-and-frame, SS316L plates standard (plates are wetted parts). CS frame.
API 661, forced draft, 2-bay typical, CS structure, Al-fin tubes. Motor power adds ~$800/kW installed.
Unfired package HRSG, single pressure, CS/CS, includes drum, headers, casing. Multi-pressure or supplementary firing adds 40–80%.

moc_factors.note: Multiply FOB equipment cost by MOC factor. Factor applies to wetted pressure parts only.

pressure_factors.note: Additional cost multiplier applied on top of MOC factor for elevated design pressure.

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: C_equip = usd_cost_baseline(equipment_subtype, area) × moc_factor(moc) × quantity × regional_index_factor
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: C_equip = usd_cost_baseline(equipment_subtype, area) × moc_factor(moc) × pressure_factor(design_pressure) × standard_premium(standard) × quantity × regional_index_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: C_installed = C_equip × (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation) × (1 + import_duty_pct) × (1 + contingency) × sub_region_factor

standard_premium.note: Additional fabrication cost premium driven by documentation, inspection, and certification requirements.

adders.note: Erection / installation includes mechanical completion, hydro-testing, insulation (cold service), and painting. HRSG installation is higher (~0.30) due to structural steel and steam piping connections.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-PROC-PIPING.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs resolved. template_name corrected from 'Process & Petrochemical' to 'Piping, Fittings & Valves'. Defects corrected: (1) F-002 Volume (L) min=max=316 L completely wrong for piping — replaced with Nominal Bore (NB) dropdown — the primary cost driver for ALL piping items; (2) F-003 Design Pressure locked 25 bar — widened 1–700 bar(g); (3) F-004 Design Temperature locked 50°C — widened -200 to 600°C; (4) F-005 Standard — defaults and conditional display added per sub-type group; (5) F-006 MOC single SS316L — expanded to 10 options, conditional defaults per sub-type. New fields: pipe schedule/wall thickness, pipe length (m) for linear items, end connection/joint type, valve trim/actuator, piping class. Engineering basis: ASME B31.3 (process piping), ASME B16.5 (flanges), ASME B16.9 (fittings), ASME B36.10/19 (pipe dimensions), ASTM A53/A106/A234/A312/A403/A105, API 600/602, IS 1239, IS 3589.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick ±30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget ±20%. Tier 3 = Detailed ±10%.

SCHEMA-PROC-PIPING.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | NB {nominal_bore_mm}mm | {pipe_schedule} | {design_pressure} bar(g) / {design_temperature}°C | MOC: {moc} | L={pipe_length_m}m | {end_connection} | Qty: {quantity} No.
SCHEMA-PROC-PIPING.spec_example: Carbon Steel Pipe (Sch 40) | NB 50mm (2") | Sch 40 | 16 bar(g) / 200°C | MOC: CS A106 Gr.B | L=100m | BW | Qty: 1 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select piping component type. Pipes: measured per metre; schedule encodes wall thickness. Fittings (BW = butt weld): elbows, tees, reducers per ASME B16.9. Flanges: weld neck and blind per ASME B16.5; class = pressure rating. Valves: gate valves per API 600/602; class = pressure class.

fields.label: Nominal Bore (NB) / Pipe Size
fields.hint: Nominal pipe size per ASME B36.10 / ASME B36.19 (SS). Pipe cost (USD/m) and fitting/flange cost (USD/No.) both scale approximately with NB^1.5 for a given schedule/class. Small bore (≤50 mm NB): socket weld or threaded common. Large bore (>50 mm NB): butt weld standard for process piping. NB is the primary Tier 1 cost driver — specify correctly before estimating.

fields.label: Nominal bore (mm, numeric)
fields.hint: Numeric value derived from 'nominal_bore_mm' for size/rating threshold compliance rules.

fields.label: Pipe Schedule / Wall Thickness
options: Sch 80 / XH (extra heavy — elevated P or reduced bore)
Not applicable (fittings / flanges / valves — size follows NB)
fields.hint: Wall thickness schedule per ASME B36.10 (CS) or B36.19 (SS). Schedule determines burst pressure and material weight (kg/m). CS process piping ASME B31.3: Sch 40 standard for up to ~40 bar; Sch 80 for elevated pressure or corrosive service. LPG / sour service: seamless mandatory (no ERW weld seam) per IS 3589 / API 5L. SS pipes: Sch 40S ≠ Sch 40 — verify per ASME B36.19. For fittings, flanges, and valves select 'Not applicable'.

fields.label: Pipe Length
fields.hint: Total installed length of pipe for this BOQ line item. Standard pipe supply lengths: 6 m (random length) or 12 m (double random). Pipe cost = USD/m × length. Erection includes cutting, fit-up, welding, NDE, and hydrotesting. For fittings, flanges, and valves: enter 0 or leave blank — those items are priced per piece (No.) in the Quantity field.

fields.label: Design Pressure (MAWP)
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure at design temperature. ASME B16.5 flange pressure ratings at 38°C: Class 150 = 19.6 bar; Class 300 = 51.1 bar; Class 600 = 102.1 bar; Class 900 = 153.2 bar; Class 1500 = 255.3 bar; Class 2500 = 425.6 bar. For pipes: wall thickness determines MAWP via ASME B31.3 Cl.304.1.2 t = P×D / (2(S×E×W + P×Y)). LPG service: per SMPV Rules / IS 3589 for seamless pipes.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum fluid temperature in the pipe at design conditions. Ambient water/utility service: 50–80°C. Process steam / hot oil: 150–400°C. LPG: -20°C to +50°C (refrigerated to ambient). LTCS required for service below -29°C (ASME B31.3 Cl.323.2). Above 450°C: Cr-Mo alloy pipe required. Temperature governs allowable stress and flange pressure rating per ASME B16.5 Table 2.

fields.label: End Connection / Joint Type
options: BW (Butt Weld) — process piping >50 mm NB, ASME B16.25
SW (Socket Weld) — small bore ≤50 mm NB, ASME B16.11
Threaded (NPT/BSP) — instrument / utility / small bore only
Plain End / PE (pipe, no end prep)
Bevelled End (BE, pipe, field weld prep)
fields.default: BW (Butt Weld) — process piping >50 mm NB, ASME B16.25
fields.hint: Connection type at pipe or fitting ends. BW (butt weld): mandatory for process piping >50 mm NB per ASME B31.3 — radiographic examination (RT) required per piping class. SW (socket weld): restricted to ≤50 mm NB (Class 3000/6000 rating); avoid in services with crevice corrosion risk (e.g. Cl⁻ solutions). Threaded: utility and instrument connections only — not for hazardous / flammable service per ASME B31.3 Cl.314. RTJ (ring-type joint) flanges: used for Class 600 and above.

fields.label: MOC (Pipe / Component Material)
options: CS ASTM A106 Gr.B (seamless — process / LPG)
CS ASTM A53 Gr.B (ERW — utility / non-critical)
LTCS ASTM A333 Gr.6 (low temperature, down to -46°C)
SS304 ASTM A312 TP304 (general corrosive / food grade)
SS316L ASTM A312 TP316L (chloride / pharma / corrosive)
SS321 ASTM A312 TP321 (high temp, sensitisation resistant)
2.25Cr-1Mo ASTM A335 P22 (HP boiler feed / HP steam >350°C)
Duplex SS ASTM A790 (seawater / chloride offshore)
CS Flanges: ASTM A105 (forgings, ambient / moderate temp)
CS Fittings: ASTM A234 WPB (BW fittings, CS)
SS Fittings: ASTM A403 WP316L (BW fittings, SS316L)
CS Valve Body: ASTM A216 WCB (cast steel gate/globe)
SS Valve Body: ASTM A351 CF8M (cast SS316 gate/globe)
fields.default: CS ASTM A106 Gr.B (seamless — process / LPG)
fields.hint: Material specification for pipe, fitting, flange, or valve body. CS A106 Gr.B: standard seamless CS pipe for process service (ASME B31.3). CS A53 Gr.B: ERW pipe acceptable for utility/instrument air — NOT for flammable or toxic service. LTCS A333 Gr.6: for cold service down to -46°C. SS316L: chloride-containing service, pharma, food. P11/P22: elevated temperature steam and hydrocarbon service. NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156 hardness limits apply for H₂S service.

fields.label: Design / Material Standard
options: ASME B31.3 (Process Piping — primary piping design code)
ASME B36.10 (Pipe dimensions — CS, welded & seamless)
ASME B16.5 (Pipe flanges and flanged fittings — Class 150–2500)
ASME B16.11 (Forged fittings — socket weld & threaded)
ASTM A53 (CS ERW / seamless pipe — utility)
ASTM A106 (CS seamless pipe — process / high-temp)
ASTM A105 (CS forged flanges and fittings)
IS 3589 (Steel pipes for water and sewage — India)
fields.default: ASME B31.3 (Process Piping — primary piping design code)
fields.hint: Governing standard for design, fabrication, and material. ASME B31.3 is the mandatory code for process plant piping in petrochemical, oil and gas, and chemical plants globally. ASME B36.10/19 defines pipe wall thicknesses by schedule. ASME B16.5 defines flange dimensions and pressure-temperature ratings. API 600: gate valves for petroleum service, bolted bonnet. IS 1239 / IS 3589: Indian standards for utility / water service. Multiple standards typically apply to one piping line — select the primary governing code.

fields.label: Piping / Material Class
options: Class A1 — CS, Class 150, ambient/low pressure (utility)
Class A2 — CS, Class 300, process service
Class B1 — CS, Class 600, high pressure process
Class C1 — SS316L, Class 150, corrosive / pharma
Class C2 — SS316L, Class 300, HP corrosive
Class D1 — LTCS, Class 300, cold service
Class E1 — Cr-Mo P11, Class 600, high-temp steam
Class E2 — Cr-Mo P22, Class 900, HP high-temp
Class LPG — CS seamless, Class 300, LPG / SMPV rules
fields.default: Class A2 — CS, Class 300, process service
fields.hint: Project piping material class — typically defined in the Piping Material Specification (PMS) issued with the PFD/P&ID. Piping class consolidates NB, schedule, MOC, flange class, fitting standard, valve type, and test requirements into a single designator. Using a piping class reference ensures consistency across the BOQ. For BOQ purposes, the class must be consistent with the NB, schedule, MOC, and design pressure/temperature specified in other fields.

fields.label: Valve Operator / Actuator
options: Gear operator (manual — large bore >150 mm NB)
fields.hint: Valve operating mechanism. Handwheel: standard for manually operated process valves up to ~150 mm NB. Gear operator: required for valves >150 mm NB (too heavy to turn by hand). Pneumatic actuator (spring-return): used for on/off automated valves — spring-return provides fail-safe position on loss of air. MOV (motor operated): used where remote/automatic operation required without compressed air. Actuator adds 50–200% to base valve cost depending on type and size.

fields.label: Valve Trim Material
options: Trim 8 — 13Cr stem / SS seat (API 600 standard for CS valve)
Trim 16 — SS316 stem + SS316 seat (corrosive service)
fields.default: Trim 8 — 13Cr stem / SS seat (API 600 standard for CS valve)
fields.hint: Valve internal trim (stem, seat, disc/gate) material per API 600 trim numbers. Trim 8: standard for CS body valves in hydrocarbon service. Trim 12 (Stellite): hard-facing on seats for erosive service (e.g. throttling valves, high-velocity slurry). NACE MR0175: H₂S service requires hardness-controlled trim (max HRC 22 for wetted parts). Trim selection adds 10–40% to base valve cost.

fields.label: Flange Face Type
options: RTJ (Ring-Type Joint) — Class 600 and above, high-integrity seal
FF (Full Face) — cast iron equipment connections only
LF (Large Male/Female) — heat exchanger / equipment connections
fields.hint: Flange face finish per ASME B16.5. RF (raised face): standard for all Class 150–600 piping flanges — used with spiral wound or ring gaskets. RTJ (ring-type joint): mandatory for Class 900 and above; recommended for Class 600 in high-temperature / high-pressure service. RTJ machining adds ~20% to flange cost. RF surface finish: 125–250 AARH for spiral wound gaskets; 63 AARH maximum for metal ring gaskets.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: For PIPES: enter 1 — length is captured in the Pipe Length (m) field. For FITTINGS, FLANGES, VALVES: enter actual count of items. Example: 12 elbows in a pipe loop → Quantity = 12.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'LPG Pipe (Seamless)' AND pipe_schedule AND 'ERW' IN pipe_schedule
compliance_rules.message: ERW (Electric Resistance Welded) pipe is NOT permitted for LPG service. IS 3589 and SMPV Rules 2016 mandate seamless pipe for LPG systems to eliminate weld seam failure risk under cyclic pressure and low-temperature conditions. Specify ASTM A106 Gr.B or API 5L seamless.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 3589; SMPV Rules 2016; ASME B31.3 Cl.305.1.1 (fluid service categories)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 19.6 AND equipment_subtype AND 'Class 150' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds Class 150 rating (19.6 bar at 38°C). ASME B16.5 Class 150 flanges are rated 19.6 bar(g) at 38°C; rating reduces further at elevated temperature. Upgrade to Class 300 (51.1 bar) or higher as appropriate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.5 Table 2-1.1 (pressure-temperature ratings); ASME B31.3

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 51.1 AND equipment_subtype AND 'Class 300' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds Class 300 rating (51.1 bar at 38°C). Upgrade to Class 600 (102.1 bar) or Class 900 (153.2 bar). Verify exact P-T rating from ASME B16.5 Table 2 for the specific flange material at the actual design temperature.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.5 Table 2-1.1; ASME B31.3 Cl.302

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature < -29 AND moc AND 'A106' IN moc AND !'LTCS' IN moc
compliance_rules.message: CS ASTM A106 is not suitable below -29°C. ASME B31.3 Table 323.2.2 requires impact testing or use of specific low-temperature materials below -29°C. For service down to -46°C specify LTCS ASTM A333 Gr.6 / A420 WPL6. For below -46°C: 3.5% Ni, 9% Ni, or austenitic SS required.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Table 323.2.2; ASTM A333 (low-temp pipe); A420 (fittings)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature > 450 AND moc AND 'A106' IN moc AND !'Cr-Mo' IN moc AND !'P11' IN moc AND !'P22' IN moc
compliance_rules.message: CS ASTM A106 design temperature above 450°C: allowable stress reduces sharply and graphitisation risk increases. ASME B31.1/B31.3 limit CS to 427°C (800°F) maximum. Specify 1.25Cr-0.5Mo (P11) for up to 540°C, or 2.25Cr-1Mo (P22) for up to 620°C. Verify with ASME Sec. II Part D allowable stress tables.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Cl.323.2.1; ASME Sec. II Part D Table 1A; API 530

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'SW' IN end_connection AND nb_mm > 50
compliance_rules.message: Socket weld (SW) connection specified for pipe size >50 mm NB. ASME B16.11 socket weld fittings are available up to 50 mm NB (2"). For larger sizes, use butt weld (BW) connections per ASME B16.9.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.11 Cl.1.1 (scope); ASME B31.3 Cl.314.2

compliance_rules.condition_expr: end_connection AND 'Threaded' IN end_connection AND design_pressure > 14
compliance_rules.message: Threaded connections at design pressure above 14 bar: ASME B31.3 Cl.314 restricts threaded connections in Category M (toxic) service and cautions use above 14 bar / 315°C. For hazardous or high-pressure service, specify socket weld or butt weld.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Cl.314; ASME B16.11

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc AND 'A53' IN moc AND equipment_subtype AND ('LPG' IN equipment_subtype OR 'High Pressure' IN equipment_subtype)
compliance_rules.message: ASTM A53 ERW pipe specified for LPG or high-pressure service. A53 ERW (Grade B) is a utility-grade pipe and is not permitted for flammable gas, LPG, or high-pressure process piping. Specify ASTM A106 Gr.B seamless for these services.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Cl.305.1.1 Table A; ASTM A53 vs A106 comparison

compliance_rules.condition_expr: nb_mm >= 600 AND 'Flange' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: Flange NB ≥600 mm (24"): large-bore flanges above 600 mm NB fall under ASME B16.47 (large diameter pipe flanges), not B16.5. Confirm whether Series A (MSS SP-44) or Series B (API 605) applies. Large-bore flanges require special gaskets and are long lead items.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.47 (pipe flanges >600 mm NB); MSS SP-44; API 605

compliance_rules.condition_expr: valve_actuator AND !'Handwheel' IN valve_actuator AND !'Gear' IN valve_actuator
compliance_rules.message: Automated valve actuator specified: confirm compressed air or electrical supply is available at valve location. Pneumatic actuators require 5–7 bar instrument air. MOV (electric) requires local JB, cable routing, and MCC feeder. Include actuator signal wiring, limit switches, and solenoid valve in scope of instrumentation and electrical BOQ.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISA 75.05.01 (valve terminology); ASME B31.3 Cl.322 (valve standards)

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype, nominal_bore_mm, pipe_schedule) × moc_factor(moc) × pipe_length_m  [pipes] OR base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype, nominal_bore_mm) × moc_factor × quantity  [fittings/flanges/valves]
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_cost × moc_factor × pressure_factor(design_pressure) × temperature_factor(design_temperature) × standard_factor(standard) × actuator_factor(valve_actuator) × [pipe_length_m OR quantity]
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_cost × moc_factor × pressure_factor × temperature_factor × standard_factor × actuator_factor × trim_factor(valve_trim) × face_factor(flange_face) × end_conn_factor(end_connection) × [pipe_length_m OR quantity] × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

adders.note: Piping is highly labour-intensive. Erection/installation 45% covers pipe fitting, tack-welding, full penetration butt welding, NDE (RT/UT), PWHT, pressure testing, painting, and insulation for CS process piping. SS piping: erection up to 65% (slower welding, purging, stricter NDE). Small bore (<50 mm NB) socket weld: 30–35% installation adder. Freight 5% (bulk commodity, local sourcing preferred). Contingency 15% for piping (complex routing, late isometrics, field changes).

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor.

usd_cost_baseline.note: USD supply-only unit costs (ex-works, 2024 basis). Pipes: USD per metre. Fittings/flanges/valves: USD per piece. CS base costs; apply moc_factor for alloy/SS uprating. Sources: Aspen ICARUS, RSMeans, Indian pipe mill catalogue (SAIL, Jindal, Ratnamani). ±30% Tier 1.

SCHEMA-PROC-VESSEL.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs resolved. Defects corrected: (1) Duplicate spec_key 'volume' (F-002 m³ + F-008 L) — F-008 renamed vessel_storage_volume_l with correct range 500–100,000 L, conditional on deaerator; (2) F-009 Flow Rate locked 1500 m³/hr — conditional display added, range widened; (3) F-010 Efficiency 88–94% — conditional on thermal items, renamed thermal_efficiency; (4) F-011 Motor Power locked 5000 kW — conditional on boiler/reformer, range widened; (5) F-012 Power (MW) — conditional on thermal/fired items; (6) F-013 Area (m²) locked 850 — conditional on heat-transfer items, range widened; (7) F-007 Length max=10 m widened to 80 m; (8) F-004 Height min=8 m corrected to 0.5 m; (9) Design Temperature min=10°C corrected to -196°C for cryogenic; (10) All fields given proper conditional_display by sub-type group; (11) MOC expanded — LTCS, 9Ni, Cr-Mo, Alloy 800H added; (12) Standard expanded — IS 2825, IS 803, IS 10028, API 620, IBR added; (13) Fuel type conditional on fired items only. Engineering basis: ASME VIII Div.1/2, ASME I (IBR India), API 650/620/2000, IS 2825, IS 803, IS 10028, API 560, ASME B&PV Code.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick ±30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget ±20%. Tier 3 = Detailed ±10%.

SCHEMA-PROC-VESSEL.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | Vol:{volume} m³ | D:{diameter} m × H:{height} m | {design_pressure} bar(g) / {design_temperature}°C | MOC:{moc} | {standard} | Duty:{thermal_duty_mw} MW | Qty:{quantity} No.
SCHEMA-PROC-VESSEL.spec_example: Process Vessel (Separator) | Vol:25 m³ | D:2.0 m × H:8 m | 16 bar(g) / 120°C | MOC:SS316L | ASME VIII Div.1 | Qty:1 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select equipment type. Groups: Atmospheric storage tanks (cone/floating roof); Pressure storage (LPG sphere, air receiver, LNG); Process pressure vessels (separator, deaerator, K-O drum); High-pressure reactors (ammonia converter, urea reactor); Thermal/fired equipment (boiler, reformer, fired heater, thermal oil heater, WHB).

fields.label: Volume (Nominal)
fields.hint: Nominal volume of vessel or tank. Storage tanks: 100–200,000 m³. Spherical LPG: 50–10,000 m³. Air receiver: 0.1–50 m³. Cryogenic LNG: 500–250,000 m³. Process separator: 1–500 m³. Reactor: 5–2000 m³. Primary cost driver for all vessel types — cost scales ≈ V^0.6. Per ASME VIII Div.1 / API 650 / IS 2825.

fields.label: Shell / Tank Diameter
fields.hint: Inside diameter of vessel or tank shell. Process vessels (separator, K-O drum): 0.6–5 m. Reactors (ammonia, urea HP): 1.5–5 m. LPG sphere: 10–25 m diameter. Atmospheric storage tanks: 6–100 m. Shell diameter sets wall thickness per ASME VIII formula: t = P×R / (S×E − 0.6P). Governs transport route — vessels >4.2 m OD may require special convoy.

fields.label: Height / Tangent-to-Tangent Length
fields.hint: Overall height (vertical vessels) or tangent-to-tangent shell length (horizontal vessels). Vertical separators/K-O drums: 2–20 m T-T. Atmospheric storage tanks: 6–25 m shell height. Reactors (ammonia converter): 10–30 m. Urea reactor: 15–30 m (high wall thickness due to carbamate service). LPG sphere: not applicable — use diameter. T-T length × π × (D/2)² = approximate shell volume.

fields.label: Equipment Length (Fired Heater / Reformer)
fields.hint: Overall length of fired heater, reformer furnace, waste heat boiler, or thermal oil heater. Small fired heater: 5–15 m. Large crude heater: 20–50 m. Ammonia reformer primary furnace: 10–20 m per cell. Waste heat boiler (shell & tube): 4–20 m T-T. Thermal oil heater (coil type): 3–10 m box length. For horizontal process vessels: TT shell length (use height field instead).

fields.label: Design Pressure (MAWP)
fields.hint: Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP) of the vessel/equipment. Atmospheric tanks (API 650): ≤1.03 kPa(g) (near atmospheric). LPG spheres: 6–18 bar(g) per IS 10028. Air receiver: 7–13 bar(g). Process separator: 4–100 bar(g). Ammonia converter: 140–220 bar(g). Urea reactor: 130–175 bar(g) (carbamate corrosion service). HP steam boiler drum: 40–180 bar(g). ASME VIII Div.2 mandatory for vessels >350 bar(g) or per owner spec.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum design temperature of the vessel or process fluid. LNG cryogenic tank: -162°C (minimum design -196°C for nitrogen purge). LPG sphere: -20°C to +55°C (refrigerated or ambient). Air receiver: ambient (typically 50°C). Ammonia converter: 450–520°C (requires Cr-Mo alloy steel). Urea reactor: 180–210°C at 160 bar(g). Steam boiler drum: 250–350°C. Reformer furnace tubes: 800–950°C (alloy 800H / 25Cr-35Ni). Temperature governs allowable stress (ASME Sec. II Part D).

fields.label: Deaerator Storage Volume
fields.hint: Storage tank volume of deaerator (horizontal storage vessel below the deaerating tray column). Sized to provide 20–30 minutes hold-up at maximum boiler feed water demand. Small industrial boiler: 500–5,000 L. Medium power plant: 5,000–100,000 L. IS 2825 atmospheric pressure vessel code applies to deaerator storage drum. IBR (Indian Boiler Regulations) applies to feed water lines above 110°C.

fields.label: Process Fluid / Steam Flow Rate
fields.hint: Process fluid or steam throughput. Steam boiler: steam generation rate in t/h. Small package boiler: 1–10 t/h. Industrial: 10–100 t/h. Power plant boiler: 100–3,000 t/h. Fired heater / thermal oil: process fluid flow rate in t/h. Waste heat boiler: steam generation from exhaust gas recovery. Deaerator: boiler feed water flow. Primary cost driver for boilers and fired equipment alongside duty (MW).

fields.label: Thermal Efficiency
fields.hint: Thermal efficiency of fired or heat-recovery equipment. Steam boilers (modern water tube): 88–94% (LHV basis). Thermal oil heaters: 88–92%. Process fired heaters: 82–92% (API 560 design). Reformer furnaces: 50–60% (high temperature export reduces efficiency). Waste heat boilers: not applicable (depends on exhaust temperature). Higher efficiency → smaller stack, lower fuel cost, smaller air preheater. Used to back-calculate fuel consumption from duty.

fields.label: Combustion Air / FD Fan Motor Power
fields.hint: Total installed motor power of combustion air (FD) fan and induced draft (ID) fan. Small boiler (5 t/h): 15–30 kW. Medium (50 t/h): 100–200 kW. Large industrial boiler (200 t/h): 800–2000 kW. Reformer furnace combustion air blower: 1000–6000 kW. Governs boiler electrical auxiliaries, MCC, and transformer sizing. Standard IE3 motor ratings: 315, 355, 400, 500, 630, 800, 1000, 1600 kW.

fields.label: Thermal Duty / Fired Heat Release
fields.hint: Total fired heat duty (heat release rate) of the equipment. Small package boiler: 1–5 MW. Industrial steam boiler: 5–80 MW. Power boiler: 80–2000 MW. Process fired heater: 2–500 MW. Reformer furnace (primary): 50–500 MW. Thermal oil heater: 0.5–20 MW. Waste heat boiler: duty = exhaust gas heat recovery (1–200 MW). Primary cost driver for all thermal and fired equipment.

fields.label: Heat Transfer Surface Area
fields.hint: Total heat transfer surface area of the equipment. Package boiler (10 t/h): 200–500 m². Industrial boiler (50 t/h): 800–3000 m². Power boiler (200+ t/h): 5000–50,000 m². Waste heat boiler: 200–5000 m² depending on exhaust flow. Thermal oil heater coil: 50–500 m². Heat flux (kW/m²) × area = duty; typical boiler radiant: 150–200 kW/m². Per ASME I / IBR / IS 2825 as applicable.

fields.label: Design Standard
options: ASME VIII Div.1 (Pressure vessels, general — up to ~350 bar)
ASME VIII Div.2 (Pressure vessels, alternative rules — HP reactors)
IBR 1950 (Indian Boiler Regulations — mandatory for India boilers)
IS 803 (Code of practice for design, fabrication and erection of vertical mild steel cylindrical welded oil storage tanks)
IS 10028 (Spherical storage vessels for LPG — India)
API 560 (Fired heaters for general refinery service)
ASME B&PV Code Sec. VIII + IBR (combined for India export boilers)
fields.default: ASME VIII Div.1 (Pressure vessels, general — up to ~350 bar)
fields.hint: Governing design and fabrication code. ASME VIII Div.1: standard for most process pressure vessels. ASME VIII Div.2: required for high-pressure vessels (ammonia converters, urea reactors) where reduced wall thickness and mandatory NDT are preferred. IBR 1950: mandatory in India for steam boilers above 22 litres capacity — requires IBR inspector approval and stamping. API 650: atmospheric storage tanks for petroleum products. IS 803: Indian standard for vertical oil storage tanks. IS 10028: mandatory for LPG spheres in India per PESO/SMPV rules.

fields.label: MOC (Shell / Head)
options: LTCS (Low Temperature CS, -46°C) — SA-516 Gr.60 / SA-333 Gr.6
Internally clad CS (SS316L cladding) — urea reactor
fields.hint: Shell and head material. CS SA-516 Gr.70: standard for pressure vessels and boiler drums. LTCS: cryogenic tanks and LPG vessels (down to -46°C). 9% Ni: cryogenic LNG service (down to -165°C). Cr-Mo steels: elevated temperature service — refer ASME Sec. II Part D for allowable stress reduction at temperature. Urea reactor: requires SS316L-clad CS or solid SS316L-UG to resist carbamate. Reformer tubes: alloy 800H or 25Cr-35Ni centrifugally cast. NACE MR0175 applies for H₂S-containing service.

fields.label: Fuel Type
options: Waste heat recovery (no fuel — exhaust gas)
fields.hint: Primary fuel for fired heater, boiler, reformer, or thermal oil heater. Natural gas: most common for industrial boilers and heaters. Hydrogen-rich gas: typical for ammonia plant reformer furnace (PSA tail gas / purge gas recycled as fuel). Dual fuel: common in refineries (fuel oil backup for gas interruption). Waste heat recovery: select for WHB — no burner, fuelled by exhaust. Fuel selection affects burner type, stack design, and emissions compliance.

fields.label: Steam Outlet Pressure / Temperature
options: Medium pressure steam: 6–25 bar(g) / saturated or superheated
Very high pressure steam: 100–180 bar(g) / superheated
fields.hint: Steam outlet conditions — determines drum thickness, superheater alloy, and overall boiler cost tier. IBR requires third-party inspection for all steam pressures. Supercritical: only for utility power plants (>500 MW) — requires P91/P92 Cr-Mo-V alloy superheater tubes. High pressure steam: standard for industrial co-generation and process plants. Cost escalates sharply above 100 bar(g) due to alloy material and thicker drum walls.

fields.label: Insulation System (Cryogenic)
options: Single containment (9% Ni inner, CS outer) — above-ground
Full containment (9% Ni inner + pre-stressed concrete outer)
Membrane type (stainless corrugated membrane + foam insulation)
Vacuum / perlite insulated (small cryogenic vessels ≤500 m³)
fields.default: Full containment (9% Ni inner + pre-stressed concrete outer)
fields.hint: Containment system for cryogenic LNG storage tank. Full containment: mandatory for onshore LNG terminals per EN 1473 and NFPA 59A — inner 9% Ni tank + outer pre-stressed concrete wall both capable of containing liquid and vapour. Single containment: cheaper but requires larger exclusion zone. Membrane type: used for LNG ships and small-scale import terminals. Full containment adds 50–100% to civil/structural cost versus single. Applicable only to cryogenic LNG storage.

fields.label: Shell Wall Thickness
fields.hint: Nominal shell wall thickness. For Tier 3 accuracy. Standard process vessel (CS, 16 bar, 2 m dia): 18–25 mm. Ammonia converter (220 bar, Cr-Mo): 100–250 mm. Urea reactor (160 bar, clad): 80–120 mm. API 650 storage tank bottom plate: 6–12 mm; shell plates: 6–25 mm depending on height and product SG. Thickness calculated per ASME VIII Cl.UG-27: t = P×R / (S×E − 0.6P) + corrosion allowance. Governs plate weight and hence material cost.

fields.label: Internals / Process Internals Type
options: Demister pad / mesh pad (separator, K-O drum)
Catalyst beds — multiple beds with quench nozzles (ammonia converter)
fields.hint: Process internals fitted inside the vessel. Internals cost can be 20–150% of bare vessel cost. Ammonia converter: multi-bed axial/radial catalyst converter internals (typically 3 catalyst beds with inter-bed heat exchangers) — adds 30–60%. Demister pad: standard for two-phase separator/K-O drum. Reformer catalyst tubes: alloy 800H centrifugal cast tubes are very high value — can exceed furnace structure cost. Fired heater coils: radiant + convection coil bundle.

fields.label: Corrosion Allowance
fields.hint: Corrosion allowance added to calculated wall thickness. CS process vessels (non-corrosive): 1.5–3 mm. CS vessels in corrosive service (H₂S, HCl, wet CO₂): 3–6 mm. API 650 storage tanks: typically 1.5 mm for clean products. Urea reactor: 0 mm (SS316L or clad surface prevents corrosion). LNG vessels: 0 mm (cryogenic service, corrosion not a concern). Higher CA increases material weight and cost — consider upgrading MOC instead if CA >6 mm required.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical units. Most pressure vessels and boilers: 1. Storage tanks: may be multiple identical tanks. Air receivers: sometimes 2 × 100% operating + standby.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Steam Boiler (Water Tube - High Pressure)') AND standard AND !'IBR' IN standard AND !'ASME I' IN standard
compliance_rules.message: Steam boilers in India must comply with the Indian Boiler Regulations (IBR) 1950 (as amended) — a statutory requirement under the Boilers Act 1923. Boilers not registered and stamped per IBR cannot be operated legally. Specify IBR 1950 or ASME I + IBR combined standard.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Boilers Act 1923; IBR 1950 Regulation 1(3); CEA Thermal Power Regulations

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Spherical Tank (LPG)' AND standard AND !'IS 10028' IN standard AND !'ASME VIII' IN standard
compliance_rules.message: LPG spherical storage vessels in India require compliance with IS 10028 and the Static and Mobile Pressure Vessels (SMPV) Rules 2016 under PESO. Third-party inspection and PESO registration are mandatory before commissioning.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 10028; SMPV Rules 2016; Explosives Act 1884; PESO regulations

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Urea Reactor (Fertilizer)' AND moc AND 'CS' IN moc AND !'clad' IN moc AND !'316' IN moc
compliance_rules.message: Plain carbon steel is NOT suitable for urea reactor service. Ammonium carbamate (NH₄COONH₂) is highly corrosive to CS at operating conditions (160 bar, 185°C). Specify SS316L UG (ultra-grade) solid or CS with SS316L cladding as minimum per Stamicarbon / Toyo / Snamprogetti urea process spec.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME VIII Div.2; urea process licensor specifications (Stamicarbon, Snamprogetti)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Cryogenic Storage Tank (LNG)' AND moc AND !'9% Ni' IN moc AND !'Alloy' IN moc AND !'membrane' IN moc
compliance_rules.message: LNG service at -162°C requires 9% Nickel steel or equivalent cryogenic-grade material for the inner tank. Plain CS and LTCS are not suitable below -46°C. Specify 9% Ni steel (SA-553 Type I) per BS 7777 / EN 14620.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: BS 7777; EN 14620-2; NFPA 59A; SA-553 Type I (ASTM)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 100 AND standard AND 'ASME VIII Div.1' IN standard
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure above 100 bar(g): ASME VIII Div.2 (Alternative Rules) should be considered. Div.2 allows higher allowable stresses (reducing wall thickness) but requires more rigorous design-by-analysis and mandatory NDT. Typically economical for HP vessels above 100 bar. Consider upgrading to Div.2 for ammonia/urea/HP reactor service.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME VIII Div.2 Part 4; ASME B&PV Code Section VIII comparison

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temperature > 450 AND moc AND ('CS' IN moc OR 'SS304' IN moc OR 'SS316' IN moc)
compliance_rules.message: Design temperature above 450°C: austenitic SS loses significant creep strength above 500°C, and CS allowable stress reduces sharply above 370°C. For vessels and tubes above 450°C, consider Cr-Mo alloy steels (P22, P91) or heat-resistant alloys (Alloy 800H, HK-40). Verify allowable stress from ASME Sec. II Part D at design temperature.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME Sec. II Part D Table 1A; API 530 (fired heater tubes)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Storage Tank' IN equipment_subtype AND design_pressure > 0.1
compliance_rules.message: Atmospheric storage tank selected with design pressure >0.1 bar(g). API 650 covers fixed-roof atmospheric tanks for pressures ≤1.03 kPa(g). If internal pressure exceeds this, use API 620 (low pressure, ≤103 kPa(g)) or ASME VIII Div.1 for pressure vessels. Confirm which standard applies to your tank design pressure.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 650 Scope Cl.1.1.1; API 620 Scope Cl.1.1

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Ammonia Converter (Fertilizer)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Reactor (High Pressure)') AND design_pressure > 200 AND moc AND 'CS' IN moc AND !'Cr-Mo' IN moc
compliance_rules.message: HP reactor above 200 bar with plain CS: verify hydrogen partial pressure. For hydrogen-containing streams (ammonia synthesis, hydrocracker), Nelson curves (API RP 941) must be consulted. At elevated temperature and pressure, hydrogen attack (HTHA) can destroy CS — Cr-Mo steel or austenitic SS cladding is typically required.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API RP 941 (Nelson Curves — Steels in H₂ service); ASME VIII Div.2

compliance_rules.condition_expr: diameter > 4.2
compliance_rules.message: Vessel diameter above 4.2 m: will likely exceed standard road transport width limits. Special transport permit and escort required for road movement. Consider field fabrication if site is not accessible to heavy abnormal loads. Verify route survey for bridge, tunnel, and overhead cable clearances.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: India Motor Vehicles Act; typical abnormal load route survey requirements

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Reformer Furnace (Fertilizer)' AND moc AND !'Alloy 800H' IN moc AND !'25Cr' IN moc
compliance_rules.message: Reformer furnace catalyst tube material appears to be non-alloy. Primary reformer catalyst tubes operate at 850–950°C in steam-hydrocarbon service and require centrifugally cast alloy tubes (typically HP40 modified 25Cr-35Ni or Alloy 800H). Plain CS or Cr-Mo is not suitable. Confirm tube material — this is the highest-cost item in a reformer.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 530; ASTM A608; typical licensor specs (Haldor Topsoe, KBR, Uhde)

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_cost(equipment_subtype, volume, thermal_duty_mw, flow_rate) × moc_factor(moc) × pressure_factor(design_pressure) × temperature_factor(design_temperature) × quantity
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_cost × moc_factor × pressure_factor × temperature_factor × standard_factor(standard) × steam_params_factor(steam_parameters) × containment_factor(cryogenic_containment) × heat_area_factor(heat_transfer_area) × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_cost × moc_factor × pressure_factor × temperature_factor × standard_factor × steam_params_factor × containment_factor × heat_area_factor × internals_factor(internals_type) × wall_thickness_factor(wall_thickness_mm) × quantity × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

adders.note: Heavy process vessels and boilers. Erection/installation 22% covers crane lifts, site assembly, refractory curing (boilers/heaters), hydrotest, and initial commissioning. Reformer furnaces/large boilers: erection up to 35% (complex civil + refractory). Cryogenic LNG tanks: erection 40–60% (specialist cryogenic contractor). Freight 7% for heavy-lift vessels (some items shipped in sections). Contingency 15% — higher than standard due to complex fabrication and statutory inspection requirements.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ

usd_cost_baseline.note: USD supply-only fabricated costs (ex-works, 2024 basis). CS/standard MOC base rates; apply moc_factor for alloy upgrades. Sources: Aspen ICARUS Process Evaluator benchmarks, AACE metrics, Indian fabricator quotations (ISGEC, HMT, L&T Heavy Engg). Scaling exponent ~0.6 (six-tenths rule). ±30% Tier 1.

Storage Tank (Cone Roof).basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 45000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 60895.52. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Storage Tank (Floating Roof).basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 45000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 60895.52. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Spherical Tank (LPG).basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 38000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 51422.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Air Receiver.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Cryogenic Storage Tank (LNG).basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 45000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 60895.52. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

moc_factor.note: Relative to CS = 1.0. Applied to vessel shell/head material cost.

design_pressure_factor.note: Pressure class multiplier on base vessel cost. Normalised at 10–40 bar = 1.0.

design_temperature_factor.note: Temperature class multiplier. Normalised at ambient (20–200°C) = 1.0.

internals_type_factor.note: Multiplier on base vessel / equipment cost for internals.

SCHEMA-PROT-RELAY.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs resolved. Defects corrected: (1) F-002 Voltage relabelled 'Auxiliary Supply Voltage (DC)' — these are relay DC power supply values (24/48/110/220 VDC), not system voltage; converted from number to dropdown; (2) F-003 Current (CT secondary) min=max=10A is non-standard — IEC standard CT secondaries are 1A or 5A; corrected to dropdown; (3) Standard expanded beyond IEC 60255 alone. New fields added: system voltage class, protection functions, communication interface, mounting/panel type, vendor platform, contact configuration (auxiliary/timer relays), timer range. Engineering basis: IEC 60255, IEC 61850, IEEE C37.90, IS 3842, CBIP Manual on Protection, CEA (India) grid code.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick ±30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget ±20%. Tier 3 = Detailed ±10%.

SCHEMA-PROT-RELAY.spec_example: Protection Relay (Motor - Numeric) | 415 V LV | 110 VDC aux | CT: 1A | 49/50/51/46/48/66 | IEC 61850 GOOSE | SEL-710 | Qty: 12 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select relay type. Numeric protection relays: multifunction IED-based relays for motor, transformer, feeder, and generator protection. Simple relays: trip circuit supervision (TCS), auxiliary multi-contact, and timer relays — hardwired panel components.

fields.label: System / Protected Circuit Voltage Class
fields.hint: Nominal system voltage of the circuit or equipment being protected. Governs CT/VT ratios, relay measuring input ratings, and insulation/isolation requirements. LV 415 V: motor protection relays mounted in MCC. MV 6.6/11 kV: switchgear protection relays. HV 33–132 kV: substation bay protection. This is the SYSTEM voltage, not the relay's own supply voltage.

fields.label: Auxiliary Supply Voltage (DC)
options: 24–250 VDC (wide-range universal input — modern numeric relays)
fields.hint: DC auxiliary supply voltage to the relay's own power supply module. This is the relay's INTERNAL supply, not the system voltage. 110 VDC: standard for Indian industrial and utility switchgear panels (IS 3842, CEA Regulations 2010). 220 VDC: large utility HV substations (132 kV and above). 24 VDC: typical for PLCs, SCADA RTUs, small relay panels. Most modern numeric relays (SEL, GE, ABB, Siemens) accept wide-range 24–250 VDC as standard.

fields.label: CT Secondary Current Rating
options: 1 A (IEC standard — metering and protection CTs, Europe/India)
5 A (IEC/ANSI — USA preference, some industrial CTs)
fields.default: 1 A (IEC standard — metering and protection CTs, Europe/India)
fields.hint: Rated secondary current of the Current Transformer (CT) connected to relay. IEC 60044-1 / IEC 61869-2 standard values: 1 A or 5 A. 10 A is NOT an IEC standard CT secondary — do not specify. India: 1 A secondary is standard per IS 2705 for protection class CTs. USA/ANSI: 5 A secondary common (ANSI C57.13). Relay current input circuits must match CT secondary — mismatching causes relay damage or measurement error.

fields.label: Protection Functions (ANSI Device Numbers)
options: Motor: 49/50/51/46/48/66/87M (+ differential) — large HV motors >2 MW
Feeder: 50/51/50N/51N (O/C + earth fault IDMT) — standard radial feeder
Feeder: 50/51/50N/51N/67/67N (+ directional O/C + E/F) — ring / interconnected
Generator: 87G/40/51V/21/59/27/81 (differential + loss of field + V/f) — standard
fields.default: Feeder: 50/51/50N/51N (O/C + earth fault IDMT) — standard radial feeder
fields.hint: Protection functions implemented in the relay (IEEE C37.2 ANSI device numbers). Common functions: 50=instantaneous O/C; 51=IDMT O/C; 50N/51N=earth fault; 46=negative sequence (NPS); 49=thermal overload; 87=differential; 21=distance; 27/59=under/overvoltage; 67=directional O/C; 40=loss of field; 81=over/underfrequency. More functions = higher relay cost. Differential (87) adds significant cost.

fields.label: Communication Interface
options: IEC 61850 Ed.1 (GOOSE + MMS, single fibre port)
IEC 61850 Ed.2 (GOOSE + MMS + SAMPLED VALUES, redundant ports)
fields.default: IEC 61850 Ed.1 (GOOSE + MMS, single fibre port)
fields.hint: Communication protocol for relay integration into SCADA / DCS / protection scheme. IEC 61850: modern standard for substation automation — enables GOOSE inter-relay communication for bus protection, interlocking, and trip signalling without hardwiring. Required by most utilities and CEA regulations for new substations. Adds 10–20% to relay cost. DNP3: dominant in USA utility SCADA. MODBUS: basic, low-cost, sufficient for simple monitoring. Not applicable for simple auxiliary/timer relays.

fields.label: Vendor / Relay Platform
options: Simple relay (Omron / Finder / Phoenix Contact — auxiliary/timer)
fields.hint: Relay vendor / platform. Governs feature set, spares availability, and after-sales support. SEL / ABB / Siemens / GE: premium (1.3–1.8× base price) but widely accepted by utilities and EPC contractors. Indian makes (Easun, L&T): 40–60% lower cost, acceptable for industrial and state utility projects. Vendor selection must consider existing installed base and spare philosophy.

fields.label: Design Standard
options: IEC 60255 (Measuring relays and protection equipment — comprehensive)
IEC 61850 (Communication in substations — mandatory for smart grid)
IEEE C37.90 (Relays for AC power systems — ANSI)
IS 3842 (Application guide — protection of AC motors, India)
IEC 60255 + IEC 61850 (combined — modern Indian utility projects)
fields.default: IEC 60255 (Measuring relays and protection equipment — comprehensive)
fields.hint: Governing design and type-test standard. IEC 60255: the primary international standard for protection relays — covers accuracy, operating times, disturbance withstand, EMC. IEC 61850: mandatory for new grid-connected substations in many countries. IEEE C37.90: equivalent ANSI standard — required for USA / ANSI projects. IS 3842: Indian standard for motor protection application guidelines. CEA Regulations 2010 (amended 2021): mandatory for grid-connected substations in India above 33 kV.

fields.label: Mounting / Panel Type
options: DIN rail mounted (small control panel / MCC auxiliary relay)
Standalone cubicle (large multifunction IED with HMI)
fields.hint: Physical mounting arrangement. Flush panel: standard for relay panels and marshalling kiosks. Draw-out: required for switchgear-integrated relays (ABB SafeRing, Siemens SIVACON, Schneider SM6 vacuum panels). DIN rail: auxiliary relays, timers, trip circuit supervision in marshalling kiosks. Mounting type affects panel space allocation in relay panel schedule.

fields.label: Contact Configuration
fields.hint: Output contact arrangement of auxiliary or timer relay. Standard 4NO+4NC: sufficient for most intertrip, annunciator, and indication functions in switchgear panels. High-speed trip relay (≤5 ms): required for CBFP (Circuit Breaker Failure Protection) and fast bus protection schemes. Contact current rating: minimum 5 A at 110 VDC for trip circuits (IEC 60255-0-20 Class C). For numeric protection relays, output contacts are configured internally — select 'Not applicable'.

fields.label: Coil / Operating Voltage (Simple Relays)
fields.hint: Operating coil voltage for auxiliary relay or timer relay. Must match the panel DC or AC supply. 110 VDC: standard Indian substation battery voltage. 24 VDC: typical for PLC-based panels and marshalling kiosks. For trip circuit supervision relays: coil voltage matches trip circuit battery voltage (typically 110 or 220 VDC). Not applicable for numeric protection relays — use Auxiliary Supply field.

fields.label: Timer Range
fields.hint: Operating time range for on-delay or off-delay timer relay. Motor starting (anti-restart, sequential starting): 0.1–10 s typical. Auto-reclosure initiation (dead time): 0.3–5 s (IEC 62271-100). Annunciator / alarm delay: 10–60 s. Accuracy: electronic timers ±2% over temperature range. Only shown for Timer Relay sub-types.

fields.label: Binary I/O Modules (Numeric Relay)
fields.hint: Number of optional I/O expansion modules added to the base relay. Standard I/O: typically 6–8 binary inputs and 6–8 output contacts. Each expansion module adds ~4 BI + 4 BO. Large feeder or transformer protection with SCADA, interlocking, position feedback may require 1–2 expansion modules. Each expansion module adds ~8–15% to relay unit cost.

fields.label: Trip Circuit Supervision Type
options: Single-channel TCS (monitors one trip coil circuit)
Dual-channel TCS (monitors two independent trip coil circuits)
Integral TCS (built into numeric protection relay — no separate relay needed)
fields.default: Single-channel TCS (monitors one trip coil circuit)
fields.hint: Trip circuit supervision type. TCS relay continuously monitors the DC trip circuit for open-circuit or low-voltage faults, alerting operators before a failure occurs. Single-channel: standard for MV switchgear with one trip coil. Dual-channel: required for HV circuit breakers with two independent trip coils (TC1 and TC2) per IEC 62271-100 / National Grid standards. Modern numeric relays have TCS function built-in — check before ordering separately.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of relay units. For duplicate / main-backup protection schemes, specify each relay separately. Auxiliary and timer relays: count includes all panel instances.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: ct_secondary_current AND '10 A' IN ct_secondary_current
compliance_rules.message: 10 A CT secondary is NOT an IEC or ANSI standard value. IEC 60044-1 / IEC 61869-2 specifies 1 A or 5 A secondary currents only. Specifying 10 A will result in non-standard CT procurement and relay damage. Change to 1 A (IEC/India standard) or 5 A (ANSI/USA).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Protection Relay (Generator - Numeric)' AND protection_functions AND !'87G' IN protection_functions AND !'87' IN protection_functions
compliance_rules.message: Generator protection without differential function (87G). Generator differential protection (87G) is mandatory for all generators >1 MVA per IEEE C37.102 and CEA Regulations 2010. Select a protection function set that includes 87G.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEEE C37.102 (Generator Protection Guide); CEA Regulations 2010 Schedule 5

compliance_rules.condition_expr: system_voltage_class AND ('HV 132' IN system_voltage_class OR 'EHV 220' IN system_voltage_class OR 'EHV 400' IN system_voltage_class) AND comms_interface AND 'None' IN comms_interface
compliance_rules.message: HV/EHV substation (132 kV and above) without communication interface. CEA Regulations 2010 (amended 2021) mandate IEC 61850 SCADA connectivity for all new grid-connected substations above 33 kV. Specify IEC 61850 Ed.1 or Ed.2 communication interface.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CEA (Technical Standards for Connectivity) Regulations 2021; IEC 61850-1

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Protection Relay (Transformer - Numeric)' AND protection_functions AND !'87T' IN protection_functions
compliance_rules.message: Transformer protection without differential function (87T). Transformer differential protection (87T) is the primary protection for transformers >1 MVA. Without 87T, only overcurrent/earth fault backup protection is available — not acceptable for critical transformers. Confirm if this is intentionally backup-only specification.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60255-187-3; IEEE C37.91 (Transformer Protection Guide); IS 3842

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Feeder' IN equipment_subtype AND system_voltage_class AND ('HV 66' IN system_voltage_class OR 'HV 110' IN system_voltage_class OR 'HV 132' IN system_voltage_class) AND protection_functions AND !'21' IN protection_functions AND !'87L' IN protection_functions
compliance_rules.message: HV feeder protection (66 kV and above) without distance (21) or differential (87L) protection. For transmission line feeders at 66 kV and above, distance protection (21) or line differential (87L) is the primary protection per CEA Regulations and grid code. Simple IDMT overcurrent is only backup protection at this voltage.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CEA Regulations 2010 Schedule 5; IEC 60255-187-2; IEEE C37.113

compliance_rules.condition_expr: contact_config AND 'High-speed trip' IN contact_config AND coil_voltage AND '24 VDC' IN coil_voltage
compliance_rules.message: High-speed trip relay with 24 VDC coil: verify trip circuit operates correctly at minimum battery voltage (0.8 × 24 = 19.2 VDC). Most CBFP and bus protection schemes use 110 VDC for reliability. Consult relay manufacturer for dropout voltage vs coil voltage specification.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60255-0-20; CIGRE TB 511 (DC auxiliary supply requirements)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Trip Circuit Supervision Relay' AND tcs_type AND 'Single-channel' IN tcs_type AND system_voltage_class AND ('HV 66' IN system_voltage_class OR 'HV 110' IN system_voltage_class OR 'HV 132' IN system_voltage_class OR 'EHV' IN system_voltage_class)
compliance_rules.message: Single-channel TCS for HV/EHV circuit breaker: confirm whether CB has one or two trip coils. IEC 62271-100 requires dual trip coils for circuit breakers on transmission systems (66 kV and above). If dual trip coils: specify Dual-channel TCS.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 62271-100 Cl.6.106; National Grid TS 3.24.11; CEA Schedule 5

compliance_rules.condition_expr: io_modules AND '2 × expansion' IN io_modules OR io_modules AND '3 × expansion' IN io_modules
compliance_rules.message: Multiple I/O expansion modules: verify total relay width fits within relay panel allocation (standard relay panel = 2100 mm × 800 mm, typically 4U–8U per relay). Highly expanded relays may require a dedicated relay panel compartment.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61439-2 (switchgear assemblies); typical relay panel GA standards

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_cost × vendor_factor × standard_factor(standard) × comms_factor(comms_interface) × contact_factor(contact_config) × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_cost × vendor_factor × standard_factor × comms_factor × contact_factor × io_modules_factor(io_modules) × mounting_factor(mounting_type) × quantity × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

adders.note: Protection relays are compact, high-value electronic items. Freight 5% (air-freight preferred for damage-sensitive items). Erection/installation 12% covers panel wiring, CT/VT secondary wiring, SCADA commissioning, and protection settings (PSM/TMS) configuration. Commissioning and secondary injection testing (relay testing) are typically separate BOQ activity items. Contingency 12% AACE Class 4 ±20%.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: Switched ENR->CEPCI: procured/manufactured equipment, supply-cost dominated. verify=true

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ

usd_cost_baseline.note: USD supply-only unit costs (ex-works, 2024 basis). Vendor-neutral / budget-grade equivalent. Premium vendors (SEL, ABB, Siemens, GE): multiply by vendor_factor. Sources: ABB Relion series, SEL relay price guide, Schneider Sepam, Easun Reyrolle India catalogue 2024. ±30% Tier 1.

Protection Relay (Motor - Numeric).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 14 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 18.95. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Protection Relay (Transformer - Numeric).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 45000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 60895.52. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Protection Relay (Feeder - Numeric).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 14 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 18.95. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Protection Relay (Generator - Numeric).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 14 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 18.95. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Trip Circuit Supervision Relay.basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 14 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 18.95. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Auxiliary Relay (Multi-contact).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 14 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 18.95. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Timer Relay (On-delay).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 14 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 18.95. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Timer Relay (Off-delay).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 14 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 18.95. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

io_modules_factor.note: Each expansion module adds ~10% to relay unit cost

contact_config_factor.note: Relative to standard 4NO+4NC = 1.0. High-speed trip relay significantly more expensive.

SCHEMA-PUMP-ACTIVITY.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs resolved. template_name / discipline_group corrected from 'Pumps & Compressors' to 'Fans, Blowers & Pump Site Activities'. Defects corrected: (1) Volume F-004 (identical values to flow_rate — duplicate) replaced → Differential Pressure (Pa / kPa); (2) Flow Rate range 8000–120000 m³/hr widened and split per sub-type (fan Nm³/h vs blower vs pump m³/h); (3) Motor Power 30–75 kW widened to 0.75–2000 kW; (4) Voltage number field locked at 415 V → dropdown; (5) Speed locked 370–370 RPM widened to 250–3000 RPM; (6) Design Temperature locked 400–400°C widened to -10–600°C; (7) MOC single option FRP expanded to 9 options per sub-type; New fields added: gas/fluid type, impeller/fan type, differential pressure, crude pump head and fluid SG, oil station scope, activity man-hours, pump size class for activities, ATEX classification. Engineering basis: IS 4894, IS 6312, AMCA 210, BS EN ISO 13349, NFPA 92 (smoke control), API 676, IS 1520, IS 5639 (crude pumps). USD baselines 2024 (Kruger, Nicotra-Gebhardt, Howden, Sullair/Aerzen blowers; Grundfos/KSB crude pumps).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick ±30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget ±20%. Tier 3 = Detailed ±10%.

SCHEMA-PUMP-ACTIVITY.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | {gas_flow_rate} Nm³/h | {fan_flow_rate} m³/h | {diff_pressure_pa} Pa | {diff_pressure_kpa} kPa | {motor_power} kW | {design_temperature}°C | MOC: {moc} | {impeller_type} | Qty: {quantity} No.
SCHEMA-PUMP-ACTIVITY.spec_example: Axial Flow Fan (Cooling Tower) | 50000 m³/h | 80 Pa | 11 kW | MOC: FRP | Axial aerofoil blade | Qty: 6 No. ; High Pressure Blower | 2000 Nm³/h | 50 kPa | 55 kW | MOC: CS | Multi-stage centrifugal | Qty: 2 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select equipment or activity. Equipment (supply): fans, blower, crude pump, oil station. Activity (labour): alignment, grouting, base frame, bearing, vibration test, piping, trial run — costed by man-hours.

fields.label: Fan Volumetric Flow Rate
fields.hint: Volumetric flow at fan inlet conditions. Cooling tower axial fan cell: 25,000–200,000 m³/h per cell. Smoke extraction fan: 5,000–300,000 m³/h depending on zone size (per NFPA 92 / BS EN 12101-3 smoke extract rate). Fan is sized at actual inlet conditions (temperature, density). Note: for High Pressure Blower use Gas Flow Rate field (Nm³/h).

fields.label: Blower Gas Flow Rate
fields.hint: Gas flow at Normal conditions (0°C, 1.013 bar(a)). High pressure blowers (Roots, multistage centrifugal, screw): 100–50,000 Nm³/h. Convert from inlet: Nm³/h = Q_inlet × (T_inlet/273.15) × (P_inlet/1.013). Primary sizing parameter alongside differential pressure.

fields.label: Fan Static / Total Pressure Rise
fields.hint: Fan total pressure rise (FTP) or static pressure rise at design flow. Cooling tower induced-draft axial fan: 30–150 Pa (low resistance ducting). Smoke extraction fan (ducted): 300–1500 Pa. Smoke extraction (open plenum): 80–300 Pa. Fan shaft power: P(kW) = Q(m³/s) × ΔP(Pa) / (η_fan × 1000). Per AMCA 210 / BS EN ISO 13349. Not applicable to HP blower — use kPa field.

fields.label: Blower Differential Pressure
fields.hint: Differential pressure across the blower (discharge − suction). Roots lobe blower (low pressure): 10–100 kPa. Multi-stage centrifugal blower: 20–300 kPa. Screw blower (high efficiency): 30–150 kPa. Pressure ratio = (P_discharge + 101.325) / 101.325. Above 150 kPa: consider reciprocating or screw compressor instead. Per ISO 13709 / API 619.

fields.label: Motor / Driver Power
fields.hint: Installed motor nameplate power. Cooling tower axial fan (per cell): 1.5–37 kW. Smoke extraction fan: 5–200 kW. HP blower: 15–2000 kW. Crude oil transfer pump: 7.5–750 kW. Apply 10–15% service factor above calculated shaft power. Standard IE3 sizes: 0.75, 1.1, 1.5, 2.2, 3, 4, 5.5, 7.5, 11, 15, 18.5, 22, 30, 37, 45, 55, 75, 90, 110, 132, 160, 200, 250, 315 kW.

fields.label: Gas / Fluid Handled
options: Biogas / landfill gas (CH₄ mix — ATEX mandatory)
fields.hint: Gas or fluid handled by the fan or blower. Determines MOC, impeller type, and motor enclosure. Smoke / fire gases: requires high-temp rated fan (EN 12101-3 F400 or F600 class). Biogas: ATEX Zone 1 motor mandatory — flammable gas. Corrosive fumes: FRP or SS impeller/casing. Dusty gas: wear-resistant hard-coat or ceramic impeller.

fields.label: Impeller / Fan Type
options: Axial aerofoil blade (adjustable pitch) — cooling tower
Axial propeller blade (low efficiency, general ventilation)
Centrifugal forward-curved (compact, high flow, higher noise)
Roots lobe (positive displacement blower, pulsating flow)
Screw (helical lobe blower, oil-free, high efficiency)
fields.default: Axial aerofoil blade (adjustable pitch) — cooling tower
fields.hint: Fan or blower impeller type. Cooling tower: axial aerofoil adjustable-pitch is standard — allows BEP optimisation as cooling load varies. Smoke fan: axial or backward-curved centrifugal, rated to EN 12101-3 F400/F600. HP blower: Roots lobe for low-medium pressure (10–100 kPa); screw for higher efficiency; multi-stage centrifugal for large flows. Backward-curved centrifugal: most efficient for process ventilation (η ≈ 80–88%). Radial paddle: abrasive/dusty gas where wear resistance > efficiency.

fields.label: Design / Maximum Gas Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum gas/fluid temperature at equipment inlet. Cooling tower fans: 30–60°C (humid warm air from fill). HP blower (air/gas): 20–80°C (ambient, slight compression heating). Smoke extraction fans: must be rated for fire scenario temperature. EN 12101-3 temperature classes: F200 (200°C/120 min), F300 (300°C/60 min), F400 (400°C/120 min), F600 (600°C/60 min). Temperature rating governs bearing grease, motor insulation class, and impeller material expansion allowances.
