fields.label: Rated Speed
fields.hint: Fan or blower rated shaft speed. Common 50 Hz synchronous speeds (motor direct drive): 2-pole 2950 RPM; 4-pole 1450 RPM; 6-pole 960 RPM; 8-pole 730 RPM; 12-pole 490 RPM; 16-pole 370 RPM. Cooling tower axial fans: 370–730 RPM (8–16 pole) for low noise. Smoke extraction fans: 960–1450 RPM. HP blowers: 1450–2950 RPM (direct or belt drive). Lower speed = larger impeller diameter = lower noise, longer bearing life.

fields.label: Motor Supply Voltage
fields.hint: Motor supply voltage. 230 V 1-phase: only for very small fans ≤1.5 kW. 415 V 3-phase: standard Indian plant supply. 6.6 kV: large HP blowers >200 kW. Cooling tower fans often use 415 V with VFD for speed control.

fields.label: MOC (Casing / Impeller / Wetted Parts)
options: FRP (Fibre Reinforced Plastic) — cooling tower / corrosive humid air
CS (Carbon Steel) — general air, clean gas, crude oil
CS high-temp rated (450–600°C) — smoke/flue gas fans
SS304 — mildly corrosive gas / food grade
SS316 — corrosive process gas / chemical fumes
Aluminium alloy — lightweight / non-sparking (ATEX Zone 1 fans)
Epoxy-coated CS — corrosive fumes, moderate temperature
Hastelloy C-276 — highly corrosive fumes (Cl₂, HCl vapour)
fields.default: CS (Carbon Steel) — general air, clean gas, crude oil
fields.hint: Casing and impeller material. Cooling tower fan: FRP standard (corrosion-resistant to humid, chemically treated tower water mist). Smoke fan: CS high-temp rated per EN 12101-3 F400/F600. HP blower (air/N₂): CS standard. Biogas / chemical fumes: SS316 or epoxy-coated CS. Crude oil pump: CS (non-corrosive crude) or SS316 (sour crude H₂S service). Non-sparking aluminium impeller: required in some Zone 1 ATEX applications.

fields.label: Motor Enclosure / ATEX
options: Ex e IIC (increased safety — Zone 1 ATEX)
High-temp rated (F-class insulation, ≤155°C ambient) — smoke fan
fields.hint: Motor enclosure protection and hazardous area classification. Smoke extraction fans per EN 12101-3: motor must withstand elevated temperature — specify high-temp insulation class. Cooling tower fans: IP65 or WP (exposed to water spray). Biogas blowers: Ex d or Ex e Zone 1 ATEX mandatory. Chemical fume fans: ATEX zone to be confirmed from area classification study.

fields.label: Design / Performance Standard
options: BS EN ISO 13349 (Fan terminology and performance)
IS 5639 (Centrifugal pumps for petroleum — crude pump)
fields.hint: Governing design and acceptance test standard. AMCA 210 / IS 4894: laboratory performance test for fans (Q-P curve, efficiency). EN 12101-3: mandatory for smoke extraction fans — classification F200/F300/F400/F600 per operating temperature. API 619: Roots and rotary PD blowers for process service. IS 5639 / API 676: crude oil transfer pump design. EN 12101-3 adds ~25–40% to smoke fan cost due to certification.

fields.label: Crude / Oil Transfer Flow Rate
fields.hint: Volumetric flow rate of crude oil or petroleum liquid. Crude oil transfer pumps: 10–2000 m³/h typical. Oil pumping station: aggregate flow of all installed pumps. Viscosity of crude oil significantly affects pump performance — high viscosity (>200 cSt) requires viscosity correction to head and efficiency per HI 9.6.7 / API RP 11S6.

fields.label: Crude / Oil Differential Head
fields.hint: Total differential head for crude oil / petroleum transfer pump. Pump-to-tank transfer: 30–120 m. Pipeline injection / boosting: 100–600 m. Oil pumping station (mainline): 300–1200 m per station. Head (m) = ΔP (bar) × 10.2 / SG_oil. Shaft power (kW) = Q(m³/h) × H(m) × SG / (367 × η_pump). Viscosity correction mandatory for crude >50 cSt per HI 9.6.7.

fields.label: Oil / Crude Viscosity at Pumping Temperature
fields.hint: Kinematic viscosity of crude oil at actual pumping temperature. Light crude: 5–30 cSt; medium: 30–200 cSt; heavy crude: 200–10,000 cSt; extra heavy: >10,000 cSt. Viscosity correction per HI 9.6.7 / API RP 11S6: >50 cSt degrades pump head and efficiency significantly. >500 cSt: heating (trace heat or jacketing) usually required. Viscosity drops sharply with temperature — specify pumping temperature and confirm viscosity at that temperature from crude assay.

fields.label: Oil / Crude Specific Gravity
fields.hint: Specific gravity of crude oil at pumping temperature. Light crude (>35° API): SG ≈ 0.82–0.85. Medium crude (25–35° API): SG ≈ 0.85–0.88. Heavy crude (10–25° API): SG ≈ 0.88–0.93. API gravity = (141.5 / SG) − 131.5. SG affects shaft power calculation and motor sizing — if SG differs from water (1.0), motor must be selected at actual SG.

fields.label: Oil Pumping Station — Scope
options: Pump station — 2 pumps in parallel + manifold
Pump station — 3 pumps (2 duty + 1 standby) + manifold
Full pumping station (pumps + piping + control valves + building)
fields.hint: Scope definition for crude oil pumping station. Single pump skid: supply of pump unit only. Full station: civil building, inter-pump piping, control valves, level instruments, isolation valves, pig trap if applicable. Pipeline booster station: higher civil and piping content. Scope selection is critical — full station cost can be 3–6× pump skid cost alone.

fields.label: Activity Man-Hours
fields.hint: Estimated man-hours for the specific pump activity. Reference ranges (India site, skilled millwright): Trial run of pump: 4–8 man-hr (includes pre-run checks, log sheet). Laser shaft alignment: 3–6 man-hr (2-man team, coupling to coupling). Pump grouting: 8–24 man-hr (depends on baseplate size, cure time not included). Base frame installation: 8–16 man-hr (crane lift + bolting + levelling). Bearing installation (pump): 4–12 man-hr. Vibration testing: 4–8 man-hr (signature + analysis report). Pump house piping (per spool): 4–16 man-hr depending on pipe size and complexity.

fields.label: Pump Size Class (for Activity Scope)
fields.hint: Size class of the pump being worked on. Drives man-hour allowance. Small: centrifugal end-suction, jockey, chemical dosing. Medium: general process, CW small cells, transfer pumps. Large: large CW, fire pumps, BFP. Very large: high-capacity CW, mainline crude, large BFP. Man-hours scale approximately with pump weight^0.5.

fields.label: Piping Scope / Pipe Class
options: Small bore CS (≤2" / 50 mm NB) — instrument / seal flush
Medium bore CS (2"–6" / 50–150 mm NB) — suction / discharge
Large bore CS (6"–12" / 150–300 mm NB) — headers / CW
Stainless steel piping (any size) — corrosive service
HDPE / GRP piping — cooling water / non-pressure
High-pressure piping (>40 bar, any size) — BFP / HP pump
fields.default: Medium bore CS (2"–6" / 50–150 mm NB) — suction / discharge
fields.hint: Pipe material and size class for pump house interconnecting piping activity. Governs fabrication and erection man-hours significantly. SS piping: welding complexity 2–3× CS at same size. HP piping (>40 bar): requires PWHT, radiographic/UT examination, significantly higher man-hours per joint. Only shown for 'Pump house piping' activity.

fields.label: Speed Control / VFD
options: Variable Frequency Drive (VFD) — integral to fan package
Adjustable pitch (axial fan — mechanical blade pitch control)
fields.hint: Flow/speed control method. Cooling tower fans: adjustable pitch preferred — changes airflow without changing speed, very energy-efficient. VFD: saves 15–50% energy at part load for variable-duty fans. Smoke fans: fixed speed mandatory — must deliver design flow reliably in fire event; VFD only if fail-safe full-speed fallback is provided. Two-speed: simple and reliable, two discrete operating points.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of units (equipment) or occurrences (activity). Cooling tower fans: typically 4–12 per cooling tower. Smoke fans: 1–4 per smoke zone per BS EN 12101. HP blowers: 1 duty + 1 standby typical. Activities: number of pumps being worked on.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Smoke Extraction Fan' AND design_temperature < 200
compliance_rules.message: Smoke extraction fan design temperature below 200°C. Smoke extraction fans must be rated to EN 12101-3 temperature class minimum F200 (200°C for 120 min) for life-safety fire scenarios. Ambient-rated fans are not suitable. Respecify with certified F200/F300/F400 fan.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: BS EN 12101-3:2015 Cl.5; NBC 2016 Part 4 (fire)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Smoke Extraction Fan' AND standard AND !'EN 12101' IN standard
compliance_rules.message: Smoke extraction fan must comply with EN 12101-3 (or equivalent national standard). Factory type test and CE marking required before installation. Non-certified fans are not permitted for life-safety smoke control applications.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: BS EN 12101-3:2015; NFPA 92 Cl.5.3; NBC 2016 Part 4

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Smoke Extraction Fan' AND speed_control AND 'VFD' IN speed_control AND !'fail-safe' IN speed_control
compliance_rules.message: VFD on smoke extraction fan: EN 12101-3 requires the fan to reach full design speed and flow within 60 seconds of activation. A VFD is only permissible if it has a hard-wired fail-safe bypass that switches to DOL on fire signal. Confirm control scheme.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'High Pressure Blower' AND diff_pressure_kpa > 150
compliance_rules.message: Blower differential pressure above 150 kPa: at this pressure ratio a standard Roots or single-stage centrifugal blower approaches its limit. Consider specifying a 2-stage blower, screw blower, or reciprocating compressor for differential pressures above 150 kPa.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 619 Ed.4 Cl.3.1.2; ISO 13709; blower manufacturer limits

compliance_rules.condition_expr: gas_type AND 'Biogas' IN gas_type AND motor_enclosure AND 'IP55' IN motor_enclosure
compliance_rules.message: Biogas is a flammable gas (typically 50–70% CH₄). Standard IP55 TEFC motor is not ATEX-rated and is not suitable for Zone 1 or Zone 2 biogas environments. Specify Ex d IIC or Ex e IIC ATEX-certified motor.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079-10-1; ATEX Directive 2014/34/EU; IS 5572

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Crude oil transfer pump' AND oil_viscosity_cst > 500
compliance_rules.message: Crude oil viscosity above 500 cSt: heating system required to maintain pumpability. Pump casing jacket heating or suction line trace heating is mandatory. Viscosity correction per HI 9.6.7 will significantly reduce head and efficiency from water-based published curves.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HI 9.6.7 (viscosity effects); API RP 11S6; IS 5639

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Crude oil transfer pump' OR equipment_subtype == 'Oil pumping station') AND moc AND 'CS' IN moc AND oil_viscosity_cst > 0
compliance_rules.message: CS casing for crude oil pump: verify H₂S content of crude. For sour crude (H₂S > 0.0003 mol fraction), NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156 applies — CS must meet hardness limits (HRC ≤22). Sour crude with high Cl⁻: specify SS316 or Duplex SS.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156; API 610 Ed.12 Table 1

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Axial Flow Fan (Cooling Tower)' AND speed_control AND 'Fixed speed' IN speed_control
compliance_rules.message: Fixed-speed cooling tower fan: consider adjustable-pitch blade control or VFD for energy optimisation. Cooling towers run at varying loads throughout the year — pitch control saves 15–40% fan power and avoids recirculation / freeze risk in cold climates. Per CTI ATC-105 and ASHRAE 90.1.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CTI ATC-105; ASHRAE 90.1 Cl.6.5.4; IS 11329

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pump shaft alignment (laser)' AND pump_size_class AND 'Very large' IN pump_size_class
compliance_rules.message: Large pump laser alignment: hot alignment check is recommended in addition to cold alignment. Thermal growth of large pump casings and drivers can shift alignment by 0.1–0.5 mm at operating temperature. Per API 686 (recommended practice for machinery installation).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 686 Ed.2 Cl.6.9; ISO 10816-3 (vibration limits)

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: Equipment: base_cost(equipment_subtype, motor_power, fan_flow_rate, gas_flow_rate, crude_flow_rate, diff_pressure_pa, diff_pressure_kpa) × moc_factor(moc) × impeller_factor(impeller_type) × quantity; Activity: labour_rate_usd_per_manhour × activity_manhours × size_factor(pump_size_class) × quantity
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: Equipment: base_cost × moc_factor × impeller_factor × temperature_factor(design_temperature) × standard_factor(standard) × enclosure_factor(motor_enclosure) × quantity; Activity: labour_rate × activity_manhours × size_factor × piping_scope_factor(piping_scope) × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: Equipment: base_cost × moc_factor × impeller_factor × temperature_factor × standard_factor × enclosure_factor × speed_control_factor(speed_control) × viscosity_factor(oil_viscosity_cst) × station_scope_factor(station_scope) × quantity × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency); Activity: labour_rate × activity_manhours × size_factor × piping_factor × quantity

adders.note: Fans and blowers: erection/installation 15% covers crane lift, ductwork/stack connections, electrical termination, commissioning. Cooling tower fans: erection 8% (simple bolt-in). Crude pump: 18–20% (baseplate grouting, piping, seal commissioning). Activity items: no freight/installation adder — already man-hour based. Contingency 12% AACE Class 4 ±20%.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: Switched ENR->CEPCI: procured/manufactured equipment, supply-cost dominated. verify=true

usd_cost_baseline.note: USD supply-only unit costs (ex-works, 2024 basis). Sources: Kruger/Nicotra (cooling tower fans), Howden/Flakt (smoke fans), Aerzen/Sullair/Kaeser (blowers), KSB/Flowserve (crude pumps). Activity items: USD per man-hour (India site, 2024 rates). ±30% Tier 1.

Axial Flow Fan (Cooling Tower).basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 12000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 16238.81. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Smoke Extraction Fan.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 12000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 16238.81. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

High Pressure Blower.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 14000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 18945.27. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Crude oil transfer pump.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Oil pumping station.basis: Reference = 75 kW crude pump × scope multiplier. Accuracy ±40%.

Trial run of pump.basis: Skilled millwright / fitter, India site rate 2024.

Pump shaft alignment (laser).basis: Precision alignment specialist with laser equipment, India 2024.

Pump grouting.basis: Civil / mechanical team for baseplate grouting, India 2024.

Pump base frame installation.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 4500 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 5725.45. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Bearing installation (pump).basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 4500 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 5725.45. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Pump vibration testing.basis: Vibration specialist + analyser equipment. Includes report.

Pump house piping.basis: Pipefitter / welder for pump house interconnecting piping fabrication and erection.

temperature_factor.note: High-temperature fan/blower cost multiplier. Normalised at ≤80°C = 1.0.

standard_factor.note: EN 12101-3 premium captures type test + certification + CE marking cost.

speed_control_factor.note: Adjustable pitch adds to fan cost but saves energy over lifecycle

pump_size_class_factor.note: Activity man-hour scaling factor. Relative to Medium = 1.0.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only.
Indicative only.
Indicative only.
Indicative only.
Indicative only.
Indicative only.
Indicative only.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ

SCHEMA-PUMP-CENTRIFUGAL.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs resolved. template_name / discipline_group corrected from 'Pumps & Compressors' to 'Centrifugal Pumps & Blowers'. Defects corrected: (1) Volume F-004 (18–15000 m³, same values as Flow Rate) replaced → Differential Head (m); (2) Duplicate flow_rate spec_key (F-003 m³/hr + F-010 Nm³/hr) resolved → F-010 renamed gas_flow_rate (conditional on blower/compressor); (3) LV voltage F-005 + HV voltage F-011 merged → single dropdown; (4) Design Temperature range 95–105°C widened to -20–350°C; (5) Design Pressure 12–35 bar(g) widened to 1–400 bar(g); (6) Motor Power max 850 kW increased to 10,000 kW; (7) Speed range widened to 400–10,000 RPM; (8) Efficiency renamed Pump Hydraulic Efficiency with context; (9) API 617 (centrifugal compressor standard — wrong) retained only for centrifugal compressor sub-type, not centrifugal pumps; (10) Enclosure expanded beyond IP55 only; (11) Fuel Type made conditional on diesel fire pump; (12) Service/activity sub-types (installation, overhaul) separated with labour-unit fields. Engineering basis: API 610 (centrifugal process pumps), API 617 (centrifugal compressors), IS 1520, NFPA 20 (fire pumps), HEI standards, IS 9137. USD baselines 2024 (KSB, Flowserve, Sulzer, Kirloskar, Grundfos, Mather & Platt, Ruhrpumpen). | Review pass v1.1 (2026-04-13): (A) Added Fluid Type field (liquid pumps) and Gas Type field (blower/compressor); (B) Added Specific Gravity / Fluid Density field for pump power/NPSH calculations; (C) Shaft seal type (F-009) restricted to liquid pumps only — blower/compressor now use separate Compressor Seal / Shaft Sealing field; (D) Differential Head (TDH) restricted to liquid pumps — blower/compressor use Differential Pressure (bar / kPa) instead; (E) NPSH already correctly pump-only — confirmed and note added. | Review pass v1.2 (2026-04-13): (F) LRVP removed from gas_flow_rate conditional — LRVP is liquid-ring, not a blower; (G) Vacuum Level field (mbar(a)) added, conditional on LRVP only; (H) fluid_type hint updated with per-sub-type default guidance; (I) Compliance rule R-016 added for LRVP deep vacuum limit.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick ±30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget ±20%. Tier 3 = Detailed ±10%.

SCHEMA-PUMP-CENTRIFUGAL.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | {flow_rate} m³/h | {diff_head_m} m | {gas_flow_rate} Nm³/h | {diff_pressure_kpa} kPa | {motor_power} kW | {design_pressure} bar(g) | Fluid: {fluid_type} | SG: {specific_gravity} | MOC: {moc} | {seal_type} | {standard} | Qty: {quantity} No.
SCHEMA-PUMP-CENTRIFUGAL.spec_example: Centrifugal Process Pump | 75 m³/h | 80 m | 22 kW | 16 bar(g) | Fluid: Hydrocarbon liquid (light) | SG: 0.85 | MOC: SS316 | Double mech seal Plan 53A | API 610 BB2 | Qty: 2 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select pump type or service activity. Supply sub-types: process pump, BFP, CW, condensate, fire, jockey, LRVP, blower. Activity sub-types (labour-based): installation & alignment, dismantling, overhauling — use man-hours or lump-sum field.

fields.label: Rated Flow Rate
fields.hint: Design point flow rate at rated conditions. Process pumps: 1–5000 m³/h. BFP: 20–500 m³/h. CW pumps: 500–50,000 m³/h. Fire pumps: per NFPA 20 / TAC design. Jockey pumps: 1–5 m³/h. LRVP: 10–500 m³/h seal liquid. Centrifugal pumps are rated at their best efficiency point (BEP); specify minimum and maximum continuous flow (MCF) to vendor.

fields.label: Vacuum Level (Suction Pressure)
fields.hint: Absolute suction pressure (vacuum level) at LRVP inlet. Expressed in millibar absolute (mbar(a)): atmospheric = 1013 mbar(a); 50 mbar(a) ≈ 50 mmHgA (high vacuum); 200 mbar(a) ≈ 150 mmHgA (moderate vacuum). LRVP practical vacuum range: 33–980 mbar(a) (25–735 mmHgA). Deep vacuum < 50 mbar(a): consider 2-stage LRVP. Seal liquid (usually water) temperature limits achievable vacuum — at 15°C CW, water vapour pressure ≈ 17 mbar, limiting vacuum to ~30 mbar(a). Per HEI / Nash Engineering / Atlas Copco LRVP selection charts.

fields.label: Differential Head (TDH)
fields.hint: Total Differential Head (TDH) at rated flow. Process pumps: 10–300 m. BFP: 500–2500 m. CW: 20–50 m. CEP: 100–600 m. Fire pumps: 50–120 m. Jockey: 60–100 m. LRVP: 5–30 m. Head (m) = ΔP (bar) × 10.2 / SG_fluid. For blowers and gas compressors use Differential Pressure field instead — head in metres of liquid column is not meaningful for gas-phase equipment.

fields.label: Pumped Fluid Type
fields.hint: Select the pumped liquid. Drives MOC, seal plan, and NPSH margin checks. Sub-type defaults: BFP → 'Water (hot >60°C / boiler feed / condensate)'; CW pump → 'Cooling water (CW, treated)'; CEP → 'Water (hot >60°C / boiler feed / condensate)'; Fire/Jockey → 'Firewater (untreated / raw water)'; LRVP → seal liquid, usually 'Water (clean, ambient temperature)'. For process pumps: select actual process fluid — drives MOC and seal compliance checks. Flammable hydrocarbons: API 610 + double seal Plan 53A required. Seawater: duplex SS or titanium. Acids/caustic: Hastelloy or lined.

fields.label: Fluid Specific Gravity (at pumping temperature)
fields.hint: Specific gravity (SG) = fluid density / water density at pumping temperature. Required for: shaft power calc P(kW) = Q(m³/h) × H(m) × SG / (367 × η); NPSHa margin check; and pressure rating verification. Reference values: water = 1.0; seawater ≈ 1.025; hot condensate at 100°C ≈ 0.958; light crude ≈ 0.82–0.87; heavy crude ≈ 0.92–0.97; H₂SO₄ 98% = 1.84; caustic 50% = 1.52; liquid NH₃ at -15°C ≈ 0.68. Low SG (< 0.7): requires NPSH margin review — cavitation risk increases. High SG (> 1.2): motor power increases proportionally — recheck motor selection.

fields.label: Gas / Air Flow Rate
fields.hint: Gas/air volumetric flow at Normal conditions (0°C, 1.013 bar(a)). Centrifugal blowers: 500–100,000 Nm³/h. Process gas centrifugal compressors: 5,000–500,000 Nm³/h. Not applicable to Liquid Ring Vacuum Pump — use the Vacuum Level field for LRVP gas suction capacity (expressed in m³/h at suction conditions).

fields.label: Gas / Process Fluid Type
fields.hint: Gas being compressed or conveyed. Determines MOC, seal type, and ATEX motor classification. Air: CI/CS casing standard. Natural gas / H₂ / hydrocarbon: flammable — ATEX Zone 1 or Zone 2 motor, dry gas seals mandatory for process gas compressors. NH₃ vapour: SS316 casing, ASHRAE 15 machinery room requirements. Cl₂: Hastelloy / specialist vendor — extreme hazard, consult specialist. H₂: NACE MR0175 hardness limits, H₂ detector mandatory.

fields.label: Differential Pressure (Blower / Compressor)
fields.hint: Differential pressure across the blower or compressor (discharge − suction). Centrifugal blowers (low pressure fans): 1–100 kPa (0.01–1.0 bar). High-pressure blowers: 100–500 kPa. Process gas centrifugal compressors: 0.5–50 bar (500–50,000 kPa). Note: for gas machines, 'head' in metres of liquid column is meaningless — always use pressure units. Pressure ratio = (P_discharge + 101.325) / (P_suction + 101.325). Single-stage centrifugal: max pressure ratio ≈ 3–4:1 (polytropic).

fields.label: Motor / Driver Power
fields.hint: Installed motor nameplate power. Select next standard IE3 frame above calculated shaft power with 10–15% service factor. Standard sizes: 0.37, 0.55, 0.75, 1.1, 1.5, 2.2, 3, 4, 5.5, 7.5, 11, 15, 18.5, 22, 30, 37, 45, 55, 75, 90, 110, 132, 160, 200, 250, 315, 355, 400, 500, 630, 800, 1000, 1250, 1600, 2000 kW. Fire pump diesel drive: rate engine BHP not kW.

fields.label: Design Pressure (Casing)
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure of the pump casing. Process pumps: 16–40 bar(g) (API 610 Class 150–300). BFP: 100–300 bar(g) (drum pressure + 20% margin). CW / fire / jockey: 6–16 bar(g). CEP: 10–40 bar(g). LRVP: 1–4 bar(g). API 610 casing flanges: ASME B16.5 Class 150 (20 bar), Class 300 (51 bar), Class 600 (103 bar), Class 900 (154 bar).

fields.label: Design Temperature (Fluid)
fields.hint: Maximum fluid temperature at pump inlet. Cold water / CW service: 5–40°C. Hot water / condensate: 60–110°C. BFP: 120–200°C (feed water at economiser inlet). High-temperature process: 200–350°C (requires API 610 hot casing). Temperature governs seal selection (API 682), bearing type, and whether single or double mechanical seals are needed.

fields.label: MOC (Casing / Impeller / Wetted Parts)
options: SS304 - dilute acids / food / mild corrosive
Hastelloy C-276 - highly corrosive / HCl / H₂SO₄
fields.hint: Casing / impeller material. CI: standard for water, cooling tower, firewater (non-corrosive). CS: hydrocarbon process pumps. SS316: process / mild corrosive / coastal firewater. Duplex/Super duplex: chloride-containing, offshore, seawater injection. Per API 610 Table 1 (material classes A, B, C, S, D).

fields.label: Shaft Seal Type (Liquid Pump)
options: Single mechanical seal (API Plan 11 - flush from discharge)
Single mechanical seal (API Plan 13 - flush to suction, hot service)
Single mechanical seal (API Plan 23 - cooled flush, >150°C)
Double mechanical seal (API Plan 52 - unpressurised buffer)
Double mechanical seal (API Plan 53A - pressurised barrier N₂)
Double mechanical seal (API Plan 53B - pressurised barrier bladder)
Sealless / Canned motor (no seal, magnetic coupling)
fields.default: Single mechanical seal (API Plan 11 - flush from discharge)
fields.hint: Shaft seal arrangement for liquid pump per API 682 / API 610. Plan 11: standard process service ≤150°C, non-flashing. Plan 23: mandatory for BFP / hot condensate >150°C — closed-loop cooled flush. Plan 53A/B: required for flammable, toxic or carcinogenic service. Gland packing: utility water / jockey pump only. Not applicable to blowers or gas compressors — see Compressor Seal field.

fields.label: Shaft Sealing (Blower / Compressor)
options: Labyrinth seal (air / clean gas, no leakage control needed)
Dry gas seal – single (API 617, process gas)
Dry gas seal – tandem with N₂ buffer (API 617, flammable gas)
Dry gas seal – double with separation gas (toxic / carcinogenic gas)
Wet seal (oil film seal, older design — centrifugal compressors)
fields.default: Labyrinth seal (air / clean gas, no leakage control needed)
fields.hint: Shaft sealing system for blowers and centrifugal compressors. Labyrinth: standard for air blowers and non-hazardous gas — allows controlled leakage. Dry gas seal (API 617): mandatory for flammable, toxic or hazardous process gas compressors. Tandem dry gas seal with N₂ buffer: required by API 617 for hydrocarbon/H₂ service. Double dry gas seal: for toxic/carcinogenic service — zero fugitive emissions. Seal gas (N₂) supply system required for dry gas seals — confirm utility availability.

fields.label: Pump Hydraulic Efficiency
fields.hint: Pump hydraulic efficiency at BEP. Used to calculate shaft power: P_shaft (kW) = Q(m³/h) × H(m) × SG / (367 × η_pump). Typical centrifugal pump BEP efficiencies: small (<5 kW): 50–65%; medium (5–50 kW): 65–78%; large (>50 kW): 78–88%; BFP barrel type: 82–87%. Higher efficiency → smaller motor → lower operating cost. Minimum efficiency values per IS 9137 / HEI / Europump.

fields.label: Rated Speed
fields.hint: Pump rated speed. Common 50 Hz motor synchronous speeds: 2-pole: 2950 RPM; 4-pole: 1450 RPM; 6-pole: 960 RPM; 8-pole: 730 RPM. BFP / high-speed: 3000–5000 RPM (via gearbox or VFD). Centrifugal compressors: 3000–15,000 RPM (gearbox mandatory). Higher speed → smaller pump diameter → lower cost but higher NPSH required. Speed affects impeller tip velocity and allowable SG (API 610 Annex F).

fields.label: Motor Supply Voltage
options: 6.6 kV, 3-phase, 50 Hz (HV - India standard for >200 kW)
11 kV, 3-phase, 50 Hz (HV - very large pumps >1000 kW)
fields.hint: Motor supply voltage and phase/frequency. 415 V LV: standard up to ~200 kW for Indian projects. 6.6 kV HV: preferred for large CW pumps, BFP motors >200 kW. Voltage selection affects switchgear, cable sizing, and starter cost. Large fire pump motors: check compatibility with diesel engine starter system.

fields.label: Motor Enclosure / Protection
options: Ex e IIC (increased safety - Zone 1 ATEX)
TEWAC (Totally Enclosed Water & Air Cooled) - large motors
fields.hint: Motor enclosure per IEC 60034-5 (IP rating) / IEC 60079 (ATEX). IP55: standard for outdoor/splash-proof. Ex d (flameproof): required in Zone 1 classified areas (flammable liquid / gas present under normal operation). Ex nA: Zone 2 areas (gas present only under abnormal conditions). TEWAC: for large motors >1 MW in confined spaces.

fields.label: Design Standard
options: API 610 (Centrifugal pumps for petroleum service — OH, BB, VS types)
HEI Standards (Condensate extraction / boiler feed pumps)
ISO 9905 / ISO 5199 (Chemical pumps — Class I, II, III)
fields.default: API 610 (Centrifugal pumps for petroleum service — OH, BB, VS types)
fields.hint: Governing design and test standard. API 610: mandatory for refinery/petrochemical process pumps. API 617: centrifugal compressors ONLY — do not apply to pumps. NFPA 20: fire pump systems — factory acceptance test mandatory. IS 1520 / IS 9137: Indian general-service centrifugal pumps. HEI: boiler feed and condensate extraction pump standards. API 610 adds 15–25% to cost over IS standard.

fields.label: Engine Fuel Type
fields.hint: Engine fuel type for diesel-engine-driven fire pump. Diesel (HSD): most common; NFPA 20 requires a separate day tank (minimum 1 hour fuel supply at rated load). Engine must be listed/approved for fire pump service per NFPA 20 / UL 1247. Natural gas: only where gas supply is reliable and pressure-regulated; not preferred per NFPA 20 as gas can fail in a fire event.

fields.label: NPSHa (Available NPSH)
fields.hint: Net Positive Suction Head available at pump suction flange. NPSHa must exceed NPSHr (required, from pump curve) by minimum 0.5 m (IS 9137) or 0.6 m (API 610 — 3% head drop criterion). Low NPSHa (<3 m): specify low-NPSH impeller or consider double-suction design. CEP / BFP: NPSHa critically governs suction drum level and min condensate/feedwater leg height.

fields.label: Number of Stages
options: Single stage (end suction OH / BB type)
Single stage double suction (BB1 / BB2 — high flow)
fields.default: Single stage (end suction OH / BB type)
fields.hint: Number of impeller stages. Single-stage: most process pumps, CW, fire, jockey — up to ~250 m head. Multi-stage: BFP (500–2500 m), high-pressure injection, CEP. Double-suction single stage: large CW pumps, low NPSHr. More stages → higher CAPEX but smaller casing diameter. API 610 types: OH (overhung), BB (between-bearing), VS (vertical).

fields.label: Activity Scope (Man-Hours)
fields.hint: Estimated man-hours for the installation, alignment, dismantling, or overhauling activity. Installation & alignment (small pump <15 kW): 8–16 man-hours. Installation & alignment (large pump >75 kW): 24–48 man-hours. Dismantling: 50–70% of installation man-hours. Overhauling (impeller, seal, bearing replacement): 12–32 man-hours. Multiplied by local labour rate to give activity cost.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of pump units or activity occurrences. For supply: include operating + standby (typical 2 × 100% for critical). For activities: number of pumps to be installed / overhauled / dismantled. Fire pump set: count electric + diesel + jockey as separate line items.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Boiler Feed Pump' AND design_pressure < 80
compliance_rules.message: Boiler feed pump design pressure appears too low. BFP casing design pressure must exceed drum operating pressure by ≥20% plus the pump shutoff head. Typical: drum at 40 bar → BFP casing ≥80 bar(g). Review drum pressure and confirm BFP casing class.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HEI Standards for BFP; API 610 Ed.12 Cl.6.1.3; ASME B16.5

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Boiler Feed Pump' AND seal_type AND !'Plan 23' IN seal_type
compliance_rules.message: BFP/CEP hot service (>120°C): API Plan 11 flush from pump discharge will flash in the seal chamber. API Plan 23 (closed-loop cooled flush) is mandatory for boiler feed water pumps to prevent seal face flashing.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 682 Ed.4 Annex G; API 610 Cl.6.8.3

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Fire Water Pump (Electric)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Fire Water Pump (Diesel Engine)') AND standard AND !'NFPA 20' IN standard AND !'IS 14751' IN standard
compliance_rules.message: Fire water pumps must comply with NFPA 20 (international) or IS 14751 (India). Standard process pump specifications are not sufficient — fire pumps require factory acceptance test, listed/approved status, and mandatory weekly auto-start testing.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NFPA 20-2019; IS 14751; TAC Rules (India)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc AND ('Cast Iron' IN moc OR 'CS' IN moc) AND design_temperature > 200
compliance_rules.message: Cast iron or CS pump at >200°C: CI is brittle at high temperature differentials (thermal shock). CS loses creep resistance above 370°C. Use SS316 or alloy-steel casing for process temperatures above 200°C.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 610 Table 1 material class limits; ASME B16.34

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Process Gas Compressor (Centrifugal)' AND standard AND 'API 610' IN standard
compliance_rules.message: API 610 applies to centrifugal PUMPS, not compressors. For Process Gas Compressor (Centrifugal), the correct standard is API 617. Please update the standard selection.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 610 Ed.12 Scope (liquids only); API 617 (gas compressors)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: npsha_m IS not None AND npsha_m < 2.0
compliance_rules.message: NPSHa below 2.0 m is very low. Risk of cavitation is high. Confirm NPSHr from pump vendor at rated flow and specify NPSHa / NPSHr margin ≥0.6 m per API 610. Consider double-suction impeller, reduced speed, or suction vessel elevation.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 610 Ed.12 Cl.6.1.9; IS 9137 NPSH margin requirement

compliance_rules.condition_expr: motor_power > 200 AND voltage AND '415' IN voltage
compliance_rules.message: Motor power >200 kW on 415 V LV supply: full-load current ~350 A. Specify 6.6 kV or 11 kV HV motor to reduce cable sizing and switchgear costs. LV at this rating requires oversized busbars.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 12615 (IE3 motors); IEC 60034-30-1; IEEE 841

compliance_rules.condition_expr: seal_type AND 'Gland packing' IN seal_type AND design_temperature > 80
compliance_rules.message: Gland packing not recommended for fluid temperatures above 80°C. Packing leaks to atmosphere, creating steam/hot fluid hazard and environmental concerns. Upgrade to mechanical seal for hot service above 80°C.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 682 Ed.4 Cl.1.1; IS 5727 (packing limits)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: diff_head_m > 250 AND num_stages AND 'Single stage' IN num_stages
compliance_rules.message: Head above 250 m with single-stage pump: verify with vendor that single-stage is achievable at this head. Most standard centrifugal pumps are limited to ~200–250 m per stage. Consider multi-stage design for heads above 250 m.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 610 BB2/BB4 type selection; HI pump handbook Cl.2.4

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Fire Water Pump (Diesel Engine)' AND fuel_type AND 'Natural gas' IN fuel_type
compliance_rules.message: Natural gas as fire pump driver fuel: NFPA 20 Cl.12.7.2 restricts gas-engine drives — gas supply pressure must be fully reliable and failure of gas supply cannot be caused by the fire. Diesel is strongly preferred for reliability in fire scenarios.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: fluid_type AND 'Hydrocarbon' IN fluid_type AND seal_type AND 'Gland packing' IN seal_type
compliance_rules.message: Gland packing is not acceptable for hydrocarbon liquid service. Hydrocarbons leak to atmosphere creating fire and vapour hazard. Specify minimum single mechanical seal (API Plan 11). For light hydrocarbons (LPG, naphtha): double seal Plan 53A required.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 610 Ed.12 Cl.6.8; API 682 Ed.4 Cl.1.1; OISD 118

compliance_rules.condition_expr: specific_gravity IS not None AND specific_gravity < 0.7 AND npsha_m < 5
compliance_rules.message: Low SG fluid (< 0.7) with low NPSHa (< 5 m): high cavitation risk. Light hydrocarbons, LPG, liquid ammonia have high vapour pressures. NPSHa must account for vapour pressure reduction: NPSHa_effective = NPSHa − (Pvap at pump suction temperature / ρg). Increase available NPSHa or specify low-NPSH impeller. Per API 610 Annex F.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 610 Ed.12 Annex F; HI 9.6.1 NPSH margin

compliance_rules.condition_expr: specific_gravity IS not None AND specific_gravity > 1.2
compliance_rules.message: Fluid SG above 1.2: shaft power increases proportionally with SG. Confirm motor power was calculated with actual SG, not water (SG=1.0). Error: if motor sized at SG=1.0, actual power = P_rated × actual_SG — motor may be undersized. Recheck motor selection.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HI 1.3 Pump power equation; API 610 Cl.6.2.4

compliance_rules.condition_expr: gas_type AND ('Natural gas' IN gas_type OR 'Hydrogen' IN gas_type OR 'Hydrocarbon gas' IN gas_type) AND comp_seal_type AND 'Labyrinth' IN comp_seal_type
compliance_rules.message: Labyrinth seal selected for flammable gas (natural gas / H₂ / HC gas) compressor. Labyrinth seals allow continuous gas leakage to atmosphere — unacceptable for flammable gas service. API 617 requires dry gas seals (tandem with N₂ buffer) for all flammable, toxic, or hazardous process gas centrifugal compressors.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 617 Ed.8 Cl.2.10; API 692; OISD 118

compliance_rules.condition_expr: fluid_type AND 'Cryogenic' IN fluid_type AND moc AND !'SS316' IN moc AND !'Duplex' IN moc AND !'Hastelloy' IN moc AND !'Titanium' IN moc
compliance_rules.message: Cryogenic service selected but MOC appears to be a carbon/ferritic material. Cryogenic liquids (LN₂, LO₂, LNG, below -46°C) require austenitic SS or other ductile materials certified for low-temperature toughness. CS and CI become brittle below -29°C. Specify SS316 or SS304 minimum.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Cl.323.2; API 610 Ed.12 Table 1 low-temp limits

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Liquid Ring Vacuum Pump' AND vacuum_level_mbara < 33
compliance_rules.message: Vacuum below 33 mbar(a) is at the practical limit for single-stage liquid ring vacuum pumps. Below this level (25 mmHgA), seal liquid vapour pressure limits performance. Specify a 2-stage LRVP or consider a steam ejector + LRVP hybrid system for vacuum levels below 25 mmHgA.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HEI Standards for LRVP; Nash Engineering selection guide; API 681

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_cost(equipment_subtype, motor_power, flow_rate, gas_flow_rate, diff_head_m, refrigeration_capacity) × moc_factor(moc) × seal_factor(seal_type) × quantity   [activity items: labour_rate × activity_manhours × quantity]
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_cost × moc_factor × seal_factor × pressure_factor(design_pressure) × temp_factor(design_temperature) × standard_factor(standard) × enclosure_factor(enclosure) × stages_factor(num_stages) × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_cost × moc_factor × seal_factor × pressure_factor × temp_factor × standard_factor × enclosure_factor × stages_factor × quantity × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

adders.note: Centrifugal pumps: erection/installation 18% covers skid landing, baseplate grouting, pipework connections, coupling alignment, electrical terminations, and mechanical running test. Freight 6% (medium-weight fabricated equipment). Fire pump sets: use 20–25% erection — includes controller panel, weekly auto-start test commissioning. Contingency 12% AACE Class 4 ±20%.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ

usd_cost_baseline.note: USD supply-only unit costs (ex-works, 2024 basis). Pump + motor + baseframe + coupling + standard mech seal. Activity items: USD per man-hour (labour rate reference). Sources: KSB, Flowserve, Sulzer, Grundfos, Kirloskar, Mather & Platt (fire), Ruhrpumpen (BFP). ±30% Tier 1.

Centrifugal Process Pump.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Centrifugal Process Pump (High Capacity).basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Boiler Feed Pump.basis: P&T-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 850000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 1150248.76. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Cooling Water Circulation Pump.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Condensate Extraction Pump.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Fire Water Pump (Electric).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 1200 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 1623.88. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Fire Water Pump (Diesel Engine).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 1200 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 1623.88. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Jockey Pump.basis: Small centrifugal + motor + pressure switch, CI or CI/SS impeller. 0.5–2.2 kW.

Liquid Ring Vacuum Pump.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Centrifugal Blower (Process).basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 14000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 18945.27. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Process Gas Compressor (Centrifugal).basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 85000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 115024.88. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Centrifugal pump – supply.note: Use base rates from 'Centrifugal Process Pump' with appropriate moc_factor and seal_factor.
Centrifugal pump – supply.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 9000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 12179.1. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Centrifugal pump – installation & alignm.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 9000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 12179.1. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Centrifugal pump dismantling.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 9000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 12179.1. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Centrifugal pump overhauling.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 9000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 12179.1. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Cooling water pump installation.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 4500 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 5725.45. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Fire water pump installation.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 4500 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 5725.45. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

seal_type_factor.note: Relative to single mech seal Plan 11 = 1.0

pressure_class_factor.note: Casing flange class / pressure rating. Normalised at ASME 150 = 1.0.

design_temperature_factor.note: High-temp variant cost (special seals, bearings, hot casing). Normalised at ≤120°C = 1.0.

standard_factor.note: API 610 adds documentation, inspection, and witness test premium.

enclosure_factor.note: Applied to motor cost (~25–35% of pump unit total cost)

stages_factor.note: Relative to single-stage = 1.0. Multistage barrel type has highest multiplier.

SCHEMA-PUMP-COMPRESSOR.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs resolved. template_name / discipline_group corrected from 'Pumps & Compressors' to 'Compressors & Gas Pumps'. Defects corrected: Volume (m³) renamed → Air Receiver Volume (conditional on air compressors); two duplicate voltage fields (F-005 LV + F-007 HV) merged into single dropdown; Flow Rate min=max=2000 Nm³/hr widened to real range 10–100,000 Nm³/hr; Design Temperature 10–35°C reinterpreted as Ambient / Inlet Air Temperature; Standard field mapped per sub-type; MOC expanded from SS316-only. New fields added: gas type, number of stages, cooling method, air quality class, refrigeration capacity, pump differential head. Engineering basis: API 618, API 619, ISO 1217, ISO 8573, ISO 5149, ASHRAE 15, IS 5456, IS 3816. USD baselines 2024 (Atlas Copco, Ingersoll Rand, Dresser-Rand, Mayekawa, Ariel, HMT / ISGEC Indian fabricators).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick ±30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget ±20%. Tier 3 = Detailed ±10%.

SCHEMA-PUMP-COMPRESSOR.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | {flow_rate} Nm³/h | {design_pressure} bar(g) | {motor_power} kW | Gas: {gas_type} | {num_stages}-stage | {cooling_method} | MOC: {moc} | {standard} | Qty: {quantity} No.
SCHEMA-PUMP-COMPRESSOR.spec_example: Air Compressor (Oil-Free Screw) | 3000 Nm³/h | 8 bar(g) | 132 kW | Gas: Air | 2-stage | Water-cooled | MOC: CI/CS | ISO 1217 Ed.4 | Qty: 2 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select compressor or gas pump type. Oil-injected screw: general plant air, instrument air (post-dryer). Oil-free screw: pharmaceutical, food, critical instrument air (ISO 8573-1 Class 0). Reciprocating HP: process gas, CNG, hydrogen service to 350 bar(g). Booster: takes existing compressed supply and boosts to higher pressure. Refrigeration (Ammonia): industrial refrigeration, cold storage, ammonia plant. Reciprocating pump: high-pressure supply/transfer pump. Ammonia pump: liquid ammonia circulation in refrigeration systems.

fields.label: Gas / Fluid Type
fields.hint: Gas or fluid being compressed/pumped. Determines MOC, seal type, motor enclosure (ATEX), and safety systems. H₂: requires NACE-compliant materials, special seals, H₂ detector. NH₃: requires SS316 or higher, ASHRAE 15 machinery room requirements. Chlorine: requires dry-gas seal, Hastelloy, extreme care — consult specialist vendor only. Flammable gases: ATEX Zone 1/2 motor mandatory.

fields.label: Rated Flow Rate (Compressor) / Capacity
fields.hint: Compressor rated capacity at Normal conditions (0°C, 1.01325 bar(a)). Typical ranges: small screw 50–500 Nm³/h; medium screw 500–5000 Nm³/h; large screw 5000–50,000 Nm³/h. Reciprocating HP: 10–2000 Nm³/h. Booster: typically 100–5000 Nm³/h. For pumps use m³/h field below — enter 0 here.

fields.label: Pump Flow Rate
fields.hint: Volumetric flow rate for reciprocating supply pump or ammonia liquid pump. Ammonia refrigerant pumps: typically 5–200 m³/h liquid NH₃. Reciprocating HP supply pumps: 1–100 m³/h depending on duty. Flow × head determines shaft power: P(kW) = Q(m³/h) × H(m) × SG / (367 × η).

fields.label: Discharge / Design Pressure
fields.hint: Discharge pressure at compressor outlet (or pump design pressure). Screw air compressors: 7–13 bar(g) standard; 25 bar(g) high-pressure. Booster: inlet + boost pressure; typically 25–100 bar(g) outlet. Reciprocating HP: 40–350 bar(g) for CNG/H₂ service. Refrigeration (NH₃): condensing pressure typically 10–20 bar(g). Ammonia pump: 10–30 bar(g). Pressure ratio = (P_discharge + 1.013) / (P_suction + 1.013). Each compression stage handles ratio ≤4:1 typically.

fields.label: Motor / Driver Power
fields.hint: Installed motor / driver nameplate power. Standard IE3 HV ratings: 45, 55, 75, 90, 110, 132, 160, 200, 250, 315, 355, 400, 500, 630, 800, 1000 kW. Apply 10–15% service factor over calculated shaft power. Reciprocating HP compressors >200 kW: consider HV motor (6.6/11 kV). VFD (variable frequency drive) option adds 8–12% to motor cost but saves 15–30% energy at part load — standard on modern screw compressors.

fields.label: Number of Compression Stages
fields.hint: Number of compression stages. Each stage compresses to a ratio ~3:1–4:1. Air screw to 8 bar(g): single-stage or 2-stage. Air screw to 13 bar(g): 2-stage. Reciprocating to 40 bar(g): 3-stage; to 200 bar(g): 4-stage. CNG/H₂ to 250 bar(g): 4–5-stage. More stages = higher capital cost but lower discharge temperature per stage (intercooling reduces work input).

fields.label: Cooling Method
fields.hint: Cooling method for compressor body and intercoolers/aftercoolers. Air-cooled: simplest, no cooling water needed — standard for small/medium screw compressors ≤200 kW. Water-cooled: better thermal efficiency, required for large units >200 kW and reciprocating HP. Needs cooling water supply (CW or closed-loop). Cooler type significantly affects site utility requirements and overall package dimensions / weight.

fields.label: Ambient / Inlet Temperature
fields.hint: Site ambient temperature (for air compressors) or suction gas temperature (for process gas compressors). Compressor FAD (free air delivery) and shaft power are rated at ISO 1217 conditions (20°C, 1 bar, 0% RH). Higher ambient reduces volumetric efficiency and increases discharge temperature. Tropical / Middle East sites use 45–50°C design. Reciprocating pump: fluid temperature at pump inlet.

fields.label: Motor Supply Voltage
options: 6.6 kV, 3-phase, 50 Hz (HV - motors >200 kW, India standard)
fields.hint: Motor supply voltage. LV (≤1000 V): standard for motors up to ~200 kW. HV (>1000 V): cost-effective for large compressors (>200 kW) — reduces cable size and switchgear cost. 6.6 kV is the most common HV selection for Indian industrial projects. Requires HV switchgear (VCB), HV cable, and separate transformer tap.

fields.label: Air Receiver / Buffer Vessel Volume
fields.hint: Volume of air receiver / wet receiver / buffer tank supplied with or associated with the compressor package. Typical sizing: 6–10 × compressor free delivery per minute (to limit starts/stops to ≤6/hour for unloaded start control). Example: 500 Nm³/h compressor → 50–80 L/min FAD → 0.4–0.8 m³ minimum receiver. Pressure vessel designed to PED / ASME VIII / IS 2825, fitted with safety valve, drain, pressure gauge.

fields.label: Compressed Air Quality Class
options: ISO 8573-1 Class 5 – general plant air (no dryer)
ISO 8573-1 Class 3 – utility / general instrument air (refrigerant dryer)
ISO 8573-1 Class 2 – clean instrument air (heatless desiccant dryer)
ISO 8573-1 Class 1 – critical instrument / pneumatic control (heated dryer)
fields.default: ISO 8573-1 Class 2 – clean instrument air (heatless desiccant dryer)
fields.hint: Compressed air quality at point of use per ISO 8573-1. Class 0 (oil-free): requires oil-free compressor — oil-injected screw CANNOT achieve Class 0 even with filtration. Class 1–2: instrument air standard for process plants; requires refrigerant + desiccant dryer. Class 3: general plant air with refrigerant dryer. Dryer and filter train adds 15–30% to compressor package cost.

fields.label: Refrigeration Capacity
fields.hint: Rated refrigeration capacity in kW. 1 TR (tonne of refrigeration) = 3.517 kW. Typical ammonia refrigeration systems: cold storage: 50–5000 kW; industrial process: 100–20,000 kW. COP for NH₃ at -15°C evaporation / +35°C condensation ≈ 3.5–4.5. Compressor shaft power = refrigeration capacity / COP.

fields.label: MOC (Casing / Cylinders / Wetted Parts)
options: Cast Iron (CI) - air / non-corrosive gas, <20 bar(g)
SG Iron (Ductile) - moderate pressure air / N₂
Carbon Steel (CS) - process gas to 200 bar(g)
SS316 - ammonia / corrosive refrigerant / wet gas
Hastelloy C-276 - Cl₂ / highly corrosive gas
Aluminium alloy - portable / lightweight screw casing
fields.default: Cast Iron (CI) - air / non-corrosive gas, <20 bar(g)
fields.hint: Casing / cylinder / wetted-parts material. Air screw compressors: CI or aluminium alloy casing standard. Reciprocating HP: CS or Cr-Mo alloy cylinders rated for high pressure. Ammonia service: SS316 mandatory (CI corrodes in wet NH₃). Hydrogen: CS with NACE MR0175 low hardness requirement. Chlorine: Hastelloy C-276 / specialist vendor only.

fields.label: Design / Test Standard
options: API 681 (Liquid ring vacuum pumps — for reference)
ASME Boiler & Pressure Vessel Code Sec. VIII (receivers)
fields.hint: Governing design and acceptance test standard. ISO 1217: performance test for screw and reciprocating air compressors. API 618: mandatory for reciprocating process gas compressors in refinery/petrochem. API 619: rotary screw / lobe for process gas service. ASHRAE 15 / EN 378: safety standards for refrigeration machinery rooms. IS 3816: Indian standard for general-purpose air compressors. API standards command 20–35% price premium over ISO / IS.

fields.label: Pump Differential Head
fields.hint: Total differential head (TDH) for reciprocating supply pump or ammonia liquid pump. Ammonia circulation pump: 20–80 m typical (low pressure difference). Reciprocating HP pump: 50–3000 m (high-pressure injection / transfer). Head (m) = ΔP (bar) × 10.2 / SG_fluid. NH₃ liquid at -15°C: SG ≈ 0.68 → 1 bar ≈ 15 m head.

fields.label: Speed Control / VFD
options: Soft starter (fixed speed, reduced inrush current)
fields.hint: Motor speed control method. VFD integral (built into compressor package): standard on modern Atlas Copco / Ingersoll Rand screw compressors — saves 15–30% energy at part-load, reduces start-up current. Adds ~8–12% to package cost. DOL direct-on-line: only for motors ≤15 kW (high inrush current). Star-delta: standard fixed-speed starting for 15–200 kW. VFD is strongly recommended for variable-demand compressed air systems.

fields.label: Area Classification (Hazardous Area)
options: Zone 2 / Div. 2 (ATEX Cat. 3G / Ex nA) – occasional gas release
Zone 1 / Div. 1 (ATEX Cat. 2G / Ex d or Ex e) – likely gas release
Zone 0 / Div. 1 (ATEX Cat. 1G) – continuous gas release (rare for compressors)
fields.hint: Hazardous area zone classification at compressor installation location. Air compressors: typically safe area (compressor room ventilated). NH₃ refrigeration: ATEX Zone 2 minimum per EN 13463 / ASHRAE 15. Process gas (flammable): Zone 1 or Zone 2 per area classification. ATEX Zone 1 motor: Ex d or Ex e enclosure — adds 40–80% to motor cost.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of compressor / pump units. Typical compressed air: 2–3 compressors at 50% duty (N+1 redundancy). Refrigeration: 2 × 100% or 3 × 50% depending on criticality. NH₃ pump: usually 2 × 100% operating + standby.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Air Compressor (Oil-Injected Screw)' AND air_quality_class AND 'Class 0' IN air_quality_class
compliance_rules.message: Oil-injected screw compressor cannot achieve ISO 8573-1 Class 0 (oil-free). Even with coalescing filters, residual oil aerosol remains. Specify an Oil-Free Screw compressor for Class 0 applications (pharmaceutical, food, semiconductor).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 8573-1:2010 Cl.5; ISO 8573-7 (oil test method)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: gas_type AND 'Ammonia' IN gas_type AND moc AND ('Cast Iron' IN moc OR 'Carbon Steel' IN moc)
compliance_rules.message: Cast iron and carbon steel are not suitable for wet ammonia (NH₃) service. Ammonia attacks iron in the presence of moisture — use SS316 minimum for all wetted parts in ammonia compressors and pumps.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASHRAE 15-2019; EN 378; IS 5456 Cl.4.3 (NH₃ materials)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: gas_type AND 'H₂' IN gas_type AND design_pressure > 25 AND standard AND !'API 618' IN standard AND !'API 619' IN standard
compliance_rules.message: Hydrogen service at >25 bar(g): API 618 or API 619 is strongly recommended. Standard commercial compressors are not designed for hydrogen embrittlement risks at elevated pressure. NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156 material hardness limits apply.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 618 Ed.5; NACE MR0175/ISO 15156; ASME B31.12 (H₂ piping)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Refrigeration Compressor (Ammonia)' AND area_classification AND 'Safe area' IN area_classification
compliance_rules.message: Ammonia refrigeration compressors require at minimum Zone 2 / Div. 2 area classification for the machinery room per ASHRAE 15 / EN 378. The machinery room must have NH₃ gas detection, ventilation (≥0.5 ACH continuous / ≥30 ACH emergency), and emergency shutdown. Standard TEFC motor is insufficient — specify Ex nA or Ex e.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASHRAE 15-2019 Cl.7.2; EN 378-3; IS 660 Part 1

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 200 AND num_stages AND '2' IN num_stages
compliance_rules.message: Discharge pressure above 200 bar(g) with only 2 stages: this implies a compression ratio >14:1 per stage, which is far beyond practical limits (max ~4:1 per stage for reciprocating; ~3:1 for screw). Specify 3–5 stages for pressures above 100 bar(g).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 618 Cl.3.1.2; Compressed Gas Association (CGA) S-1.3

compliance_rules.condition_expr: motor_power > 200 AND voltage AND '415' IN voltage
compliance_rules.message: Motor power above 200 kW on 415 V LV supply: full-load current exceeds ~350 A. Specify 6.6 kV or 11 kV HV motor to reduce cable sizes, switchgear rating, and I²R losses. LV at this rating requires oversized switchgear and busbars.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 12615 (IE3 motors); IEC 60034-30-1; IEEE 841

compliance_rules.condition_expr: gas_type AND ('Natural gas' IN gas_type OR 'Hydrogen' IN gas_type OR 'Propylene' IN gas_type) AND area_classification AND 'Safe area' IN area_classification
compliance_rules.message: Flammable process gas (natural gas / H₂ / propylene) specified but area classification set to 'Safe area'. Compressors handling flammable gases require Zone 1 or Zone 2 area classification — confirm with your SIL/hazardous area study. Motor must be ATEX certified.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079-10-1; ATEX Directive 2014/34/EU; IS 5572

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Air Compressor' IN equipment_subtype AND speed_control AND 'Fixed speed' IN speed_control
compliance_rules.message: Fixed-speed air compressor selected. Consider specifying VFD (Variable Frequency Drive) — typical compressed air systems run at 40–70% average load. A VFD saves 15–30% energy cost and pays back in 1–3 years at industrial electricity tariffs.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Compressed Air Challenge (CAC); ISO 11011 (energy audits); ECBC 2017

compliance_rules.condition_expr: receiver_volume > 0 AND design_pressure > 1.0
compliance_rules.message: Air receiver is a pressure vessel requiring statutory inspection. In India: inspection by PESO / Factories Act authority before commissioning. Receiver must be stamped with test pressure (1.5× MAWP), fitted with SRV, drain, pressure gauge, and access manway if >1 m³.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 2825 (unfired pressure vessels); Factories Act 1948 Sec. 31; ASME VIII

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_cost(equipment_subtype, motor_power, flow_rate, refrigeration_capacity_kw, design_pressure) × moc_factor(moc) × num_stages_factor(num_stages) × cooling_factor(cooling_method) × quantity
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_cost × moc_factor × num_stages_factor × cooling_factor × standard_factor(standard) × air_quality_factor(air_quality_class) × pressure_class_factor(design_pressure) × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_cost × moc_factor × num_stages_factor × cooling_factor × standard_factor × air_quality_factor × pressure_class_factor × speed_control_factor(speed_control) × atex_factor(area_classification) × quantity × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

adders.note: Compressors and large pumps are heavy fabricated packages. Erection/installation 15% covers crane lifts, baseplate grouting, piping/duct connections, electrical terminations, and commissioning. Freight 7% for heavy-duty skid-mounted units (multi-tonne lift). Reciprocating HP compressors: erection 20% (pulsation studies, foundation design, vibration monitoring). Contingency 12% AACE Class 4 ±20%.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ

usd_cost_baseline.note: USD supply-only packaged unit costs (ex-works, 2024 basis). Package = compressor / pump + motor + starter + baseframe + instrumentation + safety valves + local panel. Excludes dryer/filter train (add 20–35% for air quality Class 1/2). Sources: Atlas Copco, Ingersoll Rand, Kaeser (screw); Ariel, Dresser-Rand, HMT (reciprocating); Mayekawa, Johnson Controls (NH₃ refrigeration). ±30% Tier 1.

Air Compressor (Oil-Injected Screw).basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 85000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 115024.88. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Air Compressor (Oil-Free Screw).basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 85000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 115024.88. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Reciprocating Compressor (High Pressure).basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 85000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 115024.88. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Booster Compressor.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 85000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 115024.88. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Refrigeration Compressor (Ammonia).basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 85000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 115024.88. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Reciprocating pump – supply.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Ammonia pump.basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

num_stages_factor.note: Additional cylinders, inter/aftercoolers, piping. Relative to single-stage = 1.0

pressure_class_factor.note: Casing / cylinder pressure class upgrade. Normalised at 7–16 bar(g) = 1.0

air_quality_class_factor.note: Includes dryer and filter train add-on cost. Oil-free compressor base cost already higher.

standard_factor.note: API 618 premium reflects additional inspection, documentation, and test witness requirements

speed_control_factor.note: VFD adds to capital cost but typically pays back in 1–3 years of energy savings

atex_factor.note: Applied to motor and electrical component cost only (~25% of package total)

SCHEMA-PUMP-PD.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs resolved. template_name / discipline_group corrected from 'Pumps & Compressors' to 'Positive Displacement Pumps'. Duplicate 'Volume' fields (F-006 m³ / F-008 L, same spec_key) replaced: F-006 → Differential Head; F-008 → Metering Pump Dosing Rate. 'Efficiency' (F-009, values 1/28/40%) reinterpreted as metering pump stroke setting (0–100% conditional). 'Standard' field expanded beyond API 685 alone. Design pressure range widened per sub-type capabilities. Flow rate range corrected for metering pump (mL/h–L/h) vs gear/slurry. Engineering basis: API 676 (gear), API 675 (metering/dosing), API 685 (mag drive sealless), IS 5120, HI 3.1-3.5 (slurry). USD cost baselines 2024 (Roper, Viking, Milton Roy, CIRCOR, Warman / Weir).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick ±30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget ±20%. Tier 3 = Detailed ±10%.

SCHEMA-PUMP-PD.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | {flow_rate} m³/h | {diff_head_m} m head | {motor_power} kW | {design_pressure} bar(g) | MOC: {moc} | {seal_type} | {standard} | Qty: {quantity} No.
SCHEMA-PUMP-PD.spec_example: Gear Pump (Positive Displacement) | 25 m³/h | 60 m head | 7.5 kW | 16 bar(g) | MOC: SS316 | Mechanical seal | API 676 | Qty: 2 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select positive displacement pump type. Gear pump: viscous fluids (oils, adhesives, resins), precise flow. Metering/dosing: chemical injection at low flow / high accuracy. Slurry pump: abrasive slurries (mining, FGD, ceramic). Mag drive: sealless design for toxic/hazardous/corrosive liquids.

fields.label: Rated Flow Rate
fields.hint: Rated (design point) volumetric flow at pump outlet. Gear pumps: 0.1–500 m³/h; typical process: 5–50 m³/h. Metering pumps: 0.001–5 m³/h (use L/h field for precision dosing). Slurry pumps: 5–2000 m³/h. Mag drive: 0.1–200 m³/h. PD pumps deliver constant flow independent of head — a relief valve on discharge is mandatory.

fields.label: Dosing Rate
fields.hint: Precise dosing rate for metering/chemical injection pump. Typical chemical dosing ranges: Scale inhibitor: 1–50 L/h; corrosion inhibitor: 5–200 L/h; biocide: 10–500 L/h; flocculant: 50–1000 L/h. Metering pumps achieve ±1% accuracy at rated stroke. Set in conjunction with stroke length (%) and speed (SPM).

fields.label: Differential Head
fields.hint: Total differential head (TDH) at rated flow. Gear pumps: 10–300 m (limited by pressure rating, not head). Slurry pumps: 10–80 m per stage (rubber-lined), higher for hard-metal. Mag drive: 5–200 m. For PD pumps, head is best expressed as design pressure in bar(g) — head (m) = design_pressure (bar) × 10.2 / SG. Enter SG-corrected head or use design pressure field below.

fields.label: Motor Power (Shaft / Driver)
fields.hint: Installed motor power (nameplate kW). Select next standard frame size above calculated shaft power with 10–15% service factor. Standard IE3 motor ratings: 0.09, 0.12, 0.18, 0.25, 0.37, 0.55, 0.75, 1.1, 1.5, 2.2, 3, 4, 5.5, 7.5, 11, 15, 18.5, 22, 30, 37, 45, 55, 75, 90, 110 kW. Mag drive: motor must provide additional power for magnet coupling losses (~3–8%).

fields.label: MOC (Casing / Wetted Parts)
fields.hint: Casing / wetted-parts material. Gear pumps handling viscous hydrocarbons: CS or CI. Chemical dosing pumps: SS316L or PTFE-lined (mag drive). Slurry: High-chrome iron for coarse hard solids (Cr26%); rubber-lined for fine / corrosive slurry. Mag drive: PTFE-lined rear casing standard for hazardous service. NACE MR0175 applies for H₂S-containing services.

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure (casing design pressure). Gear pumps: 4–40 bar(g) typical; high-pressure to 200 bar(g). Metering / diaphragm pumps: 4–400 bar(g). Slurry pumps: 6–25 bar(g) (rubber-lined), up to 40 bar(g) hard-metal. Mag drive: up to 40 bar(g) per API 685. PD pumps are positive-displacement — a relief valve is mandatory; casing design pressure must exceed RV set pressure.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum fluid temperature at pump inlet. CI / CS gear pumps: up to 200°C (with appropriate seal). SS gear / mag drive: up to 300°C (high-temp variant). Rubber-lined slurry: maximum 80°C (rubber swells above this). Metering pumps: typically -20°C to 120°C. Temperature affects seal selection, bearing grease, and magnet type.

fields.label: Motor Supply Voltage
fields.hint: Motor supply voltage and frequency. 230 V single-phase: only for small metering pumps ≤1.5 kW. 415 V 3-phase: standard for Indian projects. 460 V 60 Hz: North America. HV motors (6.6/11 kV): considered for pumps >200 kW to avoid high LV current and cable costs.

fields.label: Shaft Seal Type
options: Double mechanical seal (API Plan 53A - pressurised barrier)
Double mechanical seal (API Plan 52 - unpressurised buffer)
fields.hint: Shaft seal arrangement. Mag drive pumps are inherently sealless — select 'Sealless - Magnetically coupled'. Double mechanical seals (Plan 53A): mandatory for toxic, flammable, or carcinogenic fluids per API 682. Packing: only for non-hazardous utility service; leaks to atmosphere. Seal type and plan significantly affect installed cost (+15–40%).

fields.label: Metering Pump Stroke Setting
fields.hint: Operating stroke length setting as percentage of maximum stroke. Actual flow = rated_max_flow × (stroke% / 100). Best accuracy range: 30–100% stroke. Below 30%: flow linearity degrades and accuracy deteriorates. Minimum recommended: 10% stroke for reciprocating diaphragm pumps. Set during commissioning per API 675 Cl.6.3.

fields.label: Slurry Solids Concentration
fields.hint: Solids concentration by weight. This affects slurry SG, viscosity, and wear rate. Typical ranges: fine tailings 20–45 wt%; coarse ore 30–60 wt%; FGD limestone slurry 25–35 wt%. Above 50 wt%: consult manufacturer for oversized impeller / reduced speed to control erosion. Slurry SG = 1 / [(wt_solids/SG_solids) + (wt_water/SG_water)].

fields.label: Slurry Abrasivity / Particle Type
options: Fine, soft particles (clay / calcium carbonate / < Mohs 3)
Fine, hard particles (silica sand, feldspar / Mohs 6–7)
Coarse, hard particles (granite chips, iron ore / Mohs 6–8)
Very hard, sharp particles (alumina, corundum / Mohs > 8)
fields.hint: Abrasivity of slurry solids governs liner selection and wear life. Fine soft: rubber-lined casing, long life. Fine hard: rubber or high-chrome iron depending on particle size. Coarse hard particles >6mm: rubber liner unsuitable — use high-chrome iron. Very hard: hard-metal (Cr26 white iron) or ceramic inserts. Wear life and liner replacement costs are significant OPEX items.

fields.label: Design Standard
options: API 676 (Positive Displacement Rotary Pumps - Gear / Screw)
IS 5120 (Indian Standard - Technical Provisions for Rotary Pumps)
fields.default: API 676 (Positive Displacement Rotary Pumps - Gear / Screw)
fields.hint: Governing design, fabrication, and testing standard. API 676: gear, screw, lobe pumps for general / refinery service. API 675: metering / controlled-volume dosing pumps. API 685: sealless (mag drive / canned motor) pumps — mandatory for toxic/carcinogenic duty in refinery/petrochemical. API 682: specifies mechanical seal arrangements and flush plans. IS 5120: Indian standard for general-purpose rotary pumps.

fields.label: Fluid Viscosity
fields.hint: Dynamic viscosity of pumped fluid at operating temperature. 1 cP = water; 100 cP = light oil; 1000 cP = heavy oil / glycol; 10,000 cP = gear oil / molasses; 100,000 cP = bitumen / resin. Gear pumps are efficient across 1–1,000,000 cP — their key advantage over centrifugal pumps for viscous service. High viscosity increases shaft power and gear loading. Per HI 3.6 viscosity correction charts.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Total number of pump units. Include operating + standby where specified. Typical: 1 operating + 1 standby (2 × 100%) for critical service. Metering pumps: often 2 × 100% or 3 × 50% duty/standby.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Chemical Process Pump (Mag Drive)' AND seal_type AND !'Sealless' IN seal_type
compliance_rules.message: Magnetic drive (mag drive) pump is inherently sealless. A mechanical seal cannot be fitted to a mag-drive design. Change seal type to 'Sealless - Magnetically coupled' or select a different pump type if a mechanical seal is required.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 685 Cl.1.1 — Sealless Centrifugal and Rotary Pumps

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 40 AND equipment_subtype == 'Slurry Pump (Heavy Duty)'
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds 40 bar(g) for a slurry pump. Standard rubber-lined slurry pumps are rated to 25 bar(g) maximum; hard-metal to 40 bar(g). Above this, a high-pressure slurry piston/diaphragm pump must be specified instead.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HI 12.1-12.6; Weir/Warman slurry pump design limits

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Slurry Pump (Heavy Duty)' AND design_temperature > 80 AND moc AND 'Rubber-lined' IN moc
compliance_rules.message: Rubber-lined slurry pump selected at design temperature above 80°C. Natural rubber linings degrade above 60°C; maximum for most synthetic rubbers is 80°C. Above 80°C, specify hard-metal (high-chrome iron) lining.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HI 12.1-12.6; Weir Group Engineering Design Manual Ch.4

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Metering Pump (Chemical Dosing)' AND stroke_pct < 30
compliance_rules.message: Metering pump stroke setting below 30%: dosing accuracy degrades significantly below 30% stroke. Consider specifying a smaller pump to operate at 60–90% stroke for best accuracy per API 675 Cl.4.2.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 675 Cl.4.2; HI 7.1-7.5 Controlled Volume Pump Standards

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype != 'Chemical Process Pump (Mag Drive)' AND seal_type AND 'Packing' IN seal_type AND design_pressure > 10
compliance_rules.message: Gland packing selected at design pressure above 10 bar(g). Gland packing leaks to atmosphere and is unsuitable for high-pressure or hazardous service. Upgrade to a single mechanical seal (minimum). Per API 682 / ATEX directive for hazardous area applications.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc AND ('Cast Iron' IN moc OR 'CS' IN moc) AND design_temperature > 200
compliance_rules.message: Cast iron or carbon steel selected at design temperature above 200°C. Above 200°C, CI/CS strength and creep resistance reduces significantly. Use SS316 or alloy steel for high-temperature service.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.34 Table 2; IS 2825 material temperature limits

compliance_rules.condition_expr: motor_power > 200 AND voltage AND '415' IN voltage
compliance_rules.message: Motor power above 200 kW on 415 V supply: full-load current will be ~350 A at 415 V. Consider specifying 6.6 kV supply to reduce cable sizing and starter costs. LV switchgear above 200 kW is non-standard for most Indian panel builders.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 12615 (IE3 motors); IEC 60034-30-1; IEEE 303

compliance_rules.condition_expr: viscosity_cp IS not None AND viscosity_cp > 10000 AND equipment_subtype == 'Gear Pump (Positive Displacement)'
compliance_rules.message: Fluid viscosity above 10,000 cP: gear pump is appropriate but confirm speed does not exceed manufacturer's viscosity-speed limits. High-viscosity fluids may require pump jacket heating, insulation, and trace heating on suction line to maintain flowability.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 676 Cl.2.8; HI 3.6 — Viscosity correction for rotary PD pumps

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_cost(equipment_subtype, flow_rate, motor_power) × moc_factor(moc) × seal_factor(seal_type) × quantity
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_cost(equipment_subtype, flow_rate, motor_power) × moc_factor(moc) × seal_factor(seal_type) × pressure_factor(design_pressure) × temp_factor(design_temperature) × standard_factor(standard) × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_cost × moc_factor × seal_factor × pressure_factor × temp_factor × standard_factor × slurry_abrasivity_factor(slurry_abrasivity) × solids_factor(solids_conc_wt) × viscosity_factor(viscosity_cp) × quantity × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

adders.note: PD pumps are fabricated engineered items. Erection/installation 18% covers pump skid landing, coupling alignment, piping connections, relief valve, strainer, and initial commissioning. Freight 6% (heavy fabricated equipment with baseplate). Contingency 12% AACE Class 4 ±20%.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
