Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ

usd_cost_baseline.note: USD supply-only unit costs (ex-works, 2024 basis). Pump + motor + baseplate + coupling + standard mechanical seal. Relief valve, suction strainer, and instrumentation priced separately. Sources: Roper/Viking (gear), Milton Roy/ProMinent (metering), Weir/Warman (slurry), Klaus Union/Dickow (mag drive). ±30% Tier 1.

Gear Pump (Positive Displacement).basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Metering Pump (Chemical Dosing).basis: SS316 wetted parts, PTFE diaphragm, with pulsation dampener. Control signal 4-20mA input. Without day tank.

Slurry Pump (Heavy Duty).basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Chemical Process Pump (Mag Drive).basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

design_pressure_factor.note: Casing pressure class upgrade multiplier. Normalised at 16 bar(g) = 1.0.

design_temperature_factor.note: High-temperature variants require different seals, bearings, and materials.

standard_factor.note: Captures documentation, inspection, and testing cost premium

slurry_abrasivity_factor.note: Hard-metal casing upgrade and heavier construction for abrasive solids

SCHEMA-PUMP-VACUUM.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs resolved. Engineering judgment applied for steam ejector vacuum systems per HEI Standards for Steam Jet Ejectors (4th Ed.), ASME PTC 24 (Ejector Performance), API 681 (liquid ring vacuum pumps for reference), IS 5483. Note: F-001 renamed from duplicate 'Design Pressure (bar(g))' → 'Motive Steam Pressure'; F-002 renamed from 'Design Pressure (bar(a))' → 'Suction Pressure (Vacuum)'; duplicate spec_key corrected. USD cost baselines 2024 basis (vendor budgetary quotes: Graham, Körting, Croll Reynolds, Mazda / Indian fabricators).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick ±30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget ±20%. Tier 3 = Detailed ±10%.

SCHEMA-PUMP-VACUUM.spec_formula: Steam Ejector {num_stages}-Stage | Suction: {suction_pressure_bara} bara | Gas load: {gas_load_kg_h} kg/h | Motive steam: {motive_steam_pressure_barg} bar(g) | MOC: {moc} | {condenser_type} | Qty: {quantity} No.
SCHEMA-PUMP-VACUUM.spec_example: Steam Ejector 3-Stage | Suction: 0.013 bara (10 mmHgA) | Gas load: 50 kg/h air equivalent | Motive steam: 10 bar(g) | MOC: SS316 | Surface condenser (shell & tube) | Qty: 1 No.

fields.label: Number of Stages
fields.hint: Number of ejector stages determines achievable vacuum level. Each stage provides ~10:1 compression ratio. 3-stage: most common for distillation column vacuum service (5–25 mmHgA). 5–6 stage: vacuum drying, freeze-drying, molecular distillation. Per HEI Standards for Steam Jet Ejectors.

fields.label: Suction Pressure (Vacuum)
fields.hint: Absolute suction pressure at ejector inlet (the vacuum to be achieved). Always expressed in bara (absolute). Common vacuum levels: 100 mmHgA = 0.133 bara; 25 mmHgA = 0.033 bara; 10 mmHgA = 0.013 bara; 5 mmHgA = 0.007 bara. 1 mmHg = 0.00133 bara. Lower suction pressure → more stages required → higher cost. Per HEI / ASME PTC 24.

fields.label: Gas / Vapour Load (Air Equivalent)
fields.hint: Non-condensable gas load expressed as air equivalent at suction conditions. Convert actual gas to air equivalent: W_air = W_actual × (29 / MW_actual)^0.5. Typical distillation column: 5–50 kg/h air equivalent. Large vacuum evaporators: 100–500 kg/h. This is the primary driver for ejector body size and steam consumption.

fields.label: Motive Steam Pressure
fields.hint: Motive steam supply pressure at ejector nozzle inlet. Must be dry saturated or slightly superheated (≤20°C superheat). Typical range: 4–17 bar(g). Higher pressure allows smaller nozzle throat and lower steam consumption. Minimum practical: 3.5 bar(g). Above 17 bar(g) gives diminishing returns. Per HEI Steam Jet Ejector Standards, Table 1.

fields.label: Motive Steam Temperature
fields.hint: Motive steam temperature at ejector inlet. At 10 bar(g), saturation temperature ≈ 184°C — default is dry saturated. Superheat >50°C above saturation increases steam consumption by 2–5% and should be avoided. Wet steam (quality <0.98) causes nozzle erosion. Ensure steam trap / separator on supply line per HEI.

fields.label: MOC (Ejector Body & Nozzle)
options: Cast Iron (steam / air, low-cost utility service)
fields.hint: Material of construction for ejector body, nozzle, and diffuser. CS: standard for utility/non-corrosive vacuum. SS316: pharmaceutical, food-grade, or light corrosive vapours. Hastelloy/Titanium: chlorine, HCl, acid vapours — 4–8× CS price. Nozzle tip is a wear item — specify replaceable nozzle for abrasive service.

fields.label: Inter/After-Condenser Type
fields.hint: Type of inter-condenser and after-condenser between ejector stages. Surface condensers: used when condensate must be kept separate from cooling water (closed loop), required for hazardous/toxic vapours, pharmaceutical service. Higher cost but no contamination risk. Barometric: lowest cost, cooling water contacts condensate directly — only for clean steam/water vapour service with adequate barometric leg (10.4 m). Per HEI Standards, Section 5.

fields.label: Cooling Water Inlet Temperature
fields.hint: Cooling water supply temperature to inter/after-condensers. HEI standard design basis: 21°C (70°F) or 32°C (90°F) CW inlet. Higher CW temperature reduces condenser effectiveness — increases steam consumption and may require additional stages. Critical for tropical / Middle East sites where CW can be 38–42°C.

fields.label: Condenser Tube MOC
fields.hint: Tube material for surface condensers. Admiralty brass: traditional for clean freshwater CW. SS316/304: preferred for closed-loop CW with treatment. Titanium: seawater cooling (coastal / offshore plants). Tube OD typically 19 mm (3/4") or 25 mm (1"), 18 BWG, per TEMA B/C class. Tube selection is critical for condenser life.

fields.label: Estimated Motive Steam Consumption
fields.hint: Total motive steam consumption for all stages (vendor-confirmed or estimated). Typical steam ratios: 1-stage ~6 kg steam/kg gas; 3-stage ~25–40 kg/kg; 5-stage ~80–120 kg/kg (all at 10 bar(g) motive, 0.013 bara suction). Used for utility consumption and operating cost calculations — not a CAPEX cost driver but important for lifecycle cost.

fields.label: Discharge / Backpressure
fields.hint: Discharge (backpressure) at final ejector stage outlet. Usually atmospheric: 0.013 bar(g) ≈ 0 bar(g). Elevated backpressure (e.g., discharging into a scrubber at 0.3 bar(g)) significantly reduces ejector capacity — must be confirmed with vendor. Ejectors are backpressure-sensitive: >10% over design discharge pressure causes 'breaking' (loss of vacuum). Per HEI Section 3.4.

fields.label: Entrained Vapour / Process Gas
options: Acid vapours (HCl, acetic acid, formic acid)
fields.hint: Nature of process gas/vapour being evacuated. Determines MOC selection, condensate handling, and whether barometric or surface condensers are permissible. Acid/chlorine vapours → require special MOC (Hastelloy/Ti). Toxic → surface condensers mandatory; no direct contact with CW. Hydrocarbon → check vapour pressure curve for condenser sizing.

fields.label: Design / Test Standard
options: HEI Standards for Steam Jet Ejectors (4th Ed.)
fields.default: HEI Standards for Steam Jet Ejectors (4th Ed.)
fields.hint: Governing design and acceptance test standard. HEI: international industry standard for sizing and performance guarantee. ASME PTC 24: shop performance test with witnessed test option (+8–12% cost). IS 5483: Indian standard, largely aligned with HEI. Witnessed shop test significantly increases delivery cost — confirm necessity.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of ejector systems. Typically 1 operating + 0 or 1 spare (ejectors have no moving parts; standby is unusual but sometimes specified for critical vacuum columns). Full system = all stages + all condensers + interconnecting pipework skid-mounted.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: suction_pressure_bara > 0.133 AND '2' IN num_stages
compliance_rules.message: Suction pressure above 0.133 bara (100 mmHgA): a single-stage ejector is typically sufficient at this vacuum level. A 2-stage system will be significantly over-specified and over-cost. Confirm vacuum requirement with process engineer.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HEI Standards for Steam Jet Ejectors, Table 1 — Stage vs. Vacuum range

compliance_rules.condition_expr: motive_steam_pressure_barg < 3.0
compliance_rules.message: Motive steam pressure below 3.0 bar(g) is insufficient for reliable ejector operation. Minimum practical motive steam pressure is 3.5 bar(g). Below this, the nozzle cannot develop adequate velocity to entrain process gas.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HEI Standards for Steam Jet Ejectors, Section 2.3

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Barometric' IN condenser_type AND process_gas_type AND !'Air' IN process_gas_type AND !'Water vapour' IN process_gas_type
compliance_rules.message: Barometric (direct-contact) condensers mix cooling water with condensate. For hydrocarbon, acid, solvent, or toxic vapour service, this contaminates cooling water and creates environmental / safety issues. Use surface (shell & tube) condensers for these services.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HEI Section 5.2; EPA effluent guidelines for CW discharge

compliance_rules.condition_expr: process_gas_type AND ('Acid' IN process_gas_type OR 'Chlorine' IN process_gas_type OR 'Toxic' IN process_gas_type) AND moc == 'CS (Carbon Steel - non-corrosive service)'
compliance_rules.message: Carbon steel MOC selected for corrosive / toxic vapour service. Acid, chlorine, and toxic gas services require stainless steel (minimum SS316), Hastelloy C-276, or Titanium depending on the specific compound. CS will corrode rapidly and create a safety hazard.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NACE MR0175; HEI Section 4.3 (MOC selection)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: cw_temp_c IS not None AND cw_temp_c > 38
compliance_rules.message: Cooling water temperature above 38°C: standard HEI ejector sizing tables are based on 21°C or 32°C CW inlet. At >38°C, condenser duty is severely impaired — the steam consumption increases and additional stages may be needed. Reconfirm sizing with vendor using actual CW temperature.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HEI Standards Section 5.5 — CW temperature correction factors

compliance_rules.condition_expr: discharge_pressure_barg IS not None AND discharge_pressure_barg > 0.1
compliance_rules.message: Discharge backpressure above 0.1 bar(g): ejectors are sensitive to backpressure. Elevated discharge pressure reduces capacity significantly and can cause 'breaking' (loss of vacuum). Confirm final-stage ejector sizing accounts for actual backpressure. Vendor must guarantee performance at stated discharge pressure.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HEI Section 3.4; ASME PTC 24 Cl.5

compliance_rules.condition_expr: num_stages AND ('5' IN num_stages OR '6' IN num_stages)
compliance_rules.message: 5–6 stage ejector system: at this extreme vacuum level, consider whether a hybrid system (liquid ring vacuum pump as last-stage backing pump) might be more economical. Hybrid systems can reduce steam consumption by 30–50% versus all-ejector systems at <1 mmHgA.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HEI Standards Section 6 — Hybrid ejector / LRVP systems

compliance_rules.condition_expr: motive_steam_pressure_barg > 17
compliance_rules.message: Motive steam pressure above 17 bar(g): above this level there is diminishing return in steam economy. The ejector can be designed for lower pressure to save steam utility costs — discuss with vendor.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: HEI Standards Table 1 — Optimum motive steam pressure range

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_cost(num_stages, gas_load_kg_h, suction_pressure_bara) × moc_factor(moc) × condenser_factor(condenser_type) × quantity
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_cost(num_stages, gas_load_kg_h, suction_pressure_bara) × moc_factor(moc) × condenser_factor(condenser_type) × condenser_tube_moc_factor(condenser_tube_moc) × steam_pressure_factor(motive_steam_pressure_barg) × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_cost × moc_factor × condenser_factor × condenser_tube_moc_factor × steam_pressure_factor × process_gas_factor(process_gas_type) × standard_factor(design_standard) × quantity × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

adders.note: Steam ejectors are fabricated equipment typically skid-mounted (ejector bodies + condensers + interconnecting piping + instrumentation on one skid). Erection/installation 12% for skid landing, piping connections, and commissioning. Freight 6% (heavy fabricated equipment). Contingency 12% AACE Class 4 ±20%.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ

usd_cost_baseline.note: USD ex-works supply-only costs for complete ejector system (all stages + inter/after-condensers + skid frame). 2024 basis. Sources: Graham, Körting, Croll Reynolds (USA list), Indian fabricators (Mazda, Transvac India) at 0.55× USA. Accuracy ±30% Tier 1. Costs scale approximately with gas_load^0.65 × stage_multiplier.

Steam Ejector (3-Stage).basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

gas_load_scaling.note: Cost scales with gas load raised to power 0.65. Adjusted cost = base_cost × (actual_load / 50)^0.65

moc_factor.note: Relative to CS = 1.0. Applied to ejector body cost only.

condenser_tube_moc_factor.note: Applied to condenser portion of total cost only (~35–45% of system cost)

steam_pressure_factor.note: Nozzle and body complexity changes slightly with steam pressure

process_gas_factor.note: Captures additional sealing, venting, and material requirements

design_standard_factor.note: ASME PTC 24 witnessed shop test adds ~10% to supply cost

SCHEMA-ROADS.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs resolved. Engineering judgment applied per MoRTH Specifications for Road & Bridge Works (5th Rev.), IRC:SP:20, IRC:37, IRC:58, IS 73, IS 1203, CPWD DSR 2023-24, IRC:67 (traffic signs), IRC:35 (road markings), MOSRTH standards. 'Efficiency' field (misapplied auto-gen) replaced with 'Bitumen Content' field. USD cost baselines 2024 India basis. Conditional fields for all 20 sub-types.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick ±30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget ±20%. Tier 3 = Detailed ±10%.

SCHEMA-ROADS.spec_example: Dense Bituminous Macadam (DBM) | Industrial haul road | Th=75mm | Binder: VG-30 | 7.0m wide | L=2000m

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the road or pavement activity. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Road Category / Traffic Class
options: Light traffic – plant haul road / access track (<1 MSA)
Medium traffic – industrial internal road (1–10 MSA)
Heavy traffic – factory/port/mine haul road (10–30 MSA)
fields.default: Medium traffic – industrial internal road (1–10 MSA)
fields.hint: Traffic class in MSA (Million Standard Axles over design life). Governs pavement crust thickness per IRC:37-2018 (flexible) or IRC:58-2015 (rigid). Industrial haul roads see high equivalent axle loads — do not underspec.

fields.label: Road / Section Length
fields.hint: Length of road or pavement section. Area-based items (layers, coatings): area = length × width. Linear items (kerbs, markings): quantity = length. Site grading: length of chainage to be graded.

fields.label: Carriageway Width
fields.hint: Width of carriageway (paved surface only, excluding shoulders). Typical: 3.75 m per lane (IRC:SP:20). Single lane: 3.75 m; 2-lane: 7.0–7.5 m; 4-lane: 14–15 m. Industrial haul: 7–10 m for two-way heavy truck traffic.

fields.label: Layer / Course Thickness
fields.hint: Compacted thickness of the layer. Typical MoRTH ranges: GSB/Sub-base: 150–300 mm; WMM Base: 150–250 mm; BM: 50–100 mm; DBM: 50–100 mm; BC (wearing): 25–50 mm; Rigid slab: 200–350 mm (IRC:58). Minimum compacted layer: 2× max aggregate size per MoRTH Cl.505.

fields.label: Average Cut / Fill Depth
fields.hint: Average depth of cut or fill over the graded section. Volume (m³) = length × width × avg depth. Typically 0.15–0.5 m for site levelling; up to 5 m for road embankments. Bulking factor 1.2–1.3 (soil); rock 1.5–1.7. Per IRC:SP:20 Table 2.

fields.label: Earthwork Material Type
fields.hint: Material type determines excavation method and unit rate. Hard rock (blasting): 4–6× ordinary soil rate. Black cotton soil: requires lime/cement stabilisation or replacement per IRC:SP:20 before sub-base. Affects crust design (IRC:37 CBR input).

fields.label: Granular Material Specification
fields.hint: Granular material grade per MoRTH Specifications. GSB (Granular Sub-Base): Cl.401 grading; CBR ≥20% required. WMM (Wet Mix Macadam): Cl.406; requires water binding and compaction to 98% MDD (IS:2720 Part 8). Not to be confused with WBM (dry).

fields.label: Bitumen Grade
fields.hint: Bitumen viscosity grade per IS 73 / IRC:SP:53. VG-30: standard for most of India (annual max temp <55°C). VG-40: hot arid zones or heavily trafficked roads. PMB: polymer-modified for high-traffic, SH/NH, intersections. Emulsion SS-1: tack/prime coats only. Cost: VG-30 < VG-40 < PMB < CRMB.

fields.label: Bitumen / Binder Content
fields.hint: Optimum binder content (OBC) by weight of total mix, determined by Marshall Method (IS:1203 / MoRTH Cl.500 Series). Typical OBC: BM 3.5–4.0%; DBM 4.0–4.5%; BC 5.0–5.5%; Seal coat 6.7–7.5% (by weight of aggregate). Higher binder = higher cost but better durability.

fields.label: Aggregate Source / Type
fields.hint: Aggregate type for bituminous mix. Crushed angular aggregate mandatory for wearing courses (BC/DBM) per MoRTH Cl.507. RAP blend up to 30% acceptable for binder/base courses per IRC. Limestone: softer, suitable for BM base layer only — not for BC wearing.

fields.label: Concrete Grade
fields.hint: Concrete grade (characteristic cube strength at 28 days). M30 minimum for rigid (JPCP) pavement per IRC:58-2015 Cl.5.3. M35/M40 for high-traffic expressways and heavy industrial. Flexural strength (MOR) ≥4.5 MPa at 28 days mandatory (IRC:58). Interlocking pavers: M35–M45 factory-produced per IS:15658.

fields.label: Joint Type (Rigid Pavement)
fields.hint: Pavement joint system per IRC:58-2015. JPCP with dowels: standard for NH/SH and industrial roads; dowel bars transfer load across transverse joints. CRCP: no transverse joints, lower maintenance but higher initial cost. Joint spacing and sealant are significant cost items on large areas.

fields.label: Paver Block Thickness
fields.hint: Block thickness per IS:15658. 60 mm: pedestrian areas, cycle tracks, light vehicular. 80 mm: standard for industrial yard, car parks, light trucks. 100 mm: port areas, heavy forklift/container traffic. Laid on 30–50 mm sand bedding over WMM base.

fields.label: Marking Width / Type
options: 300 mm line (stop line / pedestrian crossing bar)
fields.hint: Road marking type and width per IRC:35-2015. Thermoplastic: 2–3 mm thick, retro-reflective glass beads, life 4–7 years. Paint: 0.3–0.5 mm, life 1–2 years; suitable for car parks / internal roads. Measured per running metre of marking.

fields.label: Sign Size / Panel
fields.hint: Sign panel size per IRC:67-2012. Type A–D are inscribed circle diameters for circular signs or side length for triangular/square. All signs: retro-reflective sheeting Type IV/XI (IS:15809). Supplied with hot-dip galvanised post (76 mm dia) and foundation. Gantry signs require structural design — quote separately.

fields.label: Speed Breaker Dimensions
options: Speed bump 100mm H × 300mm W (sharp – private roads)
fields.hint: Speed control device dimensions. MoRTH speed hump: 75 mm height × 3.6 m width for public roads. IRC:99-2018 governs design. Rubber breakers: modular bolted sections, easier to relocate. Concrete bumps: integral to road, permanent. Must be preceded by warning signs and yellow markings per IRC:35/67.

fields.label: Subgrade CBR (%)
fields.hint: California Bearing Ratio of compacted subgrade soil (4-day soaked, IS:2720 Part 16). Governs crust thickness in IRC:37 design charts. Typical values: black cotton soil 2–3%; red laterite 5–8%; gravelly soil 10–20%; murrum 15–40%. Low CBR (<3%) requires sub-grade improvement (lime/cement stabilisation).

fields.label: Application Rate
fields.hint: Spray application rate for bituminous surface treatments. Prime coat (cutback/emulsion): 0.6–1.0 kg/m² on granular base. Tack coat (emulsion): 0.20–0.30 kg/m² on existing bituminous surface. Seal coat Type A (SBST): 6.7–7.5 kg/m² binder + 10–12 mm chips. Per MoRTH Cl.502–503.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of items for point/unit items: signs, speed breakers, bus shelters. For area-based items (layers, coatings, rigid/flexible pavement): area (m²) is computed from Road Length × Carriageway Width. For linear items (kerbs, markings): length (m) from Road Length field.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Rigid Pavement (Concrete)' AND layer_thickness_mm < 200
compliance_rules.message: Rigid pavement slab thickness below 200 mm. IRC:58-2015 minimum slab thickness is 200 mm for any trafficked concrete pavement. Review traffic loading and IRC:58 design charts.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IRC:58-2015 Cl.5.3; MoRTH Cl.602

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Bituminous Concrete (BC)' AND layer_thickness_mm < 25
compliance_rules.message: BC wearing course thickness below 25 mm minimum. MoRTH Cl.507 specifies minimum 25 mm compacted thickness for BC. Below this, adequate compaction cannot be achieved.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: MoRTH Specifications Cl.507; IRC:37-2018

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Sub-Base Course (Granular)' AND layer_thickness_mm < 100
compliance_rules.message: Granular sub-base thickness below 100 mm. Minimum single-layer compacted thickness per MoRTH Cl.401 is 100 mm. Below this, uniform compaction cannot be achieved.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: MoRTH Specifications Cl.401; IRC:SP:20

compliance_rules.condition_expr: subgrade_cbr < 3 AND subgrade_cbr > 0
compliance_rules.message: Subgrade CBR below 3% indicates very weak subgrade (likely black cotton or marine clay). Sub-grade improvement mandatory before pavement construction: lime stabilisation (3–5% lime), cement stabilisation, or capping layer per IRC:37-2018 Cl.4.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IRC:37-2018 Cl.4.2; IRC:SP:89 (lime stabilisation)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Dense Bituminous Macadam (DBM)' AND 'VG-10' IN bitumen_grade
compliance_rules.message: VG-10 bitumen not recommended for DBM binder/intermediate course. Use VG-30 minimum. VG-10 is too soft at ambient temperatures in India and causes rutting under heavy traffic per IS 73.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 73:2013; IRC:SP:53; MoRTH Cl.505

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Bituminous Concrete (BC)' AND '>30 MSA' IN road_category AND !'PMB' IN bitumen_grade AND !'CRMB' IN bitumen_grade
compliance_rules.message: Very heavy traffic (>30 MSA) with standard VG bitumen on BC wearing course: consider Polymer Modified Bitumen (PMB) or CRMB for improved rutting resistance and longer service life per IRC:SP:53.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Rigid Pavement (Concrete)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Interlocking Pavers (Concrete)') AND concrete_grade == 'M20 (interlocking paver bedding / speed bumps)'
compliance_rules.message: M20 grade is only for bedding layer under interlocking pavers or minor non-trafficked applications. Structural slab and kerbs require minimum M25–M30 per IRC:58.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Site Grading (Cut & Fill)' AND cut_fill_depth_m > 3.0
compliance_rules.message: Average cut/fill depth >3 m: embankment / cutting design review required. High fills need compaction in 200 mm layers with geotextile reinforcement. Deep cuts require slope stability analysis per IS:7894.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IRC:SP:20; IS:7894 (slope stability); MoRTH Cl.305

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Prime Coat' AND 'VG-' IN bitumen_grade
compliance_rules.message: Prime coat uses cutback bitumen (RC-70) or bitumen emulsion SS-1, not viscosity-grade bitumen (VG). Please select 'Cutback Bitumen RC-70' or 'Bitumen Emulsion SS-1' for prime coat applications.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: MoRTH Cl.502; IS:73 / IS:1201

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Interlocking Pavers (Concrete)' AND paver_thickness_mm == '60' AND 'Heavy' IN road_category
compliance_rules.message: 60 mm paver blocks are not recommended for heavy or very heavy traffic. Use 80 mm minimum for standard industrial/port yards, 100 mm for heavy forklift and container handling equipment per IS:15658.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS:15658; IRC:SP:63 (IPS guidelines)

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype, bitumen_grade, concrete_grade, paver_thickness_mm, sign_size, marking_type) × traffic_factor(road_category) × computed_quantity(road_length_m, pavement_width_m, layer_thickness_mm, quantity)
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_cost × traffic_factor(road_category) × material_factor(granular_spec, aggregate_type) × cbr_factor(subgrade_cbr) × earthwork_material_factor(earthwork_material) × joint_type_factor(joint_type) × computed_quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_cost × traffic_factor × material_factor × cbr_factor × earthwork_material_factor × joint_type_factor × bitumen_content_factor(bitumen_content_pct) × computed_quantity × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

adders.note: Roads & pavements are material + plant dominated. Erection/installation 28% covers equipment (rollers, pavers, batching plant), traffic management, testing and quality control. Freight 4% for aggregates and bituminous materials (local sourcing preferred). Contingency 12% AACE Class 4. Earthworks contingency should be 15–20%.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: Switched ENR->CEPCI: procured/manufactured equipment, supply-cost dominated. verify=true

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ

usd_cost_baseline.note: USD installed unit costs (India basis 2024, ₹83/USD). Supply + lay / install for the nominated unit. Bituminous layers: per tonne × yield. Area items: per m². Source: CPWD DSR 2023-24, MoRTH SoR, vendor quotations.

Site Grading (Cut & Fill).basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Sub-Base Course (Granular).basis: GSB/WMM supplied, spread and compacted to 98% MDD. Rate per 100mm layer.

Base Course (Wet Mix Macadam).basis: WMM supplied, mixed, laid by paver and compacted. Per 100mm compacted.

Bituminous Macadam (BM).basis: BM mix supplied, laid by mechanical paver, compacted. VG-30 base rate.

Dense Bituminous Macadam (DBM).basis: DBM mix supplied, paved and compacted. VG-30 base rate.

Bituminous Concrete (BC).basis: BC wearing course supplied, paved and compacted. VG-30 base rate.

Seal Coat (Type A).basis: Single bituminous surface treatment (SBST) with stone chips, mechanically applied.

Prime Coat.basis: Cutback bitumen RC-70 / emulsion sprayed on granular base at 0.6–1.0 kg/m².

Tack Coat.basis: Bitumen emulsion SS-1 sprayed at 0.20–0.30 kg/m² on existing surface.

Rigid Pavement (Concrete).basis: CPWD-DSR-class ~2018 USD/m2 32 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 40.71. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Interlocking Pavers (Concrete).basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Kerb Stone (Precast Concrete).basis: CPWD-DSR-class ~2018 USD/m 14 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 17.81. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Road Marking (Thermoplastic).basis: CPWD-DSR-class ~2018 USD/m2 28 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 35.62. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Road Marking (Paint).basis: CPWD-DSR-class ~2018 USD/m2 28 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 35.62. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Traffic Sign (Regulatory).basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Traffic Sign (Warning).basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Traffic Sign (Informatory).basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Speed Breaker (Rubber).basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Speed Bump (Concrete).basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Bus Shelter (Precast).basis: Precast RCC/steel bus shelter 3.0×1.5m, with seat, roof, info board, foundation.

traffic_category_factor.note: Layer thickness and mix specification increase with traffic class; normalised at 1–10 MSA = 1.0

bitumen_grade_factor.note: Relative to VG-30 = 1.0 for equivalent quantity

cbr_factor.description: Weak subgrade increases crust thickness. Approximate cost uplift.
cbr_factor.note: Accounts for thicker pavement crust required for low CBR subgrade

joint_type_factor.note: Adder on rigid pavement base rate. Relative to plain jointed = 1.0

SCHEMA-ROOFING.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs resolved. Engineering judgment applied per IS 1346, IS 3177, IS 2645, NBC 2016 Part 4 (Fire), ECBC 2017 (thermal), GRIHA/IGBC green roof guidelines, BS 8217, EN 13956. USD cost baselines 2024 basis (India CPWD DSR + vendor quotes). Conditional fields added for all 8 sub-types.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick ±30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget ±20%. Tier 3 = Detailed ±10%.

SCHEMA-ROOFING.spec_example: Roof Waterproofing (Bituminous) | APP Modified Bitumen | 4mm | 2-layer system | Slope: ≤5° flat | Aluminium foil finish | 500 m²

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific roofing or waterproofing type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Membrane / System Type
fields.hint: Select waterproofing system. APP torch-applied: most common for flat roofs, 15-20 yr life, IS 1346 / BS 8217. Liquid PU/PMMA: detail-friendly, good for complex geometry. Crystalline: for RC surfaces requiring positive/negative side WP. BUR: traditional but labour-intensive.

fields.label: Number of Layers / Coats
fields.hint: Number of membrane plies or liquid-applied coats. Minimum 2-layer APP/SBS for exposed flat roofs per IS 1346. 3–4 layers for reservoirs, basement slabs, and below-grade applications. Liquid membranes: typically 2–3 coats at specified DFT.

fields.label: Protection Layer / Finish
fields.hint: Protective layer over waterproofing membrane. Screed topping is standard for accessible roofs. Gravel ballast for non-accessible. Aluminium foil face is self-finished for non-trafficked areas. Screed adds load — confirm structural adequacy (typically 2–3 kN/m²).

fields.label: Insulation Grade / Density
options: XPS 700 kPa (heavy duty - inverted / green roof)
PUF Spray 50 kg/m³ (high density - protected membrane)
fields.hint: XPS compressive strength determines application: 300 kPa for non-trafficked roofs; 500 kPa for foot traffic; 700 kPa for inverted roofs or green roofs. PUF density affects thermal performance (λ ≈ 0.022–0.026 W/mK) and fire class. Per ECBC 2017 Table 6 thermal compliance.

fields.label: Insulation Thickness
fields.hint: Insulation thickness in mm. ECBC 2017 minimum for composite-walled roofs: U-value ≤ 0.4 W/m²K (ECBC+ ≤0.2 W/m²K). Typical: 50 mm XPS achieves U≈0.44; 75 mm achieves U≈0.30. PUF 50 mm: U≈0.42; 75 mm: U≈0.28. Confirm with RCC slab assembly U-value.

fields.label: Roof Slope
fields.hint: Roof pitch determines minimum waterproofing system, tile type, and labour factor. Flat: IS 1346 minimum 2-ply bituminous. Low pitch: standing seam metal minimum per manufacturer. Medium/steep: clay tiles, standing seam, polycarbonate suitable. Steep pitch adds 15–40% to labour cost (safety access, slower laying).

fields.label: Metal Sheet Material
fields.hint: Metal sheet material and thickness (TCT = total coated thickness). Galvalume: best corrosion resistance for industrial/coastal. PPGI: colour-coated, good aesthetics, IS 14246. Aluminium: lightweight (2.4 kg/m²), non-rusting, coastal/industrial. Stainless/Copper: premium, used for landmark/heritage buildings.

fields.label: Purlin / Secondary Structure
options: Sheet + insulated sandwich panel (50mm PUF core)
Sheet + insulated sandwich panel (75mm PUF core)
fields.hint: Secondary steelwork scope. Sandwich panels provide integral insulation + cladding in one element. Z/C purlins: hot-dip galvanised, designed to IS 811 / IS 801. Span between purlins governed by wind and imposed load per IS 875.

fields.label: Polycarbonate Sheet Type
fields.hint: Twin-wall 10 mm: most common for industrial skylights/walkways; U-value ≈ 3.3 W/m²K, light transmission 60–65%. Multi-wall 16 mm: better thermal (U≈2.1) for conditioned spaces. UV-protected coating on outer face is mandatory — verify 10-yr UV warranty. Solid sheet: impact-resistant, used for security/glazing applications.

fields.label: Sheet Colour / Tint
fields.hint: Light transmission (LT) affects solar heat gain and glare. Opal/diffuse: standard for industrial daylight. Bronze/dark for hot climates to reduce SHGC. Tints are minor cost premium (<5%) but impact ECBC 2017 SHGC compliance for conditioned buildings (SHGC ≤0.25).

fields.label: Clay Tile Type
fields.hint: Clay roof tile type per IS 654. Mangalore (country tile): most common in India, 10–12 tiles/m², laid on battens or lime mortar. Interlocking Marseille: faster laying, fewer laps, better wind uplift. Reclaimed heritage tiles: 2–4× cost premium for restoration work. All types require minimum 25° pitch for watertightness.

fields.label: Tile Underlay / Batten Specification
fields.hint: Underlay and batten system under clay tiles. Breathable membrane underlay prevents driving rain ingress while allowing vapour escape. GI steel battens for coastal/humid zones (no rot risk). Lime mortar bedding only: traditional practice, poor wind uplift resistance.

fields.label: Green Roof / Terrace Garden Type
options: Intensive - Lawn & shrubs (200–500 mm growing medium)
Intensive - Landscaped garden (300–600 mm growing medium)
Intensive - Trees + garden (500–1000 mm growing medium)
fields.default: Intensive - Landscaped garden (300–600 mm growing medium)
fields.hint: Intensive green roof: full garden with irrigation, 150–500 kg/m² saturated load — requires structural verification. Extensive: lightweight (60–150 kg/m²), low-maintenance sedum, no irrigation needed. All types require root-resistant waterproofing membrane below growing medium (FLL guidelines / NBC 2016 Annex).

fields.label: Irrigation System
fields.hint: Irrigation method for intensive terrace gardens. Drip irrigation is most water-efficient (GRIHA WE criterion). Automated systems required for rooftop gardens without regular maintenance. Manual watering only acceptable for small areas <50 m² or sedum roofs.

fields.label: Growing Medium Depth
fields.hint: Depth of engineered growing medium (lightweight expanded clay aggregate + composted substrate). Each 100 mm ≈ 70–90 kg/m² saturated load. Verify slab design load capacity — typically 4–8 kN/m² for intensive gardens. FLL guidelines.

fields.label: Roof Area
fields.hint: Plan area of roof in m² (measured on slope for pitched roofs). For standing seam / tile: measure along slope surface, not plan. For flat roof: plan area. Include upstands and parapets for WP.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Roof Tiles (Clay)' AND roof_slope == 'Flat (≤5° / ≤1:12)'
compliance_rules.message: Clay roof tiles require minimum 25° (approximately 1:2) pitch for watertightness. A flat or low-pitch roof with clay tiles will leak. Use bituminous or liquid-applied waterproofing for flat roofs.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 654 Cl.5.3; NBC 2016 Part 6 Sec.2

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Polycarbonate Roofing' AND roof_slope == 'Flat (≤5° / ≤1:12)'
compliance_rules.message: Polycarbonate sheeting requires minimum 5° pitch (1:12) for drainage. Flat installation causes ponding, joint failure, and algae growth. Minimum 5°–7° pitch required per manufacturer guidelines.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: BREEAM Technical Guide; polycarbonate manufacturer installation guidelines

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Terrace Garden (Intensive)' AND growing_medium_depth_mm > 400
compliance_rules.message: Growing medium depth >400 mm: saturated load exceeds 3.5 kN/m². Confirm structural slab capacity with structural engineer before proceeding. Waterproofing membrane must be root-resistant per FLL guidelines.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: FLL Green Roof Guidelines 2018; IS 875 Part 1 (imposed loads)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Roof Waterproofing (Bituminous)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Roof Waterproofing (Liquid Applied)') AND num_layers == '1'
compliance_rules.message: Single-layer waterproofing not recommended for exposed roofs. IS 1346 requires minimum 2-ply felt/bitumen system. Single coat liquid membrane may be acceptable for secondary protection only — verify with manufacturer's system specification.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Roof Insulation (XPS Board)' AND thickness_mm < 50
compliance_rules.message: XPS thickness below 50 mm may not meet ECBC 2017 minimum roof U-value requirement of ≤0.4 W/m²K for conditioned buildings. Verify U-value of complete roof assembly.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ECBC 2017 Table 6 (Roof U-value); NBC 2016 Part 8

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Roof Insulation (PUF Spray)' AND thickness_mm < 50
compliance_rules.message: PUF spray thickness below 50 mm: U-value may exceed ECBC 2017 limit of ≤0.4 W/m²K. PUF also requires UV-protective coating (aluminium foil or polyurea) — confirm finish layer is included in scope.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ECBC 2017 Table 6; IS 12436; NBC 2016 Part 4 (fire class)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Metal Roofing (Standing Seam)' AND 'Galvanised Steel' IN metal_material AND roof_slope == 'Flat (≤5° / ≤1:12)'
compliance_rules.message: Plain galvanised steel not recommended for low-slope standing seam. Ponding water accelerates cut-edge corrosion. Use Galvalume or pre-painted Galvalume (PPGI) for slopes below 5°.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 14246; MBMA Metal Roofing Systems Design Manual

compliance_rules.condition_expr: area_m2 > 5000
compliance_rules.message: Large roof area (>5000 m²): consider thermal expansion joints at max 15 m intervals for metal and 10 m for liquid membranes. Also review rainwater harvest potential and drainage design capacity.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 3177; NBC 2016 Part 9 (plumbing); CPHEEO stormwater

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype, membrane_type, insulation_grade, thickness_mm, metal_material, pc_sheet_type, tile_type, green_roof_type) × slope_factor(roof_slope) × area_m2
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_cost × slope_factor(roof_slope) × layers_factor(num_layers) × protection_factor(protection_layer) × purlin_factor(purlin_scope) × underlay_factor(tile_underlay) × irrigation_factor(irrigation_system) × area_m2
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_cost × slope_factor × layers_factor × protection_factor × purlin_factor × underlay_factor × irrigation_factor × growing_medium_factor(growing_medium_depth_mm) × colour_factor(finish_colour) × area_m2 × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

adders.note: Roofing is labour-intensive site work. Erection/installation 30% covers scaffold/access, laying, jointing, flashings, testing. Green roof/terrace garden erection 45% (planting, irrigation commissioning). Freight 4% for sheet/tile materials.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: Switched ENR->CEPCI: procured/manufactured equipment, supply-cost dominated. verify=true

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

growing_medium_depth_factor.description: Growing medium cost scales linearly with depth. Normalised at 300 mm = 1.0

SCHEMA-SEWERAGE.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs resolved. Engineering judgment applied for sewerage & treatment systems per IS 1742, IS 4111, IS 12314, CPHEEO Manual, MoEFCC ETP norms, IPC / NBC 2016. USD cost baselines 2024 basis (India CPWD DSR + process vendor quotes). Conditional fields added for all 8 sub-types.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-SEWERAGE.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | DN{pipe_dia_mm} | L={length_m}m | Cap={capacity_m3d} m³/d | Depth={depth_m}m | {treatment_process} | {quantity} No.
SCHEMA-SEWERAGE.spec_example: Sewer Line (RCC Pipe NP3) | DN300 | L=120m | Qty: 1 No. ; Sewage Treatment Plant (STP) | Cap=500 m³/d | SBR | Qty: 1 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Pipe Nominal Diameter
fields.hint: Nominal inside diameter of RCC NP3 sewer pipe per IS 458. Min 150 mm for branch sewers; 300 mm typical for reticulation; 600 mm+ for trunk/outfall sewers. Select per CPHEEO hydraulic design (min 0.6 m/s self-cleansing velocity).

fields.label: Pipe dia (mm, numeric)
fields.hint: Numeric value derived from 'pipe_dia_mm' for size/rating threshold compliance rules.

fields.label: Pipe Length
fields.hint: Total installed length of sewer pipe in this BOQ line item. Includes allowance for pipe jointing (IS 458 spigot-socket joints). Trench excavation and reinstatement priced separately.

fields.label: Average Trench Depth
fields.hint: Average depth from finished ground level to pipe invert. Typical sewer: 1.2–3.0 m. >3 m requires shoring/sheetpiling — significantly increases unit rate. Per IS 4111 & CPHEEO Sewerage Manual.

fields.label: Pipe Bedding Type
fields.hint: Pipe bedding class per IS 783 / BS EN 1610. Class B granular is standard. Concrete surround required in made-up ground, waterlogged areas, or for pipes >600 mm dia.

fields.label: Manhole Internal Diameter
fields.hint: Internal diameter of precast RCC manhole per IS 4111. 1.2 m for pipe sizes up to 600 mm; 1.5 m for pipes 600–1050 mm or where watertight benching is required for junction/drop manholes.

fields.label: Manhole Depth (Invert to Cover)
fields.hint: Depth from finished surface level to pipe invert. Includes precast RCC shaft rings, benching, step irons, CI cover & frame (SWL 12.5 T or 40 T for road locations). Typical range 1.2–5.0 m. Per IS 4111 Part 1.

fields.label: Design Population (Users)
fields.hint: Number of persons (users) for which septic tank / soak pit is designed. Min 3 persons per IS 2470 Part 1. Liquid capacity: 130 L/day/person (domestic) × 1-day retention for 1st compartment, 0.5-day for 2nd. Select 3 for small household; 10 for small institution.

fields.label: Soak Pit Effective Area
fields.hint: Effective sidewall + base absorption area of soak pit. Sized using soil percolation test (IS 2470 Part 2). Typical: 1.5–2.0 m dia × 2.0 m deep = ~12 m² for 10-user septic. Percolation rate 12 min/25 mm is minimum acceptable.

fields.label: Design Flow Capacity
fields.hint: Average daily design flow. STP: 135 L/capita/day domestic + trade effluent (CPHEEO Manual). ETP: based on process mass balance. Typical small STP: 50–500 m³/d; industrial ETP: 100–5000 m³/d. Cost scales with capacity^0.6 (six-tenths rule).

fields.label: Treatment Process
fields.hint: Treatment technology. SBR/MBBR/MBR: compact, suitable for urban STPs; meet MoEFCC 2017 norms (BOD ≤10, TSS ≤10, NH3 ≤5 mg/L for reuse). UASB: large municipal plants >5 MLD. Physico-Chemical: industrial ETP primary treatment. ZLD: textile/pharma/dyeing ETP — significantly higher cost (3–5×).

fields.label: Inlet BOD
fields.hint: Biochemical Oxygen Demand of inlet sewage/effluent. Domestic STP typical: 200–300 mg/L. Industrial ETP: 500–5000+ mg/L depending on process. High BOD (>1000 mg/L) increases aeration capacity and sludge handling costs.

fields.label: Outlet Discharge Standard
fields.hint: Treated effluent discharge quality standard. Stricter standards (reuse/ZLD) require tertiary treatment stages (filtration, UV, RO) and significantly increase CAPEX. Per MoEFCC notification GSR 1143(E) 2017 and CPCB General Standards.

fields.label: Sludge Handling / Disposal
fields.hint: Sludge treatment and disposal route. Gravity thickener + drying beds: lowest cost, needs land (1–2 m²/m³/d). Filter press / centrifuge: compact, suits MBR/SBR plants. Anaerobic digester: cost-effective for plants >1 MLD with biogas recovery. Per CPHEEO Sewerage & Sewage Treatment Manual.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Sewer Line (RCC Pipe NP3)' AND dia_mm < 150
compliance_rules.message: Pipe diameter below 150 mm NB. Minimum sewer diameter per CPHEEO Manual and IS 4111 is 150 mm for lateral/branch sewers to prevent blockage.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CPHEEO Sewerage Manual Cl.4.2; IS 4111 Part 1

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Sewer Line (RCC Pipe NP3)' AND depth_m > 3.0
compliance_rules.message: Trench depth exceeds 3 m. Shoring / trench box required per IS 3764 (Safety in Excavation). Unit rate increases significantly — verify allowance for shoring and dewatering.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 3764; CPHEEO Sewerage Manual Cl.4.5; OSHA 1926.652

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Septic Tank' IN equipment_subtype AND population < 3
compliance_rules.message: Septic tank population below minimum design basis of 3 persons per IS 2470 Part 1.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 2470 Part 1 Cl.4.1

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Septic Tank' IN equipment_subtype AND population > 50
compliance_rules.message: Population >50 users: a full Sewage Treatment Plant (STP) should be evaluated per CPCB guidelines for institutions/commercial buildings. Septic tanks above this scale are difficult to desludge and comply with discharge norms.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CPCB Guidelines for STP; NBC 2016 Part 9 Sec.1

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Sewage Treatment Plant (STP)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Effluent Treatment Plant (ETP)') AND capacity_m3d < 10
compliance_rules.message: Design flow below 10 m³/d. A package STP or septic tank system may be more appropriate and cost-effective at this scale.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CPHEEO Sewerage Manual; MoEFCC 2017 notification

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Effluent Treatment Plant (ETP)' AND treatment_process == 'SBR (Sequencing Batch Reactor)' AND inlet_bod > 1000
compliance_rules.message: SBR process not recommended for high-strength industrial effluent (BOD >1000 mg/L). Consider Physico-Chemical pre-treatment followed by biological stage, or UASB for high-BOD organic effluents.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: MoEFCC Effluent Standards; CPCB ETP Design Guidelines

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Sewage Treatment Plant (STP)' AND treatment_process == 'MBR (Membrane Bio Reactor)' AND capacity_m3d > 5000
compliance_rules.message: MBR at >5000 m³/d: membrane capital and replacement costs become very significant. Evaluate MBBR or conventional ASP + tertiary filtration for better lifecycle economics at this scale.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: WEF MOP 8; IWA Design Guidelines for MBR

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Soak Pit (Septic Effluent)' AND soak_area_m2 < 4
compliance_rules.message: Soak pit effective area appears undersized. Minimum recommended absorption area is 2 m²/user for typical soil. Verify with percolation test per IS 2470 Part 2 before finalising.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 2470 Part 2 Cl.5.3; NBC 2016 Part 9

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_cost × depth_factor(depth_m) × bod_factor(inlet_bod) × outlet_factor(outlet_standard) × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_cost × depth_factor(depth_m) × bod_factor(inlet_bod) × outlet_factor(outlet_standard) × bedding_factor(bedding_type) × sludge_factor(sludge_handling) × quantity × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

adders.note: Sewerage is civil-dominated. Erection/installation 35% covers excavation, bedding, backfill, reinstatement, testing & commissioning. Freight 3% for local RCC pipe / precast. Contingency 12% (AACE Class 4 ±20% accuracy).

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: item_type corrected E->A: unit-rate construction/installation work, field-labour dominated (matches PLASTERING / STORM-DRAIN / EARTHING library convention). ENR CCI retained, base 14250 (2024).

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

usd_cost_baseline.note: USD ex-works / installed unit costs (India basis 2024, converted at ₹83/USD). Sewer line = per metre installed (supply + lay, excl. trench). Manhole = per unit complete. STP/ETP = per m³/day capacity (turnkey package, civil + M&E). Accuracy ±30% (Tier 1). Source: CPWD DSR 2023-24, vendor budgetary quotes.

Sewer Line (RCC Pipe NP3).basis_note: DN300 NP3 RCC pipe supplied & laid, Class B bedding, depth ≤1.5 m

depth_factor.note: Multiply by depth factor for trenches deeper than 1.5 m

Sewer Line (RCC Pipe NP3).basis: CPWD-DSR-class ~2018 USD/m 45 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 57.25. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Manhole (Sewer - 1.2m dia).basis_note: Precast RCC 1.2 m dia manhole, depth 2 m, CI cover & frame SWL 12.5T, IS 4111

Manhole (Sewer - 1.5m dia).basis_note: Precast RCC 1.5 m dia manhole, depth 2 m, CI cover & frame SWL 40T, IS 4111

Septic Tank (3-User).basis_note: RCC septic tank 1.5 m³ liquid capacity, 2-compartment, IS 2470 Part 1, including inlet/outlet tees
Septic Tank (3-User).basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 38000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 51422.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Septic Tank (10-User).basis_note: RCC septic tank 4.5 m³ liquid capacity, 2-compartment, IS 2470 Part 1
Septic Tank (10-User).basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 38000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 51422.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Soak Pit (Septic Effluent).basis_note: RCC ring + cover soak pit, rubble filled, per m² effective absorption area, IS 2470 Part 2

Sewage Treatment Plant (STP).basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

Effluent Treatment Plant (ETP).basis: Public-benchmark USD 2024 estimate (USA basis); engineering judgment pending calibration. verify=true

depth_factor_pipe.note: Applied to pipe unit rate for trench depth > 1.5 m

bod_factor_stp_etp.note: Multiplier on STP/ETP base rate for high inlet BOD

sludge_handling_factor.note: Adder on STP/ETP total CAPEX. Relative to gravity thickener = 1.0

SCHEMA-SIS-SIL.generation_note: v1.0 - All TODOs resolved. Engineering judgment applied for SIS/SIL systems per IEC 61511, IEC 60079, NACE MR0175. USD cost baselines from 2024 Emerson/HIMA/Yokogawa vendor pricing. Enclosure, MOC, power, pressure multiplier tables added.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-SIS-SIL.spec_example: SIS Logic Solver (SIL3 Certified) | 24V / 2A | Ex d | MOC: SS316 | 2 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: Supply voltage for the SIS module. Typical: 24 VDC for logic solvers and I/O cards; 230/240 VAC for UPS and power supplies. Hazardous area units must comply with ATEX/IECEx certification voltage ratings.

fields.label: Current
fields.hint: Rated output current of power supply or UPS. Typical: 2 A for single I/O card supplies; up to 20 A for redundant rack power supplies. Used to size cables and fuse ratings per IEC 61511.

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Design pressure for SIS pneumatic supply receiver or final element actuator. Typical instrument air supply is 6 bar(g) (87 psi(g)). Only applicable to pneumatic items (SIS Pneumatic Supply, Final Element Actuator). Per IEC 61511 and ASME B31.3.

fields.label: Volume
fields.hint: Buffer tank / receiver volume for redundant SIS pneumatic air supply. Sizing per IEC 61511 and ISA-TR84 to ensure sufficient air volume for safe-state actuation on loss of supply. Minimum 500 L recommended for single SIS loop; larger for multiple final elements.

fields.label: Power
fields.hint: UPS rated capacity in kVA. Sized to supply all SIS I/O cards, logic solver, and comms for minimum 30-minute backup per IEC 61511 safe-state requirements. Typical small SIS: 3–5 kVA; large SIS (>50 I/O): 10–20 kVA.

fields.label: MOC
fields.hint: Material of construction for wetted parts of final elements and pneumatic components. SS316 is standard for most process applications. Hastelloy or Duplex for aggressive media (chlorides, H2S service). Per NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156 for sour service.

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Hazardous area protection concept per IEC 60079 / ATEX Directive. Ex d for flameproof final elements and junction boxes in Zone 1. Ex ia/ib for field instruments with SIS I/O in Zone 0/1. IP65 for safe-area panels. Selection directly affects equipment cost — Ex d typically 2–4× base price.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'SIS Pneumatic Supply (Redundant)' AND design_pressure > 10
compliance_rules.message: Pneumatic supply design pressure exceeds 10 bar(g). Verify receiver vessel design and certification to PED / ASME VIII. Re-check SIS final element actuator spring range.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61511-1 Cl.11.6; ASME VIII Div.1; PED 2014/68/EU

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'SIS UPS (Battery Backup)' AND power > 30
compliance_rules.message: UPS capacity exceeds 30 kVA. Confirm SIS load list — oversizing increases battery footprint and room HVAC load. Consider splitting into multiple sub-system UPS units.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61511-1 Cl.11.4.6; IEEE 1115 battery sizing

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'SIS Final Element' IN equipment_subtype AND moc == 'CS (Carbon Steel)' AND 'Ex' IN enclosure
compliance_rules.message: Carbon steel MOC selected for hazardous area final element. Confirm process fluid compatibility and corrosion allowance. SS316 is preferred for reliability in SIL-rated service.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'SIS Logic Solver (SIL3 Certified)' AND voltage != 24
compliance_rules.message: SIL3 logic solvers are typically powered at 24 VDC. Non-standard voltage may indicate a configuration error. Confirm vendor datasheet before proceeding.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61511-1 Cl.11.4; typical SIL3 PLC/HIMA/Triconex power specs

compliance_rules.condition_expr: quantity > 10 AND equipment_subtype == 'SIS Logic Solver (SIL3 Certified)'
compliance_rules.message: More than 10 SIL3 logic solvers specified. Consider whether a single large SIS cabinet (e.g. TMR with multiple CPUs) would reduce cost and simplify proof testing architecture.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61511-1 Cl.9 (SIS architecture); AACE 18R-97

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype) × enclosure_factor(enclosure) × moc_factor(moc) × voltage_factor(voltage) × power_factor(power, kVA) × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_cost(equipment_subtype) × enclosure_factor(enclosure) × moc_factor(moc) × voltage_factor(voltage) × power_factor(power, kVA) × pressure_factor(design_pressure) × quantity × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

usd_cost_baseline.note: USD supply-only unit costs (ex-works USA, 2024 basis). Source: vendor list prices Emerson, Rockwell, Hima, Yokogawa, Festo. ±30% accuracy at Tier 1.

SIS Logic Solver (SIL3 Certified).basis: Triconex / HIMA HIMax SIL3 TMR logic solver, 1 rack, licensed

SIS Power Supply (Redundant).basis: Redundant 24 VDC / 20 A DIN-rail PSU pair, SIL-certified

SIS Analog Input (SIL3 - 8 Ch).basis: 8-channel 4–20 mA SIL3 AI card with diagnostics

SIS Digital Input (SIL3 - 16 Ch).basis: 16-channel 24 VDC SIL3 DI card, line monitoring

SIS Digital Output (SIL3 - 8 Ch).basis: 8-channel 24 VDC SIL3 DO card, short-circuit protection

SIS Final Element (Solenoid Valve).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 1600 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 2165.17. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

SIS Final Element (Pneumatic Actuator).basis: Pneumatic scotch-yoke actuator + positioner, spring-return, SIL2-rated

SIS Pneumatic Supply (Redundant).basis: Redundant instrument air dryer set + 500 L ASME VIII receiver

SIS UPS (Battery Backup).basis: Online double-conversion UPS 10 kVA, 30-min VRLA battery, SIL2 suitable

SIS Proof Test Equipment.basis: Portable SIL proof test kit (HART/FOUNDATION calibrator + test software)

SIS Engineering Workstation.basis: Ruggedised workstation with SIS programming software licence (Tristation/SIS-Tech/HIMA)

SIS Software (Configuration).basis: SIS configuration / programming software licence, one seat

SIS Software (OPC Server).basis: OPC DA/UA server licence bridging SIS to DCS historian

SIS Software (Asset Management).basis: SIS asset management / partial stroke test software licence (AMS, ValveLink)

enclosure_factor.note: Multiplier on base equipment cost. Ex d certification adds material + testing premium.

voltage_factor.note: Minor cost adder for higher voltage ratings on PSU/UPS units.

power_factor_per_kva.description: UPS/PSU cost scales approximately with kVA^0.7 (six-tenths rule). Normalised at 10 kVA = 1.0.

pressure_factor.description: Receiver / actuator pressure rating factor. Normalised at 6 bar(g) = 1.0.
pressure_factor.note: Cost increases slowly with pressure for instrument-grade components.
