SCHEMA-SKID-PACKAGE.generation_note: All TODOs filled with engineering judgment. Schema contains two cost types: (1) ACTIVITY items (fabrication, erection, connection, alignment) — priced per tonne or per skid, using ENR CCI; (2) SUPPLY+INSTALL items (chemical injection, dosing, metering skids) — priced as engineered packages with capacity-based cost laws. Key fields added: skid_weight_tonnes (Tier 1), skid_type (Tier 2), design_pressure_barg (Tier 2), number_of_connections (Tier 2), dosing_rate_lph (Tier 2), number_of_streams (Tier 3), hazardous_area_class (Tier 3). References: ASME B31.3, API 6D, IEC 61511, IECEx/ATEX directives. | R02 promoted 0.6-engineering-complete->1.0-priced after structural completion + benchmark pricing (validator-gated; no remaining blocking errors).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% (subtype, skid weight, dosing rate, quantity). Tier 2 = Budget +-20% (adds skid type, design pressure, connection count, service/fluid). Tier 3 = Detailed +-10% (adds number of streams, hazardous area class).

SCHEMA-SKID-PACKAGE.spec_example: Chemical injection skid | 200 L/hr | Qty:2 | 40 barg | Process skid (valves, instruments) | Zone 2 (IECEx/ATEX)

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the skid activity or package type. ACTIVITY items (fabrication, erection, connection, alignment) are priced per tonne or per skid. SUPPLY+INSTALL items (Chemical injection/dosing, Metering) are priced as complete engineered packages including procurement, FAT, and commissioning.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical skids or activities. For fabrication/erection, set to 1 and enter total weight below.

fields.label: Skid Steel Weight (tonnes)
fields.hint: Total steel weight of the skid structure in metric tonnes. Primary cost driver for fabrication ($1,800/t at reference) and erection ($680/t). Typical: small utility skid 1-3t, process skid 5-20t, large module 20-100t. For supply+install packages (chemical injection, metering), leave blank — cost is driven by capacity.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Skid fabrication' || equipment_subtype === 'Skid erection' || equipment_subtype === 'Utility skid erection' || equipment_subtype === 'Skid-mounted equipment installation' || equipment_subtype === 'Equipment skid alignment'
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - without skid weight, fabrication and erection estimates are indeterminate

fields.label: Dosing / Injection Capacity (L/hr)
fields.hint: Maximum dosing or injection flow rate in litres per hour. Primary cost driver for chemical injection and dosing skids — cost scales as (capacity/ref)^0.55. Reference rates: Chemical injection $28,000 at 100 L/hr; Chemical dosing $22,000 at 50 L/hr. Typical: inhibitor injection 5-50 L/hr; biocide dosing 20-200 L/hr; scale inhibitor 10-100 L/hr.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Chemical injection skid' || equipment_subtype === 'Chemical dosing skid'
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary cost driver for injection/dosing packages; capacity law cost = ref × (L/ref_L)^0.55

fields.label: Skid / Package Type
options: Simple structural frame (pipe support / equipment support)
Process skid (with valves, instruments, local panel)
Pressure vessel skid (ASME VIII stamped vessels on skid)
fields.default: Process skid (with valves, instruments, local panel)
fields.hint: Skid construction complexity drives fabrication cost significantly. A simple frame is $1,800/t; a complete process module with instrumentation and local panel is $5,040/t (2.8×). Pressure vessel skids require ASME VIII code welding, NDE, and third-party inspection. Modular buildings include structural, HVAC, electrical.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Skid fabrication' || equipment_subtype === 'Skid erection' || equipment_subtype === 'Chemical injection skid' || equipment_subtype === 'Chemical dosing skid' || equipment_subtype === 'Metering skid'
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - complexity factor 1.0-3.5x on fabrication cost; most impactful Tier 2 variable

fields.label: Design Pressure (barg)
fields.hint: Maximum design pressure in barg. Above 40 barg, pipe class steps to ASME 600# and above — wall thickness, flange weight, and bolting increase significantly. Above 100 barg requires specialised high-pressure design, hydraulic testing at 1.5× DP, and witness inspection. Enter 0 for unpressurised / atmospheric skids.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Skid fabrication' || equipment_subtype === 'Chemical injection skid' || equipment_subtype === 'Chemical dosing skid' || equipment_subtype === 'Metering skid' || equipment_subtype === 'Skid piping connection'
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - pressure step-changes at 10/40/100/250 barg drive significant cost increase

fields.label: Number of Piping Connections (tie-ins)
fields.hint: Total number of flanged piping tie-in connections between the skid and the plant piping. Each connection includes: alignment, gasket, bolting, torquing, leak test. Rate $280/EA. Typical: small utility skid 4-8 connections; process skid 8-20 connections; large module 20-50 connections.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Skid piping connection' || equipment_subtype === 'Skid-mounted equipment installation'
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - connection count is the primary quantity driver for piping connection activity

fields.label: Process Fluid / Service
fields.hint: Fluid service determines material selection (wetted parts), gasket/seal specification, pressure testing medium, and safety system requirements. Toxic and corrosive services require special material certification (PMI), additional leak testing, and safety instrumentation (ESD, gas detection). Cryogenic service requires low-temperature impact testing.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Chemical injection skid' || equipment_subtype === 'Chemical dosing skid' || equipment_subtype === 'Metering skid' || equipment_subtype === 'Skid fabrication'
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - toxic/corrosive/cryogenic services add 20-50% to material and testing cost

fields.label: Number of Metering Streams
fields.hint: Number of independent metering lanes (streams) on the skid. Base price ($35,000) is for a single stream. Each additional stream adds ~$18,000 (incremental flow meter, valves, instruments, wiring). Fiscal metering skids with proving loops add 40-60% premium per stream.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - each additional stream adds $18,000; required for detailed metering skid estimate

fields.label: Hazardous Area Classification
options: Safe area — no Ex certification required
fields.default: Safe area — no Ex certification required
fields.hint: IECEx / ATEX hazardous area zone per IEC 60079. Zone 2 (occasional hazard) requires Ex d or Ex e certified instruments — adds ~30% to instrumentation cost. Zone 1 (intermittent hazard) requires more restrictive Ex d certification — adds ~55%. Zone 0 requires intrinsically safe (Ex ia) equipment — adds ~85%. Certification documentation and inspection are also required.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Chemical injection skid' || equipment_subtype === 'Chemical dosing skid' || equipment_subtype === 'Metering skid' || equipment_subtype === 'Skid fabrication'
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - Ex instrumentation factor 1.0-1.85x on instrument cost component (~20% of package cost)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Skid fabrication' AND (skid_weight_tonnes IS None OR skid_weight_tonnes IS None)
compliance_rules.message: Skid weight not entered. The fabrication base rate of $1,800/tonne requires a weight input. Without it, quantity=1 assumes 1 tonne — a 10-tonne skid costs $18,000 but a 50-tonne skid costs $90,000. This estimate may be severely understated. Please enter skid steel weight.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Industry estimating practice: structural steel fabrication is universally priced per metric tonne. RSMeans Heavy Construction 2024 — shop fabrication of structural steel: $1,500–$2,500/tonne for simple frames, $2,800–$5,500/tonne for process modules with instrumentation.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure_barg > 40 AND (equipment_subtype == 'Skid fabrication' OR equipment_subtype == 'Chemical injection skid' OR equipment_subtype == 'Chemical dosing skid' OR equipment_subtype == 'Metering skid')
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure above 40 barg triggers ASME B31.3 Class 1 or higher pipe class (typically ASME 600# or 900# flanges). This significantly increases wall thickness, flange weight, and bolt count. Above 100 barg, hydraulic testing at 1.5× design pressure with third-party witness is typically mandatory. Pressure class step-up adds 45–90% to piping material cost.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3-2022 Process Piping — Table 302.3.5 for pipe allowable stresses; ASME B16.5-2017 for flange pressure-temperature ratings. Step changes in cost occur at ASME Class 150# (≤19.6 barg), 300# (≤51 barg), 600# (≤102 barg), 900# (≤153 barg) at ambient temperature for carbon steel.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (skid_service == 'Chemical — toxic (H2S / ammonia / chlorine)' OR skid_service == 'Cryogenic (LNG / LN2 / LOX)') AND equipment_subtype != 'Utility skid erection'
compliance_rules.message: Toxic or cryogenic service requires: (1) full material traceability and PMI (Positive Material Identification) for all wetted parts; (2) enhanced NDE (dye penetrant or magnetic particle testing on all welds); (3) PSSR (Pressure System Safety Regulations) or equivalent statutory inspection before commissioning; (4) safety instrumented system (SIS) per IEC 61511. These costs must be included — they are not in the standard unit rate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61511-1:2016 — Functional safety for process industry (SIL assessment for toxic/flammable releases). ASME B31.3-2022 Chapter IX — High-pressure piping. UK PSSR 2000 / Indian Factories Act for toxic skid statutory examination. Cryogenic service: ASTM A333 Gr.6 / ASTM A182 F316L impact testing per ASME B31.3 Table A-1.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (hazardous_area_class == 'Zone 1 / Division 1 (IECEx / ATEX / NEC)' OR hazardous_area_class == 'Zone 0 (continuous hazard — rare for skids)') AND (equipment_subtype == 'Chemical injection skid' OR equipment_subtype == 'Chemical dosing skid' OR equipment_subtype == 'Metering skid')
compliance_rules.message: Zone 1 / Zone 0 classification requires Ex d (flameproof) or Ex ia (intrinsically safe) certified instruments and junction boxes per IEC 60079. Third-party Ex inspection of the completed skid by a notified body (e.g. Baseefa, SGS, Bureau Veritas) is required before issue of the ATEX/IECEx certificate. Certification adds $5,000–$15,000 per skid as a separate cost item.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60079-0:2017 — Explosive atmospheres: equipment general requirements. IEC 60079-14:2013 — Electrical installations design, selection and erection. ATEX Directive 2014/34/EU (EU). IECEx scheme (international). NEC Article 500-505 (USA).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Metering skid' AND (skid_service == 'Hydrocarbon — gas (flammable)' OR skid_service == 'Hydrocarbon — liquid (flammable)')
compliance_rules.message: Fiscal / custody transfer metering skids for hydrocarbons require flow meter calibration traceability to national standards, independent proving capability (prover loop or master meter), and approval by the relevant fiscal authority (e.g. UK OFGEM, OIML for export, local weights & measures authority). Fiscal-grade skids cost 40–60% more per stream than process metering. Verify fiscal requirements before finalising estimate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: OIML R117 / OIML R137 — Measuring systems for liquids/gases in fiscal metering. ISO 17089-1 (gas flow metering). API MPMS Chapter 5/6 (liquid/gas measurement). UKOOG / IP Part 6 (UK). Fiscal metering adds: flow computer with audit trail, secondary standards, custody transfer data management.

cost_build_up.note: Composite method — combines unit_rate (per tonne / per skid EA) for activity items and equipment_equation (capacity-based power law) for supply+install packages.

unit_rate_reference.note: USA 2024 basis (RSMeans Heavy Construction, industry benchmarks). All rates in USD.

Skid fabrication.note: Per tonne fabricated steel. Simple frame basis. Complexity factor applied in Tier 2.

Skid erection.note: Per tonne, site erection inc. crane hire allowance. Grade-level open access basis.

Skid-mounted equipment installation.note: Per skid package. Assumes 5-10 equipment items. Connections priced separately.

Skid piping connection.note: Per flanged tie-in connection. Includes gasket, bolt-up, torque, pressure test.

Equipment skid alignment.note: Per skid alignment activity. Laser alignment, 2-3 shaft couplings basis.

Utility skid erection.note: Per utility skid (air, steam, CW). Includes utility tie-ins.

Chemical injection skid.note: Per skid supply+install+FAT. Ref 100 L/hr. Two-segment power law.

Metering skid.note: Per skid. Base $35,000 first stream + $18,000/additional stream.

Chemical dosing skid.note: Per skid supply+install+FAT. Ref 50 L/hr. Two-segment power law.

skid_type_factor.note: Applied to fabrication and supply+install package cost. Reflects engineering complexity beyond base rate.

design_pressure_factor.note: Applied to piping/skid fabrication and supply+install packages. Reflects pipe class and flange cost step-changes.

service_factor.note: Applied to supply+install packages and fabrication. Reflects material upgrade, NDE, and testing cost for hazardous services.

hazardous_area_factor.note: Applied to instrumentation cost component only (~20% of package cost for supply+install items).
hazardous_area_factor.note_calculation: haf_full = [1.30, 1.55, 1.85] for zones 2/1/0. Applied to 20% instrument fraction: total_factor = 0.80 + 0.20 × haf_full

adders.note: erection_installation=0.0 — schema IS the erection/fabrication cost; adding erection on top would double-count. freight_insurance=4% covers skid delivery to site (heavy lift transport for large modules). contingency=12% — skid packages frequently encounter late vendor changes, instrument delivery delays, and late P&ID revisions.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: ENR CCI applied to all items. Activity items (fabrication, erection, connection) are correctly tracked by ENR CCI. Supply+install packages (chemical injection, dosing, metering) are engineered equipment — for highest accuracy, apply CEPCI Equipment sub-index (base 1487) to those items separately. For Class 4 estimates, ENR CCI regional factors are an acceptable approximation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ

usd_cost_baseline.note: Base = USD at USA level (matches ENR_CCI base 14250 @ 2024). Engine: base_rate = usd_cost_baseline.items[equipment_subtype].usd_per_*, then x index_now/14250 x regional_factor x fx. verify=true entries are public-benchmark estimates pending calibration.

Skid fabrication.basis: Restructured from existing schema rate source (unit_rate_reference.activity_items.Skid fabrication) — same pricing basis, exposed as standard rate table. verify=true

Skid erection.basis: Restructured from existing schema rate source (unit_rate_reference.activity_items.Skid erection) — same pricing basis, exposed as standard rate table. verify=true

Skid-mounted equipment installation.basis: Restructured from existing schema rate source (unit_rate_reference.activity_items.Skid-mounted equipment installation) — same pricing basis, exposed as standard rate table. verify=true

Skid piping connection.basis: Restructured from existing schema rate source (unit_rate_reference.activity_items.Skid piping connection) — same pricing basis, exposed as standard rate table. verify=true

Equipment skid alignment.basis: Restructured from existing schema rate source (unit_rate_reference.activity_items.Equipment skid alignment) — same pricing basis, exposed as standard rate table. verify=true

Chemical injection skid.basis: Restructured from existing schema rate source (unit_rate_reference.supply_install_packages.Chemical injection skid) — same pricing basis, exposed as standard rate table. verify=true

Utility skid erection.basis: Restructured from existing schema rate source (unit_rate_reference.activity_items.Utility skid erection) — same pricing basis, exposed as standard rate table. verify=true

Metering skid.basis: Restructured from existing schema rate source (unit_rate_reference.supply_install_packages.Metering skid) — same pricing basis, exposed as standard rate table. verify=true

Chemical dosing skid.basis: Restructured from existing schema rate source (unit_rate_reference.supply_install_packages.Chemical dosing skid) — same pricing basis, exposed as standard rate table. verify=true

SCHEMA-STORM-DRAIN.generation_note: All TODOs filled with engineering judgment. Civil drainage scope: pipe/channel subtypes priced per linear metre (LM); chamber/manhole subtypes priced per EA. Diameter field corrected to mm (range 150-1500mm). Key cost drivers: pipe diameter, installation depth, soil type, pipe material. Cost index: ENR CCI correct for civil construction site activity. References: IS 1742, IS 4111, BS EN 1295-1, ASCE MOP 36, RSMeans Civil 2024. | R02 promoted 0.6-engineering-complete->1.0-priced after structural completion + benchmark pricing (validator-gated; no remaining blocking errors).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% (subtype, pipe length, pipe diameter, quantity). Tier 2 = Budget +-20% (adds installation depth, pipe material, soil type, manhole depth). Tier 3 = Detailed +-10% (adds cover/frame type).

SCHEMA-STORM-DRAIN.spec_example: Storm Water Drain (RCC Pipe) | 150 LM | DN450 | RCC (Reinforced Concrete) | Qty:1 | 2.5m depth | Normal (clay/silt/sand)

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select drainage item type. PIPE/CHANNEL items (Open Channel, RCC Pipe, Box Culvert) are priced per linear metre — enter length below. CHAMBER/MANHOLE items are priced per EA — enter quantity.

fields.label: Pipe / Channel Diameter (mm)
fields.hint: Internal diameter of pipe or channel in mm. Primary cost driver for RCC pipe and box culvert — cost scales as (D/450)^0.80. Standard RCC sizes: 150, 225, 300, 375, 450, 525, 600, 750, 900, 1050, 1200, 1500 mm. For box culverts enter the shorter cross-section dimension. Not required for manhole/chamber items.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Storm Water Drain (RCC Pipe)' || equipment_subtype === 'Storm Water Drain (Open Channel)' || equipment_subtype === 'Storm Water Drain (Box Culvert)'
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary cost driver for pipe and channel items; cost scales as power law with diameter

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item. For pipe/channel subtypes set to 1 and enter length in the pipe length field. For manholes/chambers this is the number of units.

fields.label: Pipe / Channel Length (m)
fields.hint: Total length of pipe run, open channel, or box culvert in linear metres. This is the primary quantity multiplier for pipe/channel items. Include all bends and fittings — typically add 5-8% to straight run length for fittings allowance.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Storm Water Drain (RCC Pipe)' || equipment_subtype === 'Storm Water Drain (Open Channel)' || equipment_subtype === 'Storm Water Drain (Box Culvert)'
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - without pipe length, cost for pipe/channel items is indeterminate

fields.label: Installation Depth — Pipe Invert (m)
fields.hint: Depth from ground surface to pipe invert (bottom of pipe). Reference depth is 1.5 m. Each additional metre adds shoring, deeper excavation plant, and longer backfill time. At >3m depth, trench support (sheet piling or shoring) is typically required per IS 3764 / OSHA 29 CFR 1926.652.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Storm Water Drain (RCC Pipe)' || equipment_subtype === 'Storm Water Drain (Open Channel)' || equipment_subtype === 'Storm Water Drain (Box Culvert)'
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - depth factor 1.0-2.8x; trench support required above 3m depth

fields.label: Pipe Material
fields.hint: Pipe material drives supply cost significantly. uPVC is cheapest but limited to ≤600mm. RCC is standard for storm drains >300mm. HDPE is flexible and corrosion resistant. GRP for chemical resistance. Ductile iron for high-pressure or critical crossings. Material also determines joint type and bedding class.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Storm Water Drain (RCC Pipe)' || equipment_subtype === 'Storm Water Drain (Open Channel)'
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - material factor 0.75-1.80x relative to RCC baseline

fields.label: Soil / Ground Condition
options: Mixed (gravelly clay / dense sand / soft rock)
fields.hint: Ground condition is the single largest variable in drainage excavation cost. Rock excavation with blasting costs 3-5× normal ground. Waterlogged ground requires continuous dewatering pumps and sheet piling — add 100% to excavation cost. Contaminated ground requires licensed disposal and can add 50-200% to earthworks cost.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - soil factor 1.0-5.0x on excavation element; most impactful variable after depth

fields.label: Manhole / Chamber Depth (m)
fields.hint: Depth of manhole or chamber from finished surface to invert. Base rate is for 1.5m depth. Each additional metre adds shaft rings, extra excavation, and benching. Depths >3m require confined space entry procedures (IS 3764 / OSHA). Maximum practical precast manhole depth: 6m.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - each additional metre beyond 1.5m adds $900-$1,400 per manhole

fields.label: Cover / Frame Type
options: Light duty — pedestrian only (Class A: 5 kN)
Medium duty — light vehicles (Class B125: 12.5 tonne)
Heavy duty — road traffic (Class D400: 40 tonne)
Extra heavy — industrial / port (Class E600: 60 tonne)
fields.default: Heavy duty — road traffic (Class D400: 40 tonne)
fields.hint: Cover and frame loading class per EN 124 / IS 1726. Class D400 (40t) is the standard for all road surfaces. Class B125 for footpaths. Recessed covers add 30-50% cost but blend with paving. Gas-tight covers required for covers within 3m of buildings or over sewers carrying H₂S.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - cover class adds $80-$600/EA; D400 is reference

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Soak Pit (Percolation)' AND soil_type == 'Waterlogged / high water table (dewatering required)'
compliance_rules.message: Soak pits (percolation pits) are NOT effective in waterlogged or high water table conditions — water cannot percolate when the ground is already saturated. Consider an alternative disposal route (storm sewer connection, detention tank, or controlled outfall). Using a soak pit in these conditions is an engineering error.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: BS EN 752:2017 — Drain and sewer systems outside buildings. Section 7: Infiltration systems require minimum 1m separation between pit base and seasonal high water table. IS 2470 Part 1 — Code of practice for installation of septic tanks: percolation tests required before soak pit design.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: pipe_diameter_mm IS not None AND pipe_diameter_mm < 150 AND (equipment_subtype == 'Storm Water Drain (RCC Pipe)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Storm Water Drain (Box Culvert)')
compliance_rules.message: Storm water drainage pipes below DN150 are non-standard and prone to blockage from debris and siltation. Minimum recommended diameter for storm drains is DN150 (6 inch); DN300 is the practical minimum for surface water drains serving roads and paved areas per standard drainage design practice.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 1742:2015 — Code of practice for building drainage: minimum drain size DN100 for internal, DN150 for external. CIRIA C697 (UK): minimum 150mm for all surface water drains. ASCE MOP 36: minimum 12-inch (300mm) for public storm sewers.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (installation_depth_m > 3.0 OR manhole_depth_m > 3.0)
compliance_rules.message: Installation depth exceeds 3.0m. Trench support (sheet piling, hydraulic shoring, or benching) is mandatory per IS 3764 / OSHA 29 CFR 1926.652 to prevent trench collapse. Confined space entry procedures (permit to work, gas monitoring, rescue equipment) required for manhole construction below 3m. These costs must be added as separate BOQ items — they are not included in the unit rate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 3764:1992 — Code of safety for excavation work: shoring required for trenches >1.5m in unstable soil or >3m in any soil. OSHA 29 CFR 1926.652 — Requirements for protective systems in excavations. IS 4711: confined space entry for manholes >1.2m deep.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: pipe_diameter_mm > 600 AND pipe_material == 'uPVC (Unplasticised PVC)'
compliance_rules.message: uPVC pipes are generally limited to DN600 maximum for gravity drainage applications. Above DN600, consider RCC, HDPE, or GRP pipe which have better ring stiffness for large diameter buried applications. Large-diameter uPVC requires higher stiffness class (SN8 or SN16) and careful bedding design.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: BS EN 1401-1:2019 — Plastic piping systems: uPVC for underground drainage. Practical market availability limits uPVC to DN600 in most regions. BS EN 1295-1: Structural design of buried pipelines — ring stiffness requirements increase sharply with diameter.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: soil_type == 'Contaminated ground (special disposal required)'
compliance_rules.message: Contaminated ground conditions require a site-specific remediation and waste disposal plan. Excavated material must be profiled (chemical analysis) before disposal. Licensed hazardous waste carrier and registered disposal site required. Disposal costs for contaminated soil typically add £50-£150/tonne (UK) or equivalent. This must be separately estimated — it is not included in the drainage unit rate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: UK Waste (England and Wales) Regulations 2011. Environment Agency Waste Acceptance Criteria. IS 15386 (India) — Hazardous waste management. CERCLA/RCRA regulations (USA). A Preliminary Risk Assessment (PRA) and Detailed Quantitative Risk Assessment (DQRA) are required before work commences.

unit_rate_reference.note: USA 2024 basis (RSMeans Civil Cost Data, ASCE standards). Two rate categories: (1) per LINEAR METRE for pipes/channels — rate × pipe_length_m; (2) per EA for chambers/manholes — rate × quantity. Rates include: supply of pipe/precast unit, excavation to 1.5m depth in normal ground, Class B granular bedding, haunching and surround, backfill and compaction, testing. Excludes: road reinstatement, flow diversion, CCTV survey.

depth_factor_pipe.note: Applied to pipe/channel items. Reference depth = 1.5m invert. Interpolate linearly between values.

material_factor.note: Applied to pipe/channel supply cost component only (~55% of total rate). Labour component unchanged.

cover_factor.note: Adder per chamber/manhole EA above D400 baseline.

adders.note: erection_installation = 0.0 because unit rates already include full installation (excavation, lay, bed, haunch, backfill, test). freight_insurance = 3% covers pipe/precast delivery to site. contingency raised to 12% for civil drainage — ground conditions and existing services encounters are common cause of cost overrun.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: ENR CCI is correct for civil drainage — it tracks construction labour wages and materials (steel, concrete, aggregates) which are the dominant cost components. Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor. For 2026 project, use current ENR CCI value (~14,800 projected).

India.local_ref: DSR 2023-24 — civil drainage earthworks and RCC

Middle East (GCC).local_ref: Expat labour + imported materials — GCC civil

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines labour, civil, and inland freight factors.

import_duty.note: Indicative only — most drainage pipes and precast manholes are sourced locally in India. Import duty applies only to specialist items (GRP pipe, ductile iron fittings).

usd_cost_baseline.note: Base = USD at USA level (matches ENR_CCI base 14250 @ 2024). Engine: base_rate = usd_cost_baseline.items[equipment_subtype].usd_per_*, then x index_now/14250 x regional_factor x fx. verify=true entries are public-benchmark estimates pending calibration.

SCHEMA-STRUCT-CIVIL-ACT.generation_note: PRODUCTION READY v1.0 — Fixed from v1.0: C1 steel_weight_tonnes Tier 1 field added (supply/fab/erection priced per tonne); C2 Tier 2/Full formulas now incorporate steel_grade_factor, connection_factor, access_factor, paint_spec_factor; C3 erection_installation adder corrected to 0.0 (schema IS the activity cost); C4 spec_formula corrected to JS template literal syntax; C5 default subtype corrected to Structural erection; C6 F-001 cost_driver corrected to primary. Added: F-004 steel_grade, F-005 connection_type, F-006 access_difficulty, F-007 paint_spec (Tier 2); F-008 section_complexity (Tier 3). Added R-002 HSFG cert, R-003 paint corrosion class, R-004 erection inspection rules. | R02 promoted 0.7-production-ready->1.0-priced after structural completion + benchmark pricing (validator-gated; no remaining blocking errors). [note synced to v1.0-priced release]

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% (subtype, tonnage, quantity). Tier 2 = Budget +-20% (adds steel grade, connection type, access difficulty, paint spec). Tier 3 = Detailed +-10% (adds section complexity).

SCHEMA-STRUCT-CIVIL-ACT.spec_example: Structural erection | 85 t | Qty:1 | S355 | Bolted | Grade level - open access

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific activity type. Supply = material cost per tonne. Fabrication = workshop labour per tonne. Erection = site installation labour per tonne. Bolting = per-bolt activity. Painting = per m² or per tonne. Inspection = per visit.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of line items / packages. For single-scope items set to 1 and use the steel weight field below. For inspection use quantity as number of visits.

fields.label: Steel / Structure Weight (tonnes)
fields.hint: Total steel weight in metric tonnes. This is the primary cost driver for supply, fabrication, and erection — the unit rates ($/t) are multiplied by this weight. Typical industrial project: pipe rack 50-500 t; equipment structure 10-100 t; building frame 50-300 t. Leave blank for bolting/inspection/alignment activities.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Structural steel supply' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural fabrication' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural erection' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural retrofitting' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural painting (industrial)'
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - without this field, supply/fab/erection estimates are meaningless (1 Job could be 1t or 10,000t)

fields.label: Steel Grade
fields.hint: Steel grade per EN 10025 / ASTM. S355 is the most common industrial structural grade. S460/S690 reduces weight but increases material cost. Stainless and galvanised carry 3-5x material cost premium. Grade also determines weld procedure and paint system.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Structural steel supply' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural fabrication' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural erection' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural retrofitting'
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - grade multiplier 1.00-5.00 range; S355 baseline

fields.label: Connection Type
fields.hint: Connection method drives fabrication and erection man-hours significantly. Full-penetration butt welding requires NDE (UT/RT) and adds 40-60% to fabrication cost vs bolted. HSFG bolting requires torque verification. Weld quality class affects inspection scope.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Structural fabrication' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural erection' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural bolting (HSFG)' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural retrofitting'
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - connection type is a 1.0-1.65x fabrication cost multiplier

fields.label: Access / Erection Difficulty
fields.hint: Erection access difficulty multiplier. Scaffold and crane hire are often the largest single variable in structural erection cost — elevated work above 15m can cost 2× grade-level work. Confined access (inside existing structures) adds 30-60%.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Structural erection' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural alignment' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural leveling' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural retrofitting' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural painting (industrial)'
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - access factor 1.0 to 2.2x; dominant variable for elevated erection

fields.label: Paint / Corrosion Protection Specification
options: C3 - Medium corrosivity (primer + intermediate + finish, 3 coats)
C5-I - Very high industrial (blast + zinc primer + epoxy + polyurethane)
fields.default: C3 - Medium corrosivity (primer + intermediate + finish, 3 coats)
fields.hint: ISO 12944 corrosion category. C2 = indoor dry. C3 = urban/industrial atmosphere. C4 = chemical plant / coastal. C5 = offshore / severe industrial. A C5-I system (epoxy + PU) costs 4-5× a C2 single-coat system per m². Paint system must also match steel grade and surface preparation class (Sa 2.5 required for C4/C5).
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Structural painting (industrial)' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural steel supply' || equipment_subtype === 'Structural fabrication'
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - paint spec is 1.0-5.0x cost multiplier; often underestimated at Class 4

fields.label: Section / Structure Complexity
options: Medium (compound sections, curved members, moderate connections)
Very complex (3D nodes, special sections, tight tolerances)
fields.hint: Structural complexity drives fabrication man-hours beyond the basic tonne rate. A complex truss can take 3-4× the fabrication hours per tonne vs simple beams and columns. Very complex 3D nodes require CNC machining and tight dimensional control.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - complexity factor 1.0-3.5x fabrication cost; required for detailed estimate

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Structural retrofitting' AND quantity > 100
compliance_rules.message: Large number of retrofitting jobs (>100) may require additional engineering and coordination. Consider breaking into sub-items with individual weight and complexity inputs for a more accurate estimate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Industry standard practice for structural retrofitting projects — quantities exceeding 100 distinct locations typically introduce scope complexity that standard unit rates may not fully capture (AACE RP 56R-08 — Planning and Scheduling of Capital Construction Projects).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (connection_type == 'Bolted (HSFG / friction grip)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Structural bolting (HSFG)') AND equipment_subtype != 'Structural inspection'
compliance_rules.message: HSFG (High Strength Friction Grip) bolting requires: (1) proof load test certificates for bolts per BS EN 14399 or ASTM F3125; (2) calibrated torque wrench or tension indicator; (3) third-party torque verification before bolt heads are covered. Include inspection hold point in construction ITP.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: BS EN 14399-4:2015 — High-strength structural bolting assemblies for preloading. AISC 360-22 Section J3 — Bolted joints. IS 800:2007 Clause 10.6 — High strength bolts (India). Inadequate HSFG installation is a leading cause of structural connection failure.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (paint_spec == 'C4 - High corrosivity (blast + 3-coat epoxy system)' OR paint_spec == 'C5-I - Very high industrial (blast + zinc primer + epoxy + polyurethane)' OR paint_spec == 'C5-M - Very high marine (blast + 4-coat system)') AND (equipment_subtype == 'Structural painting (industrial)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Structural steel supply' OR equipment_subtype == 'Structural fabrication')
compliance_rules.message: C4/C5 paint systems require Sa 2.5 abrasive blast cleaning (ISO 8501-1) before application. Surface preparation represents 40-60% of total painting cost. Ensure blast cleaning is included in scope and that ambient conditions (humidity <85%, steel temp >3°C above dew point) are specified in the paint procedure.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 12944-4:2018 — Types of surface and surface preparation. ISO 8501-1:2007 — Surface cleanliness grades. SSPC SP-10 (Near-White Blast) equivalent to Sa 2.5. Inadequate surface prep is the leading cause of premature coating failure in industrial environments.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Structural erection' AND steel_weight_tonnes > 500
compliance_rules.message: Structural erection above 500 tonnes typically requires a formal lift study, crane pick plan, and erection sequence drawing. Third-party structural inspection at minimum 10% of connections is recommended. Ensure crane hire and rigging costs are included as separate BOQ line items — they are NOT included in the erection unit rate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 800:2007 Clause 15 — Fabrication and erection. AISC Code of Standard Practice Section 7 — Erection. ASME B30.5 (mobile cranes). Large erection packages (>500t) require pre-erection survey, anchor bolt survey, and temporary bracing plan.

unit_rates_usd_per_tonne.note: USA 2024 average basis (RSMeans Heavy Construction, AISC data). Rates in USD per metric tonne for tonnage-based items. For non-tonnage items (bolting, alignment, inspection) rates are per EA/visit as noted.

Structural steel supply.note: Mill/stockholder supply. S355 basis. Includes mill cert, delivery to site.

Structural fabrication.note: Workshop fabrication labour + consumables. Simple sections basis. Excludes material.

Structural erection.note: Site erection labour. Grade-level open access basis. Excludes crane hire (add separately).

Structural bolting (HSFG).note: Per HSFG bolt installed and torque-verified. Includes bolt, nut, washers, labour.

Structural alignment.note: Per alignment location/joint. Survey instrument + skilled fitter.

Structural leveling.note: Per leveling point. Pack plates, shims, grouting materials included.

Structural painting (industrial).note: C3 basis (3-coat system). Includes blast clean, primer, intermediate, finish coats.

Structural retrofitting.note: Retrofitting labour per tonne of new steel added. Complex access assumed.

Structural inspection.note: Per inspection visit. Includes inspector, report, travel within 50 km.

steel_grade_factor.note: Applied to supply and fabrication cost. S355 = reference 1.0.

connection_factor.note: Applied to fabrication and erection labour. Bolted standard = reference 1.0.

access_factor.note: Applied to erection, alignment, leveling, painting, and retrofitting labour.

paint_spec_factor.note: Applied to painting cost per m². C3 3-coat = reference 1.0 (rate $45/m²).

section_complexity_factor.note: Applied to fabrication cost only (Tier 3). Simple = reference 1.0.

adders.note: erection_installation = 0.0 because this schema IS the erection/fabrication activity cost — adding 18% erection on top of erection rates would double-count. freight_insurance = 3% covers steel delivery to site and consumables (paint, bolts). For structural steel supply, increase freight_insurance to 0.05 manually if long-haul delivery.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. ENR CCI is correct for structural steel site activities (fabrication, erection, painting) and steel material supply.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10% (not yet implemented for this schema).

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields and units are shown below.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Enter quantity in the unit shown in the Quantity Unit field. Bulk structural steel (columns, trusses, purlins, bracing) must be entered in metric tonnes (MT).

fields.label: Quantity Unit
fields.hint: Auto-derived from sub-type. Bulk steel = MT; area items = m²; linear items = m; discrete items = No.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - always visible. Displays the correct unit for the selected sub-type.

fields.label: Material Grade / Specification
fields.hint: Select material grade. SS grades apply to handrail items only. Higher grade = cost adder of ~8-15%.

fields.label: Surface Treatment / Coating
fields.hint: Coating adds to supply cost. HDG adds ~12-18%, fire intumescent adds ~20-35% over bare steel.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - significant cost driver for galvanised and fire-rated items

fields.label: Supply Scope
fields.hint: Erection scope adds ~18-25% over supply+fab cost depending on site complexity.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - determines whether erection adder applies

fields.label: PEB Floor Area
fields.hint: For Pre-Engineered Buildings only. Enter total floor area covered. PEB is priced per m² of covered area.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype == 'Pre-Engineered Building (PEB)'

fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - required for PEB cost calculation; replaces tonnage-based approach

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype IN ['Structural Steel (Columns/Beams)', 'Structural Steel (Trusses)', 'Structural Steel (Purlins)', 'Structural Steel (Bracing)']) AND (quantity > 500)
compliance_rules.message: Large tonnage order (>500 MT) — consider bulk procurement / package rate negotiation with fabricator.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Orders above 500 MT of structural steel typically qualify for 5-12% volume discount from fabricators. Verify with at least 3 vendor quotes.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: quantity <= 0
compliance_rules.message: Quantity must be greater than zero.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Minimum quantity validation.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype IN ['Handrail (SS 304)', 'Handrail (SS 304)']) AND (coating_type != 'Bare steel (no coating)')
compliance_rules.message: Stainless steel handrail selected with a paint/galvanising coating — verify this is intentional. SS is typically supplied bare or with passivation only.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Painting over SS requires special primer and surface prep; rarely specified.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Pre-Engineered Building (PEB)') AND (peb_floor_area IS None OR peb_floor_area <= 0)
compliance_rules.message: PEB selected but floor area not entered. Cost estimate will default to per-unit basis which is inaccurate for PEB. Enter floor area for a reliable estimate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: PEB cost is vendor-quoted on a per-m² basis; per-unit pricing is not meaningful for this item type.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype IN ['Structural Steel (Columns/Beams)', 'Structural Steel (Trusses)', 'Structural Steel (Purlins)', 'Structural Steel (Bracing)']) AND (quantity < 1)
compliance_rules.message: Bulk structural steel quantity below 1 MT — confirm units are in metric tonnes, not pieces.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Common data entry error: entering number of pieces instead of tonnage for bulk steel.

usd_cost_baseline.note: Base rates in USD. Unit = native pricing unit for each sub-type. Year = 2024. Source = industry benchmarks (RSMeans, Means Mechanical, Indian market surveys). All rates are SUPPLY + FABRICATION only; erection is added via cost_build_up adder. Apply regional_factors.regions[region].sub_regions[sub_region].equipment_factor to convert to local cost level.

Structural Steel (Columns/Beams).basis: IS 2062 E250, shop-fabricated, 2-coat epoxy, ex-yard. Premium grades +10-15%. Complex connections +8%.

Structural Steel (Trusses).basis: IS 2062 E250, shop-fabricated trusses including gussets and end plates, 2-coat epoxy.

Structural Steel (Purlins).basis: Cold-formed Z/C sections or hot-rolled angles. Standard roof/wall purlins.

Structural Steel (Bracing).basis: Hollow sections or angles, shop-fabricated with end plates.

Pre-Engineered Building (PEB).basis: Supply + erect, single-storey industrial PEB including portal frames, purlins, cladding (single-skin), gutters. Excludes foundation and civil. Complex spans or multi-storey: +20-40%.
Pre-Engineered Building (PEB).note: PEB is a package rate per m² of covered area. Use peb_floor_area field. quantity field = 1 for PEB.

Mezzanine Floor (Steel).basis: MS structural framing + steel grating or chequered plate deck + handrail, supply + erect. Excludes staircase.

Steel Grating (Floor).basis: Standard MS bar grating 30×5 mm bearing bar, 41×41 pitch, galvanised, supply only. HDG included in base.

Chequered Plate (Floor).basis: MS chequered plate 6 mm, including supporting MS frame, red-oxide primed, supply only.

Handrail (MS Pipe).basis: MS pipe handrail, 2-rail system, 40 NB top rail + 25 NB mid rail + MS flat uprights @ 1.5 m c/c. 2-coat epoxy finish. Supply + erect.

Handrail (SS 304).basis: SS 304 pipe handrail, 2-rail system, 40 NB top rail + 25 NB mid rail. Passivated finish. Supply + erect.

Staircase (MS).basis: Single flight straight MS staircase, ~3 m vertical rise, 900 mm wide, chequered plate treads, MS handrail both sides, 2-coat epoxy. Supply + erect. Multi-flight or spiral: use multiplier 1.4-1.8×.

Ladder (Cage Type).basis: MS cage ladder, ~4 m height, 450 mm wide, 300 mm rung pitch, full cage hoop from 2.2 m, HDG finish. Supply + erect. Rate per ladder (not per metre).

Platform (MS Grating).basis: MS structural platform including primary/secondary beams, steel grating floor, handrail on open sides, kickplate. Supply + erect. Excludes staircase/ladder access.

Cable Tray Support (MS).basis: MS cable tray support (single tier), ISMC 75 or equivalent channel, galvanised, including anchor bolts and u-clamps. Supply + erect. Rate per support location.

coating_adders.note: Multiply base rate by (1 + adder_fraction) for non-default coating

grade_adders.note: Multiply base rate by (1 + adder_fraction) for non-standard grade

supply_scope_adders.note: Applies to items where erection is optional. Base rates already include erection for most items.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: tier_1_cost * (1 + freight_insurance)
cost_build_up.full_formula: tier_2_cost — all major adders included in tier_1 via baseline rates
cost_build_up.peb_override: For PEB: replace quantity * usd_per_unit with peb_floor_area * usd_cost_baseline.items['Pre-Engineered Building (PEB)'].usd_per_unit. Set quantity = 1.

adders.note: erection_installation adder applies only when supply_scope = 'Supply only' or 'Supply + fabrication'. Baseline rates for most items already include erection.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'hydrotest' IN equipment_subtype OR 'hydrotesting' IN equipment_subtype OR equipment_subtype == 'Hydrotreater piping'
compliance_rules.message: Hydrostatic testing must be performed in accordance with ASME B31.3 (process piping) or ASME BPVC Section VIII (pressure vessels). Ensure test pressure does not exceed 1.5× design pressure for vessels and 1.5× design pressure for piping (unless otherwise specified). Verify flange ratings and temporary spade requirements.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3 Process Piping; ASME BPVC Section VIII Division 1

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Equipment cleaning (pre-commissioning)'
compliance_rules.message: Pre‑commissioning cleaning typically includes chemical cleaning, flushing, or mechanical cleaning. Ensure compatibility with metallurgy and process fluids. Refer to project‑specific cleaning specifications (e.g., ASTM A380 for stainless steel).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Industry best practice per ASTM A380 / NACE SP0178

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Utility system commissioning'
compliance_rules.message: Utility system commissioning involves energising live systems (instrument air, cooling water, steam, nitrogen). A Pre-Startup Safety Review (PSSR) is mandatory before introduction of hazardous energy. Ensure P&ID walkdown, loop checks, relief valve installation, and blind list clearance are completed prior to commissioning.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 (PSM) – Pre-Startup Safety Review; IEC 61511 Functional Safety; industry best practice per ANSI/ISA-5.1.

SCHEMA-TRANSFORMER-CTRL.spec_example: Control Transformer, 50 kVA, 415 V, 50 Hz, Enclosure IP20

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Power
fields.hint: Rated apparent power in kVA. Typical range for control transformers: 10–500 kVA. Drives primary cost model.

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: Primary voltage rating (line-to-line). Common values: 230 V, 415 V, 433 V. 415 V is standard in India/UK regions.

fields.label: Frequency
fields.hint: Supply frequency. Select 50 Hz for India, UK, Europe, Middle East; 60 Hz for USA, Canada.

fields.label: Drive Type
fields.hint: Type of motor starter or drive that the transformer will supply. 'VFD' indicates use with variable frequency drives, requiring higher harmonic withstand and K-factor consideration.

fields.label: Enclosure
fields.hint: Ingress protection rating. IP20 suitable for indoor dry locations; IP54/IP65 for outdoor or dusty environments. Higher IP rating increases cost.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: drive_type == 'VFD' AND enclosure == 'IP20'
compliance_rules.message: When supplying VFD loads, consider a transformer with K-factor rating (typically K-4 or K-13) and possibly forced air cooling. IP20 may be acceptable indoors, but ensure adequate ventilation.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEEE Std 1100 (Emerald Book) – Powering and Grounding Electronic Equipment; IEC 61378-1 Converter Transformers.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: power > 200
compliance_rules.message: Transformer rating exceeds 200 kVA. For dry-type units, verify available fault current and consider impedance requirements (typically 5-6% for control transformers).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60076-11 Dry-type power transformers.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: enclosure == 'IP65' AND equipment_subtype == 'Control Transformer'
compliance_rules.message: IP65 enclosure on a Control Transformer is unusual and likely overspecified. Verify installation environment. Consider IP54 unless washdown or submersion risk is confirmed.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60529 Degrees of protection provided by enclosures; typical control transformer installations are indoor dry-type (IP20-IP23).

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: quantity * base_rate_tier2 * regional_cost_index.factor * enclosure_factor * drive_type_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: tier_2_formula * (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation + contingency)

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: Switched ENR->CEPCI: procured/manufactured equipment, supply-cost dominated. verify=true

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% (subtype, power kVA, system voltage, motor power, quantity). Tier 2 = Budget +-20% (adds frequency, impedance %Z, LV voltage, current, fault level, standard, enclosure). Tier 3 = Detailed +-10% (adds cooling class).

SCHEMA-TRANSFORMER-POWER.spec_example: Distribution Transformer (Oil Immersed - 11/0.415kV) | 1000 kVA | 11kV | IEC 60076 | IP54 | Qty:2

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
options: Dry Type Transformer 11kV/433V 1600 kVA Cast Resin
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment type. Multiple items describing similar transformers are retained per the BOQ schedule — use whichever matches your datasheet. Power, voltage, and kVA fields below refine the estimate.

fields.label: System Voltage (HV side, kV)
fields.hint: Primary (HV) winding voltage in kV. Drives insulation class, bushing size, and overall cost. Common: 6.6, 11, 33, 66, 132 kV. For distribution transformers (11/0.415kV) enter 11. For 132/33kV enter 132.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary cost driver alongside kVA; insulation cost scales with voltage class

fields.label: System Frequency (Hz)
fields.hint: System frequency. 50 Hz standard for IEC/India/Europe/GCC/ASEAN. 60 Hz for Americas and parts of Japan. 60 Hz transformers have slightly different core design — minor cost impact (~2-3%).
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - minor cost impact; important for correct standard selection

fields.label: Impedance Voltage (%Z)
fields.hint: Percentage impedance voltage at rated current per IEC 60076-1 Table 3. Typical: 4-6% for distribution transformers (≤3.15 MVA), 6-10% for power transformers (>3.15 MVA). Higher %Z = better short-circuit protection but worse voltage regulation. Affects winding design cost.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - affects winding copper content and short-circuit withstand design cost

fields.label: Rated Power (kVA)
fields.hint: Rated apparent power in kVA. Standard IEC sizes: 100, 250, 500, 1000, 1600, 2000, 2500, 3150 kVA (distribution); 5000, 10000, 20000, 40000, 63000 kVA (power). Cost scales as kVA^0.70–0.78 depending on voltage class. Enter in kVA (1 MVA = 1000 kVA).
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary cost driver; formula uses power law scaling vs voltage class

fields.label: LV Voltage (V)
fields.hint: Secondary (LV) winding voltage. Standard IEC: 400V, 415V, 433V. For MCC/VFD/UPS this is the supply bus voltage. Must match system nominal voltage — wrong LV voltage leads to over/under-excitation.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype.includes('MCC') || equipment_subtype.includes('VFD') || equipment_subtype.includes('UPS') || equipment_subtype.includes('Motor Control') || equipment_subtype.includes('Transformer')
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - LV winding design and bushing size affected by secondary voltage

fields.label: Rated Current / Busbar Rating (A)
fields.hint: Rated continuous current for MCC/switchgear busbars or transformer LV terminals. Standard busbar ratings: 630, 800, 1000, 1250, 1600, 2000, 2500, 3200 A. MCC panel cost scales approximately as current^0.35.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - busbar rating drives copper cross-section and switchgear frame size cost

fields.label: Short-Circuit Withstand (kA)
fields.hint: Short-circuit withstand current (rms) for 1 or 3 seconds per IEC 62271-1. Standard ratings: 16, 25, 31.5, 40, 50, 63 kA. Typical 11kV distribution: 25 kA. High fault level systems: 40-50 kA. Drives winding bracing and mechanical design cost.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - higher fault ratings require heavier winding bracing and stronger tank design

fields.label: Motor / Drive Power (kW)
fields.hint: Rated motor power for VFD sizing or MCC starter selection. Match to motor nameplate kW. VFD cost scales as (kW/75)^0.72. Standard VFD sizes: 7.5, 11, 15, 22, 37, 55, 75, 110, 160, 200, 250, 315, 400, 500 kW.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary cost driver for VFD and MCC starters

fields.label: Applicable Standard
fields.hint: Design and type-test standard. IEC 60076 / IS 2026 for transformers; IEC 62271 for 11kV switchgear; IEC 61439 for MCCs and LV switchgear; IEC 61800 for VFDs; IEC 62040 for UPS; IEEE C57 / ANSI C57 for USA projects. Third-party type test certification adds ~5-8% to cost.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - standard selection affects test certification cost and documentation scope

fields.label: Enclosure Protection (IP)
fields.hint: Ingress Protection per IEC 60529. IP23: indoor ventilated (dry-type only). IP31: indoor general. IP54: standard outdoor/industrial (most common). IP55: wet/dusty outdoor. IP65: washdown. Oil-immersed transformers are inherently weatherproof — IP rating applies to marshalling box and LV panel.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - IP54/55/65 adds 8-20% vs IP31 for panel enclosures

fields.label: Drive / Starter Type
fields.hint: Motor starting/control method for MCC feeders. DOL: simple, low cost, suitable ≤5 kW. Star-Delta: reduced inrush, typical 5-55 kW. Soft Starter: smooth start, 11-200 kW. VFD: speed control, highest cost but best efficiency. Affects contactor, overload relay, and panel depth.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - starter type drives MCC feeder cost; VFD feeders cost 3-6x more than DOL

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

fields.label: Cooling Class
fields.hint: IEC 60076-2 cooling class. ONAN is standard for most distribution transformers. ONAF/OFAF used on large power transformers (>10 MVA) to increase rating by 15-30% without increasing core size. Forced cooling adds radiator fans, pumps, and control panel — typically +15-25% cost vs ONAN.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - cooling class adds 15-25% for forced cooling variants; only matters for detailed estimate

compliance_rules.condition_expr: power < 25 AND ('Power Transformer' IN equipment_subtype OR 'Distribution Transformer' IN equipment_subtype)
compliance_rules.message: Power rating below 25 kVA is not standard for industrial power/distribution transformers. Minimum standard IEC size is 25 kVA. Check whether a control transformer or instrument transformer is intended instead.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60076-1:2011 Power Transformers — General. Standard distribution transformer ratings per IEC start at 25 kVA. Smaller ratings are covered by IEC 61558 (control and safety transformers).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: system_voltage > 33 AND 'Distribution' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: Distribution transformers are typically rated up to 33 kV (34.5 kV). Voltages above 33 kV are classified as power transformers per IEEE C57.12.00. Please verify sub-type selection.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEEE C57.12.00-2015 — Standard for General Requirements for Liquid-Immersed Distribution, Power and Regulating Transformers. Distribution transformers: ≤34.5 kV primary. Power transformers: >34.5 kV.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: impedance_pct IS not None AND (impedance_pct > 15 OR impedance_pct < 1.25)
compliance_rules.message: Impedance voltage (%Z) outside standard range (1.25–15%). Typical values per IEC 60076-1 Table 3: 4–6% for distribution transformers ≤3.15 MVA; 6–10% for power transformers >3.15 MVA. Values outside this range require special design and significantly increase cost.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60076-1:2011 Table 3 — Short-circuit impedance principal values for oil-immersed transformers. IS 2026 Part 1 Table 2 for Indian projects.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: power > 2500 AND enclosure == 'IP23'
compliance_rules.message: Transformers rated above 2500 kVA with IP23 ventilated enclosure may have insufficient natural cooling. Consider upgrading to IP54 with forced air cooling (ONAF) or verify ONAN rating is adequate for ambient temperature.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60076-2:2011 — Temperature Rise for Liquid-Immersed Transformers. Large transformers require adequate cooling; IP23 ventilated enclosures limit cooling effectiveness above 2.5 MVA in hot ambient conditions (>40°C).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: fault_level IS not None AND fault_level < 16 AND ('Transformer' IN equipment_subtype OR 'Switchgear' IN equipment_subtype)
compliance_rules.message: Short-circuit withstand below 16 kA is non-standard for modern industrial distribution systems. IEC 62271-1 minimum standard rating is 16 kA. Typical minimum for 11 kV systems is 25 kA. Verify with network fault level study.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 62271-1:2017 — High-voltage switchgear and controlgear. Standard short-circuit current ratings: 16, 20, 25, 31.5, 40, 50, 63 kA. Values below 16 kA require special agreement between manufacturer and purchaser.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Switchgear' IN equipment_subtype OR 'MCC' IN equipment_subtype OR 'VFD' IN equipment_subtype OR 'UPS' IN equipment_subtype
compliance_rules.message: This item (Switchgear / MCC / VFD / UPS) is included in the transformer template for BOQ schedule continuity. Note: the cost estimate for these items uses simplified flat-rate formulas. For detailed estimates, dedicated schemas (SCHEMA-SWITCHGEAR, SCHEMA-MCC, SCHEMA-UPS-DC) should be used.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: BOQ scope note — non-transformer electrical equipment included per project BOQ schedule. Cost accuracy for these items is Class 5 (±30%) within this template.

base_rate_reference.note: USA 2024 market basis (RSMeans Electrical, industry benchmarks). All rates in USD. Cost = base × (kVA/ref_kVA)^exp × voltage_factor × type_factor × enclosure_factor × (CEPCI_Equipment_current/1487) × regional_factor.

distribution_oil_immersed_11kV.note: 11kV/0.415kV oil-immersed ONAN. Covers: Distribution Transformer (Oil Immersed - 11/0.415kV), Distribution Transformer (Oil Cooled), Distribution Transformer 11kV/433V variants, Power Transformer (Distribution 11kV/0.415kV)

distribution_dry_type_11kV.note: 11kV/0.415kV dry-type cast resin. Covers: Distribution Transformer (Dry Type), Dry Type Transformer 11kV/433V Cast Resin variants. ~30% premium over oil-immersed.

power_transformer_33kV.note: 33/11kV or 33/6.6kV oil-immersed. Covers: Power Transformer (Oil Immersed - 33/11kV), Power Transformer (33/11kV), Power Transformer 33kV/11kV 10MVA, Power Transformer (Oil Immersed - 11/6.6kV)

auxiliary_transformer.note: HV/LV auxiliary transformer (station service). Covers: Auxiliary Transformer (HV/LV)

earthing_transformer.note: ZNyn earthing transformer 11kV. Fixed cost per unit dominates over kVA. Covers: Earthing Transformer (ZNyn) 11kV

unit_substation.note: Packaged unit substation (transformer + LV switchboard in enclosure). Covers: Unit Substation Transformer (Miniature)

switchgear_11kV_vcb.note: 11kV VCB feeder panel (supply, install, test). Per panel. Covers: Switchgear Panel (11kV VCB Feeder). Class 5 estimate only in this template.

mcc_415V.note: 415V MCC. Base panel + per-feeder rate. Covers: Motor Control Centre (MCC) 415V. Class 5 estimate only in this template.

vfd.note: Low-voltage VFD (415V). Covers: VFD (Variable Frequency Drive) 75kW

ups_industrial.note: Industrial online double-conversion UPS. Covers: UPS System (Industrial) 40kVA

voltage_factor.note: Applied to transformer items only. Accounts for insulation class, bushing cost. Reference = 11 kV.

type_factor.note: Applies to transformer items based on construction type inferred from subtype.

fault_level_factor.note: Applies when fault_level entered. Reference = 25 kA.

drive_type_factor.note: Applies to MCC feeder cost multiplier

adders.note: erection_installation 10% for electrical equipment supply (transformer installation is simpler than mechanical equipment). Switchgear/MCC installation typically 12-15% — use 12% override for those items. freight_insurance 5% covers sea/road freight and marine insurance for transformer delivery.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: CEPCI Equipment sub-index (CE Index component 2) – tracks fabricated electrical equipment including copper windings, core steel, power electronics
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_equipment_index / 1487) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. Use CEPCI Equipment sub-index — NOT ENR CCI (which is a construction labour index). Projected 2026 equipment index ~1580.

SCHEMA-TURBINE-DRIVE.generation_note: PRODUCTION READY v7 - Fixed C1: Turbine casing installation added to power_factor scaling branch; C2: Gearbox oil flushing now scales with power_rating_kw^0.45 (proxy for oil volume); C3: F-003 conditional_display extended to include Turbine casing installation; C4: spec_example corrected to be internally consistent; added F-006 turbine_type (steam/gas/hydraulic/expander) with type_factor table; added R-003 (OEM alignment procedure) and R-004 (coupling torque requirement). | R02 promoted 0.7-production-ready->1.0-priced after structural completion + benchmark pricing (validator-gated; no remaining blocking errors).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% (subtype, quantity). Tier 2 = Budget +-20% (adds power rating, shaft diameter, turbine type). Tier 3 = Detailed +-10% (adds alignment method, gearbox oil volume).

SCHEMA-TURBINE-DRIVE.spec_example: Turbine – installation - Qty 1 | Steam turbine | 5000 kW

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical units (turbines, gearboxes, couplings, etc.).

fields.label: Power rating (kW)
fields.hint: Rated shaft power of turbine or gearbox at coupling. Reference = 1000 kW. Installation man-hours scale as (kW/1000)^0.4; alignment as (kW/1000)^0.2. A 10,000 kW turbine installs in ~2.5× the man-hours of a 1,000 kW unit.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Turbine – installation' || equipment_subtype === 'Turbine rotor alignment' || equipment_subtype === 'Turbine casing installation' || equipment_subtype === 'Gearbox installation' || equipment_subtype === 'Gearbox alignment' || equipment_subtype === 'Gearbox oil filling & flushing'
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - budget estimate; primary cost driver for all turbine and gearbox subtypes

fields.label: Shaft diameter at coupling (mm)
fields.hint: Shaft diameter at the coupling interface. Reference = 80 mm. Affects coupling handling, hub press/shrink-fit difficulty, and alignment time. Scales as (dia/80)^0.3.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype.includes('Coupling') || equipment_subtype === 'Turbine rotor alignment' || equipment_subtype === 'Gearbox alignment'
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - shaft diameter drives coupling installation and alignment complexity

fields.label: Turbine / Machine type
fields.hint: Machine type drives a significant cost multiplier. Gas turbines require hot-section handling, combustor installation, and OEM field engineers — typically 2.5-4× steam turbine man-hours for equivalent kW. Aero-derivative GTs require module swap tooling.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Turbine – installation' || equipment_subtype === 'Turbine casing installation' || equipment_subtype === 'Turbine rotor alignment' || equipment_subtype === 'Gearbox installation' || equipment_subtype === 'Gearbox alignment' || equipment_subtype === 'Gearbox oil filling & flushing'
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - machine type is a 1.0-4.0x labour multiplier; gas turbines are significantly more complex to install than steam turbines

fields.label: Alignment method
fields.hint: Alignment method affects man-hours only (not equipment cost). Laser reduces alignment time by 15-25% vs dial gauge. For >5000 kW, laser is required per API 686.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Coupling alignment (dial gauge)' || equipment_subtype === 'Turbine rotor alignment' || equipment_subtype === 'Gearbox alignment'
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - method factor reduces man-hours; does not affect equipment cost

fields.label: Gearbox oil volume (litres)
fields.hint: Total oil charge volume including system (sump + pipes + cooler). Drives flushing man-hours and oil cost. Typical: 200 L (small gearbox), 800 L (medium), 3000 L (large). Flush time scales linearly with volume.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - oil volume drives flush duration and oil purchase cost directly

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Coupling alignment (dial gauge)' OR equipment_subtype == 'Turbine rotor alignment' OR equipment_subtype == 'Gearbox alignment') AND alignment_method == 'Dial gauge (rim & face)' AND power_rating_kw > 5000
compliance_rules.message: For machines >5000 kW, laser alignment is recommended to achieve API 686 shaft alignment tolerances. Dial gauge rim & face method has reduced accuracy on large shafts due to sag and bracket deflection. Consider upgrading to laser alignment.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 686 RP 4E (2009) — Recommended Practices for Machinery Installation. Section 6.3: laser alignment required for critical machines and machines >5000 kW where dial gauge accuracy is insufficient.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Gearbox oil filling & flushing' AND quantity > 1
compliance_rules.message: Multiple gearboxes: consider bulk oil purchase and a common flushing rig to reduce per-unit cost. A shared flush skid amortises over all units and can reduce total flush cost by 20-35%.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: AGMA 9005-E17 — Industrial Gear Lubrication. Section 4.2: system flushing procedure and oil contamination limits. ISO 4406 cleanliness target: typically 17/15/12 before commissioning.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (equipment_subtype == 'Turbine – installation' OR equipment_subtype == 'Turbine casing installation' OR equipment_subtype == 'Turbine rotor alignment') AND (turbine_type == 'Gas turbine (industrial frame)' OR turbine_type == 'Gas turbine (aero-derivative)')
compliance_rules.message: Gas turbine installation requires OEM-approved field service engineers for hot-section assembly, combustor installation, and first-fire acceptance. OEM field service cost is typically $50,000–$250,000 and must be added as a separate line item. This estimate covers only contractor mechanical labour.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: OEM requirements (GE, Siemens, Solar, Rolls-Royce): gas turbine warranty is void if installation is not supervised or performed by OEM-certified engineers. API 616 (Gas Turbines for the Petroleum, Chemical and Gas Industries) — Section 7: installation and commissioning requirements.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Coupling installation' AND shaft_diameter_mm > 150 AND power_rating_kw > 1000
compliance_rules.message: Large coupling installation (shaft >150 mm, >1000 kW): hub fit and bolt torquing must follow OEM torque tables per API 686 Section 7.3. Hydraulic tensioning or torque multiplier equipment may be required. Include in scope.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 686 RP 4E Section 7.3 — Coupling installation requirements. ASME PCC-1 Appendix A — bolt tightening for flanged joints. OEM coupling manual torque tables mandatory for rigid and disc-pack couplings.

man_hour_reference_table.note: Reference man-hours at 1000 kW, USA average skilled mechanical fitter $120/hr. Labour rate is the only regional variable — apply labour_factor from regional_index.

Turbine – installation.notes: Raised from 160 to 180 to better reflect API 686 minimum scope at 1000 kW

Turbine rotor alignment.notes: Rotor drop-in, clearance checks, bearing seat blue-check

Turbine casing installation.notes: Raised from 80 to 90; power scaling now applied (was missing in v0.6)

Added M1: battery backup_hours Tier 3 field; M2: spec_formula corrected; M3: DG set compliance rule added. | R02 promoted 0.7-production-ready->1.0-priced after structural completion + benchmark pricing (validator-gated; no remaining blocking errors).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% (subtype, quantity, power/kVA, motor power). Tier 2 = Budget +-20% (adds voltage, current, standard, enclosure). Tier 3 = Detailed +-10% (adds backup hours for batteries, generator kVA for DG set).

'N/A'} hr autonomy` : ''}${equipment_subtype.includes('Charger') ? ` | ${voltage} V / ${current} A` : ''}${equipment_subtype === 'Diesel Generating Set (Emergency)' ? ` | ${generator_kva} kVA / ${motor_power} kW` : ''}${equipment_subtype === 'SCADA/DCS System (Electrical Network)' ? ` | ${scada_scope ?? 'scope TBD'}` : ''} | ${standard} | ${enclosure}`
