fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are relevant.

fields.label: Motor Power (kW)
fields.hint: Engine shaft power for diesel generator set (kW mechanical). Typical: 25 kW (33 HP) to 900 kW (1200 HP). Note: generator output kVA is entered separately below.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary cost driver for DG sets alongside generator kVA

fields.label: UPS Power Rating (kVA)
fields.hint: Apparent power rating for UPS systems. Typical: 10 kVA (small), 40 kVA (medium), 200 kVA (large), 2000 kVA (data centre). Cost scales as kVA^0.65 — doubling kVA increases cost by ~56%, not 100%.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - primary cost driver for all UPS subtypes; MUST be entered for realistic estimate

fields.label: Voltage (V)
fields.hint: Nominal DC voltage for batteries and chargers (e.g. 24V, 48V, 110V, 220V), or AC voltage for UPS distribution panels (230V, 415V). Higher DC voltages require more cells in series — increases cost.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - voltage drives cell count for batteries; wiring and breaker rating for panels

fields.label: Output Current (A)
fields.hint: Rated output current for battery chargers or distribution panels. Typical: 10A, 20A, 50A, 100A, 200A. Charger cost scales approximately as current^0.4.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - current rating drives transformer and rectifier sizing cost

fields.label: Applicable Standard
fields.hint: IEC 62040 for UPS systems; IEC 60896 for VRLA/SMF batteries; IEC 60623 for Ni-Cd batteries; IEC 61850 for SCADA/DCS; IEEE 485 for battery sizing (USA); IS 1554 for Indian projects.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - standard selection affects test/certification cost and documentation scope

fields.label: Enclosure Protection (IP)
fields.hint: IP20: indoor clean room only. IP31: indoor general. IP43: standard outdoor with drip protection (most common for UPS/battery rooms). IP54: dusty or wet environments. IP65: washdown / outdoor exposed.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - IP54/IP65 enclosures add 15-25% to panel/charger cost

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

fields.label: Battery Backup / Autonomy (hours)
fields.hint: Required backup time at full load in hours. Directly determines battery Ah capacity and therefore bank cost. Typical: 0.5 hr (UPS ride-through), 4 hr (DCS/SCADA), 8 hr (critical DC systems). Cost scales linearly with hours.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - autonomy hours drive battery Ah capacity and are the primary cost multiplier for battery banks

fields.label: Generator Output Rating (kVA)
fields.hint: Nameplate generator output in kVA. Typical emergency DG sets: 30 kVA ($15k), 100 kVA ($28k), 250 kVA ($55k), 500 kVA ($90k), 1000 kVA ($160k), 2000 kVA ($280k). Cost scales as kVA^0.7. This is the primary cost driver — do not leave blank.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - generator kVA is the primary cost driver for DG sets; base rate is meaningless without this

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'UPS' IN equipment_subtype AND power < 10
compliance_rules.message: UPS power rating below 10 kVA is unusual for industrial process plant applications. Typical minimum for DCS/PLC critical power is 10 kVA. Verify load schedule before finalising.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 62040-3:2021 — UPS performance and test requirements. IEC 62040-1:2017 — Safety requirements. Industry practice: process plant critical power supplies are typically 10-40 kVA minimum.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Battery' IN equipment_subtype AND 'Ni-Cd' IN equipment_subtype AND standard == 'IEC 60896'
compliance_rules.message: IEC 60896 applies to stationary lead-acid (VRLA/SMF) batteries only. For Ni-Cd batteries, the correct standard is IEC 60623 (vented) or IEC 62259 (sealed). Please update standard selection.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60896-21:2004 — Stationary lead-acid batteries (VRLA type). IEC 60623:2001 — Secondary cells and batteries containing alkaline electrolyte (Ni-Cd vented type).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Diesel Generating Set (Emergency)' AND (generator_kva IS None OR generator_kva IS None)
compliance_rules.message: Generator output kVA not entered. The DG set base rate of $15,000 is only valid for ~30 kVA. A 500 kVA set costs ~$90,000 and a 2000 kVA set ~$280,000. This estimate will be severely understated without kVA input.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CUMMINS / KOHLER / CAT generator pricing schedules 2024. Cost equation: USD = 15000 × (kVA/30)^0.7. IEEE 446 (Recommended Practice for Emergency Power Systems) — sizing basis.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'SCADA/DCS System (Electrical Network)'
compliance_rules.message: SCADA/DCS system cost is highly scope-dependent. The base rate of $25,000 covers only a basic single-substation monitoring system (~10-20 I/O points). Large multi-substation electrical SCADA systems (100+ I/O) typically cost $150,000–$800,000. Treat this line item as a placeholder only and obtain a vendor quote for budgeting.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Industry benchmarks: basic electrical SCADA (IEC 61850 single bay) $15,000–$50,000; medium system (5 bays, RTU, HMI, historian) $80,000–$200,000; full plant SCADA $200,000–$800,000+. IEC 61850 standard for communication in electrical substations.

base_rate_reference.note: USA 2024 basis. Reference points for DG set and UPS power law scaling.

adders.note: erection_installation at 12% reflects electrical equipment installation (cable terminations, testing, commissioning). Lower than mechanical equipment (18%) because electrical gear ships factory-assembled.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: CEPCI Equipment sub-index (CE Index component 2) – tracks fabricated electrical equipment including power electronics, transformers, battery cells
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_equipment_index / 1487) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. Use CEPCI Equipment sub-index — NOT overall CEPCI 816 and NOT ENR CCI (which is a construction labour index). Projected 2026 equipment index ~1580.

Middle East (GCC).local_ref: Import price - GCC 5% duty on electrical equipment

Japan.local_ref: METI corporate goods price index - electrical machinery

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

fields.label: Cooling tower type
fields.hint: Mechanical or natural draft configuration. Induced draft crossflow is most common for industrial applications.

fields.label: Water flow rate
fields.hint: Design circulating water flow rate. Primary cost driver for cooling tower supply.

fields.label: Construction material
fields.hint: Main structural material. FRP is common for packaged towers; concrete for large field-erected.

fields.label: Fan drive type
fields.hint: Mechanical drive system for fan. Gear drive offers reliability for larger towers.

fields.label: Fill type
fields.hint: Heat transfer media. Film fill more efficient than splash.

fields.label: Basin configuration
fields.hint: Water collection basin type. Divided basins allow cell isolation.

fields.label: Drift eliminator type
fields.hint: Drift loss as percentage of circulation. Environmental regulations may require high efficiency.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Cooling tower – supply' || equipment_subtype === 'Cooling tower drift eliminator'

fields.label: Number of cooling tower cells
fields.hint: Number of cells (bays) in the cooling tower. Each cell is an independent heat transfer unit with its own fan and fill. Cost scales linearly with cell count.
fields.conditional_display: equipment_subtype === 'Cooling tower – supply' || equipment_subtype === 'Cooling tower – erection'
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - cell count is a direct linear cost multiplier for multi-cell towers

fields.label: Fan motor power (kW)
fields.hint: Installed motor power per cell. Typical range: 7.5 kW (small packaged) to 375 kW (large industrial). Drives motor cost, starter/VFD cost, and cable size.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - motor power drives electrical BOP cost (starter, VFD, cabling)

fields.label: Cooling water piping length (m)
fields.hint: Total length of cooling water piping (supply + return combined). Rate is per metre installed including fittings allowance (15%). Separate from tower supply cost.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - piping cost must be length-based, not flat per job

fields.label: Number of piping supports
fields.hint: Number of pipe supports (shoes, clamps, spring hangers). Typical rule of thumb: 1 support per 3-5 m of piping depending on pipe size. Each support includes fabrication and installation.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - support cost must be count-based at per-EA rate, not flat per job

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Cooling tower – supply' AND water_flow_rate > 0 AND cooling_tower_type == 'Natural draft (hyperbolic)' AND construction_material != 'Concrete'
compliance_rules.message: Natural draft hyperbolic towers are almost always constructed of reinforced concrete. Please verify material selection.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CTI Guideline WTP-148 (2021) - Hyperbolic natural draft cooling towers require concrete shell for structural integrity.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Cooling tower – erection' AND construction_material == 'Concrete'
compliance_rules.message: Concrete cooling tower erection requires specialized formwork and longer curing time. Consider adding a temporary works allowance.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ACI 318 - Concrete construction for industrial structures.

fan_motor_power_factor.note: Applied only to Cooling tower supply. factor = (motor_kW / 37)^0.6. Reference motor = 37 kW (50 HP). Clip at 750 kW max.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_equipment_index / 1487) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. Use CEPCI Equipment sub-index (not overall CEPCI 816). For field-erected concrete towers use ENR CCI instead. Projected 2026 equipment index ~1580.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Control valve supply'
compliance_rules.message: Control valve supply requires engineering validation: verify sizing (Cv), pressure/temperature limits, material compatibility, and actuator sizing per ISA-75.01.01.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: sil_rating != 'None' AND positioner == 'None'
compliance_rules.message: SIL-rated valves require a smart positioner with partial stroke testing capability (HART/FF/Profibus). Analog positioner not acceptable.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61508-2:2010, clause 7.4.3.1 - Diagnostic coverage requirements.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

fields.label: Nominal Pipe Size (DN)
fields.hint: Nominal bore of the valve (DN). Largest single driver of installation labour and crane requirement.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - drives cost curve exponentially above DN300

fields.label: Pressure Class / Rating
fields.hint: Pressure class per ASME B16.5 or EN 1092-1. Higher classes add significant flange bolt-up labour and torquing time.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - step-change labour multiplier at 600# and above

fields.label: Valve Body Material
fields.hint: Body material affects handling precautions (e.g. alloys require grounding), torque values, and inspection hold points.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - exotic alloys carry 1.3-1.8x labour multiplier due to care and inspection requirements

fields.label: End Connection Type
fields.hint: End connection type drives fitting-up and joint completion method. BW/SW ends require welder qualification hold point (ASME B31.3).
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - refines estimate by separating bolt-up labour from weld labour

fields.label: Actuator Type
fields.hint: Required for Valve actuator installation sub-type. Pneumatic/electric actuators require instrument air/cable connections adding 0.5-2 days extra scope.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - actuator type significantly changes installation man-hours

fields.label: Installation Location / Access
fields.hint: Access difficulty multiplier. Scaffold and confined space permits add 20-60% to direct labour.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - access difficulty is a systematic labour multiplier

fields.label: Insulation Required
fields.hint: If Yes, insulation removal and re-instatement is included in scope (relevant for maintenance / dismantling sub-types).
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - insulation adds 15-25% to maintenance labour

fields.label: Valve Weight (kg)
fields.hint: Gross weight including actuator. Above 500 kg a crane lift plan is mandatory per ASME B30.5 / local rigging standards. Drives crane hire cost.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - required for detailed rigging cost calculation

fields.label: Test Pressure (barg)
fields.hint: Shell test pressure per API 598 / MSS SP-61. Typically 1.5× design pressure for shell test, 1.1× for seat test. Used to size test pump and pressure gauge range.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - required for detailed test cost calculation

fields.label: POSITIONER / SMART HART device included
fields.hint: Smart valve positioner (e.g. Fisher DVC6200, Metso ND9000) requires HART loop configuration, I/O check, and tuning - adds ~4-8 hrs instrument tech time.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - HART/FF configuration is a distinct instrument tech cost element

fields.label: Piping Specification (Pipe Class)
fields.hint: Project piping class identifier (e.g. A1A, C3B). Links valve to gasket type, bolt material, and torque table from the project piping specification.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - used for detailed compliance check against project piping spec

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: quantity < 1
compliance_rules.message: Quantity must be at least 1. Please enter a valid number of valves.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Basic scope definition requirement.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: pipe_size_dn > 600 AND equipment_subtype == 'Gate valve installation'
compliance_rules.message: Gate valves above DN600 are very heavy (typically >2 000 kg). Confirm crane lift plan and flange bolt-up torque procedure per ASME PCC-1.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.5 Table 2-1.1 - dimensional/weight limits; ASME PCC-1 appendix A - bolted joint torque.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: pressure_class == 'ASME 2500#' AND (valve_material == 'Cast Iron (A126 Cl.B)' OR valve_material == 'Ductile Iron (A536)')
compliance_rules.message: Cast iron and ductile iron valves are NOT permitted at ASME 2500# rating. Body material must be carbon or alloy steel per ASME B16.34 Table 2.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.34-2017 Table 2 - allowable pressure-temperature ratings by material group. Cast iron is limited to Class 125/250 only.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (end_connection == 'Butt-weld (BW)' OR end_connection == 'Socket-weld (SW)')
compliance_rules.message: Welded end valves require a qualified welding procedure (WPS/PQR) and weld inspector (CWI/TWI) at fit-up and after final weld. Include weld NDE (RT/UT as applicable) in scope.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3-2022 Clauses 328 and 341 - welding qualification and examination requirements for process piping.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: valve_weight_kg > 500
compliance_rules.message: Valve weight exceeds 500 kg. A crane lift plan, rigging study, and pre-lift toolbox talk are required. Ensure structural loading on pipe rack / foundation is verified.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B30.5-2018 (mobile cranes); ASME B30.9 (slings). Most sites enforce lift plan requirement at 500 kg or when lift radius exceeds 3 m.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: test_pressure_barg > 1.5 * design_pressure_barg
compliance_rules.message: Test pressure exceeds 1.5× design pressure. Verify this is intentional (e.g. owner spec override). Over-testing can damage soft seats in ball/butterfly valves.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 598-2022 Table 2 - shell test = 1.5× CWP (cold working pressure); seat test = 1.1× CWP. Excess pressure may deform PTFE/elastomer seats.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Cryogenic valve installation' AND (valve_material == 'CS (A216 WCB)' OR valve_material == 'CS (A105)')
compliance_rules.message: Standard carbon steel has poor impact toughness below -29°C. Cryogenic service requires low-temperature carbon steel (LF2/LCB) or austenitic SS per ASME B31.3 Table A-1.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3-2022 Table A-1 minimum temperature limits; ASTM A350 LF2 Charpy impact testing per ASME B31.3 Clause 323.2.2.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Emergency shutdown valve testing'
compliance_rules.message: ESD valve partial-stroke or full-stroke testing must be documented against the SIL validation report. Proof-test interval and test method must match the SIL calculation assumptions (IEC 61511-1).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61511-1:2016 Clause 16.2.4 - functional safety proof testing requirements for safety instrumented functions.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'High-pressure valve installation' AND (pressure_class == 'API 5000 psi' OR pressure_class == 'API 10000 psi')
compliance_rules.message: High-pressure (API 5000/10000 psi) valve installation requires ring-joint (RTJ) gaskets or metal seal, dedicated high-pressure bolt tightening procedure (hydraulic tensioner or torque multiplier), and exclusion zone during pressure test.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 6A-2018 (wellhead & tree equipment); ASME B16.20 ring-joint gaskets. API 6A Table E.1 - bolt load requirements for RTJ flanges.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_install_rate_USD × size_factor(pipe_size_dn) × pressure_class_factor(pressure_class) × material_factor(valve_material) × end_connection_factor(end_connection) × access_factor(install_location) × regional_factor × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: ( base_install_rate_USD × size_factor × pressure_class_factor × material_factor × end_connection_factor × access_factor × actuator_factor × insulation_factor × crane_cost_if_weight_gt_500kg ) × regional_factor × (1 + contingency_pct/100) × quantity

base_install_rates_USD_per_valve.note: USA average (RSMeans Mechanical Cost Data, 2024 basis). Apply regional_factor from regional_index below. Size_factor and pressure_factor are additional multipliers.

size_factor_formula.note: size_factor = (DN / 50) ^ size_exp. Reference diameter is DN50. Clip at DN1200 max.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-VALVE-SAFETY.generation_note: TODOs filled with engineering judgment. Safety/relief valve scope is safety-critical: governed by ASME Sec.VIII Div.1 UG-125 to UG-136, API 520/521/526, ISO 4126, and local statutory inspection authority. All compliance rules carry error or warning severity — no cost item in this template is purely administrative. | R02 promoted 0.6-engineering-complete->1.0-priced after structural completion + benchmark pricing (validator-gated; no remaining blocking errors).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-VALVE-SAFETY.spec_example: Safety relief valve installation | Spring-loaded conventional | DN 50/80 | ASME 300# | Set Pt: 12.5 barg | SS 316 (A351 CF8M) | Qty: 2

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

fields.label: Valve Type
fields.hint: Valve type determines body size, calibration complexity, and statutory witness requirements. Pilot-operated and bellows types require specialist technician and add 40-80% to calibration labour.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - type drives calibration man-hours, test bench requirement, and compliance hold points

fields.label: Inlet Size (DN)
fields.hint: Inlet (upstream) nominal bore. API 526 Table 1 defines standard orifice area designations D through T. Larger orifices require heavier handling and longer test bench setup times.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - inlet DN is primary cost and orifice area selector

fields.label: Outlet Size (DN)
fields.hint: Outlet (discharge) nominal bore. Per API 526 the outlet is always one or two sizes larger than inlet. Required to size discharge piping and silencer/vent header.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - outlet DN is needed for discharge piping scope and reaction force check

fields.label: Set Pressure (barg)
fields.hint: Pressure at which the valve lifts (set point). Must not exceed MAWP of protected vessel per ASME Sec.VIII UG-134. Drives spring selection, test bench pressure range, and HAZOP documentation.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - set pressure is the defining parameter for calibration, stamping, and statutory approval

fields.label: Pressure Class / Rating
fields.hint: Flange pressure class of the inlet connection per ASME B16.5 / EN 1092-1. Must be consistent with set pressure and temperature.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - pressure class refines installation bolt-up and hydrostatic test cost

fields.label: Body / Bonnet Material
fields.hint: Body material determines handling precautions and surface inspection scope. Exotic alloys require PMI (positive material identification) check at receipt and before installation.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - exotic alloys add PMI, handling care, and 1.2-1.5x labour multiplier

fields.label: Process Fluid Phase
fields.hint: Fluid phase governs orifice sizing method (API 520 Part I Gas/Liquid equation), discharge system design (closed vs open header), and safety precautions during calibration. Toxic and flammable service require closed-loop test bench and additional PPE.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - toxic/flammable service adds closed-loop bench cost and specialist labour

fields.label: API 526 Orifice Designation
fields.hint: Standard orifice area per API 526 Table 1. D = 0.110 in² (0.71 cm²) through T = 26.0 in² (167.7 cm²). Used to cross-check sizing calculations and confirm correct valve selection.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - orifice letter confirms sizing compliance, mandatory for PSV datasheets

fields.label: Statutory Certification / Witness Required
options: Statutory authority (IBR / PESO / BV / DNV / Lloyd's)
ASME National Board (NB) stamped - new stamp
fields.hint: PSVs on pressure vessels under statutory jurisdiction (IBR in India, HSE/PSSR in UK, PED in EU, NBIC in USA) require witnessed calibration and stamped certification. IBR valves on steam boilers require IBR Form III-A and CE stamp. Missing certification is a statutory offence.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 - TPI/statutory witness adds mobilisation cost and schedule hold point; ASME NB stamp significantly increases scope

fields.label: Downstream Discharge Header Type
options: Closed header to flare / knockout drum
fields.hint: Discharge configuration affects installation scope. Closed header requires reaction force calculation (API 520 Annex B) and mechanical support design. Dual PSV installations require changeover valve interlock.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - discharge arrangement drives structural and piping interface cost

fields.label: Operating Temperature (°C)
fields.hint: Maximum operating temperature. Used to derate set pressure via ASME B16.5 PT tables and to flag low-temperature or high-temperature service requiring special spring/seat material. Below -29°C triggers impact test requirements.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - needed for temperature derate check and spring/seat material confirmation

fields.label: Protected Vessel MAWP (barg)
fields.hint: Maximum Allowable Working Pressure of the protected equipment per ASME Sec.VIII UG-125. Set pressure must not exceed MAWP. Required for statutory documentation and NB stamping.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 - MAWP cross-check is a mandatory compliance gate for all statutory jurisdictions

compliance_rules.condition_expr: quantity < 1
compliance_rules.message: Quantity must be at least 1.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Basic scope definition requirement.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: vessel_mawp_barg IS not None AND set_pressure_barg > vessel_mawp_barg
compliance_rules.message: Set pressure exceeds the protected vessel MAWP. This is not permitted — the PSV set point must be ≤ MAWP for single-valve installations, or ≤ 1.05× MAWP for supplemental valves (fire case), per ASME Sec.VIII UG-134(b).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code Section VIII Division 1, UG-134(b) and UG-125(c) — maximum set pressure limits relative to MAWP.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: inlet_size_dn > 400
compliance_rules.message: Inlet DN exceeds 400 mm (16 in). PSV inlet sizes above DN400 are non-standard per API 526. Verify custom sizing with manufacturer and confirm suitability with the certifying authority.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 526 Sixth Edition Table 1 — standard orifice sizes and flange connections up to T-orifice (DN400 inlet). Larger sizes require individual engineering review.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: outlet_size_dn IS not None AND outlet_size_dn <= inlet_size_dn
compliance_rules.message: PSV outlet size should be larger than inlet size. API 526 requires outlet DN to be at least one standard size larger than inlet to prevent choked discharge flow.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 526 Sixth Edition Table 1 — all standard orifice configurations have outlet connection one or two sizes larger than inlet. API 520 Part II Clause 4.3 — discharge piping must not create back pressure that reduces rated capacity by more than 10% (conventional) or 30% (balanced bellows).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: pressure_class == 'ASME 150#' AND set_pressure_barg > 19.6
compliance_rules.message: Set pressure of {set_pressure_barg} barg exceeds the ASME 150# flange rating at ambient temperature (~19.6 barg / 285 psi). Upgrade to ASME 300# or higher, or verify the temperature derate does not reduce allowable pressure further.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.5-2017 Table 2-1.1 Group 1.1 (CS WCB) — 150# class maximum pressure at 38°C is 19.6 barg (285 psig). Above this pressure, 300# is the minimum class.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: body_material == 'Cast Iron (A126 Cl.B)' AND set_pressure_barg > 10.0
compliance_rules.message: Cast iron body valves are not recommended above ~10 barg set pressure due to brittleness and susceptibility to hydraulic shock on opening. Review material selection against ASME B16.34 Table 2 and local pressure vessel regulations.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.34-2017 Table 2 — Cast iron (Group 4.1) is restricted to Class 125/250 only. Many process industry standards (e.g. EEMUA 190, Shell DEP) prohibit cast iron in PSV/SRV service above 10 bar.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (fluid_phase == 'Toxic gas (IDLH service)' OR fluid_phase == 'Flammable gas (ATEX zone)') AND discharge_header_type == 'Open vent / atmosphere'
compliance_rules.message: Open atmospheric discharge is NOT acceptable for toxic (IDLH) or flammable gas service. Discharge must be routed to a closed header (flare, scrubber, or knockout drum) per API 521 and local environmental regulations (e.g. CPCB norms in India, EPA 40 CFR in USA, IED Directive in EU).
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 521 Seventh Edition Section 5.4 — disposal systems for flammable and toxic gases must prevent hazardous accumulation at grade. OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119 PSM standard — open venting of IDLH substances is not permitted.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: statutory_cert == 'None (unwitnessed, records only)' AND (fluid_phase == 'Steam' OR fluid_phase == 'Toxic gas (IDLH service)')
compliance_rules.message: Safety valves on steam boilers (IBR jurisdiction in India, PSSR 2000 in UK) and toxic-service pressure vessels require witnessed calibration and statutory stamp. Unwitnessed certification for these services may be a regulatory non-compliance.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Indian Boilers Act 1923 (amended 2022) and IBR 1950 Rule 274 — safety valves on steam boilers must be tested and stamped by an IBR Inspector. UK PSSR 2000 Regulation 8 — written scheme of examination required for pressure systems including safety devices. ASME NBIC NB-23 Part 3 — VR stamp required for repair/replacement of NB-stamped valves.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: discharge_header_type == 'Rupture disc upstream of PSV'
compliance_rules.message: Rupture disc upstream of PSV installation requires a pressure gauge (or tell-tale indicator) in the space between the disc and valve per ASME Sec.VIII UG-132(a)(4). Ensure the rupture disc is ASME-stamped and compatible with the fluid and temperature. Trapped leakage may cause backpressure and premature disc failure.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME BPVC Section VIII Division 1 UG-132(a)(4) — mandatory gauge/pressure indicator between rupture disc and relief valve. API 520 Part I Annex C — guidance on rupture disc/SRV combinations. ASME Section VIII UG-127 — rupture disc device requirements.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: operating_temp_c IS not None AND operating_temp_c < -29
compliance_rules.message: Operating temperature below -29°C (-20°F). Low-temperature service requires impact-tested body material (e.g. A350 LF2, A352 LCB, SS 316L) and verification that spring and seat materials retain elasticity at minimum metal temperature. Standard CS valves are not acceptable.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3-2022 Table A-1 minimum temperature without impact testing for A105/WCB is -29°C. API 520 Part I does not specifically cover cryogenic PSVs — manufacturer must confirm suitability for Cv, set pressure stability, and blowdown at low temperature.

cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: base_rate_USD(equipment_subtype, valve_type) × size_factor(inlet_size_dn) × set_pressure_factor(set_pressure_barg) × regional_factor × quantity
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_rate_USD × size_factor(inlet_size_dn) × set_pressure_factor(set_pressure_barg) × pressure_class_factor(pressure_class) × material_factor(body_material) × fluid_factor(fluid_phase) × cert_factor(statutory_cert) × regional_factor × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: ( base_rate_USD × size_factor × set_pressure_factor × pressure_class_factor × material_factor × fluid_factor × cert_factor × discharge_config_factor(discharge_header_type) × access_factor ) × regional_factor × (1 + contingency_pct/100) × quantity

base_install_rates_USD_per_valve.note: USA average basis (RSMeans Mechanical + instrument tech rates, 2024). Reference at DN50 inlet, spring-loaded conventional, open vent, CS body, ASME 150#, gas service, TPI witnessed. Apply multiplier tables below.

size_factor_formula.note: size_factor = (inlet_DN / 50) ^ size_exp. Clip at DN400 maximum for standard API 526 orifices.

set_pressure_factor.note: Multiplier applied to base rate based on set pressure range. Higher pressures require higher-class flanges, heavier spring, higher-rated test bench, and longer torquing time.

adders.note_contingency: 12% default contingency for safety valve work — higher than general valves due to calibration schedule uncertainty, statutory inspector availability, and potential for re-test if valve fails pop test at first attempt.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

SCHEMA-VALVE-SUPPLY.spec_example: Gate valve supply-4 inch-150#-CS-10 (10 off 4 inch 150# Carbon Steel Gate Valves)

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

fields.label: Valve Size (Nominal Diameter)
fields.hint: Nominal pipe size (NPS) per ASME B36.10M. Cost scales exponentially with size.

fields.label: Pressure Class
fields.hint: ASME B16.5 pressure class. Higher class = thicker walls, higher cost.

fields.label: Body Material
fields.hint: Material affects cost significantly. CS = base, SS316 = 2.5x, Alloy = 4x.

fields.label: End Connection Type
fields.hint: Flanged = standard. Butt weld = lower cost but requires welding.

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Operating temperature. Triggers material upgrade if > 425°C.

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Operating pressure. Must be compatible with pressure class.

fields.label: Manufacturer / Brand
fields.hint: Brand tier affects price by 20-40%.

fields.label: Trim Material
fields.hint: Internal trim material for erosion/corrosion resistance.

fields.label: Seat Type
fields.hint: Soft seats for bubble-tight shutoff. Metal for high temp.

fields.label: Operation Type
fields.hint: Actuation adds 30-200% to valve cost.

fields.label: Testing Requirement
fields.hint: Testing level affects cost and delivery.

fields.label: Special Coating / Lining
fields.hint: Corrosion protection for aggressive services.

fields.label: Delivery Terms
fields.hint: Incoterm affects freight/insurance adders.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temp > 425 AND material == 'Carbon Steel (WCB)'
compliance_rules.message: WARNING: Carbon steel (WCB) is not recommended above 425°C per ASME B16.34 - carbide phase may convert to graphite. Select alloy steel or stainless steel.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.34 Table 2-1.1 Note (1): Upon prolonged exposure to temperatures above 425°C, the carbide phase of steel may be converted to graphite.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (pressure_class == '150#' AND design_pressure > 19.6) OR (pressure_class == '300#' AND design_pressure > 51.1) OR (pressure_class == '600#' AND design_pressure > 102.1)
compliance_rules.message: ERROR: Design pressure exceeds ASME B16.34 rating for selected pressure class at ambient temperature. Upgrade pressure class or reduce operating pressure.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B16.34 Table 2-1.1 - Pressure class ratings for Group 1.1 materials (WCB/A105) at -29°C to 38°C.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: seat_type != 'Metal-to-metal' AND design_temp > 200
compliance_rules.message: WARNING: Soft seats (NBR/Viton/PTFE) degrade above 200°C. Verify seat material temperature limit or switch to metal-to-metal seating.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 598 / ANSI/FCI 70-2 - Resilient seat materials typically limited to 200°C max.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: valve_size > 12 AND pressure_class == '1500#'
compliance_rules.message: WARNING: Large bore high-pressure valves (>12 inch, 1500#) require extended delivery and special manufacturing. Confirm supplier capability.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Industry practice - Large high-pressure valves are specialty items with 20-40 week lead times.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: testing_req == 'Full NDT (RT/PT)'
compliance_rules.message: INFO: Full NDT testing adds 15-25% to valve cost and 2-4 weeks to delivery. Ensure specification is justified by service criticality.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 598 Section 5.3 - Supplementary examinations when specified by purchaser.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: quantity × base_rate × (valve_size^1.8 / 4^1.8) × pressure_factor × material_factor × connection_factor × brand_factor × temp_factor × (current_cepci / 816) × regional_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: quantity × base_rate × (valve_size^1.8 / 4^1.8) × pressure_factor × material_factor × connection_factor × brand_factor × temp_factor × trim_factor × seat_factor × operation_factor × testing_factor × coating_factor × (current_cepci / 816) × regional_factor × (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation + contingency)

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure_barg > 210
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds ASME Section VIII Div. 1 practical limit of 210 barg. Switch to Div. 2 or Div. 3 basis and verify wall thickness / material qualification.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME BPVC Section VIII Div. 1 – general upper bound; Div. 2 required above ~210 barg for optimised design

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temp_degC > 450 AND material_of_construction == 'CS'
compliance_rules.message: Carbon steel is not suitable above 450 °C. Specify alloy steel (Cr-Mo, 1.25Cr-0.5Mo minimum) or stainless steel.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME BPVC Section II Part D – allowable stress tables; Nelson Curves (API 941) for H₂ service

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Floating roof tank' AND vessel_capacity_m3 < 500
compliance_rules.message: Floating roof tanks are generally uneconomical below ~500 m³. Consider a fixed roof tank with internal floating cover instead.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 650 Annex C – floating roof guidance; industry practice threshold

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Underground water tank' AND design_pressure_barg > 0
compliance_rules.message: Underground tanks are typically atmospheric (0 barg). Confirm pressurised underground service – may require cathodic protection and special backfill design.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 3370 / ACI 350 – liquid retaining structures; NFPA 22 for fire water underground tanks

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'HEPA filter installation' AND design_pressure_barg > 0.05
compliance_rules.message: HEPA filter housings are typically rated for low differential pressure (≤0.05 barg). Verify filter media and housing pressure class.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ISO 29463 / EN 1822 – HEPA/ULPA filter performance; housing design per manufacturer DDP

compliance_rules.condition_expr: vessel_capacity_m3 > 5000
compliance_rules.message: Large vessel (>5000 m³): confirm field-erected vs. shop-fabricated basis. Field erection significantly changes labour content and schedule.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 650 Section 1 – scope; transport limits typically restrict shop-fab to ~200 m³ for tall columns

cost_build_up.basis_note: Base erection / installation unit rates are USD 2024, USA Gulf Coast basis (RSMeans mechanical + millwright labour). Apply regional_cost_index and regional_factors to localise. Equipment supply (for Reactor – supply) uses equipment_equation (Guthrie / CAPCOST correlation).
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_erection_rate_usd × capacity_complexity_factor(vessel_capacity_m3, design_pressure_barg) × quantity × regional_cost_index_factor × sub_region_labour_factor × access_difficulty_factor
cost_build_up.full_formula: ( base_erection_rate_usd × capacity_complexity_factor × pressure_class_factor × material_factor × nozzle_count_adder + special_activity_adder ) × quantity × regional_cost_index_factor × sub_region_labour_factor × access_difficulty_factor × (1 + contingency_pct/100)

unit_rate_table_usd_2024.note: USA Gulf Coast base erection / installation rates. $/job unless otherwise stated. Exclude supply cost for 'erection' line items.

Pressure vessel – erection.size_breakpoints: small < 10 m³ | medium 10–100 m³ | large > 100 m³
Pressure vessel – erection.notes: Includes unloading, rigging, setting on foundation, bolt-up, alignment. Excludes hydro-test, insulation, painting.

Storage tank – erection.size_breakpoints: small < 100 m³ | medium 100–2000 m³ | large > 2000 m³
Storage tank – erection.notes: Field-erected carbon steel tank per API 650. Includes shell plates, roof structure, bottom annular plates, stairway/ladder.

Reactor – supply.correlation: Guthrie (2024 CEPCI 820 basis): C_reactor = 12500 × (V_m3)^0.65 × f_material × f_pressure
Reactor – supply.notes: Purchased equipment cost (PEC) only. Add installation separately.

Reactor – installation.notes: Lang factor basis. Includes crane hire, rigging, alignment, nozzle bolt-up, instrument tap commissioning.

Tank shell erection.notes: Plate rolling, welding, erection only. Excludes roof, nozzles, appurtenances.

Separator installation.size_breakpoints: small < 5 m³ | medium 5–30 m³ | large > 30 m³
Separator installation.notes: Horizontal / vertical two-phase or three-phase separators. Includes rigging, setting, bolt-up.

Filter installation.notes: Cartridge or bag filter housing installation. Excludes filter media replacement (separate O&M item).

Cyclone separator installation.notes: Single cyclone or multi-cyclone bank. Includes duct flanging and support steelwork.

Air filter installation.notes: Panel filter frame or full housing unit. Excludes AHU if part of HVAC package.

HEPA filter installation.notes: Includes clean-room rated housing, gel-seal frame, pressure drop instrumentation tap.

Effluent treatment tank.notes: FRP or concrete-lined GS tank for ETP. Includes agitator nozzle, vent, overflow. Civil foundation excluded.

Water tank installation.size_breakpoints: small < 20 m³ | medium 20–200 m³ | large > 200 m³
Water tank installation.notes: Prefabricated MS / GRP tank. Includes unloading, setting on plinth, inlet/outlet connections.

Underground water tank.notes: Includes RCC civil structure, waterproofing, manhole, sump pump pit. Excludes excavation and backfill.

Tank farm piping.size_breakpoints: small bore DN ≤ 100 | large bore DN > 100
Tank farm piping.notes: Tank-to-manifold and manifold-to-battery-limit interconnecting piping. Includes supports, isolation valves, drain valves.

Floating roof tank.notes: External floating roof per API 650 Annex C. Includes pontoon / full-deck roof, seal system, rolling ladder, wind girder.

Fixed roof tank.notes: Cone or dome roof per API 650. Includes roof structure, vents, manways, stairway.

capacity_complexity_factor.note: Multiplier on base erection rate for non-standard size

pressure_class_factor.note: Multiplier for design pressure impact on erection complexity (torque, hydro-test, NDT)

material_factor_erection.note: Premium for alloy/SS over CS – surface protection, special tools, cleanliness requirements

adders.note_erection: erection_installation adder = 0 for this template because erection IS the scope. The unit_rate_table already captures full installation labour. For supply-only items (Reactor – supply), apply 0.35 as installation adder.

small_order_premium.note: Apply when quantity = 1 and vessel is non-standard. Crane mobilisation + specialist rigger call-out.

hydro_test_and_NDT_adder_pct.note: Add if hydrostatic test + post-weld heat treatment + full RT/UT is in scope (not always included in base rate)

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item.

fields.label: Capacity / Volume
fields.hint: Geometric internal volume of the vessel or tank. Primary cost driver for equipment pricing equations.
fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - key sizing parameter for quick estimate

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure (MAWP). Drives wall thickness and hence fabrication cost. Use 0 for atmospheric tanks.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 – major cost driver once volume is set

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum design temperature (MDT). Affects allowable stress values and material selection per ASME Sec II Part D.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 – impacts alloy selection and insulation adders

fields.label: Material of Construction (Shell)
fields.hint: Shell/plate material. Stainless and exotic alloys carry 2–6× cost premium over carbon steel. Drives both material and labour cost.

fields.label: Design Code
fields.hint: Applicable fabrication standard. Div 2 adds ~10-15% to vessel cost vs Div 1 due to higher documentation and inspection burden.
fields.tier_note: Tier 2 – moderate adder for Div 2 or PED compliance

fields.label: Radiography / NDE Level
fields.hint: NDE and heat treatment level. Full RT + PWHT can add 8–12% to fabricated vessel cost but may allow thinner wall (joint efficiency E=1.0).

fields.label: Insulation / Lining Type
fields.hint: Insulation or lining significantly adds to installed cost. Cryogenic or refractory linings can add 20–40% over bare vessel.

fields.label: Number of Nozzles / Manways
fields.hint: Total count of process nozzles, manways, and instrumentation connections. Each large nozzle (>4 in.) adds measurable fabrication cost.
fields.tier_note: Tier 3 – nozzle count refines labour hours

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure_barg > 150 AND material_of_construction == 'CS (Carbon Steel A516-70)'
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds 150 barg for carbon steel – review material selection. High-strength low-alloy (HSLA) or alloy steel is required above this threshold per ASME Sec VIII Div 2 design limits.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME BPVC Section VIII Div 1 UG-20 / Div 2 Part 3 – allowable stress limits for SA-516-70 fall below 150 barg practical range without Div 2 special design.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temp_degC > 425 AND 'CS' IN material_of_construction
compliance_rules.message: Design temperature exceeds 425 °C for carbon steel. Carbon steel loses significant creep resistance above 427 °C (800 °F). Consider Cr-Mo alloy steel (1.25Cr-0.5Mo or 2.25Cr-1Mo) per API 530 / ASME Sec II Part D.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME BPVC Sec II Part D Table 1A – SA-516-70 allowable stress drops steeply above 427 °C; API RP 530 recommends Cr-Mo alloys for fired heater / high-temp service.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'LPG bullet tank' AND design_pressure_barg < 7
compliance_rules.message: LPG bullet tanks typically operate at 7–18 barg vapour pressure (propane/butane). Verify design pressure is consistent with stored fluid's vapour pressure at maximum ambient temperature.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 2510 / IS 2825 – LPG storage bullets designed to MAWP ≥ vapour pressure at 46 °C (115 °F) ambient plus surge margin.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: 'Oil storage tank' IN equipment_subtype AND design_pressure_barg > 0.07
compliance_rules.message: Atmospheric oil storage tanks are designed to very low pressure (typically <75 mbarg / 1 psig). Pressures above this suggest an API 620 low-pressure tank is required instead of a standard API 650 tank.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 650 (atmospheric storage tanks, ≤2.5 kPa / 0.36 psig internal pressure); API 620 covers tanks up to 103 kPag (15 psig).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_temp_degC < -46 AND NOT material_of_construction IN ['SS304 / 304L','SS316 / 316L','SS321','Duplex SS (2205)','Super Duplex (2507)','Hastelloy C-276','Inconel 625','Titanium Gr.2']
compliance_rules.message: Service temperature is below -46 °C (−51 °F). Carbon steel loses ductility below this range. Austenitic stainless, 9% Ni steel, or aluminium alloy is required for cryogenic service.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME BPVC Sec VIII Div 1 UCS-66 impact test curves – CS minimum design temperature limits; BS EN 10028-4 for low-temp alloy steels.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_cost = 35000 × (capacity_m3 ^ 0.65) × pressure_factor × mat_factor_t2 × quantity × regional_factor × (CEPCI_current / 816); pressure_factor = 1.0 + 0.012 × design_pressure_barg; mat_factor_t2 per material_of_construction lookup table
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_cost = 35000 × (capacity_m3 ^ 0.65) × pressure_factor × temp_factor × mat_factor × nde_factor × nozzle_adder × insulation_adder × quantity × regional_factor × (CEPCI_current / 816); where pressure_factor = 1 + 0.012 × design_pressure_barg; temp_factor = 1.0 if DT ≤ 200°C, 1.08 if 200–350°C, 1.18 if >350°C; nde_factor: spot RT=1.0, full RT=1.06, PWHT+full RT=1.12; nozzle_adder = 1 + 0.008 × max(0, nozzle_count - 4); insulation_adder per lookup; total_cost = base_cost × (1 + freight_insurance) × (1 + erection_installation) × (1 + contingency)

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)

SCHEMA-VESSEL-INTERNALS.template_name: Vessels & Tanks — Internal Fabrication & Reactor Activities
SCHEMA-VESSEL-INTERNALS.item_type_note: Type A = Site Activity / Service. All items are skilled labour activities — structural welding, process tray fitting, refractory lining, and specialist catalyst handling. Cost is labour-dominated. ENR CCI (labour index) applies — NOT CEPCI (equipment index). No import duty on labour services. Consumables (welding rods, refractory, catalyst handling equipment) are included in adders.
SCHEMA-VESSEL-INTERNALS.generation_note: v1.0 RELEASE — All fields, cost formulas, compliance rules, adders and spec strings completed by cost estimate engineer. ENR CCI 2024 baseline (14250) — corrected from skeleton CEPCI (inappropriate for site activities). Three technical domains covered: structural metalwork (manhole/nozzle), process internals (trays), reactor work (lining, catalyst load/unload). Base costs USD 2024 per job, scaled by vessel geometry, nozzle/tray count, catalyst volume, service class, and access constraints. | v1.0-priced: usd_cost_baseline + multiplier tables added (USA-base ENR/CEPCI; public-benchmark, all verify=true).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-VESSEL-INTERNALS.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | Dia={vessel_diameter_m} m | H={vessel_height_m} m | N={item_count} No. | Cat={catalyst_volume_m3} m³ | Service={service_class} | Material={lining_or_material_type} | HotWork={hot_work_required} | Qty={quantity} Job
SCHEMA-VESSEL-INTERNALS.spec_example: Reactor catalyst loading | Dia=3.2 m | H=12 m | Cat=45 m³ | Service=Hydrocarbon — High temp (>350°C) | Loading method: Dense loading | Qty=1 Job

activity_scope_notes.Vessel manhole installation: Supply and installation of pressure vessel manhole (manway) assembly including reinforcing pad, nozzle flange, blind flange/davit-arm cover, and gasket. Welding per ASME IX / IS 2825. Includes fit-up, root pass, fill and cap passes, PWHT (if P-number requires), MPI/DPT on weld seams, radiography or UT on nozzle weld, and hydraulic leak test at 1.5× design pressure. Manhole diameter: typically 450–600 mm (DN450/DN600 per API 650 / ASME VIII). Material: CS, SS304, SS316L, or matching vessel MOC.
activity_scope_notes.Vessel nozzle installation: Field installation of process nozzles (inlet, outlet, drain, vent, instrument taps) into an existing vessel. Includes cutting opening, fit-up, welding, reinforcing pad, flange facing, PWHT, NDE (MPI/DPT on weld, UT on HAZ), pressure test. Rated to vessel design pressure. Flanges per ASME B16.5 / IS 6392. Nozzle sizes typically DN 25–DN 600. Cost per nozzle scales with nozzle diameter and wall thickness.
activity_scope_notes.Vessel tray installation: Installation of distillation / absorption column trays (valve trays, sieve trays, bubble-cap trays, structured packing) inside a process column. Includes tray deck panels, downcomer bars, weir plates, tray support rings, manway blanks during installation, levelness check (±3 mm per IS 5311 / TEMA / vendor spec), and post-installation downcomer clearance verification. Tray count (number of theoretical stages) is the primary cost driver.
activity_scope_notes.Reactor lining installation: Application and curing of internal protective lining to reactor vessel: refractory (castable, gunite, or brick), rubber lining (NR, EPDM, neoprene), epoxy/phenolic lining, acid-brick with potassium silicate mortar, or HDPE/FRP sheet lining. Includes surface preparation (blasting to Sa 2.5), lining application, curing per manufacturer procedure, spark/holiday test (electrical inspection), and thickness verification. Lining type is selected based on service temperature, pH, and chemical resistance requirements.
activity_scope_notes.Reactor catalyst loading: Loading fresh or regenerated catalyst into fixed-bed or radial-flow reactor. Methods: sock loading (gravity feed, ~75% bulk density), dense loading (vibro-sock or Catapac, ~92% bulk density), or inert ball loading for support/topping layers. Includes pre-loading checks (bed support inspection, thermocouple positioning, inert support layer placement), loading procedure per licensor specification, post-load level measurement, and gas-freeing before loading if prior service. Precious metal catalysts require specialist contractor and chain-of-custody documentation.
activity_scope_notes.Reactor catalyst unloading: Removal of spent catalyst from reactor for regeneration, disposal, or precious metal recovery. Includes vessel cool-down, inert purge, gas-freeing, confined space entry, manual excavation or vacuum unloading (IVAC / air-conveying), screening and segregation (active vs. fines vs. contaminated), drum/big-bag filling, labelling, and transport to regeneration facility or licensed disposal site. Pyrophoric catalyst (spent hydroprocessing catalyst) requires special handling — nitrogen blanket, steam passivation, or inhibited unloading — significantly increases cost and requires specialist contractor.

fields.label: Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the activity. Structural metalwork (manhole/nozzle) requires coded welders, PWHT, and NDE. Tray installation is precision mechanical work requiring survey instruments. Reactor work (lining, catalyst) is specialist — requires licensor-approved contractors for catalyst and refractory manufacturers' supervision for lining. All are confined space activities requiring permit-to-work.

fields.label: Quantity (Jobs / Vessels)
fields.hint: Number of vessels or reactors for which this activity is performed. For manhole/nozzle/tray/lining: 1 job = 1 vessel. For catalyst activities: 1 job = 1 reactor bed (multi-bed reactors with inter-bed quench zones should be split into separate line items per bed if significantly different in size or catalyst type).

fields.label: Vessel / Reactor Internal Diameter
fields.hint: Internal diameter (ID) of the vessel or reactor shell. For tray installation: diameter determines tray panel size, number of tray sections per level, and scaffolding requirement inside the column. For catalyst loading/unloading: diameter determines loading hopper size, access constraints, and crew positioning. For manhole/nozzle: diameter determines scaffold ring and hot-work exclusion zone size. Typical column ID range: 0.6–6 m; reactor ID: 1–5 m.

fields.label: Vessel / Reactor Height (T-T)
fields.hint: Tangent-to-tangent height of the vessel or reactor in metres. Determines internal scaffold requirements for tray installation and catalyst loading. For columns with many trays (>30 trays), internal scaffolding is typically erected from the bottom head upward — this is a major cost component, often 20–30% of total tray installation cost. For tall reactors (>15 m), top-entry catalyst loading via man-baskets or hoisting platforms is required.

fields.label: Item Count (Nozzles / Trays / Manholes)
fields.hint: Number of items within the single vessel/reactor job. For nozzle installation: total number of nozzles to be installed in one vessel (e.g., 8 nozzles on one separator). For tray installation: total number of trays in one column. For manhole installation: typically 1–2 per vessel. For lining: not applicable — use lining area instead. Economy of scale: installing 20 trays in one column is significantly less per-tray than 20 trays across 10 separate columns due to shared scaffolding and mobilisation.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Vessel manhole installation','Vessel nozzle installation','Vessel tray installation']

fields.label: Service Class / Operating Conditions
options: Hydrocarbon — high temp / high pressure (> 40 bar or > 350°C)
Sour service (H₂S > 50 ppm, NACE MR0103 / HIC/SSCC)
High-alloy / exotic material (Inconel, Hastelloy, Titanium, Duplex)
fields.hint: Operating service class of the vessel/reactor. Determines welder qualification requirements (ASME IX P-number/F-number), PWHT necessity, consumable specification, weld inspection extent, and catalyst type. Hydrogen and sour service mandate NACE MR0175 hardness requirements on all weld HAZs — requires post-weld heat treatment verification (Brinell hardness ≤ 200 HB) and significantly increases inspection scope and cost. High-alloy materials require specialist welders and non-standard consumables.

fields.label: Nozzle / Manhole Size
fields.hint: Nominal diameter range of nozzle or manhole opening. Larger nozzles require heavier reinforcing pads, more weld volume, larger flanges (ASME B16.5 / IS 6392), and more PWHT cycles. DN 25–50 nozzles can be installed in 0.5–1 shift by a 2-man crew. DN 400+ nozzles require lifting equipment, multi-pass welding over multiple shifts, and extended NDE. Cost per nozzle scales roughly as DN^1.3.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Vessel manhole installation','Vessel nozzle installation']

fields.label: Tray Type
options: Sieve tray (simple perforated deck — lowest cost)
Valve tray (Koch / Sulzer / Nutter type — standard)
Fixed valve tray (MVG / Koch MV-GT — no moving parts)
Chimney tray / draw-off tray (total draw-off or feed tray)
Structured packing (Sulzer Mellapak / Koch FlexiPac — high efficiency)
Random packing (Raschig rings / Pall rings / IMTP — bulk fill)
fields.default: Valve tray (Koch / Sulzer / Nutter type — standard)
fields.hint: Tray or packing type determines installation complexity, required tools, and levelness tolerance. Valve trays: individual valve caps snapped in — fastest installation. Bubble-cap trays: each cap bolted — slowest, highest labour. Structured packing: cut-to-size corrugated metal sheets stacked in sections — requires careful orientation and sealing. Random packing: bulk-filled by gravity through manhole — simplest loading, cost dominated by packing material value not labour. Chimney trays require leak-tight installation — weld seal to vessel wall in some cases.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Vessel tray installation'

fields.label: Lining Type
options: Refractory brick (dense fired brick, acid-resistant, potassium silicate mortar)
Ceramic fibre blanket (module or blanket — high-temp insulation lining)
Rubber lining (NR / EPDM / neoprene / Hypalon, 6–12 mm, bonded)
Glass-flake epoxy lining (tank lining, 1–3 mm, brush/spray)
HDPE / PP sheet lining (mechanically fixed or welded, 6–10 mm)
FRP lining (hand lay-up or spray-up, 3–8 mm)
Acid-brick with potassium silicate mortar (full brick lining)
fields.hint: Reactor or vessel internal lining type. Refractory (castable/brick) for high-temperature service (>400°C) — e.g., cracking reactors, reformers, incinerators. Rubber lining for aggressive acid/alkali service at ambient temperature. Epoxy/phenolic for mild corrosion protection. Glass-flake epoxy for tank linings in petroleum/water service. HDPE/PP for strong acid/alkali at low temperature. Lining type dictates surface preparation standard (Sa 2.5 minimum per ISO 8501), cure time (2–14 days), and holiday test voltage. Lining area (m²) is used for cost calculation — see F-010.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Reactor lining installation'

fields.label: Lining Surface Area
fields.hint: Total internal surface area to be lined in m². For a cylindrical reactor: Area = π × D × H + 2 × π × (D/2)². For example: 3.2 m ID × 12 m T-T height ≈ 141 m² shell + 16 m² heads ≈ 157 m² total. Actual lined area may be less if nozzles, internals, or existing lining are excluded. Measured area from reactor isometric drawing is the most accurate basis. This field drives cost for all lining types in conjunction with the lining_cost_per_m2 reference table.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Reactor lining installation'

fields.label: Catalyst Volume
fields.hint: Bulk volume of catalyst to be loaded or unloaded in m³ (bulk, not packed). For a cylindrical fixed-bed reactor: catalyst volume ≈ π/4 × D² × catalyst bed height. Typical reactor catalyst volumes: small pilot-scale 0.5–5 m³; industrial fixed-bed 10–500 m³; large HDS/hydrotreater 100–800 m³. Catalyst volume is the primary cost driver for loading/unloading activities — labour scales approximately as V^0.70. For precious metal catalysts (Pt, Pd, Rh), handling cost is a fraction of catalyst value — precision and accountability are paramount.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Reactor catalyst loading','Reactor catalyst unloading']

fields.label: Catalyst Loading Method
options: Sock loading — gravity feed via flexible sock hose (standard, ~75% bulk density)
Dense loading — vibro-sock / Catapac / CatLoad (specialist, ~88–92% bulk density)
Manual loading — hand shovelling via manhole (small reactors < 2 m³ only)
Inert ball / support layer loading — Al₂O₃ balls via top manhole
Vacuum unloading — IVAC / air-conveying system (catalyst unloading only)
Manual excavation + vacuuming — spent catalyst with heavy fouling
fields.default: Sock loading — gravity feed via flexible sock hose (standard, ~75% bulk density)
fields.hint: Catalyst loading method determines equipment, crew, and loading rate. Sock loading is standard for most fixed-bed reactors — lower equipment cost but lower bulk density. Dense loading (vibro-sock Catapac) achieves 15–20% more catalyst per bed, improving conversion — mandatory for reformers and HDS where activity per unit volume is critical. Dense loading adds 40–60% to labour cost but typically pays back in catalyst savings. Vacuum unloading (IVAC) is 3–5× faster than manual for spent catalyst — preferred for reactors >20 m³. Pyrophoric (self-igniting in air) spent catalyst from hydroprocessing must be unloaded under nitrogen blanket or steam-passivated before air exposure.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Reactor catalyst loading','Reactor catalyst unloading']

fields.label: Catalyst Hazard Classification
options: Base metal oxide (NiMo, CoMo, NiW — standard HDS/HDT — spent is hazardous)
Precious metal (Pt, Pd, Rh, Re — reforming, hydrogenation — chain-of-custody)
Pyrophoric when spent (hydroprocessing NiMo/CoMo — air-reactive, nitrogen blanket)
Chromium-bearing (Cr₂O₃ — shift catalyst, potential Cr⁶⁺ hazard)
fields.default: Base metal oxide (NiMo, CoMo, NiW — standard HDS/HDT — spent is hazardous)
fields.hint: Catalyst hazard class determines PPE level, handling protocol, waste classification, and disposal route. Fresh base metal catalysts are generally non-hazardous to handle. Spent HDS/HDT catalysts are classified as hazardous waste in most jurisdictions due to heavy metal (V, Ni, Mo) content. Pyrophoric spent catalyst requires nitrogen inerting or steam passivation before and during unloading — cannot be exposed to air. Precious metal catalysts require documented chain-of-custody (contractor accountability for every kg), third-party assay, and specialist recovery contractor — significantly increases contract administration cost.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Reactor catalyst loading','Reactor catalyst unloading']

fields.label: Hot Work / PWHT Requirement
options: No hot work — cold assembly only (tray bolting, catalyst loading)
Hot work — welding, no PWHT required (CS ambient service, t < 13 mm)
Hot work — welding + PWHT required (CS high-temp, P-1 Group 2, t ≥ 13 mm)
Hot work — welding + PWHT + hardness test (sour / H₂ service, NACE MR0175)
Hot work — high-alloy welding (SS, Duplex, Inconel — special consumables)
fields.default: Hot work — welding, no PWHT required (CS ambient service, t < 13 mm)
fields.hint: Post-weld heat treatment (PWHT) is required per ASME VIII Div.1 UCS-56 and AS 1210 when: (1) CS wall thickness ≥ 13 mm (ASME Code Group 1) or ≥ 32 mm (after code case exemptions), (2) P-number materials with PWHT mandatory groups, or (3) hydrogen/sour service requiring ≤ 200 HB Brinell hardness. PWHT adds: furnace/resistance heating equipment mobilisation, temperature recorder calibration, and soak time at 580–620°C for 1 hr/25 mm thickness. PWHT for a 2 m diameter nozzle addition: typically USD 3,000–8,000 additional per nozzle location in India.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Vessel manhole installation','Vessel nozzle installation']

fields.label: NDE Extent (Welding Inspection)
options: Visual inspection only (VI) — low-pressure utility nozzles ≤ 7 bar
10% RT / UT — spot radiography on nozzle welds (standard Code compliance)
100% RT — full radiography on all nozzle welds (lethal / toxic service)
MPI / DPT on all welds — surface NDE only (standard in sour service)
TOFD + PAUT — advanced volumetric UT (high-pressure, high-alloy)
fields.default: 10% RT / UT — spot radiography on nozzle welds (standard Code compliance)
fields.hint: Extent of weld non-destructive examination. ASME VIII Div.1 requires spot radiography (10% RT) as minimum for Category D nozzle welds in Class 1 vessels. 100% RT is required for lethal service (ASME UW-2) and Class 1 pressure vessels per IBR/CCOE for boiler-pressure parts. MPI/DPT is mandatory in sour/H₂S service (NACE SP0472) to detect surface cracks in HAZ. Advanced TOFD/PAUT is required for safety-critical nozzles in nuclear, ultra-high-pressure, or hydrogen service. NDE cost is 10–30% of total weld installation cost — not a minor item.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Vessel manhole installation','Vessel nozzle installation']

fields.label: Catalyst Disposal / Recovery Route
options: Ex-situ regeneration — return same catalyst (third-party regenerator)
Precious metal recovery — refinery / licensed recovery contractor
Hazardous waste disposal — licensed landfill / thermal treatment
Reuse in lower-grade service (downgraded, no offsite processing)
New catalyst — fresh load, no spent handling required
fields.default: Ex-situ regeneration — return same catalyst (third-party regenerator)
fields.hint: Disposal or recovery route for spent catalyst from unloading, or receipt route for loading. Ex-situ regeneration (offsite) is the most common route for base metal HDS/HDT catalysts — typically USD 200–500/tonne processing fee, plus transport. Precious metal recovery contractors take custody of the catalyst and return refined metal or credit — recovery rates: Pt 98%, Pd 97%, Rh 95%. Hazardous waste disposal: USD 300–800/tonne in India, USD 1,500–4,000/tonne in EU/USA. Disposal route does NOT include catalyst material value — only the handling, transport, and processing service cost.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Reactor catalyst loading','Reactor catalyst unloading']

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['Vessel manhole installation','Vessel nozzle installation'] AND service_class IN ['Hydrogen service (NACE MR0175 / HIC/SSC risk)','Sour service (H₂S > 50 ppm, NACE MR0103 / HIC/SSCC)'] AND hot_work_pwht == 'Hot work — welding, no PWHT required (CS ambient service, t < 13 mm)'
compliance_rules.message: NACE VIOLATION: Hydrogen and sour service (H₂S > 50 ppm) require PWHT on ALL carbon steel welds to achieve ≤ 200 HB Brinell hardness in the weld and HAZ per NACE MR0175 / MR0103. Selecting 'no PWHT required' for sour/H₂ service is non-compliant and risks hydrogen-induced cracking (HIC) and sulphide stress corrosion cracking (SSCC) — which can cause catastrophic brittle fracture. Update PWHT requirement to 'Hot work — welding + PWHT + hardness test'.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NACE MR0175 / ISO 15156 (Materials for H₂S-Containing Environments) — hardness limits for CS welds: ≤ 22 HRC (200 HB) maximum. NACE SP0472 — methods for control of H₂ blistering and HIC in refinery equipment. ASME VIII Div.1 UCS-56 — PWHT requirements for P-1 material. API 571 — hydrogen damage and SSCC damage mechanisms in refinery equipment.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['Vessel manhole installation','Vessel nozzle installation'] AND service_class == 'Hydrocarbon — high temp / high pressure (> 40 bar or > 350°C)' AND nde_extent == 'Visual inspection only (VI) — low-pressure utility nozzles ≤ 7 bar'
compliance_rules.message: Visual inspection only is NOT acceptable for nozzle welds on vessels operating above 40 bar or 350°C. Minimum requirement: 10% RT/UT spot examination per ASME VIII Div.1 UW-51/UW-52 and IBR regulations for Class 1 pressure parts. Upgrade NDE extent to '10% RT / UT' minimum. For lethal service or Category D welds in critical service: 100% RT is mandatory.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME Boiler & Pressure Vessel Code Section VIII Div.1 — UW-51 (Full Radiography) and UW-52 (Spot Radiography) requirements. IBR 1950 (India) — mandatory inspection of all pressure part welds above 3.5 kg/cm² by a competent inspector. ASME B31.3 Process Piping Code (Chapter II) — radiography requirements for piping category.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Reactor lining installation' AND lining_type IN ['Castable refractory (gunite / hand-packed, 75–150 mm thick)','Refractory brick (dense fired brick, acid-resistant, potassium silicate mortar)','Ceramic fibre blanket (module or blanket — high-temp insulation lining)'] AND service_class IN ['Ambient / low-pressure utility (water, air — ≤ 10 bar, ≤ 100°C)','Acid / caustic / corrosive chemicals (pH < 3 or > 11)']
compliance_rules.message: Refractory / ceramic fibre lining is typically specified for high-temperature service (> 400°C). For ambient temperature corrosive service (acid/alkali), rubber lining, glass-flake epoxy, or HDPE/PP sheet is more appropriate and significantly less expensive. Verify with process engineer that refractory is the correct lining choice for this service.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASTM C401 (Classification of Castable Refractories) — refractory is designed for thermal service > 400°C. For corrosion protection at ambient temperature: NACE SP0178 (Surface Finish Requirements for Coated Steel) + SSPC SP10 surface prep. Rubber lining per ASTM D3053 / IS 4682 for chemical service.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Reactor catalyst unloading' AND catalyst_hazard_class == 'Pyrophoric when spent (hydroprocessing NiMo/CoMo — air-reactive, nitrogen blanket)' AND catalyst_loading_method == 'Manual excavation + vacuuming — spent catalyst with heavy fouling'
compliance_rules.message: SAFETY CRITICAL: Pyrophoric spent catalyst (NiMo/CoMo hydroprocessing) MUST NOT be manually excavated without confirmed passivation. Unpassivated pyrophoric catalyst exposed to air can self-ignite, causing fire and serious burns to personnel. Mandatory protocol: (1) complete nitrogen blanket or steam passivation before any air exposure, (2) specialist catalyst handling contractor with nitrogen purged equipment, (3) ensure catalyst temperature is confirmed < 50°C before entry, (4) continuous gas monitoring during unloading. Do not proceed with standard manual excavation on this catalyst type.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API RP 2026 (Safe Access/Egress Involving Floating Roofs — broader safety principle for confined space + hazardous material). Catalyst manufacturer's spent catalyst handling procedure (Haldor Topsoe, Criterion, Albemarle). OSHA 1910.119 PSM standard — management of change and safe work practices. Indian Petroleum Rules 2002 — handling of pyrophoric materials in refineries.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Reactor catalyst loading' AND catalyst_hazard_class == 'Precious metal (Pt, Pd, Rh, Re — reforming, hydrogenation — chain-of-custody)' AND catalyst_loading_method == 'Manual loading — hand shovelling via manhole (small reactors < 2 m³ only)'
compliance_rules.message: Precious metal catalyst (Pt/Pd/Rh) must be loaded using controlled, accountable procedures — not manual shovelling. Required: (1) certified weight of catalyst in each container before and after loading, (2) chain-of-custody documentation signed by owner's representative and contractor supervisor, (3) maximum allowable loss per licensor spec (typically < 0.1% by weight), (4) retained sample for assay verification. Manual loading has highest risk of catalyst loss (spillage into drain, crusher fines). Use sock loading with collection tray for any precious metal catalyst.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Catalyst licensor specifications (UOP, Axens, Haldor Topsoe) — precious metal catalyst loading procedures. ISO 9001 (Quality Management) — chain-of-custody and documentation requirements. OSHA HazCom (29 CFR 1910.1200) — SDS for precious metal catalyst dust (Pt compounds can cause platinum sensitisation / occupational asthma).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Vessel tray installation' AND item_count > 30 AND vessel_height_m > 20
compliance_rules.message: Column with more than 30 trays and height above 20 m will require internal scaffolding from the bottom head — a major cost item often 25–35% of total tray installation cost. Ensure scaffold erection and dismantling is included in the BOQ as a separate line item or confirmed as included in this activity rate. For columns above 40 m total height, consider staged scaffolding or rope access for upper trays to reduce cost.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 3696 (Safety Code for Scaffolds and Ladders) Parts 1 and 2. OSHA 29 CFR 1926.451 (Scaffolding Standard). Industry practice for distillation column tray installation: internal scaffolding is standard for columns > 10 m height with manway access at multiple elevations. For tall columns (> 30 m), a separate scaffold BOQ item of USD 15,000–50,000 is typical for India.

cost_build_up.basis: Three distinct cost methods by activity domain: (1) Structural metalwork (manhole/nozzle): lumpsum per nozzle, scaled by diameter and NDE scope. (2) Tray installation: unit rate per tray, scaled by tray type and column diameter. (3) Reactor activities (lining): unit rate per m², scaled by lining type. Catalyst activities: lumpsum per job scaled by catalyst volume and hazard class. All base costs USD 2024, India Metro reference. Apply ENR CCI escalation and regional labour factor.
cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: Cost = base_cost_USD × item_count × (vessel_diameter_m / ref_diameter)^0.5 × service_cost_multiplier × quantity  [nozzle/manhole/tray]  OR  base_cost_USD × (catalyst_volume_m3 / ref_volume)^0.70 × quantity  [catalyst]  OR  lining_cost_per_m2 × lining_area_m2 × quantity  [lining]
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: Cost = Tier_1_Cost × nozzle_size_multiplier × tray_type_multiplier × catalyst_method_multiplier × catalyst_hazard_multiplier
cost_build_up.full_formula: Total_Activity_Cost = Base_Cost × size_and_count_factors × service_multiplier × method_multiplier × hazard_multiplier × (1 + nde_adder_pct) × (PWHT_adder × item_count) × (ENR_CCI_current / 14250) × regional_labour_factor × sub_region_factor × (1 + consumables_mobilisation) × (1 + contingency)

base_activity_costs_USD_2024.note: Reference conditions: India Metro labour rates, ENR CCI 14250, standard service class, manway access available, no PWHT.

Vessel manhole installation.typical_range_usd: 2000 (DN450, CS, no PWHT) – 35000 (DN900, alloy, PWHT + 100% RT)
Vessel manhole installation.crew_size: 3–5 (coded welder, fitter, helper, NDE technician, safety observer)
Vessel manhole installation.notes: Includes: nozzle fabrication or supply check, fit-up, root pass, fill/cap passes, PWHT (if applicable), MPI/DPT, 10% RT, hydraulic leak test. Excludes scaffold (add separately for manholes above 3 m elevation).

Vessel nozzle installation.notes: Includes: hole cutting, nozzle alignment, welding, reinforcing pad, NDE, leak test. Apply nozzle_size_multiplier from F-007. Quantity = total number of nozzles in single vessel job.

Vessel tray installation.typical_range_usd: 250 (small column, sieve tray, ID < 1 m) – 8000 (large column, bubble-cap, ID > 4 m)
Vessel tray installation.typical_duration_days: 2–5 trays per day depending on tray type and column diameter
Vessel tray installation.crew_size: 4–8 (tray fitters, rigger, survey instrument operator, safety observer)
Vessel tray installation.notes: Includes: tray panel placement, downcomer bar installation, weir plate setting, levelness survey (±3 mm tolerance), downcomer clearance check. Excludes internal scaffolding (add USD 15,000–50,000 as separate line item for columns > 10 m). Apply tray_type_multiplier from F-008.

Reactor lining installation.typical_duration_days: 7–30 days depending on lining type and area
Reactor lining installation.notes: Cost = lining_area_m2 × lining_cost_per_m2. Includes: surface preparation (blasting Sa 2.5), lining application, curing, holiday test (electrical spark test at 5V/μm), thickness measurement, and issue of lining completion certificate. Refractory curing requires controlled heat-up schedule (24–72 hours) — mobilise portable burner for reactor dry-out. Catalyst vendor/licensor may witness lining completion before catalyst loading.

Reactor catalyst loading.typical_range_usd: 3000 (5 m³, non-hazardous, sock load) – 250000 (500 m³, precious metal, dense load)
Reactor catalyst loading.crew_size: 4–10 (loading crew, safety officer, shift supervisor, licensor rep if required)
Reactor catalyst loading.notes: Includes: pre-loading bed inspection, inert support ball placement, catalyst loading via specified method, post-load level survey, bed temperature thermocouple installation check. Excludes catalyst material value (separate purchase order). Apply catalyst_method_multiplier and catalyst_hazard_multiplier. Catalyst licensor representative attendance: add USD 3,000–8,000 per mobilisation if required.

Reactor catalyst unloading.typical_range_usd: 4000 (5 m³, non-hazardous, vacuum unload) – 320000 (500 m³, pyrophoric, N₂-blanketed)
Reactor catalyst unloading.crew_size: 5–15 (unloading crew, safety officer, gas tester, waste handling team)
Reactor catalyst unloading.notes: Catalyst unloading is more expensive than loading due to: confined space excavation or vacuum equipment, catalyst sampling and screening, drum/big-bag filling and labelling, waste manifest documentation, and transport logistics. Pyrophoric catalyst requires nitrogen inerting system (N₂ generator or liquid N₂ dewar). Catalyst disposal/recovery cost (F-016) is ADDITIONAL — apply as USD/tonne on estimated catalyst bulk density (~700–900 kg/m³ for base metals).

notes.consumables_mobilisation: Welding consumables (electrodes, wire, flux, shielding gas), PWHT equipment hire, NDE consumables (film, chemicals), lining materials (included in lining cost/m² table), catalyst handling equipment (sock hopper, vibro device, IVAC machine). Higher for reactor catalyst activities: 0.25 for dense loading (specialist equipment hire).
