notes.safety_equipment_ppe: Confined space entry equipment, gas detectors, SCBA sets, fire watch equipment, hot-work barriers. Increase to 0.15 for pyrophoric catalyst or hydrogen service.
notes.scaffold_access_internal: Internal scaffolding inside column for tray installation — this is a major cost item for tall columns. For columns > 30 m: scaffold cost may equal or exceed tray installation labour. Price separately as a standalone BOQ item for detailed estimates.
notes.quality_inspection_documentation: Weld record books (WPS/PQR/WQT), material certificates (MTCs), NDE reports, hardness test records, PWHT charts, lining holiday test records, catalyst loading sheets (signed by licensor). Third-party QA/QC inspector: add 0.10 of activity cost.
notes.contingency: Site activities have higher uncertainty than equipment purchase — 15% standard. Increase to 25% for catalyst unloading (unknown spent catalyst condition, potential for blocked beds or heavy fouling requiring additional labour). For first-time lining of reactive or novel catalyst system: 20%.

cost_build_up.freight_insurance_note: NOT APPLICABLE — Type A activity. No goods imported for service labour. Catalyst material value is a separate procurement line item. Specialist catalyst loading equipment (IVAC, vibro-sock device) is mobilised by the specialist contractor and included in their rates.
cost_build_up.erection_installation_note: NOT APPLICABLE as a separate adder — for site activities, all labour is captured in the base activity cost and multipliers above.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: CORRECTED from skeleton: CEPCI was incorrectly specified for this Type A (Activity) schema. ENR CCI is the appropriate labour-based construction cost index for site activities. Cost = base_activity_cost_USD × (ENR_CCI_current / 14250) × regional_labour_factor × multipliers × (1 + contingency). No equipment import duty applies to labour services.

India.local_ref: CPWD DSR 2023-24 mechanical erection / skilled welder rates

USA.local_ref: RSMeans Mechanical Cost Data 2024 / NECA labour rates

Europe.local_ref: Eurostat Labour Cost Index — construction / mechanical

regional_factors.equipment_duty_category: Site activity — no import duty applicable
regional_factors.note: For Type A activities, only labour_factor sub-region values are meaningful. equipment_factor and civil_factor are set to 1.0 as they do not apply to labour-only activity costing.

import_duty.components: No goods imported — labour service contract
import_duty.note: GST 18% (SAC 998714 — welding services; SAC 998731 — maintenance of industrial machinery) applies to service invoices in India. Factor separately into project cost.
No import duty on service contracts.
No import duty on service contracts.
No import duty. UK VAT 20% on service contracts — factor separately.
No import duty on service contracts.
No import duty on service contracts.
No import duty on service contracts.
No import duty on service contracts.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level. Type A (Activity) items — equipment_origin and import duty are not applicable. Cost is entirely labour-driven.
project_settings.equipment_origin: N/A — site activity, no equipment procurement
project_settings.escalation_factor_note: ENR CCI escalation = 14480/14250 = 1.016 (+1.6% 2024→mid-2025). Dominant cost driver is regional labour factor: India Metro 0.28× vs USA 1.0× vs UK 1.35×. Catalyst handling activities with specialist contractors may carry a global market rate irrespective of regional labour — verify with specialist catalyst contractor for precious metal and pyrophoric catalyst work.

usd_cost_baseline.note: USA-base USD (matches index base year 2024). Engine applies index_now/base x regional x sub_region factor x fx. ALL rates are public-benchmark ESTIMATES (verify=true) pending vendor/DSR calibration. base_cost_USD per formula.

Vessel manhole installation.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 4500 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 5725.45. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Vessel nozzle installation.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 4500 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 5725.45. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Vessel tray installation.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 4500 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 5725.45. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Reactor lining installation.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 4500 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 5725.45. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Reactor catalyst loading.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 120000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 162388.06. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Reactor catalyst unloading.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 120000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 162388.06. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

reference._basis: Scaling references for diameter^0.5 / volume^0.70 terms.

SCHEMA-VESSEL-TEST.template_name: Vessels & Tanks — Inspection, Testing & Cleaning Activities
SCHEMA-VESSEL-TEST.item_type_note: Type A = Site Activity / Service. These are labour-intensive inspection, testing, cleaning, and gauging services, NOT purchasable equipment. Cost method is lumpsum or unit_rate based on labour-hours and consumables. CEPCI equipment escalation does NOT apply — use ENR CCI (labour index). No import duty applies to services.
SCHEMA-VESSEL-TEST.generation_note: v1.0 RELEASE - All fields, cost formulas, compliance rules, adders and spec strings completed by cost estimate engineer. ENR CCI 2024 baseline (14250). Activity-based costing: lumpsum per job, scaled by vessel volume, diameter, pressure class, fluid/contamination category, and access type. No equipment import duty applies. Consumables, waste disposal, and statutory certification fees are included in adders. | v1.0-priced: usd_cost_baseline + multiplier tables added (USA-base ENR/CEPCI; public-benchmark, all verify=true).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-VESSEL-TEST.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | Vol={vessel_volume_m3} m³ | Dia={vessel_diameter_m} m | P={design_pressure_barg} bar(g) | Fluid={fluid_contamination_class} | Access={access_method} | Cert={certification_authority} | Qty={quantity} Job
SCHEMA-VESSEL-TEST.spec_example: Pressure vessel inspection | Vol=25 m³ | Dia=1.8 m | P=15 bar(g) | Fluid=Hydrocarbons (Class B) | Access=Internal manway entry | Cert=IBR/CCOE | Qty=1 Job

activity_scope_notes.Pressure vessel inspection: Third-party external and internal inspection per statutory requirements (IBR/CCOE/PESO India; API 510 USA; PED 2014/68/EU). Includes visual examination, thickness gauging (UT), weld NDE (MPI/DPT), pressure test if required, and inspection report with fitness-for-service recommendation.
activity_scope_notes.Pressure vessel internal cleaning: Mechanical or hydro-jetting cleaning of internal surfaces to remove scale, fouling, sediment, or product residue before internal inspection or maintenance. Includes confined space entry, decontamination, waste collection, and disposal to approved facility.
activity_scope_notes.Tank calibration: Strapping/calibration survey of atmospheric storage tank to produce capacity (strapping) table per API 2550 / IS 4847. Includes external measurements, deadwood allowances, floating roof correction, and issue of calibration certificate. Required for custody transfer and statutory purposes.
activity_scope_notes.Tank settlement testing: Differential settlement monitoring of tank foundation during initial hydrostatic test fill (API 650 Annex B). Includes survey pegs, settlement measurement at defined fill levels, and report. Triggered when tank is on pile foundation, soft soil, or has a history of uneven settlement.
activity_scope_notes.Tank gauging system: Supply, installation, and commissioning of automatic tank gauging (ATG) system for level, temperature, water bottom, and inventory measurement. Typically servo gauge or radar-based. Includes transmitter, float assembly (if servo), junction box, cabling to control room, and software integration.
activity_scope_notes.Tank cleaning: Cleaning of atmospheric storage tank (fixed or floating roof, crude/product tank) for maintenance or change of service. Includes degassing, sludge removal, hydrocarbon waste disposal, water washing, gas-freeing, and issue of gas-free certificate. Scope varies enormously by previous product and tank size — estimate with caution.

fields.label: Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific activity. Drives cost method, applicable fields, compliance rules, and base rate. All items are site services (Type A) — costed as lumpsum per job scaled by vessel/tank size and complexity. Tank cleaning and pressure vessel inspection are the highest-cost activities and most sensitive to contamination classification.

fields.label: Quantity (Jobs)
fields.hint: Number of separate job executions of this activity. Each job = one vessel or one tank. Enter the total number of identical vessels/tanks requiring this activity. For a plant with 12 identical heat exchangers requiring internal cleaning, enter 12. If tanks differ significantly in size or contamination class, use separate BOQ line items per tank.

fields.label: Vessel / Tank Volume
fields.hint: Internal geometric volume of the vessel or tank in m³. For pressure vessels: shell volume excluding nozzles. For atmospheric tanks: nominal capacity (API 650 / IS 803 nominal volume). Volume is the primary cost driver for cleaning and calibration activities — cost scales approximately as V^0.65. For ATG system: enter tank volume to size the gauge range and assess complexity.

fields.label: Vessel / Tank Diameter
fields.hint: Internal diameter (ID) of the vessel or tank shell in metres. For atmospheric tanks: nominal diameter per API 650 table. For pressure vessels: shell ID. Diameter determines scaffold size for external inspection, access platform requirements, and calibration measurement method (internal strapping vs. external optical). Tanks above 30 m diameter require specialist optical calibration equipment (EODR method per API 2550).

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure (MAWP) of the pressure vessel in bar(g). For atmospheric storage tanks: enter 0. Design pressure determines the statutory inspection authority (CCOE/PESO/IBR in India), the inspection scope (hydraulic pressure test requirements), and confined space gas testing protocol. Vessels above 25 bar(g) require hot-work-free and ATEX-rated equipment during internal cleaning.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Pressure vessel inspection','Pressure vessel internal cleaning']

fields.label: Fluid / Contamination Classification
options: Hydrocarbons — Class A (flash point > 65°C, e.g. diesel, lube oil, HFO)
Hydrocarbons — Class B (flash point 23–65°C, e.g. kerosene, ATF)
Hydrocarbons — Class C (flash point < 23°C, e.g. petrol, crude, naphtha)
Toxic / hazardous chemicals (acid, caustic, chlorine compounds)
Scale / hard deposit (boiler scale, calcium carbonate, inorganic fouling)
Sludge / slurry (high solids, petroleum sludge, biological sludge)
fields.hint: Previous contents and contamination class of the vessel or tank. This is the single most important cost multiplier for cleaning activities — toxic, sour, or Class C hydrocarbon service requires full SCBA, specialist contractors, hazmat waste disposal, and atmospheric monitoring throughout. Water/utility service is base case (1.0×). Sour service or toxic chemicals add 100–200% to cleaning cost due to PPE, decontamination, and waste treatment requirements.

fields.label: Access Method
options: External only — no internal entry (robotic / ROV / CCTV inspection)
Nozzle access only (no manway, restricted internal access)
Scaffolded external access — large tank (>10 m height or >15 m dia)
Floating roof — additional entry and working platform required
fields.hint: Method of physical access for cleaning, inspection, or survey. Manway entry (standard confined space entry) is the base case. External-only / robotic inspection avoids confined space entry permit but requires specialist equipment (IRIS, TOFD, phased array UT scanners). Floating roof tanks require additional working platform, safety netting, and anti-rotation stops — adds 20–35% to access cost. Scaffolded large tank access adds significant labour.

fields.label: Certification / Statutory Authority
options: Internal / owner inspection only (no statutory cert required)
IBR (Indian Boiler Regulations) — CCOE / State Boiler Inspector
PESO (Petroleum and Explosives Safety Organisation) — India
Lloyd's Register / Bureau Veritas / DNV (third-party TPI)
fields.default: IBR (Indian Boiler Regulations) — CCOE / State Boiler Inspector
fields.hint: Statutory or third-party certification authority whose inspector must witness and certify the inspection, test, or cleaning. IBR/CCOE certification in India requires State Boiler Inspector attendance — schedule 4–8 weeks in advance. PESO certification required for all petroleum storage tanks above 1000 litres in India. API 510/653 inspections require a certified API inspector (CWI/API 510 cert) — third-party TPI agency cost included in base rate. Statutory inspection adds 15–40% to base activity cost vs. owner-only inspection.

fields.label: NDE / Inspection Technique
options: Visual + Ultrasonic Thickness Survey (UT) — spot checks
Visual + UT thickness mapping (full shell scan)
Visual + MPI (Magnetic Particle Inspection) — weld seams
Visual + DPT (Dye Penetrant Testing) — weld seams
Phased Array UT (PAUT) — advanced weld and corrosion mapping
IRIS (Internal Rotating Inspection System) — heat exchanger tubes
TOFD (Time of Flight Diffraction) — pressure vessel welds
fields.default: Visual + Ultrasonic Thickness Survey (UT) — spot checks
fields.hint: NDE (Non-Destructive Examination) techniques to be applied during inspection. Visual only is lowest cost but insufficient for statutory inspections. UT spot checks are standard for routine in-service inspection. Full UT mapping required if corrosion rates are unknown or accelerated pitting is suspected. PAUT and TOFD are advanced techniques with higher day-rates (USD 1,500–3,500/day vs USD 600–900/day for standard UT). IRIS for heat exchanger tube inspection: USD 25–60/tube depending on length and diameter. Select per inspection plan — NDE scope is a major cost variable.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Pressure vessel inspection','Pressure vessel internal cleaning']

fields.label: ATG Gauge Technology
options: Servo gauge (float-and-tape, ±1 mm accuracy) — custody transfer
Guided wave radar (GWR) — atmospheric tanks, ±3 mm
Free-space radar (FMCW) — large tanks, floating roof, ±1 mm
Magnetostrictive float gauge — clean products, ±0.5 mm
Hybrid (radar + servo, dual measurement) — fiscal metering
fields.default: Guided wave radar (GWR) — atmospheric tanks, ±3 mm
fields.hint: Automatic tank gauging technology. Servo gauges are traditional high-accuracy devices for custody transfer (fiscal metering) at terminals — include float, displacer, measurement head, and counterweight. Radar (GWR and FMCW) is now preferred for most applications — non-contacting, lower maintenance. For custody transfer (OISD-117 / MPMS Chapter 3), ±1 mm accuracy is required — use servo or FMCW radar. Hydrostatic gauging is lowest cost but ±5 mm accuracy limits use to process/inventory only. Hybrid dual measurement for critical fiscal applications.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Tank gauging system'

fields.label: Tank Calibration Method
options: Manual internal strapping (API 2550) — tanks ≤ 10 m dia
Optical Reference Line (ORL / optical triangulation) — tanks 10–30 m dia
Electro-Optical Distance Ranging (EODR) — tanks > 30 m dia
3D laser scanning (Leica / Faro) — high-accuracy, complex geometry
Incremental volumetric method (API 2555 — ship tanks / irregular vessels)
fields.default: Optical Reference Line (ORL / optical triangulation) — tanks 10–30 m dia
fields.hint: Method of tank strapping / capacity calibration per API MPMS Chapter 2 / IS 4847. Internal manual strapping is the traditional method for small tanks (≤ 10 m diameter). EODR is required for large tanks above 30 m diameter where internal access is impractical or unsafe. 3D laser scanning is the most accurate and rapid modern method — preferred for complex geometries, Horton spheres, and bullet tanks. Method drives both labour cost and calibration equipment mobilisation cost. EODR and laser scanning require specialist equipment import/mobilisation — add 20–30% for India.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Tank calibration'

fields.label: Settlement Monitoring Points
fields.hint: Number of settlement monitoring peg points installed around the tank periphery and on the chequered plate. API 650 Annex B recommends a minimum of 8 points for tanks ≤ 30 m diameter and 12 points for tanks > 30 m. Each measurement point is surveyed by a licensed surveyor at prescribed fill levels (0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%, and 24-hour full). More points improve the differential settlement map and are required by geotechnical engineers for pile design verification.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Tank settlement testing'

fields.label: Waste Volume & Disposal Class
options: Negligible / dry solids only (< 0.5 m³ waste)
Large volume — hazardous petroleum sludge (50–500 m³)
Very large — crude oil / refinery sludge (> 500 m³)
fields.hint: Estimated volume and classification of waste generated during cleaning. This is often the largest single cost element for tank cleaning — petroleum sludge disposal at a licensed treatment facility costs USD 80–250/tonne in India, and up to USD 600–1200/tonne for classified hazardous waste in Europe/USA. For crude oil tanks: sludge volume = 1–3% of nominal capacity. Always obtain waste analysis (TCLP/SPLP test) before final disposal cost is fixed. Pre-treatment (oil recovery, dewatering) reduces disposal cost significantly.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Tank cleaning','Pressure vessel internal cleaning']

fields.label: Pressure Test Type (Post-Cleaning / Repair)
options: Pneumatic leak test only (snoop / soap solution, ambient pressure)
Hydraulic pressure test (hydrostatic) — 1.1× MAWP per IBR/ASME
Hydraulic pressure test (hydrostatic) — 1.3× MAWP per ASME VIII Div.1
Pneumatic strength test (1.1× MAWP) — where hydraulic test impractical
Vacuum box test — for floor weld seams (atmospheric tank)
fields.hint: Pressure test to be performed as part of or following the inspection/cleaning activity. Hydraulic pressure test (hydrotest) is required after any weld repair or when the inspector determines fitness-for-service cannot be confirmed by NDE alone. IBR hydrotest: 1.1× MAWP. ASME VIII hydrotest: 1.3× MAWP (shop) or 1.1× (field). Vacuum box test for API 653 atmospheric tank floor weld inspection is mandatory per API 653 Section 9 after floor plate replacement. Hydrotest cost includes: test water procurement/disposal, temporary blanks, pressure gauges, and standby for 30 minutes minimum at test pressure.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Pressure vessel inspection','Tank cleaning']

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Pressure vessel inspection' AND design_pressure_barg > 1.0 AND certification_authority == 'Internal / owner inspection only (no statutory cert required)'
compliance_rules.message: STATUTORY VIOLATION: Pressure vessels operating above 1.0 bar(g) in India are regulated under the IBR 1950 (Indian Boiler Regulations) and/or PESO rules. Internal/owner-only inspection is NOT permitted for statutory inspection — a competent person from the CCOE, State Boiler Inspector, or approved third-party inspection agency MUST witness and certify the inspection. Upgrade certification authority to IBR/CCOE or approved TPI.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Indian Boiler Regulations (IBR) 1950 (amended) — all vessels above 1 kg/cm² (0.981 bar) and 22.75 litres capacity require inspection by a competent person and issue of certificate before return to service. PESO Act 2002 (India) — petroleum storage vessels require PESO inspection. ASME PCC-2 / API 510 (international) — similar requirement for certified inspector.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['Tank cleaning','Pressure vessel internal cleaning'] AND fluid_contamination_class IN ['Hydrocarbons — Class C (flash point < 23°C, e.g. petrol, crude, naphtha)','Sour service (H₂S present, NACE MR0175)','Toxic / hazardous chemicals (acid, caustic, chlorine compounds)'] AND access_method == 'Manway entry (standard, ID ≥ 500 mm manway)'
compliance_rules.message: SAFETY ALERT: Confined space entry into a vessel previously containing Class C hydrocarbons, sour service (H₂S), or toxic chemicals requires a formal gas-free certificate with <1% LEL, <0.5 ppm H₂S, and >19.5% O₂ before entry is permitted. Entry without gas-free verification is a fatal accident risk. Ensure: (1) gas-free certificate issued by certified gas tester, (2) continuous atmospheric monitoring during entry, (3) full SCBA for rescue team at entry point, (4) permit-to-work system active.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 10594 (Entering Enclosed Spaces Aboard Ships — also applied to industrial vessels in India). Factory Inspectorate / DGFASLI guidelines for confined space entry. API RP 2016 (Guidelines and Procedures for Entering and Cleaning Petroleum Storage Tanks). OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146 (Permit-Required Confined Spaces). Petroleum Act 1934 and Rules 1976 (India).

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['Pressure vessel inspection','Pressure vessel internal cleaning'] AND vessel_diameter_m < 0.5 AND access_method == 'Manway entry (standard, ID ≥ 500 mm manway)'
compliance_rules.message: Vessel diameter is below 500 mm — standard manway entry is not physically possible. Change access method to 'Nozzle access only' or 'External only — no internal entry (robotic / ROV / CCTV inspection)'. For vessels below 500 mm ID, internal inspection requires fibre-optic borescope or remote visual inspection (RVI) through nozzles. Cleaning must be done through nozzle flush / chemical clean-in-place (CIP) methods.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME PCC-2 Article 3 — minimum dimensions for confined space entry. EN 13445-5 (Inspection and Testing of Unfired Pressure Vessels) — minimum access dimensions for internal inspection. Safe confined space entry requires manway ≥ 450 mm diameter (oval) or ≥ 400 mm circular per IS 9432.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Tank calibration' AND vessel_diameter_m > 30 AND calibration_method == 'Manual internal strapping (API 2550) — tanks ≤ 10 m dia'
compliance_rules.message: Tank diameter exceeds 30 m — manual internal strapping per API 2550 is not practical or permitted for tanks above 30 m diameter. Use EODR (Electro-Optical Distance Ranging) per API MPMS Chapter 2.2B or 3D laser scanning. Update calibration method selection to EODR or 3D laser scanning.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API MPMS Chapter 2.2B (Calibration of Upright Cylindrical Tanks Using the EODR Method) — applicable for tanks above 10 m diameter where internal strapping is impractical. For tanks above 30 m diameter, API recommends EODR as the preferred method. IS 4847 (Glossary of Terms Relating to Tank Calibration) — supported by Directorate of Weights and Measures India for custody transfer.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Tank settlement testing' AND settlement_points < 8
compliance_rules.message: Settlement monitoring point count below API 650 Annex B recommended minimum of 8 for tanks under 30 m diameter. Below 8 points, the differential settlement profile cannot be adequately characterised — risk of missing tilt or uneven settlement that may cause floating roof binding, nozzle misalignment, or shell plate buckling.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: API 650 Annex B (Settlements of Storage Tanks) — minimum 8 measurement points for tanks ≤ 30 m, 12 points for > 30 m. IS 803:1976 (Code of Practice for Design, Fabrication and Erection of Vertical Mild Steel Cylindrical Welded Oil Storage Tanks) — settlement survey mandatory during initial hydrotest. OISD-118 — operational safety requirements for petroleum storage terminals.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Tank gauging system' AND atg_gauge_type IN ['Hydrostatic (pressure transmitter) — non-custody, ±5 mm','Guided wave radar (GWR) — atmospheric tanks, ±3 mm'] AND fluid_contamination_class IN ['Hydrocarbons — Class A (flash point > 65°C, e.g. diesel, lube oil, HFO)','Hydrocarbons — Class B (flash point 23–65°C, e.g. kerosene, ATF)','Hydrocarbons — Class C (flash point < 23°C, e.g. petrol, crude, naphtha)']
compliance_rules.message: For petroleum products used in custody transfer (sales metering), the ATG system must meet OISD-117 accuracy requirements (±1 mm) and be approved by the Weights and Measures department. Hydrostatic (±5 mm) and GWR (±3 mm) accuracy may be insufficient for fiscal metering. Consider upgrading to servo gauge or FMCW radar (±1 mm) for custody transfer applications.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: OISD-117 (Oil Industry Safety Directorate) — Tank Gauging Standard for petroleum installations in India. API MPMS Chapter 3 (Tank Gauging) — accuracy requirements for custody transfer: ±1 mm or 0.02% of tank height. Legal Metrology Act 2009 (India) — all weights and measures for trade must be verified and stamped by Weights and Measures Inspector.

cost_build_up.basis: Activity-based lumpsum costing per job. Base cost calibrated to a reference vessel/tank (25 m³ volume, 1.8 m diameter, water service, manway entry, standard UT inspection, India Metro, owner inspection). Scale using volume exponent, contamination multiplier, access multiplier, NDE multiplier, and certification adder. All base costs in USD 2024. Apply ENR CCI escalation and regional labour factor.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: Cost = Tier_1_Cost × access_multiplier × (1 + certification_cost_adder_pct) × nde_multiplier × atg_cost_multiplier × calibration_cost_multiplier
cost_build_up.full_formula: Total_Activity_Cost = Base_Cost × volume_factor × contamination_multiplier × access_multiplier × nde_multiplier × (1 + certification_adder) × atg_multiplier × calibration_multiplier × (ENR_CCI_current / 14250) × regional_labour_factor × sub_region_factor × (1 + waste_disposal_adder_fraction) × (1 + consumables_mobilisation) × (1 + contingency)

base_activity_costs_USD_2024.note: Base costs per job at reference conditions: 25 m³ volume, 1.8 m diameter, water/utility service, manway entry, India Metro location, ENR CCI 14250. Scale with multipliers above.

Pressure vessel inspection.typical_range_usd: 800 (small vessel, visual only) – 45000 (large vessel, PAUT + TPI cert)
Pressure vessel inspection.notes: Includes vessel opening/closing, confined space entry, visual inspection, UT thickness survey (spot checks), report preparation. Excludes scaffolding (add 30–60% of activity cost if required) and NDE premium (apply nde_multiplier).

Pressure vessel internal cleaning.typical_range_usd: 1200 (small, water service) – 80000 (large, toxic/sour service with waste disposal)
Pressure vessel internal cleaning.crew_size: 3–6 (cleaning crew, safety observer, standby BA wearer)
Pressure vessel internal cleaning.notes: Includes decontamination, high-pressure hydro-jetting (if required), waste collection in drums/IBC, and gas-free certification. Waste disposal cost is ADDITIONAL to above — apply waste_disposal_cost_adder separately.

Tank calibration.typical_range_usd: 1200 (small tank, manual strapping) – 35000 (large 60m dia tank, EODR/laser scan + certificate)
Tank calibration.notes: Includes measurements, deadwood corrections, floating roof corrections (if applicable), computation of capacity table, and issue of certified strapping table. Certificate renewal required every 5 years for custody transfer tanks in India (Legal Metrology Act). Apply calibration_cost_multiplier for method.

Tank settlement testing.typical_range_usd: 1500 (small tank, 8 points) – 18000 (large 60m tank, 16 points + pile load analysis)
Tank settlement testing.notes: Requires 5–7 survey visits at prescribed fill levels during initial hydrostatic test fill. Survey must be performed by a licensed surveyor. Includes pegs installation, final settlement report, and tilt profile. API 650 Annex B — maximum allowable differential settlement between adjacent points: 25 mm for 10 m tank diameter.

Tank gauging system.typical_range_usd: 2500 (hydrostatic, small tank) – 85000 (servo/dual, large custody transfer tank with full integration)
Tank gauging system.notes: Includes ATG transmitter supply, mounting hardware, junction box, cabling (up to 50 m), control room integration, calibration against reference standard, and commissioning report. Excludes SCADA/DCS software licence (separate IT cost). Apply atg_cost_multiplier for gauge technology. Intrinsically safe (Ex ia) certification mandatory for all petroleum service tanks — add 15% for ATEX/IECEx certified equipment.

Tank cleaning.typical_range_usd: 3000 (small utility tank, water service) – 2500000 (large crude tank, petroleum sludge removal)
Tank cleaning.crew_size: 4–20 (cleaning crew, safety officer, gas tester, waste disposal team)
Tank cleaning.notes: Includes degassing, sludge/sediment removal, water washing, drying/gas freeing, and issue of gas-free certificate. For crude oil tanks: slop oil recovery (may offset cost by USD 50–200/tonne recovered oil value). Waste disposal cost is a SEPARATE and often DOMINANT cost — apply waste_disposal_cost_adder. Final cost is extremely variable and MUST be quoted by specialist contractors based on tank history and waste characterisation.

adders.waste_disposal: See waste_disposal_cost_adder_usd in VESSEL-TEST-F-013 — apply as fixed USD adder per job, not percentage. Waste disposal is often the largest single cost element for tank cleaning.

notes.consumables_mobilisation: Cleaning chemicals, hydro-jetting consumables, weld consumables, UT couplant, transport of equipment to site, mobilisation of specialist crew. 0.15 for typical pressure vessel work; 0.25 for remote site.
notes.safety_equipment_ppe: SCBA sets, gas detectors (4-gas multi-gas detector), disposable coveralls, respirators, decontamination station. Add 0.20 for toxic/sour service — extra SCBA bottles, monitoring frequency, escape sets.
notes.scaffolding_access_platforms: Applicable to tall vertical pressure vessels (>3 m height) and large atmospheric tanks. Scaffolding cost 0.30–0.80 of base activity cost depending on height. Can dominate cost for tall towers. Consider alternative access systems (MAST, rope access) for cost reduction.
notes.contingency: Site activities carry higher uncertainty than equipment procurement — 15% standard. Increase to 25% for tank cleaning (unknown contamination, potential for sludge volume exceeding estimate) and 20% for inspection (potential for found condition requiring repair before return to service).

cost_build_up.freight_insurance_note: NOT APPLICABLE for site activities (Type A items). No equipment is imported. Mobilisation cost is included in consumables_mobilisation adder above.
cost_build_up.erection_installation_note: NOT APPLICABLE as a separate adder for site activities. Labour for all installation/testing/cleaning work is included in the base activity cost rate above.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_activity_cost_USD × (ENR_CCI_current / 14250) × regional_labour_factor × sub_region_factor × contamination_multiplier × access_multiplier × (1 + certification_adder) × (1 + contingency). ENR CCI is a labour-cost index — appropriate for activity/service costing. For Type A items, equipment import duty does NOT apply.

India.local_ref: CPWD DSR 2023-24 mechanical labour / OISD contract rates

Middle East (GCC).local_ref: Faithful+Gould MENA labour rates 2024 (expat + local mix)

regional_factors.equipment_duty_category: Site activity — no import duty applicable
regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. For site activities, the labour_factor sub-region multiplier is most relevant. equipment_factor and civil_factor are not applicable for Type A items.

import_duty.components: No goods imported — service contract only
import_duty.note: Import duty does not apply to site activity / service contracts. GST applies to service invoices in India at 18% (SAC 998731 — maintenance and repair services for industrial machinery). Factor GST separately into project cost if applicable.
Import duty does not apply to service contracts.
Import duty does not apply to service contracts.
Import duty does not apply. UK VAT (20%) applies to domestic service contracts — factor separately.
Import duty does not apply to service contracts.
Import duty does not apply to service contracts.
Import duty does not apply to service contracts.
Import duty does not apply to service contracts.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ. For Type A (Activity) items, equipment_origin and import duty are not applicable.
project_settings.equipment_origin: N/A — site activity, no equipment procurement
project_settings.escalation_factor_note: ENR CCI escalation = 14480/14250 = 1.016 (+1.6% from 2024 base to mid-2025). All activity costs are labour-dominated — regional labour rate variations (India 0.25× vs USA 1.0× vs UK 1.35×) dwarf the ENR CCI annual escalation. Review regional_index factors when estimating outside India.

usd_cost_baseline.note: USA-base USD (matches index base year 2024). Engine applies index_now/base x regional x sub_region factor x fx. ALL rates are public-benchmark ESTIMATES (verify=true) pending vendor/DSR calibration. base_cost_USD scaled by (volume/25)^0.65.

Pressure vessel internal cleaning.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 38000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 51422.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Pressure vessel inspection.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 38000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 51422.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Tank calibration.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 38000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 51422.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Tank settlement testing.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 38000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 51422.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Tank gauging system.basis: Turton-parametric ~2018 USD/unit 38000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 51422.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Tank cleaning.basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/job 3200 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 4071.43. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

SCHEMA-VFD-SOFTSTARTER.generation_note: v1.0 RELEASE - All fields, cost formulas, compliance rules, adders and spec strings completed by cost estimate engineer. CEPCI 2024 baseline (816) for equipment items; ENR CCI 2024 (14250) retained for regional index per skeleton. Covers LV VFD (wall-mount and cabinet), MV VFD, soft starters, and industrial/standard socket outlets. F-007 corrected from erroneous efficiency field to DC Bus / Output Voltage field. Base costs USD 2024 lumpsum catalogue pricing. | v1.0-priced: usd_cost_baseline + multiplier tables (USA-base; public-benchmark, verify=true).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-VFD-SOFTSTARTER.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | {motor_power} kW | {system_voltage_kv} kV / {voltage} V | {current} A | IP{enclosure} | {mounting_type} | {communication_protocol} | Qty={quantity} No.
SCHEMA-VFD-SOFTSTARTER.spec_example: Variable Frequency Drive (Wall Mount - 11kW) | 11 kW | 415 V | 22 A | IP54 | Wall-mount | Modbus RTU | Qty=2 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select equipment type. LV VFDs (415 V) are wall-mount up to ~55 kW and floor-standing cabinet above. MV VFD at 6.6 kV for large drives above 400 kW. Soft starters are lower capital than VFDs but provide no speed control — only reduced voltage starting. Socket outlets are separately priced catalogue items.

fields.label: Motor / Load Power Rating
fields.hint: Rated motor shaft power in kW that the VFD or soft starter must drive. Select the drive rating equal to or one frame above the motor nameplate kW. Standard IEC frame sizes: 11, 15, 18.5, 22, 30, 37, 45, 55, 75, 90, 110, 132, 160, 200, 250, 315, 400, 500, 630, 800, 1000, 1250, 1700 kW. Motor power is the single largest cost driver for VFDs and soft starters — cost scales approximately as kW^0.75.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype NOT IN ['Socket Outlet (Industrial - 16A)','Socket Outlet (Industrial - 32A)','Socket Outlet (Standard - 13A)']

fields.label: Supply Voltage (LV)
fields.hint: LV supply voltage to VFD or soft starter input terminals. India standard: 415 V ±6%, 3-phase, 50 Hz (IS 12360). USA standard: 460/480 V, 60 Hz. EU standard: 400 V, 50 Hz. Supply voltage determines drive model selection — ordering a 400 V drive for a 415 V system is acceptable (within tolerance); do not use a 230 V drive on 415 V supply. Not applicable for MV VFD (use F-006 System Voltage instead).

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Variable Frequency Drive (Wall Mount - 11kW)','Variable Frequency Drive (Wall Mount - 45kW)','Variable Frequency Drive (Cabinet - 250kW)','Soft Starter (Standard - 75kW)','Soft Starter (Heavy Duty - 200kW)','Socket Outlet (Industrial - 16A)','Socket Outlet (Industrial - 32A)','Socket Outlet (Standard - 13A)']

fields.label: Output / Full Load Current (FLC)
fields.hint: Full load current (FLC) of the motor at rated voltage and power factor. Used to size the VFD/soft starter output current rating — drive FLC must be ≥ motor FLC. Calculated from: FLC = (kW × 1000) / (√3 × V × pf × η). Approximate for 415 V, pf=0.85, η=0.92: 11 kW→22 A, 45 kW→85 A, 75 kW→140 A, 200 kW→360 A, 250 kW→450 A. For socket outlets: rated current of the outlet (13A/16A/32A) — determines current rating, not motor FLC.

fields.label: Output Frequency Range
fields.hint: Output frequency range of the VFD. Standard process applications: 0–50 Hz (India/EU, 50 Hz supply) or 0–60 Hz (USA). Extended 0–120 Hz for centrifugal pumps and fans requiring overspeed capability. 0–400 Hz only for special high-speed spindle drives or servo applications — significant cost premium. Not applicable for soft starters (no variable frequency output) or socket outlets.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Variable Frequency Drive (Wall Mount - 11kW)','Variable Frequency Drive (Wall Mount - 45kW)','Variable Frequency Drive (Cabinet - 250kW)','Medium Voltage VFD (6.6kV - 1700kW)']

fields.label: MV System Voltage
fields.hint: Medium voltage supply level for MV VFD. India standard MV distribution: 6.6 kV and 11 kV (IS 13947). 3.3 kV used in some older industrial installations and mining. MV VFDs require input transformer (typically included in package supply), MV switchgear coordination, and high-resistance grounding. Cost is significantly higher than LV VFD — a 6.6 kV, 1700 kW MV VFD is ~15–20× the cost of an equivalent LV drive per kW. MV system voltage is a primary cost driver after motor power.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Medium Voltage VFD (6.6kV - 1700kW)'

fields.tier_note: Tier 1 - Quick Estimate (always visible for MV VFD)

fields.label: DC Bus / Output Voltage
fields.hint: DC bus voltage of the VFD (intermediate DC link). For 415 V input VFD: DC bus is typically 560–600 V. For 460/480 V input: ~650 V DC bus. DC bus voltage determines IGBT rating and affects harmonic filter specification. Note: library values (350 V, 450 V) were misclassified — these correspond to partial DC bus voltages in older 230 V input drives and special servo VFDs respectively. Standard 415 V LV VFD DC bus = 560 V; standard 6.6 kV MV VFD DC bus (diode front-end) = ~9.4 kV.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Variable Frequency Drive (Wall Mount - 11kW)','Variable Frequency Drive (Wall Mount - 45kW)','Variable Frequency Drive (Cabinet - 250kW)','Medium Voltage VFD (6.6kV - 1700kW)']

fields.label: Enclosure Protection Rating
options: IP42 (indoor, limited dust — general plant room)
IP66 (severe outdoor, heavy dust and high-pressure wash)
fields.hint: Ingress protection per IEC 60529 / IS 13947. IP20 is minimum for MCC-room-mounted drives in clean, air-conditioned environments. IP54 is standard for general industrial indoor/outdoor applications. IP55 mandatory for food, pharma, and washdown areas. IP66/67 for severe outdoor or aggressive process environments. Higher IP rating adds 10–30% to drive cost. For socket outlets: minimum IP44 for outdoor/wet areas (IS 1293); IP67 for complete immersion-proof applications.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical units. For VFDs and soft starters: include all duty units — spare/standby drives are generally NOT stocked for LV VFDs (hot-swap or cold-standby strategy). MV VFDs above 500 kW: consider 1 installed spare per 3–4 operating drives due to long lead times (16–24 weeks). For socket outlets: enter total outlet count per type.

fields.label: Mounting / Installation Type
options: Wall-mount (direct to wall or panel back-plate)
Rack-mount (19" rack, data centre / SCADA room)
DIN rail (small drives ≤ 2.2 kW, control panel)
fields.default: Wall-mount (direct to wall or panel back-plate)
fields.hint: Physical mounting arrangement. Wall-mount is standard for LV VFDs up to ~55 kW. Floor-standing cabinet for 90 kW and above. MCC-integrated cubicle (Form 3b or 4 separation per IEC 61439) adds ~20–35% premium over standalone unit — includes withdrawable chassis, busbar connection, and door interlocking. Cabinet/MCC integration cost is factored separately as an MCC line item; enter drive-only cost here and add MCC cubicle under the MCC schema.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype NOT IN ['Socket Outlet (Industrial - 16A)','Socket Outlet (Industrial - 32A)','Socket Outlet (Standard - 13A)']

fields.label: Communication Protocol
fields.hint: Fieldbus / industrial communication protocol for drive integration into DCS, PLC, or SCADA. Modbus RTU is standard and lowest cost — included in most drives by default. PROFIBUS DP is standard for Siemens S7 PLC installations. PROFINET / EtherNet/IP for modern Ethernet-based control architectures. Each protocol option typically requires a plug-in option card (~USD 200–600 adder per drive). Not applicable for socket outlets.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype NOT IN ['Socket Outlet (Industrial - 16A)','Socket Outlet (Industrial - 32A)','Socket Outlet (Standard - 13A)']

fields.label: Soft Starter Duty Class
options: Class 10 — Standard duty (pumps, fans, light conveyor)
Class 20 — Heavy duty (compressors, loaded conveyors, crushers)
Class 30 — Extra heavy duty (ball mills, high-inertia loads)
Bypass contactor integrated (reduced losses after start)
fields.default: Class 10 — Standard duty (pumps, fans, light conveyor)
fields.hint: IEC 60947-4-2 starter duty class defines the permissible number and duration of starts. Class 10: ≤10 starts/hour, normal loads. Class 20: ≤20 starts/hour or high-inertia loads (compressors). Class 30: ≤30 starts/hour or very high inertia. Heavy-duty and extra heavy-duty soft starters have larger thyristor silicon ratings — adds 20–40% to cost vs. Class 10. Bypass contactor option reduces ongoing losses once motor reaches full speed — recommended for all continuous duty applications.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Soft Starter (Standard - 75kW)','Soft Starter (Heavy Duty - 200kW)']

fields.label: Socket Outlet Standard & Mounting
fields.hint: Socket outlet type and mounting standard. IS 1293 / BS 1363 13A: standard office/building outlets — not suitable for industrial plant areas. IEC 60309 (IS 6538) industrial plugs and sockets: colour-coded by voltage (blue = 230 V, red = 415 V 3-phase). IP44 minimum for indoor industrial environments; IP67 for outdoor or washdown. 32A outlets are typically 3-phase for portable equipment — verify phase configuration. Include socket, socket housing, and cable entry in unit cost.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Socket Outlet (Industrial - 16A)','Socket Outlet (Industrial - 32A)','Socket Outlet (Standard - 13A)']

fields.label: System Voltage (numeric)
fields.hint: Select equipment type. LV VFDs (415 V) are wall-mount up to ~55 kW and floor-standing cabinet above. MV VFD at 6.6 kV for large drives above 400 kW. Soft starters are lower capital than VFDs but provide no speed control — only reduced voltage starting. Socket outlets are separately priced catalogue items.

fields.label: Enclosure IP Rating (numeric)
fields.hint: Select equipment type. LV VFDs (415 V) are wall-mount up to ~55 kW and floor-standing cabinet above. MV VFD at 6.6 kV for large drives above 400 kW. Soft starters are lower capital than VFDs but provide no speed control — only reduced voltage starting. Socket outlets are separately priced catalogue items.

fields.label: Installation Type
fields.hint: Select equipment type. LV VFDs (415 V) are wall-mount up to ~55 kW and floor-standing cabinet above. MV VFD at 6.6 kV for large drives above 400 kW. Soft starters are lower capital than VFDs but provide no speed control — only reduced voltage starting. Socket outlets are separately priced catalogue items.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: motor_power > 55 AND equipment_subtype == 'Variable Frequency Drive (Wall Mount - 45kW)'
compliance_rules.message: Motor power exceeds typical wall-mount VFD envelope. Wall-mount drives are generally rated up to 55 kW (at 415 V). Above 55 kW, floor-standing cabinet VFDs are standard due to thermal dissipation and physical size requirements. Consider re-categorising to 'Variable Frequency Drive (Cabinet - 250kW)' for accurate costing.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61800-5-1 (Adjustable Speed Electrical Power Drive Systems — Safety Requirements). Standard LV VFD ratings: wall-mount typically ≤ 55 kW (e.g., ABB ACS580, Siemens G120 series). Floor-standing cabinet from 75 kW and above for standard industrial VFD products.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: current > 0 AND motor_power > 0 AND voltage_numeric > 0 AND (current < (motor_power * 1000) / (1.732 * voltage_numeric * 0.95))
compliance_rules.message: Full Load Current (FLC) entered appears understated for the given motor power and voltage. Verify using: FLC = kW × 1000 / (√3 × V × pf × η). For 415 V, pf=0.85, η=0.92: FLC ≈ motor_kW × 1.73. An undersized FLC will result in an undersized drive frame — risk of nuisance tripping on overload.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60034-1 (Rotating Electrical Machines — Rating and Performance). VFD sizing rule: drive output current rating ≥ motor FLC × service factor (typically 1.0–1.1 for normal loads, 1.5 for heavy duty per IEC 61800-2). IS 325:1996 (Three Phase Induction Motors) — FLC tabulated per frame size.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Medium Voltage VFD (6.6kV - 1700kW)' AND motor_power > 1700
compliance_rules.message: Motor power exceeds the maximum catalogue rating (1700 kW) for this MV VFD item. Above 1700 kW at 6.6 kV, custom multi-level drive designs are required (e.g., NPC or CHB topology). Lead time increases to 36–52 weeks. Request a separate quotation from the drive manufacturer for this rating.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61800-2 (General Requirements for AC Drives above 1 kV). Standard MV VFD catalogue ratings: 6.6 kV series typically up to 5000 kW (multi-level CHB or ANPC topology — Siemens SINAMICS GM150, ABB ACS1000, GE LV5 series). 1700 kW is the standard library item; above this is treated as custom order.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: enclosure_rating_numeric < 54 AND (equipment_subtype IN ['Variable Frequency Drive (Wall Mount - 11kW)','Variable Frequency Drive (Wall Mount - 45kW)','Variable Frequency Drive (Cabinet - 250kW)','Soft Starter (Standard - 75kW)','Soft Starter (Heavy Duty - 200kW)']) AND installation_type == 'Outdoor'
compliance_rules.message: IP rating below IP54 is not recommended for outdoor or process-area installation of VFDs and soft starters. Minimum IP54 required for outdoor wall-mount. Consider IP55 or IP66 for coastal, chemical plant, or washdown areas. Low IP rating leads to premature failure of power electronics from dust ingress and condensation — a common and expensive failure mode.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60529 (Degrees of Protection — IP Code). IS 13947-1 — specifies IP ratings for industrial switchgear and controlgear. For outdoor VFD installation: minimum IP54 per most drive manufacturers' installation guides (ABB, Siemens, Danfoss). ATEX zone applications require additional Ex classification — consult drive manufacturer.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['Soft Starter (Standard - 75kW)','Soft Starter (Heavy Duty - 200kW)'] AND motor_power > 200
compliance_rules.message: For motor power above 200 kW, consider whether a VFD (Variable Frequency Drive) may be more appropriate than a soft starter. While soft starters are less expensive, VFDs provide continuous speed control, better energy savings on variable torque loads (pumps/fans), and active harmonic control. Life cycle cost analysis recommended above 200 kW.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 60947-4-2 (AC Semiconductor Motor Controllers and Starters). Industry guidance: soft starters preferred for fixed-speed applications needing reduced start current only. VFDs preferred for variable torque loads (pumps, fans, compressors) where energy savings at part load exceed the capital cost premium within 2–4 years.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['Variable Frequency Drive (Cabinet - 250kW)','Medium Voltage VFD (6.6kV - 1700kW)'] AND communication_protocol == 'None (terminal strip I/O only)'
compliance_rules.message: Large drives (250 kW+) without a fieldbus communication protocol will require extensive hardwired I/O for remote start/stop, speed reference, fault feedback, and running status. This increases cable and marshalling panel costs significantly. Strongly recommend adding Modbus RTU or PROFIBUS DP as minimum for drives above 90 kW.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEC 61800-7 (Generic Interface and Use of Profiles for Power Drive Systems). Industry practice: all drives above 55 kW in process plants should have fieldbus integration for remote monitoring, diagnostics, and predictive maintenance (IEEE Std 1686 for intelligent electronic devices).

cost_build_up.basis: Catalogue-priced electrical equipment. Base equipment cost (BEC) from manufacturer published list prices (USD 2024), discounted to project net price. Cost scales non-linearly with motor power (exponent ~0.75 for LV VFDs, ~0.70 for MV VFDs). Socket outlets are fixed catalogue price per unit. Apply IP rating multiplier, protocol adder, and regional factor for final estimate.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: BEC = Tier_1_BEC × ip_cost_multiplier × soft_starter_duty_multiplier
cost_build_up.full_formula: Total_Installed_Cost = BEC × (1 + freight_insurance + installation_wiring + commissioning + protocol_option_adder_fraction) × (CEPCI_current / CEPCI_base) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × (1 + import_duty_if_imported) × (1 + contingency)

base_equipment_costs_USD_2024.note: Net project prices (list price × 0.60–0.70 typical trade discount for project quantities). Reference: IP54, Modbus RTU, standard enclosure, 415 V supply. Scale using power exponent for intermediate kW ratings.

Variable Frequency Drive (Wall Mount - 1.notes: ABB ACS580 / Siemens G120 / Danfoss FC302 equivalent, IP54, Modbus RTU. Wall-mount, 415 V 3-phase. Inclusive of EMC filter (C2 category). Excludes installation and cabling.

Variable Frequency Drive (Wall Mount - 4.notes: ABB ACS580 / Siemens G120X / Schneider ATV630 equivalent, IP54, Modbus RTU. Wall-mount up to 55 kW. EMC filter included. Braking chopper: optional (USD 300–500 adder for hoisting/high-inertia loads).

Variable Frequency Drive (Cabinet - 250k.notes: ABB ACS880 / Siemens S120 / Danfoss FC102 floor-standing cabinet, IP54, Modbus RTU. Includes main contactor, fuses, EMC filter, and control panel. Floor-standing powder-coated steel cabinet. Excludes civil foundation pad.

Medium Voltage VFD (6.6kV - 1700kW).notes: ABB ACS1000 / Siemens SINAMICS GM150 / GE MV7000 equivalent at 6.6 kV. Multi-level NPC or CHB topology. Includes input transformer (if air-cooled, cell design), control cubicle, bypass switch. Excludes MV cable and MV switchgear. Factory acceptance test (FAT) included. Lead time: 20–28 weeks typical.

Soft Starter (Standard - 75kW).notes: ABB PSTX / Siemens 3RW50 / Schneider ATS48 equivalent, 75 kW, 415 V, IP20 (MCC mounting). Class 10 duty. Includes bypass contactor and overload relay. Thyristor 3-phase controlled. Excludes main switch and fuses.

Soft Starter (Heavy Duty - 200kW).notes: ABB PSTX / Siemens 3RW55 heavy duty equivalent, 200 kW, 415 V, IP54, Class 20. Integrated bypass contactor. Suitable for compressors, loaded conveyors, crushers. Motor thermistor input included.

Socket Outlet (Industrial - 16A).notes: IEC 60309 / IS 6538 industrial socket outlet, 16A, 1-phase or 3-phase (specify), IP44 standard. Colour: blue (230V single-phase) or red (415V 3-phase). Includes flush/surface housing, cable entry gland. Excludes MCB protection (in distribution board).

Socket Outlet (Industrial - 32A).notes: IEC 60309 industrial socket outlet, 32A, typically 3-phase 415V (red). IP44 standard, IP67 for outdoor/wet areas (+30%). Includes housing and cable entry. Used for portable welding sets, compressors, and construction equipment.

Socket Outlet (Standard - 13A).notes: IS 1293 / BS 1363 standard 13A 3-pin outlet. For office, instrumentation room, and administrative building use. NOT for heavy industrial plant area use. Surface or flush mount. Includes shuttered socket with earth pin.

notes.freight_insurance: LV VFDs and soft starters are manufactured by ABB, Siemens, Schneider, Danfoss — primarily imported for India. Use 0.04 for CIF India. MV VFDs: 0.06 due to size and fragile transformer/converter cells.
notes.installation_wiring: Includes mechanical fixing, power cable terminations, control wiring, earth bonding. VFDs above 90 kW: add screened control cable and EMC earthing bonding — increases installation cost. LV VFD wall-mount: ~0.10. Cabinet VFD: ~0.18. MV VFD: ~0.25.
notes.commissioning_parameterisation: Parameter setup, motor auto-tuning run, protection relay settings, fieldbus commissioning, and 4-hour load test. MV VFD commissioning is more extensive — vendor engineer attendance typically required (add USD 5,000–15,000 as lumpsum for MV).
notes.cable_conduit_trunking: Power and control cables from distribution board/MCC to drive, and from drive to motor. Screened cable mandatory for VFD motor cables (to limit dV/dt and EMC emissions per IEC 61800-3). Separate line item in cable schedule — estimate here at 0.12 of drive cost as a quickcheck.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_cost_USD × (ENR_CCI_current / 14250) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × ip_multiplier × (1 + contingency). regional_factor converts USA base to project region. Note: for electrical equipment, CEPCI is arguably more appropriate — ENR CCI is retained here per skeleton template standard. Escalation difference is minor (~1–2% per year).

India.local_ref: BEE / IEEMA electrical equipment price index 2024

regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: Switched ENR->CEPCI: procured/manufactured equipment, supply-cost dominated. verify=true

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. VFDs: HS 8504.40; Soft starters: HS 8536.50; Socket outlets: HS 8536.69. Verify current rate and HS code with customs broker. Some VFD sub-components may qualify for EPCG / project import concessions.
Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.
