project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ
project_settings.escalation_factor_note: ENR CCI escalation = 14480/14250 = 1.016 (+1.6% from 2024 base to mid-2025). VFDs and soft starters are predominantly imported from Europe (ABB, Siemens, Danfoss, Schneider) or China — apply import duty 30.8% for India. Indian-made drives (Kirloskar, CG) available at LV for standard ratings — reduce import duty to 0% if domestically sourced.

usd_cost_baseline.note: USA-base USD (CEPCI 816 / 2024). Engine applies index_now/base x regional x fx. Public-benchmark ESTIMATES, verify=true. base_cost_USD at ref_motor_power; (P/Pref)^0.75 applied.

Variable Frequency Drive (Wall Mount - 1.basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 6500 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 8796.02. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Variable Frequency Drive (Wall Mount - 4.basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 6500 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 8796.02. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Variable Frequency Drive (Cabinet - 250k.basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 6500 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 8796.02. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Medium Voltage VFD (6.6kV - 1700kW).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 6500 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 8796.02. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Soft Starter (Standard - 75kW).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 3000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 4059.7. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Soft Starter (Heavy Duty - 200kW).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 3000 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 4059.7. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

SCHEMA-VFD-SOFTSTARTER._step5_engineering_gap: voltage_numeric / enclosure_rating_numeric — derived numeric fields not defined; rules R-002/R-004 need them

SCHEMA-WASTE-EQUIP.template_name: Solid & Liquid Waste - Solid Waste Equipment
SCHEMA-WASTE-EQUIP.discipline_group: Solid & Liquid Waste - Solid Waste Equipment
SCHEMA-WASTE-EQUIP.generation_note: v1.0 RELEASE - All fields, cost formulas, compliance rules, adders, and spec strings completed by cost estimate engineer. CEPCI 2024 baseline (816). Covers 7 technology families: MRF/sorting, landfill, incineration/WTE, flue gas treatment, composting, anaerobic digestion, biogas upgrading. Base costs USD 2024, power-law scaling throughout. | v1.0-priced: usd_cost_baseline + multiplier tables (USA-base; public-benchmark, verify=true).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-WASTE-EQUIP.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | Cap={throughput_capacity_t_day} t/day | Pwr={motor_power} kW | Vol={volume} m³ | T={design_temperature}°C | P={design_pressure} bar(g) | Eff={efficiency}% | MOC={moc} | Qty={quantity} No.
SCHEMA-WASTE-EQUIP.spec_example: Waste Compactor (Stationary) | Cap=50 t/day | Pwr=37 kW | MOC=Mild Steel (AR400 liner) | Qty=1 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Drives cost method and conditional field visibility. Equipment is grouped by technology family: MRF/Sorting, Size Reduction, Conveying/Baling, Transfer Station, Landfill, Incineration/WTE, Composting, and Anaerobic/Biogas.

fields.label: Motor / Drive Power
fields.hint: Installed motor/drive shaft power in kW. For shredders and grinders this is the primary cutting drive — typical range 30–400 kW. For sorting conveyors and screens: 3–30 kW. For incinerator grate drives and WTE auxiliaries: up to 400 kW total installed. Motor power drives MCC/LV panel sizing and is a major cost indicator for high-power equipment.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Waste Compactor (Stationary)','Waste Compactor (Mobile)','Waste Shredder','Waste Grinder','Waste Pulper','Ballistic Separator','Air Classifier','Eddy Current Separator','Magnetic Separator','Optical Sorter','Trommel Screen','Vibrating Screen (Waste)','Disc Screen','Conveyor (Waste)','Conveyor (Sorting)','Baling Press','Wrapping Machine','Transfer Station Compactor','Landfill Gas Engine']

fields.label: Vessel / Digester / Cell Volume
fields.hint: Internal working volume of reactor, digester vessel, leachate pond, landfill cell, or composting pad. For Anaerobic Digesters: HRT × daily input volume (typical 500–5000 m³ for wet AD, 200–2000 m³ for dry AD). For Leachate Treatment Plant: buffer tank volume. For Ash Landfill Cell: airspace volume in m³. For In-Vessel Composting: reactor drum/tunnel volume. Primary sizing and cost driver for all vessel-type equipment.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Anaerobic Digester (Dry)','Anaerobic Digester (Wet)','Leachate Treatment Plant','Ash Landfill Cell','Composting (In-Vessel)','Transfer Station Container','Landfill Gas Collection']

fields.label: Leachate / Liquid Flow Rate
fields.hint: Daily liquid throughput for Leachate Treatment Plant or wet process systems. Leachate generation rate: typically 0.1–0.3 m³/tonne of waste in humid climates. For a 500 t/day landfill in India, expect 50–150 m³/day leachate. Range 500–1000 m³/day corresponds to a large regional landfill (2000–3000 t/day intake).

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Leachate Treatment Plant','Leachate Collection System']

fields.label: Solid / Gas Throughput Capacity
fields.hint: Rated solid waste / refuse-derived fuel (RDF) throughput capacity in metric tonnes per day (t/day) at design conditions. For MRF sorting equipment: input waste feed rate. For incinerators and WTE: waste firing rate. For composting: input organic waste per day. For biogas upgrading: raw biogas input in Nm³/hr — enter as t/day equivalent (1 t/day ≈ 40 Nm³/hr for MSW biogas). Primary cost driver for all large waste processing equipment.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Waste Shredder','Waste Grinder','Waste Pulper','Ballistic Separator','Air Classifier','Eddy Current Separator','Magnetic Separator','Optical Sorter','Trommel Screen','Vibrating Screen (Waste)','Disc Screen','Conveyor (Waste)','Conveyor (Sorting)','Baling Press','Transfer Station Compactor','Waste Compactor (Stationary)','Waste Compactor (Mobile)','Incinerator (Grate)','Incinerator (Rotary Kiln)','Incinerator (Pyrolysis)','Incinerator (Plasma)','WTE Boiler (Grate)','WTE Boiler (CFB)','Flue Gas Treatment (Dry)','Flue Gas Treatment (Semi-Dry)','Flue Gas Treatment (Wet)','Bottom Ash Conveyor','Fly Ash Handling','Ash Stabilization','Composting (Windrow)','Composting (In-Vessel)','Composting (Aerated Static Pile)','Compost Turner','Compost Screener','Anaerobic Digester (Dry)','Anaerobic Digester (Wet)','Biogas Upgrading (Membrane)','Biogas Upgrading (PSA)','Landfill Compactor']

fields.label: Design Temperature
fields.hint: Maximum design operating temperature of combustion chamber, boiler, or reactor. For grate incinerators: 850–950°C (mandatory minimum 850°C per EU Directive 2010/75/EU and CPCB MSW Rules 2016 for dioxin/furan destruction). For rotary kiln hazardous waste: 1100–1200°C. For plasma: up to 1500°C. For pyrolysis reactor: 400–600°C. Directly drives refractory specification, alloy selection, and equipment cost premium.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Incinerator (Grate)','Incinerator (Rotary Kiln)','Incinerator (Pyrolysis)','Incinerator (Plasma)','WTE Boiler (Grate)','WTE Boiler (CFB)','Flue Gas Treatment (Dry)','Flue Gas Treatment (Semi-Dry)','Flue Gas Treatment (Wet)']

fields.label: WTE / Engine Electrical Output
fields.hint: Net electrical power output for WTE turbine or landfill gas engine. For WTE: gross output typically 550–650 kWh/tonne of MSW burned; net ~70–75% of gross after parasitic load. For a 500 t/day plant: expect ~10–12 MW gross. For Landfill Gas Engine: output depends on gas yield; typical 1–5 MW for a 500 t/day active landfill. Primary commercial revenue driver and key sizing parameter for WTE projects.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['WTE Turbine','WTE Boiler (Grate)','WTE Boiler (CFB)','Landfill Gas Engine']

fields.label: Separation / Capture Efficiency
fields.hint: Recovery / capture efficiency of the sorting, separation, or gas treatment system. For Optical Sorter: purity of sorted stream, typically 90–98%. For Magnetic Separator: ferrous recovery ≥ 95% standard. For Flue Gas Treatment: pollutant removal efficiency — HCl, SO₂ ≥ 95% (dry), ≥ 97% (semi-dry/wet) per EU IED Directive. For Landfill Gas Collection: gas capture rate, typically 60–85% of generated LFG. Affects both operational performance and regulatory compliance.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Optical Sorter','Magnetic Separator','Eddy Current Separator','Air Classifier','Flue Gas Treatment (Dry)','Flue Gas Treatment (Semi-Dry)','Flue Gas Treatment (Wet)','Landfill Gas Collection','Biogas Upgrading (Membrane)','Biogas Upgrading (PSA)']

fields.label: WTE Boiler / Steam Design Pressure
fields.hint: Steam drum design pressure for WTE boiler. Typical small-to-medium WTE: 40 bar(g), 400°C (subcritical). Large plants: 60–80 bar(g), 450°C for higher turbine efficiency. Higher pressure increases boiler cost significantly — upgrading from 40 to 60 bar adds ~20–30% to boiler cost. Boiler must comply with IBR (Indian Boiler Regulations 1950) or ASME Sec.I. WTE boiler MOC is critical: superheater tubes must be alloy steel (SA-213 T22 or T91) to resist chlorine-induced high-temperature corrosion.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['WTE Boiler (Grate)','WTE Boiler (CFB)','WTE Turbine']

fields.label: MOC / Construction Material
options: Alloy Steel (SA-213 T22 / T91 for boiler tubes)
fields.hint: Material of construction for primary structural/wetted/contact parts. For shredders, grinders, and conveyors: MS + AR400 liner is standard to resist abrasion from MSW. For anaerobic digesters and leachate tanks: SS316L or epoxy-coated CS to resist H₂S and VFA corrosion. For WTE boiler superheaters: alloy steel mandatory. For landfill liners and leachate sumps: HDPE geomembrane. MOC impacts both equipment cost and maintenance lifecycle cost.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical equipment units in this BOQ line item. For incinerators and WTE lines: typically 1–3 parallel lines. For conveyors and separators in MRF: multiple units on the sorting line are typical. Spare equipment strategy should be factored into quantity (e.g., 2 = 1 duty + 1 standby for critical equipment).

fields.label: Conveyor Length
fields.hint: Conveyor centre-to-centre length in metres. For waste reception conveyors: typically 15–60 m. For sorting line conveyors: 20–40 m with adjustable speed drive (VFD). Length is the primary cost driver — includes belt/chain, structure, drives, take-up, and walkway. Quote as total conveyor run length; bends/transfer points are priced as lumpsum per transfer point.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Conveyor (Waste)','Conveyor (Sorting)','Bottom Ash Conveyor']

fields.label: Composting Pad / Windrow Area
fields.hint: Total composting pad / covered shed floor area for windrow or ASP composting. Sizing rule of thumb: ~3–5 m² per tonne of daily input for windrow open systems, ~2–3 m² per tonne for ASP with forced aeration. Example: 100 t/day input requires ~300–500 m² operational windrow area plus curing and storage pads. Area drives civil foundation, leachate collection slab, and aeration pipe network costs.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Composting (Windrow)','Composting (Aerated Static Pile)']

fields.label: Landfill Cell Area / Airspace
fields.hint: Engineered landfill cell area in hectares for lining, leachate collection, and gas collection cost estimation. Typical cell size: 2–10 ha per operational phase. Total landfill area includes buffer, leachate pond, access roads. Cell area is the primary cost driver for HDPE liner, geocomposite, and drainage blanket materials. Rule: ~1 ha of base liner costs USD 80,000–120,000 for double-liner system.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Ash Landfill Cell','Leachate Collection System','Landfill Gas Collection']

compliance_rules.condition_expr: throughput_capacity_t_day < 10 AND equipment_subtype IN ['Incinerator (Grate)','Incinerator (Rotary Kiln)','WTE Boiler (Grate)','WTE Boiler (CFB)']
compliance_rules.message: Incinerator / WTE capacity below 10 t/day is sub-economic for grate or CFB technology. Capital cost per tonne processed becomes disproportionately high below 10 t/day. Consider pyrolysis or small rotary kiln for small-scale biomedical/industrial waste. For MSW below 50 t/day, windrow composting or transfer to a regional facility may be more appropriate.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CPCB Guidelines for Municipal Solid Waste Management (2016) — minimum viable scale for mechanised MSW incineration is 300 t/day. EU BREF on Waste Incineration (2019) — economics of grate technology favour scale above 100,000 t/year.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['Incinerator (Grate)','Incinerator (Rotary Kiln)'] AND design_temperature < 850
compliance_rules.message: REGULATORY VIOLATION: Combustion chamber temperature below 850°C is not compliant for municipal solid waste incineration. Minimum 850°C for at least 2 seconds residence time is mandatory per EU Directive 2010/75/EU and CPCB MSW Rules 2016 to ensure dioxin/furan destruction. Correct design temperature to ≥ 850°C. For hazardous waste (rotary kiln): minimum 1100°C.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: EU Directive 2010/75/EU (IED) Art. 50 — combustion conditions: ≥ 850°C, ≥ 2s gas residence time. CPCB MSW (Management and Handling) Rules 2016 — incinerators must maintain ≥ 850°C. For hazardous waste: Hazardous Waste Rules 2016, Schedule IV — ≥ 1100°C.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['Anaerobic Digester (Wet)'] AND volume < 200
compliance_rules.message: Wet anaerobic digester volume below 200 m³ is below practical minimum for mesophilic continuous stirred tank reactor (CSTR) operation. Mixing inefficiencies and heat loss disproportionately increase at small scale. Consider dry plug-flow digestion or batch systems for volumes below 200 m³.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IWA Anaerobic Digestion Model (ADM1). Typical HRT for wet MSW AD: 20–30 days. Minimum economic scale for CSTR wet AD: ~5 t/day input ≈ ~200 m³ digester volume at 10% TS feed.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['Flue Gas Treatment (Dry)','Flue Gas Treatment (Semi-Dry)','Flue Gas Treatment (Wet)'] AND efficiency < 95
compliance_rules.message: REGULATORY VIOLATION: Flue gas treatment efficiency below 95% does not meet EU IED emission limits for HCl, SO₂, and dust from waste incinerators. Minimum required removal efficiencies: HCl ≥ 97% (wet scrubber), SO₂ ≥ 95% (dry/semi-dry), particulates to < 10 mg/Nm³. Review scrubber design and reagent dosing rates.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: EU Directive 2010/75/EU Annex VI — emission limit values for waste incineration: HCl < 10 mg/Nm³, SO₂ < 50 mg/Nm³, dust < 10 mg/Nm³ (half-hour average). CPCB WtE/Incineration Guidelines (2019) — equivalent Indian emission standards.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['WTE Boiler (Grate)','WTE Boiler (CFB)'] AND design_pressure > 60
compliance_rules.message: WTE boiler design pressure above 60 bar(g) requires supercritical or high-subcritical design. Significantly increases boiler cost, alloy steel content, and IBR inspection requirements. Typical Indian WTE projects operate at 40–45 bar(g) to balance efficiency vs. capital cost. Justify pressure selection with turbine efficiency gain calculation.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Indian Boiler Regulations (IBR) 1950 amended — all steam-generating boilers above 1 kg/cm² and 22.75 litres require IBR certification. ASME Sec.I (Power Boilers) for imported boilers. WTE boiler industry practice: 40–45 bar(g) for plants up to 1000 t/day; 60–80 bar(g) only for plants above 1500 t/day where turbine efficiency gains justify the cost.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['Ash Landfill Cell','Leachate Collection System'] AND moc != 'HDPE (geomembrane / liner)'
compliance_rules.message: Ash landfill cells and leachate collection systems MUST use HDPE geomembrane as the primary containment liner. Other materials are not acceptable for hazardous ash or leachate containment. Minimum liner specification: 1.5 mm HDPE primary liner + geosynthetic clay liner (GCL) as secondary barrier per CPCB landfill design guidelines.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CPCB Criteria for Hazardous Waste Landfill Design (2013) — double liner mandatory for Class I hazardous waste (fly ash, bottom ash). EPA Subtitle D (US) — composite liner requirement for MSW landfills. IS 15826:2009 (Geosynthetics — HDPE Geomembrane) — minimum 1.0 mm for MSW, 1.5 mm for hazardous waste.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['Biogas Upgrading (Membrane)','Biogas Upgrading (PSA)'] AND throughput_capacity_t_day < 20
compliance_rules.message: Biogas upgrading below 20 t/day input (equivalent ~800 Nm³/hr raw biogas) may not be economically viable for biomethane injection or CNG vehicle fuel production. Consider direct use of raw biogas in a CHP engine instead. Upgrading economics improve significantly above 500 Nm³/hr clean methane output.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IEA Bioenergy — biomethane upgrading techno-economics: break-even typically at >300 Nm³/hr CH₄ output. MNRE (India) biomethane guidelines — CBG (Compressed Bio-Gas) plant minimum viable scale 1000 kg/day CBG output per SATAT scheme.

cost_build_up.basis: Mixed costing methodology by technology family. MRF/sorting and size-reduction equipment: power-law scaling on motor power and throughput. Thermal treatment (incineration/WTE): capacity-based equation on t/day with temperature and pressure multipliers. Composting and AD: capacity-based on t/day with volume scaling. Landfill items: area-based unit rates. All base costs in USD 2024, CEPCI = 816. Apply CEPCI escalation and regional factors for project-specific estimates.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: BEC = Tier_1_BEC × temperature_factor × pressure_factor × moc_multiplier
cost_build_up.full_formula: Total_Installed_Cost = BEC × (1 + civils_foundations + erection_installation + piping_electrical_instrumentation + commissioning) × (CEPCI_current / 816) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × (1 + import_duty_if_imported) × (1 + contingency)

base_equipment_costs_USD_2024.note: All base costs at reference conditions (ref throughput, ref power, standard MOC, India Metro base, CEPCI 816). Typical ranges given for sanity check. Scale using capacity/power exponents provided.

Waste Compactor (Stationary).notes: Stationary channel compactor or hook-lift compactor. Steel fabricated, AR400 liner. Includes hydraulic power pack, control panel.

Waste Compactor (Mobile).notes: Self-propelled or truck-mounted compactor. Includes cab, hydraulics, collection body. Price is for equipment only — excludes chassis for truck-mount variants.

Waste Shredder.notes: Single-shaft or dual-shaft slow-speed shredder. AR400/Hardox cutting tools. Includes infeed conveyor, control panel. Tooling replacement is significant OPEX — factor separately.

Waste Grinder.notes: Horizontal hammermill or slow-speed grinder. High-chrome hammers. Suitable for MSW, organic waste, wood waste.

Waste Pulper.notes: Wet pulper for organic fraction pre-treatment. SS316L wetted parts. Includes pump, pulper drum, dilution water system.

Ballistic Separator.notes: 2D/3D ballistic separator. Separates flat (2D), rolling (3D), and fines fractions. Polypropylene paddles, MS structure. Standard in European MRF design.

Air Classifier.notes: Zig-zag or drum-type air classifier. Separates light fraction (film plastic, paper) from heavy (glass, metal, stone). Includes fan, classifier body, air lock.

Eddy Current Separator.notes: Rare-earth eccentric pole drum ECS. Recovers non-ferrous metals (Al, Cu, brass). Belt conveyor with high-frequency magnetic rotor. Recovery ≥ 80% Al from MSW.

Magnetic Separator.notes: Overhead belt magnet (OHM) or drum magnet. Permanent or electro-magnet. Ferrous recovery ≥ 95%. Simplest and most cost-effective MRF sorting unit.

Optical Sorter.notes: NIR/VIS optical sorter with high-pressure compressed air ejectors. Sorts plastic by polymer type or paper/board fractions. European supplier (Tomra, Stadler, Bollegraaf). High capital but lowest opex for high-purity streams.

Trommel Screen.notes: Rotary drum screen, perforated steel panels. Standard screen sizes: 60 mm + 80 mm for MSW processing. First separation unit in most waste treatment facilities.

Vibrating Screen (Waste).notes: Single or double-deck vibrating screen. Screen panels: polyurethane or woven wire. For RDF/SRF fraction separation and compost screening.

Disc Screen.notes: Star/disc screen for 2D/3D waste fraction separation. PVC star or polyurethane disc elements on steel shafts. Self-cleaning design reduces blockages vs. vibrating screen.

Conveyor (Waste).notes: Heavy-duty belt conveyor for MSW reception, tipping hall, pre-sort. Width 1000–1400 mm, rubber chevron belt, MS structure. Rate per metre inclusive of drive, take-up, guarding.

Conveyor (Sorting).notes: Sorting/picking belt conveyor. Width 800–1000 mm, flat top belt, ergonomic raised sides, VFD drive. Includes lighting and picking station steel platform.

Baling Press.notes: Two-ram horizontal baling press. Bale size 1.1 × 0.7 × 0.5 m typical. Suitable for cardboard, paper, plastic film, aluminium. Output: 10–30 bales/hr.

Wrapping Machine.notes: Bale wrapper / stretch wrapper for baled secondary materials. Keeps bales dry and clean. Often paired with baling press in integrated RDF/SRF production line.

Transfer Station Compactor.notes: Heavy-duty stationary compactor for waste transfer station. Rams waste into hook-lift or roll-off containers. Includes hydraulic power unit, control system.

Transfer Station Container.notes: Steel hook-lift/roll-off container for compacted waste. Volume 20–40 m³. Includes tipping mechanism and cover. Requires transfer vehicle (separate cost).

Landfill Compactor.notes: Dedicated landfill compactor with steel drum and tamping feet. 30–55 tonne operating weight. Includes cab, dozer blade. Key equipment for achieving design compaction (≥ 0.7 t/m³).

Landfill Dozer.notes: Track dozer (D6–D8 class) for daily waste spreading and cover soil operations at landfill. 20–35 tonne. Essential landfill operating plant.

Landfill Excavator.notes: Track excavator (20–30 tonne, 0.9–1.5 m³ bucket) for cell preparation, liner installation supervision, leachate collection trench excavation.

Leachate Collection System.notes: Per hectare of lined cell area. Includes HDPE drainage blanket, perforated collection pipes (HDPE DN 200), sump, and collection header to leachate pond. Excludes liner cost (priced under Ash Landfill Cell).

Leachate Treatment Plant.notes: Compact LTP: MBR (membrane bioreactor) + RO or evaporation. For India landfill leachate: high ammonia, COD, TDS. Includes equalization tank, aeration system, membrane modules, RO reject management.

Landfill Gas Collection.notes: Per hectare of active landfill area. Includes vertical gas extraction wells (HDPE, DN 200), horizontal collection headers, condensate traps, and wellhead valves. Excludes flare and engine (separate items).

Landfill Gas Flare.notes: Enclosed ground flare with auto-ignition, flame arrestor, and flow control. Temperature ≥ 1000°C at burner. Complies with CPCB and EU flaring standards. Includes controls and gas analyser.

Landfill Gas Engine.notes: Gas-fuelled reciprocating engine genset (Jenbacher, Caterpillar, MWM). Includes engine, generator, cooling, gas treatment, exhaust. Electrical efficiency ~35–40%. Package supply including enclosure.

Incinerator (Grate).notes: Moving/reciprocating grate MSW incinerator. Includes grate, primary and secondary combustion chambers, refractory lining, induced draft fans, ash extraction. Excludes boiler, FGT, and stack — priced separately. Note: minimum viable scale ~100 t/day; below this cost per tonne becomes prohibitive.

Incinerator (Rotary Kiln).notes: Rotary kiln incinerator for hazardous waste, clinical waste, and industrial waste. Refractory-lined kiln + afterburner chamber at 1100°C. High alloy content. Includes kiln drive, seals, waste feeding system.

Incinerator (Pyrolysis).notes: Pyrolysis reactor + syngas combustion chamber. Operates at 400–600°C in oxygen-limited conditions. Suitable for plastics, tyres, biomass. Includes feedstock dryer, pyrolysis vessel, syngas handling.

Incinerator (Plasma).notes: Plasma gasification/vitrification technology. Plasma torches at 1500–5000°C. Highest capital cost of all thermal treatment technologies. Produces vitrified slag (landfillable). Suitable for hazardous, clinical, mixed difficult waste. Very limited operational references at scale.

WTE Boiler (Grate).notes: Water-tube boiler for grate-fired WTE. Alloy steel superheaters (T22/T91). IBR certified. Includes drum, evaporator, superheater, economiser, air heater, steam drum. Excludes grate and FGT.

WTE Boiler (CFB).notes: Circulating fluidised bed (CFB) boiler. Better fuel flexibility than grate — can handle RDF, biomass, and mixed waste. Higher capital than grate but lower OPEX for irregular waste streams. Includes cyclone, loop seal, bed material handling.

WTE Turbine.notes: Condensing or back-pressure steam turbine + generator. Includes gearbox, condenser, cooling tower, lube oil system, governor. Turbine efficiency: 15–25% for small WTE (condensing, 40 bar); up to 30% at 60 bar with reheat.

Flue Gas Treatment (Dry).notes: Dry sorption FGT: activated carbon injection (dioxins/Hg) + sodium bicarbonate/hydrated lime injection (HCl, SO₂) + baghouse filter (dust). Lowest capital. Reagent consumption is significant OPEX. Removal: HCl ≥ 95%, SO₂ ≥ 90%, dust < 10 mg/Nm³.

Flue Gas Treatment (Semi-Dry).notes: Semi-dry (spray-dry absorber/SDA) FGT: slaked lime slurry sprayed into reaction tower + baghouse filter. Better removal than dry, lower cost than wet. Effluent: dry residue — no wastewater. Removal: HCl ≥ 97%, SO₂ ≥ 95%.

Flue Gas Treatment (Wet).notes: Wet scrubber FGT: quench + acid scrubber (HCl, HF) + alkaline scrubber (SO₂). Highest removal efficiencies. Generates wastewater requiring treatment (included in scope). Includes wastewater neutralisation and dewatering. Best for large WTE plants (>500 t/day).

Bottom Ash Conveyor.notes: Wet extractor (ram-pusher type) or vibrating ash conveyor from grate to ash bunker. Water-cooled. Includes ash bunker, crane/grab, truck loading station. Bottom ash: ~20–25% by weight of MSW input.

Fly Ash Handling.notes: Pneumatic fly ash conveying system from ESP/baghouse hoppers to fly ash silo. Includes rotary valves, pneumatic conveying lines, ash silo (200–1000 m³), big-bag filling station or tanker loading. Fly ash is hazardous waste — closed handling mandatory.

Ash Stabilization.notes: Pug mill or drum mixer for cement/lime stabilization of fly ash before disposal. Reduces leachability of heavy metals to TCLP limits. Includes cement silo, dosing system, mixer, cured product conveyor. Cement dose: 10–15% by weight of ash.

Ash Landfill Cell.notes: Engineered hazardous waste landfill cell for stabilised fly ash and bottom ash. Per hectare. Includes double HDPE liner (1.5 mm + GCL), leachate collection system, gas venting layer, geotextile protection, and capping. Hazardous class I cell per CPCB 2013 guidelines.

Composting (Windrow).notes: Per m² of composting pad area. Includes concrete pad, leachate collection, windrow formation equipment, aeration, perimeter drain. Excludes compost turner (separate item). Minimum 8–12 week composting cycle.

Composting (In-Vessel).notes: Enclosed reactor drum, tunnel, or channel composting system. Controlled temperature (55–65°C), aeration, and leachate recirculation. Faster cycle: 14–21 days in-vessel + 4 weeks curing. Suitable for urban dense sites with odour constraints.

Composting (Aerated Static Pile).notes: Per m² of ASP pad area. Includes perforated floor aeration pipes, blowers, biofilter for odour control, leachate collection. Higher air control than windrow — suits wetter organic fractions.

Compost Turner.notes: Self-propelled ride-on windrow turner. Turning width 2.5–5 m, 500–2000 t/hr turning capacity. Diesel-powered. Used in windrow and ASP composting. Larger straddle turners for high-capacity sites.

Compost Screener.notes: Trommel or star screen for compost product grading. Screen size 10–20 mm. Removes contaminants (plastic, glass, stones) from finished compost. May include optical sorter for quality compost from mixed waste.

Anaerobic Digester (Dry).notes: Dry (high-solids, >20% TS) anaerobic digester: horizontal plug-flow (Dranco/Valorga/Kompogas type) or batch garage digester. Includes concrete reactor vessel, gas storage (HDPE membrane holder), digestate dewatering. HRT: 20–25 days.

Anaerobic Digester (Wet).notes: Wet (low-solids, 8–12% TS) mesophilic CSTR digester. Epoxy-coated steel or concrete vessel, mechanical mixing, gas-tight floating cover. Includes pre-treatment (shredder, pulper, grit/impurity removal), heat exchanger, digester vessel, biogas storage. HRT: 20–30 days.

Biogas Upgrading (Membrane).notes: Membrane separation biogas upgrading to biomethane (>95% CH₄). Includes compressor, membrane modules, methane slip management, drying, CO₂ vent or utilisation. Lower OPEX than PSA for large scale (>500 Nm³/hr).

Biogas Upgrading (PSA).notes: Pressure Swing Adsorption (PSA) upgrading. Activated carbon or zeolite molecular sieve beds. CH₄ purity >97%. Higher capital than membrane but better methane recovery (>99%) — preferred for biomethane injection to gas grid or CNG vehicle fuel (SATAT scheme India).

notes.freight_insurance: Most solid waste equipment is imported (European/Chinese suppliers). Use 0.05 for imported CIF India. Use 0.02 for domestic procurement (compactors, conveyors assembled locally).
notes.erection_installation: Mechanical erection, grouting, structural steel assembly. Higher than typical process plant due to bulky MRF equipment. WTE boiler erection: 0.30–0.35 of boiler cost.
notes.civil_structural: Significant civil component: tipping hall, sorting building, composting sheds, digester foundation, landfill cell preparation. Use 0.35 for WTE (turbine hall, bunker, control room). Use 0.15 for simple MRF sorting equipment only.
notes.piping_electrical_instrumentation: MCC panels, cable trays, field instruments, SCADA for process control. WTE plants have complex PLC/DCS — use 0.25 for thermal treatment. Use 0.15 for simple MRF/composting.
notes.contingency: AACE Class 4 standard for waste treatment: 15% (higher than standard due to technology risk, permit uncertainty, and waste composition variability). Use 20% for plasma and pyrolysis (limited Indian references). Use 12% for well-defined windrow composting.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_cost_USD × (current_CEPCI / 816) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × moc_multiplier × temperature_factor × (1 + contingency). All base costs calibrated to USA market 2024.

USA.local_ref: Richardson / Aspen ICARUS / EPA WTE cost data

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.note: Indicative only. WTE boiler/turbine HS 8402/8406; shredders HS 8436/8479; conveyors HS 8428; optical sorters HS 8543. Verify current rate and HS code with customs broker.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ
project_settings.escalation_factor_note: CEPCI escalation = 830/816 = 1.017 (+1.7% from 2024 base to mid-2025). Note: WTE equipment is predominantly imported from Europe (CEPCI + regional India factor 0.55 applied). Confirm CEPCI current value at time of estimate.

usd_cost_baseline.note: USA-base USD (matches index base year 2024). Engine applies index_now/base x regional x sub_region x fx. Public-benchmark ESTIMATES, verify=true. base_cost at ref throughput/power.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields, cost method, and compliance rules apply. Piping items are costed per metre run; civil chambers are lumpsum; tanks and WTP are volume/capacity-based.

fields.label: Tank / Sump Volume
fields.hint: Internal water storage capacity of the RCC tank or sump. Typical: OHT 50–500 m³ for industrial/township; underground sump 100–1000 m³. Volume is the primary cost driver — drives concrete volume, reinforcement, and shuttering costs.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Overhead Tank (RCC)','Underground Sump (RCC)']

fields.label: OHT Staging Height / Sump Depth
fields.hint: For Overhead Tank: height of RCC staging/support structure from GL to tank soffit (typical 6–20 m). For Underground Sump: excavation depth below GL (typical 3–6 m). Staging height significantly increases cost due to column/beam reinforcement — each additional 3 m stage adds ~8–12% to structural cost.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Overhead Tank (RCC)','Underground Sump (RCC)']

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of units (for chambers, tanks, hydrants, borewells) OR length in metres (for pipes). For piping, enter total route length in metres — cost is computed as unit rate (INR/USD per metre run including laying, jointing, and bedding).

fields.label: Pipe Nominal Diameter (DN)
fields.hint: Nominal pipe diameter per IS 8329 (DI), IS 3589 (MS), IS 4985 (uPVC), IS 1239 (GI). Pipe diameter is the single largest cost driver for all piping line items — larger diameter increases material, laying, and bedding costs non-linearly. Select per hydraulic design; minimum 80 mm for service pipes, 150 mm for distribution mains.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Water Main (DI Pipe K9)','Water Main (MS Pipe)','Distribution Pipe (PVC)','Service Pipe (GI)']

fields.label: Pipe Pressure Class / Wall Thickness
fields.hint: Pipe pressure class determines wall thickness and hence material weight and cost. DI K9 is standard for water mains up to PN 12.5. Upgrading from K9 to K12 adds ~15–20% to pipe material cost. For uPVC distribution: Class 4 (PN 10) for pressure zones, Class 3 for gravity-fed low-pressure areas.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Water Main (DI Pipe K9)','Water Main (MS Pipe)','Distribution Pipe (PVC)','Service Pipe (GI)']

fields.label: Laying Condition / Trench Type
fields.hint: Laying condition determines civils, bedding, and reinstatement costs. Normal soil open trench is base case. Rocky / urban crossings can increase laying cost by 2–5x. Jack & bore or HDD crossings are priced separately as lumpsum — use quantity = No. of crossings.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Water Main (DI Pipe K9)','Water Main (MS Pipe)','Distribution Pipe (PVC)','Service Pipe (GI)']

fields.label: Borewell Depth
fields.hint: Total drilled depth of borewell from GL. Typical range: 60–300 m for medium-depth aquifer access in hard rock terrain (Deccan Trap, Vindhyan). Cost is dominated by drilling rate (INR/m) plus casing and development. Yield testing and pump installation quoted separately.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Tube Well (Borewell)'

fields.label: Borewell Casing Diameter
fields.hint: MS casing pipe diameter governs the submersible pump that can be installed. 200 mm casing accepts up to ~7.5 kW submersible. 250 mm and above for higher-yield wells. Casing length typically 20–40% of total depth. Larger casing adds ~20–35% to drilling + casing cost.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Tube Well (Borewell)'

fields.label: WTP Treatment Capacity
fields.hint: Rated treated water output per day at design capacity for compact WTP. Typical compact modular units: 100–5000 m³/day. Includes clarification, filtration, and disinfection. Cost per m³/day drops significantly above 1000 m³/day (economy of scale, exponent ~0.65).

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Water Treatment Plant (Compact)'

fields.label: WTP Treatment Process
fields.hint: Treatment train determines number of unit processes and chemical dosing systems. Iron/manganese and arsenic removal units carry 25–50% cost premium over standard clarification trains. Membrane (UF) process is 60–80% higher than conventional. Select based on raw water quality analysis.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype === 'Water Treatment Plant (Compact)'

fields.label: Chamber / Structure Type
fields.hint: Construction material for valve, meter, air valve, or washout chambers. RCC (M20) is standard for buried chambers below roads and traffic loading areas per IRC SP 20. Brick masonry acceptable for chambers in non-traffic areas and depths ≤ 1.5 m. GRP precast adds 20–30% to material cost but reduces on-site labour significantly.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Air Valve Chamber','Sluice Valve Chamber','Washout Chamber','Water Meter Chamber']

fields.label: MOC / Pipe Material
fields.hint: Pipe / structure material of construction. DI is the preferred material for water mains above DN 150 due to high tensile strength, corrosion resistance, and ease of jointing (push-fit rubber ring). uPVC for distribution networks DN 80–250 due to lower cost and no internal corrosion. HDPE for flexible/rural distribution. GI for service connections only.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Water Main (DI Pipe K9)','Water Main (MS Pipe)','Distribution Pipe (PVC)','Service Pipe (GI)']

fields.label: Fire Hydrant Outlet Size & Standard
options: Double outlet 100 mm + 63 mm (IS 908 Type B, heavy duty)
fields.hint: Fire hydrant outlet configuration per IS 908. Single post hydrant has one 63 mm outlet. Double post has two 63 mm outlets. Type B heavy-duty has a 100 mm steamer outlet plus 63 mm hose outlet — required for high-rise and industrial estates per NBC 2016 Clause 4. Minimum hydrant flow: 1000 LPM at 1.0 kg/cm² residual pressure.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Fire Hydrant (Double Post)','Fire Hydrant (Single Post)']

fields.label: Pipe Length (m)
fields.hint: Total pipeline route length in metres for this diameter and class. Do not net off fittings — fittings allowance (tees, bends, couplings) is included in the unit rate at 12–15% of straight pipe cost. For multiple diameters or laying conditions, use separate BOQ line items.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Water Main (DI Pipe K9)','Water Main (MS Pipe)','Distribution Pipe (PVC)','Service Pipe (GI)']

fields.label: Pipe Diameter (numeric)
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields, cost method, and compliance rules apply. Piping items are costed per metre run; civil chambers are lumpsum; tanks and WTP are volume/capacity-based.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Service Pipe (GI)' AND pipe_diameter_mm_numeric > 50
compliance_rules.message: GI service pipes above DN 50 are not recommended for new water supply installations. GI is prone to tuberculation, scale build-up, and corrosion reducing effective bore within 10–15 years. Use uPVC (IS 4985) or HDPE (IS 4984) for service connections above DN 50.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 1239:2004 (GI pipes) — recommended maximum DN 50 for service connections. CPHEEO Manual on Water Supply (2000) Chapter 8 — recommends uPVC/HDPE for distribution.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: chamber_type == 'Brick masonry (1-brick wall)' AND equipment_subtype IN ['Sluice Valve Chamber','Air Valve Chamber']
compliance_rules.message: Brick masonry chambers under road/traffic loading areas are susceptible to settlement and cracking. Verify chamber is in a non-traffic zone. If under road, upgrade to RCC M20 construction per IRC SP 20 recommendations.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IRC:SP:20-2002 (Rural Roads Manual) — chamber construction under road carriageway must be RCC. IS 456:2000 — M20 concrete minimum for structural members in contact with ground.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Overhead Tank (RCC)' AND staging_depth_m > 15
compliance_rules.message: OHT staging height exceeds 15 m. Structural design must account for wind load, seismic zone classification, and torsion per IS 11682. Cost estimate for staging may be understated — request detailed structural BOQ for staging cost above 15 m.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 11682:1985 (Criteria for Design of RCC Staging for Overhead Water Tanks). IS 1893 (Seismic Design) — elevated tanks are more vulnerable to earthquake damage; staging design must be reviewed by structural engineer above 15 m height.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Tube Well (Borewell)' AND borewell_depth_m > 200
compliance_rules.message: Borewell depth exceeds 200 m. Deep borewells in hard rock require DTH (Down-the-Hole) hammer drilling — daily progress reduces significantly and cost per metre increases by 30–50% beyond 150 m depth. Consider multiple shallower wells as an alternative if hydrogeological report supports it.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CGWB Guidelines for Borewell Drilling (2019) — DTH hammer recommended below 60 m in hard rock. BIS Drinking Water Standard IS 10500:2012 — post-drilling water quality test mandatory before commissioning.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Water Treatment Plant (Compact)' AND wtp_capacity_m3_day > 2000
compliance_rules.message: WTP capacity exceeds 2000 m³/day. A compact modular unit at this scale may be uneconomical compared to a conventional designed-and-built plant. Request techno-commercial comparison between compact WTP and conventional RCC-structure WTP at this capacity.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: CPHEEO Manual on Water Supply (2000) — compact package plants typically economical up to 2 MLD (2000 m³/day). Above this, conventional gravity-fed plants with RCC structures are generally more cost-effective and maintainable.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype IN ['Fire Hydrant (Double Post)','Fire Hydrant (Single Post)'] AND (quantity < 1)
compliance_rules.message: Fire hydrant quantity must be at least 1. Hydrant spacing per NBC 2016 Fire Chapter: maximum 45 m for high-risk industrial areas, 90 m for general industrial, 150 m for residential areas. Verify spacing on site layout drawing.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: NBC 2016 Part 4 (Fire & Life Safety) Clause 4.9 — hydrant spacing requirements. IS 908:2016 (Post and Sluice Type Fire Hydrants) — manufacturing and testing standard. TAC (Tariff Advisory Committee) fire protection requirements for insured premises.

cost_build_up.basis: Mixed costing methodology: piping items use unit_rate (INR per metre run); civil chambers use lumpsum unit cost; tanks and WTP use capacity-based equipment_equation with power-law scaling. All base costs in USD 2024, escalated via ENR CCI. Regional factor per project location.
cost_build_up.tier_1_formula: Cost = base_unit_rate_USD × pipe_length_m × quantity  [for piping]  OR  base_lumpsum_USD × quantity  [for chambers/hydrants]  OR  base_cost_USD × (volume_or_capacity / ref_capacity)^exponent × quantity  [for tanks/WTP/borewell]
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: Cost = Tier_1_Cost × laying_condition_multiplier × pipe_pressure_class_factor × process_cost_multiplier  [as applicable to item type]
cost_build_up.full_formula: Total_Installed_Cost = BEC × (1 + civils_foundations + erection_installation + piping_valves_fittings + electrical_instrumentation + commissioning) × (ENR_CCI_current / ENR_CCI_base) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × (1 + contingency)

base_unit_rates_USD_2024.note: Piping unit rates: USD per metre run, inclusive of pipe material, jointing, excavation in normal soil, bedding (sand), reinstatement and compaction. Reference: DN 200, normal soil. Escalate with ENR CCI and regional factor.

Water Main (DI Pipe K9).notes: DI K9 per IS 8329. Rubber ring push-fit joints. Includes pipe, joint, excavation, sand bed, compaction, reinstatement. Fittings allowance 12% included.

Water Main (MS Pipe).notes: MS pipe IS 3589 with internal cement mortar lining and external bitumen coating. Welded joints. Includes laying, jointing, testing.

Distribution Pipe (PVC).notes: uPVC IS 4985 or HDPE PE100 IS 4984. Solvent cement / compression fittings for uPVC; butt-fusion for HDPE. Normal soil open trench.

Service Pipe (GI).notes: GI IS 1239 medium grade. Screwed joints. For service connections from distribution main to consumer meter. Maximum recommended DN 50.

Fire Hydrant (Double Post).notes: Includes double-outlet hydrant IS 908, supply valve, hydrant tee on main, 200 mm depth concrete pad, painted post. Excludes fire tender access road.

Fire Hydrant (Single Post).notes: Single-outlet 63 mm hydrant IS 908, supply valve, concrete pad, post. Excludes connection tee to main (priced under piping item).

Water Meter Chamber.notes: RCC chamber, lockable CI cover, meter and isolating valves. Cost varies by meter size (DN 50 – DN 200).

Air Valve Chamber.notes: RCC chamber, double-acting air valve (DSAW) IS 14845, CI cover, bypass valve. High-point locations on pipeline profile.

Sluice Valve Chamber.notes: RCC or brick chamber, sluice valve IS 780 (CI/DI), spindle and cap, CI cover, step irons. Cost varies by valve diameter DN 80–DN 400.

Washout Chamber.notes: RCC chamber, sluice valve, washout pipe to drain/nala. Located at low points on pipeline profile.

Overhead Tank (RCC).notes: RCC Intze type or circular. Includes staging, tank shell, dome, inlet/outlet/overflow pipes, ladder, fencing, painting. Structural design per IS 11682 and IS 456.

Underground Sump (RCC).notes: RCC M25 (IS 456) waterproof sump with internal waterproofing treatment, manhole covers, vent pipes, inlet/outlet/overflow. Excludes dewatering if groundwater encountered.

Tube Well (Borewell).notes: Includes mobilisation, drilling, MS casing, slotted screen, gravel pack, development and yield testing. Excludes submersible pump and rising main (separate line item under SCHEMA-WATER-UTIL).

Water Treatment Plant (Compact).notes: Modular skid-mounted compact WTP. Includes civil foundation slab, electrical panel, dosing systems, sludge handling. Standard coagulation-filtration-chlorination train. Apply process_cost_multiplier for non-standard treatment trains.

notes.freight_insurance: Applies to manufactured items (pipes, valves, hydrants, WTP skid). Domestic procurement: 0.02. Imported equipment: 0.05.
notes.erection_installation: Mechanical/civil erection. For piping: included in unit rate. For tanks, WTP, borewell: applies to mechanical components.
notes.civil_structural: Dominant cost component for RCC tanks, sumps, and chamber construction — applies at 0.30 of equipment value. For piping: civil cost (trench, bedding, reinstatement) included in unit rate.
notes.piping_valves_fittings: Fittings allowance 12% already included in piping unit rates above. Apply 0.12 additionally only if fittings are being priced separately.
notes.contingency: AACE Class 4: 12% standard. Increase to 20% for borewell (geological uncertainty) and greenfield rural WTP.

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate_USD × (current_ENR_CCI / 14250) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × laying_multiplier × process_multiplier × (1 + contingency). regional_factor converts USA base to project region.
regional_cost_index.index_basis_decision: item_type corrected E->A: unit-rate construction/installation work, field-labour dominated (matches PLASTERING / STORM-DRAIN / EARTHING library convention). ENR CCI retained, base 14250 (2024).

regional_factors.equipment_duty_category: Civil construction and water supply infrastructure
regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

import_duty.category: Civil infrastructure and water supply materials
import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use. DI pipes and fittings: HS 7303. uPVC pipes: HS 3917. WTP compact units: HS 8421.

import_duty.category: Civil infrastructure and water supply materials
import_duty.note: Indicative only. Verify HS code and current rate with customs broker before use.

project_settings.note: Set these at project level - apply to all items in the BOQ
project_settings.escalation_factor_note: ENR CCI escalation = 14480/14250 = 1.016 (+1.6% from 2024 base to mid-2025)

usd_cost_baseline.note: USA-base USD (matches index base year 2024). Engine applies index_now/base x regional x sub_region x fx. Public-benchmark ESTIMATES, verify=true. unit per item (per-m / lumpsum).

Water Main (DI Pipe K9).basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/m 72 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 91.61. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Water Main (MS Pipe).basis: RSMeans-class ~2018 USD/m 38 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 48.35. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Distribution Pipe (PVC).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/m 28 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 37.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Service Pipe (GI).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/m 28 escalated via CEPCI to 2024 = 37.89. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Fire Hydrant (Double Post).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 1800 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 2290.18. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Fire Hydrant (Single Post).basis: vendor-survey-class ~2018 USD/unit 1800 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 2290.18. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Water Meter Chamber.basis: CPWD-DSR-class ~2018 USD/unit 850 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 1081.47. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Air Valve Chamber.basis: CPWD-DSR-class ~2018 USD/unit 850 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 1081.47. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Sluice Valve Chamber.basis: CPWD-DSR-class ~2018 USD/unit 850 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 1081.47. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Washout Chamber.basis: CPWD-DSR-class ~2018 USD/unit 850 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 1081.47. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Overhead Tank (RCC).basis: CPWD-DSR-class ~2018 USD/m3 380 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 483.48. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Underground Sump (RCC).basis: CPWD-DSR-class ~2018 USD/m3 290 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 368.97. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

Tube Well (Borewell).basis: CPWD-DSR-class ~2018 USD/m 75 escalated via ENR to 2024 = 95.42. Confirm against local quote/DSR.

SCHEMA-WATER-SUPPLY._step5_engineering_gap: pipe_diameter_mm_numeric derived field not defined; rule R-001 needs it

SCHEMA-WATER-UTIL.generation_note: v1.0 RELEASE - All fields, cost formulas, compliance rules, adders, and spec strings completed by cost estimate engineer. Based on AACE Class 4 methodology, CEPCI 2024 baseline, Indian market library data. | v1.0-priced: usd_cost_baseline + multiplier tables (USA-base; public-benchmark, verify=true).

tier_summary.note: Tier 1 = Quick +-30% estimate. Tier 2 = Budget +-20%. Tier 3 = Detailed +-10%.

SCHEMA-WATER-UTIL.spec_formula: {equipment_subtype} | Q={flow_rate} m³/hr | P={motor_power} kW | MOC={moc} | DP={design_pressure} bar(g) | Vol={volume} L | Eff={efficiency}% | Std={standard} | Qty={quantity} No.
SCHEMA-WATER-UTIL.spec_example: Domestic Water Pump | Q=20 m³/hr | P=3.7 kW | MOC=cast iron | DP=15 bar(g) | Qty=2 No.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields and cost formula are applied below.

fields.label: Motor Power
fields.hint: Rated motor shaft power in kW. Typical range: 2.2 kW (small domestic pump) to 12 kW (large RO high-pressure pump). Drives equipment cost and MCC/panel sizing.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Domestic Water Pump','Submersible Sewage Pump','Reverse Osmosis System','Demineralization Plant (2-Bed)']

fields.label: Flow Rate
fields.hint: Rated design flow at normal operating conditions. For pumps: normal duty flow. For RO/DM/Softener: treated water output. Typical range: 10–50 m³/hr for industrial utility service.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Domestic Water Pump','Submersible Sewage Pump','Reverse Osmosis System','Demineralization Plant (2-Bed)','Water Softener (Twin Tank)']

fields.label: Tank / Vessel Volume
fields.hint: Internal vessel/tank volume for DM plant exchange vessels, RO pressure vessels, or treated water storage. Typically 10–50 m³ for utility-scale installations. Not applicable for pumps.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Demineralization Plant (2-Bed)','Reverse Osmosis System','Water Softener (Twin Tank)']

fields.label: Voltage
fields.hint: Supply voltage for motor or control panel. Standard industrial supply in India/most markets: 415 V, 3-phase, 50 Hz. Affects MCC panel and cable sizing costs. Override if site is 380 V or 480 V (USA).

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Domestic Water Pump','Submersible Sewage Pump','Reverse Osmosis System','Demineralization Plant (2-Bed)','Water Softener (Twin Tank)','Ozone Generator']

fields.label: Design Pressure
fields.hint: Maximum allowable working pressure (MAWP) of pressure-containing parts. For RO membranes: typically 10–16 bar(g). For DM vessel shells: 6–10 bar(g). Drives shell wall thickness and flange rating (ASME B16.5 / IS 6392).

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Reverse Osmosis System','Demineralization Plant (2-Bed)','Water Softener (Twin Tank)']

fields.label: Tank Volume (Litres)
fields.hint: Resin/media bed volume or brine tank capacity for softener/DM plant. UV chamber volume for UV sterilizer sizing. Typical range: 316 L (small UV unit) to 5000 L (large DM twin bed). Primary sizing driver for resin and media costs.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Demineralization Plant (2-Bed)','Water Softener (Twin Tank)','UV Sterilizer']

fields.label: Efficiency
fields.hint: Overall system/pump efficiency at rated duty point. For pumps: hydraulic efficiency, typically 60–80%. For RO: water recovery ratio, typically 55–75%. Affects operating cost estimation and motor sizing adequacy check.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Domestic Water Pump','Submersible Sewage Pump','Reverse Osmosis System']

fields.label: MOC (Wetted Parts)
fields.hint: Material of construction for all wetted/process-contact parts. SS316L required for potable water, pharma, and seawater-adjacent service. Cast Iron / CS acceptable for raw water and sewage. MOC is a significant cost multiplier — SS316L adds ~30–50% over CI baseline.

fields.label: Design Standard
fields.hint: Primary governing design and manufacturing standard. Affects third-party inspection scope, documentation requirements, and vendor pool. Specifying international standards (ISO, ASME) may attract 10–20% cost premium over IS equivalents.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Number of identical units in this line item. Includes installed spares. Example: 2 = 1 duty + 1 standby.

fields.label: UV Lamp Power / Ozone Output
fields.hint: For UV Sterilizer: rated UV lamp power in Watts (typical: 55–500 W). For Ozone Generator: ozone production rate in g/hr (typical: 10–500 g/hr). Primary sizing and cost driver for these equipment types.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['UV Sterilizer','Ozone Generator']

fields.label: Pump Head (TDH)
fields.hint: Total Dynamic Head at rated flow. Determines impeller sizing and motor power. Typical: 15–50 m for domestic water service, 20–60 m for sewage, up to 150 m for RO high-pressure pumps. Verify against system curve before finalising.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Domestic Water Pump','Submersible Sewage Pump']

compliance_rules.condition_expr: design_pressure > 10.5 AND equipment_subtype == 'Demineralization Plant (2-Bed)'
compliance_rules.message: Design pressure exceeds 10.5 bar(g) for DM vessel. Verify shell wall thickness complies with IS 2825 / ASME VIII Div.1 and confirm MAWP with vessel vendor. Cost premium of 15–25% expected for high-pressure vessel design.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: IS 2825:1969 (Unfired Pressure Vessels) / ASME VIII Div.1 — wall thickness mandatory recalculation above 10.5 bar; Class 150 flange limit.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: motor_power > 0 AND flow_rate > 0 AND pump_tdh > 0 AND (flow_rate * pump_tdh * 1000 * 9.81) / (3600 * efficiency/100 * motor_power * 1000) > 1.15
compliance_rules.message: Hydraulic power check: computed shaft power exceeds motor rating by more than 15%. Risk of motor overload. Review motor selection or pump efficiency assumption. Under-sized motor will impact OPEX and reliability.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Pump power formula: P_shaft = (Q × H × ρ × g) / η. Motor rated power must be ≥ P_shaft × 1.10 service factor per IS 325 / IEC 60034.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: efficiency < 65 AND equipment_subtype IN ['Domestic Water Pump','Submersible Sewage Pump']
compliance_rules.message: Pump efficiency below 65% is below industry benchmark for this flow/head range. Consider reviewing impeller selection or upgrading to a higher-efficiency model. Low efficiency increases OPEX and lifecycle cost.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: BEP efficiency guidance per Hydraulic Institute ANSI/HI 1.3. IE3 motor efficiency class per IS 12615:2018 / IEC 60034-30-1.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: moc == 'Cast Iron (CI)' AND equipment_subtype == 'Reverse Osmosis System'
compliance_rules.message: Cast Iron is not acceptable for RO system wetted parts. RO permeate and concentrate streams are aggressive to ferrous materials. Minimum MOC: SS316L for all membrane housings, headers, and instrument connections.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASTM A276 / ASME B31.3 corrosion allowance requirements. RO system design typically requires SS316L per membrane manufacturer warranties and WHO drinking water guidelines.

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'UV Sterilizer' AND flow_rate > 25
compliance_rules.message: Flow rate above 25 m³/hr for UV sterilizer requires multi-lamp chamber or parallel units. Single-pass UV dose (min 40 mJ/cm²) compliance must be verified at peak flow. Single unit cost estimate may be understated.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: WHO Guidelines for Drinking Water Quality (4th Ed) — UV dose ≥ 40 mJ/cm² at 254 nm. NSF/ANSI 55 Class A (40 mJ/cm²) for disinfection.

cost_build_up.basis: AACE Class 4 order-of-magnitude. Base equipment cost (BEC) derived from power-law / capacity scaling equations calibrated to 2024 Indian market data (CEPCI = 816). Regional factor and CEPCI escalation applied per project settings.
cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: BEC = base_cost_USD × (flow_rate / ref_flow_rate)^0.6 × (motor_power / ref_motor_power)^0.3 × moc_multiplier × pressure_factor × quantity
cost_build_up.full_formula: Total_Installed_Cost = BEC × (1 + freight_insurance + erection_installation + commissioning + piping_electrical_instrumentation) × (CEPCI_project / CEPCI_base) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × (1 + import_duty_if_imported) × (1 + contingency)

base_equipment_costs_USD_2024.note: Reference costs at standard conditions: ref_flow = 20 m³/hr, ref_motor = 5.5 kW, MOC = CI/CS, India Metro base. Escalate with CEPCI and regional factor.

notes.freight_insurance: CIF basis, India import. Use 0.03 for domestic procurement.
notes.erection_installation: Includes mechanical erection, grouting, alignment, bolted connections. Range: 0.15 (simple pumps) to 0.30 (package plants with skid piping).
notes.piping_electrical_instrumentation: Interconnecting piping to battery limits, MCC/LCP panel, field instruments. Adjust to 0.15 if package unit supplied pre-piped.
notes.civil_structural: Equipment foundation, anchor bolts, plinth. Increase to 0.18 for underground sump/pump pit.
notes.contingency: AACE Class 4 standard. Reduce to 0.10 for well-defined scope; increase to 0.18 for remote/greenfield.

pressure_factor_reference.note: Applied to vessels/RO systems only. For pumps: use motor power scaling.

regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate_USD × (current_CEPCI / 816) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor × moc_multiplier × pressure_factor × adders. regional_factor converts USA base to project region.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.

fields.label: Equipment / Activity Sub-type
fields.hint: Select the specific equipment or activity type. Controls which fields are shown below.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.hint: Quantity of this item. Use dominant unit per sub-type (welds for RT/UT, joints for orbital, LM for structural/handrail).

fields.label: Unit of Measure
fields.hint: RT/UT → Weld (each). Orbital → Joint (each). Structural / Handrail welding → LM or kg.

fields.label: Material Group
fields.hint: Material group per ASME Section IX / ISO 15608. Drives preheat, PWHT and welder qualification requirements. High-alloy = multiplier on labour rate.

fields.label: Nominal Pipe / Section Diameter (OD)
fields.hint: Outer diameter in mm. For structural sections enter equivalent flange width. Used to compute weld volume and RT/UT shot count.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Radiography testing of welds','Ultrasonic testing of welds','Orbital welding']

fields.label: Wall Thickness
fields.hint: Wall thickness in mm. Drives weld passes (and thus welder-hours), RT IQI sensitivity class and UT probe selection.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Radiography testing of welds','Ultrasonic testing of welds','Orbital welding','Structural welding']

fields.label: Welding / NDT Process
fields.hint: Welding process or NDT technique. Controls productivity rate, consumable cost and equipment mob/demob cost.

fields.label: Weld / Test Position
fields.hint: Welding position per ISO 6947 / AWS D1.1. Overhead and all-position incur a labour multiplier vs flat shopwork.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Structural welding','Handrail welding','Orbital welding','Radiography testing of welds','Ultrasonic testing of welds']

fields.label: Service / Design Pressure Class
options: Low pressure (≤ 15 barg) – utility / ASME B31.1
Medium pressure (16–40 barg) – ASME B31.3 Normal Fluid
High pressure (41–150 barg) – ASME B31.3 Category D/M
Very high (>150 barg) – ASME B31.3 High Pressure
fields.default: Medium pressure (16–40 barg) – ASME B31.3 Normal Fluid
fields.hint: Governs NDT extent (% RT/UT required), PWHT mandatory threshold and welder qualification class.

fields.label: NDT Extent / Examination Level
options: Spot check only (structural per AWS D1.1)
fields.hint: Percentage of welds subject to RT or UT. Drives direct NDT labour and film/equipment cost. Higher % = significant cost adder.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Radiography testing of welds','Ultrasonic testing of welds']

fields.label: PWHT Required
fields.hint: Post-Weld Heat Treatment per ASME VIII Div.1 UCS-56 / ASME B31.3 Table 331.1. Mandatory for Cr-Mo (P5A) and heavy CS >38mm. Adds significant mob/demob + heating cost.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Structural welding','Orbital welding','Handrail welding','Radiography testing of welds']

fields.label: Preheat Required
fields.hint: Interpass preheat per AWS D1.1 / ASME B31.3. Increases labour time per weld (heater setup, temp monitoring). Critical for P5 materials.

fields.label: Location / Access Difficulty
options: Brownfield / operating plant – permit to work
fields.hint: Access difficulty drives a labour uplift. Confined space and brownfield (PTW) are significant multipliers on productive time.

fields.label: Weld Repair Rate (assumed)
fields.hint: Expected weld rejection / repair rate (%). Industry baseline: 3–5% CS, 8–12% SS/Duplex. Each repair = re-weld + re-test cost. Drives contingency in detailed estimate.

fields.label: Mob / Demob Required
options: No separate mob (included in site labour)
fields.default: No separate mob (included in site labour)
fields.hint: Specialist NDT crews (TOFD, PAUT, RT) often require separate mobilisation. RT requires radiation source transport permit.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype IN ['Radiography testing of welds','Ultrasonic testing of welds']

fields.label: WPS / PQR Status
options: WPS required – procedure qualification trials needed
WPS + WPQT required (special material or process)
fields.hint: Welding Procedure Specification / Procedure Qualification Record per ASME IX / ISO 15614. Procedure qualification is a separate lump-sum cost item if not yet qualified.

fields.label: Radiographic / Source Film Size
fields.hint: RT film/CR plate size per ASME V Article 2. Larger diameter pipes require larger film. Affects shot count and film/CR consumable cost.

conditional_display.show_when: equipment_subtype = 'Radiography testing of welds'

compliance_rules.condition_expr: pressure_class == 'Very high (>150 barg) – ASME B31.3 High Pressure' AND ndt_extent != '100% examination (Category M / lethal service)'
compliance_rules.message: ASME B31.3 Clause 341.4.1: High-pressure (Category M) service requires 100% radiographic or ultrasonic examination of all butt welds. Update NDT Extent to 100%.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3-2022 Clause 341.4.1 & Table 341.3.2; Category M fluid service mandatory 100% examination

compliance_rules.condition_expr: (material_group == 'P5A – Chrome-Moly (Cr-Mo 5%)' OR material_group == 'P21 – Nickel Alloy (Inconel/Hastelloy)') AND pwht_required == 'No PWHT'
compliance_rules.message: WARNING: P5A Cr-Mo and P21 Nickel Alloy materials typically require PWHT per ASME B31.3 Table 331.1.1 and VIII Div.1 UCS-56. Confirm with metallurgist before excluding PWHT.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME B31.3-2022 Table 331.1.1; ASME VIII Div.1 UCS-56; WRC Bulletin 452

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Orbital welding' AND pipe_diameter_mm > 168.3
compliance_rules.message: WARNING: Orbital GTAW (auto-TIG) is typically limited to OD ≤ 168.3mm (6-inch). Above this diameter, confirm orbital head availability or revert to manual GTAW.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: AWS C5.7 Recommended Practices for Electrogas Welding; orbital machine OD range per manufacturer spec (e.g. Polysoude, Arc Machines Inc.)

compliance_rules.condition_expr: ('RT' IN weld_process OR 'UT' IN weld_process) AND NOT equipment_subtype IN ['Radiography testing of welds','Ultrasonic testing of welds']
compliance_rules.message: ERROR: RT/UT process selected but activity sub-type is not an NDT item. Change sub-type to Radiography or Ultrasonic testing, or select a welding process.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: Schema consistency rule – NDT process codes only valid for NDT activity sub-types

compliance_rules.condition_expr: equipment_subtype == 'Radiography testing of welds' AND weld_process == 'RT – X-Ray Film (Radiography)' AND access_difficulty == 'Brownfield / operating plant – permit to work'
compliance_rules.message: WARNING: Gamma/X-ray radiography in an operating plant requires a radiation exclusion zone (typically 30–50m). Confirm shutdown window, radiation survey and safety shutdown procedure before pricing.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: AERB Safety Code No. AERB/NRF-RP/SC/1; IAEA Safety Standards SSR-6; ASME V Article 2 T-277

compliance_rules.condition_expr: material_group != 'P1 – Carbon Steel (CS)' AND wps_status == 'WPS/PQR already qualified – no cost'
compliance_rules.message: INFO: Non-P1 material selected. Confirm that existing WPS/PQR covers this material group, process, and thickness range before assuming no qualification cost.
compliance_rules.engineering_basis: ASME Section IX QW-200 – essential variables; ISO 15614-1 qualification range rules

base_unit_rates_usd.note: Base rates in USD for USA average region (TX/IL/OH/FL). All other regions = base_rate × regional_cost_index.factor × sub_region.labour_factor. Rates = all-in (labour + supervision + small tools + consumables). Year basis: 2024.

Handrail welding.basis: SMAW / GMAW, carbon steel, flat/horizontal, ≤50mm OD tube, greenfield access. Inclusive of fit-up, tacking, final weld and visual inspection.

Orbital welding.basis: Orbital GTAW, SS 304/316 or CS, ≤ 2-inch spool, shop/spool fabrication environment. Includes machine setup, purge, weld and borescope check.

Radiography testing of welds.basis: RT X-Ray film, CS, DN100 (4-inch), 5G position, 5% extent, single-wall single-image technique. Includes film, processing, interpretation and report.

Structural welding.basis: SMAW, CS, horizontal/vertical, 10mm fillet, greenfield access, AWS D1.1. Includes fit-up, welding and visual inspection.

Ultrasonic testing of welds.basis: UT Manual A-Scan, CS, DN100, 5G position, 5% extent. Includes calibration block, couplant, scanning and report.

cost_build_up.tier_2_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype, tier_2) × quantity × regional_cost_index.factor × sub_region.labour_factor × material_cost_multiplier(material_group) × process_productivity_factor(weld_process) × position_labour_multiplier(weld_position) × (1 + ndt_extent_multiplier(ndt_extent) × 0.8) × (1 + preheat_labour_adder_pct(preheat_required))
cost_build_up.full_formula: base_unit_rate(equipment_subtype, tier_3) × quantity × regional_cost_index.factor × sub_region.labour_factor × material_cost_multiplier(material_group) × process_productivity_factor(weld_process) × position_labour_multiplier(weld_position) × (1 + ndt_extent_multiplier × 0.8) × (1 + preheat_labour_adder_pct) × access_labour_multiplier(access_difficulty) × (1 + repair_rate_pct/100 × 1.5) × (1 + pwht_cost_adder_pct) + mob_cost_adder_usd(mob_demob) + wps_adder_usd(wps_status)
formula_notes: ndt_extent_multiplier × 0.8 → 80% of the unit rate applies to additional welds beyond the base 5%.
repair_rate_pct × 1.5 → Each repair weld = 1× re-weld + 0.5× re-test cost premium.
pwht_cost_adder_pct is applied to the subtotal labour cost before mob/WPS adders.
mob_cost_adder_usd and wps_adder_usd are fixed lump sums added after the rate-based subtotal.
All costs are in USD. Convert to local currency using exchange rate at project base date.

adders.note: Welding & NDT is a site labour activity. erection_installation adder = 0 (labour is the primary cost driver, not equipment supply). freight_insurance covers consumable delivery (electrodes, film, gases).

regional_cost_index.universal_basis: ENR CCI (Engineering News Record Construction Cost Index)
regional_cost_index.note: Cost = base_rate × (current_index / base_value) × regional_factor × sub_region_factor. regional_factor converts USA base to project region. local_ref = regional index for validation only - not used in calculation.

regional_factors.note: Apply one region based on project location. Sub-region determines eq/labour/civil factors and inland freight.
